school_name: Alexandra Hills TAFE Senior Studies Program
overview: TAFE Queensland offers Merit Scholarships to recognize and support students who have achieved outstanding academic merit or demonstrate strong work performance. These scholarships assist students looking to advance their careers through vocational training at TAFE Queensland.
description: Merit scholarships are awarded to students who have achieved outstanding academic merit or demonstrate strong work performance along with the demonstrable desire to secure ongoing employment within the industry to which they applied for the scholarship. The scholarship provides $5,000 in financial assistance, with $3,000 paid after commencement of study in Semester 1, 2026, on proof of enrolment and attendance, and the remaining $2,000 paid later in the course depending on the type of course being studied. The scholarship can be used to cover course fees, tuition costs, textbooks, other study materials, uniforms, childcare, travel costs and other related expenses. Selection is based on demonstrated likelihood to successfully complete the qualification and whether the applicant is already employed or has a strong chance of being employed in the industry to which they applied. Applicants must provide evidence of achievement through previous study, industry experience or a combination of both, which may include certificates, study results, school reports and/or letters from employers.
eligibility_criteria: Australian citizen or permanent resident; intending to enrol in any certificate III or higher-level qualification in Semester 1 of the following year, or a first-year apprentice enrolled with TAFE Queensland at the time of submitting the application; demonstrated outstanding academic merit or strong work performance; clear demonstrable desire to secure ongoing employment within the chosen industry; provide evidence of achievement; submit proof of Australian citizenship or permanent residency via passport, birth certificate, visa or citizenship certificate
special_conditions: Scholarship money is paid via electronic funds transfer directly into the bank account. For scholarship recipients studying a Bachelor degree with TAFE Queensland, payment 2 of $2,000 is provided on completion of the first half of the degree. For scholarship recipients who are apprentices, payment 2 is provided on completion of two years of the apprenticeship, with a J-Competency Achieved result for at least half of the units needed for the apprenticeship/completion certificate. Non-refundable application fee included. Scholarships available for any certificate III or higher-level course across all study areas. Degree programs offered in conjunction with partner universities are not eligible.

school_name: Alexandra Hills TAFE Senior Studies Program
overview: TAFE Queensland offers Athlete Merit Scholarships to support students who are pursuing athletic careers. These scholarships enable athletes to gain vocational qualifications without forfeiting their sporting opportunities.
description: Athlete Merit scholarships are available under the Merit category to support athletes across a variety of disciplines to gain a qualification with TAFE Queensland without forfeiting their sporting opportunities. The scholarship provides $5,000 in financial assistance, with $3,000 paid after commencement of study in Semester 1, 2026, on proof of enrolment and attendance, and the remaining $2,000 paid later in the course depending on the type of course being studied. The scholarship can be used to cover course fees, tuition costs, textbooks, other study materials, uniforms, childcare, travel costs and other related expenses. Applicants in this category must identify which sporting associations they are affiliated with, and must be competing in a sport that is either listed as a funded sport in the Sports Australia - Australian Sports Directory, or an Olympic, Paralympic or Commonwealth Games sport. Applicants must also include details of sporting achievements to date along with evidence of achievement through previous study or industry experience.
eligibility_criteria: Australian citizen or permanent resident; intending to enrol in any certificate III or higher-level qualification in Semester 1 of the following year, or a first-year apprentice enrolled with TAFE Queensland at the time of submitting the application; competing in a sport listed as a funded sport in the Sports Australia - Australian Sports Directory, or an Olympic, Paralympic or Commonwealth Games sport; identify sporting associations you are affiliated with; provide details of sporting achievements; submit evidence of achievement through previous study, industry experience or combination of both; provide proof of Australian citizenship or permanent residency
special_conditions: Scholarship money is paid via electronic funds transfer directly into the bank account. For scholarship recipients studying a Bachelor degree with TAFE Queensland, payment 2 of $2,000 is provided on completion of the first half of the degree. For scholarship recipients who are apprentices, payment 2 is provided on completion of two years of the apprenticeship, with a J-Competency Achieved result for at least half of the units needed for the apprenticeship/completion certificate. Applicants must be competing in a sport eligible under Sports Australia - Australian Sports Directory or be an Olympic, Paralympic or Commonwealth Games sport. Non-refundable application fee included.

school_name: Alexandra Hills TAFE Senior Studies Program
program_name: TAFE Queensland Access and Equity Scholarship
overview: TAFE Queensland offers Access and Equity Scholarships to provide equitable access to study for students facing social disadvantage or financial hardship. These scholarships support Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, individuals with disability, sole parents, and those experiencing financial or social barriers.
description: Access and Equity scholarships provide equitable access to study at TAFE Queensland to students who can demonstrate a genuine interest in and commitment to their chosen career and identify themselves with one or more of the following categories: Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent; significant caring responsibilities; a disability or long-term medical condition or illness; long-term unemployed (longer than 6 months); sole parent status; suffering significant financial difficulties or social barriers to study; or another specified disadvantage. The scholarship provides $5,000 in financial assistance, with $3,000 paid after commencement of study in Semester 1, 2026, on proof of enrolment and attendance, and the remaining $2,000 paid later in the course depending on the type of course being studied. The scholarship can be used to cover course fees, tuition costs, textbooks, other study materials, uniforms, childcare, travel costs and other related expenses. Selection is based on demonstrated genuine interest in and commitment to the chosen career and identification with one or more access and equity categories.
eligibility_criteria: Australian citizen or permanent resident; intending to enrol in any certificate III or higher-level qualification in Semester 1 of the following year, or a first-year apprentice enrolled with TAFE Queensland at the time of submitting the application; demonstrate genuine interest in and commitment to chosen career; identify with one or more of the following: Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent; significant caring responsibilities; disability or long-term medical condition or illness; long-term unemployed (longer than 6 months); sole parent; significant financial difficulties or social barriers to study; submit proof of Australian citizenship or permanent residency; provide evidence of access and equity category membership
special_conditions: Scholarship money is paid via electronic funds transfer directly into the bank account. For scholarship recipients studying a Bachelor degree with TAFE Queensland, payment 2 of $2,000 is provided on completion of the first half of the degree. For scholarship recipients who are apprentices, payment 2 is provided on completion of two years of the apprenticeship, with a J-Competency Achieved result for at least half of the units needed for the apprenticeship/completion certificate. Evidence of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent should be provided by Confirmation of Aboriginality documentation; if unavailable, a Statutory Declaration may be accepted. Applicants must provide documentation demonstrating their access and equity category. Non-refundable application fee included.

school_name: Alexandra Hills TAFE Senior Studies Program
program_name: TAFE Queensland Career Pathways Merit Scholarship
overview: TAFE Queensland offers Career Pathways Merit Scholarships to support Queensland public sector staff members, particularly Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, who are pursuing career advancement through vocational training.
description: Career Pathways Merit Scholarships are available under the Merit category to support Queensland public sector staff members who are registered with the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander careers pathways service. The scholarship provides $5,000 in financial assistance, with $3,000 paid after commencement of study in Semester 1, 2026, on proof of enrolment and attendance, and the remaining $2,000 paid later in the course depending on the type of course being studied. The scholarship can be used to cover course fees, tuition costs, textbooks, other study materials, uniforms, childcare, travel costs and other related expenses. Selection is based on demonstrated strong achievement levels and clear career advancement goals within the public sector. Applicants must be employed in the Queensland public sector in an AO5 to AO8 level role and be registered with the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander careers pathways service.
eligibility_criteria: Australian citizen or permanent resident; intending to enrol in any certificate III or higher-level qualification in Semester 1 of the following year, or a first-year apprentice enrolled with TAFE Queensland at the time of submitting the application; employed with Queensland public sector (excluding TAFE Queensland) in an AO5 to AO8 level role; registered with the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander careers pathways service; demonstrate strong achievement levels and clear career advancement goals; submit proof of Australian citizenship or permanent residency; provide evidence of Queensland public sector employment and careers pathways service registration
special_conditions: Scholarship money is paid via electronic funds transfer directly into the bank account. For scholarship recipients studying a Bachelor degree with TAFE Queensland, payment 2 of $2,000 is provided on completion of the first half of the degree. For scholarship recipients who are apprentices, payment 2 is provided on completion of two years of the apprenticeship, with a J-Competency Achieved result for at least half of the units needed for the apprenticeship/completion certificate. Applicants must be employed with Queensland public sector in an AO5 to AO8 level role and registered with the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander careers pathways service. Non-refundable application fee included.

overview: Aldridge State High School operates a system-endorsed Sporting Excellence program in Volleyball, the only such program in the Wide Bay region. The program recognises students demonstrating exceptional sporting ability in volleyball and develops them through specialist training from junior school through to senior competition.
description: The Volleyball Excellence Program is an Education Queensland endorsed excellence program established in 2006 and is the only system-endorsed sporting excellence program in the Wide Bay region. The junior school program is offered as a subject timetabled at 4 x 50-minute lessons per week, with specialist volleyball training, biomechanics, training principles, refereeing and scoring concepts. Students analyse their own performance using video footage and computer analysis software. The senior program minimises disruption to academic needs by running training sessions during negotiated lunch times and before/after school. Coaches run multiple training sessions outside of class time. The program has consistently medalled at the highest national level (Volleyball Australia – National Cup) for each of the last 10 years with at least one team. Several students have been offered college volleyball scholarships in the USA upon completion of their Aldridge studies. Financial benefits and specific values are managed through the school's general scholarship application process.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated exceptional volleyball ability; interest in joining the program; multiple year commitment expected; available to both boys and girls; selection based on skill level and potential
special_conditions: Program involves year-round training and competition commitments. Students in the excellence program must be available for regular training sessions and state/national competitions. The school provides pathways for students to excel through Wide Bay, State, and National representation, with opportunities for international collegiate scholarships.

program_name: Golf Excellence Program - Tournament Squad Scholarship
overview: Aldridge State High School offers a recently established Excellence in Golf Program with a structured Tournament Squad that provides scholarships to competing golfers. The program develops golfers from training level through to tournament competition.
description: The Golf Excellence Program uses Golf Australia Modules and SMART goals to develop player ability. Students start as training players and progress toward their individual golfing goals. The school provides supervision, practice balls, and transport to/from Maryborough Golf Course. Upon obtaining an 18-hole Australian Golf Union (AGU) handicap, students are invited to join the Tournament Squad, which receives a scholarship valued at $500 per year. This scholarship includes four annual Queensland Intercollegiate Golf Association (QIGA) tournament entries, yearly Thursday morning green fees, free coaching clinics, and a tournament shirt plus Titleist hat. The QIGA is a university-style competitive format established in 1996 with over 100 players who have become golf professionals and state/territory representatives. Tournament Squad trains every Thursday 7:45am-11:00am at Maryborough Golf Course.
eligibility_criteria: Must obtain an Australian Golf Union (AGU) handicap of less than 33.4; must be 11 years or older; demonstrated commitment to golf program; ability to attend Thursday morning training sessions; invitation-based selection upon achieving AGU handicap
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarship is awarded upon obtaining an AGU handicap of less than 33.4. Recipients must attend Thursday morning training sessions at Maryborough Golf Course. The scholarship covers tournament entries, green fees, coaching, and uniform items. Students progress from training players to tournament squad based on achievement of AGU handicap and demonstrated ability. The program is part of QIGA which competes in both individual and team tournament play once per term against metropolitan-based teams.

overview: Aldridge State High School operates a Gifted and Talented program that begins in Year 5 with pathways extending to the tertiary sector. The program tailors individual curriculum plans for students whose academic ability sits more than one year above their peer group.
description: The Gifted and Talented Program at Aldridge State High School begins in Year 5 and has established pathways extending into the tertiary sector. The program provides individualised curriculum planning for students whose academic ability is more than one year above their peer group. This tailored approach ensures that gifted students are appropriately challenged and supported to reach their full potential. The program recognises exceptional academic ability and provides differentiated learning experiences. Students in the Gifted and Talented Program have access to enrichment opportunities and extended curriculum challenges. The program operates across junior and senior school years, providing continuity of support throughout the student's high school journey.
eligibility_criteria: Academic ability demonstrably more than one year above peer group; identified through assessment processes; commitment to extended learning opportunities; referral or application through school processes
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Individual curriculum plans are developed based on assessment of academic ability. Identified through school-based assessment processes. Pathways extend from Year 5 through to tertiary studies. Students must be committed to participating in extended and enriched learning opportunities.

overview: Aldridge State High School offers an Extension Music program in Years 7 and 8 that supports students with a passion and flair for music performance. The program is closely linked with classroom and instrumental music offerings.
description: The Extension Music Program operates in Years 7 and 8 and is designed to support students who demonstrate a passion and flair for music performance. The classroom and instrumental music programs are closely linked, providing comprehensive music education and development. Students in the Extension Music Program receive specialist instruction and have opportunities to participate in school musical performances and competitions. The program recognises and nurtures students with demonstrated musical ability and commitment to music study. Extension music students have access to instrumental instruction, ensemble participation, and performance opportunities throughout their junior school years.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated passion and flair for music performance; music ability at or above age level; commitment to regular practice and participation; acceptance into extension program through audition or teacher recommendation
special_conditions: Program operates in Years 7 and 8 only. Students must demonstrate commitment to music study and regular practice. Close linkage with classroom and instrumental music programs ensures comprehensive music education. Extension music students participate in school performances and may compete in music competitions.

overview: Alexandra Hills State High School offers the STEM Academy Program to high-achieving students in Years 7 to 9. This selective enrichment program recognises and develops students who demonstrate exceptional ability in Mathematics, Science, and Digital Technology through access to cutting-edge resources at the Centre of Excellence in Automation and Robotics.
description: The STEM Academy is a dynamic and innovative enrichment program for high-achieving students in Years 7 to 9. This academically rigorous program is enhanced by cutting-edge resources and unique learning opportunities to extend students beyond what is routinely offered in Science, Technology (Robotics, Coding, Electronics), Engineering (CAD, 3D printing) and Mathematics classes in secondary schools. Students benefit from increased access to Education Queensland's only 'Centre of Excellence in Automation and Robotics', including a multi-million dollar Robotics and Design complex. Students participate in enrichment programs including STEM-focused workshops, competitions and industry excursions. STEM Academy students have shared classes year-long for Science, Mathematics and the 'Alex and Me' wellbeing program. Entry is by application and academic merit selection process, with students demonstrating high achievement in Maths and Science (with an interest in Digital Technology also desirable) and satisfying high standards in effort and behaviour. Places in this academic excellence program are limited.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7-9; Must demonstrate high achievement in Mathematics and Science; Interest in Digital Technology desirable; Must satisfy high standards in effort and behaviour; Application and academic merit selection process required; Limited places available
special_conditions: Students can join STEM Academy along with Dance Academy or a Sporting Academy, but only one of the latter two due to conflicting times for lessons and training. Non-refundable program fees apply for resources and activities.

overview: Alexandra Hills State High School offers the Sports Excellence Program to students in Years 7 to 10 who demonstrate elite sporting ability. This selective program recognises students with exceptional athletic talent and provides specialised training and development opportunities.
description: The Sports Excellence Program of Excellence is a specialist program for students in Years 7 to 10 who demonstrate elite sporting ability. This program aims to develop the performance and skills required across a broad range of sports, while linking these elements to the theoretical knowledge needed by athletes. Students benefit from specialist coaching and training facilities. Entry into the program is by application and merit selection process, with students needing to demonstrate elite sporting ability and satisfy high standards in effort and behaviour. The school has capacity to enrol 112 students across Years 7-10 in this sports excellence program. Selection is based on sporting ability and try-outs, and students must meet the school's specific criteria for placement in the program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 7-10; Must demonstrate elite sporting ability; Must satisfy high standards in effort and behaviour; Application required with try-outs or assessment; Limited places available; Enrolment restricted to catchment and out-of-catchment priority basis
special_conditions: Students can apply for this Sports Excellence program as out-of-catchment applicants with priority given to those accepted into Programs of Excellence. Limited places available. Enrolment must meet school's capacity and catchment area management requirements.

program_name: Rugby League Development Program of Excellence
overview: Alexandra Hills State High School offers the Rugby League Development Program to boy students in Years 7 to 12. This specialist program recognises young rugby league players and provides expert coaching and training to advance skills, fitness and knowledge of the game.
description: The Rugby League Academy program aims to improve the skills of boy rugby league players in Years 7 to 12. This specialist program enables students to advance skills, improve fitness and gain knowledge of the game of Rugby League. The school has highly accredited coaches and two full-size rugby league fields plus a gym facility. Students can join the Rugby League Academy along with the STEM Academy, but cannot also be part of either Basketball or Dance Academy due to conflicting times for lessons and training. Rugby League Academy has an annual program fee to pay for program consumables and specialist training clinics through external providers, and students must purchase a training uniform. Teams participate in the Brisbane Broncos Schoolboy Challenge, Struddy's Cup, plus local gala days. Entry into the program is by application and merit selection process.
eligibility_criteria: Open to boy students in Years 7-12; Must demonstrate rugby league ability or commitment to the sport; Application required; Limited places available; Can be combined with STEM Academy only; Annual program fees apply; Training uniform must be purchased
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Rugby League Academy has an annual program fee for consumables and specialist training clinics. Students must purchase a training uniform. It is possible to join STEM Academy along with Rugby League Academy, but students cannot be part of Basketball or Dance Academy due to conflicting times. Non-refundable fees apply.

overview: Alexandra Hills State High School offers the Dance Academy program to students with ballet interest and dance ability. This selective cultural excellence program recognises students with performing arts talent and provides intensive training and performance opportunities.
description: The Dance Academy is a program for students with ballet interest and different dance skills and experience levels. The academy offers different performance groups that each specialise in a particular dance genre. This cultural excellence program has a long history of great success in numerous competitions, where participants compete against other schools at different venues and interact with students from other schools. For many students, participating in the Dance Academy program is one of the highlights of their time at Alexandra Hills State High School. Entry into the Dance Academy program is by application and merit selection process, with limited placements available. A dance audition may be required for entry. Students participate in Dance curriculum lessons for both semesters in years 7-9, plus semester 1 for year 10, with performance opportunities throughout the year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all year levels; Must demonstrate dance ability or ballet interest; Application required; Audition may be required; Limited placements available; Cannot be combined with Basketball or Rugby League Academy due to conflicting times
special_conditions: Entry is by application and merit selection process. Placements in this cultural excellence program are limited. A dance audition may be required. Students cannot also be part of Basketball or Rugby League Academy due to conflicting times for lessons and training.

overview: Alexandra Hills State High School offers the Basketball Academy program to students in Years 7 to 12. This selective sports program recognises students with basketball ability and provides development through coaching, training and competition opportunities.
description: The Basketball Academy program aims to improve girl and boy basketball players in Years 7 to 12. Students who are successful in selection into the Basketball Academy will train as separate girl and boy teams in three program divisions – Introductory (Year 7), Junior (Years 8-9) and Senior (Years 10-12). Our teams participate in the annual Champion Basketball School of QLD competition, the Southside Premier League throughout the year, plus local gala days. Players can also choose to trial for the Bayside School Sport team. Students also participate in enrichment programs held throughout the year, including the school's own Basketball Festival and coaching clinics run by local Basketball QLD club - Red-City Roar and NBL team - Brisbane Bullets. Training sessions occur weekly on scheduled days before, during and after school. Years 7 and 8 students do basketball-focused HPE lessons all year-long instead of the usual one semester program and do not study compulsory Creative Arts like other students.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 7-12; Must demonstrate basketball ability; Separate teams for boys and girls; Application and try-outs required; Limited places available; Cannot be combined with Dance or Rugby League Academy due to conflicting times
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Students train as separate girl and boy teams in three program divisions – Introductory (Year 7), Junior (Years 8-9) and Senior (Years 10-12). Basketball Academy has curriculum lessons for both semesters in Years 7-9, plus semester 1 for Year 10. For Years 11, 12 and semester 2 Year 10, academy has training sessions in period 4 each Wednesday. Students cannot also be part of Dance or Rugby League Academy due to conflicting times.

overview: Alexandra Hills State High School offers the Honours Academy Program to the highest-performing academic students in Years 11 and 12. This selective academic program recognises students with exceptional achievement and provides mentorship from experienced teachers.
description: The Honours Academy Program consists of the highest-performing academic students in Years 11 and 12, with twenty students accepted from each year level. Each student is paired with a teacher who will be an individual mentor to the student for the two years of senior school. The mentor guides students to manage their learning independently through monitoring their performance and assisting them to gain greater insight into themselves as learners. Studies indicate that formal youth mentoring programs can promote positive outcomes, such as improved self-esteem, social skills and career development. This program is designed specifically for the highest achievers in Years 11 and 12 who demonstrate exceptional academic excellence.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 11-12; Must be among the highest-performing academic students in the cohort; Selection is merit-based; Limited to twenty students per year level
special_conditions: Limited to twenty students per year level (20 from Year 11 and 20 from Year 12). Each student is paired with a dedicated teacher mentor for two years of senior school. Students must demonstrate highest academic performance to be considered.

overview: All Saints offers Academic Scholarships to reward students who are committed to their studies and strive for excellence. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional ability to thrive academically while contributing to school life in a positive and enthusiastic way.
description: Academic Scholarships are awarded to students who are committed to their studies and strive for excellence. They are based on a student's ability to thrive academically, while contributing to school life in a positive and enthusiastic way. The scholarship provides a concession of 25% or 50% off academic tuition fees. Selection is determined based on the candidate's performance in the ACER Scholarship Test, their resume and an interview with the School Principal or a delegate. All scholarships are subject to annual review based on academic performance and adherence to school values.
eligibility_criteria: Students in Year 6 (in 2026) applying for Year 7 entry in 2027; Students in Year 9 (in 2026) applying for Year 10 entry in 2027; Students in Year 10 (in 2026) applying for Year 11 entry in 2027; Open to external applicants and current All Saints students
special_conditions: Scholarships are subject to annual review based on academic performance and adherence to school values. Scholarship awards are determined based on the candidate's performance in the ACER Scholarship Test, their resume and an interview with the School Principal or a delegate.

overview: All Saints offers Music Scholarships for skilled instrumentalists and vocalists who bring passion and positivity to the arts at their current school. Recipients receive a concession of 25% or 50% off academic tuition fees plus free music lessons on one instrument.
description: Music Scholarships are available for skilled instrumentalists or vocalists who bring passion and positivity to the arts at their current school. The scholarship provides a concession of 25% or 50% off academic tuition fees. Music Scholarship recipients also receive free music lessons on one instrument as nominated by the Director of Performing Arts. Applicants are required to submit a video audition as part of their online application, and shortlisted Music Scholarship applicants will participate in an in-person audition on Scholarship Day. Selection is based on the candidate's performance in the ACER Scholarship Test, their resume and an interview with the School Principal or a delegate. All scholarships are subject to annual review based on academic performance and adherence to school values.
eligibility_criteria: Students in Year 6 (in 2026) applying for Year 7 entry in 2027; Must be at least Grade 4 AMEB (or equivalent) for Year 7 entry; Students in Year 9 (in 2026) applying for Year 10 entry in 2027; Must be at least Grade 5 AMEB for Year 10 entry; Students in Year 10 (in 2026) applying for Year 11 entry in 2027; Must be at least Grade 5 AMEB for Year 11 entry; Open to external applicants and current All Saints students; Must be skilled instrumentalist or vocalist
special_conditions: Music applicants must submit video audition by application deadline. Shortlisted Music Scholarship applicants will participate in an in-person audition on Scholarship Day. Music Scholarship recipients receive free music lessons on one instrument as nominated by the Director of Performing Arts. All scholarships are subject to annual review based on academic performance and adherence to school values.

overview: All Saints offers General Excellence Scholarships to recognise students who display good academic performance, actively contribute to school life and excel in a particular area such as the arts, leadership, sport or community service. The scholarship provides a concession of 25% or 50% off academic tuition fees.
description: General Excellence Scholarships recognise students who display good academic performance, actively contribute to school life and excel in a particular area such as the arts, leadership, sport or community service. The scholarship provides a concession of 25% or 50% off academic tuition fees. Selection is determined based on the candidate's performance in the ACER Scholarship Test, their resume and an interview with the School Principal or a delegate. These scholarships acknowledge students who demonstrate excellence across multiple domains while maintaining strong academic standing. All scholarships are subject to annual review based on academic performance and adherence to school values.
eligibility_criteria: Students in Year 6 (in 2026) applying for Year 7 entry in 2027; Students in Year 9 (in 2026) applying for Year 10 entry in 2027; Students in Year 10 (in 2026) applying for Year 11 entry in 2027; Good academic performance required; Active contribution to school life; Excellence in at least one area: arts, leadership, sport or community service; Open to external applicants and current All Saints students
special_conditions: Scholarships are subject to annual review based on academic performance and adherence to school values. Scholarship awards are determined based on the candidate's performance in the ACER Scholarship Test, their resume and an interview with the School Principal or a delegate.

overview: All Hallows' School offers the Catherine McAuley Bursary Fund to provide financial assistance to young women who would not otherwise be able to attend the school due to financial hardship and social disadvantage. The fund continues the legacy of Catherine McAuley, the founder of the Sisters of Mercy, by enabling students to experience an All Hallows' education regardless of their family's financial circumstances.
description: The Catherine McAuley Bursary Fund is All Hallows' School's primary financial assistance program designed to support families experiencing financial hardship and social disadvantage. The bursary provides variable financial support to eligible students, with the amount determined based on individual family circumstances and assessed need through the application process. Recipients benefit from assistance that may include tuition fee reduction, partial fee remission, or other forms of financial support as determined by the school. The program is administered through the All Hallows' Foundation and applications are assessed on a needs-based basis. Applicants must complete both the standard Application for Enrolment Form and the dedicated Catherine McAuley Bursary Fund Application form. The bursary continues throughout a student's enrolment at the school, subject to ongoing assessment and review. The school maintains that this fund embodies Catherine McAuley's vision of providing education to all, regardless of financial circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students enrolling at All Hallows' School; Families experiencing financial hardship or social disadvantage; Assessment of financial need required; Applicants must complete Application for Enrolment Form and Catherine McAuley Bursary Fund Application form; Available for entry years (Year 5 and Year 7) and mid-year enrolments (Years 6, 8, 9, 10, 11)
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Bursary applications are submitted alongside standard enrolment applications. Financial need is assessed based on information provided in the dedicated bursary application form. The school maintains discretion in determining bursary amounts and may conduct follow-up inquiries regarding family financial circumstances. Annual review may be conducted to ensure ongoing eligibility and appropriateness of support. Sibling discounts are available separately for tuition fees only, with third siblings receiving 50% tuition discount and fourth siblings receiving 75% tuition discount when three or more siblings attend concurrently.

program_name: Ambrose Treacy College Foundation Bursary Program
overview: Ambrose Treacy College offers bursary support through the Ambrose Treacy College Foundation. The Foundation provides financial assistance to boys from Years 4-12 whose families have limited financial means. The program aims to ensure inclusive access to Catholic education in the Edmund Rice tradition.
description: The Ambrose Treacy College Foundation is grounded in the long-held traditions of Edmund Rice and Patrick Ambrose Treacy, providing access to education and community. The Foundation aims to uphold the premise of inclusive education for those of limited financial means. Boys from families with limited financial resources may apply through the College for bursary assistance. The Foundation provides need-based financial assistance designed to support families in meeting school fees and ensure that talented students from disadvantaged backgrounds can access a quality Catholic education. Families can apply via the College and contact the Foundation Administration Officer for further information about eligibility criteria, application processes, and the extent of financial support available.
eligibility_criteria: Open to boys in Years 4 to 12; Families must have limited financial means; Application through the College; Contact Foundation Administration Officer required
year_levels: Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications are means-tested based on financial need. At the time of enrolment acceptance, families pay a $1,075 enrolment fee, of which $500 is refunded and applied to the final term's fees in Year 12. Families are invited to donate this $500 to the College Foundation exclusively to the Bursary Fund, which is fully tax deductible.

overview: Arcadia College offers financial assistance and bursaries to families in special circumstances. The college recognises that some families may face financial hardship and provides support through its Finance Office to help ensure all eligible students can access education.
description: Arcadia College provides support for families in special circumstances through its financial assistance program. As a Special Assistance School, the college does not charge traditional school tuition fees and instead charges a materials levy of $260 per term (as of 2025) to cover learning resources, facilities, iPads/devices, stationery, excursions, transport and daily meals. Beyond this, the college offers additional bursaries and fee assistance for families experiencing financial hardship. Specific financial benefit amounts and detailed eligibility criteria are determined on a case-by-case basis. Applications for financial assistance are made directly through the college's Finance Office during normal office hours (7:30 AM - 3:30 PM, Monday to Friday).
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing special financial circumstances; Demonstrated financial hardship
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications are made through the college's Finance Office. The college does not charge tuition fees as a Special Assistance School; levy covers essential resources and services. Support is provided on a case-by-case basis depending on individual family circumstances.

overview: Churchie offers Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding achievement in students entering Year 7 or later. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability through scholarship testing, previous academic records and interview, providing them with partial remission of tuition fees through to the end of Year 12.
description: Academic Scholarships provide partial remission of tuition fees from Year 7 (or a later year of entry) through to the end of Year 12, subject to a semester review. Scholarships are awarded on the basis of outstanding results in scholarship testing, previous academic records, and an interview with the Headmaster or his delegate. Students may sit the scholarship test in Years 5 to 10, with scholarships commencing no earlier than Year 7. The percentage of fee remission offered will vary depending on test results, school reports and interviews with the Deputy Headmaster. Scholarship testing is conducted annually in February each year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or later year; Must sit scholarship test in Years 5 to 10; Outstanding results in scholarship testing required; Strong previous academic records; Must participate in interview with Headmaster or delegate; Scholarships subject to semester review and ongoing academic performance
special_conditions: Scholarships subject to semester review. School receives data from AAS preferred but regularly awards scholarships to boys who have sat ACER exam. Percentage offered varies depending on test results, school reports and interview performance.

overview: Churchie offers Music Excellence Scholarships for talented young musicians entering Year 7 and above. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional musical ability and provides remission of school fees through to Year 12, with the level of award determined by the student's ability and the school's needs.
description: Music scholarships are for students entering Year 7 and above and provide remission of school fees through to Year 12. The level of scholarship awarded depends on the ability of the student and the needs of the School. Competitive entry is based on audition, academic test results and interview. Music Scholarships are available for instrumentalists who have achieved at least AMEB Grade 5 on a wind instrument, AMEB Grade 6 on a string instrument or AMEB Grade 7 for changed voices. Applications from students playing trumpet, tuba, French horn, bassoon, violin, viola or double bass are actively encouraged. Audition requirements are similar to those for an AMEB exam. All Music Scholarship applicants are required to sit the Academic Scholarship exam in February 2026.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 and above; Must achieve minimum AMEB Grade 5 on wind instrument, Grade 6 on string instrument, or Grade 7 for changed voices; Must sit Academic Scholarship exam; Competitive audition required; Must maintain academic test results and pass interview; Applications from specific instruments (trumpet, tuba, French horn, bassoon, violin, viola, double bass) actively encouraged
special_conditions: Music Scholarship applicants must also sit Academic Scholarship exam in February 2026. Auditions held 31 March to 2 April 2026. Students must provide their own accompanist if required. Scholarships awarded at discretion of Anglican Church Grammar School. Level of scholarship depends on ability of student and needs of school.

overview: Churchie offers Sporting Excellence Scholarships for exceptional young athletes entering Year 7 or above. The scholarship recognises students who have demonstrated outstanding commitment to their chosen sport at state level or higher, providing up to full remission of fees from entry through to graduation.
description: Churchie offers a limited number of scholarships for new students who have demonstrated an exceptional level of commitment to their chosen sport and are currently excelling at a state level or higher. Sporting Excellence Scholarships provide for up to 100 percent remission of fees from commencement (Year 7 or above) to graduation, subject to regular review by the Headmaster and Head of Cocurricular. Sports scholarship recipients are selected on a basis of their sporting credentials, potential for ongoing success and the applicant's willingness to commit themselves across Churchie's four tenets of academic excellence, spiritual awareness, personal growth and service. Expressions of interest are invited via the online form on the school website.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students entering Year 7 or above; Must demonstrate exceptional level of commitment to chosen sport; Must be currently excelling at state level or higher; Must demonstrate potential for ongoing sporting success; Must commit to Churchie's four tenets of academic excellence, spiritual awareness, personal growth and service; Subject to regular review by Headmaster and Head of Cocurricular
special_conditions: Scholarships provide up to 100 percent remission of fees from commencement to graduation, subject to regular review. Recipients must demonstrate willingness to commit to school's four founding tenets: academic excellence, spiritual awareness, personal growth and service.

overview: Churchie offers Choral Scholarships for boys who love to sing and wish to be part of the St John's Cathedral Choristers. The scholarship recognises the choristers' significant contribution to the worship life of St John's Cathedral and provides partial remission of tuition fees from Year 5 through to the end of Year 8.
description: Churchie offers an opportunity for boys who love to sing to be a part of the St John's Cathedral Choristers. In response to the choristers' significant contribution to the worship life of St John's Cathedral, they are offered a scholarship at Churchie from Year 5 until the end of Year 8 (subject to review). These scholarships provide a rich and disciplined environment in which to gain outstanding choral skills. Scholarships are available for part remission of tuition fees only. The scholarship recognises the unique contribution of Cathedral Choristers and provides support for students who commit to excellence in vocal performance and choral participation.
eligibility_criteria: Must be member of St John's Cathedral Choristers; Open to students entering Year 5; Continues until end of Year 8, subject to review; Must demonstrate commitment to excellence in choral skills; Must continue participation in Cathedral Choristers programme
special_conditions: Scholarships offered from Year 5 until end of Year 8, subject to review. Must be member of St John's Cathedral Choristers. Provides part remission of tuition fees only. Scholarships provide disciplined environment for gaining outstanding choral skills.

overview: Churchie offers Boarding Bursaries to support families with the significant cost of boarding education. The bursary provides remission of boarding fees and is particularly targeted at students from rural and remote parts of Australia who would not otherwise be able to access the boarding school experience.
description: Churchie offers financial assistance through boarding bursaries to students from rural and regional Australia. These bursaries support families who may not otherwise be able to afford the opportunity, while strengthening and enriching the boarding community. The boarding bursaries provide a remission of boarding fees. An expression of interest form is completed which includes a request for the student to provide a written response to eligibility criteria. The provision of financial assistance to Churchie families through bursaries extends to current and potential students, including those from rural and remote parts of Australia. This support assists those who, as a result of unforeseen events or circumstances, may otherwise have to leave the school for financial reasons.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students from rural and remote areas of Australia; Open to current and potential students; Must demonstrate financial need; Boarders entering Year 7 in 2026 and above; Must complete expression of interest form; Must provide written response to eligibility criteria addressing financial circumstances; Families experiencing financial hardship eligible
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Expressions of interest for 2026 boarding bursaries close 31 March 2025. Expressions of interest for Year 7 2027 will open in late 2025. Bursaries provide remission of boarding fees. Selection based on expression of interest form and written response to eligibility criteria. Particularly targeted at students from rural and remote Australia.

school_name: Arcadia College - Varsity Lakes Temporary Facility
Australian International Islamic College - Buranda Campus
Australian International Islamic College - Carrara Campus
Australian International Islamic College - Logan Reserve Campus
Autism Queensland Education & Therapy Centre - Brighton Campus
Autism Queensland Education & Therapy Centre - East Mackay Campus
Autism Queensland Education & Therapy Centre - Edmonton Campus

program_name: Smith Family Learning for Life Scholarship
overview: Beaconsfield State School has partnered with The Smith Family to offer scholarships to selected students throughout their schooling. The program supports disadvantaged children who need extra support to stay at school and progress to further studies or employment.
description: The Smith Family's Learning for Life program recognises that disadvantaged children need extra support to stay at school and go on to further studies or a job. Learning for Life helps create better futures for children in need by providing financial assistance from a sponsor to help disadvantaged families afford the cost of their child's education, support from a Learning for Life Program Coordinator to connect the child and their family to local learning opportunities and other supports. Students receive ongoing support throughout their schooling at Beaconsfield State School. The program is designed to help eligible families manage educational costs and access additional resources.
eligibility_criteria: Open to disadvantaged students; Financial hardship demonstrated; Student must remain enrolled at school; Family must engage with program coordinator and support services
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients and their families receive ongoing support from a Learning for Life Program Coordinator throughout their involvement with the program. Students must demonstrate commitment to their education and engagement with school. Sponsorship continues for the duration of the student's enrolment at the school.

school_name: Eagleby Learning Centre - Centre for Continuing Secondary Education

overview: Beaudesert State High School in conjunction with the P&C recognises outstanding student achievers in leadership at state primary schools with scholarship opportunities.
description: Beaudesert State High School offers a Leadership Scholarship recognising exceptional leadership qualities and commitment to community involvement. The scholarship is available to primary school students transitioning to Beaudesert State High School. The specific financial value, duration, selection process, and renewal conditions are not detailed on the publicly accessible scholarship page. Interested candidates should contact the school directly for complete program details including application procedures and assessment criteria.
eligibility_criteria: Open to state primary school students; Must demonstrate outstanding leadership qualities; Must be transitioning to Beaudesert State High School

overview: Beaudesert State High School in conjunction with the P&C recognises outstanding student achievers in sporting areas at state primary schools with scholarship opportunities.
description: Beaudesert State High School offers a Sporting Scholarship to recognise and support exceptional athletic ability and sporting achievement. The school's Sporting Program of Excellence is designed to provide students with the opportunity to develop their skills and to use the study of their elected sport to improve their academic opportunities and provide pathways to tertiary study. The specific financial value, duration, selection process through trials or assessment, and renewal conditions are not detailed on the publicly accessible scholarship page. Interested candidates should contact the school directly for complete program details.
eligibility_criteria: Open to state primary school students; Must demonstrate exceptional sporting ability; Must be transitioning to Beaudesert State High School

overview: Beaudesert State High School in conjunction with the P&C recognises outstanding student achievers in academic areas at state primary schools with scholarship opportunities.
description: Beaudesert State High School offers an Academic Scholarship to recognise and reward outstanding academic achievement and excellence. The school values high academic performance and commitment to learning across all subject areas. The specific financial value, duration, selection process through assessment or testing, and renewal conditions are not detailed on the publicly accessible scholarship page. Interested candidates should contact the school directly for complete program details including application procedures, assessment criteria, and financial benefits.
eligibility_criteria: Open to state primary school students; Must demonstrate outstanding academic achievement; Must be transitioning to Beaudesert State High School

overview: Beerwah State High School offers Academic, Sporting and Cultural Scholarships to support students demonstrating excellence in academic achievement, sporting prowess, or cultural contributions. The school recognises and rewards students who display exceptional commitment to learning and co-curricular involvement.
description: The Academic, Sporting and Cultural Scholarship program is designed to identify and support students who demonstrate outstanding achievement across academic, sporting or cultural areas. Applications are evaluated based on student performance, effort and behaviour as evidenced in recent school reports, along with demonstration of the required skills or talents in their respective areas. The scholarship recognises students who commit to supporting Beerwah State High School's values and community. The exact financial benefit and duration of the scholarship are not specified in publicly available documentation. Selection involves a combination of assessment including review of academic records, demonstrated talent or achievement in sporting or cultural pursuits, and character assessment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Students currently enrolled in Year 6; Must demonstrate exceptional achievement in academic studies, sporting ability, or cultural talent; Must maintain good behaviour and effort standards; Open to students from all school sectors
special_conditions: Applications for all programs and scholarships close Friday 19 September 2025. Successful applicants will be notified in Term 4. Applicants must complete an online application form and submit supporting documentation including recent report card emphasising effort, behaviour and grades.

overview: Beerwah State High School's Athlete Extension Program (AEP) is a specialist program available to students demonstrating exceptional athletic ability. The program provides targeted support and additional physical education opportunities for Year 7 students showing commitment to athletic excellence.
description: The Athlete Extension Program (AEP) is a selective entry program designed for students who demonstrate outstanding sporting ability and commitment to athletic development. Students selected for the AEP program study this as an elective subject, which is a year-long commitment. The program is available in Years 7, 8 and 9, with an extension program continuing through Years 8, 9 and 10. The exact financial value and specific selection criteria are not detailed in publicly available documentation. Selection involves identification of students showing exceptional athletic ability and performance. The program provides specialised coaching and training within the school curriculum structure.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or those already in Years 8, 9 and 10; Students must demonstrate exceptional athletic ability; Selection based on demonstrated sporting performance and potential; Must meet behavioural and attendance expectations; Open to all school sectors
special_conditions: AEP is studied as a year-long elective subject. Students selected for this extension program in Years 7-9 have this specialist program available. The extension program continues in Years 8, 9 and 10. Applications close Friday 19 September 2025, with successful applicants notified in Term 4.

overview: Beerwah State High School offers the Pinnacle Class program to support high-achieving students in Year 7. The program provides extension and enrichment opportunities for gifted learners who demonstrate exceptional academic potential and commitment to education.
description: The Pinnacle Class is an academic extension program designed for high-achieving students entering Year 7. This program provides enrichment and extension opportunities to allow gifted learners to reach their fullest academic potential. Students in Pinnacle classes receive differentiated curriculum and advanced learning experiences across subjects. The exact financial benefit is not specified in publicly available documentation, though this appears to be a non-monetary program benefit. Selection involves identification of high-achieving students based on prior academic performance and potential. Students have the opportunity to be in Pinnacle and/or AEP classes in Year 7, as they are invited to apply while in Year 6.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Currently enrolled in Year 6; Must demonstrate high academic achievement; Identified as gifted or demonstrating exceptional learning potential; Open to all school sectors
special_conditions: Students are invited to apply for Pinnacle and/or AEP classes in Year 6 for Year 7 entry. Applications for all programs close Friday 19 September 2025. Successful applicants will be notified in Term 4. The program provides extension of high achieving students to reach their fullest potential.

overview: Beerwah Dance Company (BDC) is a selective performance team providing opportunities for students with exceptional dance skills. The company competes and performs at eisteddfods and other events, requiring demonstrated technical and expressive dance abilities.
description: Beerwah Dance Company is a performance team that competes and performs at eisteddfods and other events. To join BDC, students must be studying Dance as a subject, or if the subject is not offered in their year level, they must demonstrate performance skills of an exceptional standard. Financial eligibility to cover competition and performance costs is a requirement. Selection involves submission of two videos demonstrating technical and expressive skills in dance, each demonstrating two distinct styles such as jazz and contemporary or hip hop and lyrical. Applicants must also submit a recent report card emphasising effort, behaviour and grades. The program requires ongoing financial commitment from families for competition, travel and performance expenses.
eligibility_criteria: Must be studying Dance as a subject; If Dance not offered in year level, must have exceptional performance skills; Must demonstrate financial capacity to cover costs of eisteddfod competition, travel and performance expenses; Open to students across year levels based on skill level; Co-ed
special_conditions: Financial eligibility to cover competition costs is a necessity. Applicants must submit two videos of dance performance demonstrating distinct styles, clearly labelled with student name and genre. Videos may be emailed or stored on USB for larger files. A copy of recent report card is required with emphasis on effort, behaviour and grades. Contact: Jessica Rossberg, jross247@eq.edu.au

overview: Beerwah State High School offers a specialist Music Extension Program to students demonstrating exceptional musical ability and commitment. The program provides specialised music instruction and performance opportunities in Years 8, 9 and 10.
description: The Music Extension Program is a specialist selective entry program designed for students with demonstrated musical excellence. The program is available in Years 8, 9 and 10. Students selected for the Music Extension Program receive specialised musical instruction and opportunities to develop their performance and musical knowledge. The exact financial value and specific selection criteria are not detailed in publicly available documentation. Selection involves assessment of musical ability and commitment. The program provides advanced musical training and ensemble opportunities as part of the school curriculum.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 8, 9 and 10; Must demonstrate exceptional musical ability; Selection based on demonstrated performance or aptitude; Open to all school sectors
special_conditions: Music Extension Program is a specialist program available in Years 8, 9 and 10. Students are selected based on demonstrated musical ability and commitment.

overview: Beerwah State High School offers a Dance Extension Program (DEP) with audition entry for Year 8 students. The program provides specialised dance instruction and performance opportunities for students demonstrating exceptional dance ability and commitment.
description: The Dance Extension Program is a specialist selective entry program for Year 8 students demonstrating exceptional dance ability. Entry is by audition, requiring students to demonstrate technical proficiency, expression and commitment to dance. The program provides advanced training in various dance styles and techniques as part of the school curriculum. The exact financial value is not specified in publicly available documentation. Selection is conducted through audition process where students perform to demonstrate their abilities. The program offers extended learning opportunities in choreography, performance and technique.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 8; Audition entry required; Must demonstrate exceptional dance ability; Must pass audition assessment; Open to all school sectors
special_conditions: Dance Extension Program (DEP) entry is by audition only for Year 8 students. Audition assessment determines selection based on demonstrated dance ability across various styles and techniques.

overview: Bellbird Park State Secondary College offers academic scholarships to reward outstanding student achievement in incoming Year 7 students. The scholarship recognises students who have succeeded academically and demonstrate exceptional commitment to their learning.
description: These excellence scholarships reward outstanding students who have succeeded academically, are good role models to their peers, and are active participants in the community, sporting, and/or cultural activities. The scholarships are open to newly enrolled Year 7 students in 2026. Specific financial benefits, selection processes and conditions are outlined in the official Scholarship Application Package 2026.pdf which candidates must complete and submit by 3.00pm Friday 21 November 2025. Applicants must reside within the enrolment catchment boundary or submit an Out of Catchment Expression of Interest Form.
eligibility_criteria: Open to newly enrolled Year 7 students in 2026; Must apply by 3.00pm Friday 21 November 2025; Must reside within enrolment catchment boundary or submit Out of Catchment Expression of Interest Form; Must be a good role model to peers; Must be active in community, sporting and/or cultural activities
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients must remain enrolled at the college until completion. Scholarships are limited in number and based on application merit.

overview: Bellbird Park State Secondary College offers sporting scholarships to reward outstanding achievement in sports in incoming Year 7 students. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional sporting excellence and commitment to their chosen sport.
description: These excellence scholarships reward outstanding students who are active participants in sporting activities and demonstrate excellence in their sport. The scholarships are open to newly enrolled Year 7 students in 2026. Candidates must complete the Scholarship Application Package 2026.pdf and submit by 3.00pm Friday 21 November 2025. Selection is based on demonstrated sporting excellence and commitment. Applicants must reside within the enrolment catchment boundary or submit an Out of Catchment Expression of Interest Form.
eligibility_criteria: Open to newly enrolled Year 7 students in 2026; Must apply by 3.00pm Friday 21 November 2025; Must reside within enrolment catchment boundary or submit Out of Catchment Expression of Interest Form; Must demonstrate sporting excellence; Must be an active participant in sporting activities
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients must remain enrolled at the college until completion. Sporting scholarships are limited in number and based on demonstrated sporting excellence.

overview: Bellbird Park State Secondary College offers cultural scholarships to reward outstanding achievement in cultural pursuits in incoming Year 7 students. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional cultural excellence and active participation in cultural activities.
description: These excellence scholarships reward outstanding students who are active participants in cultural activities and demonstrate excellence in their cultural pursuits. The scholarships are open to newly enrolled Year 7 students in 2026. Candidates must complete the Scholarship Application Package 2026.pdf and submit by 3.00pm Friday 21 November 2025. Selection is based on demonstrated cultural excellence and commitment to community and cultural participation. Applicants must reside within the enrolment catchment boundary or submit an Out of Catchment Expression of Interest Form.
eligibility_criteria: Open to newly enrolled Year 7 students in 2026; Must apply by 3.00pm Friday 21 November 2025; Must reside within enrolment catchment boundary or submit Out of Catchment Expression of Interest Form; Must demonstrate cultural excellence; Must be active participant in cultural activities
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients must remain enrolled at the college until completion. Cultural scholarships are limited in number and based on demonstrated cultural excellence.

overview: Bellbird Park State Secondary College awards one Phoenix Junior Scholarship to each of the school's feeder primary schools. The scholarship is presented to students transitioning to Year 7 who demonstrate outstanding overall achievement.
description: The Phoenix Junior Scholarship is a prestigious award presented by Bellbird Park State Secondary College to selected primary school students transitioning to Year 7. One scholarship is awarded to each of the college's local partner feeder primary schools. Unlike other scholarships, these are submitted by the primary school Principal and therefore students are unable to apply directly. The scholarships recognise students who have demonstrated outstanding academic achievement and overall excellence at primary school level. Specific financial benefits and conditions are determined by the college.
eligibility_criteria: Must be entering Year 7 at Bellbird Park State Secondary College; Must be nominated by primary school Principal from a feeder school; Must be currently enrolled in Year 6 at feeder primary school; Selected based on outstanding overall achievement
special_conditions: Scholarships are submitted by primary school Principal only. Students cannot apply directly. One scholarship awarded to each feeder primary school. Scholarship recipients must remain enrolled at the college until completion.

overview: Benowa State High School offers the Academic Excellence Scholarship to Year 7 students who demonstrate outstanding achievement. The scholarship recognises students who excel academically and commit fully to the school community.
description: Benowa State High School supports high-achieving Year 7 students through its Academic Excellence Scholarship, which is supported by the school's P&C (Parents and Citizens Association). Students who excel academically may apply for this scholarship by completing the application form and sitting the school's academic entrance exam. The scholarship is awarded to Year 7 students who have demonstrated a high degree of excellence in their chosen area, combined with a high degree of personal commitment. Scholarship recipients are expected and encouraged to be leaders within the school community through participating fully in all school activities. Applicants must demonstrate continued excellence in academic performance, behaviour, and effort according to their school reports.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 students; Must sit the Academic Entrance Exam; School reports must reflect continued excellence in academics, behaviour and effort; Demonstrated commitment to community participation
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients are expected and encouraged to be leaders within the school community through participating fully in all school activities. Students are assessed based on merit at the sole discretion of the school.

overview: Benowa State High School offers Arts Excellence Scholarships to Year 7 students who excel in music, dance, drama and visual art. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional artistic ability and commitment to their chosen discipline.
description: Benowa State High School supports Year 7 students with exceptional arts abilities through its Arts Excellence Scholarship programme, offered with support from the school's P&C. Scholarships in The Arts are awarded to students who excel in music, dance, drama and/or visual art. Applicants are required to complete the application form and either register for an audition (for music, dance and drama) or submit a portfolio of work (for visual art) to demonstrate their ability. The scholarship recognises students who have demonstrated a high degree of excellence in their chosen artistic area, combined with a high degree of personal commitment. Scholarship recipients are expected and encouraged to participate fully in all school activities and serve as leaders within the school community.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 students; Must excel in music, dance, drama and/or visual art; Must complete application form; Must attend audition or submit portfolio of work; School reports must reflect continued excellence in academics, behaviour and effort
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients are expected and encouraged to be leaders within the school community through participating fully in all school activities. Students are assessed based on merit at the sole discretion of the school. Applicants in visual arts must submit a portfolio instead of attending an audition.

overview: Benowa State High School offers Sports Excellence Scholarships to Year 7 students who demonstrate exceptional sporting ability. The scholarship recognises students who excel in sports and demonstrate high personal commitment to athletic development.
description: Benowa State High School supports talented young athletes through its Sports Excellence Scholarship programme, supported by the school's P&C. Sporting scholarships are awarded to students through an application process based on demonstrated sporting excellence. Applicants for this scholarship are required to complete the application form, provide appropriate certificates to validate information provided in their application, and participate in the Sports Testing day to demonstrate their sporting ability. The Sports Excellence program is designed for Year 7, 8 and 9 students who wish to pursue excellence in athletics and related sports. Students who receive the scholarship are expected to demonstrate a high degree of excellence in their sporting area, combined with a high degree of personal commitment. Scholarship recipients are expected and encouraged to be leaders within the school community through participating fully in all school activities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 students; Must demonstrate sporting achievement with supporting certificates; Must participate in Sports Testing day; School reports must reflect continued excellence in academics, behaviour and effort
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients are expected and encouraged to be leaders within the school community through participating fully in all school activities. Students are assessed based on merit at the sole discretion of the school. Applicants must provide certified copies of sporting achievement documentation.

overview: Birali Steiner School maintains a Financial Hardship budget to support families experiencing temporary extenuating circumstances. The school prioritises keeping families enrolled during periods of financial difficulty rather than requiring student withdrawal due to fee payment challenges.
description: Birali Steiner School has established a Financial Hardship budget and formal processes for applying for short-term Fee Concessions. The program is designed to assist families who experience extenuating circumstances—typically due to injury, illness, death, family separation, or job loss—where full fee payment becomes very difficult. As a not-for-profit, developing independent school, Birali is not able to offer discounted rates for pension/health care card holders or provide long-term financial hardship concessions. However, the school is committed to supporting families through temporary periods of financial instability. Families requiring assistance should contact the school directly to discuss their circumstances and explore available support options.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing temporary financial hardship; Experiencing extenuating circumstances such as injury, illness, death, family separation, or job loss; Short-term support only (not long-term concessions); Current enrolled students or applicants
special_conditions: Short-term support only. As a not-for-profit, developing independent school, Birali cannot offer discounted rates for pension/health care card holders or long-term financial hardship concessions. Application process requires discussion with school leadership to assess circumstances.

overview: Birali Steiner School has partnered with Edstart to provide flexible payment plans for families. The service fee associated with the Edstart plan is covered by the school, making it accessible for families needing payment flexibility.
description: To support families requiring payment flexibility, Birali Steiner School has partnered with Edstart to provide an alternative payment plan option. Families can set up an Edstart Plus account to pay school fees in instalments rather than as a lump sum. A key benefit of this partnership is that the service fee normally charged by Edstart is covered by the school itself, removing the additional cost burden to families. This initiative provides flexibility and support for families needing to manage their fee payments across multiple instalments throughout the school year.
eligibility_criteria: All families seeking flexible fee payment arrangements; No requirement to demonstrate financial hardship
special_conditions: Service fee covered by the school. Families must set up an Edstart Plus account to access the payment plan service.

overview: Blackheath & Thornburgh College offers the Academic Scholarship to recognise students who demonstrate exceptional achievement in academic pursuits. The scholarship supports talented students who show commitment to academic excellence and are recognised through awards and academic extension programs.
description: The Academic Scholarship may cover up to 50% of tuition fees applicable to enrolment. The scholarship is available for the duration of enrolment at the College. Selection is based on a written scholarship test, school references, and evidence of academic achievement including awards and participation in academic extension events and activities. Recipients must maintain continued academic performance at a level deemed satisfactory by the Principal, demonstrate sound character traits, and maintain participation in the co-curricular life of the College.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 6 or Year 7 in 2026; Must be Australian citizen or permanent resident of Australia; Must demonstrate commitment to achieving academic excellence evidenced through school references and awards; Must have participated in or been selected for academic extension programs or activities
special_conditions: Scholarship holder will be subject to all College rules and regulations. No Sibling Discount will apply if granted a Scholarship. Scholarships are not transferable to another school or student. Scholarships do not include incidental expenses such as uniforms, textbooks, stationery, or transport costs. Continuation of Scholarship is contingent upon: continued academic performance at level deemed satisfactory by Principal; continued participation in extra-curricular life to Principal's satisfaction; dress, bearing, manners and behaviour continuing to be of high standard; fees and monies owing to College being paid promptly. College may terminate any Scholarship where terms of award are not being met.

overview: Blackheath & Thornburgh College offers the Sporting Scholarship to recognise students with exceptional athletic ability and sportsmanship. The College has a rich sporting history and offers outstanding opportunities in Rugby League, Netball, Cricket, Tennis, Softball and Interschool competitions.
description: The Sporting Scholarship may cover up to 50% of tuition fees applicable to enrolment. The scholarship is available for the duration of enrolment at the College. Selection is based on a written scholarship test combined with evidence of strong sportsmanship and athletic ability at local or regional representative level or above, demonstrated through references and sporting results. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic performance and continue active participation in sports and the College community throughout their enrolment.
