eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 6 or Year 7 in 2026; Must be Australian citizen or permanent resident of Australia; Must demonstrate strong sportsmanship and athletic ability at local or regional representative level or above; Must provide references and sporting results demonstrating excellence
special_conditions: Scholarship holder will be subject to all College rules and regulations. No Sibling Discount will apply if granted a Scholarship. Scholarships are not transferable to another school or student. Scholarships do not include incidental expenses such as uniforms, textbooks, stationery, or transport costs. Continuation of Scholarship is contingent upon: continued academic performance at level deemed satisfactory by Principal; continued participation in extra-curricular life to Principal's satisfaction; dress, bearing, manners and behaviour continuing to be of high standard; fees and monies owing to College being paid promptly. College may terminate any Scholarship where terms of award are not being met.

overview: Blackheath & Thornburgh College offers the Creative and Performing Arts Scholarship to recognise students who will enhance the cultural and artistic life of the school. Recipients are typically students who demonstrate strengths in areas such as drama, music, visual arts, dance and language.
description: The Creative and Performing Arts Scholarship may cover up to 50% of tuition fees applicable to enrolment. The scholarship is available for the duration of enrolment at the College. Selection is based on a written scholarship test combined with supporting evidence of achievement and participation in creative and performing arts programs. Recipients must be academically capable and demonstrate strengths across multiple areas which may include drama, music, visual arts, dance and language. Continuation requires maintained academic performance and participation in arts and cultural activities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 6 or Year 7 in 2026; Must be Australian citizen or permanent resident of Australia; Must be academically capable and demonstrate strengths in creative and performing arts; Must have evidence of active participation and achievement in co-curricular programs such as drama, music, visual arts, dance or language
special_conditions: Scholarship holder will be subject to all College rules and regulations. No Sibling Discount will apply if granted a Scholarship. Scholarships are not transferable to another school or student. Scholarships do not include incidental expenses such as uniforms, textbooks, stationery, or transport costs. Continuation of Scholarship is contingent upon: continued academic performance at level deemed satisfactory by Principal; continued participation in extra-curricular life to Principal's satisfaction; dress, bearing, manners and behaviour continuing to be of high standard; fees and monies owing to College being paid promptly. College may terminate any Scholarship where terms of award are not being met.

overview: Blackheath & Thornburgh College offers Boarding Bursaries to support students who are passionate about education but face barriers of distance and access. Bursaries help qualified students from rural and remote areas overcome geographical challenges to boarding at the College.
description: The Boarding Bursary may cover up to 50% of boarding costs applicable to enrolment. Bursaries are available for the duration of enrolment at the College and can provide ongoing support for students in Years 5 through Year 12. Selection is based on demonstration of financial need, commitment to education, and circumstances that would otherwise prevent attendance due to distance or access issues. Bursary holders must maintain satisfactory academic and behavioural standards and participate in the College community.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 6 or Year 7 in 2026; Must be Australian citizen or permanent resident of Australia; Must demonstrate passion for education; Must face barriers of distance or access from rural or remote Queensland locations; Must show financial need to overcome boarding costs
special_conditions: Scholarship holder will be subject to all College rules and regulations. No Sibling Discount will apply if granted a Bursary. Bursaries are not transferable to another school or student. Bursaries do not include incidental expenses such as uniforms, textbooks, stationery, or transport costs. Continuation of Bursary is contingent upon: continued academic performance at level deemed satisfactory by Principal; continued participation in extra-curricular life to Principal's satisfaction; dress, bearing, manners and behaviour continuing to be of high standard; fees and monies owing to College being paid promptly. College may terminate any Bursary where terms of award are not being met.

overview: Blackheath & Thornburgh College offers the Local Champions Scholarship to recognise outstanding young people from rural and remote Queensland who have demonstrated exceptional leadership skills. The scholarship supports students who have made significant contributions through leadership roles, community service or volunteering.
description: The Local Champions Scholarship may cover up to 50% of tuition fees applicable to enrolment. The scholarship is available for the duration of enrolment at the College. Selection is based on a written scholarship test combined with evidence of demonstrated leadership skills and community involvement. Students must provide evidence of active participation and achievement in leadership roles such as school or house captain positions, community group leadership (e.g., Scouts, Girl Guides, sporting clubs), or recognition of significant volunteering efforts and coordination of charitable initiatives. Continuation requires maintained academic standards and ongoing community involvement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 in 2027; Must be Australian citizen or permanent resident of Australia; Must be from rural and remote Queensland; Must demonstrate sound character traits and potential to lead; Must provide evidence of active participation and achievement in leadership roles; Must have evidence of involvement in school or house captain positions, community group leadership (e.g., Scouts, Girl Guides, sporting clubs), or recognised volunteering efforts and coordination of charitable initiatives
special_conditions: Scholarship holder will be subject to all College rules and regulations. No Sibling Discount will apply if granted a Scholarship. Scholarships are not transferable to another school or student. Scholarships do not include incidental expenses such as uniforms, textbooks, stationery, or transport costs. Continuation of Scholarship is contingent upon: continued academic performance at level deemed satisfactory by Principal; continued participation in extra-curricular life to Principal's satisfaction; dress, bearing, manners and behaviour continuing to be of high standard; fees and monies owing to College being paid promptly. College may terminate any Scholarship where terms of award are not being met. Applicants can apply for multiple Scholarship categories within a single application provided supporting documentation meets all category requirements.

overview: Blackheath & Thornburgh College provides Financial Hardship Assistance to support students and families experiencing financial difficulties. Applications are considered individually on a case-by-case basis and are separate from the formal scholarship and bursary process.
description: Financial Hardship Assistance is available to students and families experiencing genuine financial hardship that would otherwise prevent attendance at the College. Applications are considered on a case-by-case basis and are subject to a formal application submitted to the Principal. This program is distinct from the scholarship and bursary process and offers flexibility in meeting individual family circumstances. Supported enrolments (with percentage of tuition or boarding fees covered) are made at the discretion of the Principal and are subject to vacancies being available following consideration of all offers of enrolment for full fee-paying students. The College works with families to identify appropriate levels of support.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current and prospective students; Demonstrated financial hardship preventing attendance; Application submitted formally to the Principal; Subject to individual assessment and discretion of the Principal; Subject to vacancy availability after full fee-paying enrolments processed
special_conditions: Financial Hardship Assistance applications are separate to the scholarship and bursary process. Offers for supported enrolment are made at the discretion of the Principal. Offers are subject to vacancies being available following consideration of all full fee-paying enrolments. Individual assessment is required and circumstances are considered on case-by-case basis.

overview: Border Rivers Christian College offers scholarships to aspiring and outstanding students entering Year 3, Year 7, or Year 10. Scholarships recognise academic excellence and commitment to the College's Christian values.
description: Border Rivers Christian College offers scholarship opportunities for new students entering Year 3, 7, or 10. The value of each scholarship is determined on a case-by-case basis and can take the form of a discount on fees or uniforms or both. Scholarship applications open on the 1st of November each year and close in January, 2 weeks before the start of Term 1. Applicants are required to contact the College Office for a Scholarship Information Sheet which outlines the specific selection process and assessment requirements.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 3, Year 7, or Year 10; New students to the College; Must demonstrate aspiring or outstanding ability
special_conditions: Scholarship value is determined on a case-by-case basis. Value can take the form of a discount on fees or uniforms or both. Applicants must contact the College Office to receive the Scholarship Information Sheet for full details of assessment and selection process.

program_name: Bowen State High School Year 6 to Year 7 Scholarships (Four Categories)
overview: Bowen State High School offers four scholarships to Year 6 students transitioning to Year 7. These scholarships are awarded to students who effectively demonstrate criteria across multiple categories of excellence.
description: In 2025, Bowen State High School introduced four scholarships which are awarded to Year 6 students who effectively demonstrate the criteria for each scholarship. The exact categories, financial values, selection processes, and specific eligibility requirements for these four scholarships were not detailed in publicly available sources accessed. The school's enrolments section indicates these scholarships are available, but complete program details including exact financial benefit, duration, selection methods, and renewal conditions were not accessible through public sources.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6; Eligible to enrol in Year 7 at Bowen State High School; Must effectively demonstrate criteria for selected scholarship category
special_conditions: Specific scholarship categories and detailed criteria are not publicly documented. Students must demonstrate excellence in their chosen category.

program_name: Queensland Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Foundation (QATSIF) Scholarship
overview: The QATSIF Scholarship supports senior Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students at Bowen State High School. The scholarship is funded from unclaimed stolen wages of Indigenous Australian elders and provides financial support to help recipients attain their Queensland Certificates of Education or Queensland Certificates of Individual Achievement.
description: The Queensland Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Foundation (QATSIF) Scholarship offers financial support valued at $2,000 per recipient to help senior First Nations students complete their senior secondary education. Bowen State High School has had 16 QATSIF recipients currently studying at the school, with 10 students successful in Round 14 of the scholarship program. The scholarship recognises the educational achievements of Indigenous students and supports them in their final years of schooling. Recipients are encouraged to take on leadership roles and represent the school as role models to junior students. The scholarship was developed from the unclaimed stolen wages of Indigenous Australian elders through QATSIF, formed in 2008.
eligibility_criteria: Must identify as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander; Must be a senior secondary student (Years 11-12); Must be enrolled at Bowen State High School; Selection occurs through QATSIF rounds (evidenced by Round 13 and Round 14 recipients)
special_conditions: Recipients are expected to take on leadership responsibilities and serve as role models to junior students. Scholarship recipients wear QATSIF School Leaders badges and receive certificates recognising their achievement.

school_name: Brisbane Christian College - Fairlie Terrace Campus
Brisbane Girls Grammar School - Primary Campus
Brisbane Youth Education and Training Centre
Wacol Youth Remand Education Support Hub
West Moreton Education and Training Centre

overview: Burnside SHS offers a unique Year 7 Gifted and Talented Scholarship to extend students in their areas of talent and skill across the Arts, Technology, Problem Solving, Numeracy, Literacy or general academia. Students are mentored so that every opportunity to excel in their chosen field is explored and harnessed.
description: The Year 7 Gifted and Talented Scholarship recognises and supports students who demonstrate exceptional ability across multiple domains including the Arts, Technology, Problem Solving, Numeracy, Literacy or general academia. The scholarship includes the Student Resource Scheme fee waived for one year. Selection is based on a submitted application including a covering letter and supporting documentation such as awards, letters of recommendation, and samples of work. Students receive individual mentoring to ensure every opportunity to excel in their chosen field is explored and harnessed. The scholarship is offered to one Year 7 student annually. Successful applicants will be notified of their selection following the October application deadline.
eligibility_criteria: Currently in Year 6; Open to all genders; Demonstrated talent or ability in one or more areas including Arts, Technology, Problem Solving, Numeracy, Literacy or general academia; Able to provide supporting documentation of achievement
special_conditions: Application includes covering letter and supporting documentation such as awards, letters of recommendation, and samples of work. Student Resource Scheme fee waived for one year only.

overview: Burnside State High School offers a Horizons Excellence Program Scholarship to one Year 7 student annually. The scholarship provides access to state-of-the-art technology and intensive technology-based curriculum delivery, equipping students with 21st-century digital learning skills.
description: The Horizons Excellence Program Scholarship is awarded to one Year 7 student annually who demonstrates interest in technology and aptitude for technology-based learning. The scholarship includes the Student Resource Scheme fee waived for one year and the Horizons Excellence Program laptop fee waived for one year. The program features immersive digital learning across core subjects of English, Humanities, Mathematics and Science, with students receiving 24/7 access to a state-of-the-art laptop for classroom and home use. Students must achieve satisfactory academic results at year level and demonstrate good behaviour and effort across all subjects. Selection is based on application suitability and home internet access is required. Students continue using the laptop through to the Senior phase of their schooling.
eligibility_criteria: Currently in Year 6; Demonstrated interest in using technology and aptitude for technology-based learning; Achieving satisfactory academic result at year level; Demonstrating good behaviour and effort across all subjects; Must have access to home internet; Not required to have high-level technology skills prior to entry
special_conditions: Places are limited and offers are made based on applicant suitability. Student must have access to home internet. Laptop is expected to be used by student whilst in the Horizons Excellence Program and also in the Senior phase of schooling. Student Resource Scheme fee and Horizons Excellence Program laptop fee waived for one year only.

overview: Burnside SHS offers a Music Excellence Scholarship for Year 7 students with demonstrated musical talent across various genres including Brass, Strings, and Rock and Roll. This scholarship supports students who are lovers of music in its many forms and wish to pursue a music industry pathway.
description: The Music Excellence Scholarship is awarded to one Year 7 student annually who demonstrates excellence in music across various genres including Brass, Strings, Rock and Roll and other musical forms. The scholarship includes the Student Resource Scheme fee waived for one year. The program is designed for students with a gift for music who may be interested in pursuing a music industry pathway. Selection is based on a submitted application including a covering letter and supporting documentation demonstrating musical achievement and commitment. Students receive mentoring to ensure they can fully explore their musical talents and pursue music industry pathways.
eligibility_criteria: Currently in Year 6; Demonstrated musical ability in Brass, Strings, Rock and Roll or other musical genres; Evidence of commitment to music study; Able to provide supporting documentation such as performance records, music teacher recommendations, or samples of musical achievement
special_conditions: Scholarship is for Year 7 entry. Suitable for students pursuing music industry pathways. Student Resource Scheme fee waived for one year only.

overview: Burnside SHS offers a Sports Scholarship for Year 7 students who want to fully immerse themselves in school-based sports. The scholarship is designed for students with sporting ability and passion for athletic participation and development.
description: The Sports Scholarship is awarded to one Year 7 student annually who wishes to fully immerse themselves in the school-based sports offered at Burnside SHS. The scholarship includes the Student Resource Scheme fee waived for one year and free sports uniform upon enrolment consisting of sports shorts and polo shirt. The school's experienced HPE staff across a range of disciplines will encourage the scholarship recipient to make the most of sporting opportunities at Burnside SHS. Selection is based on submitted application including covering letter and supporting documentation demonstrating sporting achievement and commitment to sport.
eligibility_criteria: Currently in Year 6; Demonstrated sporting ability and commitment to sport; Eager to fully engage in school-based sports programs; Able to provide supporting documentation such as sports achievement records, coach recommendations, or evidence of sporting participation
special_conditions: Includes Student Resource Scheme fee waived for one year and free sports uniform upon enrolment (sports shorts and polo shirt). HPE staff will provide mentoring and encouragement to maximise sporting opportunities.

overview: Burnside SHS offers a Volleyball Excellence Scholarship as part of Queensland's most successful Volleyball Excellence Program. The school has produced hundreds of representative players for the Sunshine Coast, Queensland and Australia.
description: The Volleyball Excellence Scholarship is awarded to students entering Year 7 who demonstrate exceptional volleyball ability and commitment to elite sport. Burnside SHS has produced hundreds of representative players for the Sunshine Coast, Queensland and Australia. The scholarship includes the Student Resource Scheme fee waived for one year. To qualify for the Volleyball Excellence scholarship, current Year 6 students must attend the annual Burnside Cup event, which serves as a form of selection trial. The Burnside Cup event is held in Term 3 each year. Selection is based on performance at the Burnside Cup trial event. Students selected for the volleyball excellence program participate in the Pursuit of Excellence Studies: Volleyball class.
eligibility_criteria: Currently in Year 6; Must attend the annual Burnside Cup event held in Term 3 as a selection trial; Demonstrated elite-level volleyball ability; Commitment to pursuing volleyball at representative level for Sunshine Coast, Queensland or Australia
special_conditions: Mandatory attendance at annual Burnside Cup event held in Term 3, which acts as the selection trial. Applications for participation in the Burnside Cup close Friday 15 August 2025. Student Resource Scheme fee waived for one year. Participants enter the Pursuit of Excellence Studies: Volleyball program.

overview: Burnside SHS offers a Japanese Enrichment Scholarship to the most outstanding Year 11 student in the school's Japanese Language Program. The successful student studies for one month at one of the school's sister schools in Japan.
description: The Japanese Enrichment Scholarship is awarded annually to the most outstanding Year 11 student in the Japanese Language Program at Burnside SHS. The scholarship provides a unique opportunity for the successful student to study for one month at one of the school's sister schools in Japan. This immersive experience is designed to extend the student's knowledge and skill in Japanese language and culture through direct engagement with native speakers and Japanese educational settings. Selection is based on demonstrated excellence in the Year 11 Japanese Language Program and commitment to Japanese language and culture studies. This represents a non-monetary but highly valuable cultural enrichment opportunity.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 11 Japanese Language Program; Demonstrated as the most outstanding student in the Year 11 Japanese Language Program; Commitment to Japanese language and culture study; Ability to undertake one month study abroad
special_conditions: One-month study period at sister school in Japan. Award is based on demonstrated excellence in Year 11 Japanese Language Program. Non-monetary award providing cultural enrichment and language immersion opportunity.

overview: Caboolture State High School offers a Rugby League Academy for students entering Year 7 who demonstrate sporting excellence in rugby league. The academy provides selective entry for athletes with exceptional ability in this sport.
description: The Rugby League Academy is a selective entry program for Year 7 entry that provides specialised coaching and training opportunities for students who demonstrate elite-level performance in rugby league. Students must apply through an online form and meet the school's selection criteria. The program includes integration with regular school curriculum while providing enhanced coaching and development in rugby league. No direct financial assistance is provided through the school, but students gain access to specialist facilities including the school's sports performance centre. The program is available for the duration of high school enrolment, subject to continued sporting performance and academic progress.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 6 students applying for Year 7 entry; Must demonstrate elite-level rugby league performance; Must complete the online application form; Selection based on sporting ability and interview process
special_conditions: Selection is competitive and based on demonstrated sporting excellence. Students must maintain academic and behavioural standards to remain in the academy program.

overview: Caboolture State High School offers a Volleyball Academy for students entering Year 7 who demonstrate exceptional volleyball ability. The academy provides selective entry and access to state-of-the-art volleyball facilities.
description: The Volleyball Academy is a selective entry program for Year 7 entry that caters to students with elite-level volleyball performance. Students must apply through an online form and meet the school's selection criteria for sporting excellence. The program provides specialised coaching and training integrated with the regular school curriculum. Students gain access to the school's Sports Performance Centre, a state-of-the-art facility specifically updated for the Volleyball Academy. No direct financial assistance is provided through the school. The program is available for the duration of high school enrolment, subject to continued sporting performance and academic progress.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 6 students applying for Year 7 entry; Must demonstrate elite-level volleyball performance; Must complete the online application form; Selection based on sporting ability and interview process
special_conditions: Selection is competitive and based on demonstrated sporting excellence. The school updated its gymnasium specifically for the Volleyball Academy. Students must maintain academic and behavioural standards to remain in the academy program.

overview: Caboolture State High School offers a Music Extension Program for students in Years 7-9 who demonstrate advanced musical ability and commitment to music study. The program is designed for students with previous music experience who wish to study music at an advanced level.
description: The Music Extension Program is a selective entry subject available to students in Years 7-9 who have genuine interest in music, previous experience with music learning, and wish to study music at a more advanced level. Acceptance into the program is via application only and selection takes into account music ability, performance standard, behaviour and academic achievement. Students who have learned a musical instrument throughout primary school, have taken private singing or instrument lessons, or are skilled in a particular area of music are encouraged to apply. The program provides advanced music study and preparation for the senior Music subject. It is recommended that students continue in this program through Years 8 and 9 to prepare for senior Music study. No direct fees or financial assistance are associated with the program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 7-9; Must have prior experience with learning a musical instrument or private singing/instrument lessons; Must demonstrate musical ability and performance standard; Selection based on music ability, performance standard, behaviour and academic achievement
special_conditions: Acceptance is via application only. Selection process considers music ability, performance standard, behaviour and academic achievement. Continuation is recommended through Years 8-9 to prepare for senior Music subject.

overview: Caboolture State High School offers a Year 7 Academic Enrichment Program for students entering Year 7 who demonstrate high academic achievement and potential. The enrichment program is designed to cater for students with particular interests in advanced academic study.
description: The Year 7 Academic Enrichment Program is a selective entry program for students entering Year 7 who demonstrate academic excellence and commitment to learning. Year 6 students may apply for the Academic Enrichment Program by downloading the application form and completing it in the student's own handwriting. The program is designed to cater for students with particular interests in academic enrichment and provides advanced curriculum opportunities. Selection is based on academic achievement, demonstrated ability, behaviour and school report references. The program provides extended learning opportunities integrated with the regular school curriculum. No direct financial assistance is provided through the school. The program is available for the duration of high school enrolment, subject to continued academic performance and appropriate conduct.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 6 students applying for Year 7 entry; Must demonstrate high academic achievement; Must complete the application form in student's own handwriting; Selection based on academic performance and school reports
special_conditions: Application must be completed by the student in their own handwriting. Information brochures are available from the school to provide further details about the program.

overview: Cairns State High School offers a Sport Program of Excellence for talented athletes seeking to develop their skills at a high-performance level. The program recognises students who demonstrate outstanding athletic ability and commitment to competitive sport.
description: The Sport Program of Excellence provides access to specialist coaching, training facilities, and competition opportunities in sports including basketball, soccer, hockey, swimming, and other athletic disciplines. Students receive training outside normal school hours, typically before school from 6:00am-7:00am or after school until 5:30pm depending on the sport. The program requires commitment to attend timetabled sessions, associated competitions, and performance events. Students and parents must be aware of their commitment to pay program fees and attend related workshops and activities. Admission requires a competitive selection process involving trials, auditions, and assessment of athletic performance.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 6 students entering Year 7; Selection via application and trials or auditions; Commitment to attend sessions outside normal school hours; Willingness to pay program fees
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Students must attend timetabled program sessions outside normal school hours. Students are expected to attend performances, competitions, and workshops related to the sport. Program is cost-involving and may require fundraising participation.

overview: Cairns State High School offers a Performing Arts Program of Excellence for talented students in music, dance, and drama. The program recognises students who demonstrate outstanding performance ability and artistic excellence.
description: The Performing Arts Program of Excellence includes opportunities in instrumental music, vocal performance, orchestral music, chorale, dance, drama, and technical theatre. Students develop skills in a supportive high-performance environment with access to specialists, master classes, and opportunities to attend performances. The program operates before or after school depending on the discipline, with sessions potentially running from 6:00am-7:00am or until 5:30pm. Students must attend timetabled program sessions and participate in related performances and workshops. Admission requires application and audition assessment. Students and parents must understand the commitment to pay fees and participate in associated events.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 6 students entering Year 7; Selection via application and audition process; Commitment to attend sessions outside normal school hours; Willingness to pay program fees and participate in performances
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Students must attend timetabled program sessions outside normal school hours. Students are expected to participate in performances, concerts, and workshops. Program is cost-involving and may require fundraising participation. Students who reside outside the Cairns region must arrange suitable accommodation.

overview: Cairns State High School offers a Visual Arts Program of Excellence for students demonstrating exceptional talent in art, design, and creative visual expression. The program recognises artistic excellence and creative ability.
description: The Visual Arts Program of Excellence enables students to develop advanced skills in various visual art disciplines including painting, sculpture, digital art, fashion design, and public art. Students work with specialists and engage in group exhibitions, creative projects, and artistic performances. The program operates outside normal school hours, with classes potentially running before school from 6:00am or after school until 5:30pm depending on the discipline. Assessment is based on attendance, involvement, making, responding, and participation in group exhibitions throughout the course. Students must commit to attending all timetabled sessions and related artistic events and workshops. Admission requires application and portfolio assessment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 6 students entering Year 7; Selection via application and portfolio review; Commitment to attend sessions outside normal school hours; Willingness to pay program fees
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Students must attend timetabled program sessions outside normal school hours. Assessment includes attendance and involvement in group exhibitions. Program is cost-involving and may require fundraising participation.

overview: Cairns State High School offers an Engineering Program of Excellence for students demonstrating exceptional aptitude in science, technology, and engineering disciplines. The program recognises students with outstanding technical and problem-solving abilities.
description: The Engineering Program of Excellence provides advanced training in aerospace engineering, technical design, and applied engineering disciplines. Students engage in project-based learning, industry partnerships, and real-world engineering activities. The program operates outside normal school hours with sessions potentially running from 6:00am or until 5:30pm. Entry into the Engineering Program requires students to sit an academic aptitude test to assess scientific and mathematical reasoning. Students must commit to attending all timetabled sessions and related technical workshops and industry activities. The program is cost-involving and may require participation in fundraising. Students from outside the Cairns region must arrange suitable accommodation.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 6 students entering Year 7; Academic aptitude test required; Strong achievement in mathematics and science; Commitment to attend sessions outside normal school hours; Willingness to pay program fees
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Students must sit an academic aptitude test for entry. Students must attend timetabled program sessions outside normal school hours. Program is cost-involving and may require fundraising participation. Students who reside outside the Cairns region are required to find suitable accommodation.

overview: Cairns State High School offers a Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics (STEM) Program of Excellence for high-achieving students in these disciplines. The program recognises students with outstanding scientific and technical abilities.
description: The STEM Program of Excellence provides advanced instruction across science, technology, engineering, and mathematics subjects integrated with innovative teaching approaches. Students engage in extended learning, real-world problem solving, industry partnerships, and access to specialist mentors and resources. The program operates outside normal school hours with sessions potentially running from 6:00am or until 5:30pm. Students participate in competitions, workshops, and projects at local, state, national, and international levels. Students must commit to attending all timetabled sessions and related STEM events and activities. The program is cost-involving and may require participation in fundraising. Students from outside the Cairns region must arrange suitable accommodation.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 6 students entering Year 7; Outstanding achievement in mathematics and science; Strong academic performance; Commitment to attend sessions outside normal school hours; Willingness to pay program fees
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Students must attend timetabled program sessions outside normal school hours. Program is cost-involving and may require fundraising participation. Students who reside outside the Cairns region are required to find suitable accommodation.

overview: Cairns State High School offers an International Student Specialisation Program for students from overseas seeking to study in Australia. The program supports international students in accessing quality education in a supportive multicultural environment.
description: The International Student Specialisation Program provides tailored support for overseas students studying at Cairns State High School. Students engage in cultural integration activities, language support, and community connections with homestay families. The school offers a safe, inclusive, and supportive environment where international students can thrive academically, socially, and personally. The International Student Program team assists with student journey planning, innovative classroom programs, and exciting extra-curricular opportunities. International students experience the school's strong traditions and reputation for excellence while accessing quality education integrated with the broader school community.
eligibility_criteria: Must be an overseas student; Visa Grant Notice or appropriate visa status required; Commitment to engage in school community; Need to arrange homestay or suitable accommodation
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: International students must arrange suitable homestay accommodation. Preference is given to long-term homestay families with whom the school has established relationships. Students must be able to pay international student fees.

overview: Cairns SDE assists eligible Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students with applications for QATSIF Scholarships. These scholarships provide financial support to Year 11 and Year 12 Indigenous students to help with the costs of their senior secondary studies.
description: QATSIF (Queensland Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Foundation) offers scholarships through all Queensland secondary schools, including Cairns School of Distance Education, to support Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Year 11 and Year 12 students. Scholarships are valued at up to $1,000 per student per year and are designed to help families with the costs of senior education. The scholarship is a two-year commitment for students commencing in Year 11. Schools apply on behalf of eligible students around mid-May each year. Selection is based on demonstrated Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent, Australian citizenship, Queensland residency, QCE completion intentions, academic performance (A-C in English and C or higher in other subjects in Year 10), behaviour of C or above, participation in cultural activities, and willingness to be a role model.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander descent and identified as such by their community; Australian citizen; Queensland resident; Enrolled at a Queensland school as a student; Intends to undertake and complete Queensland Certificate of Education by Year 12; Currently achieving A-C in English and C or higher in other subjects in Year 10 (or on track if in Year 11); Demonstrates effort and behaviour of C or above; Participates in school activities that enhance Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander cultural identity; Demonstrates willingness to participate in school life and be a worthy role model
special_conditions: Scholarship is a two-year commitment for students commencing in Year 11. Schools apply on behalf of eligible students. Applications are processed annually with schools notified of outcomes in late October to mid-November. QATSIF funds are administered by schools for the specific educational benefit of individual students. Current Year 11 QATSIF students do not need to reapply; scholarships carry across Year 11 and 12. Cairns SDE assists students with the application process.

program_name: Distance Education Fee Exemption and Waiver Program
overview: Cairns SDE offers eligible students exemptions or waivers of the distance education enrollment fee for home-based learners experiencing financial hardship or educational disadvantage. Students may apply for full or partial fee relief on a case-by-case basis.
description: Home-based students at Cairns School of Distance Education may be exempt from paying the distance education enrollment fee if they satisfy specific criteria relating to financial hardship or educational disadvantage (through lack of educational continuity). In certain circumstances, including exceptional cases, students may also apply for a waiver of the distance education enrollment fee under the Education (General Provisions) Act 2006. Fee exemption and waiver applications are assessed on a case-by-case basis by the Principal. Students must provide sufficient supporting evidence to enable the Principal to make an informed decision. Approved waivers and exemptions are applied to the school year and cover only the Distance Education fee. Other school fees issued by Cairns SDE remain payable.
eligibility_criteria: Home-based students enrolled in distance education programs at Cairns School of Distance Education; Demonstrated financial hardship; OR Educational disadvantage through lack of educational continuity; OR Exceptional circumstances; Must provide sufficient supporting evidence to the Principal
special_conditions: Applications assessed on case-by-case basis. Students experiencing financial difficulty are encouraged to contact the school to discuss available options in confidence. Approved waivers and exemptions apply only to the Distance Education fee and school year; other fees issued by the school remain payable. A minimum of sixteen weeks enrolment applies, otherwise an administrative fee may be charged. Fee exemption and waiver decisions are subject to natural justice in the decision-making process.

overview: Calliope State High School offers a Program of Excellence in Football to students demonstrating elite sporting ability. The program is a selective entry program that provides specialized coaching and training for talented football players.
description: The Football Program of Excellence at Calliope State High School is a selective entry program designed for students who demonstrate exceptional football ability. Selection is based on sporting achievement and potential. As a Queensland state school, there is no tuition fee cost, but the program is selective and students must meet specific sporting criteria determined by the school. The program operates as part of the school's curriculum offering. Selection takes place during the enrolment process, with criteria available directly from the school.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated elite football ability; Selection based on school criteria; Application required for out-of-catchment students
special_conditions: As a state school, this is not a fee-remission scholarship. Students enrol in the program based on demonstrated sporting excellence. Enrolment criteria for the Program of Excellence is available from the school.

overview: Calliope State High School offers a Program of Excellence in STEAM (Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts, Mathematics) to academically talented students. The program provides enriched learning opportunities in STEAM disciplines through specialized teaching and co-curricular activities.
description: The STEAM Program of Excellence at Calliope State High School is a selective entry program recognizing students with outstanding ability in science, technology, engineering, arts, and mathematics. The program delivers enhanced learning experiences including makerspace activities, robotics competitions, drone projects, and problem-based learning in creative contexts. As a Queensland state school, there is no tuition fee involved. Students selected for the program benefit from specialized facilities and curriculum enrichment. Selection is based on demonstrated academic achievement and interest in STEAM disciplines.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated academic excellence in STEAM subjects; Interest and aptitude in science, technology, engineering, arts or mathematics; Selection based on school criteria; Application required for out-of-catchment students
special_conditions: As a state school, this is not a fee-remission scholarship. Program provides curriculum enrichment and specialized facilities. Enrolment criteria for the Program of Excellence is available from the school.

overview: Calliope State High School offers a Program of Excellence in Music to students demonstrating musical talent and commitment to musical performance. The program provides specialized instruction and performance opportunities for musically gifted students.
description: The Music Program of Excellence at Calliope State High School is a selective entry program for students with demonstrated musical excellence. The program provides opportunities for instrumental and ensemble performance, specialized music tuition, and participation in cultural performances. As a Queensland state school, there is no tuition fee cost. Students benefit from expert teaching teams and access to performance opportunities including partnerships with regional cultural organizations. Selection is based on musical ability and commitment to pursuing music at an advanced level.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated musical ability; Musical performance achievement; Commitment to music study; Selection based on school criteria; Application required for out-of-catchment students
special_conditions: As a state school, this is not a fee-remission scholarship. Program provides specialized music tuition and performance opportunities. Enrolment criteria for the Program of Excellence is available from the school.

overview: Calliope State High School offers a Program of Excellence in AUSLAN (Australian Sign Language) to students interested in deaf culture and sign language proficiency. The program provides specialized instruction in AUSLAN language and deaf culture.
description: The AUSLAN Program of Excellence at Calliope State High School provides students with the opportunity to develop proficiency in Australian Sign Language and understand deaf culture and communication. This selective entry program offers specialized language instruction and cultural awareness training. As a Queensland state school, there is no tuition fee involved. The program serves both hearing students and deaf or hard-of-hearing students seeking to develop or enhance their AUSLAN skills. Selection is based on demonstrated interest and aptitude in language learning and cultural engagement.
eligibility_criteria: Interest in AUSLAN and deaf culture; Language learning aptitude; Selection based on school criteria; Application required for out-of-catchment students
special_conditions: As a state school, this is not a fee-remission scholarship. Program provides specialized language instruction. Enrolment criteria for the Program of Excellence is available from the school.

overview: Caloundra City Private School's Principal's Scholarship is a prestigious award designed to recognise and support students who excel in cultural pursuits, academic achievement, sports, or community service. The scholarship provides opportunities to students who demonstrate exceptional talent and dedication but may otherwise be unable to afford a CCPS education. Through financial assistance, the school seeks to give deserving students from all backgrounds the opportunity to experience the exceptional education that CCPS offers.
description: The Principal's Scholarship is an overarching merit-based and means-tested award that celebrates students' diverse talents across academic, cultural, community service, and sporting excellence. Students applying must demonstrate evidence of achievement in one of the designated scholarship award categories. The scholarship recognises that each student is unique, with individual strengths and passions. The value of the scholarship varies based on the means test and family financial circumstances, with parents required to submit a Financial Position Form in strict confidence. Selection involves shortlisting of candidates who are invited to an interview and required to sit an examination. The principal has full discretion over both the awarding and continuation of the scholarship. The scholarship is offered for the duration of enrolment, subject to satisfactory academic and behavioural progress.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students entering Years 5-11 in 2025; Must demonstrate evidence of academic, cultural, community service or sporting excellence in one of the designated categories; Shortlisted candidates invited to interview; Required to sit examination; Parents must undergo means test and submit Financial Position Form; All information handled in strict confidence
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Principal has full discretion over both the awarding and continuation of the scholarship. Parents undergo means test and submit Financial Position Form with all information handled in strict confidence. Scholarship subject to satisfactory academic progress and good conduct. Students must maintain eligibility standards throughout their enrolment.

overview: Caloundra City Private School's Academic Excellence Scholarship is awarded to students who demonstrate exceptional academic achievement. The scholarship recognises students with a Grade Point Average (GPA) of 13 or above and rewards outstanding academic performance. Recipients are placed in a learning environment that challenges, inspires, and nurtures excellence, helping them achieve their full potential.
description: The Academic Excellence Scholarship provides recognition and support for students demonstrating consistent high academic achievement. Eligibility requires a GPA of 13 or above indicated on at least the last two semester reports, along with evidence of academic achievements from two years of school reports and latest NAPLAN results. Applicants must submit a portfolio of supporting documents demonstrating their academic excellence. Shortlisted candidates are invited to an interview and required to sit an examination as part of the selection process. Successful scholarship recipients benefit from access to enriched resources that support their educational journey and nurture their continued academic growth. The value of the scholarship is determined based on individual circumstances and merit, and is offered for the duration of enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 5-11 in 2025; GPA of 13 indicated on at least the last two semester reports; Evidence of academic achievements required from two years of school reports; Latest NAPLAN results required; Portfolio of supporting documents required; Shortlisted candidates invited to interview and examination
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: GPA of 13 required on at least last two semester reports. Academic performance must be maintained throughout enrolment. Scholarship subject to continued academic excellence and satisfactory conduct.

overview: Caloundra City Private School's Elite Sports Scholarship is designed to support future Olympians, elite athletes, individual sporting champions and those who represent their sport at state, national or international level. The scholarship recognises and supports students' athletic ambitions while enabling them to receive a world-class education. Recipients benefit from personalised learning pathways and flexible timetabling to accommodate demanding training and competition schedules.
description: The Elite Sports Scholarship provides comprehensive support for elite young athletes entering Years 5-11. The scholarship value varies based on individual circumstances and merit. Recipients receive personalised learning and flexible timetabling, allowing custom study plans that accommodate training sessions, competitions, and recovery periods. Academic support is provided to ensure students stay on track with studies during intensive training periods or when away for competitions. The school leverages technology through blended learning platforms to enable remote access to course materials, assignments, and online discussions. Support from the Careers Development Coach, Head of Senior School and Head of Learning Pathways provides clear roadmaps for success. Shortlisted candidates are invited to an interview and required to sit an examination as part of the selection process. Selection requires evidence of representation in sport or sports at state, national or international level.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 5-11 in 2025; Must demonstrate evidence of representation in sport/sports at state, national or international level; Minimum state level representation required; Shortlisted candidates invited to interview and examination; Parents may undergo means test depending on scholarship structure
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Must maintain state, national or international level representation in sport. Training and competition schedules accommodated through flexible timetabling. Academic support provided alongside athletic development. Custom study plans developed for individual athletes. Blended learning available for remote participation when competing. Support from specialist staff including Careers Development Coach and Head of Learning Pathways.

overview: Caloundra City Private School's Cultural Scholarship awards talented students with demonstrated excellence in music, arts, drama, performing arts or dance the opportunity to shine. The scholarship recognises students' cultural achievements and provides opportunities to seamlessly integrate their cultural pursuits with their academic curriculum. Recipients are supported through flexible timetabling and study plans while receiving a high-quality education.
description: The Cultural Scholarship provides recognition and support for students demonstrating excellence in music, arts, drama, performing arts or dance. Eligibility requires evidence of participation in cultural activities outside of school, such as eisteddfods, competitions, and other recognised performance or art events. Applicants must demonstrate a portfolio of supporting documents showing their cultural achievements and participation in external competitions or performances. Shortlisted candidates are invited to an interview and required to sit an examination as part of the selection process. The school offers flexible timetabling and personalised study plans to allow students to balance their academic commitments with their cultural pursuits. The value of the scholarship is determined based on individual circumstances and merit. Recipients have the unique opportunity to integrate their cultural pursuits seamlessly with their academic curriculum, receiving both strong academic education and support for their artistic development. The scholarship is offered for the duration of enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 5-11 in 2025; Must provide evidence of participation in music, arts, performance art or other cultural activity outside of school; Examples include eisteddfods, competitions and recognised external performances; Portfolio of supporting documents demonstrating cultural achievements required; Two years of school reports and NAPLAN results required; Shortlisted candidates invited to interview and examination
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: External evidence of cultural participation required (eisteddfods, competitions, performances). Flexible timetabling provided to accommodate cultural pursuits. Students encouraged and supported in developing cultural talents while maintaining academic standards. Integration of cultural pursuits with academic curriculum is a key feature of the scholarship.

overview: Caloundra City Private School's Community Scholarship recognises outstanding young people who are going above and beyond to create a positive impact in their communities. The scholarship supports students' passion for service, personal growth, and positive impact. Recipients benefit from a personalised, flexible approach that allows them to balance their academic life with their community service commitments.
description: The Community Scholarship provides recognition and support for students demonstrating exceptional commitment to community service and volunteer work. Applicants must provide evidence of involvement in a minimum of 150 hours of community service conducted per year, supported by letters from organisations or charities verifying their service. A reference letter from at least one organisation or charity that the student volunteers with must outline the service work involved. Applicants must also submit two years of school reports, latest NAPLAN results, and a portfolio of supporting documents demonstrating their commitment to community service and personal growth. Shortlisted candidates are invited to an interview and required to sit an examination as part of the selection process. The school provides a personalised, flexible approach allowing students to balance their academic commitments with their service work. The value of the scholarship is determined based on individual circumstances and merit. Recipients are part of a school that values community involvement and the invaluable learning that happens through service.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 5-11 in 2025; Must provide evidence of minimum 150 hours of community service conducted per year; Letter from organisation/charity verifying involvement required; Reference letter from at least one organisation or charity required; Two years of school reports and NAPLAN results required; Portfolio of supporting documents demonstrating community service commitment required; Shortlisted candidates invited to interview and examination
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Minimum 150 hours of community service per year required and must be verified by organisation or charity. External reference letters from volunteering organisations required. Flexible approach to balance academic and service commitments. School provides support for continued community engagement alongside academic success. Students must maintain commitment to community service standards throughout enrolment.

overview: Calvary Christian College offers Academic Scholarships to recognise students demonstrating exceptional academic ability and outstanding performance. These scholarships are available to students entering Year 5, 7, 9, and 11 who achieve strong results on the ACER Academic Aptitude Assessment.
description: Academic Scholarships are awarded to students who demonstrate exceptional academic excellence through an ACER Academic Aptitude Assessment. The scholarship recognises outstanding intellectual ability and strong academic performance. The assessment consists of level-appropriate tests administered through the Australian Council of Educational Research (ACER) at the College Carbrook campus. Each candidate must undertake the required ACER test based on their year level (Level 1 for Year 7, Level 2 for Year 9, Level 3 for Year 11). Successful candidates are awarded scholarships on the basis of their assessment performance, with the Scholarship Panel determining the value appropriate to the strength of their application. Renewal conditions include maintaining consistent meeting of College academic and behavioural expectations throughout the scholarship period.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 5; Open to students entering Year 7; Open to students entering Year 9; Open to students entering Year 11; Must undertake ACER Academic Aptitude Assessment; Assessment candidates must achieve exceptional results on the ACER test; Scholarship holders must consistently meet College academic and behavioural expectations
special_conditions: Scholarship candidates are required to undertake an Academic Aptitude Assessment through the Australian Council of Educational Research (ACER). Assessment exams are held at the College Carbrook campus on 7 March 2026. The Scholarship Panel determines the value of each scholarship offer based on the strength of the application. Scholarship holders are required to consistently meet College academic and behavioural expectations.

overview: Calvary Christian College offers Excellence Scholarships to recognise students demonstrating exceptional accomplishment in specific fields such as sport, music, leadership, or community contribution. These scholarships acknowledge students in Years 5-12 who display outstanding talent and make significant contributions to the vibrant College community.
description: The Excellence Scholarship acknowledges exceptional achievement in particular areas including sport, music, leadership, and other cocurricular contributions. Recipients demonstrate significant progress in academic learning, develop unique talent in fields such as music, sport, leadership, or cultural activities, and contribute meaningfully to the Vibrant Community of the College. Excellence Scholarships are awarded on a two-year duration basis and are renewable, subject to maintaining the exceptional performance level. The Scholarship Panel (comprising at least 3 members of the Executive Leadership Team) determines the value of each scholarship offer based on the application strength and the candidate's demonstrated level of excellence. Once the College becomes aware that a student is performing at an exceptionally high level (state or national representative in a school-endorsed program), the scholarship panel convenes to consider the award.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 5-12; Must demonstrate exceptionally high level of accomplishment in a specific field; Must demonstrate significant academic progress and learning; Must develop unique talent in music, sport, leadership or other cultural/community activity; Must contribute to the Vibrant Community of the College; Must exemplify College vision of developing agile minds, compassion for others, and courage to act on beliefs; Scholarship holders must consistently meet College academic and behavioural expectations; For students performing at state or national representative level in school-endorsed programs
special_conditions: Excellence Scholarships have a two-year duration and are renewable. Applications are considered annually during Term 1 for the following academic year, with offers made in Term 2. A limited number of Excellence Scholarships can be awarded per year level, and this may vary from year to year at the discretion of the College Principal. Interested students are required to prepare a brief portfolio for the scholarship application and may be asked to attend an interview with the scholarship panel early in Term 2. Portfolios must be submitted to reception by the application deadline. Excellence Scholarships are awarded for the period that the student maintains performance at the exceptionally high level for which the scholarship was awarded, for example remaining a member of a state or national sporting team or state/national youth ensemble, choir or orchestra. Scholarship holders are required to consistently meet College academic and behavioural expectations.

overview: Caloundra State High School, in conjunction with the Parents & Citizens Association, offers 15 Academic Scholarships to Year 7 students enrolling. These excellence scholarships reward outstanding students who have succeeded academically, are good role models to their peers, and are active participants in community, sporting and cultural activities.
description: The Year 7 Horizons Academic Scholarship program provides recipients with one everyday school uniform including tie and hat, sponsored by the CSHS P&C Association. The scholarship value is not transferrable as cash and can only be used for the specified uniform purposes. Applicants are required to complete a comprehensive scholarship application form along with supporting documentation including a copy of their Year 5 NAPLAN results, a letter from their Year 6 teacher confirming exemplary conduct, details of leadership roles and positions held, and documentation of involvement in school and community activities. Selection criteria include demonstrated academic excellence, exemplary conduct at all times, leadership qualities, and active participation in community, sporting and cultural pursuits. The scholarship is valued at one everyday school uniform (blouse/shirt, tie, and skirt/shorts, and hat) and is awarded for enrolment at Year 7 through the duration of secondary schooling at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be currently enrolled in Year 6; Academic performance demonstrating excellence; Exemplary conduct and behaviour; Active participation in community, sporting and cultural activities; Leadership qualities demonstrated through recent school roles or positions; Current student of a Queensland primary school
value_aud: One everyday school uniform including tie and hat
special_conditions: Recipients of scholarships will be notified on Thursday 2 April 2026. The scholarship value is not transferrable as cash and can only be used for the specified uniform purposes. All applications must include documentary evidence with photocopies attached to the application. Caloundra State High School reserves the right to cancel any scholarship if information provided is found to be false or misleading.

program_name: Sporting Excellence Signature Program - Rugby League
overview: Caloundra State High School offers a Sporting Excellence program in Rugby League designed to assist students in reaching and extending their full potential in this sport. The program provides specialist coaching and training opportunities for students demonstrating excellence in Rugby League.
description: The Rugby League Sporting Excellence Signature Program is available to students in Years 7 through Year 12, providing comprehensive development opportunities in rugby league. Students in this program receive specialist coaching and are part of a targeted training and competition program. The program requires significant commitment of time and effort. As each program requires substantial dedication, it is recommended that students participate in only one Program of Excellence so they can experience the full benefit of the program. Application requires completion of the Sports Programs of Excellence application form with supporting documentation demonstrating rugby league ability and commitment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 and continuing through Year 12; Demonstrated rugby league ability and skill; Commitment to the program's training and competition schedule; Currently enrolled in Year 6 or transferring from another school; Willingness to commit significant time and effort to the program
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12

overview: Caloundra State High School offers a Sporting Excellence program in Surfing designed to assist students in reaching and extending their full potential in this sport. The program provides specialist coaching and training opportunities for students demonstrating excellence or interest in surfing.
description: The Surfing Sporting Excellence Signature Program is available to students in Years 7 through Year 12, providing comprehensive development opportunities in surfing. Located just minutes from pristine beaches including Kings Beach, Moffat Beach, and Dicky Beach, the program offers experienced surfers the opportunity to improve their skills and beginners the chance to develop foundational surfing abilities. Students gain parallel knowledge including surfboard building and repair, coastal ecology, and coastal protection. The program requires significant commitment of time and effort. As each program requires substantial dedication, it is recommended that students participate in only one Program of Excellence so they can experience the full benefit of the program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 and continuing through Year 12; Demonstrated surfing ability or interest in developing surfing skills; Commitment to the program's training and competition schedule; Currently enrolled in Year 6 or transferring from another school; Willingness to commit significant time and effort to the program

overview: Calvary Christian College offers an Academic Scholarship to Year 5 students entering the school who demonstrate exceptional academic ability. The exact financial benefit and value varies depending on the candidate's performance and the category of award determined by the scholarship panel. The scholarship is subject to annual review and renewal. Scholarship holders are required to consistently meet College academic and behavioural expectations. Applications close 25 February 2026 for entry in 2027.

overview: Calvary Christian College offers the Excellence Scholarship to students in Years 5 to 12 who demonstrate exceptionally high levels of accomplishment in a specific field or context such as sport, music, or cultural activities. Recipients must share their unique talent for the enrichment of the College community.
description: The Excellence Scholarship acknowledges excellence in particular areas such as sport, music, leadership, or other cultural or community activities, along with a student's learning progress, development of effective learning habits, and co-curricular contribution to the College. Recipients must demonstrate significant progress in academic learning and exemplify the College vision of developing agile minds, compassion for others, and courage to act on beliefs. The scholarship is awarded for the period that the student maintains performance at the exceptionally high level, such as remaining a member of a state or national sporting team or youth ensemble. The value is determined on the basis of the student's application and interview, with the Scholarship Panel making an offer appropriate to the strength of the application. Scholarships have a two-year duration and are renewable.

Once the College is aware that a student is performing at an exceptionally high level, the scholarship panel will convene to consider the award (which may occur at any point in the year). Offers made in Term 2 for the following academic year. Scholarship holders are required to consistently meet College academic and behavioural expectations. Scholarships are awarded for the period that the student maintains performance at the exceptionally high level for which the scholarship was awarded.

school_name: Camp Hill State Infants and Primary School

overview: Caningeraba State School operates a Gifted and Talented and Enrichment program designed to support students who are identified as gifted and talented. The school is committed to developing effective partnerships with parents and providing quality education for gifted students through a comprehensive identification process and targeted enrichment activities.
description: Caningeraba State School's Gifted and Talented/Enrichment Program aims to recognise and support students identified as gifted and talented across all year levels from Prep to Year 6. The school employs a dedicated Gifted and Talented/Enrichment Coordinator whose roles and responsibilities include providing tailored educational opportunities for identified students. The identification process involves multiple assessment methods including classroom teacher observations, standardised testing, IQ assessments (such as WISC III, used specifically for acceleration purposes), and social/emotional/behavioural assessments. A Gifted and Talented/Enrichment Committee meets to review data and determine areas of giftedness for each student. Enrichment activities are offered to broaden and develop students' knowledge, skills, processes and experiences, with opportunities to participate in extension and enrichment programs appropriate to individual needs. The school recognises that giftedness becomes talent through quality learning experiences supported by personal contexts (motivation, confidence, perseverance) and environmental contexts (school provisions and significant persons). There is no monetary value or fee associated with this program as it is provided as part of standard school offerings within the government system.
eligibility_criteria: Identified by classroom teacher observations; Assessment through standardised testing; Social/emotional/behavioural assessments; Committee review process; Open to students in all year levels Prep to Year 6; IQ testing available for acceleration purposes
special_conditions: IQ tests such as WISC III are used specifically for the purpose of acceleration only. The school is committed to continuing this program and has employed a dedicated teacher to coordinate and deliver services. Enrichment opportunities may vary depending on individual student needs and school resources. The program operates within the Queensland state school system and is subject to state government education requirements and procedures.

overview: Cannon Hill Anglican College offers Academic Scholarships for students entering Year 7, affording partial remission of tuition fees. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability through competitive examination, previous school performance and principal interview.
description: Cannon Hill Anglican College is pleased to offer a number of Academic Scholarships for students entering the College in Year 7, affording partial remission of tuition fees. All students, including those already enrolled for Year 7 at CHAC or at other schools, are eligible to apply to sit for the Scholarship Exam. Offers of scholarships for successful candidates are based on the scholarship examination results, previous school performance and an interview with the Principal. Scholarships apply in the first instance for Years 7 to 10. A review in Year 10 determines the possible continuation of a Scholarship for Years 11 and 12 based on academic performance and conduct. The examination is administered by Edutest and covers reading comprehension, mathematics, verbal reasoning and numerical reasoning.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be Australian or New Zealand citizens or Australian permanent residents; Temporary visa holders (188, 457, 500 visas) not eligible; All students eligible to apply, including those already enrolled at CHAC or other schools
special_conditions: Scholarships apply in the first instance for Years 7 to 10. A review in Year 10 determines the possible continuation of a Scholarship for Years 11 and 12 based on academic performance, attendance and conduct. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress and conduct standards.

overview: In partnership with the Kela Foundation, CHAC offers the Financial Needs Scholarship to support a Secondary student to attend Cannon Hill Anglican College who may otherwise be unable to afford a CHAC education. The scholarship provides full tuition remission plus uniform allowance for eligible Year 9 students entering Year 10.
description: The CHAC/Kela Foundation Financial Needs Scholarship provides financial support for a Secondary student to attend Cannon Hill Anglican College who may otherwise be unable to attend the College without financial assistance. The scholarship is open to Year 9 students not currently enrolled at CHAC who can demonstrate family financial need. Applicants must show solid academic records through three recent school reports and submit a 300-word personal statement. Successful candidates are invited for an interview with the College Principal. The scholarship provides full remission of annual tuition fees, uniforms and some identified incidentals. Recipients may hold the Scholarship for up to three years (Years 10-12), subject to annual review and maintenance of academic standards and conduct requirements. An academic scholarship examination administered by Edutest is required as part of the selection process.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 9 students; Must be currently enrolled at other schools in Queensland (not CHAC); Must demonstrate family financial need with combined taxable income approximately $100,000 or less (amount adjusts based on number of dependent children); Must demonstrate solid academic record via three recent school reports; Must provide 300-word personal statement; Australian or New Zealand citizenship or permanent residency status
special_conditions: Scholarship tenable for up to three years (Years 10-12). Recipient must maintain satisfactory academic progress and conduct standards. Parents/guardians must enter into Enrolment Agreement with the College. Annual review of key criteria determines possible continuation. The Kela Foundation subsidises the academic scholarship examination registration fee. Scholarship includes financial support for uniforms and education-based standard incidentals.

overview: Canterbury College offers Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding achievement in students entering Years 5-10. These scholarships recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and excel in STEM subjects including Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics.
description: The Academic Scholarship program at Canterbury College provides recognition and support for students entering Years 5-10 who demonstrate exceptional academic ability, with particular emphasis on STEM achievement. Applicants may apply for either an Academic Scholarship or an Academic and Mathematics Scholarship. The scholarship is awarded on a merit basis following a written examination held each year. Applications close on Friday 20 March 2026, with the scholarship examination held on Saturday 28 March 2026 from 8:00am to 12:00pm at Canterbury College. The Mathematics component is included in the overall exam, not offered separately. Students must sit the entire exam. Selection is based purely on academic merit demonstrated in the examination. Students must be entering Years 5, 6, 7, 8, 9 or 10 in 2027. The specific financial value of the scholarship is determined on an individual basis.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 5-10 in 2027; Applications close 20 March 2026; Merit-based on written examination; Examination held 28 March 2026; Students may apply for Academic or Academic and Mathematics Scholarship
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10
