special_conditions: Students applying for an Academic Scholarship must lodge that application first and pay the Enrolment Application Fee through the online process. All students must sit the entire exam. The Mathematics component is included in the overall exam, not offered separately.

overview: Canterbury College offers Music Scholarships to students entering Years 7-10 who demonstrate exceptional musical ability. Applications are strongly encouraged for students excelling in Voice, Clarinet, Cello, Double Bass, Drum Kit, Trombone and Trumpet.
description: Canterbury College's Music Scholarship program provides recognition and support for students entering Years 7-10 in 2027 who demonstrate outstanding musical ability. Applicants are strongly encouraged if they excel in Voice, Clarinet, Cello, Double Bass, Drum Kit, Trombone or Trumpet. The selection process requires submission of a Music Scholarship Enrolment Application, a Music Scholarship Supplementary Information Form, and two to three 30-second examples of music performances demonstrating different styles, techniques and tempos, to be submitted to the College Registrar by USB or email. Applications open on 1 January 2026 and close on 30 April 2026 for entry into 2027. A limited number of scholarships are awarded each year based on musical merit. Both the Music Scholarship Enrolment Application and Supplementary Information Form must be lodged to be eligible for an enrolment offer. The specific financial value of the scholarship is determined on an individual basis.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7-10 in 2027; Applications open 1 January 2026 and close 30 April 2026; Demonstrated musical ability in listed instruments or voice; Must submit performance examples; Merit-based selection
special_conditions: Both Music Scholarship Enrolment Application and Supplementary Information Form must be lodged to be eligible for an enrolment offer. Applicants must provide 2 or 3 x 30 second examples of music performances demonstrating different styles, techniques and tempo by USB or email. Music, Sport and Leadership Scholarship Applications may be submitted on the same Enrolment Application Form.

overview: Canterbury College offers Sport Scholarships to students entering Years 7-11 who demonstrate exceptional sporting ability and commitment. Scholarships are awarded to students who excel in approved sports including Athletics, Basketball, Cricket, Cross Country, Football, Hockey, Netball, Rugby, Swimming, Tennis, Touch Football and Volleyball.
description: Canterbury College's Sport Scholarship program provides recognition and support for a limited number of students entering Years 7-11 in 2027 who demonstrate outstanding sporting ability and commitment to excellence. Applicants must demonstrate exceptional ability in one or more sports that Canterbury participates in via an approved competition, attain very high levels of representative honours in that sport or sports, maintain the highest standards of behaviour, motivation and effort in academic studies as evidenced by report cards, and show a willingness to contribute across a range of Canterbury sports while thriving in the school's high expectations culture. The scholarship program emphasises core elements including Skills, Resilience, Leadership, Teamwork, Inclusivity, High Performance, Holistic Development and Mentorship. Both a Sport Scholarship Enrolment Application and Sport Scholarship Supplementary Information Form must be lodged to be eligible for an enrolment offer. Applications open on 1 January 2026 and close on 30 April 2026 for entry into 2027. The specific financial value of the scholarship is determined on an individual basis.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7-11 in 2027; Applications open 1 January 2026 and close 30 April 2026; Demonstrate outstanding ability in one or more approved sports; Attainment of very high levels of representative honours; Highest standards of behaviour, motivation and effort in academic studies; Willingness to contribute across range of sports; Ability to thrive in high expectations school culture; Entry is purely merit-based
special_conditions: Both Sport Scholarship Enrolment Application and Sport Scholarship Supplementary Information Form must be lodged to be eligible for an enrolment offer. Entry is purely merit-based. Limited vacancies available each year, so parents are encouraged to lodge applications as soon as possible. Scholarship subject to annual review and student must maintain high academic standards.

overview: Canterbury College awards Leadership & Community Excellence Scholarships each year to new students commencing in Year 7 who demonstrate exceptional leadership qualities and community contribution. These scholarships recognise students who hold senior leadership positions and show commitment to community service.
description: Canterbury College's Leadership & Community Excellence Scholarship program recognises and rewards a limited number of new students entering Year 7 in 2027 who demonstrate exceptional leadership capability and commitment to community service. Scholarship criteria include holding a senior leadership position in Primary or Junior School such as Junior School Captain, Vice-Captain, Sports Captain, Arts Captain, or SRC President; demonstrating ongoing contribution to community service, volunteering or participation in public awareness campaigns; and showing recognition of 'servant leadership' qualities as evidenced by teachers or school leaders in letters of recommendation or similar documentation. Evidence required to support applications may include letters of appointment, certificates of achievement or recognition, and letters of recommendation from school leaders or teachers. Applications must include the Leadership & Community Excellence Scholarship Enrolment Application. Applications open on 1 January 2026 and close on 30 April 2026 for entry into 2027. The specific financial value of the scholarship is determined on an individual basis.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students entering Year 7 in 2027; Applications open 1 January 2026 and close 30 April 2026; Must hold senior leadership position in primary/junior school (Junior School Captain, Vice-Captain, Sports Captain, Arts Captain, SRC President, etc.); Ongoing contribution to community service, volunteering or public awareness campaigns; Recognition of servant leadership qualities by teachers or school leaders
special_conditions: Open to new students entering Year 7 only. Evidence required includes letters of appointment, certificates of achievement or recognition, and letters of recommendation by school leaders or teachers. Music, Sport and Leadership Scholarship Applications may be submitted on the same Enrolment Application Form.

school_name: Capricornia (Emerald Campus) School of Distance Education
Carinity Education - Rockhampton - Mount Chalmers Campus
Capricornia (Rockhampton Campus) School of Dist Ed

program_name: Jenny Medland Search Light Inc. Bursary
overview: Carinity Education Southside offers the Jenny Medland Search Light Inc. Bursary to support graduating Year 12 students in their transition to further education. The bursary, worth $1,000 annually, recognises the commitment and achievements of students completing their secondary education while parenting or pregnant.
description: The Jenny Medland Search Light Inc. Bursary is worth $1,000 and can be used in any way that helps support students with their studies. The bursary is awarded each year to one student from Carinity Education Southside who intends to continue their education at a recognised institution. Established in honour of Jenny Medland, a founding member of Search Light Inc. who was dedicated to supporting and seeking opportunities for young women in need, the bursary recognises the unique challenges faced by young mothers completing Year 12. Recipients are selected from graduating Year 12 students, with preference given to those continuing into further education, vocational training, or higher education pathways.
eligibility_criteria: Graduating Year 12 student; Intends to continue education at a recognised institution; Preference for young mothers or pregnant students; Student of Carinity Education Southside
special_conditions: Award is presented to one student per year from Carinity Education Southside. The bursary can be used in any way that helps support students with their studies. Established by Search Light Inc. in honour of Jenny Medland.

overview: Carinity Education Southside offers the QATSIF (Queensland Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Foundation) Scholarship to First Nations students. The scholarship provides financial assistance to help students achieve their learning goals while recognising their individual commitments and aspirations for the future.
description: Each year First Nations students at Carinity Education Southside are given an opportunity to apply for and receive financial assistance through the QATSIF scholarship program that provides funding to assist students to achieve their learning goals. The QATSIF scholarship provides two-year funding to support students commencing in Year 11 and continuing through Year 12. Students are required to prove their suitability and determination through several application questions about their individual commitments and goals for the future. The specific financial value of individual QATSIF scholarships varies based on student circumstances and assessed need. Students receiving QATSIF funds understand the historical significance of these monies, which were built from the labour of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Queenslanders under now-repealed control laws and policies.
eligibility_criteria: First Nations / Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander student; Enrolled at Carinity Education Southside; Year 11 or Year 12 student; Must demonstrate suitability and determination through application process; Queensland resident
special_conditions: Open to First Nations students only. QATSIF administers scholarships through funded schools which then provide scholarships to individual students. School assists with applications. Students must remain engaged with their studies and maintain eligibility throughout the scholarship period.

overview: Centenary Heights State High School offers the Music Excellence Program Scholarship to recognise and support outstanding young music students. The scholarship provides financial support to successful applicants entering the two-year Music Excellence Program in Years 7 and 8.
description: Centenary Heights State High School provides a two-year Music Excellence Scholarship valued at $460 for two successful applicants in 2026. The scholarship is part of the Music Excellence Program, which consists of a two-year classroom specialist music course across Years 7 and 8 alongside participation in extra-curricular instrumental and/or vocal ensemble programs. Throughout the year, students perform at various school and community events and participate in music workshops with visiting instrumental and vocal specialists, including conductors and master teachers from the Queensland Conservatorium Griffith University. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional musicianship and commitment to music performance. Selection is through an audition process where applications are accepted in two rounds. Round 1 applications close Friday 5 June 2025 with auditions on Wednesday 25 June 2025. Round 2 applications close Friday 10 October 2025 with auditions on Wednesday 29 October 2025.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Successfully pass Music Excellence Program audition; Must demonstrate musicianship and performance ability; Entry offered to students across the Toowoomba region (both in and out of catchment area)
special_conditions: Scholarship is for two years duration (Year 7 and Year 8). Recipients must participate in the two-year Music Excellence classroom course and extra-curricular instrumental and/or vocal ensemble programs. Students must maintain participation in the program to retain scholarship eligibility.

overview: Centenary Heights State High School offers the Mandarin Chinese Program of Excellence, a selective entry program for students who wish to pursue advanced study of Mandarin Chinese language and culture. The program is part of the school's specialist offerings and provides enhanced learning opportunities in Chinese language studies.
description: The Mandarin Chinese Program of Excellence is a selective entry program at Centenary Heights State High School that provides enhanced opportunities for students interested in studying Mandarin Chinese. The program is supported through the allocation of defined enrolment places, with capacity for up to 140 students including international student enrolments. Entry into the program is through application and selection criteria determined by the school. The program is open to both in-catchment and out-of-catchment students, with places available to out-of-catchment enrolments once in-catchment demand has been met. Students in Years 7 and 8 study Chinese as part of their curriculum, with options for continuation through Years 9 and 12. The program supports students who demonstrate interest and aptitude in language learning and cross-cultural understanding.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or existing students; Interest and aptitude in language learning; Out-of-catchment applications considered after in-catchment demand is met; Up to 140 total places available including international students
special_conditions: Program places are defined and managed through the school's Enrolment Management Plan. Specific selection criteria are available directly from the school. Enrolment criteria for the Program of Excellence should be obtained from the school.

overview: Centenary Heights State High School operates the Toowoomba Flexi School, an alternate learning environment providing specialized support for students in Years 10, 11 and 12 who have been disengaged from mainstream schooling. The program offers a responsive, small-scale learning environment tailored to individual student needs.
description: The Toowoomba Flexi School is an award-winning annexe of Centenary Heights State High School providing a responsive learning environment for young people to complete their senior education. The program caters to students in Years 10, 11 and 12 who have been otherwise disengaged from mainstream schooling. The Flexi School is staffed with accredited teachers, teacher aides, a part-time school health nurse, social worker, wellbeing worker, guidance officer, and an industry liaison officer. The program is designed to break the cycle of disengagement and assists young people to successfully transition to work, further study and become confident young adults. All students are expected to work towards achieving a Queensland Certificate of Education (QCE), vocational certificates and school-based traineeships. The success rate of Flexi School graduates achieving a QCE or moving to tertiary study or fulltime employment is very high. Enrolment capacity is limited to 65 students and expressions of interest are accepted throughout the year.
eligibility_criteria: Students in Years 10, 11 or 12; Must be seeking alternative to mainstream schooling; Must demonstrate commitment to learning; Capacity limited to 65 students; Expression of Interest required; Applicants assessed based on individual need and capacity of program to support
special_conditions: Flexi cannot guarantee placement of all applicants due to growing demands and specific support needs assessment. Flexi may not accept applications if it cannot adequately cater to educational needs or if student poses unacceptable risk to safety of others. Students placed on waiting list if year level capacity is reached. Expression of Interest accepted throughout the year. Contact Enrolments Officer at 4636 7500 or enrolments@centheigshs.eq.edu.au.

overview: Centenary State High School offers the Year 7 Academic Scholarship to recognise and reward high-achieving students entering Year 7. This scholarship is designed for students who demonstrate exceptional academic excellence and potential.
description: The Year 7 Academic Scholarship is available to students entering Year 7 at Centenary State High School. This scholarship recognises outstanding academic achievement and commitment to learning. The scholarship is offered through a competitive application process. As a Queensland government school, this scholarship provides recognition and support for high-achieving students. Further details regarding the exact financial value and specific eligibility criteria are available through the school's enrolment documentation. The scholarship application is submitted separately from the main enrolment application, with separate deadlines noted in the Year 7 enrolment package.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Outstanding academic achievement; Demonstrated excellence in previous schooling
special_conditions: Scholarship applications are submitted separately from the main enrolment application and are due at a later date.

overview: Centenary State High School offers the Year 7 Instrumental Music Scholarship for talented young musicians. This scholarship is designed to support dedicated students who are committed to instrumental music and wish to continue their musical development at the school.
description: The Year 7 Instrumental Music Scholarship is offered by the school's P&C Arts Sub-committee to dedicated and talented Year 6 students enrolled in instrumental music programs at local state primary schools. Selected students are nominated by their primary school instrumental music teachers and must undertake an interview process before receiving confirmation of acceptance. This scholarship covers the instrumental music levy, the cost of the school music camp, and the purchase of an Arts shirt. The scholarship is designed to support talented young musicians in continuing their instrumental music participation through secondary school. This recognises the school's commitment to maintaining an active and important instrumental music program within the school community.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled in instrumental music program at primary school; Nominated by primary school instrumental music teacher(s); Year 6 students; Must undertake interview process; Demonstrated musical talent and commitment
special_conditions: Nomination by primary school instrumental music teacher(s) is required. Selected students must undertake an interview process before receiving confirmation of acceptance. Covers instrumental music levy, school music camp costs, and Arts shirt purchase only.

overview: Music Plus is an extension program offered by Centenary State High School that caters to students in Years 7–9 with advanced musical abilities. The program is designed for instrumentalists and vocalists who wish to explore and extend their musical capabilities across a range of genres.
description: Music Plus is an extension program which caters to students in Years 7–9 with advanced musical abilities. Instrumentalists and vocalists are given the opportunity to explore and extend their musical capabilities across a range of genres. This excellence program utilises curriculum time (3 x 70-minute lessons) of the learning area. The program provides extended learning experiences and opportunities tailored for high achieving students, who will be required to provide evidence of their prior related achievements. Students may apply for, and study in, more than one excellence program. All programs are subject to class size limits and entry requirements. The Music Plus program continues through the junior secondary years and is part of the school's philosophy of providing pathways to meet the needs of individual students.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Year 7 or already enrolled in Years 7-9; Demonstrated advanced musical ability; Prior musical achievements and experience required; Provision of evidence of related achievements
special_conditions: Program subject to class size limits and entry requirements. Application required with evidence of prior musical achievement. Multiple applications permitted across excellence programs.

overview: Performance Plus is an extension program offered by Centenary State High School that caters to students in Years 7–9 with advanced skills in dance, drama, or musical theatre. The program allows students to explore and extend their performance skills across multiple genres and styles.
description: Performance Plus is an extension program which caters to students in Years 7–9 with advanced skills in dance, drama or musical theatre. Students are given the opportunity to explore and extend their skills across a range of musical theatre genres, dance and drama styles. This excellence program utilises curriculum time (3 x 70-minute lessons) of the learning area and provides extended learning experiences and opportunities tailored for high achieving students, who will be required to provide evidence of their prior related achievements in performing arts. Students may apply for, and study in, more than one excellence program. All programs are subject to class size limits and entry requirements. The program continues through the junior secondary years as part of the school's commitment to fostering pathways for high-achieving students.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Year 7 or already enrolled in Years 7-9; Advanced skills in dance, drama, or musical theatre; Prior achievements in performing arts required; Evidence of related achievements must be provided
special_conditions: Program subject to class size limits and entry requirements. Application required with evidence of prior performing arts achievement. Multiple applications permitted across excellence programs.

program_name: Mathematics and Science eXcel Excellence Program
overview: Centenary State High School offers the Mathematics and Science eXcel excellence program for students in Years 7–9 who demonstrate advanced capabilities in mathematics and science. This program provides acceleration and extension opportunities for high-achieving students in these subject areas.
description: Mathematics and Science eXcel is an excellence program offered to beginning Year 7 students and continuing through Years 7–9. This program is designed for a small number of students who have already demonstrated a very high level of capability in both mathematics and science. The program provides extended learning experiences and opportunities specifically tailored for high achieving students, who will be required to provide evidence of their prior related achievements. The eXcel program utilises curriculum time (3 x 70-minute lessons) of the learning area each week. The school capacity for eXcel is 28 students in each year (Years 7-9). Students may apply for, and study in, more than one excellence program. All programs are subject to class size limits and entry requirements.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Year 7 or already enrolled in Years 7-9; Very high level of demonstrated capability in both mathematics and science; Prior achievements in mathematics and science required; Evidence of related achievements and advanced ability must be provided
special_conditions: Program subject to maximum class size of 28 students per year level (Years 7-9) and entry requirements. Application required with evidence of prior mathematics and science achievement. Multiple applications permitted across excellence programs.

overview: Centenary State High School offers the Chinese Mandarin Excellence Program for students with advanced language abilities in Chinese Mandarin. The program is part of the school's commitment to providing pathways of excellence and developing students' skills in this important global language.
description: The Chinese Mandarin Excellence Program is offered through application process to Years 7 and 8 students who have advanced language abilities in Chinese Mandarin. The school has developed strong links with schools in China supporting this program. Centenary State High School was granted Confucius Classroom status in 2011, being one of only a few Queensland schools to achieve this recognition. The program provides extended learning experiences and opportunities tailored for high achieving students with advanced Mandarin capabilities who will be required to provide evidence of their prior related achievements. Currently the school has capacity to enrol 128 students in the Mandarin Program of Excellence. Students may apply for, and study in, more than one excellence program. All programs are subject to class size limits and entry requirements.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Year 7 or enrolled in Years 7-8; Advanced language abilities in Chinese Mandarin demonstrated; Prior achievements in Mandarin language study required; Evidence of advanced language capability must be provided
special_conditions: Application required with evidence of prior Mandarin language achievement. Maximum capacity 128 students across Years 7-9. School has Confucius Classroom status. Strong links with schools in China. Multiple applications permitted across excellence programs.

school_name: Charters Towers School of Distance Education
Christ the King Catholic Primary School

overview: Chisholm Catholic College provides scholarships to senior Indigenous students through QATSIF funding. The scholarship recognises and supports Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students who have gained enrolment into the College, providing financial assistance to further their opportunities for success.
description: This funding enables senior Indigenous students to receive scholarships that provide financial assistance. Chisholm Catholic College has been a QATSIF school for five years, and through this partnership, the school provides targeted support to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. The scholarship helps make Catholic education more accessible to Indigenous families. The College reports 100% of its Indigenous students have graduated with Year 12 certificates and proceeded to exciting career pathways. The exact value of the scholarship and specific duration are not publicly detailed on the school website, though it appears to be ongoing support for eligible Indigenous students throughout their secondary education.
eligibility_criteria: Must be Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander; Must desire a Catholic education; Must demonstrate a sound foundation for learning; Must successfully gain enrolment into the College
special_conditions: Progress is reviewed on an ongoing basis. The Principal reserves the right to withdraw the scholarship if the student does not commit to all aspects of the teaching and learning programme of the College. Funding provided through the Queensland Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Student Support (QATSIF) program.

program_name: Fee Concession Program for Financial Hardship
overview: Chisholm Catholic College offers fee concessions to families experiencing financial hardship. The program is designed to ensure that financial difficulties do not prevent enrolment or continued attendance at the College, with applications considered confidentially by the College.
description: In cases of financial hardship, families may apply to the College for a fee concession. Applications are considered in the strictest confidence by the College Fees Committee. The committee considers the family's circumstances, attempts to meet fees, commitment to payment, and communication regarding any previous debts when evaluating each application. Assistance is only available for fees in the current school year, not for balances from previous years. Families who are provided with fee assistance must complete and sign a Fee Commitment Agreement, authorise any applicable government assistance allowances to be transferred to the College, and agree to pay via direct debit authority. Contact the Business Manager in the first instance to discuss hardship or to obtain further information about the concession application process.
eligibility_criteria: Family experiencing financial hardship; Must complete application form with supporting documentation; Must enter into Fee Commitment Agreement; Must establish direct debit authority
special_conditions: All matters handled on confidential basis. Assistance applies only to current year fees. Families must maintain direct debit payments and comply with the Fee Commitment Agreement. The College reserves the right to review assistance annually.

overview: Citipointe Christian College offers Academic Scholarships to Year 7 entry students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and strong potential. A limited number of academic scholarships are awarded annually, offering substantial tuition fee discounts to recognise and support outstanding students through to Year 12.
description: Citipointe invites applications for Year 7 entry academic scholarship from students currently in Year 5 or Year 6. A limited number of academic scholarships are awarded annually, offering up to 50% discount on tuition fees for Years 7-12. Scholarship recipients are determined based on Edutest Scholarship Testing results, academic track record, and an interview with the Principal. Applicants must demonstrate qualities aligned with the ethos of Citipointe Christian College and meet all standard enrolment criteria. The scholarship is valid for the duration of enrolment from Year 7 through to Year 12. Scholarships are not available to Full Fee Paying Overseas Students. Selection involves a rigorous assessment process combining standardised testing, academic performance review, and principal interview.
eligibility_criteria: Currently in Year 5 or Year 6; Open to entry to Year 7 2026 or 2027; Must achieve results of Edutest Scholarship Testing; Must demonstrate qualities aligned with Citipointe Christian College ethos; Must meet all standard enrolment standards; Not available to Full Fee Paying Overseas Students
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients are required to maintain commitment to the College Christian ethos. Scholarships are awarded on the basis that recipients remain enrolled at the College and continue to meet academic and conduct standards.

overview: Citipointe Equity Scholarships are designed for students whose parents wish to enrol at the College but face financial barriers preventing them from covering full tuition fees. The College typically awards one Equity Scholarship annually through the Cardo Foundation Scholarship Fund to cover tuition and agreed expenses.
description: Citipointe Equity Scholarships provide financial support for worthy students whose family circumstances would otherwise prevent them from attending the College. Applications are open for Year 7 entry and other year levels from Year 7 to Year 10, depending on vacancies. The College will normally offer one Equity Scholarship through the Cardo Foundation Scholarship Fund each year to cover tuition and other agreed expenses, funding permitting. The value of the scholarship varies based on assessed need and funding availability. The College's normal enrolment application form together with a completed Equity Scholarship application form must be completed and submitted in the year prior to entry. A committee composed of Cardo Foundation directors/members evaluates applications and makes recommendations to the College regarding acceptance of enrolment linked to the award of an Equity Scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: Parents or students facing financial barriers preventing enrolment; Open to entry at Year 7 or other year levels from Year 7 to Year 10 depending on vacancies; Must apply in the year prior to entry; Must complete both standard enrolment application form and Equity Scholarship application form
special_conditions: Terms and conditions apply to the Equity Scholarship and will be supplied with the acceptance form. Scholarship is subject to continued meeting of academic and conduct standards. Must support the College Christian ethos. Recipients must maintain commitment to completing their secondary education at the College.

overview: Clairvaux MacKillop College offers a limited music scholarship for students seeking to learn an unusual instrument. The scholarship provides tuition support for one year on the student's chosen instrument.
description: This scholarship is available to students wishing to learn an unusual or specialized instrument at Clairvaux MacKillop College. The scholarship covers the cost of individual music tuition on the nominated instrument for a period of one year. Students must demonstrate interest and commitment to learning their chosen unusual instrument. The selection process involves demonstration of musical aptitude and interest. The college recognises that instrumental music tuition represents a significant financial commitment for families, and this scholarship aims to provide access to music education in less commonly studied instruments. Details regarding application process and specific eligible instruments should be confirmed directly with the college.
eligibility_criteria: Students wishing to learn an unusual or specialized instrument; Interest in music education; Demonstrated commitment to learning an instrument
special_conditions: Scholarship covers individual music tuition on the nominated instrument for one year only. Renewal beyond the initial year is not guaranteed.

program_name: Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund (MacKillop Fund) Bursary
overview: The Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund provides financial assistance bursaries to families experiencing extreme financial hardship seeking Catholic education at Clairvaux MacKillop College. The bursary is designed to make Catholic education accessible to students whose families lack the financial means to provide this opportunity.
description: The MacKillop Fund Bursary is administered through the Catholic Foundation and provides comprehensive financial assistance to eligible families. The bursary covers tuition fees, textbooks, uniforms, and local excursions, offering substantial support to families experiencing extreme hardship, trauma, and financial devastation. The fund is named in honour of Australia's first saint, St Mary of the Cross MacKillop, who dedicated her life to making Catholic education accessible to the poor. Bursary applications for enrolment in the following school year must reach the selection committee no later than 15 April. The MacKillop Fund commits to funding a child through the completion of the stage of school they are enrolled in (e.g., a secondary student will be funded through to the end of Year 12 as long as the family remains in need of assistance). Late applications may be considered depending on availability of funds.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing extreme financial hardship; Deep desire for child to receive Catholic education; Student seeking enrolment at a Brisbane Catholic Education school (new students only); Not currently enrolled in a Brisbane Catholic Education school; Financial need demonstrated through application process
special_conditions: Fund covers tuition, textbooks, uniforms, and local excursions. Bursary is renewed annually subject to continued financial need. Students currently enrolled in a Brisbane Catholic Education school are not eligible to apply for the MacKillop Bursary at their current school (should contact school principal directly for assistance). Committee meets in May annually to award bursaries for the following school year. Applications submitted after 15 April may be considered at the next May disbursement if funds are available.

overview: Clayfield College offers Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding academic achievement in incoming students. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability across all subjects and commitment to learning.
description: Academic Scholarships are awarded to new and current students entering Years 7 to 11 in 2027 based on demonstrated excellence in academic performance. The financial value and duration of the scholarship are determined following interview with the College's Senior Leadership Team, with scholarships applied to tuition fees. Recipients must maintain high standards in conduct and academic results throughout the scholarship period and actively participate in College life. The selection process involves sitting the ACER Scholarship Test on Saturday 28 February 2026, followed by submission of a portfolio including school reports, NAPLAN results, and a teacher reference, with shortlisted applicants invited to interview.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 to 11 in 2027; Must sit the ACER Scholarship Test on Saturday 28 February 2026; Current and new students eligible; Must submit portfolio with four recent school reports, NAPLAN results, and teacher/Principal reference; Shortlisted applicants must attend interview
special_conditions: Enrolment Registration Fee waiver code CC27SBP removes the $275 application fee. In accepting a scholarship, parents/caregivers agree their child will complete their education at Clayfield College. The College reserves the right to request full repayment of scholarship value if students/parents do not fulfil this expectation without valid cause. Students must maintain high standards in conduct and academic results throughout the scholarship period and actively participate and embrace College life.

overview: Clayfield College offers General Excellence Scholarships to recognise students who demonstrate excellent academic results alongside excellence in sports, the arts, leadership, or community service. The scholarship supports well-rounded students who contribute across multiple areas of school life.
description: General Excellence Scholarships are awarded to new and current students entering Years 7 to 11 in 2027 who demonstrate excellent academic results combined with excellence in one or more of the following areas: Sport (requiring State or National selection), The Arts/Music (requiring AMEB Grade 4 for Years 7-9, Grade 6 for Years 10-11), Leadership, or Community Service. The financial value and duration are determined by the College following interview with Senior Leadership Team. Selection involves submission of a portfolio including school reports, NAPLAN results, and references from coaches, teachers, or principals relevant to the area of excellence. Arts applicants must audition. The application fee of $275 is waived with code CC27SBP.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 to 11 in 2027; New and current students eligible; Must demonstrate excellent academic results; Must demonstrate excellence in Sport (State/National selection), The Arts/Music (AMEB Grade 4 minimum for Years 7-9; Grade 6 minimum for Years 10-11), Leadership or Community Service; Must submit portfolio with school reports, NAPLAN results, and relevant reference; Arts applicants must audition
special_conditions: Enrolment Registration Fee waiver code CC27SBP removes the $275 application fee. Excellence in Sport applicants must provide evidence of State or National selection in their portfolio. Excellence in The Arts (Music) applicants for Years 7 to 9 must have reached AMEB Grade 4 (or equivalent); applicants for Years 10 and 11 must have reached AMEB Grade 6 (or equivalent). Arts applicants shortlisted will be required to audition. In accepting a scholarship, parents/caregivers agree their child will complete education at Clayfield College. College reserves right to request full repayment if expectations not fulfilled without valid cause.

overview: The Clayfield College Foundation Scholarship provides a 50% reduction in tuition fees for domestic students entering Year 10. The scholarship aims to foster access, excellence, and long-term engagement within the Clayfield community for students with strong academic performance and genuine commitment to college life.
description: The Foundation Scholarship provides a 50% reduction in annual tuition fees for domestic students entering Year 10 in 2027, lasting for Years 10 to 12 (a minimum three-year commitment). Applicants must demonstrate strong academic performance and consistent progress as well as genuine commitment to Clayfield College and engagement in College life. The selection process includes submission of a portfolio to enrol@clayfield.qld.edu.au containing a covering letter, personal resume, four recent school reports, NAPLAN results, and a letter of support from the current Principal or teacher. Shortlisted applicants are invited to interview with a member of the College's Senior Leadership Team. The $275 application fee is waived with code CC27SBP.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students entering Year 10 in 2027; Must demonstrate strong academic performance and consistent progress; Must demonstrate genuine commitment to Clayfield College and engagement in College life; Domestic students only
special_conditions: New students entering Year 10 in 2027 only. Application fee waiver code CC27SBP removes $275 fee. Duration is for Years 10 to 12 (three years minimum). In accepting a scholarship, parents/caregivers agree their child will complete education at Clayfield College. College reserves right to request full repayment of total scholarship value if students/parents do not fulfil expectations without valid cause. Students must maintain high standards in conduct and academic results and actively participate in College life.

overview: Clayfield College offers Boarding Bursaries to support students from remote and rural areas who demonstrate willingness to contribute positively to the boarding and college community. The bursary provides financial assistance to enable students from rural Queensland and beyond to access the College's boarding facilities.
description: Boarding Bursaries are awarded to students from remote and rural areas who demonstrate a willingness to contribute in a positive way to the Boarding and College community and who currently require financial assistance to attend boarding school. The bursary supports students from rural Queensland, interstate and overseas locations. The financial value and duration are determined by the College based on individual circumstances. Selection involves portfolio submission including a covering letter, personal resume, two recent school reports, NAPLAN results, and a reference letter from a teacher, coach, or other non-family referee supporting the application. Shortlisted applicants are invited to interview with the Head of Boarding (Zoom interviews available). The $275 application fee is waived with code CC27SBP.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 to 11; From remote and rural areas; Currently require financial assistance to attend boarding school; Must demonstrate willingness to contribute positively to Boarding and College community; Must provide teacher, coach or non-family referee support
special_conditions: Application fee waiver code CC27SBP removes $275 fee. For remote and rural area students. Zoom interviews can be arranged for out-of-area applicants. Portfolio must include reference from teacher, coach, or non-family person (not parents/family). In accepting a scholarship, parents/caregivers agree their child will complete education at Clayfield College. College reserves right to request full repayment if expectations not fulfilled without valid cause. Students must maintain high standards in conduct and academic results.

overview: Through the generosity of an Old Collegian, a Swimming Scholarship is available for a student who shows exceptional talent within the sport of swimming. The scholarship recognises elite swimming ability and commitment to competitive excellence in the water.
description: A Swimming Scholarship is available for a student entering the College who demonstrates exceptional talent in competitive swimming. The scholarship is made possible through the generous donation of an Old Collegian and supports students with demonstrated elite swimming ability. Specific financial value, duration, and detailed selection criteria are not publicly specified on the college website. Interested applicants should contact the College's Enrolments Team for complete information about application requirements, selection process, and financial details.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated exceptional talent in competitive swimming; Likely requires evidence of high-level swimming achievement or potential
special_conditions: Funded by generous donation from Old Collegian. Specific application details, financial value and selection criteria should be obtained directly from Enrolments Team at enrol@clayfield.qld.edu.au or 07 3262 0220.

school_name: Columba Catholic College - St Mary's Campus
overview: Columba Catholic College offers the St. Columcille Bursary to provide financial assistance to eligible students and families. The bursary recognises students with demonstrated need and supports families in financial hardship to access quality Catholic education.
description: The St. Columcille Bursary is a means-tested financial assistance program offered by Columba Catholic College to support families experiencing financial hardship. The bursary is available across multiple categories including academic merit, rural and remote access, and boarding support. The program provides financial assistance in the form of fee reductions or partial fee remission to eligible applicants. Selection is based on application forms, financial assessment documentation, and specified criteria for each bursary category. The bursary is subject to annual review to ensure continued financial need and satisfactory academic progress, and recipients are expected to maintain good conduct and engagement with the school community.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; Demonstrated financial need; May include category-specific criteria for academic merit, rural/remote location, or boarding requirements; Good academic standing and satisfactory behaviour
special_conditions: Bursary awards are reviewed annually. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress and good conduct. Financial need must be reassessed each year. The bursary may be withdrawn or reduced if conditions are not met. Detailed categories and criteria are provided in the downloadable application documents.

school_name: Columba Catholic College - St Mary's Campus
program_name: Zonta Club of Charters Towers Bursary
overview: The Zonta Club of Charters Towers offers annual bursaries to female secondary students who demonstrate outstanding achievements and contribution to the community. Two bursaries valued at $750 each are awarded annually to female students from five secondary schools in Charters Towers.
description: The Zonta Club of Charters Towers Bursary is an external award program providing direct financial support to outstanding female students in the Charters Towers region. Two bursaries are awarded annually, each valued at $750, to female secondary school students who demonstrate exceptional achievement and meaningful community contribution. The funds are awarded directly to the recipient to be utilised for furthering her education and life goals. The bursary is available to students entering Year 11 or Year 12 in the coming academic year, with funds available in the year the bursary is awarded. Each individual student is eligible to receive this award once only.
eligibility_criteria: Female students only; Attending one of five secondary schools in Charters Towers; Entering Year 11 or Year 12 in the coming academic year; Demonstrated achievement and community contribution; Each student eligible once only
special_conditions: Bursary is awarded to female students only. Award is limited to one per eligible student. Funds are awarded directly to the recipient. Application forms available through the school. Award presented at Zonta Club Women Inspiring Women Luncheon.

overview: Columba Catholic College offers the St. Columcille Bursary to support students experiencing financial hardship. This bursary recognises the College's commitment to providing accessible Catholic education across multiple categories including academic, sporting, and cultural achievement.
description: The St. Columcille Bursary is available to students across the College's primary and secondary campuses. The bursary is offered in multiple categories to recognise excellence and support access: Academic Bursary for outstanding academic achievement, Sporting Bursary for exceptional sporting performance, and Cultural Bursary for cultural excellence. The application process involves submitting a detailed application form and supporting documentation. Recipients are selected based on demonstrating exceptional promise in their respective category alongside demonstrated need or merit. The bursary provides financial assistance towards school fees and operates as part of the College's holistic approach to making quality Catholic education accessible to families across North Queensland and beyond.
eligibility_criteria: Must be applying to or enrolled at Columba Catholic College; Application through official bursary application form; Different criteria apply for Academic, Sporting, and Cultural categories; Recipients demonstrate exceptional achievement in their respective category
special_conditions: Applications available for 2026 school year. Categories include Academic, Sporting, and Cultural bursaries. Specific criteria and application requirements detailed in downloadable St. Columcille Bursary Categories and Criteria documents.

overview: Columba Catholic College offers Academic, Sporting, and Cultural Bursaries to support students who demonstrate exceptional achievement in these areas. These bursaries are designed to reward excellence while supporting access to quality Catholic education for talented students.
description: The Academic, Sporting and Cultural Bursary program encompasses three distinct categories recognising different forms of student excellence. The Academic Bursary supports students demonstrating outstanding academic achievement. The Sporting Bursary recognises exceptional sporting talent and performance. The Cultural Bursary acknowledges excellence in cultural pursuits including music, performing arts, and visual arts. Applications are actively being accepted for 2026. Each category has specific selection criteria and demonstration requirements. The bursaries provide financial assistance with school fees to enable talented and high-achieving students to access the College's comprehensive educational offerings across its two campuses.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated excellence in Academic, Sporting, or Cultural pursuits; Enrolled or applying to enrol at Columba Catholic College; Application through official application process; Specific achievement thresholds apply based on category
special_conditions: Applications being accepted for 2026 school year. Three distinct categories: Academic, Sporting, and Cultural. Detailed information and application forms available on College website.

program_name: Zonta Club of Charters Towers Bursary
overview: The Zonta Club of Charters Towers offers annual bursaries to female students in secondary education. The bursary recognises and supports young women pursuing education in the region's secondary schools.
description: The Zonta Club of Charters Towers offers two bursaries annually, valued at $750 each, awarded to two female students attending one of the five secondary schools in Charters Towers, including Columba Catholic College. The funds are awarded directly to the recipient to be utilised for furthering her education and life goals. The bursary is awarded to a student entering either Year 11 or Year 12 in the coming year, with the funds available to the student in the year the bursary is awarded. The bursary will be awarded to an individual student only once in their secondary schooling. Application forms and more information are available through the College.
eligibility_criteria: Female student; Entering Year 11 or Year 12 in the coming year; Attending one of five secondary schools in Charters Towers; Candidate receives bursary once only; First-time Year 11 or Year 12 entrant
special_conditions: Two bursaries awarded annually. Funds provided directly to recipient for education/life goals. Awarded to student entering Year 11 or 12. Bursary awarded to individual once only. Available through five secondary schools including Columba Catholic College.

school_name: Concordia Lutheran College - Martin Luther Primary Campus
overview: Concordia Lutheran College offers Academic Scholarships to recognise exceptional students demonstrating outstanding academic ability. These scholarships provide remission of tuition fees and are available to new and existing students entering Year 7 and Year 10 commencing at the College.
description: Academic Scholarships providing remission of tuition fees are available to new and existing students commencing at Concordia Lutheran College in 2027. Students sitting the Academic Scholarship for Year 7 have scholarship tenure for Years 7–9. Following a successful review at the conclusion of Year 9, an application for continuation of the scholarship will be considered. For students sitting the scholarship for Year 10, the tenure is for Years 10–12. Scholarships will be reviewed each semester and are subject to continued excellence in academic performance and adherence to the College policies and procedures. Recipients will be required to meet high academic standards each semester and to participate in the co-curricular program offered by the College. Registration for 2027 Scholarships opens in late 2025.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new and existing students; Commencing Year 7 or Year 10; Demonstrated exceptional academic ability; Continued excellence in academic performance; Adherence to College policies and procedures; Active participation in College co-curricular program
special_conditions: For students sitting the Academic Scholarship for Year 7, tenure is for Years 7–9. Following a successful review at the conclusion of Year 9, an application for continuation will be considered. For Year 10 students, tenure is for Years 10–12. Scholarships are reviewed each semester and are subject to continued excellence in academic performance and adherence to College policies and procedures.

school_name: Concordia Lutheran College - Martin Luther Primary Campus
program_name: Head of College Scholarship – General Excellence
overview: Concordia Lutheran College offers Head of College Scholarships for General Excellence to recognise students demonstrating outstanding character and all-round achievement. This scholarship is available to students entering Years 7 to 12 and recognises qualities such as integrity, leadership, initiative and compassion.
description: The Head of College Scholarship for General Excellence is awarded to students demonstrating outstanding character and all-round achievement across academics, co-curricular involvement and community contribution. Recipients must demonstrate high standards of academic performance, active participation in college activities and commitment to the college community. The scholarship provides remission of tuition fees. Scholarship applications are subject to a competitive selection process involving assessment of academic performance, personal statement, school reports and interviews. Recipients must participate actively in college activities as determined by key college personnel and maintain adherence to college policies.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 to 12; Demonstrated exceptional academic ability; Outstanding character and integrity; Leadership qualities; Initiative and compassion; Active participation in college activities; Commitment to college community; Adherence to College policies and procedures
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Recipients must be active participants in College activities as determined by key College personnel. Scholarships are subject to annual review and continued adherence to College policies.

school_name: Concordia Lutheran College - Martin Luther Primary Campus
overview: Concordia Lutheran College offers Specialist Scholarships in Sport Excellence to recognise students demonstrating exceptional sporting ability and achievement. This scholarship is available to students entering Years 7 to 12 and supports athletic excellence within the college community.
description: The Specialist Scholarship for Sport Excellence is awarded to students demonstrating exceptional sporting ability, achievement and commitment. Recipients must provide evidence of high-level sporting performance, either through competition at state, national or international level or through demonstrated excellence within recognised sporting codes. The scholarship provides remission of tuition fees for eligible candidates. Selection involves assessment of sporting achievements and performance through portfolio submission, interview and evaluation by college personnel. Recipients must maintain academic standards and participate in College policies.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 to 12; Evidence of high-level sporting achievement; Competition at state, national or international level or excellence in recognised sporting codes; Demonstrated commitment to sports; Adherence to College policies and procedures
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Candidates will have evidence of high-level sporting achievement. Scholarships are subject to annual review and continued adherence to College policies and academic standards.

school_name: Concordia Lutheran College - Martin Luther Primary Campus
overview: Concordia Lutheran College offers Specialist Scholarships in Music to recognise students demonstrating exceptional musical ability and achievement. This scholarship is available to students entering Years 7 to 12 and supports excellence in instrumental or vocal music.
description: The Specialist Scholarship for Music is awarded to students demonstrating exceptional musical ability, achievement and commitment. Candidates may apply in the categories of Instrumental or Vocal music. Recipients must provide evidence of high-level musical achievement and performance through formal examinations, competition results and portfolio submission. The scholarship provides remission of tuition fees for eligible candidates. Selection involves assessment of musical achievements, audition performance and interview by college personnel. Recipients must maintain academic standards and participate in College co-curricular programs.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 to 12; Evidence of achievements and examinations at a high level in chosen music discipline; High-level musical ability in Instrumental or Vocal music; Demonstrated commitment to music; Adherence to College policies and procedures
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Candidates will have evidence of achievements and examinations at a high level in their chosen music discipline. Categories available: Instrumental or Vocal. Scholarships are subject to annual review and continued adherence to College policies.

school_name: Concordia Lutheran College - Martin Luther Primary Campus
overview: Concordia Lutheran College offers Specialist Scholarships in The Arts to recognise students demonstrating exceptional ability in Drama, Dance and Visual Arts. This scholarship is available to students entering Years 7 to 12 and supports excellence across the arts disciplines.
description: The Specialist Scholarship for The Arts is awarded to students demonstrating exceptional ability and achievement in Drama, Dance or Visual Arts disciplines. Candidates may apply in any of these arts categories. Recipients must provide evidence of high-level artistic achievement and performance through formal examinations, competition results and portfolio submission. The scholarship provides remission of tuition fees for eligible candidates. Selection involves assessment of artistic achievements, portfolio review and interview by college personnel. Recipients must maintain academic standards and participate in College co-curricular programs.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 to 12; Evidence of achievements and examinations at a high level in chosen arts discipline; High-level ability in Drama, Dance or Visual Arts; Demonstrated commitment to the arts; Adherence to College policies and procedures
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Candidates will have evidence of achievements and examinations at a high level in their chosen arts discipline. Categories available: Drama, Dance or Visual Arts. Scholarships are subject to annual review and continued adherence to College policies.

school_name: Concordia Lutheran College - Martin Luther Primary Campus
overview: Concordia Lutheran College offers Specialist Scholarships for Rural Boarding to recognise students from rural and regional locations who demonstrate commitment to their studies and extra-curricular activities. This scholarship supports boarding access for regional students.
description: The Specialist Scholarship for Rural Boarding is awarded to students based in rural or regional locations who are applying for a position as a boarding student at Concordia Lutheran College. Candidates must demonstrate commitment to their studies and active participation in extra-curricular activities. The scholarship provides remission of tuition fees for eligible candidates. Recipients must reside in a rural or regional location and commit to boarding at the college. Selection involves assessment of academic performance, commitment to studies and co-curricular activities. Recipients must maintain academic standards and boarding residency.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 to 12; Based in rural or regional location; Demonstrated commitment to studies; Active participation in extra-curricular activities; Applying for boarding position at Concordia Lutheran College; Adherence to College policies and procedures
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Candidates must be based in a rural or regional location and be applying for a position as a boarding student. Must demonstrate commitment to studies and extra-curricular activities. Scholarships are subject to annual review and continued adherence to College policies.

school_name: Concordia Lutheran College - Martin Luther Primary Campus
overview: Concordia Lutheran College offers Head of College Concessions to families experiencing financial difficulties who wish to enrol at the college. This financial assistance program is available on application for students in any year level.
description: The Head of College Concession is a financial assistance program available for families experiencing financial difficulties that may otherwise prevent enrolment at Concordia Lutheran College. Applications are considered on a case-by-case basis at the discretion of the Head of College. Recipients must undergo a confidential financial assessment. The concession provides partial tuition fee reduction or remission to eligible families. This program is available for current families and new applicants. Applications should be made directly to the Head of College Office to ensure confidential treatment of financial information.
eligibility_criteria: Experiencing financial difficulties; May otherwise be unable to afford college fees; Open to new and existing students; Willing to undertake confidential financial assessment; Applying to Head of College Office
special_conditions: Applications are considered on a case-by-case basis at the discretion of the Head of College. All financial information is treated confidentially. Contact the Head of College Office directly by calling 07 4688 2714 for confidential discussion.

school_name: Concordia Lutheran College - Concordia Primary Campus

overview: The Smith Family's Learning For Life Scholarship provides students with access to educational programs and long-term financial support to assist with education-related expenses. The scholarship is designed to support families experiencing financial disadvantage and demonstrates commitment to education. Recipients receive direct financial assistance to help with essential school costs.
description: The Smith Family's Learning For Life Scholarship provides financial support to students from low-income families. The scholarship offers two payments per annum totalling $420 per year, which can be used for education-related expenses such as uniforms, excursions, shoes, school photos, stationery, music lessons and sports fees. Families must be referred through an established referral pathway and meet all eligibility criteria. All students and their families work with an Education Support Worker who provides ongoing support and advice throughout the scholarship period. The scholarship is renewable annually subject to continued eligibility and commitment to education.
eligibility_criteria: Students from Prep to Year 6; Attendance over 80%; Family must have a current Health Care Card or Pension Concession Card; Demonstrate strong commitment to child's education; School referral required; Must be attending a non-fee paying school; Australian resident
special_conditions: Scholarships are based on financial need and demonstrated commitment to education. Families must maintain school attendance above 80%. Referral through school is required. All families work with a Learning For Life Education Support Worker. Scholarship is renewable annually subject to continued eligibility. Funds must be used for education-related expenses only.

overview: Coomera Anglican College offers Academic Scholarships to recognize outstanding achievement in Year 7 to Year 12 students. The scholarship is awarded to students who demonstrate exceptional academic performance and excel in the ACER scholarship examination.
description: Academic Scholarships are available for students entering Years 7 to 12 who demonstrate outstanding academic performance in their studies and excel in the Australian Council for Educational Research (ACER) scholarship examination. Applicants must complete a cover letter and two-page resume/CV. The exact financial benefit varies based on the number and quality of applicants in each year and is determined at the discretion of the College Council. Historically, scholarship discounts have ranged between 25-50% of tuition fees. Scholarships remain current to the end of Year 9 (for Year 7-9 entrants) or to the end of Year 12 (for Year 10-12 entrants), subject to annual review and satisfactory academic performance, involvement, and behaviour.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, or Year 12; Must demonstrate outstanding academic performance; Must excel in ACER scholarship examination; No disciplinary issues; Must maintain satisfactory academic performance for retention
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Retention of a Scholarship is subject to annual review and satisfactory levels of performance, involvement and behaviour by the recipient. College does not traditionally offer full-tuition scholarships. Applicants must have a standard application to attend the College submitted separately.

overview: Coomera Anglican College offers Sports Scholarships to recognize excellence and commitment to sporting endeavour. The scholarship is designed for students entering Year 7 to Year 12 who demonstrate high-level participation in sports, typically at regional, state, or national representation level.
description: Sports Scholarships are available for students entering Years 7 to 12 who show excellence and commitment to sporting endeavour. That commitment is typically demonstrated through higher-level participation, such as Regional, State, or National representation. Applicants will not be required to sit the ACER Test but must submit a Cover Letter, a two-page resume/CV, and two written references from Coaches as part of their application. The exact financial benefit varies based on the number and quality of applicants and is determined at the discretion of the College Council. Historically, scholarship discounts have ranged between 25-50% of tuition fees. Scholarships remain current to the end of Year 9 (for Year 7-9 entrants) or to the end of Year 12 (for Year 10-12 entrants).
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, or Year 12; Must demonstrate excellence in sports; Must show commitment through higher-level participation (Regional, State, or National representation); Must provide two written references from Coaches; Must maintain satisfactory conduct and athletic performance for retention
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Retention of a Scholarship is subject to annual review and satisfactory levels of performance, involvement and behaviour by the recipient. College does not traditionally offer full-tuition scholarships. Two written references from Coaches are required as part of the application.

overview: Coomera Anglican College offers Music Scholarships to recognize excellence and commitment to musical endeavour. The scholarship is designed for students entering Year 7 to Year 12 who demonstrate high standards of musical ability and commitment to music performance.
description: Music Scholarships are available for students entering Years 7 to 12 who show excellence and commitment to musical endeavour. That commitment is typically demonstrated through reports, performances, and an audition with the College. Students are expected to be of a medium to high standard of playing ability, which equates to approximately Grade 4 Music Examination standard and above. Students do not have to show that they have completed an examination but need to be playing or singing at or beyond this standard. Applicants must submit a Cover Letter, a two-page resume/CV, and attend an audition with the College's Head of Music. The exact financial benefit varies based on the number and quality of applicants and is determined at the discretion of the College Council. Historically, scholarship discounts have ranged between 25-50% of tuition fees.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, or Year 12; Must demonstrate excellence in music with playing/singing ability at approximately Grade 4 Music Examination standard or above; Must demonstrate ability to read music and answer questions from sheet music of performed pieces; Must attend audition with College Head of Music; Must maintain satisfactory musical engagement and conduct for retention
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Retention of a Scholarship is subject to annual review and satisfactory levels of performance, involvement and behaviour by the recipient. College does not traditionally offer full-tuition scholarships. Applicants must attend an audition with the College's Head of Music. Shortlisted applicants will be contacted to arrange an audition.

overview: Coomera Anglican College offers a Year 10 Service and Leadership Scholarship for students entering Year 10 in 2027 who demonstrate outstanding commitment to their community. This scholarship may be issued as a full academic scholarship and recognizes excellence in service and community engagement.
description: Year 10 2027 Service and Leadership Scholarships are available for students entering Year 10 in 2027, who show outstanding commitment to their community through different avenues. This scholarship may be issued as a full academic scholarship. Applicants will be required to sit the ACER Test and submit a Cover Letter and a two-page resume/CV. Once the College has received the examination results, shortlisted applicants will be contacted to arrange an interview with the Principal. The discount amount, length, and number of scholarships offered depend on the number and quality of applicants in each year, with the final decision remaining at the discretion of the College Council. Retention is subject to annual review and satisfactory levels of performance, involvement, and behaviour.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 10; Must demonstrate outstanding commitment to community service and leadership; Must complete ACER Test; Must attend interview with Principal; Must maintain satisfactory academic performance and community engagement for retention
special_conditions: This scholarship may be issued as a full academic scholarship. Retention of a Scholarship is subject to annual review and satisfactory levels of performance, involvement and behaviour by the recipient. Applicants must complete ACER Test and attend Principal interview. College does not traditionally offer full-tuition scholarships as standard but this program indicates full scholarship possibility.

overview: The Coomera Anglican College Foundation Scholarship Fund provides financial assistance to support students experiencing genuine financial hardship. The program offers scholarships to both prospective meritorious students from low-income families and current enrolled students facing financial difficulties.
description: The Coomera Anglican College Scholarship Fund was established to create opportunities for all students to study at Coomera Anglican College and reach their full potential. The Fund supports two groups: meritorious students from low income or disadvantaged families who wish to enter from Year 7 onwards to complete their Junior Secondary and Secondary schooling, and current enrolled students who require full or part scholarships to continue their education despite genuine financial hardship. The program also offers senior secondary students opportunities for extended learning, personal challenge, and cultural growth. The specific financial benefit varies based on individual circumstances and assessed need. Applications and assistance are arranged through the College Foundation office.
eligibility_criteria: For prospective students: Demonstrated merit; Demonstrated financial need or disadvantaged background; Entry from Year 7; For current students: Currently enrolled; Experiencing genuine financial hardship; Demonstrated commitment to studies
special_conditions: Applications are handled on a case-by-case basis through the College Foundation. Full or part scholarships are available depending on individual circumstances and assessed need. Contact foundation@cac.qld.edu.au for inquiries.

overview: Coombabah State High School offers the Ignite Academic Program of Excellence to students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability across core subjects. The program recognises students who excel in Mathematics, Science, English and Humanities and provides extension opportunities through specialist teaching and inquiry-based learning.
description: The Ignite Academic Program of Excellence is designed for all-round students who excel in Mathematics, Science, English and Humanities or a combination of these subjects. Emphasis is placed on the acquisition of knowledge, the development of higher order thinking skills and the formation of effective work habits. Students enrolled in this program are provided with extension opportunities and will experience an inquiry-based learning approach designed to facilitate creativity, curiosity and deeper thinking. This will contribute to success in senior studies, tertiary education and beyond. Successful Academic Excellence students will receive an early offer. Students benefit from specialist teachers, a competitive edge for their learning, and excellent foundations for ATAR subjects and tertiary pathways. Access to scholarships is available through the excellence program support structure.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7-10; Must demonstrate minimum 2 As and 2 Bs in core subjects; Provide current report card as evidence; Open to all genders; Located within or able to access school catchment area
special_conditions: Places in Programs of Excellence are subject to available student enrolment capacity. Enrolment criteria and specific application dates must be obtained from the school. Priority is given to in-catchment students before out-of-catchment enrolments are considered.

program_name: Ignite Visual Arts Program of Excellence
overview: Coombabah State High School offers the Ignite Visual Arts Program of Excellence for aspiring and talented artists. The program partners with local artists to develop student abilities across a range of visual arts dimensions in a unique course with streamed classes.
description: The Ignite Visual Arts Program of Excellence is a unique course for aspiring or talented artists. In conjunction with a streamed class, expert teachers partner with local artists to develop student abilities across a range of visual arts dimensions. Students will create a folio of work that demonstrates their skill level. The folio can be used when applying for employment and/or tertiary courses. Student work will be exhibited in public displays. The program runs from Year 9 onwards and provides access to scholarships through the excellence program support structure. Students benefit from working with industry professionals, creating professional-quality portfolios, and experiencing public exhibition opportunities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 7-10; Demonstrates artistic talent and ability; Provide portfolio or evidence of visual arts achievement; Open to all genders; Participate in trials or workshops as required
special_conditions: Enrolment limited to 100 places. Places subject to available capacity and in-catchment demand. Application process involves trials or workshops. Specific application dates and criteria available from the school.

program_name: Ignite Performing Arts Program of Excellence
overview: Coombabah State High School offers the Ignite Performing Arts Program of Excellence for students with talent in drama, dance and performance arts. The program provides tailored curriculum and specialist teaching to develop performance skills across various styles.
description: The Ignite Performing Arts Program of Excellence offers tailored curriculum and guidance by specialist teachers for students excelling in performing arts. The iGniTe Dance Program immerses aspiring dancers in an enriching experience that hones their skills across various performance styles including Hip-hop, Jazz, contemporary, and lyrical dance. Participants gain a versatile foundation through engaging workshops, performances, and collaborative projects while working with experienced instructors and industry professionals. The program provides access to scholarships and numerous co-curricular opportunities. Students benefit from expert teaching, performance opportunities, mentoring from peers, and a competitive edge for tertiary pathways in performing arts.
