eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 7-10; Demonstrates performing arts talent in dance, drama or performance; Participate in auditions, trials or workshops; Open to all genders; Evidence of performance experience or training preferred
special_conditions: Enrolment limited to 100 places. Applicants must participate in auditions, trials or workshops. Places subject to available capacity and in-catchment demand. Specific application dates and audition requirements available from the school.

program_name: Ignite Sport and Exercise Science Program of Excellence
overview: Coombabah State High School offers the Ignite Sport and Exercise Science Program of Excellence for students pursuing careers in sport, health and exercise science. The program recognises students with elite sporting abilities and commitment to health and exercise science pathways.
description: The Ignite Sport and Exercise Science Program of Excellence is dedicated to cultivating passionate sports enthusiasts, with special emphasis on elite athlete development and careers in health and exercise science. The program aims to produce graduates equipped with the skills and mindset inspired by athletes to make a positive impact on professional sport, health and exercise science careers. Students experience evidence-based training programs and systematic support aimed at fostering connections between performance, health and exercise science. The program provides access to scholarships and mentoring. Students benefit from cutting-edge facilities, expert coaching, industry partnerships, work experience opportunities, and tertiary pathway support.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 7-10; Demonstrates elite sporting ability or commitment to health and exercise science; Participate in trials or assessments; Open to all genders; Strong academic performance in relevant subjects
special_conditions: Enrolment limited to 100 places. Places subject to available capacity and in-catchment demand. Applicants must participate in trials or workshops. Specific application dates and selection criteria available from the school.

program_name: High Performance Sports Academy Program of Excellence
overview: Coombabah State High School offers the High Performance Sports Academy Program of Excellence for elite athletes in Years 7-12. The program recognises students who demonstrate exceptional sporting ability and commitment to high-level athletic development.
description: The High Performance Sports Academy Program of Excellence at Coombabah State High School is designed for elite athletes wanting to specialise in sport at professional levels. The program focuses on developing knowledge and skills to take performance to the next level across a range of sports. Students participate in training sessions with industry experts, have dedicated sports classes timetabled, compete in pre-season trials and seasonal games at various venues, and receive ongoing mentoring and pastoral support. The program includes opportunities for work experience and pre-tertiary courses. Students benefit from expert coaching, competitive opportunities, mentoring, and a competitive edge for professional athletic pathways. Access to scholarships is available through the excellence program support structure.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 7-12; Demonstrates elite sporting ability; Exemplary discipline, behaviour, academic rigour, attendance and attitude required; Participate in trials or assessments; Open to all genders
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Enrolment limited to 144 places. All students enrolled in the course must have exemplary discipline, behaviour, academic rigour, attendance and attitude. Places subject to available capacity and in-catchment demand. Specific application dates and selection criteria available from the school.

overview: Coombabah State High School offers the Rugby League Program of Excellence for students in Years 7-12 wanting to specialise in rugby league at elite levels. The program recognises students with exceptional rugby league ability and commitment to the sport professionally.
description: The Rugby League Program of Excellence has been designed for athletes wanting to specialise in Rugby League at elite levels or pursue a career in the Rugby League on or off the field. In this course, students focus their knowledge and skills to take their performance to the next level. Students participate in a range of learning experiences in the fields of coaching, officiating, strength and conditioning, first aid, nutrition and a variety of other industry related areas to prepare them for success. Coombabah SHS works in partnership with the Gold Coast Titans to provide specialist skills lessons. Students also work with highly experienced specialist staff who coach and mentor students to excel. Students benefit from guest speakers in the sporting and rugby league industries. The program provides access to scholarships. Many alumni have progressed to NRL careers.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 7-12; Demonstrates elite rugby league ability; Exemplary discipline, behaviour, academic rigour, attendance and attitude required; Participate in trials; Open to all genders
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Enrolment limited to 140 places. All students enrolled in the course must have exemplary discipline, behaviour, academic rigour, attendance and attitude. Places subject to available capacity and in-catchment demand. Partnership with Gold Coast Titans provides specialist skills lessons. Specific application dates and selection criteria available from the school.

overview: Coombabah State High School offers the COOEE Surf Program of Excellence for elite surfing athletes in Years 11-12. The program recognises students who demonstrate exceptional surfing ability and commitment to competitive surfing at high levels.
description: The COOEE Surf Program of Excellence is a selective entry program for elite surfers in Years 11-12 wanting to pursue competitive surfing at high levels. The program provides tailored curriculum supporting both academic studies and competitive surfing development. Students participate in training sessions with experienced coaches and mentors, competitions at various venues, and receive ongoing pastoral support to balance their commitments in a supportive educational environment. The program provides access to scholarships through the excellence program support structure. Students benefit from expert coaching, competitive opportunities, mentoring from peers and specialist teachers, and support for balancing academic and athletic demands.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 11-12; Demonstrates elite surfing ability; Exemplary discipline, behaviour, academic rigour, attendance and attitude required; Participate in trials or assessments; Open to all genders
special_conditions: Enrolment limited to 40 places. All students must have exemplary discipline, behaviour, academic rigour, attendance and attitude. Places subject to available capacity and in-catchment demand. Year 11-12 specialisation only. Specific application dates and selection criteria available from the school.

program_name: ALPHA Academy (Academic Learning Program for High Achievers)
overview: Coolum State High School offers the ALPHA Academy to extend academically talented students and allow them the opportunity to grow, creating a culture of excellence as they develop into independent learners. The program is designed for students entering Year 7 who demonstrate exceptional academic ability.
description: ALPHA classes are designed to extend academically talented students and allow them the opportunity to grow, creating a culture of excellence as they develop into an independent learner. The ALPHA students are supported to cultivate a base understanding of curriculum more quickly and autonomously than core Junior Secondary classes. This allows the class to spend more time in relating knowledge gained to their own context, build critical thought processes and apply new knowledge to other areas. ALPHA empowers students to be critical and creative thinkers who use reason, logic, resourcefulness, imagination and innovation in both teacher facilitated and collaborative inquiry based learning activities. The ALPHA classes provide an environment where similar minded learners establish positive relationships, working effectively in teams, handling challenging situations constructively and developing leadership skills to build and enhance skills of self-regulation. There is no financial cost associated with this selective program as it is offered within the government school system. The program operates for the duration of the student's enrolment at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate exceptional academic ability; Require shortlisted completion of Stage 1 written application; Selected applicants proceed to Stage 2 with aptitude and problem-solving examination; Selection based on demonstrated academic excellence and critical thinking ability
special_conditions: Closing Date for Applications: Friday 23 May 2025. Shortlisted students will participate in an aptitude/problem solving examination at Stage 2. Successful applicants will be notified and proceed through the selection process. Parents and students will be notified of the outcome of the application process.

overview: Coolum State High School offers a Basketball Academy Program as part of its specialist Academies designed to broaden and enhance the learning experiences of students who demonstrate excellence in basketball. The program provides specialist tuition and experiences for talented basketball players.
description: The Basketball Academy is one of Coolum State High School's specialist Academies developed to provide further opportunities to excel. The Academy offers students the opportunity to experience events, competitions and excursions. Every participating student must be an exemplar to the entire schooling body in regards to their very high attendance record, exceptional behaviour, commitment to academic success and the school's values of Care, Respect and Excellence. There is no direct scholarship or financial award associated with this program, as it operates within the government school system. However, students gain access to specialist coaching, training facilities, and competitive opportunities. The program has led to students achieving regional, state and national honours.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students demonstrating excellence in basketball; Must maintain very high attendance record; Must demonstrate exceptional behaviour; Must show commitment to academic success; Must uphold school values of Care, Respect and Excellence; No specific year level restriction stated
special_conditions: Every participating student must be an exemplar to the entire schooling body in regards to their very high attendance record, exceptional behaviour, commitment to academic success and the school's values of Care, Respect and Excellence.

overview: Coolum State High School offers an Instrumental Music Academy Program as part of its specialist Academies. The program provides specialist tuition and experiences for talented musicians to enhance their musical development and performance capabilities.
description: The Instrumental Music Academy is one of Coolum State High School's specialist Academies developed to provide further opportunities to excel in music. The Academy offers students the opportunity to experience events, competitions and excursions. Every participating student must be an exemplar to the entire schooling body in regards to their very high attendance record, exceptional behaviour, commitment to academic success and the school's values of Care, Respect and Excellence. There is no direct scholarship or financial award associated with this program, as it operates within the government school system. However, students gain access to specialist music instruction, state-of-the-art facilities including the Gagal Gira Instrumental Centre with music recording studio and soundproof rehearsal spaces. The program has led to students achieving regional, state and national honours.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students demonstrating excellence in instrumental music; Must maintain very high attendance record; Must demonstrate exceptional behaviour; Must show commitment to academic success; Must uphold school values of Care, Respect and Excellence; No specific year level restriction stated
special_conditions: Every participating student must be an exemplar to the entire schooling body in regards to their very high attendance record, exceptional behaviour, commitment to academic success and the school's values of Care, Respect and Excellence.

overview: Coolum State High School offers an award-winning Surfing Academy Program as part of its specialist Academies. The program provides exceptional training opportunities with highly experienced and nationally accredited surfing coaches for students demonstrating elite surfing ability.
description: Against the backdrop of the beautiful, unspoiled beaches of Coolum, is the award winning Surfing Academy Program. With graduates such as Isabella Nichols, the 2016 Junior World Champion, this renowned program offers students exceptional training opportunities with highly experienced and nationally accredited surfing coaches. Every participating student must be an exemplar to the entire schooling body in regards to their very high attendance record, exceptional behaviour, commitment to academic success and the school's values of Care, Respect and Excellence. There is no direct scholarship or financial award associated with this program, as it operates within the government school system. However, students gain access to specialist coaching from elite coaches and competitive opportunities. The program has led to students achieving regional, state and national honours and recognition on the world stage.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students demonstrating excellence in surfing; Must maintain very high attendance record; Must demonstrate exceptional behaviour; Must show commitment to academic success; Must uphold school values of Care, Respect and Excellence; Preference for experienced surfers; International students can apply
special_conditions: Every participating student must be an exemplar to the entire schooling body in regards to their very high attendance record, exceptional behaviour, commitment to academic success and the school's values of Care, Respect and Excellence. The school has produced elite surfers including Isabella Nichols, 2016 Junior World Champion.

overview: Coolum State High School offers a Cheerleading Academy Program as part of its specialist Academies. The program is based on commitment, passion and teamwork and includes energetic exercises and training sessions designed to develop athletic, dance and performance skills.
description: The Cheerleading Academy's program is based on commitment, passion and teamwork and includes a series of energetic exercises and training sessions designed to develop the athletic skills of the participants, including strength and stability as well as dance and performance skills. These skills can be demonstrated in cheerleader excursions and state school competitions. Every participating student must be an exemplar to the entire schooling body in regards to their very high attendance record, exceptional behaviour, commitment to academic success and the school's values of Care, Respect and Excellence. There is no direct scholarship or financial award associated with this program, as it operates within the government school system. However, students gain access to specialist coaching and competitive opportunities. The program has led to students achieving regional, state and national honours.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students demonstrating excellence in cheerleading; Must maintain very high attendance record; Must demonstrate exceptional behaviour; Must show commitment to academic success; Must uphold school values of Care, Respect and Excellence; No specific year level restriction stated
special_conditions: Every participating student must be an exemplar to the entire schooling body in regards to their very high attendance record, exceptional behaviour, commitment to academic success and the school's values of Care, Respect and Excellence.

overview: Coolum State High School offers a Touch Football Academy Program as part of its specialist Academies designed to broaden and enhance the learning experiences of students who demonstrate excellence in touch football.
description: The Touch Football Academy is one of Coolum State High School's specialist Academies developed to provide further opportunities to excel. overview: Coolum State High School offers a Young Entrepreneurs Academy Program as part of its specialist Academies designed to develop entrepreneurial thinking and business skills in students who demonstrate aptitude and leadership potential.
description: The Young Entrepreneurs Academy is one of Coolum State High School's specialist Academies developed to provide further opportunities to excel. school_name: Coorparoo Centre for Continuing Secondary Education

overview: Coorparoo Secondary College offers Academic Scholarships to students demonstrating academic excellence entering Year 7. The scholarship recognises students who show evidence of exceptional achievement in their academic field and supports their continued learning at the college.
description: Academic scholarships are available for students entering Year 7 who demonstrate evidence of excellence in academics. The scholarship value is $300, used toward school resources and uniforms. Selection is based on demonstrated academic achievement and excellence in the student's field. These scholarships are offered to recognise academic merit and excellence, with the value applied toward school resources and uniforms for successful recipients.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate evidence of academic excellence; Current Year 6 students eligible to apply

overview: Coorparoo Secondary College offers Sports Scholarships to students demonstrating sporting excellence entering Year 7. The scholarship is awarded to recognise students who show evidence of exceptional achievement in sporting pursuits and commitment to athletic development.
description: Sports scholarships are available for students entering Year 7 who demonstrate evidence of excellence in sporting fields. The scholarship value is $300, used toward school resources and uniforms. Selection is based on demonstrated sporting achievement and excellence in the student's athletic field. The college offers a wide variety of intra and interschool sporting opportunities including Swimming, Cross Country, Athletics, Hockey, Basketball, Netball, Touch Football, Volleyball, Soccer and Badminton.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate evidence of sporting excellence; Current Year 6 students eligible to apply

overview: Coorparoo Secondary College offers Cultural Scholarships for students demonstrating excellence in music, dance, and visual arts entering Year 7. The scholarship recognises students who show evidence of exceptional achievement in cultural and artistic pursuits.
description: Cultural scholarships are available in the fields of music, dance, and visual arts for students entering Year 7 who demonstrate evidence of excellence in their chosen cultural discipline. The scholarship value is $300, used toward school resources and uniforms. Selection is based on demonstrated excellence in music, dance, or visual arts. The college provides extracurricular opportunities in Music, Dance Troupes, and Vocal Groups, supporting student development in the cultural and performing arts.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate evidence of excellence in music, dance, or visual arts; Current Year 6 students eligible to apply

overview: Craigslea State High School offers the Craigslea Volleyball Academy (CVA), a selective entry program of excellence in volleyball for students seeking to challenge themselves at the highest level. The program provides athletes with opportunities to compete at state and national level, with direct links to Volleyball Queensland, Queensland Academy of Sport, and Volleyball Australia.
description: The Craigslea Volleyball Academy is a selective entry program that aims to create unique learning and growth opportunities for capable students seeking to excel in volleyball. The program has capacity for 180 students across Year 7 to Year 12. It is expected that students who enter the program will maintain high achievement levels in CVA classes and display suitable behaviour and effort not only within the CVA but in all subjects. Students will also be required to attend extra-curricular CVA sessions and tournaments. The program provides more than a volleyball focus - it emphasises personal development, interpersonal and life skills, and collaboration in pursuit of individual and team goals. The school reserves the right to withdraw students from the program at any time. As a non-fee-paying government school, no tuition costs are associated with the program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 through Year 12; Must demonstrate volleyball ability and potential; Must maintain high achievement levels in CVA curriculum and suitable behaviour and effort in all subjects; Must attend extra-curricular CVA sessions and tournaments; Selective entry through application and trials process; Available to in-catchment students first, then out-of-catchment students if places remain
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Students must be placed on a waitlist and are not guaranteed enrolment until a position is formally offered. Out-of-catchment students applying for the program must also complete an Out of Catchment Expression of Interest Form. Acceptance into the program does not guarantee automatic enrolment for siblings. All CVA curriculum staff are Level 3 'Elite Coach' qualified. Volleyball trials take place shortly after online application closes. The school reserves the right to withdraw students from the program at any time.

overview: Craigslea State High School offers the Signature Music Program, a selective entry program for students who demonstrate excellence in music ability. The program is designed for students with strong interest and high achievement in music who wish to extend their learning in this specialist area.
description: The Signature Music Program is a specialist class for students who demonstrate excellence in music ability, offered as an invitation-only subject to students who meet selection criteria. The program has capacity for 50 students. Students must be members of the Instrumental Music Program and/or Vocal Program to apply. Selection includes completion of an online application with a two-minute audition video showcasing musical talent, two scales, and one prepared piece of music. Applicants must demonstrate consistently high academic results, a willingness to go beyond potential, compliance with school expectations regarding effort, behaviour and attendance, and enrolment in at least one vocal or instrumental extra-curricular ensemble. As a non-fee-paying government school, no tuition costs are associated with the program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 through Year 12; Must be enrolled in Instrumental Music Program and/or Vocal Lessons; Demonstrated excellence in music ability; Consistently high academic results and willingness to go beyond potential; Compliance with school expectations regarding effort, behaviour and attendance; Enrolment in at least one vocal or instrumental extra-curricular ensemble; Selective entry through audition and online application; Available to in-catchment students first, then out-of-catchment students if places remain
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Students must submit a two-minute audition video that is well-lit with clear audio and video quality. The video must include two scales demonstrating proficiency and understanding of musical theory, plus one prepared piece of music. Students must be placed on a waitlist and are not guaranteed enrolment until a position is formally offered. Out-of-catchment students must also complete an Out of Catchment Expression of Interest Form. It is envisioned that students continue with music studies in Years 11 and 12, with some choosing Music Extension in Year 12 which contributes to ATAR calculation.

overview: Craigslea State High School offers the Excellence in Science Program, a selective entry program designed to inspire and provide greater opportunities for high-achieving students in Years 7 to 9. The three-year program allows capable students to learn together in core science classes where they are nurtured and challenged.
description: The Excellence in Science Program is a three-year selective entry program for high-achieving students in Years 7-9 with capacity for 78 students. Students in this program study the Australian Curriculum for Science and experience learning above and beyond this curriculum framework. Teachers are senior science staff from specialist science areas who allow students to cover core content at a faster pace, creating time for deeper understanding of concepts, exploration of other topics and enrichment activities such as Queensland University of Technology (QUT) programs and World Science Festival presentations. Year 7 students undertake hands-on STEM workshops at QUT Gardens Point campus, while Year 8 students attend World Science Festival presentations in March and engage with industry professionals. It is expected that students who enter the program will maintain high academic achievement and display suitable behaviour and effort at all times. Students must also provide identified resources. As a non-fee-paying government school, no tuition costs are associated with the program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 through Year 9; High academic achievement in science, English and mathematics; Demonstrated ability to maintain high academic achievement; Must display suitable behaviour and effort at all times; Must provide identified resources; Selective entry through application process; Available to in-catchment students first, then out-of-catchment students if places remain; Students who do not continue may study Science Extension in Year 10 if meeting criteria
special_conditions: This is a three-year program running from Year 7 to Year 9. Participation is by invitation only and is marked as such in the school booklist. The school reserves the right to withdraw students from the program at any time if they do not maintain high academic achievement or suitable behaviour and effort. Students must be placed on a waitlist and are not guaranteed enrolment until a position is formally offered. Out-of-catchment students must also complete an Out of Catchment Expression of Interest Form. High-performing Year 9 students with strong interest and achievement in Science, English and Mathematics may study Science Extension in Year 10 Semester 1.

overview: Dakabin State School operates the POWER Positive Learning Centre, a specialist support program. This centre helps children with challenging behaviours learn to fit back into regular classrooms through a structured reintegration program.
description: The POWER Positive Learning Centre is a specialized facility within Dakabin State School designed to support students who experience behavioral challenges. The program provides targeted intervention and skill-building to help students develop strategies for managing their behavior and successfully reintegrate into mainstream classroom settings. Students work with trained staff in a supportive environment that emphasizes positive learning strategies and personal growth. The program is no-cost to families as it is funded through Queensland government education funding. Eligibility is determined through referral and assessment by school leadership and the Department of Education.
eligibility_criteria: Referral from school leadership; Assessment by Department of Education; Behavioral support needs; Currently enrolled or seeking enrollment at Dakabin State School
special_conditions: Participation based on referral and assessment. Program focus is on skill development and successful reintegration into regular classrooms. No application fee as this is a government-funded program.

overview: Dakabin State School offers a Special Education Program to support students with identified disabilities and special educational needs. The program provides targeted support services and individualized learning plans to help students achieve their educational goals.
description: The Special Education Program at Dakabin State School is designed to support students with disabilities and special educational needs. Around 55 students participate in this program, which provides specialized teaching, support services, and individualized education plans tailored to each student's needs. The program is integrated within the mainstream school setting, allowing students to participate in school life while receiving additional support from specially trained staff. Services may include speech pathology, occupational therapy, and behavioral support. The program is fully funded through Queensland government education allocations with no cost to families.
eligibility_criteria: Identified disability or special educational need; Educational assessment and review; Recommendation from Department of Education; Parent/guardian consent
special_conditions: Requires formal assessment and recommendation from the Department of Education. Support services vary depending on individual student needs. HOSES (Home and Online Support for Enrolment Services) discussions occur during the enrolment process for students with disabilities.

overview: Dalby Christian College offers an Indigenous Scholarship Programme to support Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students seeking quality Christian education. The programme recognises and supports Indigenous students in achieving their educational potential.
description: Dalby Christian College offers an Indigenous Scholarship Programme designed to provide financial support for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. The programme aims to increase access to Christian education for Indigenous families. Specific financial benefits, selection criteria, and application requirements are not detailed on the publicly available school website. Interested families should contact the College directly at (07) 4672 4222 to discuss eligibility requirements, application processes, and the level of financial assistance available.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage; Contact school for specific criteria
special_conditions: Programme details should be confirmed directly with the College. The school has 8% Indigenous student enrollment as of 2023.

overview: Dalby Christian College provides sibling discounts on tuition fees to reduce the financial burden on families with multiple children enrolled. The discount structure rewards families enrolling more than one child in the school.
description: Dalby Christian College offers a structured sibling discount programme on tuition fees to support families with multiple children. The second child in a family receives $400 discount on Primary tuition and $600 discount on Secondary tuition fees. The third and all subsequent children in a family receive full tuition fee remission, meaning no charge for tuition fees. This programme applies to all year levels and both Primary and Secondary students. The discount does not apply to Early Learning Centre (ELC) or PrePrep fees. Families can claim these discounts automatically when enrolling multiple children.
eligibility_criteria: Must have one or more siblings already enrolled at the school; Second child in family; Third and subsequent children in same family
special_conditions: Sibling discount does not apply to ELC or PrePrep fees. Second student receives $400 discount on Primary tuition or $600 discount on Secondary tuition. Third and subsequent students receive full tuition fee remission.

overview: Dalby Christian College recognises that families may experience financial hardship and offers a means-tested concession scheme to eligible families. The scheme provides fee reductions based on demonstrated financial need.
description: Dalby Christian College operates a means-tested concession scheme designed to support families experiencing financial hardship. The school recognises the financial sacrifices that families make to provide an independent Christian education and balances the needs of individual families with the needs of the school. Families can apply for fee reductions based on demonstrated financial need through this scheme. The application process typically involves completing a financial assessment form. Eligibility and the amount of fee reduction are determined based on the family's financial circumstances. Interested families should contact the College Administration Office directly to request application forms and discuss their situation confidentially.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated financial hardship; Means-tested assessment required; Contact school for application process
special_conditions: Assessment is means-tested and confidential. Applications can be made at any time. Contact College Administration Office for forms and eligibility discussion.

overview: Dalby Christian College offers an early payment discount to families who pay their tuition fees in full by a specified early payment date. This discount rewards families who can pay fees upfront.
description: Dalby Christian College provides a $150 discount per fee-paying student when tuition fees are paid in full by February 7 each year. This early payment discount is available to all families and provides a financial incentive for early settlement of fees. The discount applies per student enrolled, so families with multiple children receive $150 per child when all fees are paid by the deadline. This is in addition to any sibling discounts or concession scheme reductions that may apply. Families should note the annual February 7 deadline and contact the College Administration Office for payment details and banking information.
eligibility_criteria: Full tuition fees paid by February 7 of the school year
special_conditions: Discount applies per fee-paying student. Deadline is February 7 each year. Discount is in addition to other fee reductions or concessions.

school_name: Dalby State High School - Bunya Campus
overview: Dalby State High School offers Arrow Energy Agricultural Scholarships to support students undertaking agricultural study at Bunya Campus. These scholarships recognise students from farming or rural backgrounds and assist with boarding costs to enable them to pursue agricultural education and careers.
description: Arrow Energy Agricultural Scholarships cover a portion of the boarding fees for successful applicants undertaking agricultural programs and/or electives at Dalby State High School's Bunya Campus residential facility. Each scholarship is valued at $5,000 for each recipient. Scholarships are available across most year levels with up to 10 awards distributed annually (typically 1 each in Years 7, 8 and 9; 2 in Year 10 and 11; and 3 in Year 12, or another combination deemed appropriate by Arrow Energy). Selection is based on application materials including written responses, community contributions, agricultural involvement, and references from non-family members. Arrow Energy and Dalby State High School have partnered for over 10 years to provide these scholarships to students from farming or rural backgrounds, with preference given to applicants residing in the regional area.
eligibility_criteria: Currently studying or planning to study agricultural programs and/or electives at Dalby State High School; undertaking agricultural extra-curricular activities; preference given to students from farming or rural backgrounds; eligible regardless of region; open to Years 7 to 12; must be boarding at Bunya Campus or planning to board
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered to assist with boarding fees for residential students. Recipients must continue to undertake agricultural study or be involved in agricultural extra-curricular activities. Scholarship allocation per year level may vary as determined by the assessment panel. For 2025 applications have closed; applicants should contact Bunya Campus for future application timelines.

school_name: Dalby State High School - Bunya Campus
program_name: Matt Hughes Foundation Senior Secondary Bursary
overview: Dalby State High School offers the Matt Hughes Foundation Senior Secondary Bursary to support Year 11 and 12 students facing challenges and adversity. The bursary honours the legacy of local builder and football identity Matt Hughes and provides financial assistance to students with demonstrated need and commitment to their education.
description: The Matt Hughes Foundation Senior Secondary Bursary provides $1,000 annually to Year 11 and 12 students at Dalby State High School who are facing challenges, adversity and difficulty and demonstrate a plan to engage fully in their senior secondary learning. Matt Hughes was a former Dalby builder and football identity who passed away from cancer in 2019 aged 39. His legacy is honoured through the Matt Hughes Foundation, which fundraises through the annual Matt Hughes Charity Shield, a joint initiative of Dalby Diehards Rugby League Football Club and Dalby & District Rugby Football Club. Applications open to Year 10 students during Term 4 for entry to Year 11 the following year. The selection process considers demonstrated need, personal circumstances, and commitment to completing senior secondary education.
eligibility_criteria: Year 10 students applying for Year 11 entry, or Year 11 and 12 students; facing challenges, adversity or difficulty; demonstrated plan to engage fully in senior secondary learning; enrolled at Dalby State High School
special_conditions: Applications open to Year 10 students during Term 4 for the following year. The scholarship recognises students facing challenges and adversity who are committed to completing their senior secondary education. Award is intended as financial assistance for eligible students in Years 11 and 12.

school_name: Dalby State High School - Bunya Campus
overview: Dalby State High School offers QATSIF Scholarships to support Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students commencing Year 11. QATSIF provides two-year scholarship funding to help Indigenous students access quality secondary education and prepare for post-secondary pathways.
description: QATSIF Scholarships provide two-year scholarship funding to support Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander students commencing in Year 11 at Dalby State High School. An eligible QATSIF Scholarship applicant must be a person of Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander descent who identifies as an Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander person and is accepted as such by the community in which they live. The scholarship is designed to reduce financial barriers to education for Indigenous students and is part of a broader framework of support including mentorship through the Dalby Academy football program, university experience days, and art workshops. Students participating in QATSIF-supported programs must continue to work at school and embrace the objectives of the foundation.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander descent; identifies as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander; accepted as such by community where student lives; commencing in Year 11; enrolled at Dalby State High School
special_conditions: Scholarship duration is two years. Recipients must maintain engagement with school and embrace the objectives of the QATSIF foundation. Participants in the broader indigenous education program (Dalby Academy) must continue to work at school to remain in the program. Letter from an Elder confirming acceptance by the Indigenous community may be required. Additional support services available including mentoring, university visits, and workshops.

overview: Dalby State High School offers Arrow Energy Agricultural Scholarships to support students undertaking agricultural study at the school's Bunya Campus. The scholarships are designed to assist rural students from farming backgrounds to pursue their careers in agriculture.
description: Arrow Energy Agricultural Scholarships cover a portion of the boarding fees for successful applicants undertaking agricultural study at Dalby SHS. Scholarships are valued at $5,000 per recipient. Available across most year levels with one each in years 7, 8 and 9, two in year 10 and 11, and three for year 12, or another combination deemed appropriate by Arrow Energy. Students must be undertaking agricultural study and/or agricultural extra-curricular activities to be eligible. The selection process involves an application form and assessment by a panel. These scholarships are administered through the Bunya Campus residential facility to help reduce the financial burden on rural families.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 7, 8, 9, 10, 11 and 12; Must be currently enrolled or accepted as a student at Dalby State High School; Must be undertaking agricultural study and/or agricultural extra-curricular activities; Students from farming or rural backgrounds preferred; Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander status eligibility with community letter may apply
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
number_awarded: Up to 10 per year (typically 1 each Years 7,8,9; 2 each Years 10,11; 3 for Year 12)
special_conditions: Scholarships assist with tuition and boarding costs at Bunya Campus. Partnership between Dalby State High School and Arrow Energy has been ongoing since 2011. 2025 applications now closed. Contact Bunya Campus on 4660 0333 or email admin@dalbyshsbunya.eq.edu.au for current application information.

program_name: Matt Hughes Foundation Senior Secondary Bursary
overview: Dalby State High School offers the Matt Hughes Foundation Senior Secondary Bursary to support Year 11 and 12 students facing challenges, adversity and difficulty. The bursary helps eligible students engage fully in their senior secondary learning.
description: The Matt Hughes Foundation Senior Secondary Bursary provides $1,000 annually to year 11 and 12 students at Dalby State High School who are facing challenges, adversity and difficulty and have a plan to engage fully in their senior secondary learning. Recipients are selected based on demonstrated need and commitment to their education. Applications open to Year 10 students during Term 4 for the following year. Students must have a demonstrated plan for engaging in senior secondary education. The bursary is renewable annually based on continued eligibility and need. This bursary honours the legacy of Matt Hughes, a former Dalby builder and football identity who passed away from cancer in 2019.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 11 and Year 12 students; Currently enrolled at Dalby State High School; Must be facing challenges, adversity or difficulty; Must have a plan to engage fully in senior secondary learning; Year 10 students may apply during Term 4 for the following year
special_conditions: Applications open to Year 10 students during term 4 for the following year. 2025 applications now closed. Renewal is subject to continued eligibility. Established in honour of Matt Hughes and his legacy through the Matt Hughes Foundation.

overview: Dalby State High School provides QATSIF (Queensland Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Foundation) Scholarships to eligible Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students commencing Year 11. The scholarship provides two-year funding to support these students through their senior secondary education.
description: QATSIF provides two-year scholarship funding to support students commencing in Year 11. The scholarship provides $500 per semester ($2,000 total across Years 11 and 12) to eligible Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students to support their senior secondary studies. An eligible QATSIF Scholarship applicant must be a person of Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander descent who identifies as an Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander and is accepted as such by the community in which they live. Students must be Australian citizens and residents of Queensland, enrolled or with confirmed enrolment at the school. Applications are made through the school during Year 10. The school applies on behalf of students and families typically in mid-year. Current Year 11 QATSIF recipients do not need to reapply as their scholarships carry over.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander descent, identifying as such; Accepted as such by the community in which they live; Australian citizen and resident of Queensland; Enrolled or with confirmed enrolment at Dalby State High School; Commencing Year 11 (applications made in Year 10); Participates in school activities enhancing Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander cultural identity; Demonstrates willingness to fully participate in school life and be a role model
special_conditions: Two-year scholarship spanning Years 11 and 12. School applies on behalf of students; parents/carers cannot apply directly. Applications typically open mid-May each year. Current Year 11 recipients do not need to reapply. 2025 applications now closed. Contact school for further information on Round 16 and Round 17 applications. QATSIF Round 16 for 2026 entries was current at time of extraction.

overview: Dalby South State School offers an Enrichment Program for identified students in Years 1 to 6 who demonstrate exceptional potential. The program provides extra stimulus opportunities in STEAM (Science, Technology, Engineering, Art and Mathematics) and extends classroom learning through advanced design thinking and problem-solving experiences.
description: The Enrichment Program at Dalby South State School is designed for students identified as benefiting from extra stimulus opportunities. Students engage in enrichment-based learning that encourages them to think outside the box, take risks, generate ideas, use design strategies, and solve problems using reason, logic, resourcefulness, imagination and innovation. The program focuses on developing creative and critical thinking skills through collaborative, inquiry-based approaches. In 2025, the Enrichment Program focuses on Industry 4.0 links, bringing together technology including 3D Additive Manufacturing, Coding and Robotics with discovery-based learning. Some Enrichment Program learning experiences may incur a small cost to families, which would be outlined in the letter of invitation. Students selected for the program must maintain their class work and curriculum standards to remain enrolled. The Enrichment Program changes each semester and year to year, but core ideologies around teamwork, communication, collaboration, and problem-solving remain constant.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Year 1 through Year 6; Identified through school-based report cards each semester; Selection based on testing measures and teacher observation; Administration team and Enrichment teacher review student achievement levels; Classroom teachers provide input on identified students; Students must maintain class work and curriculum standards to remain in the program; Depending on program focus, there may be other qualifying standards
year_levels: Year 1, Year 2, Year 3, Year 4, Year 5, Year 6
special_conditions: Selection is based on identification through school-based report cards, testing measures, and teacher observation at the start of each semester. Selected students receive a letter of invitation. Students are expected to maintain their class work and curriculum standards to remain in the Enrichment Program. Some learning experiences may incur a small cost to families.

program_name: QATSIF Scholarship Program (Queensland Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Foundation)
overview: Deception Bay State High School participates in the QATSIF scholarship program to support Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. The program provides financial assistance to eligible Year 10 students who meet participation criteria including cultural engagement and attendance requirements.
description: The Queensland Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Foundation (QATSIF) provides educational scholarships to support eligible Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students in Queensland secondary schools. Under the QCE Scholarship Program, QATSIF provides two-year scholarship funding of $2,000 per year ($500 per semester) to support students commencing in Year 11. The funds are intended to help cover curriculum-related costs such as uniforms, books, equipment, and excursions. Students must maintain 85% attendance and demonstrate active participation in cultural activities. The scholarship is available to Year 10 and 11 Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students who apply through their school and meet all eligibility criteria including being accepted as an Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander community member.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander descent; Identified as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander by their community; Australian citizen and Queensland resident; Enrolled at Deception Bay State High School; Minimum 85% school attendance; Participate in school cultural activities enhancing Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander cultural identity; Demonstrate willingness to fully participate in school life; Year 10 or 11 enrolment at time of application
special_conditions: QATSIF funds successful schools which then provide scholarships to individual students. The intention of the funding is to increase Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander student retention in Years 11 and 12. Funding must be used to complement and build on existing school and community programmes. Schools apply when students are in Year 10, with students commencing scholarships in Year 11. Renewal is contingent upon continued eligibility and participation in school cultural activities.

overview: Depot Hill State School participates in The Smith Family Learning Grants program to support students from disadvantaged families. Students whose families hold a health care card are eligible for financial assistance to help cover education essentials such as books, shoes, uniforms and excursion costs.
description: The Smith Family Learning Grants provides approximately $500 per student per year to eligible families at Depot Hill State School. The scholarship is available to all students who attend the school and whose families hold a current health care card or pension concession card. The funds can be used to purchase books, school uniforms, shoes, and to pay for school excursions and camps. Students must demonstrate a strong commitment to education through regular school attendance. The financial assistance is renewed annually and is not means-tested beyond the requirement for a health care card. This is a non-competitive program focused on reducing financial barriers to education for students experiencing disadvantage.
eligibility_criteria: Current health care card or pension concession card holder in family; Student enrolled at Depot Hill State School; Demonstrated strong commitment to education through regular school attendance; Open to all year levels Prep to Year 6
special_conditions: Scholarship is provided through The Smith Family's Learning for Life program. Annual review may be required. Families must hold current health care card or pension concession card. School referral may be required. Students must maintain strong school attendance commitment.

Recipients are supported with up to 50% remission on tuition fees. Successful applicants are assessed through the scholarship examination administered by Edutest, which provides an impartial assessment of students' academic and general capabilities, combined with review of previous school report cards. Applicants must demonstrate exceptional behaviour, effort and commitment to their studies throughout the application process.

overview: Faith Lutheran College Redlands offers the Academic Scholarship to reward outstanding academic achievement. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning, supporting new students entering the college.
description: The Academic Scholarship at Faith Lutheran College Redlands is awarded on the basis of merit and takes the form of a whole or partial rebate of a student's tuition fees. Scholarships are designed primarily to support new students attending FLCR, though existing students may be considered at the discretion of the Scholarship Review Panel and the Principal. The financial benefit is awarded as a tuition fee reduction, with the exact amount determined based on merit and the annual scholarship budget. Recipients must maintain an excellent standard of academic results, encourage academic rigour among peers, act as positive role models and mentors, and adhere to the College code of conduct and values. Successful applicants undergo a 12-month review to ensure continued eligibility. Applications require copies of the last two years of school reports, NAPLAN test results (Years 3, 5, 7, 9), and copies of academic achievement certificates. A non-refundable scholarship application fee of $50.00 is required.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students entering Faith Lutheran College Redlands; May be awarded to existing FLCR students at Principal's discretion; Behaviour and participation in school life must align with College ethos and values; Must maintain high standard of application and commitment; Copies of last two school reports and NAPLAN testing results required; Copies of certificates of academic achievements required
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded at the discretion of the Principal within set budget limits and aligned with the College mission and aims. The decision of the Principal is final and no correspondence will be entered into. Recipients must undergo a 12-month review. Scholarship recipients must be prepared to display a high standard of conduct in all areas, represent the College in a positive manner, maintain personal presentation and wear the College uniform as required, be ambassadors of the College, commit to the College ethos and support the total life of the College, and commit to the College Academic, Sporting and/or Arts program.

overview: Faith Lutheran College Redlands offers the Staff Sponsored Leadership and Service Scholarship, funded by voluntary contributions from staff members. The scholarship supports students who demonstrate leadership qualities and commitment to service, making independent education more accessible to local families experiencing financial constraints.
description: The Staff Sponsored Leadership and Service Scholarship at Faith Lutheran College Redlands is a unique program established by staff members who make voluntary contributions from their own pay to support new students and families. The scholarship is designed to provide financial assistance to families undergoing financial hardship and to recognise students who demonstrate outstanding leadership qualities and commitment to service within the College community. The financial benefit is awarded as a partial or whole rebate of tuition fees, with the exact amount determined based on assessed financial need, demonstrated leadership potential, and annual scholarship funds available. Recipients are expected to demonstrate exceptional commitment to service, leadership among peers, positive conduct and representation of the College, and support for the College ethos and values. Applications require evidence of leadership experience, service contributions, financial documentation demonstrating family circumstances, and completion of the scholarship application form. A non-refundable application fee of $50.00 applies.

special_conditions: The Staff Sponsored Leadership and Service Scholarship is funded by voluntary contributions from Faith Lutheran College staff members and is therefore limited by available funds each year. This scholarship demonstrates the College community's commitment to providing quality education to families facing financial difficulties.

program_name: Learning Excellence at Ferny Grove (LEAF) Program
overview: Ferny Grove State High School offers the Learning Excellence at Ferny Grove (LEAF) Program as a selective academic program for high-achieving students. The program recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and potential in an integrated learning environment. It is a three-year program running from Year 7 through to the end of Year 9.
description: The LEAF Program is designed for students of similar abilities who benefit from more in-depth exploration of core content delivered by specialised teachers. Students learn within an integrated curriculum that recognises the underlying relationships between English, Mathematics, Science and Humanities, with learning experiences designed using authentic contexts and real-world applications. Selection is based on an entrance examination held in Term 3, combined with school report card assessment and potential interviews if necessary. Participation in a mandatory range of extracurricular activities and competitions is required to maintain membership in the program. Students must maintain high academic results in all relevant subjects as well as high effort and behaviour to retain their place. The program is three years in duration (Year 7-9) and provides no financial remission as it is a government school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be selected based on entrance exam held in Term 3; Combined assessment of exam results and school report card; May be interviewed if necessary; Must maintain high academic results and behaviour; Participation in extracurricular activities mandatory; Currently in Year 6 at time of application
special_conditions: Program is three years in duration (Year 7 to Year 9). Students must maintain high academic results in all relevant subjects as well as high effort and behaviour to retain their place in the Signature Program. Participation in a mandatory range of extracurricular activities and competitions is required to maintain membership in the LEAF program.

overview: Ferny Grove State High School offers the German Immersion Program as a selective excellence program for students interested in developing high-level German language proficiency. The program allows students to learn the regular curriculum through the German language over Years 7-10, developing exceptional language skills and cultural competence.
description: The German Immersion Program offers students an exciting opportunity to develop very high-level German language proficiency through learning the regular curriculum in German. Students learn exactly the same curriculum as other students during Years 7, 8, and 9, but through the German language. In Year 10, students continue to learn Science, Humanities, and Health and Physical Education in German, and begin the Year 11 program of the Senior German course. Subjects taught in German include core subjects and may include Agriculture, The Arts, Digital Technology, and Design/STEM units. Selection is based on primary school results, recommendations from primary classroom and language teachers, and evidence of student motivation to learn. Successful applicants are above-average academically and are motivated students expected to commit to the program for four years. Fully-funded scholarships to Germany are awarded annually to one-two outstanding students of German through the PASCH network.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be selected based on primary school results; Recommendations required from primary classroom teacher and language teacher; Evidence of motivation to learn required; Above-average academic performance; Must commit to four-year program (Years 7-10); Year 6 students at time of application in primary school
special_conditions: Students are expected to commit to the program for four years. Students must maintain high academic results in all relevant subjects as well as high effort and behaviour to retain their place in the Signature Program. Fully-funded scholarships to Germany are awarded annually to one-two outstanding students of German. Students have opportunity to sit the 'Fit in Deutsch 2' examination conducted by the Goethe Institut to demonstrate German language ability at A2 European benchmark. Towards the end of Year 10, students may travel to Germany on a three-week exchange program.

program_name: Instrumental and Choral Music Excellence Program
overview: Ferny Grove State High School offers a multi-award-winning Instrumental and Choral Music Excellence Program recognised as one of the most highly regarded in Queensland. The program recognises students demonstrating musical talent and potential to develop as performers within a community of 400 students across 20 ensembles.
description: Ferny Grove State High School's multi-award-winning instrumental and choral music program involves around 400 students in 20 ensembles, including choirs, string groups, orchestras, stage bands, and percussion, brass and wind ensembles. The program has achieved international recognition, with research about its teaching methodology published in international academic journals through Cambridge University. Performing music to a high level develops students' ability to analyse, interpret and communicate complex intellectual and emotional concepts while nurturing creativity and imagination. Students wishing to nominate for the program must tick the relevant box on the Expression of Interest Form and provide all relevant academic records. Selection is based on academic performance and musical ability. Applications are accepted only in the year immediately prior to the year of entry. Students can participate in Instrumental Music in addition to any of the Signature Programs (LEAF, FAST, or German Immersion). The program is available throughout Years 7-12 with no specific end year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Selection based on academic records and musical ability; Expression of Interest required with all relevant academic documentation; Applications accepted only in year immediately prior to entry; May participate in addition to any other Signature Program; Current students can continue through Years 7-12
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Students participating in instrumental music and German immersion programs are required to pay an additional set fee through the Student Resource Scheme. Capacity for Instrumental Music Program of Excellence is 450 students. Students must maintain high academic results and behaviour to retain their place. Out of catchment students must meet specific audition requirements as detailed in the Out Of Catchment Audition Information sheet.

overview: Ferny Grove State High School offers the Ferny Advanced Sporting Teams (FAST) Program as a selective excellence program for junior elite athletes in Rugby League and Touch Football. The program is designed to develop well-rounded athletes who understand both mental and physical aspects of performance and become positive role models and leaders.
description: The FAST program is an innovative Rugby League and Touch Football program that runs from Year 7 to Year 10 with identified pathways into senior years. The program allows students the opportunity to enrol in a sport-centred and athlete-focused class for the whole school year. Students commit to participating in two practical lessons (one skills-based and one conditioning session) and one theory lesson each week. The program aims to support individual athletes to set and achieve their sporting goals while working towards maximising their performance. The focus is on developing well-rounded athletes who understand both the mental and physical aspects of performance and assists them in becoming positive role models and leaders within their sporting communities. Students incur a yearly program fee as part of participation. Selection is based on applicant trials. Successful applicants must be able to meet the financial requirements of the program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be selected based on applicant trials; Ability to meet financial requirements of program required; Must maintain high academic results and behaviour to retain place; Commitment to two practical and one theory lesson weekly; Participation in Rugby League and Touch Football only; Years 7-10 with pathways into Years 11-12
special_conditions: Successful applicants will incur a yearly fee as part of the program. Applicants must be able to meet the financial requirements of the program. Students must maintain high academic results in all relevant subjects as well as high effort and behaviour to retain their place in the Signature Program. Students can apply for multiple programs but will only be selected for either FAST, LEAF or German Immersion, not multiple from these three. Program runs from Year 7 to 10 with identified pathways into senior years for Years 11-12.

program_name: STEMx Program (Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics)
overview: Ferny Grove State High School offers the STEMx signature program for students interested in science, technology, engineering and mathematics with a focus on design thinking and real-world problem solving. The program engages students in building empathy, global competencies, and 21st-century skills through practical STEM applications.
description: Students engaging in the STEMx signature program experience a journey of building empathy, real-world understanding, global competencies and 21st-century skills to enable holistic responses to design problems. The program explores the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals and concepts like Ripple Effects to build rigor into design-making decisions. Students apply scientific concepts through applied technologies, engineering principles, and mathematics methods from mainstream classes to real-world solutions. It is a unique combination of Mathematics, Technologies, and Science where students apply these subjects in outcomes that are not simply repetition. The subject is based on the Design Technology KLA and utilises the Ferny Grove State High School Design Thinking Model. Students engage in prototyping and practical technology machinery within the STEMx state-of-the-art Maker Space Lab. It is offered as a Technology elective across Year 8 and Year 9 as a composite class and team-taught. In Year 10, it is offered as an elective with an Engineering focus pathway into Year 11/12 Engineering. Students are expected to commit to the program for two years with an option to continue into Year 10.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Year 8 and Year 9; Commitment to program for two years required; Option to continue into Year 10; Reduced range of other electives due to program requirements; Interest in design thinking and real-world problem solving
special_conditions: Students selecting the STEMx program are expected to commit to the program for two years, with an option to continue into Year 10. Students will have a reduced range of other electives to choose from. Pathways continue into Year 11 and 12 Engineering subjects.

overview: Ferny Grove State High School recognises that parents may experience financial hardship and provides payment options and assistance to support student participation in school programs. The school encourages families experiencing financial difficulties to contact the school office to discuss available support.
description: Parents experiencing financial hardship are encouraged to contact the school office to discuss a range of payment options that are available. The school implements a Student Resource Scheme that provides a more economical option to purchasing new textbooks and resources each year. The Textbook and Resource Allowance provides assistance to parents/caregivers to offset textbook and resource costs and is paid directly to the school. Students participating in instrumental music and German immersion programs may be eligible for assistance to help offset additional program fees. The school works with families to find flexible payment arrangements that support student access to all programs and educational resources.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; Parents/caregivers required to contact school office directly; All students eligible to discuss payment arrangements; Participants in music and language programs may access additional support; No specific income thresholds stated
special_conditions: Parents must contact the school office directly to discuss available options. The Textbook and Resource Allowance is paid to the school. Flexible payment options are offered on a case-by-case basis. Not a formal scholarship but support available for families in need.

overview: Freshwater Christian College offers the General Excellence Scholarship to recognise outstanding achievement and progress across all curriculum areas. The scholarship is awarded annually to both new and current students and acknowledges student contributions to the College community through expression of core values including compassion, courage, honour, humility, integrity and wisdom.
description: The General Excellence Scholarship applies to every area of the curriculum and is awarded annually to all new and current students in Years 5 to 12. This scholarship provides an annual reduction in fees of 50% for the duration of the scholarship. Students are required to prepare a portfolio of evidence demonstrating outstanding achievement and progress in one or more subjects, as well as contribution to the College community through expression of College character virtues. Interested students must attend an interview with the Principal, and awarding of scholarships is announced by letter shortly after the interview process is completed.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new and current students; Year 5 to Year 12; Must demonstrate outstanding achievement and progress in one or more subjects; Must show contribution to College community through expression of college character virtues (compassion, courage, honour, humility, integrity, wisdom)
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarship is awarded annually. Portfolio of evidence and Principal interview required for all applicants.

overview: Freshwater Christian College offers three-year and two-year Academic Scholarships for high-achieving students entering secondary school. These scholarships recognise exceptional academic ability through formal examination of key subjects and are available for students entering Year 7 or Year 11.
description: Three-year Academic Scholarships are available for students who will enter Year 7, and two-year Academic Scholarships are available for students who will enter Year 11. Academic scholarships attract a 50% reduction in fees for the duration of the scholarship. All applicants are invited to sit the Scholarship Exam which is held at Freshwater Christian College. The scholarship is assessed through formal examination of general reasoning, mathematics, reading comprehension, spelling and written expression. High achieving candidates will be shortlisted and invited for an interview with the Principal.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or Year 11; Must sit formal Scholarship Exam; Academic performance in general reasoning, mathematics, reading comprehension, spelling and written expression
special_conditions: Three-year scholarship for Year 7 entry; Two-year scholarship for Year 11 entry. High achieving candidates only proceed to Principal interview stage.

overview: The Principal's Opportunity Scholarship is awarded at the Principal's discretion to families who would not otherwise be financially able to attend Freshwater Christian College. This needs-based scholarship is available to students from Prep to Year 12 and applications are accepted and assessed year-round.
description: The Principal's Opportunity Scholarship is awarded, at the Principal's discretion, to those who would not otherwise be financially able to attend Freshwater Christian College. Applications are accepted and assessed year-round for students in Prep to Year 12. Principal's Opportunity Scholarships are means-tested and thus applicants are required to provide financial information to the school to support their application. The scholarship provides financial assistance to eligible families based on demonstrated financial need and the Principal's assessment of the applicant's capacity to benefit from the College experience.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students Prep to Year 12; Financial need assessment required; Must provide financial information to school; Assessed by Principal discretion
special_conditions: Applications accepted and assessed year-round. Means-tested scholarship requiring financial documentation. Award amount determined at Principal's discretion based on demonstrated need.

overview: Flagstone State Community College offers junior secondary scholarships worth $500 per year to recognise academic achievement in Years 7-9. These scholarships support students demonstrating strong academic performance in the junior secondary years.
description: Flagstone State Community College provides junior secondary scholarships valued at $500 per year for Years 7-9 students. The scholarship supports students with demonstrated academic merit and commitment to learning. Applications for the scholarship can be found in enrolment packs or collected from the college office. The scholarship is awarded annually and provides financial support to assist eligible students continuing their secondary education.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 7, 8, or 9; Must demonstrate academic merit; Application required
special_conditions: Applications must be submitted using the form available in enrolment packs or from the college office.

overview: Flagstone State Community College offers QATSIF Scholarships to Indigenous students in Years 11 and 12 to support them in continuing their secondary education. These scholarships are specifically designed to provide financial assistance to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students.
description: QATSIF Scholarships are available for Year 11 and Year 12 Indigenous students at Flagstone State Community College to support their continuation of studies. The scholarship provides financial assistance to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students who are enrolled or planning to enrol in senior secondary education. Eligibility and application details should be obtained through the college's Indigenous support services office.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Indigenous students enrolled in Year 11 or Year 12; Must be Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander; Must be current or prospective student at the college
special_conditions: Applicants must be able to provide Confirmation of Identity documentation as per the Indigenous support protocols.

overview: The Jude Fox Bursary Fund provides $3,000 in total support to Flagstone State Community College students who are progressing to university and facing barriers to attending higher education. The bursary recognises students who demonstrate strong potential and commitment to their education.
description: The Jude Fox Bursary Fund grants $3,000 to Flagstone State Community College students facing barriers to attending university, including financial constraints, travel costs, and lack of support. Successful participants are awarded $2,000 upon confirmation of enrolment at a Queensland-based university and receive a further $1,000 upon successful completion of their first year of study. The bursary is designed to support first-generation university students and reduce financial pressure during transition to higher education. Students can use funds for travel costs, textbooks, technology, or other university-related expenses.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a current Flagstone State Community College student; Must be enrolling at a Queensland-based university; Must face barriers to attending university including financial, travel, or support-related challenges; Open to all students
special_conditions: $2,000 is awarded upon confirmation of enrolment at a Queensland university, with a further $1,000 awarded upon successful completion of the first year of study. Funds can be used for travel costs, textbooks, technology, or other costs associated with university study.

program_name: Program of Excellence in Creative Arts
overview: Flagstone State Community College offers a Program of Excellence in Creative Arts for students with demonstrated talent and commitment to the performing arts. The program provides specialised instruction and learning opportunities for eligible students across the school's creative arts curriculum.
description: Flagstone State Community College operates a Program of Excellence in Creative Arts supporting students with demonstrated talent in performing and visual arts disciplines. The program allocates a defined number of places and gives priority to in-catchment students who meet the school's specific enrolment criteria for creative arts excellence. Out-of-catchment enrolments to the program are available only after demand from in-catchment applications has been met. The program offers specialised pathways aligned with the school's focus on dance, drama, music, and visual arts, with opportunities to engage in co-curricular performances and competitions.
eligibility_criteria: Must meet school's Creative Arts Excellence Program criteria; In-catchment students prioritised; Out-of-catchment applications considered subject to available capacity; Demonstration of creative arts talent or commitment required
special_conditions: Enrolment criteria for the Program of Excellence are available from the school. Places in the program are limited and allocated according to capacity. In-catchment students receive priority; out-of-catchment places only available after in-catchment demand met.

program_name: Peter Doherty Outstanding Senior STEM Student Award
overview: Flagstone State Community College students are eligible for the Peter Doherty Outstanding Senior STEM Student Award, which recognises exceptional achievement and innovation in STEM education. Award recipients receive a bursary to support their future studies in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics.
