description: The Peter Doherty Outstanding Senior STEM Student Award, created 20 years ago by Nobel Prize-winning scientist Peter Doherty, recognises Year 12 students who demonstrate outstanding and innovative contributions to STEM education in Queensland. Fourteen Year 12 students are selected annually from across Queensland for this prestigious award. Recipients receive a bursary to help support their future studies in STEM fields. The award acknowledges students who show exceptional passion for STEM, participate in competitions, and contribute to science education programs and initiatives.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 12 students in Queensland; Must demonstrate outstanding achievement in STEM; Must show exceptional contributions to STEM education innovation; Must demonstrate sustained engagement with STEM activities
special_conditions: Selection is competitive and statewide across Queensland. Award recognises students demonstrating exceptional achievement, leadership, and innovation in STEM education. Nomination or selection process involves review of achievements and contributions.

overview: Fraser Coast Anglican College offers the Academic Scholarship to recognise and support students who consistently achieve strong academic results. This scholarship is available to students from any school commencing in Years 7, 8, 9, 10, 11 or 12, providing a pathway for exceptional students to join one of Queensland's top-performing regional schools.
description: The FCAC Academic Scholarship is designed for students who demonstrate consistent achievement of strong academic results and seek support in advancing their educational goals at a premier regional school. Applicants may be asked to provide evidence supporting their application, including recent school report cards, ICAS certificates, NAPLAN reports, or other evidence demonstrating high academic achievement levels. Applicants may be required to take part in a short general abilities test. The scholarship value can be Full, Half or Part, with the size dependent on the strength of the application. Additional financial support is available for low-income families. Awardees will retain their scholarship in the following years and will have annual goal-setting and support meetings with senior academic staff.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students from any school; Commencing in Years 7, 8, 9, 10, 11 or 12 in 2027; Must demonstrate consistent strong academic results; Evidence of high academic achievement required
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or 50% Fee Reduction or Partial Fee Remission
special_conditions: Awardees will retain their scholarship in the following years and will have annual goal-setting and support meetings with senior academic staff. Students may only hold one scholarship at any given time.

overview: Fraser Coast Anglican College offers the Sports Scholarship to support students with a proven track record in their chosen sport(s) who are dedicated to continuous improvement. This scholarship is available to students from any school commencing in Years 7, 8, 9, 10, 11 or 12 in 2026, providing elite athletes the opportunity to further develop their sporting potential.
description: The FCAC Sports Scholarship is designed for students who have demonstrated excellence and commitment in their chosen sport(s), combining consistent training, feedback and willingness to embrace new challenges. Applicants should ideally have been selected at Wide Bay level or higher in their chosen sport, preferably through Queensland School Sport or equivalent pathways, as this significantly improves the probability of being shortlisted. We seek students who demonstrate a commitment to contributing positively to the broader sporting community. Applicants may be encouraged to supply evidence of their sports achievements including certificates, medals, and references. Shortlisted applicants will be invited to an interview with the Director of Sport. The scholarship value can be Full, Half or Part, with the size dependent on the strength of the application. Additional financial support is available for low-income families. Awardees will retain their scholarship in the following years with annual goal-setting and support meetings with the Director of Sport.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students from any school; Commencing in Years 7, 8, 9, 10, 11 or 12 in 2026; Proven track record of performance in chosen sport(s); Dedication to constant improvement through consistent training, feedback and challenges; Wide Bay level selection or higher preferred; Commitment to contributing to sporting community
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or 50% Fee Reduction or Partial Fee Remission
special_conditions: Applicants should provide evidence of sporting achievements such as certificates, medals, and references. Shortlisted applicants will be invited to interview with Director of Sport. Awardees will retain their scholarship in the following years and will have annual goal-setting and support meetings with the Director of Sport. Students may only hold one scholarship at any given time.

overview: Fraser Coast Anglican College offers the Arts Scholarship for creative and dedicated students with strong passion for the arts including Music, Singing, Visual Art, Dance and Drama. This scholarship recognises students seeking to add to the cultural life of the college community and is available for students from any school commencing Years 7, 8, 9, 10, 11 or 12 in 2026.
description: The FCAC Arts Scholarship is designed for students who are passionate about the arts and wish to develop their skills in Music, Singing, Visual Art, Dance or Drama at an advanced level. Successful applicants must demonstrate high levels of skill in their chosen art form and commitment to improving their craft through regular practice, challenges and participation in a range of curricular and extra-curricular activities. Applicants may be contacted to provide evidence supporting their application, which could include portfolios, filmed performances, references, and relevant certificates such as AMEB qualifications. Musicians and vocalists may be asked to attend an audition. All shortlisted applicants will be invited to attend an interview with the Director of Visual and Performing Arts. The scholarship value can be Full, Half or Part, with size dependent on strength of the application. Additional financial support is available for low-income families. Awardees will retain the scholarship in the following years.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students from any school; Commencing in Years 7, 8, 9, 10, 11 or 12 in 2026; Strong passion for the Arts; High level of demonstrated skill in chosen art area; Commitment to improving craft through regular practice and challenges; Participation in curricular and extra-curricular activities
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or 50% Fee Reduction or Partial Fee Remission
special_conditions: Applicants may provide portfolios, filmed performances, references, and AMEB or equivalent certificates. Musicians and vocalists may be required to attend audition. All shortlisted applicants will attend interview with Director of Visual and Performing Arts. Awardees will retain scholarship in following years. Students may only hold one scholarship at any given time.

overview: Fraser Coast Anglican College offers the Body Mind and Spirit Scholarship, which is symbolic of the college's motto 'Enriching Body, Mind and Spirit'. This scholarship is designed for well-rounded students who may not be high achievers in one particular area but demonstrate involvement across multiple co-curricular activities spanning physical, intellectual and spiritual development.
description: The Body Mind and Spirit Scholarship recognises the holistic development philosophy at Fraser Coast Anglican College. This scholarship is designed specifically for students who may not be exceptional achievers in one particular academic or co-curricular area but demonstrate balanced, meaningful involvement across a range of activities spanning the Body (physical pursuits), Mind (intellectual and academic engagement) and Spirit (character development, leadership and community service) spheres. The scholarship supports students who exemplify the college's commitment to developing the whole child across all dimensions of human development. Applicants should demonstrate engagement in activities across multiple categories and show commitment to personal growth and community contribution.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students from any school; Involvement in range of activities across Body, Mind and Spirit spheres; Not necessarily high achievers in single particular area; Demonstrated commitment to holistic personal development; Participation in curricular and co-curricular activities
special_conditions: This scholarship is symbolic of FCAC's motto of 'Enriching Body, Mind and Spirit' and recognises holistic development across multiple spheres rather than single area excellence. Students may only hold one scholarship at any given time.

overview: Fraser Coast Anglican College offers the Grahame Ginn Founder's Scholarship as a financial support program named in honour of the college's Foundation Principal Mr Grahame Ginn. This scholarship is designed specifically for non-FCAC students who wish to enrol at the college but have been unable to do so due to economic, social or other disadvantages.
description: The Grahame Ginn Founder's Scholarship is a financial assistance program designed to remove barriers to access for students who would benefit from an FCAC education but have faced economic, social or other disadvantages preventing their enrolment. This scholarship honours the vision of Mr Grahame Ginn, the college's Foundation Principal (1994-2004), whose commitment to inclusive education shaped the college's foundational values. The scholarship provides financial support to eligible students to enable participation in the full range of educational and co-curricular programs offered by the college. This program demonstrates the college's commitment to equity and access, ensuring that talented and deserving students from all backgrounds can access a quality Anglican education.
eligibility_criteria: Open to non-FCAC students; Seeking to enrol at FCAC; Experience of economic disadvantage; Experience of social disadvantage; Or other barriers to enrolment; Demonstrated academic or co-curricular potential
special_conditions: This scholarship is specifically designed for students unable to enrol at FCAC due to economic, social or other disadvantages. Financial assessment may be required as part of application process. Awardees will retain scholarship in following years. Students may only hold one scholarship at any given time.

overview: Foxwell State Secondary College's Generation STEM program offers enriched learning experiences in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics for students demonstrating exceptional aptitude and enthusiasm in STEM disciplines. The program provides advanced learning pathways with extension opportunities beyond the standard curriculum. Students accepted into this Academy of Excellence may be considered for enrolment at Foxwell regardless of their residential catchment area.
description: Generation STEM is a specialized Academy of Excellence program designed to foster talent and high achievement in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics. The program provides enriched learning experiences with extension activities and advanced curriculum tailored to STEM-focused students. Each Academy of Excellence program includes a levy fee that applies upon commencement. A limited number of scholarships are available and may cover the cost of school fees and the Academy of Excellence program levy. Eligible students must have clear passion or interest in STEM as outlined in their personal statement, a supporting recommendation from a relevant teacher detailing engagement and experience, and participate in a compulsory workshop or Connect@Foxwell Day.
eligibility_criteria: Very high effort and behaviour ratings; Demonstrated passion or interest in STEM; Supporting recommendation from a relevant teacher or coach; Participation in compulsory workshop or Connect@Foxwell Day; May allow out-of-catchment enrolment if Academy criteria met
special_conditions: Limited number of scholarships available and may cover the cost of school fees and Academy of Excellence program levy. Each Academy of Excellence program includes a levy fee. Students who meet eligibility criteria may be considered for enrolment regardless of residential catchment area. All documentation and Enrolment Expression of Interest Form must be submitted in full by the closing date to be considered. Alternate selection process may apply for mid-year enrolments.

program_name: G-STEM (Girls in Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics) Program of Excellence
overview: Foxwell State Secondary College offers the G-STEM Program of Excellence to support girls demonstrating exceptional aptitude and enthusiasm in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics. This program provides specialized enriched learning experiences designed specifically to encourage and support female students in STEM fields. Girls accepted into this program may be considered for enrolment at Foxwell regardless of their residential catchment area.
description: The G-STEM (Girls in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics) Program of Excellence provides enriched learning experiences specifically designed for girls demonstrating exceptional aptitude in STEM disciplines. The program aims to foster talent, ignite passion, and support high achievement in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics. Each Academy of Excellence program includes a levy fee that applies upon commencement. A limited number of scholarships are available and may cover the cost of school fees and the Academy of Excellence program levy. Selection requires very high effort and behaviour ratings, demonstrated passion or interest in STEM outlined in a personal statement, a supporting recommendation from a relevant teacher or coach detailing engagement and experience, and participation in a compulsory workshop or Connect@Foxwell Day.
eligibility_criteria: Girls only; Very high effort and behaviour ratings; Demonstrated passion or interest in STEM; Supporting recommendation from a relevant teacher or coach; Participation in compulsory workshop or Connect@Foxwell Day; May allow out-of-catchment enrolment if Academy criteria met
special_conditions: Limited number of scholarships available and may cover the cost of school fees and Academy of Excellence program levy. Each Academy of Excellence program includes a levy fee. Students who meet eligibility criteria may be considered for enrolment regardless of residential catchment area. All documentation and Enrolment Expression of Interest Form must be submitted in full by the closing date to be considered. Alternate selection process may apply for mid-year enrolments. Gender-specific program for girls.

overview: The Advanced Learning Pathways (ALP) Program at Foxwell State Secondary College is an Academy of Excellence for academically excelling students in Years 9 and 10. The program offers extension in specific subjects through a faster-paced curriculum with complex tasks and challenges designed to promote high achievement and support potential early university entry. Students accepted into ALP may be considered for enrolment regardless of their residential catchment area.
description: The Advanced Learning Pathways (ALP) Academic Excellence program provides extension opportunities in specific subjects for students demonstrating exceptional academic ability in Years 9 and 10. The program features a faster-paced curriculum, complex tasks, and challenges designed to promote high achievement and support students with potential for early university entry. Each Academy of Excellence program includes a levy fee that applies upon commencement. A limited number of scholarships are available and may cover the cost of school fees and the Academy of Excellence program levy. Eligible students must demonstrate very high effort and behaviour ratings, clear passion and interest in the chosen area of excellence outlined in a personal statement, a supporting recommendation from a relevant teacher or coach detailing engagement and experience, and participate in a compulsory workshop or Connect@Foxwell Day.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Year 9 or Year 10; Very high effort and behaviour ratings; Academic performance demonstrating exceptional ability; Demonstrated passion or interest in chosen subject area; Supporting recommendation from relevant teacher or coach; Participation in compulsory workshop or Connect@Foxwell Day; May allow out-of-catchment enrolment if Academy criteria met
special_conditions: Limited number of scholarships available and may cover the cost of school fees and Academy of Excellence program levy. Each Academy of Excellence program includes a levy fee. Year 9 and Year 10 only. Students who meet eligibility criteria may be considered for enrolment regardless of residential catchment area. All documentation and Enrolment Expression of Interest Form must be submitted in full by the closing date to be considered. Alternate selection process may apply for mid-year enrolments.

program_name: Creative Voices – Musical Theatre (Dance, Drama, Singing)
overview: Creative Voices Musical Theatre is an Academy of Excellence program at Foxwell State Secondary College designed for students demonstrating exceptional talent and passion in dance, drama, and singing. The program engages students in the performing arts through collaborative industry partnerships and performance opportunities. Students accepted into Creative Voices may be considered for enrolment at Foxwell regardless of their residential catchment area.
description: Creative Voices Musical Theatre stream provides enriched learning experiences for students demonstrating exceptional aptitude and enthusiasm in dance, drama, and singing. The program engages participants to collaborate with industry professionals, teachers, and peers to develop performance skills, culminating in community showcases and productions that inspire career pathways in the performing arts. Each Academy of Excellence program includes a levy fee that applies upon commencement. A limited number of scholarships are available and may cover the cost of school fees and the Academy of Excellence program levy. Selection requires very high effort and behaviour ratings, demonstrated passion or interest in performing arts outlined in a personal statement, a supporting recommendation from a relevant teacher or coach detailing engagement and experience, and participation in a compulsory workshop or Connect@Foxwell Day.
eligibility_criteria: Very high effort and behaviour ratings; Demonstrated passion or interest in musical theatre, dance, drama and/or singing; Supporting recommendation from a relevant teacher or coach; Participation in compulsory workshop or Connect@Foxwell Day; May allow out-of-catchment enrolment if Academy criteria met
special_conditions: Limited number of scholarships available and may cover the cost of school fees and Academy of Excellence program levy. Each Academy of Excellence program includes a levy fee. Students who meet eligibility criteria may be considered for enrolment regardless of residential catchment area. All documentation and Enrolment Expression of Interest Form must be submitted in full by the closing date to be considered. Alternate selection process may apply for mid-year enrolments. Program emphasizes mentoring and skill-building to nurture artistic excellence alongside academic success.

overview: Creative Voices Visual Art is an Academy of Excellence program at Foxwell State Secondary College for students demonstrating exceptional creativity and passion in visual arts. The program extends creative talents through collaborative projects with guest artists, focusing on design, innovation, and exhibition opportunities to prepare students for arts-related professions. Students accepted into Creative Voices may be considered for enrolment at Foxwell regardless of their residential catchment area.
description: Creative Voices Visual Art stream provides enriched learning experiences for students demonstrating exceptional aptitude and enthusiasm in visual arts, design, and artistic expression. The program extends creative talents through collaborative projects with guest artists, focusing on design, innovation, and exhibition opportunities to prepare students for arts-related professions. Each Academy of Excellence program includes a levy fee that applies upon commencement. A limited number of scholarships are available and may cover the cost of school fees and the Academy of Excellence program levy. Selection requires very high effort and behaviour ratings, demonstrated passion or interest in visual arts outlined in a personal statement, a supporting recommendation from a relevant teacher or coach detailing engagement and experience, and participation in a compulsory workshop or Connect@Foxwell Day.
eligibility_criteria: Very high effort and behaviour ratings; Demonstrated passion or interest in visual arts and design; Supporting recommendation from a relevant teacher or coach; Participation in compulsory workshop or Connect@Foxwell Day; May allow out-of-catchment enrolment if Academy criteria met
special_conditions: Limited number of scholarships available and may cover the cost of school fees and Academy of Excellence program levy. Each Academy of Excellence program includes a levy fee. Students who meet eligibility criteria may be considered for enrolment regardless of residential catchment area. All documentation and Enrolment Expression of Interest Form must be submitted in full by the closing date to be considered. Alternate selection process may apply for mid-year enrolments. Program emphasizes mentoring and skill-building to nurture artistic excellence alongside academic success.

program_name: Sporting Minds – High Performance Sports Program
overview: Sporting Minds is Foxwell State Secondary College's high-performance sports program designed for elite athletes demonstrating exceptional ability and commitment to competitive sport. The program is structured around multiple sports streams including netball, basketball, and futsal, integrating advanced training with academic commitments. Students accepted into Sporting Minds may be considered for enrolment at Foxwell regardless of their residential catchment area.
description: Sporting Minds is the college's high-performance sports program supporting elite athletes across multiple streams including Elite Athlete Development, Netball, Basketball, and Futsal. The program provides specialized training and support for students demonstrating exceptional sports performance and commitment. Each Academy of Excellence program includes a levy fee that applies upon commencement. A limited number of scholarships are available and may cover the cost of school fees and the Academy of Excellence program levy. Eligible students must demonstrate very high effort and behaviour ratings, clear passion and interest in their chosen sport outlined in a personal statement, a supporting recommendation from a relevant coach or teacher detailing engagement and experience, and participate in a compulsory workshop or Connect@Foxwell Day.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated elite or high performance in chosen sport; Very high effort and behaviour ratings; Demonstrated passion or interest in high-performance sport; Supporting recommendation from relevant coach or teacher; Participation in compulsory workshop or Connect@Foxwell Day; May allow out-of-catchment enrolment if Academy criteria met
special_conditions: Limited number of scholarships available and may cover the cost of school fees and Academy of Excellence program levy. Each Academy of Excellence program includes a levy fee. Available sports streams include Elite Athlete Development, Netball, Basketball, and Futsal. Students who meet eligibility criteria may be considered for enrolment regardless of residential catchment area. All documentation and Enrolment Expression of Interest Form must be submitted in full by the closing date to be considered. Alternate selection process may apply for mid-year enrolments. Program integrates advanced training with academic commitments.

overview: Glenala State High School offers the eSTEAM Academic Excellence Program to extend and challenge students with a passion for technology and the sciences. The program is designed for students entering Year 7 who demonstrate excellent academic ability and commitment to achieving their full potential in Science, Technology, Engineering, the Arts and Mathematics.
description: The eSTEAM Academic Excellence Program extends through Years 7, 8 and 9 as a three-year commitment. Students engage in curriculum-linked experiences including Science, Robotics, Media, 3D Printing, Computer Coding and App Development delivered through transdisciplinary units drawing from Maths, Science, Technology and Media Arts. The program includes special projects and participation in extension activities with a focus on problem-solving, personal and teamwork skills. Students must have access to a laptop, which can be hired from the school. Selection is based on results from an entry test, academic performance, behaviour and effort data from primary school reports. Continuation in the program requires students to maintain set standards in Academic Performance, Attendance, Behaviour and Effort. Some curriculum-linked experiences, excursions and specialised workshops may require additional fees to cover materials, transport or external provider costs.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be within the school's catchment area; Candidates must demonstrate excellent work ethic; Must show commitment to achieving full academic potential; Successful applicants must book enrolment interview in timely manner
special_conditions: Students need to understand they are making a three-year commitment to the program. It is a condition of continued enrolment that students maintain suitable academic, effort and behaviour results. Students at risk of falling behind will be supported to improve. In rare cases, a student may forfeit their place if, after support, their attitude or results fall short of required standard. The school reserves the right to withdraw students from the program at any time.

overview: Glenala State High School offers the Academy of Creative Arts (ACA) as an excellence program designed for students entering Year 7 with a deep passion for the performing and visual arts. The program offers students insight into professional performance and production techniques in singing, public speaking, recital, dance, drama, design, stage technology, media techniques and visual arts.
description: The Academy of Creative Arts (ACA) is an extension of the Australian Curriculum and enables students to develop skills in Making and Responding across Dance, Drama, Media Arts, Music and Visual Arts. Students enrolled in ACA are required to pay $60 per year for the Instrumental Music component of the program. Student accounts are invoiced at the beginning of the school year with full payment required by the end of Term 3. Additional events, excursions or performance requirements may incur additional costs. To be considered for entry, students must have a passion for the Arts and have achieved a minimum C standard in both English and at least one Arts subject on their most recent report card. Students should lodge an Application Form and be prepared for an interview with the Head of Department - The Arts and Junior School Director of Student Achievement. The program provides opportunities for creative thinking, performing skills and group work applicable to all career pathways.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be within the school's catchment area; Must have achieved minimum C standard in English on most recent report card; Must have achieved minimum C standard in at least one Arts subject on most recent report card; Students must have passion for the Arts
special_conditions: Students enrolled in ACA will be required to pay $60 per year for the Instrumental Music component. Full payment is required by the end of Term 3. Any additional events, excursions or performance requirements may incur additional costs. Backgrounds and differences of every student are deliberately included in ACA.

overview: Glenala State High School offers the Glenala Netball Academy (GNA), an elite sports program designed to develop students as accomplished netball athletes while maintaining strong academic performance. The academy provides expert coaching and personalised training to optimise skill development, performance and game strategy at the highest level.
description: The Glenala Netball Academy costs $100 per year and student accounts are invoiced at the beginning of the school year with full payment required by the end of Term 3. Fees include payment for selected competitions, transport for Super 7's tournaments in Terms 1, 2 and 3, and a GNA Merchandise item. Any additional events, camps or activities will incur additional costs. The exclusive program accommodates 28 students and selection involves a trial day. Students must show evidence (Report Card) that reflects appropriate effort and demonstrates high standards of attitude and behaviour. Eligibility requires academic grades reflecting appropriate effort and high standards of attitude and behaviour. To be eligible, written applications must be returned by the stated deadline with successful applicants notified within specified timeframes. The coaching team comprises highly qualified and experienced professionals including State League netball players and Southern Scorpions District specialists.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students interested in elite netball; Must be within the school's catchment area; Must show evidence on Report Card reflecting appropriate effort; Must demonstrate high standards of attitude and behaviour; Academic Performance, Attendance, Behaviour and Effort must align with sporting commitment; Exclusive program limited to 28 students
special_conditions: Scholarship subject to annual review. The Netball Academy Program is exclusive and limited to 28 students. Students must ensure that their Academic Performance, Attendance, Behaviour and Effort at school are aligned with their sporting commitment. All students involved must adhere to high expectations. A trial day may be required to assist with the selection process. Written applications must be returned no later than stated deadline with successful applicants notified within specified timeframes. Non-refundable fees include competitions, transport and merchandise.

program_name: P&C Junior School Scholarship of Excellence
overview: Glenala State High School offers the P&C Junior School Scholarship of Excellence to recognise and support outstanding achievement in junior secondary students. This scholarship is provided through the school's Parent and Citizens association.
description: The P&C Junior School Scholarship of Excellence is offered through Glenala State High School's Parent and Citizens (P&C) association. The scholarship recognises students demonstrating exceptional academic achievement and commitment to excellence. Applicants must submit a completed application to the Administration Office by specified due dates. As part of the submission process, students are required to complete an enrolment interview which can be scheduled when submitting the completed application. The exact financial value, duration and specific eligibility criteria are not fully detailed in the publicly available information, indicating this is a community-funded award program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to junior secondary students; Must be enrolled at Glenala State High School; Must demonstrate outstanding academic achievement; Completion of enrolment interview required
special_conditions: Students must submit completed application to Administration Office by due dates listed. Enrolment interview is required as part of the submission process and can be scheduled when submitting application. This scholarship is provided through the school's P&C association.

overview: Gold Coast Christian College offers Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding achievement in incoming Year 7 and Year 10 students. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability through previous performance and internal testing, with a strong commitment to learning excellence.
description: Academic Scholarships at Gold Coast Christian College are granted to students who demonstrate outstanding achievement, with eligibility determined through a comprehensive evaluation of their previous academic performance and results from internal testing. These scholarships provide up to 75% discount on tuition fees until the end of Year 9 for Year 7 students and until the end of Year 12 for Year 10 students. Applicants will be shortlisted based on their academic performance, and shortlisted candidates are invited to the school for an interview and must complete a scholarship test. Recipients must maintain an 'Above Standard' academic performance in all core subjects, including English, Mathematics, Biblical Studies, Science, and Humanities. The scholarships are open to both current and new students.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or Year 10; Demonstrated outstanding academic achievement in previous schooling; Must achieve results from internal testing demonstrating excellence; Must maintain 'Above Standard' academic performance in English, Mathematics, Biblical Studies, Science, and Humanities; Shortlisted candidates must attend interview and complete scholarship test; Open to both current and new students
special_conditions: Recipients must maintain 'Above Standard' academic performance in all core subjects throughout their scholarship period. Scholarships are provided for the duration of enrolment: until the end of Year 9 for Year 7 entry, and until the end of Year 12 for Year 10 entry. Shortlisted candidates must attend an interview and complete the school's scholarship test.

overview: Gold Coast Christian College offers Leadership Scholarships to students who demonstrate genuine passion for leadership and the motivation to enhance their leadership skills. The scholarship recognises students already taking initiative through leading clubs, overseeing projects, or driving initiatives.
description: Leadership Scholarships at Gold Coast Christian College are designed for students who are genuinely passionate about leadership and eager to enhance their skills. The scholarship provides up to 75% discount on tuition fees until the end of Year 9 for Year 7 students and until the end of Year 12 for Year 10 students. Ideal candidates are those already taking initiative, such as leading a club, overseeing a project, or driving an initiative. Preference is given to applicants with demonstrated leadership experience within their school, showing a strong commitment to making a positive impact and inspiring those around them. Shortlisted candidates are invited to attend an interview and complete a scholarship test as part of the selection process. Recipients must maintain an 'Above Standard' academic performance in core subjects.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or Year 10; Demonstrated genuine passion for leadership; Already taking initiative such as leading a club, overseeing a project, or driving an initiative; Preference given to applicants with leadership experience within their current school; Strong commitment to making a positive impact and inspiring others; Must maintain 'Above Standard' academic performance in core subjects; Open to both current and new students
special_conditions: Recipients must maintain 'Above Standard' academic performance in all core subjects throughout their scholarship period. Scholarships are provided for the duration of enrolment: until the end of Year 9 for Year 7 entry, and until the end of Year 12 for Year 10 entry. Shortlisted candidates must attend an interview and complete the school's scholarship test. Preference is given to applicants demonstrating existing leadership experience and commitment to positive impact.

overview: Gold Coast Christian College offers Co-Curricular Scholarships to recognise students who demonstrate outstanding achievement and sustained commitment in extracurricular areas such as music, sports, or visual arts. The scholarship rewards excellence in co-curricular pursuits alongside academic engagement.
description: The Co-Curricular Scholarship at Gold Coast Christian College is designed for students who demonstrate outstanding achievement and a sustained commitment in a particular extracurricular area, such as music, sports, or visual arts. Applicants must show evidence of excellence in their chosen co-curricular field and consistent dedication to developing their talents outside the classroom. The scholarship provides up to 75% discount on tuition fees until the end of Year 9 for Year 7 students and until the end of Year 12 for Year 10 students. This scholarship recognises students who have excelled in their chosen field and consistently shown dedication to developing their talents. Shortlisted candidates are invited to attend an interview and complete a scholarship test as part of the selection process. Recipients must maintain an 'Above Standard' academic performance in core subjects including English, Mathematics, Biblical Studies, Science, and Humanities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or Year 10; Demonstrated outstanding achievement in a particular extracurricular area such as music, sports, or visual arts; Sustained commitment and dedication in the chosen co-curricular field; Evidence of excellence and consistent development of talents outside the classroom; Must maintain 'Above Standard' academic performance in English, Mathematics, Biblical Studies, Science, and Humanities; Shortlisted candidates must attend interview and complete scholarship test; Open to both current and new students
special_conditions: Recipients must maintain 'Above Standard' academic performance in all core subjects throughout their scholarship period. Scholarships are provided for the duration of enrolment: until the end of Year 9 for Year 7 entry, and until the end of Year 12 for Year 10 entry. Shortlisted candidates must attend an interview and complete the school's scholarship test. Focus is on demonstrated sustained commitment and outstanding achievement in the specific extracurricular area of music, sports, or visual arts.

program_name: Fee Concession Scheme for Financial Hardship
overview: Good Counsel College offers a Fee Concession Scheme to assist families who genuinely do not have the financial capacity to pay tuition fees in full. The scheme provides fee relief to students from families experiencing financial difficulty, ensuring no child is refused a Catholic education due to verified financial hardship.
description: The Fee Concession Scheme is intended to assist families who genuinely do not have the financial capacity to pay tuition fees in full. The specific financial benefit amount is not published and varies based on individual family circumstances. Parents experiencing financial difficulty are encouraged to discuss fee concessions directly with the Principal or Parish Priest to apply for special consideration. The scheme operates in keeping with Gospel values and the school's commitment to ensuring access to education regardless of financial hardship. There are no published conditions beyond the requirement that families provide verification of genuine financial difficulty.
eligibility_criteria: All students enrolled or seeking enrolment at Good Counsel College; Families experiencing genuine financial difficulty requiring verified documentation; Must discuss application directly with Principal or Parish Priest
special_conditions: Enrolment is conditional upon commitment to pay school fees and levies as agreed. In keeping with Gospel values, no child will be refused a Catholic education due to genuine financial difficulty which has been verified by the school. Parents must raise concerns about payment of school fees at the enrolment interview.

overview: Good Counsel College offers family discounts to families with more than one child attending Catholic schools in the Diocese of Cairns. This program provides fee reductions based on family enrollment across the Catholic school system.
description: Family discounts apply for families with more than one child attending Catholic schools in the Diocese of Cairns. The specific discount amount varies and is determined by the Diocese. The discount automatically applies once multiple children from the same family are enrolled in the Catholic school system. There are no special application requirements beyond standard enrolment procedures. Tuition fees for future terms only will be adjusted once the family discount eligibility has been verified by school administration.
eligibility_criteria: More than one child enrolled in Catholic schools within the Diocese of Cairns; All Year Levels eligible
special_conditions: Discount applies to tuition fees for future terms only once the family's multiple enrollment status has been verified by school administration.

overview: Good Counsel College honors Cairns Catholic Education's means-tested concession card discount program. Families holding eligible government concession cards receive automatic tuition fee reductions as part of the Diocese of Cairns financial assistance framework.
description: Cairns Catholic Education offers a means-tested concession card discount for families who hold eligible government concession cards. The discount amount varies depending on the type of concession card held. Card holders are required to advise the school of their concession card at enrolment or when the card is gained. Once the concession card is sighted by the school's administration, tuition fees for future terms only will be adjusted. The discount is automatically applied and requires no separate application process beyond providing evidence of the concession card.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold an eligible government concession card; Card must be sighted by school administration at enrolment or when gained; All Year Levels eligible
special_conditions: Card holders must advise the school of their concession card at enrolment or when the card is gained. Tuition fees for future terms only will be adjusted once the card has been sighted by school administration.

overview: Good Shepherd Catholic College offers a concession on tuition fees for families who hold an eligible Centrelink Concession Card. This financial assistance program recognises families experiencing financial difficulty and provides fee relief on tuition costs.
description: Good Shepherd Catholic College offers a concession on tuition fees for families holding an eligible Centrelink Concession Card. The concession applies to tuition fees only and does not apply to levies or extra costs incurred for uniforms, camps, or other extras. Families must present their Centrelink Concession Card to the College office, where a copy will be taken and kept on file. It is the responsibility of families to ensure the College has a current copy of their card; if the card expires and a new card is not presented before fees are generated for a term, the concession will not be applied to that term's fees. The exact percentage of fee reduction varies based on the Centrelink card type held.
eligibility_criteria: Family must hold an eligible Centrelink Concession Card; Must present card to College office; Card must remain current; Applies to tuition fees only
special_conditions: Concession does not apply to levies or extra costs incurred for uniforms or camps. Families must maintain a current copy of their Centrelink Concession Card on file with the College office.

overview: Good Shepherd Catholic College provides fee concessions to parents and guardians who are experiencing financial difficulty or hardship. Families in genuine financial need can apply to the Principal for assistance with school fees.
description: Good Shepherd Catholic College provides fee concessions for families experiencing financial difficulty or hardship. Parents and guardians who face genuine financial challenges can arrange an appointment with the Principal to discuss their circumstances confidentially. The College recognises that financial circumstances can change and impact families, and aims to provide flexible arrangements to support access to Catholic education. Fee concessions are considered on a case-by-case basis and arrangements are made between the family and the Principal. The College operates on the principle that no family should be denied a Catholic education due to financial hardship.
eligibility_criteria: Family experiencing financial difficulty or hardship; Must arrange confidential appointment with Principal; Genuine incapacity to pay school fees
special_conditions: Application is confidential and arrangements are made on a case-by-case basis. Families must contact the Principal or Principal's Assistant to arrange an appointment.

program_name: Financial Assistance for Health Care Card Holders
overview: Good Shepherd Catholic School provides fee assistance to families who hold a means-tested Health Care Card or Pension Concession Card. The school offers discounted tuition fees to eligible families as recognition of financial need and commitment to Catholic education accessibility.
description: Families who are holders of a means-tested Health Care Card or Pension Concession Card are eligible to receive discounts applied to tuition fees at Good Shepherd Catholic School. Parents or carers must present their current card to the school office for photocopying, and must resubmit the card upon expiry during the year to retain the discount. This ongoing support ensures continuous fee relief for eligible families. The school's policy affirms that no child will be refused enrolment due to financial difficulty, and encourages families to discuss their circumstances with the school Principal on a confidential basis.
eligibility_criteria: Parent or carer must hold a current means-tested Health Care Card or Pension Concession Card; Card must be presented to school office for photocopying; Card must be resubmitted upon expiry to retain discount; Open to all families regardless of student year level
special_conditions: Discount applied to tuition fees only. Parents must present original card for photocopying to school office. Card must be resubmitted upon expiry during the school year to maintain eligibility. New and existing parents should discuss financial matters with the school Principal with understanding, sensitivity and confidentiality.

overview: Good Shepherd Catholic School offers a discounted fee structure for families with multiple children enrolled. The diocesan fee structure provides discounts for the second, third and subsequent children across both primary and secondary systemic Catholic schools in the Townsville Diocese.
description: A new fee structure implemented by Townsville Catholic Education provides discounted fees for the second, third and subsequent children in families with multiple enrolments across both secondary and primary systemic Catholic schools in the Diocese. Parents must advise the primary school office of older siblings attending other systemic Catholic schools to ensure the discount is applied correctly to their fees. This scheme recognises the financial burden of educating multiple children and provides tangible support to larger families. The discount structure applies across all year levels and is administered through the school's fee schedule.
eligibility_criteria: Family must have two or more children enrolled at Good Shepherd Catholic School or other systemic Catholic schools in Townsville Diocese; Parent must inform school office of older siblings at other Catholic schools; Applicable to second, third and subsequent children; Open to all families
special_conditions: Parent/carer must advise the primary school office in writing of older siblings attending systemic Catholic schools. Discount does not include Ignatius Park or St Patrick's Colleges. Discount automatically applied once notification is received by school office.

overview: Good Shepherd Catholic School is committed to ensuring no child is refused enrolment due to financial difficulty. The school offers confidential assistance to families experiencing financial hardship through direct discussion with the school Principal, who has discretionary authority to provide tailored support solutions.
description: Families experiencing genuine financial difficulty or hardship are encouraged to contact the school Principal to discuss their circumstances confidentially. The school's policy states that no child who meets the enrolment criteria will be refused enrolment or disadvantaged because of a family's financial circumstance. The Principal treats all applications with understanding, sensitivity and confidentiality, and exercises discretionary power to assess each family's situation on its individual merits and provide appropriate financial assistance. This program ensures that Catholic education remains accessible to all families regardless of economic circumstances, reflecting the school's mission to serve the wider community.
eligibility_criteria: Family experiencing genuine financial hardship; Family must meet school enrolment criteria; Parent/carer must initiate confidential discussion with school Principal; Open to all year levels and all families; No income threshold published
special_conditions: Applications must be made directly to school Principal. All information is treated with confidence and discretion. Assessment is made on individual merits. Each family situation considered separately. No child refused enrolment due to financial circumstances if they meet other enrolment criteria.

overview: Good Shepherd Lutheran College offers Academic Excellence Scholarships to recognise and reward outstanding achievement in incoming Year 6 to 11 students. The scholarship is awarded to students who demonstrate excellence in academic potential and achievement along with strong conduct and citizenship.
description: Academic Excellence Scholarships are offered to students entering Year 6 to Year 11 in 2026 who demonstrate excellence in academic potential and achievement. Successful Academic Scholarship candidates will typically be awarded 25% remission of tuition fees for the duration or balance of Middle Years (Years 6-9) or Senior Years (Years 10-12) provided that they meet general College expectations and any specific requirements for support of College activities in the student's area of excellence. Selection is based on Edutest written assessment results, school reports including NAPLAN results, certificates of achievement, personal introductory letter, references from school and external organisations, and evidence of academic endeavour, conduct and citizenship. The awarding and ongoing honouring of the Scholarships is at the discretion of the Principal.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 6 to Year 11; Academic performance demonstrated through school reports and NAPLAN results; Outstanding academic endeavour required; Demonstrated conduct and citizenship; Capacity to contribute to College activities
year_levels: Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered for the duration or balance of Middle Years (Years 6-9) or Senior Years (Years 10-12). Awarding and ongoing honouring is at the discretion of the Principal. Recipients must meet general College expectations and support College activities in their area of excellence. An interview may be conducted. Registration closes Friday March 2026 at 5:00pm with late applications accepted until 5:00pm AEST Monday March 2026 (incurring a late fee of $245).

overview: Good Shepherd Lutheran College offers Sporting Excellence Scholarships to recognise and reward outstanding achievement in sporting endeavours. The scholarship is awarded to students entering Year 6 to 11 who demonstrate excellence in sports.
description: Sporting Excellence Scholarships are offered to students entering Year 6 to Year 11 in 2026 who demonstrate excellence in sporting endeavours. Successful Sporting Excellence Scholarship recipients will typically be awarded 25% remission of tuition fees for the duration or balance of Middle Years (Years 6-9) or Senior Years (Years 10-12) provided that they meet general College expectations and any specific requirements for support of College activities in the student's area of excellence. Selection is based on Edutest written assessment results, school reports including NAPLAN results, certificates of achievement, personal introductory letter, history of participation in sporting endeavours, references from school and external sporting organisations, and evidence of sporting endeavour with high levels of conduct and citizenship. The awarding and ongoing honouring of the Scholarships is at the discretion of the Principal.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 6 to Year 11; Demonstrated excellence in sporting endeavours; Outstanding athletic achievement required; Evidence of high conduct and citizenship; Capacity to contribute to College activities and sports programs
year_levels: Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered for the duration or balance of Middle Years (Years 6-9) or Senior Years (Years 10-12). Awarding and ongoing honouring is at the discretion of the Principal. Recipients must meet general College expectations and support College activities in their area of excellence. Registration closes Friday March 2026 at 5:00pm with late applications accepted until 5:00pm AEST Monday March 2026 (incurring a late fee of $245).

overview: Good Shepherd Lutheran College offers Cultural Excellence Scholarships to recognise and reward outstanding achievement in cultural endeavours. The scholarship is awarded to students entering Year 6 to 11 who demonstrate excellence in music, drama, dance and other cultural pursuits.
description: Cultural Excellence Scholarships are offered to students entering Year 6 to Year 11 in 2026 who demonstrate excellence in cultural endeavours including music, drama, dance and other performing arts. Successful Cultural Excellence Scholarship recipients will typically be awarded 25% remission of tuition fees for the duration or balance of Middle Years (Years 6-9) or Senior Years (Years 10-12) provided that they meet general College expectations and any specific requirements for support of College activities in the student's area of excellence. Selection is based on Edutest written assessment results, school reports including NAPLAN results, certificates of achievement, personal introductory letter, history of participation in cultural activities, references from school and external cultural organisations, and evidence of high levels of conduct and citizenship. Applicants should demonstrate outstanding talent in playing musical instruments, singing, drama, dance or other cultural pursuits. The awarding and ongoing honouring of the Scholarships is at the discretion of the Principal.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 6 to Year 11; Demonstrated excellence in cultural endeavours (music, drama, dance, etc.); Outstanding cultural achievement required; Evidence of high conduct and citizenship; Capacity to contribute to College activities and cultural programs
year_levels: Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered for the duration or balance of Middle Years (Years 6-9) or Senior Years (Years 10-12). Awarding and ongoing honouring is at the discretion of the Principal. Recipients must meet general College expectations and support College activities in their area of excellence. Registration closes Friday March 2026 at 5:00pm with late applications accepted until 5:00pm AEST Monday March 2026 (incurring a late fee of $245).

overview: Good Shepherd Lutheran College offers General Bursaries to provide financial support to deserving students. The bursary is awarded to students entering Year 6 to 11 who demonstrate excellence in academic, sporting or cultural endeavours and have genuine financial need.
description: A General Bursary is offered to students entering Year 6 to Year 11 in 2026 who demonstrate excellence in one or more of academic, sporting or cultural endeavours where financial support of the College is required. Successful General Bursary recipients will typically be awarded 25% remission of tuition fees for the duration or balance of Middle Years (Years 6-9) or Senior Years (Years 10-12) provided that they meet general College expectations and any specific requirements for support of College activities in the student's area of excellence. Selection is based on demonstrated excellence across academic, sporting or cultural domains combined with assessed financial need. The application process includes sitting the Edutest assessment and providing evidence of excellence and financial circumstances. The awarding and ongoing honouring of the Bursary is at the discretion of the Principal.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 6 to Year 11; Demonstrated excellence in academic, sporting or cultural endeavours; Evidence of financial need/hardship required; High conduct and citizenship; Capacity to contribute to College activities
year_levels: Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Bursaries are offered for the duration or balance of Middle Years (Years 6-9) or Senior Years (Years 10-12). Awarding and ongoing honouring is at the discretion of the Principal. Recipients must meet general College expectations and support College activities in their area of excellence. A Confidential Parents' Bursary Application Form is available by contacting the College Registrar. Registration closes Friday March 2026 at 5:00pm with late applications accepted until 5:00pm AEST Monday March 2026 (incurring a late fee of $245).

overview: Good Shepherd Lutheran College offers the Principal's Bursary as a special bursary program for deserving students. The bursary is awarded to students entering Year 10 or 11 who demonstrate excellence in academic, sporting or cultural endeavours and have genuine financial need.
description: A Principal's Bursary is offered to students not currently attending Good Shepherd Lutheran College who are entering Year 10 or Year 11 in 2026, demonstrating excellence in one or more of academic, sporting or cultural endeavours where financial support of the College is required. The Principal's Bursary recipient will typically be awarded 25% remission of tuition fees for the duration or balance of Senior Years (Years 10-12) provided students meet general College expectation and any specific requirements for support of College activities in the student's area of excellence. Selection is based on demonstrated excellence combined with assessed financial need. The application process includes sitting the Edutest assessment and providing evidence of excellence and financial circumstances. The awarding and ongoing honouring of the Bursary is at the discretion of the Principal.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students not currently enrolled at GSLC; Must be entering Year 10 or Year 11; Demonstrated excellence in academic, sporting or cultural endeavours; Evidence of financial need/hardship required; High conduct and citizenship; Capacity to contribute to College activities
special_conditions: Bursaries are offered for the duration or balance of Senior Years (Years 10-12). Open to students not currently attending GSLC only. Awarding and ongoing honouring is at the discretion of the Principal. Recipients must meet general College expectations and support College activities in their area of excellence. A Confidential Parents' Bursary Application Form is available by contacting the College Registrar. Registration closes Friday March 2026 at 5:00pm with late applications accepted until 5:00pm AEST Monday March 2026 (incurring a late fee of $245).

overview: Grand Avenue State School offers the STAR+X Academic Excellence Program to high-achieving students with a passion for STEM. The program is designed to unlock student potential through fostering a culture of curiosity, resilience, and passion for learning.
description: The STAR+X Academic Excellence Program is a selective academic extension program for gifted and talented students. Students participate in advanced STEM learning incorporating creative science projects and technology-rich activities. The program builds on evidence-based approaches including explicit teaching, the Gradual Release of Responsibility model, and the 6Cs framework (Critical Thinking, Collaboration, Communication, Creativity, Character and Citizenship). Entry is through application and academic testing, with the annual exam held in October. The program is open to both in-catchment and out-of-catchment students. Limited places are available.
eligibility_criteria: Students in Years 4 and 5; Open to both in-catchment and out-of-catchment students; Academic excellence and demonstrated passion for STEM required; Application and academic testing required
special_conditions: Limited places available in the excellence program. Families interested in the STAR+X Program can apply online and register for the annual October exam. Open to both in-catchment and out-of-catchment students.

school_name: Groves Christian College - Upper Primary Campus
Gulf Christian College - Thompson Street Campus
overview: Gulf Christian College offers student bursaries to families who need financial assistance with paying the Tuition Fees and have a genuine desire for their child to attend a Christian school. The bursary program is designed to support families facing financial hardship.
description: Gulf Christian College offers student bursaries to families experiencing financial difficulty who wish to enrol their children in the school. The bursaries are designed to assist with tuition fee payments and are available to families who demonstrate genuine commitment to Christian education. To apply for a bursary, families are required to contact the College Principal directly to discuss the bursaries available and to establish whether their family qualifies. The program operates on a case-by-case basis, with the Principal reviewing each application individually. Financial assistance is provided subject to the College's assessment of demonstrated financial need and commitment to the school's Christian ethos.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students; Genuine desire for child to attend a Christian school; Financial need must be demonstrated; Application by contacting the College Principal
special_conditions: Contact the College Principal to discuss bursaries and establish qualification eligibility. Bursaries are assessed on a case-by-case basis. Families must have demonstrated genuine desire for their child to attend the Christian school.

overview: Gulf Christian College offers student bursaries to families who need financial assistance with paying the Tuition Fees and have a genuine desire for their child to attend a Christian school. The program provides financial support to eligible families to enable their children to access quality Christian education.
description: Gulf Christian College provides bursaries for families experiencing financial difficulty who wish to enroll their children at the college. The bursary program is designed to assist with tuition fee payments for students who demonstrate genuine commitment to attending the school and receive demonstrated financial assistance. Prospective families are encouraged to contact the College Principal directly to discuss available bursary options and to determine eligibility based on their individual circumstances. The college aims to ensure that financial constraints do not prevent suitable students from accessing its Christ-centered educational programs. Support varies based on individual family circumstances and available funding.
eligibility_criteria: Families who need financial assistance with paying tuition fees; Genuine desire for child to attend a Christian school; Contact with Principal required to establish eligibility
special_conditions: Applicants must contact the College Principal directly to discuss bursaries on offer and establish if their family qualifies for assistance. Bursary eligibility is determined through individual discussion with the Principal based on demonstrated financial need and commitment to the school's Christian ethos.

program_name: Fee Concession for Families Experiencing Financial Hardship
overview: Guardian Angels' Primary School offers fee concessions to families experiencing financial hardship. Applications for concession of fees may be made by families facing genuine financial difficulty, providing support to ensure all students can access quality Catholic education at the school.
description: Guardian Angels' Primary School recognises that families may experience financial hardship and provides a formal process for applying for fee concessions to reduce the financial burden of school fees. The school's fees and levies are essential for providing high-quality education, including teaching and administrative staff, resources, materials, facilities, and maintenance of buildings and grounds. Families experiencing difficulty meeting their school fee commitments can submit an application for fee concession. The amount of concession available is determined on a case-by-case basis depending on family circumstances and the school's capacity to assist. The application form is available from the Finance Office or via the Parent Portal. Applications are reviewed individually to provide appropriate financial assistance.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing genuine financial hardship; Currently enrolled students or prospective families; Application submission required with supporting documentation
special_conditions: It is every family's responsibility to meet their school fee commitments. Failure to do so means the school is entitled to seek reimbursement through debt collection or legal means. Applications must be made to the Finance Office and are assessed on individual merit. Application forms are available from the Finance Office or via the Parent Portal.

overview: Guardian Angels' Catholic Primary School offers fee concessions to families experiencing financial hardship. The school applies a compassionate and just approach, consistent with Brisbane Catholic Education principles, to assess applications for fee relief. Families facing genuine financial difficulty are invited to approach the school confidentially.
description: Guardian Angels' Catholic Primary School provides fee concessions for families experiencing financial hardship due to unexpected events or unforeseen changes in financial circumstances. Concessions are issued for a maximum period of 12 months within a calendar year and can be applied for at the commencement of each year or at any point initiated by the family. A compassionate and just approach under the mission and values of Brisbane Catholic Education and Guardian Angels Primary School is used when reviewing applications. Concession application forms are available at the school finance office or online via the Parent Portal. All matters are dealt with on a confidential basis. Concessions need to be submitted annually in Term 1, and any late submissions are unable to be backdated.
eligibility_criteria: Family experiencing financial hardship; Unexpected events or unforeseen changes in family circumstances; Genuine inability to pay school fees; Application must be submitted in Term 1 for annual concessions or at any point when hardship is experienced
special_conditions: Concessions are issued for a maximum period of 12 months within a calendar year. Concession application forms are available at the school finance office or online via the Parent Portal. All matters are dealt with on a confidential basis. Concessions need to be submitted annually in Term 1 and any late submissions are unable to be backdated. No student will be refused enrolment because of a family's genuine inability to pay fees.

program_name: Accelerated Academic Academy Program of Excellence
overview: Harristown State High School's Academic Academy provides students with programs of extension and enrichment in their identified areas of ability in Mathematics, English, Science and Humanities. The academy allows capable students to participate in academic competitions and enrichment activities while potentially accelerating through learning areas as gifted and talented students.
description: The Academic Academy aims to provide students with a program of extension and enrichment in their identified areas of ability, specifically in Mathematics and/or English and/or Science and/or Humanities. Students in the academy have opportunities to participate in a variety of academic competitions and enrichment activities. Selected students may accelerate through learning areas where identified as potentially gifted and talented, and may commence study of University subjects or other school-endorsed options in Year 12. The program operates for students identified as demonstrating high academic ability across multiple subject areas. Entry into the academy is selective and based on demonstrated performance, behaviour and effort reviewed by Heads of Department during Semester 2 of Year 7, with classes beginning at the start of Year 8. No financial assistance is provided as this is a public school; however, students benefit from specialised extension curriculum and enrichment activities.
eligibility_criteria: Interested students participate in entry process during Semester 2 Year 7; Must demonstrate high level of ability in focus areas; Must achieve acceptable academic standards in units studied; Must remain highly motivated; Must demonstrate satisfactory behaviour and effort; Entry process led by relevant curriculum area Head of Department
special_conditions: Student performance, behaviour and effort will be reviewed by the Heads of Department. Students will be expected to demonstrate a high level of ability in the focus areas of the academy and achieve acceptable academic standards in units studied. This review process will be conducted by the Head of Department and staff within the Academy.

overview: Harristown State High School offers a Sports Academy Program of Excellence founded in 1993, providing students with the opportunity to combine the study of their chosen sport at a high level with their academic studies. The academy includes specialised training in Football (Soccer), Volleyball, Basketball, Rugby League and Netball, with students receiving coaching from highly specialised teachers.
description: The Sports Academy programs provide students with the opportunity to combine the study of their chosen sport at a high level, with their academic studies. Sports specialisation is offered for both girls and boys in Football (soccer), Volleyball, Basketball, Rugby League and Netball. The academy has strong focus on skills, strategies, strength and conditioning as well as building character in students so they can best serve their teammates and make ongoing contributions to school and wider community. Through the Sports Academy, the school has seen students go on to study on sporting scholarships in the USA as well as go on to play at the professional level, even representing their country. Students study their chosen sport as a subject while completing their standard curriculum requirements. No financial assistance is provided as this is a public school; the program provides specialised coaching and curriculum integration focused on high-level sport development.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students wishing to specialise in Football (soccer), Volleyball, Basketball, Rugby League or Netball; Co-educational program open to both girls and boys; Selection based on demonstrated sporting ability and potential; Capacity allocated through School Enrolment Management Plan with 140 places in Programs of Excellence
special_conditions: Places in the Programs of Excellence are allocated through the School Enrolment Management Plan. Out-of-catchment students applying for the Sports Academy must satisfy the school's criteria for placement in that particular Program of Excellence. In-catchment student demand must be met first before out-of-catchment places are allocated. School reserves the right to restrict out-of-catchment enrolments to ensure in-catchment students can enrol at their local state school.

overview: Harristown State High School's Industrial Design and Technology Academy allows students to extend their minds when developing and designing material objects with focus on practical capabilities. The academy serves as an introduction to senior subjects of Engineering, Furnishing and Construction.
description: The Industrial Design and Technology Academy provides students with specialised curriculum focused on developing and designing material objects. The program emphasises extending students' minds through hands-on practical work and developing their practical capabilities in engineering, design and technology. Enrolment in the academy allows students to develop foundational skills and knowledge that serve as an introduction to senior subjects of Engineering, Furnishing and Construction. The program operates within Harristown's four specialised academies spanning academic, industrial, sporting and arts fields. No financial assistance is provided as this is a public school; students benefit from access to specialised industrial design and technology facilities including the Trade Training Centre and creative technology centre. Students apply during their Year 7 studies for entry beginning in Year 8.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students interested in Industrial Design and Technology; Must apply during Year 7 for entry in Year 8; Based on demonstrated interest and aptitude in design and technology fields; Part of the school's four academy specialisations
special_conditions: Enrolment in the academy allows students to extend their minds when developing and designing material objects with focus on improving practical capabilities. Program serves as introduction to senior subjects of Engineering, Furnishing and Construction.

overview: Harristown State High School's Arts Academy offers highly regarded arts programs with comprehensive curriculum in Dance, Drama, Film, Television and New Media, Visual Art and Music from Years 7-12. Students are taught by specialist teachers with access to co-curricular instrumental music tuition and opportunities for public performances and competitions.
description: The Arts Programs at Harristown are very highly regarded with a full complement of Arts subjects including Dance, Drama, Film, Television and New Media, Visual Art and Music being offered from Years 7-12. The school's co-curricular Instrumental Music program offers students tuition in woodwind, brass, percussion and bass guitar. The variety of extra-curricular activities on offer provide students with the opportunity to extend and showcase their talents through involvement in public performances, exhibitions and competitions. Arts specialisation is available as one of four academies within the school alongside academic, industrial and sports specialisations. No financial assistance is provided as this is a public school; students benefit from access to specialised arts facilities including an auditorium for 1500 people, 330-seat tiered multimedia theatre, editing suites and dance studios.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students interested in Dance, Drama, Film, Television, New Media, Visual Art or Music; Arts subjects offered from Years 7-12; Co-curricular instrumental music tuition available in woodwind, brass, percussion and bass guitar; Application during Year 7 for Year 8 entry
special_conditions: Arts Academy is one of four specialised academies within the school. Specialist teachers provide tuition in all arts disciplines. Extra-curricular activities provide opportunities for public performances, exhibitions and competitions. Full complement of arts subjects available from Years 7-12.

school_name: Townsville-Thuringowa Centre for Continuing Secondary Education

overview: Hillcrest Christian College offers scholarships to contribute to the richness and diversity of the College's community. The Middle Learning Community Scholarship is available to students entering Years 5-8 who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and character aligned with the College's values.
description: Hillcrest Christian College offers a scholarship program designed to foster diversity and ensure that a Hillcrest education is accessible to students who will contribute meaningfully to the College community. Scholarships are available in the Middle Learning Community (Years 5-8) for students who demonstrate academic excellence and personal character. The scholarship program seeks to offer places to students who reflect the College's values of excellence in Christ. Specific financial benefits, selection processes, and application timelines should be confirmed directly with the College Enrolments Team.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 5-8; Must demonstrate academic excellence; Must display personal character aligned with Hillcrest's School Values; Open to current and external students
special_conditions: Scholarships exist to foster diversity by ensuring that an education at Hillcrest Christian College is available to as many students as possible. Recipients are expected to contribute positively to the College community.

overview: Hillcrest Christian College offers scholarships to students in the Senior Learning Community (Years 9-12). These scholarships are designed to reward students who demonstrate exceptional academic achievement and align with the College's Christian values and commitment to diversity.
description: The Senior Learning Community Scholarship program at Hillcrest Christian College provides opportunities for talented students in Years 9-12 to access a quality Christian education. Scholarships are awarded to students who demonstrate strong academic potential, personal character, and commitment to learning, supporting the College's mission to foster diversity and ensure educational access. The scholarship program recognises students who will contribute to the richness and diversity of the College's community through their character and commitment to excellence. Recipients must maintain high standards of achievement and conduct throughout their enrolment.
