eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 9-12; Must demonstrate academic excellence; Must display personal character aligned with Hillcrest's School Values; Open to current and external students
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered to foster diversity and ensure access to a Hillcrest education. Recipients are expected to maintain commitment to the College and its values.

overview: The Bright Futures Scholarship is established to ensure that a Hillcrest education remains accessible to students of character and promise whose families may not have the financial means to fund it independently. The program supports students who demonstrate strong academic potential, commitment to personal growth, and alignment with College values.
description: The Bright Futures Scholarship was established by the Hillcrest Foundation to provide financial assistance to students who demonstrate academic potential, personal character aligned with Hillcrest's School Values, and commitment to their education, with priority given to students for whom financial support would significantly increase access to Christian schooling. Recipients are expected to reflect Hillcrest's core values, particularly Courage, including personal integrity, resilience in the face of challenges, and willingness to grow spiritually, academically, and socially. The scholarship provides more than financial assistance; it opens the door to a holistic, Christ-centred education where students are emotionally supported and nurtured academically and spiritually through dynamic learning environments, mentorship, and community of faith. Selection is made by a scholarship panel that includes College leadership.
eligibility_criteria: Must demonstrate academic potential; Must display personal character aligned with Hillcrest's School Values; Must show commitment to education; Priority given to students for whom financial support would significantly increase access to Christian schooling; Open to all year levels
special_conditions: Recipients are expected to reflect Hillcrest's core values with particular emphasis on Courage, including personal integrity, resilience in the face of challenges, and willingness to grow spiritually, academically, and socially. All donations to the Bright Futures Scholarship Fund are fully tax-deductible. 100% of contributions go directly to student support with no portion retained for administration or overhead.

overview: Hillcrest Christian College offers a dedicated Gifted and Talented program to cater for students who have been identified as significantly more advanced academically than their age group. The program recognises students with advanced cognitive abilities requiring higher-level learning opportunities.
description: Hillcrest Christian College offers full and part-time Gifted and Talented programs in the Junior Learning Community and a part-time program in the Senior Learning Community. The College understands that highly-gifted students have the capacity for higher-level learning and thought and require a more challenging academic curriculum tailored to their needs. In recognition of diverse learning needs of gifted students, the College provides tailored programs and support options informed by Professor Gagné's Model of Giftedness and Talent and Renzulli's three-ring model. The College acknowledges that some gifted students may be twice exceptional or face barriers contributing to underachievement, and thoughtfully integrates these considerations into programs and support strategies. All students have their own Individual Learning Profile allowing teachers to tailor programs for specific areas of giftedness. Gifted students can also apply for the Lumos Gifted & Talented Program.
eligibility_criteria: Must be identified as significantly more advanced academically than age group; Open to all year levels; Assessment based on cognitive abilities and academic potential
special_conditions: Program is designed to provide advanced cognitive and academic support. Students receive individual learning profiles tailored to their giftedness. Full and part-time options available depending on year level.

overview: APEX Science is a selective program for students Years 7-9 with strong capability and interest in science. The program provides students with deeper curriculum understanding and higher levels of application in science through specialist teachers and rigorous academics.
description: The APEX Science program exists for students from Years 7 to 9, giving opportunities to access the curriculum at a deeper level of understanding and with higher levels of application. Students work with specialist teachers in Biology, Chemistry and/or Physics using curriculum aimed to extend academic rigour of Senior Science subjects. Year 7 students work with the same teacher for both Mathematics and Science, allowing integration of numeracy skills into science curriculum. Students are introduced to assessment modes and skill sets needed for senior science pathways in Years 11-12. Entry is selective based on merit, demonstrated through a Mathematics/Science enrichment evening for Year 6 students featuring team and individual challenges in higher order thinking.
eligibility_criteria: Current Year 6 student; Demonstrated higher order thinking skills in Mathematics and Science; Invitation to participate in enrichment evening; Acceptance based on merit
special_conditions: Entry is selective, with acceptance being on merit. Selection process includes participation in Mathematics/Science enrichment evening featuring team and individual challenges.

overview: APEX English and Humanities is a selective program for high-achieving students Years 7-10 with demonstrated love of writing and passion for inquiry. The program provides extended learning opportunities and specialist curriculum to extend academic achievement.
description: Students in APEX English and Humanities are provided with extended learning opportunities and specialist curriculum to extend their academic achievement. APEX students have excellent work ethic, are highly motivated, achieve high results, and have interest in expanding experiences in both English and Humanities. The program is available to students in years 7 through to 10. Year 7 and Year 8 APEX students work with the same teacher for English and Humanities, allowing students to integrate learning and assessment with cross-curricular extension. Students have opportunity to continue in the program until year 10. The program offers a rigorous approach to curriculum and assessment while developing skills for Years 11-12 English. Students participate in community events to showcase learning and access real-world learning opportunities, including designing proposals for redevelopment projects and presenting to local government officials.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated love of writing; Passion for inquiry; Ability to come up with interesting ideas; High motivation and work ethic; Acceptance based on APEX application process; Entry after demonstrating achievement in year 7
special_conditions: Entry is selective, with acceptance being on merit. Students must demonstrate love of writing and passion for inquiry through application process. Continuation in the program requires sustained high achievement.

overview: The STEM Academy provides students with passion for Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics with learning opportunities from Year 7 through Year 10. The program focuses on real-world application with emphasis on sustainability and prepares students for senior engineering and technology subjects.
description: The STEM Academy at Hervey Bay State High School focuses on real-world application, with emphasis on sustainability. STEM is about creating a better future for communities by using technology to make creative solutions to real-world problems. Students in the STEM Academy gain knowledge and skills to make them shapers of the future. The program provides learning opportunities from year 7 through to year 10. In Year 7, students take part in a non-assessable enrichment program. In years 8-10, students are provided with challenging and real-life learning experiences aligned with Australian Curriculum. Students are involved in projects including a fully operational miniature wind farm, underwater drones for ocean floor mapping, automated farming practices, coding and robotics. Students may be provided with opportunities to succeed outside the classroom through competitions and external events. Enrolment gives students skills to be successful in academically challenging senior subjects, including General Engineering (Systems).
eligibility_criteria: Passion for Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics; Demonstrated interest in sustainability; Acceptance based on merit; Open to Year 7 entry
special_conditions: Entry is selective, with acceptance being on merit. Year 7 students participate in non-assessable enrichment program. Years 8-10 students engage in project-based learning with real-world application focus.

overview: The Performing Arts (Music) Academy is designed to deepen student knowledge and understanding of music theory and practice. The program engages students in enriching and collaborative experiences, available to Year 7 students who have already developed sound music reading and performance skills.
description: The Performing Arts (Music) Academy is designed to deepen student knowledge and understanding of music theory and practice, and engages students in enriching and collaborative experiences. The program is designed to develop and extend students in the areas of music performance, composition and analysis. It challenges and engages instrumental students who have already developed sound music reading and performance skills. The program provides students with pathways to achieve future QCE success through AMEB (Australian Music Examination Board) exams, with students engaging with AMEB level one curriculum to develop skills for Grade One Theory. Enrolment gives students foundations to be successful in future arts subjects including Music and Music Extension. The program also provides opportunity to create partnerships and pathways with primary school instrumental students. The program is available to Year 7 students who wish to engage in rich and rewarding music experiences in addition to their involvement in the instrumental program.
eligibility_criteria: Year 7 entry; Sound music reading skills; Established music performance skills; Participation in instrumental program; Acceptance based on demonstrated musical ability
special_conditions: Entry is selective, with acceptance being on merit. Students must demonstrate established music reading and performance skills. Participation in instrumental program is required.

overview: The Performing Arts (Dance and Drama) Academy provides a positive learning environment for students to broaden and develop their performance skills beyond Australian Curriculum expectations. The program offers opportunities for students passionate about the arts to extend skills and techniques in preparation for careers in the Performing Arts industry.
description: The Performing Arts (Dance and Drama) Academy provides a positive learning environment for students to broaden and develop their performance skills beyond the expectations of the Australian Curriculum. Students are provided with many opportunities to develop their skills in performance and are invited to join the school's Performance Team. A range of performance opportunities are available to students in the academy, including school and wider community events. Enrolment gives students foundations to be successful in future arts subjects, including Dance and Drama. The program provides numerous fantastic opportunities for students passionate about the Arts looking to extend their skills and techniques in preparation for careers in the Performing Arts industry. Students develop skills to become strong performers and develop a deep appreciation for the performing arts.
eligibility_criteria: Passion for performing arts; Dance and/or Drama experience; Demonstrated performance ability; Acceptance based on merit and audition
special_conditions: Entry is selective, with acceptance being on merit. Audition process required. Students are invited to join the school's Performance Team and participate in performances.

overview: The Global Leadership Academy provides students with passion for collaboration, community and making an impact with valuable experiences both in and out of the classroom. The program begins in Year 7 to develop future leaders and is designed to provide students enriching and engaging leadership opportunities.
description: At Hervey Bay State High School, the school takes pride in providing students enriching and engaging leadership opportunities. The leadership program begins in year 7, providing students experiences that will assist them throughout their schooling and beyond. The goal is to provide a solid educational program in effort to develop the future leaders of the world. The Global Leadership Academy provides students with a passion for collaboration, community and making an impact valuable experiences both in and out of the classroom. This program, provided to year 7 students for one semester, is a non-assessable enrichment program that has been guided by the Leaders of Evolution program and Young Leaders in Schools – Advanced e-learning course. Students in the program receive a head start into the school's leadership program structure which includes three tiers: gold, silver and bronze recognition. Students receive rewards for participation in community events.
eligibility_criteria: Year 7 entry; Passion for collaboration and community; Interest in leadership; Acceptance based on merit; Open to all backgrounds
special_conditions: Entry is selective, with acceptance being on merit. Program runs for one semester in Year 7 as non-assessable enrichment. Students gain head start into broader school leadership program with three-tier recognition system.

overview: The Entrepreneurship & Innovation Academy is a Signature Program for Year 7 entry. The program provides students with opportunities to develop business acumen, entrepreneurial thinking and innovation skills to prepare for future business and career pathways.
description: The Entrepreneurship & Innovation Academy is a Signature Program at Hervey Bay State High School for students entering Year 7. The program is designed to develop entrepreneurial thinking, innovation skills and business acumen. Students in the program engage with business-focused curriculum and real-world learning experiences. The academy aims to inspire and support students to shape their preferred futures through developing specialist programs. The program provides a closely-knit community which motivates students to develop and improve their academic and social skills. Students develop strong partnerships with local businesses and corporations to facilitate real-world experiences. The program develops and maintains a progressive technological operating environment to help students acquire necessary knowledge and abilities in business and entrepreneurship. Acceptance is based on merit through the selective entry process.
eligibility_criteria: Year 7 entry; Interest in entrepreneurship and business; Demonstrated innovation thinking; Acceptance based on merit and application
special_conditions: Entry is selective, with acceptance being on merit. This is a Signature Program for Year 7 2026 entry. Students develop partnerships with local businesses and corporations for real-world learning experiences.

overview: Holland Park State High School offers the P and C Scholarship to Year 6 students from local primary schools who demonstrate commitment to their studies and ability to contribute to school culture. The scholarship provides financial assistance towards school costs including the Resource Scheme and uniforms.
description: The P and C Scholarship is designed to support students entering Year 7 who have demonstrated strong academic commitment and leadership potential. The scholarship will cover $150-$300 towards the Resource Scheme and $50-$100 towards uniforms. Recipients are selected by members of the school leadership team including the Principal based on demonstrated commitment to studies, ability to contribute to school culture as a conscientious leader, and strong capacity for active involvement across a range of school activities with high level of conduct. Potential candidates are expected and encouraged to embody the school's three core values of learning, respect and co-operation. All considerations are private and confidential.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 6 students from local primary schools; Must have demonstrated commitment to studies; Must demonstrate ability to contribute to school culture as a conscientious leader; Must show strong capacity for active involvement across school activities; Must maintain high level of conduct; Must embody school values of learning, respect and co-operation
value_aud: $150-$300 towards Resource Scheme and $50-$100 towards uniforms
special_conditions: Scholarships are selected by members of the school leadership team including the Principal. All considerations are private and confidential, and no correspondence will be entered into once decisions have been made. Recipients must continue to embody school values and maintain good conduct.

overview: Hills International College offers the Attitude and Effort Scholarship to students from Prep to Year 12. This scholarship recognises students who may not qualify for academic scholarships based solely on grades but demonstrate positive attitude and sustained effort towards learning, providing access to quality personalised education.
description: The Attitude and Effort Scholarship is designed to support students who demonstrate commitment to learning through positive attitude and effort rather than academic grades alone. The scholarship provides financial relief from tuition fees and is available to all students from Prep to Year 12. The College recognises that not all students achieve excellence through traditional academic measures, but all students can thrive in a positive and engaging learning environment. Recipients are assessed by application, and the scholarship recognises the individual's capacity to benefit from Hills College's quality education and supportive community environment. The financial benefit and exact value varies depending on individual circumstances and College assessment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students from Prep to Year 12; Currently enrolled or entering student; Demonstrated positive attitude towards learning; Sustained effort in academic work; Can be applied for at time of enrolment or annually as ongoing student
special_conditions: Scholarships are available at the time of enrolment and annually as ongoing students. Recipients must maintain positive attitude and effort towards learning. Scholarships provide financial relief from tuition fees.

overview: Hills International College offers Academic Scholarships to students in Years 8 to 12 based on exceptional academic achievement. This scholarship recognises students who demonstrate strong academic ability and commitment to their studies.
description: Academic Scholarships are available to students in Years 8 to 12 who demonstrate exceptional academic achievement and sustained commitment to learning. The scholarship provides financial relief from tuition fees as assessed by application. These scholarships are designed to attract and support academically talented students and to enable them to access Hills College's quality educational programs. The exact financial benefit varies based on individual academic performance and College assessment. Recipients are selected through the application and assessment process which evaluates academic excellence and potential.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 8 to 12; Strong academic performance; Demonstrated commitment to learning; Application-based assessment
special_conditions: Limited to Years 8 to 12 students. Available at time of enrolment and annually for ongoing students. Financial relief from tuition fees based on assessed application.

overview: Hills International College works closely with the Australian Defence Force to offer a bursary program for worthy students. This partnership recognises the contribution of Defence Force families and provides financial support to eligible students.
description: Hills International College has established a partnership with the Australian Defence Force to provide bursary support for worthy students. This bursary is designed to support students from Defence Force families or those with connections to the Australian Defence Force. The program provides financial assistance to help eligible students access quality education at Hills College. The exact financial value and eligibility criteria are assessed on a case-by-case basis through application and consideration of individual circumstances. Recipients benefit from financial relief of fees to enable participation in the College's comprehensive academic and co-curricular programs.
eligibility_criteria: Connection to Australian Defence Force; Demonstrated need for financial support; Assessed as 'worthy' based on College criteria; Application-based selection
special_conditions: Partnership with Australian Defence Force. Bursary awarded to worthy students demonstrating both potential and financial need. Financial support varies based on individual circumstances and College assessment.

overview: Highland Reserve State School offers an Academic Excellence Program as part of its Programs of Excellence designed to challenge and support students with exceptional academic abilities in their Primary School learning and prepare them for advanced secondary pathways.
description: The Academic Excellence Program is a selective program offered at Highland Reserve State School designed to identify and develop students with exceptional academic potential. This program challenges students through differentiated curriculum and advanced learning experiences that extend beyond standard classroom instruction. The program is part of the school's commitment to maximizing student learning potential through personalised learning pathways. Students selected for this program are linked to secondary school pathways of their choice, particularly Excellence programs available in Year 7. The program operates throughout the student's enrolment and is subject to annual review based on ongoing academic performance and engagement.
eligibility_criteria: Students with exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning; Demonstrated high academic performance; Selection through identification process (criteria not publicly specified)
special_conditions: This is a selective program requiring identification and selection by the school. Students participate in advanced learning experiences and are linked to secondary school pathways. Continuation in the program may be subject to maintaining academic standards.

overview: Highland Reserve State School offers a Music Excellence Program as part of its Programs of Excellence designed to challenge and develop students with demonstrated musical talent through instrumental and ensemble instruction, including Strings, Band, Rock School, and Guitar Academy.
description: The Music Excellence Program at Highland Reserve State School provides advanced musical instruction and performance opportunities for students with musical talent and commitment. The program includes multiple instrumental pathways including Strings (Years 3-6), Band (Years 4-6), Rock School (Years 5-6, audition-based), and Guitar Academy (Years 5-6). Students learn to read and interpret notation, perform individually and in groups, and develop technical musical skills through weekly lessons. The Strings program focuses on violin, viola, cello and double bass instruments in a range of sizes to accommodate different year levels. The Band program covers orchestral instruments including woodwind, brass and percussion. Rock School is an auditioned-based excellence program emphasizing skill, attendance and enthusiasm in rock band performance.
eligibility_criteria: Musical interest and demonstrated ability; Age-appropriate (Strings: Years 3-6; Band: Years 4-6; Rock School: Years 5-6; Guitar Academy: Years 5-6); For Rock School: must audition with vocals, guitar, bass, drums or keyboard; Attendance and engagement requirements
special_conditions: Recruitment for Strings and Band programs usually starts in Term 4 for commencement in the following year. Rock School requires audition. Weekly lessons are 30 minutes for Strings program. Students in Rock School are expected to demonstrate skill, attendance and enthusiasm. Guitar Academy is based on rock school exam books and delivers modules over two-year periods.

overview: Holy Spirit College offers fee concession assistance to eligible families experiencing financial hardship. The college is committed to ensuring that genuine inability to pay fees does not compromise a student's enrolment.
description: Holy Spirit College provides fee concessions for eligible families facing short or long-term genuine inability to pay school fees. The concession program is administered through the college finance office on a confidential basis. Concession applications are accepted at the commencement of each year or when financial hardship within the family occurs. Concessions are issued for a maximum period of 12 months within a calendar year, with consideration for subsequent 12-month periods requiring new applications. The amount of concession varies based on demonstrated financial need. Interested families should contact the college business manager or finance office to discuss their circumstances and obtain a concession application form.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing genuine financial hardship; Families facing short or long-term inability to pay fees; Applications accepted at commencement of each year or when hardship occurs
special_conditions: All matters are dealt with on a confidential basis. Concessions are issued for a maximum period of 12 months within a calendar year. Consideration for a subsequent 12-month period will require a new application. Families may contact the college finance office to discuss individual circumstances.

program_name: Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund Bursary
overview: The Mary MacKillop Education Fund Bursary is available to eligible families seeking enrolment at Brisbane Catholic Education schools including Holy Spirit College. Bursaries are awarded based on demonstrated financial hardship, independent of academic ability.
description: The Mary MacKillop Education Fund Bursary is administered by the Catholic Foundation and is available to families enrolling at Brisbane Catholic Education schools, including Holy Spirit College. Bursaries are awarded on the basis of demonstrable, means-tested inability to afford Catholic education. Support from the fund is not dependent on the academic ability of the student. The bursary covers tuition fees, with the extent of support determined by assessed financial need. Applications must be submitted by 15 April for enrolment in the following school year, though late expressions of interest may be considered depending on fund availability. The committee meets in May each year to award bursaries for the following school year.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing significant financial hardship; Living within the Archdiocese of Brisbane; Students enrolling at a Brisbane Catholic Education school; Not currently enrolled at a Brisbane Catholic Education school; Demonstrated means-tested inability to afford Catholic education
special_conditions: Students currently enrolled in a Brisbane Catholic Education school are not eligible to apply for a MacKillop Bursary at their current school; enrolled students should contact their school principal for financial assistance. Application deadline is 15 April for the following school year. Late applications may be considered if funds are available. Healthcare card copy may be requested.

overview: Hillcrest Christian College offers the Bright Futures Scholarship to ensure that a quality Christian education remains accessible to students of character and promise whose families may not have the financial means to fund it independently. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate strong academic potential, personal character aligned with the College's values, and commitment to their education.
description: The Bright Futures Scholarship was established to support students demonstrating academic potential, personal character aligned with Hillcrest's School Values, and a commitment to their education. Priority is given to students for whom financial support would significantly increase access to Christian schooling. Recipients are expected to reflect Hillcrest's core values, with a particular emphasis on Courage, including personal integrity, resilience in the face of challenges, and a willingness to grow spiritually, academically, and socially. The scholarship provides financial assistance and opens the door to holistic, Christ-centred education with emotional support and academic and spiritual nurturing. Selection is made by a scholarship panel that includes College leadership. Applications are assessed based on academic potential, demonstrated need, and a personal statement from the student. The exact financial value varies based on individual circumstances and donor support.
eligibility_criteria: Students must demonstrate academic potential; Must demonstrate personal character aligned with Hillcrest's School Values; Must show commitment to their education; Priority given to students for whom financial support would significantly increase access to Christian schooling; Open to all year levels; Must align with Hillcrest's core value of Courage, including personal integrity, resilience, and willingness to grow spiritually, academically, and socially
special_conditions: Selection is made by a scholarship panel that includes College leadership. Applications are assessed based on academic potential, demonstrated need, and a personal statement from the student. Confidentiality is respected throughout the process. The scholarship is more than financial assistance—it opens the door to a holistic, Christ-centred education with dynamic learning environments, mentorship, and a community of faith.

overview: Hillcrest Christian College offers scholarships to students entering its Middle Learning Community (Years 5-8). These scholarships are designed to foster diversity and ensure that a Hillcrest education is available to students who will contribute to the richness and diversity of the College community.
description: Hillcrest Christian College offers a number of scholarships to current and external students. The scholarship program exists to foster diversity by ensuring that an education at Hillcrest Christian College is available to as many students as possible. Scholarships are available in the Middle Learning Community for Years 5-8. The scholarship program seeks to offer places to students who will contribute to the richness and diversity of the College's community. These scholarships recognise academic excellence and character qualities that align with the College's Christian values and commitment to developing well-rounded students who excel across multiple areas of school life.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 5-8; Current and external students welcome to apply; Must demonstrate qualities that will contribute to the richness and diversity of the College community; Academic and character-based selection
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded based on merit and character qualities aligned with Hillcrest's Christian values. Contact the College directly for specific application procedures, timelines, and financial details.

overview: Hillcrest Christian College offers scholarships to students entering its Senior Learning Community (Years 9-12). These scholarships foster diversity and ensure access to a quality Christian education for students who will contribute meaningfully to the College community.
description: Hillcrest Christian College offers scholarships to current and external students entering the Senior Learning Community for Years 9-12. The scholarship program exists to foster diversity by ensuring that an education at Hillcrest Christian College is available to as many students as possible. Scholarships are available in the Senior Learning Community for Years 9-12. The scholarship program seeks to offer places to students who will contribute to the richness and diversity of the College's community. These scholarships recognise academic excellence and character qualities aligned with the College's Christian ethos and its commitment to developing leaders and well-rounded individuals.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 9-12; Current and external students welcome to apply; Must demonstrate qualities that will contribute to the richness and diversity of the College community; Academic and character-based selection criteria
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded based on merit and character qualities aligned with Hillcrest's Christian values and ethos. Contact the College directly for specific application procedures, selection criteria, timelines, and financial details.

overview: Holy Spirit School offers fee concessions to eligible families experiencing genuine financial hardship. The concession is available on the basis of a just and equitable formula reflecting financial hardship and is consistent with Brisbane Catholic Education guidelines. This program ensures that no family is denied a Catholic education due to financial inability to pay.
description: Holy Spirit School provides fee concessions for families facing genuine financial hardship, ensuring access to Catholic education for all. The concession amount varies based on individual family circumstances and demonstrated need, assessed through a formal application process. Families must complete an Application for Concession on Fees form, which can be obtained by contacting the School Finance Officer. Fee concessions must be applied for annually, and eligibility is determined on the basis of a just and equitable formula reflecting financial hardship consistent with Brisbane Catholic Education guidelines. The school encourages families experiencing difficulty meeting fee payment obligations to contact the Principal for a confidential discussion.
eligibility_criteria: Experiencing financial hardship; Must apply annually; Eligible for families of students in Prep to Year 6; Assessment based on just and equitable formula reflecting financial hardship consistent with Brisbane Catholic Education guidelines
special_conditions: Fee concessions must be applied for annually. An Application for Concession on Fees form must be obtained by contacting the School Finance Officer. Families experiencing difficulty meeting fee payment obligations by the due date are encouraged to contact the Principal for a confidential discussion before missing payment deadlines.

school_name: Horizons College of Learning and Enrichment
Indie School, Logan - Brisbane City Campus
Indie School, Logan - Browns Plains Campus
Indie School, Logan - Ipswich Central Campus
Indie School, Logan - Parramatta Park Campus
Indie School, Logan - Thuringowa Central Campus
Innisfail State College - Diverse Learning Centre
Ipswich Girls' Grammar School including Ipswich Junior Grammar School

overview: Kawana Waters State College P&C Association offers excellence scholarships to incoming Year 7 students who demonstrate outstanding academic achievement and excellence across multiple areas. These scholarships reward students who have succeeded academically and are strong role models.
description: The P&C Association offers 11 excellence scholarships to incoming Year 7 students attending Kawana Waters State College. These scholarships reward outstanding students who have succeeded academically and are good role models to their peers while demonstrating strength in one or more areas including sport, art, dance, drama, music, public speaking or information technology. Winners receive money credited to the students' account for use towards the Students Resource Scheme (SRS) and uniform purchases. The selection process involves assessment of academic performance, character, and demonstrated excellence in nominated areas. Scholarships are available for the duration of the student's enrolment at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be currently enrolled in Year 6 or equivalent; Outstanding academic achievement; Demonstrated excellence in one or more areas including sport, art, dance, drama, music, public speaking or information technology; Good role model to peers
special_conditions: Winners of the scholarships will receive money credited to the students' account to be used towards the Students Resource Scheme (SRS) and the purchase of uniforms. Scholarships are based on demonstrated excellence across multiple areas.

overview: The Academic Curriculum Extension (ACE) Program is a selective program for highly able and gifted students in Years 7 to 9. The program provides advanced instruction in humanities, science, mathematics and English for students demonstrating exceptional academic ability.
description: The Academic Curriculum Extension (ACE) Program is designed for highly able and gifted students in Years 7 to 9, with instruction in the subject areas of humanities, science, mathematics and English. The program can accommodate up to 190 students across the year levels. Selection involves application assessment and evaluation of student abilities across multiple academic domains. Students in the program receive specialized curriculum designed to extend and challenge their academic capabilities. The program operates with defined fees and requires completion of an application form including parent assessment of student abilities and demonstrated characteristics of giftedness.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or current Years 7-9 students; Demonstration of high academic ability in humanities, science, mathematics and/or English; Application submission required; Parent and teacher assessment of giftedness characteristics
special_conditions: Full payment for ACE program fee is due by the end of Term 1 of the year of commencement. Selection prioritises in-catchment students and reserves places for in-catchment growth before offering places to out-of-catchment students.

overview: The Arts Excellence Program is a selective program for students demonstrating exceptional talent in performing and visual arts. The program provides specialized instruction in music, drama, dance and visual arts with a focus on developing artistic excellence.
description: Kawana Waters State College offers an Arts Excellence Program as part of its Programs of Excellence, with capacity to enrol 104 students. The program focuses on music, drama, dance and visual arts, providing specialized instruction led by industry professionals. Selection into the program involves assessment of artistic ability and potential. The program is designed to nurture and develop talent in students who demonstrate natural strengths in the arts. Places in the program are primarily available to in-catchment students, with out-of-catchment places offered only when in-catchment demand has been met.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated exceptional talent in performing arts (music, drama, dance) or visual arts; Selection through audition or portfolio assessment; Priority given to in-catchment students; Out-of-catchment enrolment subject to available capacity after in-catchment demand met
special_conditions: Selection criteria for the Programs of Excellence are available from the school. Places available to out-of-catchment students only after in-catchment demand is met and sufficient capacity is reserved for future in-catchment growth.

overview: The Football Excellence Program is a selective sporting program for students demonstrating exceptional talent in football and futsal. Established in 1999, the program provides specialized training and development in football skills alongside Australian Curriculum instruction.
description: The Football Excellence Program, established in 1999, provides an opportunity for players to further develop their football and futsal skills while working through theory elements of skill acquisition and game training based on the Australian Curriculum. The program can accommodate up to 140 students and is funded through a combination of school fees, sports organization sponsorship and government grants. Selection involves assessment of football ability and commitment. The program is designed for students demonstrating exceptional talent and potential in football, with instruction led by experienced coaches and industry professionals.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated exceptional talent in football or futsal; Selection through trials or assessment; Priority given to in-catchment students; Out-of-catchment enrolment subject to available capacity after in-catchment demand met; Students must commit to the program's training and performance requirements
special_conditions: Program is funded by school fees, sports organization sponsorship and government grants. Places available to out-of-catchment students only after in-catchment demand is met and sufficient capacity is reserved for future in-catchment growth. Specific enrolment criteria available from the school.

overview: The Aquatics Excellence Program is a selective program for students demonstrating exceptional talent in water-based sports and activities. The program leverages the school's unique location near Lake Kawana and the Kawana Aquatics Centre to provide specialized water sports training.
description: The Aquatics Excellence Program is a specialized program for students demonstrating exceptional ability in water-based sports and activities, with capacity to enrol 198 students. The program offers training in surfing, kitesurfing, kayaking, swimming, scuba diving, snorkelling and water polo, conducted at the nearby Kawana Aquatics Centre and Lake Kawana. The program is designed to further develop students' aquatic skills while incorporating Australian Curriculum elements. Selection involves assessment of water sports ability and commitment to the program's requirements.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated exceptional talent in water-based sports (swimming, surfing, kayaking, snorkelling, scuba diving, water polo); Selection through trials or assessment; Priority given to in-catchment students; Out-of-catchment enrolment subject to available capacity after in-catchment demand met
special_conditions: Program benefits from the school's unique location between Lake Kawana and the Pacific Ocean. Places available to out-of-catchment students only after in-catchment demand is met and sufficient capacity is reserved for future in-catchment growth. Specific enrolment criteria available from the school.

overview: Kepnock State High School's P&C Association sponsors bursaries annually for Year 8 students. The bursaries recognise and support achievement across Academics, Sport and Culture.
description: The Kepnock High P&C sponsors bursaries for Year 8 students in three key areas: Academics, Sport and Culture. Four bursaries are awarded each year to deserving students who demonstrate excellence in one or more of these areas. These bursaries are funded by the P&C Association and provide financial support to students and families. The selection process involves application and review by the P&C committee based on student achievement and contribution to school life. Bursary awards recognise both academic achievement and co-curricular excellence.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 8 students; Must be enrolled at Kepnock State High School; Application required; Selection based on excellence in Academics, Sport or Culture
special_conditions: Four bursaries are awarded annually. Selection is based on demonstrated excellence in the areas of Academics, Sport or Culture.

overview: The Academic Challenge and Excellence (ACE) program at Kepnock State High School provides extension and acceleration for academically able students. The program operates in English, Mathematics, Science and Music for Year 8 and 9 students.
description: Kepnock State High School offers the Academic Challenge and Excellence (ACE) program to provide extension and acceleration opportunities for academically able students. The program operates across core subjects including English, Mathematics, Science and Music during Years 8 and 9. Entry to the ACE program is through individual application at the time of enrolment. This program enables gifted and talented students to pursue accelerated learning pathways and develop their academic potential in these key areas. The program recognises that students have varying academic abilities and provides tailored learning opportunities for those demonstrating high achievement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 8 and Year 9 students; Individual application required at time of enrolment; Demonstrated academic ability in English, Mathematics, Science and/or Music
special_conditions: Entry to this program is through individual application at the time of enrolment. Program focuses on extension and acceleration in English, Mathematics, Science and Music.

overview: Keebra Park's Academic Excellence Program is designed for high-achieving students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning. The program encourages students to develop creative and analytical skills through collaborative projects and authentic learning experiences, providing accelerated pathways aligned with elite academic outcomes.
description: The Academic Excellence Program encourages students to develop their creative and analytical skills through collaborative projects and authentic learning experiences. Keebra Park is committed to providing the best possible contemporary academic pathway to suit each individual student. Students are given the opportunity to experience a challenging, supportive and inclusive program that empowers them to take responsibility for their learning. The program provides enrichment experiences and competitions at local, state and national levels, with students intellectually challenged and supported through greater depth of curriculum, more density of learning, and higher levels of difficulty including increasing complexity and abstract thinking. Keebra Park's academic excellence program has no ceiling, ensuring that your goals are met. Financial value: Varies based on placement type. Selection process includes assessment of academic performance and potential. Duration is for the duration of enrolment. Acceptance into the program is a selective process and students are expected to demonstrate a very high level of achievement and commitment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 students; Exceptional academic performance; High achievement in NAPLAN or equivalent assessments; Demonstrated commitment to learning; All applications should demonstrate school values of respect, responsibility and commitment; Very high level of attendance and behaviour expected
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Acceptance into Academic Excellence is a selective process. Students are expected to demonstrate a very high level of attendance, behaviour and achievement in their chosen field. All applications should demonstrate school values of respect, responsibility and commitment.

overview: Keebra Park's Basketball Academy provides student-athletes with a unique opportunity to combine quality academic education with elite basketball development. The program allows driven young students to pursue their basketball aspirations while successfully completing their secondary education.
description: The Basketball Academy aims to provide student-athletes with a unique opportunity to combine a quality academic education with development in basketball. The program allows driven young students to pursue their basketball aspirations while successfully completing their secondary education. The program is committed to upholding high expectations of each and every student-athlete through constant monitoring of academic progress and performance reviews, as well as providing strong pastoral care and helping students achieve qualifications that will provide opportunities for future employment within the industry. Selected students participate in a highly structured program which focuses heavily on skill development and advanced game play. The Gold Coast basketball development officer oversees operations and students will have unprecedented access to high level competition and representative success. Financial value: Varies depending on placement. Program operates within school timetable and lunchtimes, supplemented by club environment participation. Duration is for the duration of enrolment. Selection process involves development day and trial assessment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students Year 7 to Year 12; Demonstrated elite basketball ability; Commitment to training and competition; High level of attendance and behaviour expected; Very high level of achievement in basketball; All applications should demonstrate school values of respect, responsibility and commitment
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Acceptance into Basketball Academy is a selective process. Students are expected to demonstrate a very high level of attendance, behaviour and achievement in their chosen field. All applications should demonstrate school values of respect, responsibility and commitment. High performing athletes must demonstrate excellent understanding of skill development and game play.

overview: Keebra Park's Rugby League Academy focuses on educating boys and identifying what will make learning and achieving better. The academy allows students to combine a love of sport while completing their secondary education from Year 8 to Year 12.
description: The Rugby League Academy initiated from a focus on 'educating boys' and at the time, identifying what it was that would make learning and achieving better. The academy provides a safe learning environment that recognises and values success, numerous opportunities and resources for individuals to develop their full potential, and fair treatment without prejudice, intimidation or harassment. Highly skilled, committed, caring teachers give freely of their time to support student development. The ability to combine a love of sport while completing study from Year 8 to Year 12 sets very early achievable goals. The program is unique in that students focus not only on learning but also on their sport, health and fitness. Financial value: Varies based on placement. Selection process involves development day and trial assessment. Duration is from Year 8 to Year 12. Students enrolled in the Rugby League Academy are required to complete a Players Agreement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to male students Year 8 to Year 12; Demonstrated elite rugby league ability; Commitment to training and competition; High level of attendance and behaviour required; Must complete Players Agreement; All applications should demonstrate school values of respect, responsibility and commitment
special_conditions: Students must apply by emailing kprugbyleagueacademy@keebraparkshs.eq.edu.au to schedule an appointment with the Head of Rugby League Academy. The Principal is responsible for all decisions on enrolments. All students enrolled in the Rugby League Academy will be required to complete a Players Agreement. Acceptance is a selective process and students are expected to demonstrate a very high level of attendance, behaviour and achievement.

overview: Keebra Park's Girls Sports Academy focuses on developing young females as athletes within society. The program aims to build sporting abilities in a range of sports including Netball, Touch Football and other sports, while focusing on physical fitness, commitment and health.
description: The Girls Sports Academy focuses on developing young females as athletes within society. The aim is to build their sporting abilities in a range of sports as well as focusing on their physical fitness, commitment and health. The program is for the elite sporting girls of the school to compete amongst each other and other schools in the region in a selection of competitions specialising in Netball, Touch Football and a variety of other sports. Selected students participate in a structured program which focuses heavily on skill development. This program operates within the boundaries of the school timetable, lunchtimes and before and after school hours. The program consists of three levels which affords advanced students the opportunity to work in higher levels during sporting competitions. Students work within the Australian Curriculum to attain knowledge of health and fitness while also competing physically at a school level. Financial value: Varies based on placement. Duration is for the duration of enrolment. Selection process involves development day and trial assessment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to female students Year 7 to Year 12; Demonstrated elite sporting ability; Commitment to training and competition; High level of attendance and behaviour required; Selection through structured trials; All applications should demonstrate school values of respect, responsibility and commitment
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Acceptance into Girls Sports Academy is a selective process. Students are expected to demonstrate a very high level of attendance, behaviour and achievement in their chosen field. All applications should demonstrate school values of respect, responsibility and commitment. Program operates across school timetable, lunchtimes and before/after school hours with three levels of participation.

overview: Keebra Park's Girls' Rugby League Academy focuses on developing young females as athletes within society. The program aims to build sporting abilities in Netball, Touch Football and other sports, while emphasizing physical fitness, commitment and health.
description: The Girls' Rugby League Academy focuses on developing young females as athletes within society. The aim is to build their sporting abilities in a range of sports as well as focusing on their physical fitness, commitment and health. The program is for the elite sporting girls of the school to compete amongst each other and other schools in the region in a selection of competitions specialising in Netball, Touch Football and a variety of other sports. Selected students participate in a structured program which focuses heavily on skill development. This program operates within the boundaries of the school timetable, lunchtimes and before and after school hours. The program consists of three levels which affords advanced students the opportunity to work in higher levels during sporting competitions. Students work within the Australian Curriculum to attain knowledge of health and fitness while also competing physically. Financial value: Varies based on placement. Duration is for the duration of enrolment. Selection process involves structured assessment and trials.
eligibility_criteria: Open to female students Year 7 to Year 12; Demonstrated elite sporting ability in rugby league, netball or touch football; Commitment to training and competition; High level of attendance and behaviour required; Selection through trials; All applications should demonstrate school values of respect, responsibility and commitment
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Acceptance into Girls' Rugby League Academy is a selective process. Students are expected to demonstrate a very high level of attendance, behaviour and achievement in their chosen field. All applications should demonstrate school values of respect, responsibility and commitment. Program consists of three levels with opportunity for advancement during competitions.

overview: Keebra Park's Dance Academy is a selective performance program offered to students in Years 7, 8 and 9. The program provides opportunities for students to develop dance skills and perform in school productions and competitions.
description: Keebra Park's Dance Academy provides selective pathways for students interested in developing their dance skills and performance abilities. The program is available to students in Years 7, 8 and 9 who demonstrate talent and commitment to dance. Students work within the curriculum to develop technique, creativity and performance skills. The program offers opportunities to compete in competitions including the Gold Coast Eisteddfod and participate in school-based dance events. Financial value: Non-monetary support through program access. Duration is for the duration of enrolment in the academy. Selection process involves audition and demonstration of dance ability. Students must demonstrate commitment to dance and participation in performances.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7, Year 8, Year 9 students; Demonstrated dance ability and talent; Commitment to performance and rehearsal; High level of attendance and behaviour required; Willingness to participate in competitions and school performances; All applications should demonstrate school values of respect, responsibility and commitment
special_conditions: Acceptance into Dance Academy is a selective process. Students are expected to demonstrate a very high level of attendance, behaviour and commitment in dance. All applications should demonstrate school values of respect, responsibility and commitment.

school_name: King's Christian College - Chambers Flat Campus
overview: King's Christian College offers Academic Scholarships to recognise and reward students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability. The scholarship is available to students entering Years 4 to 11 at all three campuses (Reedy Creek, Pimpama, and Logan Village) in the following academic year.
description: The Academic Scholarship at King's Christian College recognises students who demonstrate outstanding intellectual achievement and academic excellence. King's scholarships cover the cost of King's tuition fees (not consumables such as stationery and excursions). Depending on their academic ability, successful candidates will be offered a full or partial scholarship. The scholarship is valid through to the end of Year 12 and requires students to commit to staying at King's until graduating in Year 12. Scholarship continuation requires the student and family to be supportive of the College and its culture and for the student to display continuing high standards of behaviour and academic results. If a student leaves the College prior to graduating in Year 12, the College may ask for fees waived by the scholarship to be repaid.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 4-11; Must be intending to stay at King's until graduating in Year 12; Must sit the academic scholarship examination; Successful candidates must maintain supportive College culture alignment and high standards of behaviour and academic results
year_levels: Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Partial Fee Remission
special_conditions: Non-refundable application fee of $100 payable at time of application. Scholarship is valid through to the end of Year 12. If a student on a scholarship leaves the College prior to graduating in Year 12, the College may ask for fees waived by the scholarship to be repaid to the College. The continuing offer of a scholarship requires the student and family to be supportive of the College and its culture and for the student to display continuing high standards of behaviour and academic results. International students receive a dollar value off international tuition fees (for example, 100% scholarship would provide $5,000 off, 50% scholarship would provide $2,500 off).

school_name: King's Christian College - Chambers Flat Campus
overview: King's Christian College offers instrumental Music Scholarships to recognise and reward students who demonstrate exceptional musical ability. The scholarship is available to students entering Years 7 to 11 (Pimpama and Reedy Creek campuses) and Years 7 to 10 (Logan Village campus) in the following academic year.
description: The instrumental Music Scholarship at King's Christian College recognises gifted musicians who demonstrate outstanding performance ability on orchestral or other eligible instruments. King's scholarships cover the cost of King's tuition fees (not consumables such as stationery and excursions). Depending on their musical ability, successful candidates will be offered a full or partial scholarship. Students applying for orchestral instrument scholarships must first sit the academic scholarship examination to demonstrate reasonable academic ability, then complete a separate audition with the Performing Arts department. The scholarship is valid through to the end of Year 12 and requires students to commit to staying at King's until graduating in Year 12. Scholarship continuation requires the student and family to be supportive of the College and its culture and for the student to display continuing high standards of behaviour and academic results.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7-11 (Pimpama and Reedy Creek) or Years 7-10 (Logan Village); Applicants must demonstrate exceptional musical ability on orchestral or eligible instruments; Must sit the academic scholarship examination; Must demonstrate reasonable level of academic ability; Must intend to stay at King's until graduating in Year 12; Applicants must state instrument and level/grade in application
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Partial Fee Remission
special_conditions: Non-refundable application fee of $100 payable at time of application. Students must complete online application form stating instrument and level/grade. Applicants must sit academic scholarship exam first. Upon meeting audition criteria, applicants will be contacted by Performing Arts department approximately 2 weeks after academic exam to arrange audition. Auditions held at Logan Village, Pimpama or Reedy Creek campus. Audition requirements must be reviewed before application. Results notified approximately three term weeks after audition. International students receive a dollar value off international tuition fees. Scholarship valid through to Year 12 with mandatory continuation requirements related to College culture and standards of behaviour and academics.

overview: King's Christian College offers Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding achievement in students entering Years 4–11. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning through a rigorous entrance examination.
description: The Academic Scholarship program is available at all three King's campuses (Reedy Creek, Pimpama and Logan Village) for students entering Years 4–11 in the following academic year. King's scholarships cover the cost of tuition fees (not consumables such as stationery or excursions). Depending on their academic ability, successful candidates will be offered a full or partial scholarship, which may be in the form of Full Fee Remission or Up to 50% Fee Reduction. Students must sit the King's scholarship academic examination, which tests existing ability rather than specific curriculum content. The scholarship is valid through to the end of Year 12, conditional upon the student remaining enrolled and maintaining high standards of behaviour and academic results. If a scholarship holder leaves the College prior to Year 12, the College may request repayment of fees waived by the scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Years 4–11 in the following school year at Reedy Creek or Pimpama campuses (Years 4–10 at Logan Village); must complete the academic scholarship examination; must intend to remain at King's until graduating in Year 12; must be supportive of the College and its culture; must maintain continuing high standards of behaviour and academic results
year_levels: Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Up to 50% Fee Reduction
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered on a multi-year basis valid through to the end of Year 12. Recipients must support the college Christian ethos and culture. Scholarship subject to annual review. College reserves the right to withdraw any scholarship in the event of unsatisfactory academic progress or conduct. Conditional upon recipient remaining at school until end of Year 12; if a student leaves prior to Year 12, the College may ask for fees waived by the scholarship to be repaid.

overview: King's Christian College offers Instrumental Music Scholarships for talented musicians entering Years 7–11. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional musical ability and commitment to performance, requiring both academic competence and advanced instrumental proficiency.
description: The Instrumental Music Scholarship program is available at all three King's campuses (Reedy Creek, Pimpama and Logan Village) for students entering Years 7–11 in the following academic year. King's scholarships cover the cost of tuition fees (not consumables such as stationery or excursions). Successful candidates will be offered a full or partial scholarship, which may be in the form of Full Fee Remission or Up to 50% Fee Reduction. All music scholarship applicants must first sit the academic scholarship examination to demonstrate reasonable academic ability, then complete an instrumental audition. The scholarship is valid through to the end of Year 12, conditional upon the student remaining enrolled, maintaining high standards of behaviour and academic results, and actively participating in performance music at the College.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Years 7–11 in the following school year at Reedy Creek or Pimpama campuses (Years 7–10 at Logan Village); must demonstrate proficiency in one or more instruments (Grade 4 or higher equivalent); must sit the academic scholarship examination and meet audition requirements; must intend to remain at King's until graduating in Year 12; must be willing to actively participate in performance music at the College; must be supportive of the College and its culture
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Up to 50% Fee Reduction
special_conditions: Music scholarship candidates are not expected to excel at the academic exam, but must demonstrate a reasonable level of academic ability. Students applying for orchestral instrument scholarships are also required to take the academic examination. Audition is conducted by King's Performing Arts department approximately 2 weeks after the academic exam for those who meet audition requirements. Scholarships are offered on a multi-year basis valid through to the end of Year 12. Recipients must actively participate in performance music and support the college Christian ethos. Scholarship subject to annual review. Conditional upon recipient remaining at school until end of Year 12; if a student leaves prior to Year 12, the College may ask for fees waived by the scholarship to be repaid.

overview: King's Christian College offers Academic Scholarships to international students who demonstrate exceptional achievement. International scholarship recipients receive a dollar value discount off international tuition fees based on academic merit.
description: International students at King's Christian College can apply for and receive scholarships on the same basis as domestic students. The main difference is that international student scholarships provide a dollar value reduction off international tuition fees rather than tuition fee remission. For example, a 100% scholarship would provide $5,000 off international tuition fees, while a 50% scholarship would provide $2,500 off fees. Scholarships cover the cost of tuition fees only (not consumables such as stationery or excursions). Successful candidates will be offered a full or partial scholarship depending on their academic ability. The scholarship is valid through to the end of Year 12, conditional upon the student remaining enrolled and maintaining high standards of behaviour and academic results.
eligibility_criteria: International students entering Years 4–11 in the following school year; must complete the academic scholarship examination; must intend to remain at King's until graduating in Year 12; must be supportive of the College and its culture; must maintain continuing high standards of behaviour and academic results
year_levels: Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered on a multi-year basis valid through to the end of Year 12. Recipients must support the college Christian ethos and culture. Scholarship subject to annual review. Conditional upon recipient remaining at school until end of Year 12; if a student leaves prior to Year 12, the College may ask for fees waived by the scholarship to be repaid. International students pay application fees in addition to scholarship application fee.

school_name: Kingston Centre for Continuing Secondary Education

overview: King's Christian College offers Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding achievement in students entering Years 4 to Year 11. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commit to staying at King's until graduating in Year 12.
description: King's Academic Scholarship is available at the Pimpama campus for students entering Years 4 to Year 11 in the following academic year. The scholarship covers the cost of King's tuition fees (not consumables, stationery, or excursions). Depending on their academic ability, successful candidates will be offered a full or partial scholarship. The scholarship is valid through to the end of Year 12. If a student on a scholarship leaves the College prior to graduating in Year 12, the College may ask for fees waived by the scholarship to be repaid to the College. Selection is based on performance in a written academic scholarship examination held at the Pimpama campus. All scholarship applications must be submitted online and include a copy of the applicant's most recent school report. Applicants will be notified in writing of their outcome approximately 3-4 term weeks after the examination. The scholarship requires the student and family to support King's Christian values and maintain the continuing offer of the scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, or Year 11 at Pimpama campus; Must intend to stay at King's until graduating in Year 12; Must submit a complete online application with most recent school report; Scholarships are offered to students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability
year_levels: Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Partial Fee Reduction
special_conditions: King's scholarships are offered to students who are intending to stay at King's until graduating in Year 12. The scholarship is valid through to the end of Year 12. If a student on a scholarship leaves the College prior to graduating in Year 12, the College may ask for fees waived by the scholarship to be repaid to the College. The continuing offer of a scholarship requires the student and family to be aligned with King's Christian values and ethos.

overview: King's Christian College offers Instrumental Music Scholarships for students entering Years 7 to Year 11 at the Pimpama campus. The scholarship recognises gifted musicians who demonstrate exceptional instrumental ability and commitment to music excellence.
description: King's Instrumental Music Scholarship is available at the Pimpama campus for students entering Years 7 to Year 11 in the following academic year. The scholarship covers the cost of King's tuition fees (not consumables, stationery, or excursions). Depending on their musical ability, successful candidates will be offered a full or partial scholarship. Students applying for orchestral instrument scholarships are also required to take the academic examination. Selection process: applicants must complete an online application form stating the instrument and level/grade, sit the academic scholarship exam, and then participate in a music audition. After sitting the academic exam, applicants who meet the criteria will be contacted by King's Performing Arts department to arrange a suitable audition time. Auditions typically occur approximately two weeks after the academic exam. Audition requirements are detailed in the Music Scholarship Audition Requirements document. All applicants will be notified in writing of their outcome approximately three term weeks after their audition. While King's does not expect music scholarship candidates to excel at the academic exam, gifted musicians must demonstrate a reasonable level of academic ability. The scholarship is valid through to the end of Year 12.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, or Year 11 at Pimpama campus; Must play an instrument at a demonstrated level of proficiency; Students applying for orchestral instruments must also take the academic examination; Must demonstrate a reasonable level of academic ability; Must intend to stay at King's until graduating in Year 12; Must submit a complete online application specifying instrument and level/grade with most recent school report
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Partial Fee Reduction
special_conditions: Students applying for orchestral instrument scholarships are also required to take the academic examination. King's scholarships are offered to students who are intending to stay at King's until graduating in Year 12. The scholarship is valid through to the end of Year 12. If a student on a scholarship leaves the College prior to graduating in Year 12, the College may ask for fees waived by the scholarship to be repaid to the College. Music scholarship candidates must demonstrate a reasonable level of academic ability even though excellence at the academic exam is not required. The continuing offer of a scholarship requires the student and family to be aligned with King's Christian values and ethos.

overview: Kingsley College offers Academic Scholarships to dedicated, high-performing students commencing in Years 5 and 6. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate excellent academic performance while upholding the college's Christian values and commitment to learning.
description: Academic Scholarships are designed for students entering Years 5 and 6 who demonstrate exceptional academic achievement and embody Kingsley College's values. Recipients must be high-performing students who take every opportunity to find effective solutions, progress in their skills, maintain curiosity, share knowledge with others, and consider a Christ-centred worldview. This scholarship is available to both new students joining the school and current students. The program is intended to recognise and support students who excel academically while demonstrating strong character and commitment to the college community. Selection involves assessment of academic performance, as well as demonstration of the college's core values.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 5 and 6; Must demonstrate high academic performance; Must uphold Kingsley College values in learning; Open to new students and current students; Christ-centred worldview consideration preferred
special_conditions: Open to both new students enrolling at the school and current students. Recipients must demonstrate commitment to upholding Kingsley College's Christian values throughout their enrolment.

overview: Kingsley College offers General Excellence Scholarships specifically for students entering Year 2. The scholarship recognises well-rounded students who embody the college's values and commitment to developing good character alongside academic achievement.
description: The General Excellence Scholarship is an opportunity specifically designed for students entering at Year 2. This scholarship is tailored for the child who embodies the essence of being a well-rounded student and reflects Kingsley College's commitment to nurturing the development of good character. The program recognises students who demonstrate achievement across academic, social, spiritual, and personal development areas. This scholarship opportunity is available to both new students entering the school and current students continuing their education. Recipients are valued for their overall contribution to the college community, not just academic performance, but also their character, values alignment, and all-around development.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 2; Must demonstrate well-rounded ability across academic and character development; Must embody college values; Open to new students and current students; Good character and conduct expected
special_conditions: Designed to recognise and support well-rounded students who reflect the college's commitment to nurturing good character and Christian values. Open to both new enrolling students and current students.

school_name: Kutta Mulla Gorinna Special Assistance School
Logan City Special School - Charles Avenue Campus

overview: Loreto College invites students who demonstrate academic excellence or potential to apply for an Academic Scholarship available for entry in Year 7 and Year 10. The scholarships provide partial concession of tuition fees and are awarded for the duration of the student's education at Loreto College. Selection is based on the students' performance in the ACER Academic Scholarship Examination.
description: Loreto College offers Academic Scholarships for Year 7 and Year 10 entry to students demonstrating academic excellence or potential. The scholarships provide partial concession of tuition fees only and are awarded for the duration of the student's education at Loreto College. Selection is based on performance in the ACER Academic Scholarship Examination held on Saturday 28 February 2026. The concessions on tuition fees do not cover other items such as uniforms, texts, stationery, laptop levy, or other miscellaneous expenses. Scholarships are reviewed annually by the Principal. If a student does not meet the conditions of the scholarship, for example, inconsistent attendance, breaches of enrolment conditions or serious breaches of the student code of conduct, the scholarship may not be renewed. Scholarship applicants do not need to be enrolled at Loreto College or have submitted an enrolment application prior to sitting the ACER examination.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 and Year 10 for 2027 entry; Must demonstrate academic excellence or potential; Must be female; Must sit the ACER Academic Scholarship Examination
