special_conditions: Scholarship applicants must register through the ACER Academic Scholarship Examination website. The fee to sit the ACER test is $170. Should an applicant be offered a scholarship and wish to accept, it is then compulsory to submit a Loreto College enrolment application and pay the $150 application fee and $600 acceptance fee. Both fees are non-refundable. Scholarships are reviewed annually by the Principal. If a student does not meet the conditions of the scholarship, for example, inconsistent attendance, breaches of enrolment conditions or serious breaches of the student code of conduct, the scholarship may not be renewed.

overview: The Mary Ward Bursary is open to students looking to commence and complete Year 11 and Year 12 at Loreto College. The bursary is means tested and provides remission of up to 100% of tuition fees only and is available to families that are otherwise unable to afford a Loreto education.
description: The Mary Ward Bursary is a means-tested financial assistance program available to students commencing Year 11 and Year 12 at Loreto College. The bursary provides remission of up to 100% of tuition fees only to families who are otherwise unable to afford a Loreto education. The bursary is designed to ensure that students from families facing financial hardship can access a Loreto education without financial barriers. Applicants must meet specified means-testing criteria to qualify. The bursary covers tuition fees only and does not extend to other expenses such as uniforms, texts, stationery, laptop levy, and other miscellaneous costs. Families must provide financial documentation to support their application.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 11 and Year 12; Must be currently enrolled or seeking enrolment at Loreto College; Families must meet means-testing criteria; Must demonstrate financial hardship or inability to afford tuition
special_conditions: This is a means-tested bursary requiring submission of financial documentation. The bursary covers tuition fees only and does not include other school expenses such as uniforms, texts, stationery, laptop levy, camps, excursions, or other miscellaneous charges. Families must demonstrate financial hardship or inability to afford tuition fees.

overview: Loreto College Coorparoo offers a unique opportunity for students in Years 7 and 8 to participate in the Beginner Instrumental Music Scholarship Program at a reduced fee. The program is open to all students, regardless of whether you have previous musical experience or not. The scope of the program has expanded to include more instruments and the college is currently offering placements in both a Band and String ensemble.
description: Loreto College Coorparoo offers the Beginner Instrumental Music Scholarship Program for Years 7 and 8 students at a reduced fee structure. The program is open to all students regardless of prior musical experience, and provides placements in Band and String ensemble ensembles. The college's Music Support Group funds music scholarships and assists with the purchase of new instruments through fundraising initiatives. Students receive structured instrumental tuition within a curriculum framework that allows them to develop sequential and cumulative musical skills. The program supports the College's Music Scholarship program through community fundraising, enabling the college to offer music education at reduced costs. Participation in the program includes enrolment in group or private instrumental lessons on the instrument relevant to the ensemble.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Year 7 and Year 8; No prior musical experience required; Must be female; Open to all applicants regardless of musical background
special_conditions: The program is open to all students regardless of previous musical experience. Placements are available in Band and String ensemble ensembles. Students must undertake lessons in private or small group tuition either at the College or externally. The Music Support Group supports the program through fundraising initiatives which are invested back into the program.

overview: Loganlea State High School offers an Academic Scholarship for Year 8 students. This scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and achievement.
description: The Year 8 Academic Scholarship is awarded to students entering Year 8 who have demonstrated outstanding academic performance. The exact financial benefit, selection process, and renewal conditions are not publicly disclosed on the school website at this time. Applications are managed through the STAR Scholarship process. Information on 2026 scholarships will be available in Semester 2, 2025. Students must meet specified academic criteria and may be required to undertake assessment or interviews as part of the selection process.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 8; Must demonstrate outstanding academic achievement; Catchment and out-of-catchment students eligible
special_conditions: STAR Scholarships are closed for 2025. Information on 2026 scholarships will be available in Semester 2, 2025. Applications managed through 2026 STAR Program Application Form.

overview: Loganlea State High School offers a Mathematics and Science Scholarship for Year 8 students. This scholarship recognises students with exceptional ability in mathematics and science, including a laptop component.
description: The Year 8 Mathematics and Science Scholarship includes a laptop provision for eligible recipients. This scholarship is designed to support students who demonstrate outstanding achievement in mathematics and science subjects. The exact financial benefit and duration are not publicly disclosed. Students selected for this scholarship will receive support to excel in STEM subjects throughout their Year 8 enrolment. Selection is based on academic performance and demonstration of aptitude in mathematics and science.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 8; Outstanding achievement in mathematics and science; Catchment and out-of-catchment students eligible
special_conditions: Includes laptop provision. STAR Scholarships are closed for 2025. Information on 2026 scholarships will be available in Semester 2, 2025.

overview: Loganlea State High School offers a Dance Scholarship for Year 8 students. This scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional ability and commitment to dance.
description: The Year 8 Dance Scholarship supports students who have demonstrated outstanding achievement and commitment in dance. Recipients will be part of the school's Loganlea Dance Collective program. The exact financial benefit and specific selection process including audition requirements are not publicly disclosed on the school website. Selection is based on demonstrated dance ability and potential. Information on 2026 scholarships will be available in Semester 2, 2025.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 8; Demonstrated excellence in dance; Catchment and out-of-catchment students eligible
special_conditions: STAR Scholarships are closed for 2025. Information on 2026 scholarships will be available in Semester 2, 2025. Applicants will participate in Loganlea Dance Collective program.

overview: LORDS offers Academic Scholarships to reward high-level academic achievers entering Years 7 to 12. These scholarships recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning, as assessed through competitive scholarship examinations held twice annually.
description: LORDS awards academic scholarships based on the results of scholarship examinations held in March and September each year. Exam results, in consultation with a Principal's interview and previous school reports, may result in a scholarship being offered for the following academic year. In most cases, the scholarship discount is between 30-50 percent of Tuition Fees. There is no reduction in levy costs. Scholarships remain current to the end of Year 12 subject to continued strong academic performance and conduct. The school utilises EduTest Scholarship Tests which require students to demonstrate a range of skills such as the ability to interpret, infer, deduce and think critically. Students only need to sit one exam per year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 to 12 for the following calendar year; Both future and current students welcome to apply; Must perform well in scholarship examination; Academic performance assessment through Principal's interview and previous school reports
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: The discount and number of scholarships offered is dependent on the number and quality of applicants in each year and the final decision is at the discretion of the Principal. Scholarships remain current to the end of Year 12 subject to continued strong academic performance and conduct. There is no reduction in levy costs.

overview: LORDS offers Academic Bursaries to support high-achieving students entering Years 7 to 12. These bursaries provide financial assistance to recognise academic excellence while offering broader support than scholarships to a wider range of qualified applicants.
description: LORDS awards academic bursaries based on the results of scholarship examinations held in March and September each year. Exam results, in consultation with a Principal's interview and previous school reports, may result in a bursary being offered for the following academic year. In most cases, the bursary discount is between 15-25 percent of Tuition Fees. There is no reduction in levy costs. Bursaries are for a 12 month period and reviewed annually by the Principal based on the student's conduct and academic performance. The selection process uses EduTest Scholarship Tests which require students to demonstrate a range of skills such as the ability to interpret, infer, deduce and think critically.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 to 12 for the following calendar year; Both future and current students welcome to apply; Must perform adequately in scholarship examination; Academic performance assessment through Principal's interview and previous school reports
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: The discount and number of bursaries offered is dependent on the number and quality of applicants in each year and the final decision is at the discretion of the Principal. Bursaries are for a 12 month period and reviewed annually by the Principal based on the student's conduct and academic performance. There is no reduction in levy costs.

overview: LORDS offers Performing and Creative Arts Bursaries to recognise and support students who show commitment and passion while excelling in performance-based and creative disciplines. These bursaries are available to both current and future students who demonstrate talent in performing and creative arts.
description: LORDS offers bursaries in the areas of Performing Arts, including drama, music, and creative performance disciplines. These bursaries are designed for students who show commitment and passion while excelling in their chosen performing or creative arts field. The school values excellence in performance and provides platforms for students to express themselves through drama, music, and innovative creative projects. Applications are accepted from both current and future students. The specific financial value of these bursaries is determined by the Principal based on the quality of applicants and the student's demonstrated talent and commitment to the performing arts.
eligibility_criteria: Show commitment and passion in performing arts; Demonstrate excellence in chosen performing or creative arts field; Open to both current and future students; Excellence and passion in disciplines such as drama, music, or creative performance
special_conditions: Applications accepted until 31st October each year. Students must demonstrate commitment and passion in performing arts. Selection is at the discretion of the Principal. Contact the Registrar, Leisa Martin, for details on how to apply.

overview: LORDS offers Sports Bursaries to recognise and support students who demonstrate exceptional achievement and commitment to sporting pursuits. These bursaries are available to both current and future students who excel in their chosen sport at representative or competitive levels.
description: LORDS offers bursaries in the area of Sports for students who show commitment and passion while excelling in their chosen sporting field. The school values excellence in sport and provides opportunities for students to engage in sporting pursuits at all levels, from junior school interschool sports teams to regional, state, and national championships. These bursaries recognise exceptional sporting achievement and dedication. Applications are accepted from both current and future students. The specific financial value of these bursaries is determined by the Principal based on the quality of applicants and the student's demonstrated excellence and commitment to sport.
eligibility_criteria: Show commitment and passion in sport; Demonstrate excellence in chosen sporting field; Open to both current and future students; Representation in inter-school sports or regional/state/national level competition
special_conditions: Applications accepted until 31st October each year. Students must demonstrate commitment and passion in sport. Selection is at the discretion of the Principal. Contact the Registrar, Leisa Martin, for details on how to apply.

overview: LORDS offers Visual Arts Bursaries to recognise and support students who demonstrate exceptional talent and commitment in visual arts disciplines. These bursaries are available to both current and future students who excel in visual arts and creative expression.
description: LORDS offers bursaries in the area of Creative Arts and Visual Arts for students who show commitment and passion while excelling in their chosen visual arts field. The school provides specialty learning environments for Visual Arts and celebrates original ideas through various school events and creative projects, providing platforms for students to express themselves in unique artistic ways. These bursaries recognise excellence in visual arts disciplines including painting, drawing, sculpture, design, and other visual art forms. Applications are accepted from both current and future students. The specific financial value of these bursaries is determined by the Principal based on the quality of applicants and the student's demonstrated talent and commitment to visual arts.
eligibility_criteria: Show commitment and passion in visual arts; Demonstrate excellence in chosen visual arts field or creative discipline; Open to both current and future students; Excellence in disciplines such as painting, drawing, visual design, or creative arts projects
special_conditions: Applications accepted until 31st October each year. Students must demonstrate commitment and passion in visual arts. Selection is at the discretion of the Principal. Contact the Registrar, Leisa Martin, for details on how to apply.

overview: Mackay Christian College offers the Premier Instrumental Music Scholarship to students demonstrating exceptional musical talent and expertise. This prestigious award provides comprehensive support for tuition and College fees, recognising students who are committed to advancing their musical development within the Creative Academy.
description: The Premier Instrumental Music Scholarship is awarded to one new student enrolling at Mackay Christian College each year. This scholarship includes full College fees, instrumental lessons, the cost of examinations for the instrument for which the student was awarded the scholarship, and may include instrument hire if required. Successful applicants must reach Grade 4 AMEB or equivalent and participate in at least one College ensemble and choose Music as an elective subject in Middle and Senior School. The scholarship is approved for one school year and will be continued if requirements are maintained. Selection involves audition with students required to perform 2 contrasting pieces of music with a total duration of 5 to 8 minutes, with assessment of aural ability, sight reading and general knowledge of chosen pieces.
eligibility_criteria: New students enrolling at MCC; Reached Grade 4 AMEB or equivalent; Must perform in at least one College ensemble or as required; Must choose Music as one of elective subjects in Middle and Senior School; Aligned with and supportive of College values and ethos
special_conditions: Scholarships are approved for one school year and will be continued if requirements are maintained. Applications will be considered for approval by the Head of Faculty, Creative Academy, in consultation with the College Principal. The College reserves the right not to offer a scholarship or scholarships in a particular year or within a specific category. All applicants and their families must support the College's mission and ethos in full.

overview: Mackay Christian College offers the Advanced Instrumental Music Scholarship to students with solid musical foundations and dedication to ensemble participation. This scholarship provides substantial support for tuition fees and instrumental lessons, supporting two new students each year.
description: The Advanced Instrumental Music Scholarship is offered to a maximum of two new students enrolling at Mackay Christian College each year. These scholarships include half College fees, half the cost of instrumental lessons, half the cost of examinations for the instrument for which the student was awarded the scholarship, and may include instrument hire if required. Successful applicants must reach Grade 3 AMEB or equivalent and must perform in at least one College ensemble and choose Music as an elective subject in Middle and Senior School. Selection involves audition with applicants required to perform 2 contrasting pieces of music with a total duration of 3 to 5 minutes, with assessment of aural ability, sight reading and general knowledge of chosen pieces. Scholarships are approved for one school year and will be continued if requirements are maintained.
eligibility_criteria: New students enrolling at MCC; Reached Grade 3 AMEB or equivalent; Must perform in at least one College ensemble or as required; Must choose Music as one of elective subjects in Middle and Senior School; Aligned with and supportive of College values and ethos
special_conditions: Scholarships are approved for one school year and will be continued if requirements are maintained. Applications will be considered for approval by the Head of Faculty, Creative Academy, in consultation with the College Principal. The College reserves the right not to offer a scholarship or scholarships in a particular year or within a specific category. All applicants and their families must support the College's mission and ethos in full.

overview: Mackay Christian College offers the Instrumental Music Lesson Assistance Scholarship to students who demonstrate musical ability but face financial barriers to participation. This scholarship supports up to eight students each year with assistance for instrumental lessons, helping ensure that financial hardship does not prevent students from pursuing their musical talents.
description: The Instrumental Music Lesson Assistance Scholarship is offered to a maximum of eight enrolling and current students at Mackay Christian College. These scholarships include either half or full cost of instrumental lessons and may include instrument hire if required. Preference is given to applicants who would greatly benefit from being an active member of the Creative Academy at MCC but without financial assistance would be unable to participate. Successful applicants must reach Grade 2 AMEB or equivalent. Short-listed applicants may be requested to audition. Successful candidates will be required to perform in a College ensemble or as required. Scholarships are approved for one school year and will be continued if requirements are maintained. Applications will be considered for approval by the Head of Faculty, Creative Academy, in consultation with the College Principal.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolling and current students; Reached Grade 2 AMEB or equivalent; Preference given to those who would greatly benefit but cannot afford to participate; Must perform in a College ensemble or as required; Aligned with and supportive of College values and ethos; May be requested to audition
special_conditions: Preference will be given to those applicants who would greatly benefit from being an active member of the Creative Academy at MCC but without financial assistance would be unable to participate. Scholarships are approved for one school year and will be continued if requirements are maintained. Applications will be considered for approval by the Head of Faculty, Creative Academy, in consultation with the College Principal. The College reserves the right not to offer a scholarship or scholarships in a particular year or within a specific category. All applicants and their families must support the College's mission and ethos in full.

overview: Mackay Christian College's Family Assistance Program provides financial support to families experiencing financial difficulty in paying school fees. The College is committed to ensuring that no student is denied access to quality Christian education due to financial hardship.
description: The MCC Family Assistance Program is designed to help families experiencing financial difficulty with school fees. Families may apply for assistance by completing a Family Assistance Application form. The level of support is evaluated based on what a family can afford to contribute, as stated in the application. Support may range from a small percentage of tuition fees to full fee remission. Both new and existing families can apply for assistance. Applications must be submitted to the Business Manager with supporting documentation. Any Family Assistance offered will be reset at the commencement of each school year, when families may reapply. The College policy ensures that assistance is given to anyone who needs help paying fees and that no student is denied a quality Christian education because of financial hardship.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial difficulty; Open to new and current families; Must complete Family Assistance Application form with supporting documentation; Assessment based on what family can afford to contribute
special_conditions: Application must be completed with supporting documentation and submitted to the Business Manager at business@mccmky.qld.edu.au. Applications are treated with utmost confidentiality. Any Family Assistance offered will be reset at the commencement of each school year. Families may reapply annually. The College policy remains that assistance will be given to anyone who needs help paying fees from time to time and that no student should be denied a quality Christian education because of financial hardship.

overview: Mackay North State High School offers Junior Secondary Scholarships to Year 7 students. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional achievement in academic, cultural, or sporting excellence over three years through Years 7, 8, and 9.
description: Mackay North State High School provides scholarships to students entering Year 7 for three years of enrolment through Years 7, 8, and 9. For 2024, scholarships were offered to six Year 7 and Year 10 students in the areas of academic, cultural and sporting excellence. The specific financial benefit value is not publicly detailed on the school website. Applications require endorsement by the respective primary school Principal and must include the previous year Semester One Report Card and NAPLAN results where possible. Selection is based on demonstrated excellence in academic performance, cultural involvement, or sporting achievement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be endorsed by primary school Principal; Semester One Report Card from previous year required; NAPLAN results where possible required; Demonstrated excellence in academic, cultural, or sporting areas
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded for three years of enrolment through Years 7, 8 and 9. Year 7 applications must be endorsed by the respective primary school Principal. Applications must include Semester One Report Card and 2023 NAPLAN results where possible.

overview: Mackay North State High School offers Senior Secondary Scholarships to Year 10 students. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional achievement in academic, cultural, or sporting excellence over three years through Years 10, 11, and 12.
description: Mackay North State High School provides scholarships to students entering Year 10 for three years of enrolment through Years 10, 11, and 12. For 2024, scholarships were offered to six Year 7 and Year 10 students in the areas of academic, cultural and sporting excellence. The specific financial benefit value is not publicly detailed on the school website. Applications must be completed and returned directly to Mackay North State High. Selection is based on demonstrated excellence in academic performance, cultural involvement, or sporting achievement as evaluated through the application and assessment process.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 10; Academic excellence required; Cultural excellence or sporting excellence required; Demonstrated high achievement in chosen area of excellence
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded for three years of enrolment through Years 10, 11 and 12. Applications must be returned directly to Mackay North State High in Valley Street. Recipients are selected from students demonstrating excellence in academic, cultural or sporting areas.

overview: Mackay North State High School offers a Music Excellence Program recognised for its tradition of excellence throughout the state and nationally. The program provides selective entry for students entering Year 7 with demonstrated musical ability and commitment to instrumental performance.
description: The Mackay North State High School Music Excellence Program has a tradition of excellence and is renowned throughout the state, nationally and internationally. The program offers two pathways for entry at Year 7. Students can enter with demonstrated instrumental music experience and commitment to continue learning an instrument, or through alternative selection pathways. The program provides enhanced music curriculum, ensemble participation opportunities, and access to the school's world-renowned instrumental program featuring multiple orchestras, bands, and ensembles including the famous Marching Band. No specific monetary value is detailed, though the program provides specialised music instruction and performance opportunities as part of the school curriculum.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must have instrumental music experience from primary school; Commitment to continue learning an instrument required; Two pathways available for selection; Students cannot participate in Japanese language option if selected for instrumental pathway
special_conditions: Two pathways for entry to the Music Excellence Program. Students selecting the Instrumental Program Only pathway must have been learning an instrument in primary school. Students accepted into the Music Excellence Program cannot participate in Japanese language electives.

overview: Mackay State High School offers the Academic Excellence Academy to high-achieving students who demonstrate exceptional performance in core subjects. The program provides accelerated learning opportunities and extension activities designed to challenge and develop gifted students across Years 7-12.
description: The Academic Excellence Academy is a selective program for students with demonstrated high levels of academic performance in Years 5 and 6, and strong results in external national testing such as NAPLAN. Students applying for entry must submit a written application and sit an entrance examination in Literacy and Numeracy (unless they were part of the Academy in the previous year). The Academy participates in the National Curriculum for Core Subjects but often at an accelerated rate, with extension opportunities including STEM projects, extracurricular activities, excursions, and external testing. The capacity is 150 places per year level.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated high performance in Years 5 and 6; Strong NAPLAN results; Written application required; Entrance exam in Literacy and Numeracy (if new to Academy); Open to all genders; Multiple year levels from Year 7 to Year 12
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Entrance testing occurs at the school auditorium. Participating students engage in extended curriculum with accelerated learning pathways and access to extracurricular extension activities and competitions.

program_name: Creative Arts Academy - Instrumental Music Program
overview: Mackay State High School offers an Instrumental Music Program as part of its Creative Arts Academy for musically talented students. The program provides specialist music instruction and performance opportunities within the school's highly regarded music framework.
description: The Instrumental Music Program is part of the Creative Arts Academy and provides students with continuing music development from primary school with weekly tuition from specialist teachers. Students have access to multiple ensembles and bands with numerous performance opportunities within the school and wider community. The school capacity is 120 places in the Instrumental Music Program. Students explore the four Arts disciplines including Music, along with opportunities to develop technical skills in various instruments and participate in school performances, including school productions and external performances.
eligibility_criteria: Current or previous participation in instrumental music; Musical ability and demonstrated performance skills; Interest in continuing music development; Open to all genders; Continuation of primary school music program participation recommended
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Weekly specialist tuition required. Multiple ensembles and performance opportunities available. Continuation from primary school music programs strongly encouraged.

overview: Mackay State High School offers three Sporting Excellence Academies in Rugby League, Football, and Netball for talented young athletes. The academies provide specialised coaching and pathway development for students seeking professional sporting opportunities.
description: Mackay State High School has three Sporting Excellence Academies offering specialist instruction and development in Rugby League, Football, and Netball. These academies are scheduled as part of the formal curriculum and provide training and playing opportunities beyond what is available in the wider Mackay area for talented players. The school maintains partnerships with the Mackay Cutters and Mackay Crusaders to develop professional pathways. Coaches focus on developing both the academic and athletic capacities of students, with player wellbeing as a top priority. The goal is to produce young people of strong character who can make positive contributions to their communities and serve as dynamic role models and leaders.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated sporting talent and skill level in one of three sports: Rugby League, Football, or Netball; Ability to train at a high level; Commitment to both academic and athletic development; Open to all genders; Students can apply to enroll as an alternative to regular HPE class
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scheduled curriculum classes offered in Years 11 and 12 as extra-curricular options. Partnerships with professional sporting organisations for pathway development. Emphasis on character development and wellbeing alongside athletic excellence.

overview: The Creative Arts Academy at Mackay State High School offers gifted and talented students opportunities to excel in a structured program combining the four Arts disciplines. The academy provides specialist arts instruction and performance opportunities.
description: The Creative Arts Academy offers gifted and talented students opportunities to excel in a structured extra-curricular program created by specialist arts staff. Over Years 7 and 8, students explore four Arts disciplines: Art, Drama, Media, and Music. Students who demonstrate passion, skill, and creative excellence have continued access to the program in Years 9 onwards. The academy emphasises student educational and personal development through participation in a range of school and community arts events including the Mackay Eisteddfod, Creative Generation, Excellence Awards in Visual Art, Mackay Orchestras and Bands Competition, and other performance opportunities.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated artistic talent, passion, and creative excellence; Application required; Shortlisted applicants invited for audition/portfolio review; Open to all genders; Must show commitment to arts participation; Years 7-8 students explore all four disciplines
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Four arts disciplines explored: Visual Art, Drama, Media, and Music. Continuation to Years 9-12 dependent on demonstrated creative excellence. Access to after-school extension programs including XL:Arts, Media clubs, and Drama clubs for CAA students.

program_name: Agriculture Show Team Program of Excellence
overview: Mackay State High School offers an Agriculture Show Team Program of Excellence for students interested in agricultural and livestock management. The program develops students' practical skills and knowledge in agricultural preparation and show participation.
description: The Agriculture Show Team Program of Excellence is a specialist program with a defined capacity of 30 places. This program provides students with opportunities to develop knowledge and skills in preparing livestock for show competitions at local, regional, and state levels. Students participate in various agricultural shows and develop practical competencies in animal husbandry, livestock preparation, and show competition. The program contributes to student development across practical, teamwork, and leadership dimensions through participation in agricultural competitions and community engagement with the local agricultural sector.
eligibility_criteria: Interest in agriculture and livestock management; Willingness to participate in show team activities; Commitment to learning practical agricultural skills; Open to all genders; Application to program required
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Participation in local, regional, and state show competitions. Development of practical livestock management and preparation skills. Community engagement with agricultural industry.

overview: The Parents and Citizens Association of Mackay State High School offers eight (8) merit-based scholarships to recognise achievement in academic, cultural, and sporting excellence. These scholarships support talented secondary students at the school.
description: The Parents and Citizens Association and Mackay State High School jointly offer eight (8) scholarships annually for students attending the school. Scholarships are awarded in the areas of academic, cultural, and sporting excellence. Details regarding the specific value of scholarships, duration, and exact selection criteria are not fully detailed in available materials, though applications must be endorsed by primary school principals and include current academic records and NAPLAN results. The program recognises and supports student achievement across multiple dimensions of school life.
eligibility_criteria: Open to secondary students at Mackay State High School; Must demonstrate excellence in academic, cultural, or sporting areas; Applications must be endorsed by primary school Principal; Current academic records and NAPLAN results required; Open to all genders
special_conditions: Applications must be endorsed by primary school Principal. Scholarship applications require submission of current academic reports and NAPLAN results where available. Eight scholarships awarded annually across academic, cultural, and sporting excellence categories.

school_name: Mackay Christian College - Providence Junior Campus
overview: Mackay Christian College offers a Principal's Music Scholarship for students demonstrating exceptional instrumental music ability. This scholarship recognises and supports talented musicians entering the college who wish to develop their musical expertise within the Creative Academy.
description: This scholarship provides full College fees, instrumental lessons, the cost of examinations for the student's instrument and may include instrument hire if required. The scholarship is approved for one school year and will be continued if requirements are maintained. Recipients are selected through an audition process assessing musical performance, aural ability, sight reading and general knowledge. The scholarship is available to one new student enrolling at MCC each year. Successful candidates must perform in at least one College ensemble or as required and choose Music as one of their elective subjects in Middle and Senior School.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students enrolling at MCC; Must have reached Grade 4 AMEB or equivalent; Must support the College's Christian mission and ethos in full; Must commit to performing in at least one College ensemble; Must choose Music as an elective subject in Middle and Senior School
special_conditions: Scholarships are approved for one school year and will be continued if requirements are maintained. Applications will be considered for approval by the Head of Faculty, Creative Academy, in consultation with the College Principal. The College reserves the right not to offer a scholarship in a particular year. All applicants and their families must support the College's mission and ethos in full.

school_name: Mackay Christian College - Providence Junior Campus
overview: Mackay Christian College offers a merit-based Music Scholarship providing 50% fee reduction to students demonstrating strong musical ability. This scholarship is designed to support developing musicians who show promise and commitment to musical excellence.
description: This scholarship provides 50% College fees reduction, half the cost of instrumental lessons, half the cost of examinations for the student's instrument and may include instrument hire if required. The scholarship is approved for one school year and will be continued if requirements are maintained. Up to two scholarships are offered each year to new students. The selection process involves an audition where applicants perform two contrasting pieces of music with a total duration of 3 to 5 minutes, with assessment of aural ability, sight reading and general knowledge of their chosen pieces.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students enrolling at MCC; Must have reached Grade 3 AMEB or equivalent; Must support the College's Christian mission and ethos in full; Aural ability, sight reading and general knowledge will be tested during audition; Must commit to performing in at least one College ensemble
special_conditions: Scholarships are approved for one school year and will be continued if requirements are maintained. Applications will be considered for approval by the Head of Faculty, Creative Academy, in consultation with the College Principal. The College reserves the right not to offer scholarships in a particular year. All applicants and their families must support the College's mission and ethos in full.

school_name: Mackay Christian College - Providence Junior Campus
overview: Mackay Christian College offers Music Assistance Scholarships to support students with demonstrated musical potential who would benefit from financial assistance to participate in the Creative Academy. These scholarships prioritise students with financial need who show musical ability.
description: This scholarship provides either half or full cost of instrumental lessons and may include instrument hire if required. Up to eight scholarships are offered to both enrolling and current students each year. Preference is given to applicants who would greatly benefit from being an active member of the Creative Academy but without financial assistance would be unable to participate. Short-listed applicants may be requested to audition. The scholarship is approved for one school year and will be continued if requirements are maintained. Successful candidates must perform in a College ensemble or as required.
eligibility_criteria: Open to enrolling and current students; Must have reached Grade 2 AMEB or equivalent; Must demonstrate financial need to participate; Must support the College's Christian mission and ethos in full; Must commit to performing in at least one College ensemble; All applicants and families must be aligned with and supportive of College values
special_conditions: Preference will be given to those applicants who would greatly benefit from being an active member of the Creative Academy at MCC but without financial assistance would be unable to participate. Scholarships are approved for one school year and will be continued if requirements are maintained. The College reserves the right not to offer scholarships in a particular year. All applicants and their families must support the College's mission and ethos in full.

school_name: Mackay Christian College - Providence Junior Campus
overview: Mackay Christian College offers a Family Assistance Program to support families experiencing financial hardship. The College is committed to ensuring that no student is denied a quality Christian education because of financial difficulty.
description: The Family Assistance Program provides fee support to families who find themselves in financial difficulty as they prepare for the school year. The level of support is evaluated based on what a family can afford to contribute, as stated in the Family Assistance Application. Applications can be submitted for the current school year, and any Family Assistance offered will be reset at the commencement of each school year when families may reapply. The assessment is conducted with utmost confidentiality by the Business Manager. The College policy states that assistance will be given to anyone who needs help paying fees from time to time.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; No specific income threshold stated; Open to all enrolled and prospective students; Assessment based on individual family circumstances and ability to contribute
special_conditions: Applications must be completed using the Family Assistance Application form. Applications can be submitted for the current school year only. Any Family Assistance offered will be reset at the commencement of each school year, when families may reapply. All applications are treated with utmost confidentiality. The College policy remains that assistance will be given to anyone who needs help paying fees from time to time and that no student should be denied a quality Christian education because of financial hardship.

overview: MacKillop Catholic Primary School offers family discounts to families with multiple children enrolled in Catholic Education – Diocese of Rockhampton schools. The discount reduces the all-inclusive education fee based on the number of siblings attending CEDR schools.
description: The Family Discount program is available to families with 2 or more children enrolled in Catholic Education – Diocese of Rockhampton (CEDR) schools, including MacKillop Catholic Primary School. Two siblings receive a 7.5% discount applied to the all-inclusive education fee of all siblings; three siblings receive a 20% discount; four or more siblings receive a 30% discount. The discount is applied for each year through the Confirmation of Enrolment form issued to all families. This program provides ongoing support for families with multiple children throughout their enrolment at CEDR schools.
eligibility_criteria: Two or more children enrolled in Catholic Education – Diocese of Rockhampton schools; Current enrolment at MacKillop Catholic Primary School; Completion of Confirmation of Enrolment form annually
special_conditions: Family discounts are applied annually through the Confirmation of Enrolment form. Different discount rates apply based on number of siblings: 2 siblings = 7.5% discount; 3 siblings = 20% discount; 4+ siblings = 30% discount.

overview: MacKillop Catholic Primary School offers concession card discounts to families holding government means-tested health care or pensioner concession cards. This discount significantly reduces the all-inclusive education fee for eligible families.
description: The Concession Card Discount program is available to families who hold a means-tested Government Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card that meet eligibility criteria established by the government. Eligible families receive a 40% discount applied to the all-inclusive education fee. The cards must meet specific eligibility criteria for the concession to apply. This program ensures that families experiencing financial difficulty have access to quality Catholic education at MacKillop Catholic Primary School.
eligibility_criteria: Hold a valid means-tested Government Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card; Card must meet current eligibility criteria; Current enrolment at MacKillop Catholic Primary School
special_conditions: Concession cards must meet current government eligibility criteria. Proof of valid card required. Discount applies to all-inclusive education fee.

overview: MacKillop Catholic College offers the Academic Scholarship to recognise and reward students who demonstrate outstanding academic excellence and achievement. The scholarship is designed to support academically gifted students entering secondary schooling or continuing in secondary years.
description: The Academic Scholarship covers up to 100% of the tuition component of MacKillop Catholic College fees. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning. Selection is based on performance in the ACER Level 2 Academic Scholarship Test, along with school reports and references. Recipients must maintain continued academic achievement and good conduct throughout their tenure. The scholarship covers tuition fees for the specified academic year and is conditional on participation in the College's academic program and maintaining satisfactory progress.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 to Year 12; Must register and sit the ACER Level 2 Academic Scholarship Test; Must demonstrate strong academic achievement; Must maintain excellent behaviour and conduct; Current school report required
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
value_aud: Up to 100% of tuition component of College fees
special_conditions: Scholarship is conditional on continued academic achievement and excellent behaviour. Applicants must register and pay $120 directly to ACER at the time of test registration. Scholarship may be reviewed annually based on sustained academic performance.

overview: MacKillop Catholic College offers the Music Scholarship to recognise students who demonstrate exceptional talent and engagement in music performance and study. The scholarship supports musically gifted students across all year levels.
description: The Music Scholarship covers up to 100% of tuition and music fees for the specified academic year. Recipients are selected based on demonstrated engagement and success in music, with evidence of participation in music ensembles and performances. The scholarship recognises students with strong musical ability and commitment to musical excellence at both individual and ensemble levels. The scholarship is conditional on continued participation and success in music ensembles throughout the year. Applicants must demonstrate proficiency in their chosen instrument and active involvement in music performance.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 to Year 12; Must demonstrate engagement and success in music; Must show evidence of musical proficiency and ensemble participation; Required to participate in music ensembles; Must maintain good conduct and academic standing; References and audition/portfolio required
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
value_aud: Up to 100% of tuition and music fees
special_conditions: Scholarship is conditional on participation and success in music ensembles and continued musical achievement. Recipients must actively participate in College music programs and maintain high standards of musical performance.

overview: MacKillop Catholic College offers the Sporting Excellence Scholarship to recognise students who demonstrate outstanding athletic ability and commitment to sport. The scholarship supports talented athletes entering or continuing at the College.
description: The Sporting Excellence Scholarship covers up to 100% of tuition fees for the specified academic year. Recipients are selected based on demonstrated sporting prowess, participation in competitive sport, and evidence of sporting achievement. The scholarship recognises students with exceptional athletic ability and commitment to sporting excellence. Applicants must present evidence of participation and success in their chosen sport through portfolios and coach references. The scholarship is conditional on continued participation and success in their chosen sport throughout the year, as well as maintaining acceptable academic standards and conduct.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 to Year 12; Must demonstrate sporting prowess and excellence; Must provide evidence of participation in competitive sport; Coach reference required; Must show sustained sporting achievement; Must maintain good conduct and acceptable academic standing; Portfolio of sporting involvement required
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarship is conditional on continued participation and success in the student's chosen sport. Recipients must maintain academic standards and positive behaviour. Annual review may be conducted to ensure continued sporting achievement and commitment.

overview: Maleny State School offers the G.E.M.S. program to extend and challenge students' learning. The program is aimed at students from Year 2 to Year 6 who are achieving high academic results in specific key learning areas.
description: The Generating Excellence for Maleny Students (G.E.M.S.) program is designed to extend and challenge students' learning in core academic areas. The program targets high-achieving students in Years 2 to 6 who demonstrate exceptional academic performance in specific key learning areas. This enrichment program provides advanced learning opportunities to support accelerated progress for gifted students within the primary school setting. The program operates during regular school hours as part of the school's commitment to educational excellence.
eligibility_criteria: Year 2 to Year 6; Achieving high academic results in specific key learning areas; Currently enrolled at Maleny State School
special_conditions: Recipient must remain enrolled until Year 12.

overview: Mango Hill State Secondary College offers the Academy Program to high-achieving students in Years 7–9 through a specialised curriculum designed to challenge and inspire. The English and Humanities Academy enriches students in English, History, Geography, Economics and Business, and Civics and Citizenship. This program recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to advanced learning.
description: The Academy Program enriches and challenges high-achieving Year 7-9 students through a tailored curriculum aligned with the College vision of 'Real Learning for the world of tomorrow'. Students will engage with an innovative curriculum, setting high standards for excellence and empowering outstanding academic success. The program fosters critical thinking, problem-solving, resilience, and passion for learning. This equips them with a strong academic foundation and skills to confidently navigate senior secondary pathways and post-schooling options. In 2026, the Academy Program is limited to one class for the English and Humanities pathway. The program is intended as a three-year course of study, with students engaged in the Academy from Year 7–9. The curriculum program offered provides enrichment in specific Academy subjects. Students will also engage with the core curriculum in areas outside of the academy subjects.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or Year 8 in 2026; Located within or willing to attend Mango Hill State Secondary College catchment area; Demonstrated high academic achievement; Successful completion of selection testing and application process
special_conditions: Students applying for both Academy programs will need to attend two testing days. Successful applicants who applied for both programs will be offered only one position in an Academy Program. The program is offered at no additional cost to families as it is part of the Queensland state school system.

overview: Mango Hill State Secondary College offers the STEM Academy Program to high-achieving students in Years 7–9 through a specialised curriculum. The STEM Academy enriches students in Science, Technology and Engineering, and Mathematics. This program recognises students who demonstrate exceptional achievement in STEM subjects and commitment to advanced learning in these areas.
description: The STEM Academy Program enriches and challenges high-achieving Year 7-9 students through a tailored curriculum aligned with the College vision of 'Real Learning for the world of tomorrow'. Students engage with an innovative curriculum in Science, Technology and Engineering, and Mathematics, setting high standards for excellence and empowering outstanding academic success. The program fosters critical thinking, problem-solving, resilience, and passion for learning in STEM disciplines. This equips them with a strong academic foundation and specialist skills to confidently navigate senior secondary pathways and post-schooling options. In 2026, the Academy Program is limited to one class for the STEM pathway. The program is intended as a three-year course of study, with students engaged in the Academy from Year 7–9. The curriculum program offered provides enrichment in specific Academy STEM subjects. Students will also engage with the core curriculum in areas outside of the academy subjects.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or Year 8 in 2026; Located within or willing to attend Mango Hill State Secondary College catchment area; Demonstrated high academic achievement in STEM; Successful completion of selection testing and application process
special_conditions: Students applying for both Academy programs will need to attend two testing days. Successful applicants who applied for both programs will be offered only one position in an Academy Program. The program is offered at no additional cost to families as it is part of the Queensland state school system.

overview: Mango Hill State Secondary College's Future Scholars program aims to build adaptable, resilient learners through igniting curiosity in critical local and global contexts. The program supports selected Year 7–9 students to develop problem-solving, critical and creative thinking, and self-reflection through deeply collaborative experiences. This program is no longer accepting new Year 7 applicants for 2026 but existing students continue.
description: Future Scholars aims to build adaptable, resilient learners through igniting curiosity in critical local and global contexts. Students engage in deeply collaborative experiences, developing problem-solving, critical and creative thinking, and self-reflection, towards personal goals and pathways for tomorrow. The program provides opportunities to engage with design thinking and problem solving through academically challenging curriculum, increased leveraging of digital environments by combining inquiry with technology and joining global environments, solving REAL world issues through projects and learning experiences within the community, extra-curricular events and national competitions, and collaborative and community teaching and learning with involvement in local and national networks. The College adheres to a maximum class size of 28 students for Future Scholars. Note: 2024 was the final year of Future Scholars for Year 7 students. Students currently enrolled in Future Scholars will continue through to the end of Year 9, with the final year of Year 9 offered in 2026. Application for Future Scholars is open for waitlisting only for students currently enrolled at the College.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled at Mango Hill State Secondary College; Application via waitlist only; Academic performance and NAPLAN results considered for placement; Results from academic assessments and applicant endorsement will be considered when shortlisting students if positions become available in current classes
special_conditions: Program closed to new Year 7 enrolments from 2024. Existing students continue through Year 9 with final cohort in 2026. Waitlist application only for current students. Maximum class size of 28 students. All students applying for the waitlist must submit an Expression of Interest and digital entry project.

overview: Mango Hill State Secondary College offers a selective entry Junior Dance Program for Years 7–9 students passionate about taking their dance skills to the next level. The program is designed for students with prior dance experience and provides expert instruction from professional dance teachers. The program recognises students who demonstrate dance ability and commitment to performing arts excellence.
description: The Junior Dance Program at Mango Hill State Secondary College is a selective entry program designed for Years 7-9 students with prior dance experience ready for advanced study. The program focuses on building confidence, perfecting technique, enhancing artistry, fostering teamwork, improving body awareness and appreciating the work of others in performance and choreography. Classes are delivered by professional dance teachers with extensive experience in both performing and teaching. Students train in the state-of-the-art Performing Arts Centre, equipped with top-tier dance floors, mirrors, and sound systems, ensuring an exceptional learning environment. The program includes Dance Appreciation opportunities to gain deeper understanding of the art form through working with guest choreographers, understanding different purposes for dance across cultures and styles, and observing and analysing performances. Students will showcase their talents in recitals, local performances, and competitive events in a positive, motivating space where they are challenged to reach their full potential.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 7, 8, or 9; Previous dance experience required; Audition required for entry; Currently studying Dance outside of school preferred (program aims to enhance existing study, not replace it)
special_conditions: Audition required to be considered for this selective entry program. Previous dance experience is required. For students currently studying Dance outside of school, this program aims to enhance the dancer's experience, not to replace existing study. State-of-the-art Performing Arts Centre facilities with professional-grade equipment.

school_name: Mackay Northern Beaches State High School
overview: Mackay Northern Beaches State High School offers the Academic Scholarship Program of Excellence to recognize exceptional academic achievement in students. This program provides scholarships to support students demonstrating outstanding academic ability across all year levels.
description: The Academic Scholarship Program of Excellence is designed for students demonstrating exceptional academic performance. The scholarship is valued at $350 per award and is used towards the student's education at MNBSHS, including academic competitions, uniform costs, and other educational expenses. One scholarship is awarded in this category for every year level from Years 7 to 12. The school has capacity to enrol up to 8 students annually in the Academic Program of Excellence. Selection is based on demonstrated academic achievement, merit, and a completed scholarship application form including references and supporting documentation. Scholarships are awarded annually, and successful recipients may reapply in subsequent years.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12 students; Must demonstrate exceptional academic performance; Scholarship recipients expected to be role models; All school sectors eligible
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded annually. Students who have been successful previously may re-apply. Scholarship recipients are expected to be role models for the school and participate fully in school extra-curricular programs. Scholarships may be withdrawn if expectations are not met. Applications should only include evidence of achievements from the previous two years.

school_name: Mackay Northern Beaches State High School
overview: Mackay Northern Beaches State High School offers the Arts Scholarship Program of Excellence to recognize exceptional cultural and artistic achievement. This program supports students demonstrating outstanding talent in performing and cultural arts across all year levels.
description: The Arts Scholarship Program of Excellence is designed to support students with exceptional talent in performing arts and cultural activities. The scholarship is valued at $350 per award and is used towards the student's education at MNBSHS, including participation in cultural performances, arts programs, and related educational expenses. One scholarship is awarded in this category for every year level from Years 7 to 12. The school has capacity to enrol up to 8 students annually in the Arts Program of Excellence. Selection is based on demonstrated artistic excellence, cultural performances, leadership in arts, and a completed scholarship application form with references. Scholarships are awarded annually, and successful recipients may reapply in subsequent years.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12 students; Must demonstrate exceptional achievement in cultural or performing arts; Scholarship recipients expected to be role models; All school sectors eligible
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded annually. Students who have been successful previously may re-apply. Scholarship recipients are expected to be role models for the school and participate fully in school extra-curricular programs. Scholarships may be withdrawn if expectations are not met. Applications should only include evidence of achievements from the previous two years.

school_name: Mackay Northern Beaches State High School
overview: Mackay Northern Beaches State High School offers the Sporting Scholarship Program of Excellence to recognize exceptional athletic achievement and sporting talent. This program supports students demonstrating outstanding commitment to sport across all year levels.
description: The Sporting Scholarship Program of Excellence is designed for students demonstrating exceptional athletic ability and sporting excellence. The scholarship is valued at $350 per award and is used towards the student's education at MNBSHS, including representative sports fees, sporting competitions, uniforms, and related educational costs. One scholarship is awarded in this category for every year level from Years 7 to 12. The school has capacity to enrol up to 8 students annually in the Sporting Program of Excellence. Selection is based on demonstrated sporting achievement, representative participation, leadership qualities, and a completed scholarship application form with references and supporting documentation. Scholarships are awarded annually, and successful recipients may reapply in subsequent years.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12 students; Must demonstrate exceptional sporting achievement; Scholarship recipients expected to be role models; All school sectors eligible
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded annually. Students who have been successful previously may re-apply. Scholarship recipients are expected to be role models for the school and participate fully in school extra-curricular programs. Scholarships may be withdrawn if expectations are not met. Applications should only include evidence of achievements from the previous two years.

Mastery Schools Australia - Springfield Central Campus

program_name: Advanced Computer-Aided Design and Manufacturing Program of Excellence
overview: Mirani State High School offers the Advanced Computer-Aided Design and Manufacturing Program of Excellence to students who demonstrate exceptional aptitude in design and manufacturing technology. The program supports students through the allocation of a defined number of places for both in-catchment and out-of-catchment enrolments.
description: The Advanced Computer-Aided Design and Manufacturing Program of Excellence is a selective entry program at Mirani State High School that focuses on developing students' skills in CAD technology, design, and manufacturing processes. The school has capacity to enrol 56 students in this program. Selection is based on enrolment criteria determined by the school, with priority given to in-catchment students. Out-of-catchment students may apply once in-catchment demand has been met and sufficient capacity remains. The program operates as part of the school's enrolment management plan and offers students access to advanced technology and industry-linked learning opportunities. No financial value or specific selection process details are publicly listed.
eligibility_criteria: Must satisfy school criteria for placement in the Advanced Computer-Aided Design and Manufacturing Program of Excellence; Out-of-catchment students considered only after in-catchment demand is met; Specific enrolment criteria available from the school
special_conditions: Enrolment criteria for the Programs of Excellence are available from the school. Places in the program will only be available to out-of-catchment enrolments once the demand for in-catchment enrolment has been met and sufficient student enrolment capacity has been reserved for future in-catchment growth.

program_name: Excellence in Performing Arts Program of Excellence
overview: Mirani State High School offers the Excellence in Performing Arts Program of Excellence to students who demonstrate outstanding talent and commitment in music, drama, dance, and related performing arts disciplines. The program supports talented performers through selective entry and dedicated learning pathways.
description: The Excellence in Performing Arts Program of Excellence is a selective entry program at Mirani State High School designed to nurture students with exceptional performing arts talents across music, drama, dance, and related disciplines. The school allocates a defined number of places for both in-catchment and out-of-catchment enrolments based on demonstrated artistic ability and potential. Selection is based on enrolment criteria determined by the school, with priority given to in-catchment students. Out-of-catchment students may apply once in-catchment demand has been met. The program offers students access to specialist facilities, coaching from experienced performing arts educators, and performance opportunities. No financial value is publicly listed.
eligibility_criteria: Must satisfy school criteria for placement in the Excellence in Performing Arts Program of Excellence; Out-of-catchment students considered only after in-catchment demand is met; Specific enrolment criteria available from the school
special_conditions: Enrolment criteria for the Programs of Excellence are available from the school. Places in the program will only be available to out-of-catchment enrolments once the demand for in-catchment enrolment has been met and sufficient student enrolment capacity has been reserved for future in-catchment growth.

overview: Moreton Bay Boys' College offers Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding achievement in incoming Year 7 to 11 students. The scholarships recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic excellence and exemplary conduct.
description: Academic Scholarships are offered to students entering Year 7 to 11 who are able to demonstrate academic excellence and excellent conduct. These Scholarships represent up to 50% remission in tuition fees. The selection process involves completion of a Scholarship Application Form by 3 February 2025, followed by shortlisting and an interview in April, with offers made in May. Recipients must maintain excellent academic and conduct standards throughout their enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 to Year 11; Must demonstrate academic excellence and exemplary conduct; Must complete online Enrolment Application and Scholarship Application Form
special_conditions: Shortlisted candidates will be invited to an interview in April. Offers will be made to successful candidates in May. Scholarship is awarded for the duration of enrolment at the college.

overview: Moreton Bay Boys' College offers Co-Curricular Excellence Scholarships to recognise outstanding achievement in sport. The scholarships are awarded to students who demonstrate excellence in sports while maintaining strong academic performance and excellent conduct.
description: Co-Curricular Excellence Scholarships are offered to students entering Year 7 to 11 and above who excel in Sport and are able to demonstrate consistent and sound academic achievement and excellent conduct. These Scholarships represent up to 50% remission in tuition fees. Selection involves completion of a Scholarship Application Form by 3 February 2025, shortlisting, and an interview process in April with offers made in May. Recipients must maintain consistent academic achievement and conduct throughout their enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 to Year 11 and above; Must demonstrate excellence in Sport; Must show consistent and sound academic achievement; Must have excellent conduct
special_conditions: Shortlisted candidates will be invited to an interview in April. Offers will be made to successful candidates in May. Scholarship recognises excellence in sport whilst maintaining academic standards.

overview: The Moreton Bay Colleges' Foundation offers bursaries based on financial need to support excelling students who would not otherwise be able to attend Moreton Bay Boys' College. These bursaries enable talented boys to access the opportunities presented by the college.
description: The Moreton Bay Colleges' Foundation offers several Bursaries based on financial need to students who are excelling and would not otherwise be able to enrol and avail themselves of the opportunities presented to attend Moreton Bay Boys' College. Bursaries are awarded to students demonstrating exceptional academic or co-curricular ability who face genuine financial hardship. The financial value varies depending on individual circumstances and college assessment. Applications are considered on a case-by-case basis through the Foundation, with eligibility determined through financial need assessment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering any year level; Must be excelling academically or co-curricular; Must demonstrate genuine financial need; Would not otherwise be able to afford fees at the college
special_conditions: Applications are assessed on a case-by-case basis. Students must be excelling in their studies. Bursaries are granted based on demonstrated financial need and merit. Contact the college directly to discuss individual circumstances.

overview: Moreton Bay College offers the Academic Excellence Scholarship to reward outstanding achievement in students entering Years 7 to 11 who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and exemplary conduct. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate outstanding academic achievement and commitment to learning.
description: The Academic Excellence Scholarship at Moreton Bay College is awarded to students entering Years 7 to 11 who demonstrate outstanding academic achievement and exemplary conduct. These scholarships offer up to 50% remission on tuition fees, providing significant financial support to outstanding students. The selection process includes completion of a Scholarship Application Form, with shortlisted candidates invited to an interview. The scholarship is offered on an annual basis and recipients are expected to maintain high standards of academic achievement and conduct throughout their enrolment. Applications are open each year, with selection based on demonstrated exceptional ability, character and passion for learning.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 to 11; Must demonstrate outstanding academic achievement; Must demonstrate exemplary conduct; Applications accepted annually
special_conditions: Shortlisted candidates will be invited to an interview in April. Offers will be made to successful candidates in May. Recipients must complete the Scholarship Application Form by 8 February 2026.

overview: Moreton Bay College offers the Co-Curricular Excellence Scholarship to recognise students entering Years 7 to 11 who excel in Sport, Music, or the Performing Arts while maintaining strong academic results and excellent conduct. This scholarship celebrates well-rounded students with exceptional ability in co-curricular pursuits alongside academic commitment.
description: The Co-Curricular Excellence Scholarship at Moreton Bay College is awarded to students entering Years 7 to 11 who excel in Sport, Music, or the Performing Arts and consistently achieve strong academic results and excellent conduct. These scholarships offer up to 50% remission on tuition fees. The selection process includes completion of a Scholarship Application Form, with shortlisted candidates invited to an interview in April. Recipients must demonstrate exceptional ability in their chosen co-curricular area alongside maintaining strong academic achievement and exemplary conduct. The scholarship recognises the College's commitment to developing well-rounded students who excel both in and out of the classroom.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 to 11; Must excel in Sport, Music, or Performing Arts; Must consistently achieve strong academic results; Must demonstrate excellent conduct; Applications accepted annually
special_conditions: Shortlisted candidates will be invited to an interview in April. Offers will be made to successful candidates in May. Recipients must complete the Scholarship Application Form by 8 February 2026. Applicants may be required to provide evidence of excellence in their chosen co-curricular area.

overview: Moreton Bay College offers Sporting Excellence Scholarships to students entering Year 7 and above who excel in Sport while demonstrating consistent and sound academic achievement and excellent conduct. These scholarships recognise elite athletes who contribute to the College's sporting success.
description: Sporting excellence scholarships are offered to students entering Year 7 and above, who excel in Sport and are able to demonstrate consistent and sound academic achievement and excellent conduct. Sport scholarships represent up to 50% remission in tuition fees, providing substantial financial support to talented athletes. The selection process includes an application and interview to assess both sporting ability and academic standing. Recipients must maintain high standards of academic achievement, conduct and sporting commitment throughout their enrolment. The College's Athlete Development Programme supports high performing student-athletes in achieving their sporting and academic goals.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 and above; Must excel in Sport; Must demonstrate consistent and sound academic achievement; Must demonstrate excellent conduct; Sports performance must be demonstrated
special_conditions: Shortlisted candidates will be invited to an interview in April. Offers will be made to successful candidates in May. Athletes are supported through the College's Athlete Development Programme which tailors academic and sporting needs. Recipients must maintain commitment to both academic and sporting pursuits.

overview: The Jennifer Haynes Memorial Bursary provides girls with significant academic potential the opportunity of a secondary school education at Moreton Bay College who would not otherwise have the opportunity to attend due to financial circumstances. The bursary honours the legacy of former Principal Jennifer Haynes and her advocacy for equitable access to education.
description: The Jennifer Haynes Memorial Bursary aims to provide boys and girls with significant academic potential the opportunity of an education at Moreton Bay College or Moreton Bay Boys' College. The Bursary is an initiative of the family of Jennifer Haynes, who was Principal of Moreton Bay College from 2006 to 2011 and then Executive Principal of the amalgamated colleges until 2014. These are full remission bursaries, awarded to suitable persons entering Year 7 for six years of secondary education (Years 7 to 12). The bursary is based on financial need and is designed for students who are excelling academically but would not otherwise be able to enrol and avail themselves of the opportunities presented at the College.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Year 7; Must demonstrate significant academic potential; Must demonstrate financial need; Must be excelling academically; Financial hardship must be demonstrated
special_conditions: The bursary is awarded for six years of secondary education (Years 7 to 12). The bursary is based on demonstrated financial need and significant academic potential. Not currently taking applications at this time. Applications should be directed to The Moreton Bay Colleges' Foundation.

overview: The Quentin Bryce Bursary provides girls from socially disadvantaged backgrounds with demonstrated potential to achieve high academic success the opportunity of a secondary school education at Moreton Bay College.
