Named in honour of Dame Quentin Bryce, former Governor-General of Australia and past student of the College, this bursary supports equitable access to education.
description: The Quentin Bryce Bursary provides girls from socially disadvantaged backgrounds who demonstrate potential to achieve high academic success with the opportunity of a secondary school education at Moreton Bay College. Social disadvantage refers to any girl whose family circumstances mean that she could not otherwise afford an education of the kind offered by MBC. Family circumstances, accident or illness, dislocation, migrant status or economic hardship are all considered. Girls of Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander descent are encouraged to apply. These are full remission bursaries, awarded to suitable persons entering Year 7 for six years of secondary education (Years 7 to 12). The bursary is named in honour of Dame Quentin Bryce AD, CVO, former Governor-General of the Commonwealth of Australia and past student of Moreton Bay College.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Year 7; From socially disadvantaged background; Must demonstrate potential to achieve high academic success; Must meet definition of social disadvantage; Girls of Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander descent encouraged to apply
special_conditions: The bursary is awarded for six years of secondary education (Years 7 to 12). Applicants must demonstrate clear evidence of social or financial disadvantage. Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander applicants are particularly encouraged. Not currently taking applications. Applications should be directed to The Moreton Bay Colleges' Foundation.

overview: Montessori International College offers bursaries to existing families who require financial assistance. The College recognises the importance of ensuring that a quality Montessori education remains accessible to families across a broad cross-section of the community.
description: Bursaries are available to existing families at Montessori International College. The College provides financial assistance to support families who may be experiencing financial hardship or difficulty in meeting tuition fees. The specific financial benefit amount is not specified on the website and families are directed to contact the College directly for more information. Selection is based on demonstrated financial need and families must be currently enrolled at the school to be eligible. The College operates on the principle that affordability is important, and they aim to keep fees as low as possible while maintaining financial viability. Payment plans and prepayment discounts are also offered alongside bursary support.
eligibility_criteria: Must be currently enrolled at Montessori International College; Demonstrated financial need or hardship; Contact College directly for application process and specific requirements
special_conditions: Available only to existing families; Families must contact the College directly for details on application process and eligibility criteria; Bursaries are subject to the College's financial policies and procedures; No specific financial amounts or coverage percentages are published on the website.

overview: Mount Archer State School offers the Specialist Instrumental Music Program to students in Years 4, 5, and 6 who demonstrate musical ability. Selected students receive free instrumental tuition in brass, woodwind, percussion, and string instruments, with the program continuing through to Year 12 at the state high school level.
description: The Specialist Instrumental Music Program provides free lessons in Brass, Woodwind, Percussion, and String instruments to selected students. The program offers full tuition fee remission for instrumental lessons and allows children to continue with free tuition through to Year 12 at a State High School. Selection is undertaken through an audition/selection process. Students who are selected are expected to participate in the school concert band, Combined Schools Workshops and Concert at the end of the year, and school events. There is an expectation that after the first 12 months of loan, students will purchase their own instrument, particularly smaller instruments such as flutes, trumpets, violins, etc. Parents who wish their children to participate must contact the school to arrange the selection process.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled in Year 4, Year 5, or Year 6; Must pass selection process; Must meet certain requirements as participating students
special_conditions: Program allows children free tuition through to Year 12 at a State High School. Students are expected to participate in the school concert band, Combined Schools Workshops and Concert at the end of the year, and other school events. After the first 12 months of loan, students are expected to purchase their own instrument, particularly smaller instruments such as flutes, trumpets, violins, etc.

overview: Mount Alvernia College offers the Academic Scholarship Level 1 to support education opportunities for students commencing in Year 7. The scholarship recognises general excellence or high potential in academic disciplines and provides substantial fee assistance for students entering secondary education.
description: Mount Alvernia College's Level 1 Academic Scholarship is designed for students sitting the ACER Scholarship Test during their final year of primary school for entry into Year 7. The scholarship offers 50-100% fee reduction for tuition fees only, covering Years 7, 8, and 9. Applicants are selected based on their performance in the ACER Scholarship Test administered in February. Students must demonstrate commitment to academic achievement, support for the College's Franciscan ethos and mission, adherence to the Student Behaviour Code, and capacity to serve as a role model for academic excellence. The scholarship is awarded at the discretion of the College Principal following completion of the standardised test.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6; Must sit ACER Scholarship Test in February; Demonstrate commitment and dedication to achieve in academic studies; Support the College's ethos, mission, and vision; Support all aspects of the Student Behaviour Code; Demonstrate capacity to be a role model encouraging pursuit of excellence; Open to all applicants regardless of current school sector
special_conditions: Scholarships cover tuition fees only. Awards are offered on a 50-100% basis for the duration of enrolment in Years 7, 8, and 9. All scholarships are awarded at the discretion of the College Principal. Test registration and application must be completed through the ACER Scholarship Test website.

overview: Mount Alvernia College offers the Academic Scholarship Level 2 to support education opportunities for students commencing in Year 10. The scholarship recognises general excellence or high potential in academic disciplines and provides substantial fee assistance for students entering the senior years.
description: Mount Alvernia College's Level 2 Academic Scholarship is designed for students in Year 9 sitting the ACER Scholarship Test for entry into Year 10. The scholarship offers 50-100% fee reduction for tuition fees only, covering Years 10, 11, and 12. Applicants are selected based on their performance in the ACER Scholarship Test administered in February. Students must demonstrate commitment to academic achievement, support for the College's Franciscan ethos and mission, adherence to the Student Behaviour Code, and capacity to serve as a role model for academic excellence. The scholarship is awarded at the discretion of the College Principal following completion of the standardised test.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 9; Must sit ACER Scholarship Test in February; Demonstrate commitment and dedication to achieve in academic studies; Support the College's ethos, mission, and vision; Support all aspects of the Student Behaviour Code; Demonstrate capacity to be a role model encouraging pursuit of excellence; Open to all applicants
special_conditions: Scholarships cover tuition fees only. Awards are offered on a 50-100% basis for the duration of enrolment in Years 10, 11, and 12. All scholarships are awarded at the discretion of the College Principal. Test registration and application must be completed through the ACER Scholarship Test website.

overview: Mount Alvernia College offers fee concessions to families experiencing financial hardship. This program provides eligible applicants with relief from school fees to ensure students can continue their education regardless of financial circumstances.
description: Mount Alvernia College recognises that some families may experience financial hardship during their child's enrolment. The College offers fee concessions on a case-by-case basis for eligible applicants. Families experiencing genuine financial difficulty are encouraged to contact the Finance Manager to discuss available options and the application process. Specific documentation and supporting evidence of financial hardship must be submitted with the application. The College works with families on a collaborative basis to find reasonable solutions and maintain student enrolment despite financial challenges. Eligibility and relief amounts are determined individually based on family circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled or applying for enrolment; Family experiencing genuine financial hardship; Demonstrated need through submission of financial documentation; Non-discriminatory assessment of each application; Good standing regarding existing financial obligations when applicable
special_conditions: Applications must be made directly to the College Finance Manager. Specific documentation supporting the financial hardship claim must be submitted. The College reserves the right to investigate applications and may require additional information. Fee concessions are available on a case-by-case basis and are not automatic. The College's commitment to non-discrimination means that educational need is carefully considered in all decisions.

overview: Mount Gravatt High School offers the Academic EDGE Program as a selective entry program for students entering Year 7. This Junior Secondary signature program aims to promote the pursuit of excellence by equipping students with the skills of creative and critical thinking, inquiry, collaboration and a sense of community.
description: Academic EDGE is designed for gifted students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and potential for advanced learning. Students in this program interact with peers who are able to think at the same depth and pace in a supportive, low-risk, high-challenge learning environment. The program provides additional opportunities including Writers' Workshop, participation in extra-curricular activities such as Maths Team Challenge, Robotics, STEM, Public Speaking competitions, Debating, and Drama. Entry into the Academic EDGE cohort is based on merit, and candidates must undertake an application process to be considered for this program. There is no direct financial benefit, but students gain access to exclusive curriculum experiences and extension activities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Merit-based selection; Australian students; Demonstrated academic excellence and potential
special_conditions: Selective entry based on merit. Students may only participate in one EDGE program. If successful, the program becomes their Year 7 class.

overview: Mount Gravatt High School offers the Music EDGE Program as a selective entry program for students entering Year 7. This Junior Secondary signature program provides students with an enhanced music curriculum designed for musically talented students to develop their skills and passion for music.
description: Music EDGE is a one-year course open to Year 7 students accepted for entry in 2025. The program provides extensive opportunities for musically talented students to develop their abilities through practical skills. In Term 1, students develop practical skills on both keyboard and guitar. Term 2 focuses on students creating their own original compositions using notation software. The year culminates in a performance project that leads to a live or virtual class performance. Students also benefit from multi-disciplinary excursions to QPAC backstage, QAG and/or Sparklab. Entry into the Music EDGE cohort is based on merit, and candidates undertake an application process. Students can participate in both Music EDGE and Instrumental Music Excellence, which are complementary programs. There is no direct financial benefit.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Merit-based selection; Demonstration of musical ability or interest; Must be accepted for Year 7 entry
special_conditions: Selective entry based on merit. Students may nominate for all EDGE programs but can only participate in one program that becomes their Year 7 class. Students can be in both Music EDGE and Instrumental Music Excellence as complementary programs. Dependent upon availability of places.

overview: Mount Gravatt High School offers the Japanese EDGE Program as a selective entry program for students entering Year 7. This Junior Secondary signature program provides extensive opportunities for students to develop and gain confidence in their Japanese language knowledge, skills and abilities while broadening their cultural awareness.
description: Japanese EDGE is designed for Year 7 students who have studied Japanese in primary school or are able to speak Japanese. The program provides an engaging curriculum that delivers strong foundations and opportunities for a diverse range of language learners to extend their comprehension and improve their communication abilities. Students are enriched through targeted teaching and learning experiences and enjoy additional extension opportunities. Entry into the Japanese EDGE cohort is merit-based. Candidates must undertake an application process to be considered for this program. The program offers no direct financial benefit but provides enhanced language and cultural education.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Merit-based selection; Prior Japanese language study in primary school or ability to speak Japanese; Demonstrated language potential
special_conditions: Selective entry based on merit. Candidates must have studied Japanese in primary school or be able to speak Japanese. Students may nominate for all EDGE programs but can only participate in one program that becomes their Year 7 class.

overview: Mount Gravatt High School offers the Instrumental Music Excellence program as a selective entry program for Year 7 students. This excellence program provides extensive opportunities for musically high-performing students to further develop and refine performance skills on their chosen instrument.
description: The Instrumental Music Excellence program is designed for Year 7 students who demonstrate high-level instrumental music ability. The program is suitable for students playing brass, woodwind, percussion, or strings instruments (not piano). This excellence program focuses on developing and refining performance skills on the student's chosen instrument. Students participate in group tuition from specialist visiting teachers and develop their skills through weekly participation in a variety of both large and small ensembles. Each student is placed in one compulsory core ensemble (Concert Band or String Ensemble) and is encouraged to participate in Jazz or small ensembles. Applications for the program are considered based on musical ability and performance standards. There is no direct financial benefit provided.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 students; Demonstration of high instrumental music ability; Proficiency in brass, woodwind, percussion, or strings; Excluding piano students; Merit-based selection
special_conditions: Opening and closing dates for IM Excellence applications updated in February 2026. Participation in at least one ensemble is mandatory. 95% attendance is expected for rehearsals. Students can participate in both Music EDGE and Instrumental Music Excellence as complementary programs. Specialist teachers provide tuition in brass, woodwind, strings and percussion.

overview: Mount Gravatt High School offers the Dance Excellence Program to foster excellence in dance. Mount Gravatt High offers classroom Dance in Years 7 to 12, and the program is further augmented by an Elite Dance Troupe and a Junior Troupe where students can develop their skills, technique and performance ability.
description: The Dance Excellence Program aims to foster the specific talents and aspirations of each individual student, allowing students at an early age the opportunity to develop their talent to its fullest potential. Students can study dance from Years 7 to 12 and have the opportunity to work toward the Certificate III in Dance. The program is supported through the allocation of a defined number of places, with out-of-catchment enrolments considered once in-catchment demand has been met. Students can participate in both the Elite Dance Troupe and Junior Troupe, which meet weekly for rehearsals. Auditions are held early in Term 1. The program culminates in performances at eisteddfods where students compete in Contemporary and Jazz genre sections. Mount Gravatt has a long history of success in performing arts with many past students following professional careers in dance. There is no direct financial benefit provided.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7-12; Dance ability and potential; Out-of-catchment students considered subject to availability; Successful audition required; Commitment to weekly rehearsals
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Auditions held early Term 1. Weekly rehearsals Monday, Thursday and Friday 12:45-1:25pm. Out-of-catchment students only enroled after in-catchment demand met and capacity reserved for future growth. Eligibility criteria available from school. Performance at eisteddfods in Contemporary and Jazz genres. Certificate III in Dance available.

overview: Mount Gravatt High School offers the Athlete Development Program (ADP) as a complete sports education and training program aimed to assist students in the pursuit of sporting excellence. The ADP is an extra-curricular opportunity available to students throughout the school year.
description: The Athlete Development Program is a comprehensive sports education and training program designed for students pursuing sporting excellence. The ADP runs for the entire year as an extra-curricular opportunity at Mount Gravatt High. The program includes a physical conditioning component run before school by strength and conditioning coaches and service providers. The program provides systematic training and development opportunities for elite athletes. According to third-party sources, the school pays 50% of fees for participating students in the elite athletics program, making it an attractive offer for aspiring athletes. The ADP works in conjunction with the school's representative sporting opportunities at district, regional, state and national levels through the Queensland Representative School Sport (QRSS) program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students; Demonstrated sporting ability and commitment; Selection based on potential and dedication to athletic development; Previous club or representative experience may be required for some pathways
special_conditions: Program runs for entire year. Physical conditioning sessions held before school. Nomination process available for district/representative sport trials. Students selected via school performance at Mount Gravatt High carnivals for key events (swimming, cross-country, athletics). Strength and conditioning coaches and external service providers involved.

overview: Mount Gravatt High School offers an Instrumental Music Program that provides students the opportunity to receive tuition on a musical instrument. Students develop musicianship in a fun and supportive environment with specialist teachers visiting weekly.
description: The Instrumental Music Program at Mount Gravatt State High School provides students the opportunity to receive tuition on a musical instrument from four families: Brass, Woodwind, Percussion and Strings. Specialist teachers visit the school each week to offer group tuition. Skills learned are developed through weekly participation in a variety of both large and small ensembles. Each child is placed in one compulsory core ensemble (Concert Band or String Ensemble). Students are then encouraged to participate in Jazz or small ensembles. Throughout the year ensembles participate in a wide variety of events. All students participate in at least one weekly rehearsal before or after school. The program supports students to develop a high level of musicianship and offers multiple performance opportunities including musical evenings and school events.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students interested in learning an instrument; Enrolment in Years 7-12; Commitment to weekly ensemble participation mandatory; 95% attendance expected
special_conditions: 95% attendance is expected. Participation in at least one ensemble is mandatory if enrolled in Instrumental Music. Specialist teachers provide tuition in Brass, Woodwind, Strings and Percussion. All students participate in at least one weekly rehearsal before or after school. Coordinated by Instrumental Music Coordinator: mfell12@eq.edu.au.

school_name: Mount Isa School of the Air

program_name: Sr Mary McHugh General Excellence Scholarship 2026
overview: Mount St Bernard College offers the Sr Mary McHugh General Excellence Scholarship to recognise outstanding achievement across academic and co-curricular endeavours. This scholarship supports students who demonstrate excellence and commitment to their studies while contributing positively to the College community.
description: The Sr Mary McHugh General Excellence Scholarship is available for entry to Years 7-12 at Mount St Bernard College. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional general excellence across academic performance and co-curricular activities. The award may be full or partial towards the cost of tuition, and awards will commence in 2026. Subject to the conditions detailed in the offer letter, the scholarship will generally continue for the duration of the student's enrolment at Mount St Bernard College until graduation from Year 12. All scholarships and bursaries are subject to specific terms and conditions.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7-12; Must demonstrate general excellence in academics and co-curricular activities; Subject to Mount St Bernard College application and selection process; All scholarships subject to specific terms and conditions
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Awards will commence in 2026, and, subject to the conditions detailed, the offer letter will generally continue for the duration of the student's enrolment at Mount St Bernard College until graduation from Year 12.

overview: The Colleen Frank Sims Scholarship is offered by Mount St Bernard College to support students of demonstrated merit. This scholarship was established through the generosity of Colleen and Frank Sims to provide financial assistance to eligible students.
description: The Colleen Frank Sims Scholarship is available for entry to Years 7-12 at Mount St Bernard College and recognises students of demonstrated merit. The award may be full or partial towards the cost of tuition. Awards will commence in 2026 and will generally continue for the duration of the student's enrolment at Mount St Bernard College until graduation from Year 12. This scholarship was established through the generosity of Colleen and Frank Sims and Mount St Bernard College. All scholarships and bursaries are subject to specific terms and conditions.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7-12; Must demonstrate merit as determined by College assessment; Must be selected through Mount St Bernard College application process; Subject to specific terms and conditions
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Awards will commence in 2026, and, subject to the conditions detailed, the offer letter will generally continue for the duration of the student's enrolment at Mount St Bernard College until graduation from Year 12.

program_name: Sr Anne Moule Principal's 2026 – 2028 General Excellence Scholarship
overview: Mount St Bernard College offers the Sr Anne Moule Principal's General Excellence Scholarship to recognise exceptional students across multiple dimensions of excellence. This scholarship is awarded for a three-year period from 2026-2028.
description: The Sr Anne Moule Principal's General Excellence Scholarship is available for entry to Years 7-12 at Mount St Bernard College. This scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional excellence and is awarded for a three-year period from 2026 through 2028. The award may be full or partial towards the cost of tuition. Scholarships are offered on a three-year basis. The offer letter will generally continue for the duration of the student's enrolment at Mount St Bernard College until graduation from Year 12. All scholarships and bursaries are subject to specific terms and conditions.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7-12; Must demonstrate exceptional general excellence; Selected through Mount St Bernard College application and assessment process; Award period 2026-2028; Subject to specific terms and conditions
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered on a three-year basis (2026-2028). The offer letter will generally continue for the duration of the student's enrolment at Mount St Bernard College until graduation from Year 12. All scholarships and bursaries are subject to specific terms and conditions.

overview: Mount St Bernard College offers the Senior Academic Scholarship to recognise outstanding academic achievement in senior students. This scholarship supports students entering Years 10-12 who demonstrate exceptional academic ability.
description: The Senior Academic Scholarship is available for entry to Years 10-12 at Mount St Bernard College and recognises students who demonstrate outstanding academic achievement. The application process includes an essay (up to 500 words) by the applicant outlining what it would mean to receive the scholarship. The award may be full or partial towards the cost of tuition. Awards will commence in 2026 and will generally continue for the duration of the student's enrolment at Mount St Bernard College until graduation from Year 12. Students not currently attending Mount St Bernard College must have an Application for Enrolment form accompanying their scholarship application. All scholarships and bursaries are subject to specific terms and conditions.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 10-12; Must demonstrate outstanding academic achievement; Must submit essay (up to 500 words); If not currently enrolled at MSB, must submit Application for Enrolment; Subject to specific terms and conditions
special_conditions: Applicants must submit an essay (up to 500 words) outlining what it would mean to receive the scholarship. If currently attending a school other than Mount St Bernard College and have not already applied for enrolment, an Application for Enrolment form must accompany the scholarship application. Awards will commence in 2026 and will generally continue for the duration of the student's enrolment at Mount St Bernard College until graduation from Year 12.

program_name: Sr Pam Murray Performing Arts Scholarship 2026
overview: Mount St Bernard College offers the Sr Pam Murray Performing Arts Scholarship to recognise outstanding talent in music and the performing arts. This scholarship supports students who demonstrate exceptional ability and commitment to the arts.
description: The Sr Pam Murray Performing Arts Scholarship is available for entry to Years 7-12 at Mount St Bernard College and recognises students who demonstrate exceptional performing arts ability, particularly in music and related disciplines. The award may be full or partial towards the cost of tuition. Awards will commence in 2026 and will generally continue for the duration of the student's enrolment at Mount St Bernard College until graduation from Year 12. This scholarship was established to recognise excellence in the performing arts. Students demonstrate their talent through the application and audition process. All scholarships and bursaries are subject to specific terms and conditions.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7-12; Must demonstrate exceptional performing arts talent; Selection through application and audition process; Subject to specific terms and conditions
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Awards will commence in 2026, and, subject to the conditions detailed, the offer letter will generally continue for the duration of the student's enrolment at Mount St Bernard College until graduation from Year 12. Selection includes audition process to assess performing arts talent.

program_name: Sr Mary Randle Year 7 Academic Scholarship 2026
overview: Mount St Bernard College offers the Sr Mary Randle Year 7 Academic Scholarship to recognise outstanding academic achievement in students entering Year 7. This scholarship rewards students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability at the point of entry to secondary schooling.
description: The Sr Mary Randle Year 7 Academic Scholarship is specifically available for entry to Year 7 at Mount St Bernard College. This scholarship recognises students who demonstrate outstanding academic achievement and exceptional ability. The award may be full or partial towards the cost of tuition. Awards will commence in 2026 and will generally continue for the duration of the student's enrolment at Mount St Bernard College until graduation from Year 12. Selection is based on demonstrated academic excellence and merit. All scholarships and bursaries are subject to specific terms and conditions.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate outstanding academic achievement; Must meet Mount St Bernard College merit assessment requirements; Subject to specific terms and conditions
special_conditions: Scholarships are limited to students entering Year 7. Awards will commence in 2026, and, subject to the conditions detailed, the offer letter will generally continue for the duration of the student's enrolment at Mount St Bernard College until graduation from Year 12.

overview: Mountain Creek State High School offers the Young Scholars Program to Year 5 and Year 6 students from local key partner primary schools. The program is designed to provide enrichment opportunities for bright and motivated students who demonstrate outstanding academic achievement and excellence across multiple disciplines.
description: The Young Scholars Program is designed to reward outstanding young students who have succeeded academically, are good role models to their peers, and are strong in one or more areas of sport, art, dance, drama, music, public speaking and/or leadership, debating and information technology. Young Scholars will participate in a number of challenging activities throughout the year covering a range of subject areas including Science, Maths, English, Humanities, Information Technology, Creative Industries (Music, Art, Dance, Drama), Business, Home Economics, Japanese and Spanish. The program recognises 'all-roundedness' in students to provide them with an education for life in the 21st century. Students maintain their enrolment at their primary school while participating in the Young Scholars Program at Mountain Creek State High School.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 5 and Year 6 students; Must be enrolled in one of Mountain Creek State High School's local key partner primary schools; Must demonstrate outstanding academic achievement; Must be a good role model to peers; Must demonstrate strength in one or more areas including sport, art, dance, drama, music, public speaking, leadership, debating and/or information technology
special_conditions: Young Scholars will not gain automatic entry into the Zenith Program. They will need to complete a separate Zenith application.

overview: Mountain Creek State High School offers the Zenith Program for Year 7 students who are academically gifted. The program is designed for bright, highly motivated students to be grouped together and challenged by their peers to work hard and continue to succeed in their studies.
description: The Zenith Program is designed for academically-gifted students beginning Junior Secondary, introduced in 2007. The program allows bright, highly motivated students to be grouped together so they can be challenged and encouraged by their peers to work hard and continue to succeed in their studies. The program is characterised by accelerated learning and the encouragement of students to study subjects in greater depth. Students develop rigorous study skills and real capacity to perform academically. They are involved in community service projects and enriching extracurricular activities, including participating in an array of competitions across faculty areas and greater emphasis on reading novels. The program ensures that students who achieve academically at primary school remain engaged during critical ages 12-15, keeping them motivated for their senior years (Years 10-12) where they will work at higher levels and faster pace.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 students; Must demonstrate academic excellence; Must be highly motivated; Students must register expression of interest and complete Zenith application; Young Scholars do not automatically progress to Zenith and must apply separately
special_conditions: Expression of interest must be registered and returned by Friday 30 May 2025. Applications available from school website or in-person from school administration.

overview: Mountain Creek State High School offers the Kin'Ma Program for Year 8 and Year 9 students. The program is tailored to students' specific academic abilities in individual core subjects and allows extension in selected core subjects.
description: The Kin'Ma program is tailored to students' specific academic abilities in individual core subjects. Unlike the Zenith program which expects students to achieve at the highest level across all core subjects, Kin'Ma allows for extension in just some or all core subjects based on individual student strengths. This is an invite-only program, with offers going out late the year before the student commences Year 8 or Year 9. The program provides students with extension and acceleration opportunities in their strongest academic areas while maintaining appropriate challenge levels across all subjects.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 8 and Year 9 students; Must achieve at the highest level in their cohort for individual core subjects; Invite-only program based on prior academic performance; Offers issued late in the year preceding Year 8 or Year 9 entry
special_conditions: This is an invite-only program. Students do not apply; invitations are issued by the school based on academic performance.

overview: Mountain Creek State High School offers comprehensive Sports Programs of Excellence in AFL, Basketball, Rugby League, Netball and Volleyball. These programs are designed for students who wish to specialise in these sports through structured, timetabled classes.
description: The Sports Programs of Excellence at Mountain Creek State High School exemplify the school motto of Quality, Opportunity and Excellence and are purposefully designed to cultivate, develop and enhance student potential. The sports excellence programs enhance learning by engaging students in specific physical performance objectives. Programs are offered in AFL, basketball, rugby league, netball and volleyball and provide timetabled classes that sit within both the Australian Curriculum and QCAA Applied curriculum. Each program is led by highly accomplished and specialist teachers with extensive playing and coaching experience at state, national and international levels. Staff are dedicated to developing students' skills and gameplay at the highest level. Participation requires a significant commitment of time and effort outside of school hours, including attending all training sessions, meetings and events.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students interested in specialising in AFL, basketball, rugby league, netball or volleyball; Must consistently display high standards of conduct and behaviour in classes and school community; Must participate positively in learning with effort and engagement; Must be prepared for learning daily with charged device; Must complete all assessment tasks by due date; Must follow uniform policy and wear formal uniform when travelling to events; Must be prepared with correct program uniforms, equipment and footwear; Must commit to attending all training sessions, meetings and events including outside school hours; Must meet criteria in school enrolment management plan for Programs of Excellence
special_conditions: Out-of-catchment students applying for Programs of Excellence must pay a non-refundable processing fee of $50. Enrolment in Programs of Excellence is subject to available capacity. Criteria available from school. Program participation requires significant time commitment outside school hours.

overview: Mountain Creek State High School offers the Creative Industries Collective, a comprehensive development and excellence program that nurtures the passion and skill of emerging artists. The program supports students in music, drama, dance and visual arts.
description: The Creative Industries Collective is a comprehensive development and excellence program that nurtures the passion and skill of Mountain Creek State High School's emerging artists. The program provides structured pathways for students interested in pursuing excellence in the arts including music, drama, dance and visual arts. Students have opportunities to participate in musical ensembles, drama productions and various cultural performances and events throughout the year, as well as opportunities for international exposure trips and connections with other schools and cultural institutions.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students with demonstrated passion and skill in music, drama, dance or visual arts; Students must be willing to commit to regular rehearsals, performances and cultural activities; Ability to work collaboratively in ensemble and group settings
special_conditions: This is a program for students with demonstrated arts abilities. Participation requires commitment to regular rehearsals, performances and cultural activities outside regular school hours.

overview: Education Queensland International (EQI) offers scholarships for international students aged 15-17 to study at Mountain Creek State High School. Eligible students receive a waiver of AUD $5,000 in the first year of their studies.
description: EQI scholarships give exemplary international students aged 15-17 years old the opportunity to enjoy a rewarding and life-changing study experience in a Queensland Government School. Eligible students will receive a waiver of AUD $5,000 in the first year of their studies at Mountain Creek State High School. Scholarship recipients receive a portion of the tuition fees for their study program. To apply, you must enroll in an EQI International Student program for at least one year. Mountain Creek State High School is situated at the Sunshine Coast Campus, close to Mooloolaba Beach, and offers quality education including study in the IB Diploma with an extensive range of subjects, dynamic learning environment and quality facilities. Scholarships are awarded based on merit at the sole discretion of the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to international students aged 15-17 years old; Must be enrolled in an EQI International Student program for at least one year; Must demonstrate exemplary academic achievement; All applicants must register as students via Scholarships2u.com; All applicants must make application via Scholarships2u.com portal; Must submit certified copy of recent exam results upon request; Scholarship covers tuition fees only
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded based on merit at sole discretion of school. Any or all scholarship values published are indicative and do not indicate actual awards. Scholarships information subject to change without notice. Submission of application and meeting minimum criteria does not guarantee scholarship. Terms and conditions apply.

program_name: Mt St Michael's College Sisters of Charity Scholarship
overview: Mt St Michael's College offers the Sisters of Charity Scholarship to provide financial support to a young woman from a disadvantaged background for her secondary schooling education. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate financial hardship and commitment to achieving their academic potential while supporting the ethos and values of the College.
description: The Mt St Michael's College Sisters of Charity Scholarship was established to provide financial support to a young woman from a disadvantaged background for her secondary schooling education at MSM. The scholarship provides assistance to students who are identified as experiencing financial hardship and who are not currently enrolled at the College at the time of being offered the scholarship. The exact financial value of the scholarship is not specified on the College website, but it provides meaningful financial support to assist eligible students with their educational costs. The selection process involves application, with applicants required to demonstrate commitment to achieving their academic potential and support for the College's ethos and values. Scholarships awarded for longer than one year are subject to an annual review to ensure continued eligibility and satisfactory progress.
eligibility_criteria: Student not currently enrolled at Mt St Michael's College at time of scholarship offer; Student and family demonstrate support for the ethos and values of Mt St Michael's College; Student demonstrates commitment to achieving their academic potential; Student and family identified as experiencing financial hardship; Australian Citizen or Permanent Resident; Resident in Queensland
special_conditions: Applications advertised via College website, local Parish newsletters and direct contact with Catholic Primary schools. A scholarship awarded for longer than one year is subject to an annual review. The College is committed to giving special consideration to enrolment of students who are materially, socially, spiritually, physically or emotionally disadvantaged.

program_name: Financial Hardship Assistance for Current Families
overview: Mt St Michael's College provides financial hardship assistance to current families who are experiencing financial difficulties. This support program helps families who are already enrolled at the College to access financial relief to ensure their daughters can continue their education.
description: Mt St Michael's College has assistance available for current families suffering financial hardship. This program provides flexible support options to families who are experiencing financial difficulties while their daughters are enrolled at the College. The assistance is designed to help alleviate financial pressures and enable students to continue their secondary education without disruption. The exact structure, application process, and financial values available through this program are not detailed on the public scholarship page. Families seeking this assistance are encouraged to contact the Finance Manager directly to discuss their circumstances and available support options. The program demonstrates the College's commitment to ensuring that financial barriers do not prevent students from accessing their education.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled at Mt St Michael's College; Family experiencing financial hardship; Australian resident
special_conditions: Program is confidential and flexible based on individual family circumstances. Applications and enquiries should be directed to the Finance Manager at the College.

school_name: Mungalla Silver Lining School - Carins Temporary Facility

overview: Music Industry College established the Ken Wood Scholarship Fund to remove financial barriers for students passionate about music. The fund provides partial financial assistance to eligible students who wish to enrol but face financial constraints. Approximately 25% of MIC students require fee assistance annually, and this fund supports their participation.
description: The Ken Wood Scholarship Fund is a charity fund named after Brett Wood's father, established to ensure fees are not a barrier to attendance at Music Industry College. The fund offers partial scholarships for tuition fees rather than full scholarships. Students who experience financial hardship can apply through an application process assessed by the Charity Sub-Committee of the Board. The fund operates on a means-tested basis, evaluating individual financial circumstances. Applicants must demonstrate financial need through the application process, and decisions are made on a case-by-case basis. The scholarship supports Year 11 and Year 12 students at the college.
eligibility_criteria: Open to prospective students years 11-12; Demonstrated financial need required; Application to charity fund; Students must be passionate about music; Australian residency recommended
special_conditions: Applications assessed on individual basis through Charity Sub-Committee of the Board. Committee recommendations are final. No full scholarships available. Partial scholarships may be awarded depending on available funds and individual circumstances.

program_name: The Fortitude Music Hall & The Triffid Scholarship
overview: The Fortitude Music Hall and The Triffid venues jointly offer a fully funded scholarship to Music Industry College students. The scholarship recognises passion for music and commitment to the industry. Recipients also receive mentoring support to enrich their educational experience.
description: This scholarship is a fully funded opportunity for a successful applicant to attend Music Industry College for two years at no cost. The partnership between two of Brisbane's leading music venues demonstrates community support for music education. The scholarship includes a mentoring program that provides additional support beyond financial assistance. Recipients are selected to benefit from the industry experience and connections provided by these established music venues. The scholarship was created through fundraising efforts by The Fortitude Music Hall and The Triffid, with over $8,000 raised in an initial fundraiser in November 2023. Full tuition fee remission is provided for the duration of the two-year award.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 11-12 students enrolling at Music Industry College; Passionate about music and the music industry; Selection by partnership venues based on potential and need
special_conditions: Full scholarship for two years. Includes mentoring program attached to scholarship. Recipients must actively participate in mentoring and maintain engagement with both music venues as ambassadors.

overview: Murrumba State Secondary College offers the Engineering Excellence Program to provide students with an extension from their core studies into the Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics fields. The program paves the way for students to move into Senior Engineering, Mathematics and Science subjects with the aim to continue on in these areas at university level.
description: The Engineering Excellence Program (EEP) is a selective admission program available to Years 7-10 that extends students into STEM disciplines. Students engage with units including Robotics, Design, Engineering, Sustainability, Food Technology, Environmental Engineering, Polymers, Biotechnology and Forensic Science. All successful applicants must participate in a variety of competitions throughout the program. Students in the EEP are also enrolled in Mathematics Extension and Science Extension, completing similar programs to core classes but extended in both depth and breadth. The program prepares students for senior engineering and advanced mathematics subjects. Entry is via entrance examination with a required application fee. Costs are variable; please refer to the Fee Matrix on the College website for current pricing.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7, 8, 9, 10; Must successfully pass entrance examination; Entrance exam attracts a fee; Must maintain high academic results and effort to retain placement
special_conditions: All successful applicants are required to participate in a variety of competitions. Students must maintain high academic results in all relevant subjects as well as high effort and behaviour to retain their place in the Program of Excellence. Students can apply for multiple programs; however, they will only be selected for one Program of Excellence.

overview: Murrumba State Secondary College offers the Football Academy to develop talented young footballers into holistic athletes while emphasising the importance of academic success and exemplary behaviour. The Academy employs a student-athlete model combining academic performance with positive behaviour expectations and sporting performance to achieve success.
description: The Football Academy is an Excellence program available to students from Years 7-10 that aims to provide a pathway to a career in football for talented secondary-aged players. The program employs a student-athlete model where qualified and experienced staff work with students to develop on- and off-field skills. The practical lessons develop technical fundamentals that progress to team-based application, while the theory component covers student-athletes, football excellence, performance management and development. Students receive exposure to sessions including fielding, indoor futsal, strength and conditioning, match-specific conditioning, sports psychology and video analysis. The Football Academy is proudly affiliated with Brisbane Roar, Football Queensland and TAFE QLD Academy of Sport. Senior students can pursue Certificate II Sport Coaching or Certificate III in Sport, Aquatics and Recreation through TAFE Queensland. Entry is via expression of interest and successful trial. Students must be prepared to participate in all practical lessons including before-school sessions and conduct themselves according to the Football Academy player code of conduct. Costs vary; refer to Fee Matrix on College website.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7, 8, 9, 10; Must submit expression of interest; Must complete successful trial; Must be prepared to participate in all practical lessons including before-school sessions; Must conduct themselves in accordance with Football Academy player code of conduct; Must maintain high academic results and effort to retain placement
special_conditions: Entry via expression of interest and successful trial. Students must be prepared to participate in all practical lessons including before-school sessions where required. Students must conduct themselves in accordance with the Football Academy player code of conduct. Students must maintain high academic results in all relevant subjects as well as high effort and behaviour to retain their place in the Program of Excellence. Students can apply for multiple programs; however, they will only be selected for one Program of Excellence. The Football Academy provides numerous opportunities for students to represent the College in Football and Futsal, as well as Queensland School Sport representation at District, Metropolitan, State and National levels.

overview: Murrumba State Secondary College offers the High Performance Music Program to assist in the development of students' all-round musicianship and to use their passion for music to enhance their academic success. The program provides students in the Murrumba area with access to specialised teaching and performance opportunities.
description: The High Performance Music Program is an Excellence program available to Years 7-10 that develops student musicianship at an advanced level. A strong emphasis is placed on both the practical and theoretical side of music, offering students many opportunities to develop their skills through specialised teaching and performance experiences. Students complete an application and audition for entry into the program. All students must be prepared to fully commit to both class requirements and their chosen performance pathways. The program offers students opportunities to develop instrumental and vocal skills through strings, percussion, wind bands, contemporary ensembles and vocal groups. Performance opportunities are available through school concerts, assemblies, community events and partnerships with higher education institutions. Costs are variable; refer to Fee Matrix on College website.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7, 8, 9, 10; Must complete application and audition; Must be prepared to fully commit to both class requirements and chosen performance pathways; Must maintain high academic results and effort to retain placement
special_conditions: Entry via application and audition. All students must complete an audition and application for successful entry. Students must be prepared to fully commit to both class requirements and their chosen performance pathways. Students must maintain high academic results in all relevant subjects as well as high effort and behaviour to retain their place in the Program of Excellence. Students can apply for multiple programs; however, they will only be selected for one Program of Excellence.

overview: Murrumba State Secondary College offers the Dance Academy to deliver a high standard of dance education focused on achieving both physical and academic success. The program strives to develop students' overall technique with a focus on their performance skills (expressive and technical).
description: The Dance Academy is an Excellence program available to Years 7-10 that delivers high-quality dance education combining technical training with academic learning. The program takes a holistic approach to dance education, covering units including anatomy, food and nutrition, physical conditioning for performances, grooming and personal presentation. Expert teaching staff are committed to providing authentic learning experiences through exposure to high-calibre industry professionals and connections with higher education institutions. Students participate in ongoing dance workshops, live dance performances and industry partnerships. Entry is via application and audition. The audition assesses ability to comprehend choreography (Contemporary, Jazz, Hip Hop, Lyrical) and participation in physical warm-up demonstrations (strength, stamina and flexibility). Course fees apply for the Murrumba Dance Academy; costs are variable and refer to Fee Matrix on College website.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7, 8, 9, 10; Must complete application and audition; Audition tests comprehension of choreography and physical warm-up ability (strength, stamina and flexibility); Must maintain high academic results and effort to retain placement
special_conditions: Entry via application and audition. Audition assesses ability to comprehend choreography (Contemporary, Jazz, Hip Hop, Lyrical) and participation in physical warm-up (strength, stamina and flexibility). Course fees apply for the Murrumba Dance Academy. Students must maintain high academic results in all relevant subjects as well as high effort and behaviour to retain their place in the Program of Excellence. Students can apply for multiple programs; however, they will only be selected for one Program of Excellence.

overview: Murrumba State Secondary College offers the Spanish Acceleration Program to establish the College's commitment to innovatively enrich student learning and support their success. The program provides students with an exciting opportunity to reach high levels of academic achievement and participation in Spanish language studies.
description: The Spanish Acceleration Program is an Excellence program available to Year 7 students that provides enrichment in the Spanish language and culture. As part of their compulsory LOTE (Spanish) class, Acceleration students have the opportunity to explore new vocabulary and grammar structures, developing high levels of language proficiency. The program offers a greater depth of exposure to the Spanish language and provides challenging opportunities within a nurturing learning environment. Students develop proficiency in the four macro skills: speaking, listening, reading and writing. Future pathways include undertaking General senior Spanish in Years 11-12, attending head start university courses or studying abroad. Entry is via interview. Fees apply covering cultural incursions and events celebrated as a cohort throughout the college year; refer to Fee Matrix on College website.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must complete interview; Must maintain high academic results and effort to retain placement
special_conditions: Entry via application and interview. The Spanish Academy fee covers the cultural incursions and events that are celebrated as a cohort throughout the College year. Students must maintain high academic results in all relevant subjects as well as high effort and behaviour to retain their place in the Program of Excellence. Students can apply for multiple programs; however, they will only be selected for one Program of Excellence.

overview: Murrumba State Secondary College offers the Spanish Immersion Program to provide students with an intensive Spanish language learning experience where the language becomes the medium of instruction across multiple subjects. Students learn not just Spanish language but also academic content through the Spanish language.
description: The Spanish Immersion Program is an Excellence program available to Years 8, 9, 10 students that provides intensive Spanish language learning and cultural immersion. In addition to their compulsory LOTE (Spanish) class, Immersion students learn Mathematics, Access/Health, Humanities and HPE in Spanish (at least 80% of courses). This program allows students to develop advanced language proficiency while progressing through their academic studies. The immersion approach enhances cognitive development and intercultural understanding. Students develop speaking, listening, reading and writing skills at a high level through subject-based learning. Future pathways include undertaking General senior Spanish in Years 11-12, attending head start university courses or studying abroad. Entry is via interview. Refer to Fee Matrix on College website for current costs.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 8, 9, 10; Must complete interview; Must maintain high academic results and effort to retain placement
special_conditions: Entry via application and interview. In addition to their compulsory LOTE (Spanish) class, Immersion students will also learn Mathematics, Access/Health, Humanities and HPE in Spanish (at least 80% of courses). Students must maintain high academic results in all relevant subjects as well as high effort and behaviour to retain their place in the Program of Excellence. Students can apply for multiple programs; however, they will only be selected for one Program of Excellence.

program_name: Nambour Academy of Creative Arts (NACA) Scholarship
overview: Nambour State College offers NACA Scholarships to students who demonstrate creative potential, commitment to an Arts pathway, and evidence of achievement in Music, Dance, Drama, or Visual Art. The scholarship recognises outstanding talent in the performing and visual arts and supports excellence in these disciplines.
description: The Nambour Academy of Creative Arts (NACA) offers motivated and creative students in Years 7 to 10 the opportunity to extend their talents in Music, Dance, Drama, or Visual Art through a high-quality excellence program. Scholarships are awarded to students who demonstrate creative potential, commitment to an Arts pathway, and evidence of achievement. The scholarship covers the Student Resource Scheme (SRS) for the year and does not include extra-curricular fees. Students wishing to be considered must complete the scholarship section as part of their NACA audition application. Successful applicants will participate in additional excellence classes throughout the year while still participating in all rotation electives. Applications and auditions are completed electronically.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 to Year 10 students; Must demonstrate creative potential and commitment to an Arts pathway; Must provide evidence of achievement; Must complete NACA audition application; Auditions must be completed electronically
special_conditions: The scholarship covers the Student Resource Scheme (SRS) for the year and does not include extra-curricular fees. Recipients must participate in additional excellence classes throughout the year and maintain participation in the NACA program. Recipients continue to participate in all rotation electives on both elective lines.

overview: Nambour State College offers the Pinnacle Program for highly motivated students in Years 7 to 9. The program provides extended learning opportunities connecting like-minded students who are supported to develop character, citizenship, collaboration, communication, creativity and critical thinking skills.
description: The Pinnacle Program provides the opportunity to connect and learn with like-minded students. Students will be supported to improve their skills across the six key capabilities of character, citizenship, collaboration, communication, creativity and critical thinking. Students in the Pinnacle Program will be grouped according to their interests and have the opportunity for teachers to tailor the learning experiences to suit this. Students will be together for the core curriculum areas of English, maths, science and humanities, and they will also have opportunities to extend their learning in their interest area. The Pinnacle Program continues into Years 8 and 9. Students must apply to enter the program, and results are reviewed each term. Multiple pathways are available including Pinnacle Scholars, Pinnacle Creatives, Pinnacle Innovators, and Pinnacle Athletes.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 to Year 9 students; Must demonstrate history of good behaviour and effort; Must show interest and willingness to engage with the program; Preference given to students who have participated in Young Pinnacle Program; Not restricted to highest academic performers
special_conditions: Applications must be lodged in person to front office administration by 31st July. Application must be submitted with Enrolment Forms to be eligible. Results are reviewed each term. Preference given to Young Pinnacle Program participants. Specialist lessons occur once a week.

overview: Nambour State College offers the Young Pinnacle Program to highly motivated students in Years 5 and 6 across the Sunshine Coast. The program provides enrichment and early high-school transition opportunities designed to prepare students for progression into the full Pinnacle Program.
description: The Young Pinnacle Program is a pathway program available to students in Years 5 and 6 across the Sunshine Coast. The program is designed to provide enrichment and early high-school transition opportunities for highly motivated students. Successful applicants will participate in four 'Young Pinnacle Scholar Days' at Nambour State College throughout Year 5 and again in Year 6. Students participate in activities grouped by interests, including Creatives, Cultivators, Innovators and Scholars. The program identifies children's passions and interests and tailors learning around them. Students in the Young Pinnacle Program must complete an Enrolment EOI (expression of interest) to remain in the program during Year 6, which is a prerequisite to transition into the full Pinnacle Program in Year 7.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 5 and Year 6 students; Across the Sunshine Coast; Designed for highly motivated students; Must demonstrate interest and engagement; Students must complete Enrolment EOI during Year 6 to continue into Year 7 Pinnacle Program
special_conditions: Successful applicants participate in four 'Young Pinnacle Scholar Days' at Nambour State College throughout Year 5 and again in Year 6. Students must complete Enrolment EOI during Year 6 to remain eligible for transition into the full Pinnacle Program in Years 7-9. Serves as a pathway program to the full Pinnacle Program.

program_name: Nambour State College Rising Stars Dance Performance Team
overview: Nambour State College offers the Rising Stars Dance Performance Team on the Junior Campus for passionate young dancers in Years 2 to 6. The program focuses on growth, skill development and teamwork while providing a launchpad for aspiring young performers.
description: The Nambour State College Rising Stars Dance Performance Team is more than just movement – it's a launchpad for passionate young performers from Year 2 to Year 6. With a focus on growth, skill development and teamwork, students in this program learn to express themselves, refine their technique and embrace the joy of dance. Rising Stars gives students the opportunity to perform and compete in local events and eisteddfods, building confidence, resilience and pride in their achievements. The program also nurtures a deep appreciation for the Arts and serves as a pathway into our senior campus NACA program – a specialist extension in the Arts and the creative industries. The program provides structured dance training with experienced educators focusing on performance readiness.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 2 to Year 6 students; Must be enrolled at Nambour State College; Passion for dance and movement required; Interest in performing and competing in local events
special_conditions: Program serves as a pathway to senior campus NACA program. Students participate in local events and eisteddfods. Focus on building confidence, resilience and achievement through performance opportunities.

overview: Nambour State College offers the Thunder Volleyball Program, a renowned excellence program for competitive volleyball players. The program consistently nurtures athletes who achieve remarkable success both on and off the court.
description: The Nambour Thunder Volleyball program is renowned for its excellence and competitive spirit, consistently nurturing athletes who achieve remarkable success both on and off the court. The program caters for all abilities, with students able to strive to be part of one of the teams or study volleyball as part of the junior Physical Education program. The college is a proud 5-time National Champions program, has secured numerous gold, silver, and bronze medals at state-level competitions, and has had four exceptional players reach the international level representing Australia. The program provides expert coaching with specialized training techniques designed to enhance performance, teamwork, and strategic understanding of the game.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students interested in competitive volleyball; Must demonstrate commitment to training and competition; All ability levels catered for; Can participate in team or general PE program
special_conditions: Two outstanding students have earned prestigious scholarships to universities in the United States. Expert coaching with experienced coaches dedicated to developing players' skills in a competitive yet supportive environment. Multiple teams available for different ability levels.

overview: Nambour State College offers an Instrumental Music Program providing instruction and performance opportunities for students. The program supports students developing musical skills across various instruments and ensembles.
description: Nambour State College provides an Instrumental Music Program as a specialist offering within its curriculum. Students have the opportunity to participate in individual instrumental music lessons and join various award-winning ensembles and groups. The program includes instruction on various instruments and supports student participation in college concerts, performances and musical activities. Students can develop their instrumental skills through the College Concert Band, String Ensemble and other vocal groups. The program provides high-quality support for students pursuing instrumental music study alongside their core academic curriculum.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students interested in instrumental music; Must be enrolled at Nambour State College; Can participate in lessons and/or ensemble groups; No specific academic threshold required
special_conditions: Instrumental music lessons are available as a specialist offering. Students can participate in award-winning ensembles including College Concert Band, String Ensemble and vocal groups. Performance opportunities available through school concerts and events.

program_name: NACA Scholarships (Nambour Academy of Creative Arts)
overview: Nambour State College offers scholarships to students who demonstrate creative potential, commitment to an Arts pathway, and evidence of achievement in the Nambour Academy of Creative Arts (NACA) program. The NACA scholarships support students pursuing excellence in Music, Dance, Drama, and Visual Art through a high-quality excellence program.
description: A limited number of NACA Scholarships are available to students enrolled in the Nambour Academy of Creative Arts (NACA) program. Scholarships are awarded to students who demonstrate creative potential, commitment to an Arts pathway, and evidence of achievement. The scholarship covers the Student Resource Scheme (SRS) for the year and does not include extra-curricular fees. Students wishing to be considered must complete the scholarship section as part of their NACA audition application. This is a merit-based award that recognizes artistic talent and dedication to creative pursuits in the performing and visual arts.
eligibility_criteria: Year 7 to Year 10 students; Must audition for chosen discipline (Music, Dance, Drama, or Visual Art); Must demonstrate creative potential; Must show commitment to an Arts pathway; Must have evidence of achievement; Enrollment in NACA program required
special_conditions: Scholarship covers Student Resource Scheme (SRS) for the year only. Extra-curricular fees are not included. Recipients must maintain enrollment in NACA program. Scholarship application is completed as part of NACA audition application process.

overview: Nambour State College offers eight scholarships for students entering Year 7, sponsored by the NSC P&C Association. Successful applicants receive financial assistance toward tuition costs, uniforms and resources. The scholarships are awarded across four key areas of excellence.
description: Nambour State College offers eight scholarships for students entering Year 7 in total. This Scholarship opportunity is sponsored by the NSC P&C Association. Successful applicants will receive $500.00 which will be put towards the year's tuition costs, uniforms and resources. The college offers two scholarships in each of four key areas. Applications close on 31st July and must be lodged in person to the front office administration. This application must be submitted with Enrolment Forms to be eligible for a scholarship. Applicants must submit school reports for Semester 2 Year 5 and Term 1 and 2 Year 6, along with a written statement in 150 words explaining why they deserve the scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Year 7; Currently enrolled in Year 6; Must submit with Enrolment Forms; Must provide Semester 2 Year 5 and Term 1-2 Year 6 school reports; Australian citizen or permanent resident preferred
special_conditions: Applications must be lodged in person to front office administration. Applications close 31st July. Application must be submitted with Enrolment Forms to be eligible. Two scholarships awarded in each of four key areas. Funds can be used for tuition costs, uniforms and resources.

overview: Nambour State College offers the Pinnacle Program, a specialist extension program for highly motivated students in Years 7-9. The program connects like-minded students and supports development across six key capabilities: character, citizenship, collaboration, communication, creativity and critical thinking.
description: The Pinnacle Program is a specialist extension program available to Year 7 students and continues into Years 8 and 9. Students in the Pinnacle Program will be grouped according to their interests and have the opportunity for teachers to tailor learning experiences to suit their chosen strand. Students will be together for core curriculum areas of English, Maths, Science and Humanities, and will have opportunities to extend their learning in their interest area. The program includes specialized strands such as Pinnacle Scholars (academic excellence), Pinnacle Innovators (technology and science), Pinnacle Athletes (sporting focus), Pinnacle Creatives (artistic focus), Pinnacle Change Makers (community improvement and leadership), and Pinnacle Cultivators (agriculture and hands-on team work). Students must apply to enter the Pinnacle Program and their results are reviewed each term. Preference is given to students who have participated in the Young Pinnacle program.
eligibility_criteria: Year 7 to Year 9 students; Must complete application form; History of good behaviour and effort required; Does not require highest academic performance; Must show interest and willingness to give the program their best effort; Preference given to Young Pinnacle program participants
special_conditions: Application required for entry. Results reviewed each term. Preference given to Young Pinnacle program participants. Program emphasizes passion and interest areas rather than top academic performance. Students are grouped by interest areas and receive tailored learning experiences. Specialist lesson once per week in chosen strand.

overview: Nambour State College offers the Young Pinnacle Program, a pathway program available to students in Years 5 and 6 across the Sunshine Coast. The program is designed to provide enrichment and early high-school transition opportunities for highly motivated students.
description: The Young Pinnacle Program is a pathway program to the full Pinnacle Program at Nambour State College, available to students in Years 5 and 6 from across the Sunshine Coast. The program is designed to provide enrichment and early high-school transition opportunities for highly motivated students. Successful applicants will participate in four 'Young Pinnacle Scholar Days' at Nambour State College throughout Year 5 and again in Year 6. Students in the Young Pinnacle Program must complete an Enrolment EOI (expression of interest) to remain in the program during Year 6. The program explores four main strands: Athletes, Creatives, Scholars, and Cultivators, with later expansion including Innovators and Change Makers. Immersion days provide rich learning experiences including dance and drama activities, farm visits, robotic technology exploration, and gaming mathematics.
eligibility_criteria: Year 5 to Year 6 students; From any school across the Sunshine Coast; Must be highly motivated; Must be interested in early high-school transition; Expression of interest (EOI) required to continue in Year 6
special_conditions: Open to students from any Sunshine Coast primary school. Participants attend four Young Pinnacle Scholar Days per year. Must complete EOI to continue in Year 6. Program serves as pathway into full Pinnacle Program for Year 7. Immersion days expose students to different strand options (Athletes, Creatives, Scholars, Innovators, Cultivators, Change Makers).

overview: Nambour State College offers the Thunder Volleyball Program, providing opportunities for students to develop volleyball skills at various levels. The program has a strong track record of sporting excellence and has helped students secure university athletic scholarships.
description: Nambour State College's Thunder Volleyball Program caters for all abilities, with students able to strive to be part of elite teams or study volleyball as part of the junior Physical Education program. The program has an outstanding track record with two students earning prestigious scholarships to universities in the United States, opening doors to further their education and athletic careers. The program provides structured training, competition opportunities, and pathways to high-level athletic achievement. While specific scholarship details are not formalized for the high school program, the program is recognized for producing elite athletes.
eligibility_criteria: All ability levels welcome; Junior and senior students can participate; Students can choose to join elite teams or study volleyball in PE program; Interest and commitment to volleyball required
special_conditions: Program caters for all abilities from beginners to elite athletes. Students can participate in competitive teams or study volleyball within PE curriculum. The program has produced elite athletes who have secured university athletic scholarships in the United States.

program_name: Science and Maths Academy (SMA) Program of Excellence
overview: The Science and Maths Academy (SMA) at Narangba Valley State High School is a beacon of excellence in STEM education since its inception in 2012. Designed for students in years 7, 8, and 9, the SMA offers a targeted approach to nurture advanced learners in science, mathematics, and technology.
description: The SMA Primary School Program immerses students in Science and Maths topics aligned with the Australian Curriculum, extending beyond regular classroom content to foster high levels of applied and design thinking. In the SMA Secondary School Program, students delve deeper into Science, Maths, and STEM courses, with three lessons per week dedicated to each subject. The program is non-fee paying, as it is part of the Queensland government school system. Students are selected based on entrance testing. Year 7 applicants complete an enrolment form indicating Programs of Excellence - Science Maths Academy. Year 8 and 9 students from other schools or existing NVSHS students apply through their enrolment application or directly to Secondary-SMA@narangbavalleyshs.eq.edu.au with entrance test payment details. Assessment occurs each term, with comprehensive report cards provided at the end of each semester.
