eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 8, and Year 9; Must demonstrate advanced academic ability in science and mathematics; Must complete entrance assessment/test; Subject to available places (Year 8 2026 currently full; Year 9 2026 has 3 vacancies)
special_conditions: Entrance test required for entry. Assessment occurs each term with comprehensive report cards provided at semester end. Participants may undertake QCE points and AMEB examinations.

overview: The Music Program of Excellence is a comprehensive curriculum program spanning years 7 to 10, meticulously crafted to nurture excellence in music performance, composition, and appreciation. Since its inception in 2003, the Music Academy has remained an integral component of NVSHS's music curriculum.
description: The Music Program of Excellence caters for students who have been learning an instrument either privately or at school and aims to continue the development of these skills as well as a passion for music. The program is non-fee paying as part of the Queensland government school system. Students must complete an enrolment form indicating Programs of Excellence and then submit a separate Program of Excellence application. In partnership with NVSHS's Instrumental Music program, the Music Academy ensures a consistent flow of regular lessons and ensemble performances. The program provides a platform for students with a fervent love for music performance to further hone their technical prowess through opportunities such as QCE points, AMEB examinations, and IMEP.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Music proficiency required - students must be learning an instrument either privately or at school; Must complete Program of Excellence application; Currently accepting auditions at entry
special_conditions: Applicants must audition for the program. Capacity is 100 students for the Performing Arts Music Academy Program of Excellence. Students are supported through regular instrumental lessons with specialist teachers and ensemble performances at competitions and concerts.

overview: The AFL Program of Excellence is designed to cultivate both athletic prowess and academic excellence, offering a unique blend of top-tier coaching, tailored skill development, and a supportive educational environment. As a member of the esteemed AFL Schools of Excellence network, the program offers students an unmatched opportunity for top-tier training sessions.
description: The AFL Program of Excellence operates the Vipers Squad, providing students with unmatched opportunities for top-tier training sessions and regular participation in the AFLQ Schools of Excellence competition. From grades 7 to 10, the AFL Academy classes prioritize holistic athlete development, seamlessly integrated with core curriculum studies and future educational pathways. The program is non-fee paying as part of the Queensland government school system. Selection is based on demonstrated athletic ability and potential in Australian Rules Football. Students must indicate on their enrolment form that they wish to apply for a Program of Excellence and then complete the Program of Excellence application.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate athletic ability and potential in Australian Rules Football; Must complete Program of Excellence application; Currently accepting 248 students in the Sports Academy—AFL/Netball Program of Excellence
special_conditions: Part of the AFL Schools of Excellence network. Participants compete in the AFLQ Schools of Excellence competition. Program emphasizes holistic athlete development with focus on academic achievement, leadership skills, and personal growth. Both male and female participants accepted.

overview: The Netball Program of Excellence is designed to cultivate both athletic prowess and academic excellence, offering top-tier coaching, tailored skill development, and a supportive educational environment. The Novas Squad offers students an unmatched opportunity for top-tier training sessions and regular participation in the Vicki Wilson competition.
description: The Narangba Valley Netball Developmental Academy is a program for students to excel in the field of Netball. The Netball Developmental Academy is an elective subject which incorporates theoretical, skill and game development. The program is closely linked to Health and Physical Education Essential Learnings with students completing four comprehensive terms of Netball through years 7, 8, 9 and 10. The program is non-fee paying as part of the Queensland government school system. From grades 7 to 10, the Netball Academy classes prioritize holistic athlete development, seamlessly integrated with core curriculum studies and future educational pathways.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Students must demonstrate athletic ability and potential in netball; Must complete Program of Excellence application; Part of the Sports Academy—AFL/Netball Program of Excellence with capacity for 248 students
special_conditions: Netball is offered as an elective subject within the program. Participants compete in the Vicki Wilson Cup. Program emphasizes holistic athlete development with academic enrichment, leadership opportunities, and personal growth initiatives.

overview: The Dance Academy at Narangba Valley State High School provides opportunities for students to develop and excel in dance. Students interested in dance have the opportunity to audition for specialized dance instruction within the Programs of Excellence structure.
description: The Dance Academy provides training and development opportunities for students with talent and interest in dance. The program is non-fee paying as part of the Queensland government school system. Students audition at the beginning of Year 7 for the Dance Academy. Students in the Dance Academy program are given opportunities to extend and refine their dance skills and performance abilities. The school also operates a Dance Troupe for Year 8 to 12 students which involves training after school, learning and rehearsing routines, with students having opportunities to perform at the Brisbane Eisteddfod and other competitions.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate dance ability or talent; Must audition for the program; Entry to Dance Troupe (Year 8-12) is audition-based
special_conditions: Entry is audition-based. Students complete higher-level work than ordinary classes, with assessment expected to be of a higher standard. Dance Troupe members are required to attend all rehearsals. Not all interested students may be successful in audition.

overview: The Drama Academy at Narangba Valley State High School provides opportunities for students to develop excellence in drama and theatrical performance. Students audition at the beginning of Year 7 for specialized drama instruction within the Programs of Excellence structure.
description: The Drama Academy provides training and development opportunities for students with talent and interest in drama and theatrical performance. The program is non-fee paying as part of the Queensland government school system. At the beginning of Year 7, students are asked to audition for either of the drama academy. Students complete work faster than ordinary classes, and their assessment is expected to be of a higher standard. The program supports students in developing performance skills through dramatic presentation, theatrical rehearsal, and participation in school productions and competitions.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate drama ability or talent; Must audition for the program at beginning of Year 7
special_conditions: Entry is audition-based. Students complete work faster than ordinary classes, with assessment expected to be of a higher standard. The school produces a musical biennially which contains a large number of students from the performing arts faculty.

overview: Nerang State High School offers the COMETS Academic Program of Excellence to students demonstrating exceptional academic ability. The program provides advanced learning opportunities in a supportive environment for students entering Years 7-9. Students selected for this program benefit from professional teachers offering advanced lessons and opportunities to excel in their chosen field of academic excellence.
description: The COMETS Academic Program of Excellence is a selective signature program designed for academically gifted students. The program accommodates 84 students across Years 7-9, with places available for both in-catchment and out-of-catchment students (subject to capacity). The program provides advanced lessons and opportunities for selected students to excel academically. Students in this program have a direct pathway to distinct senior programs and links to careers beyond school. Many students in the COMETS program are eligible to complete TAFE certificate courses and University programs whilst still studying at Nerang State High School. The selection process involves academic assessment, though specific testing details are not publicly detailed. Entry is available in Years 7, 8, and 9, with application through the school's enrolment procedures.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7, 8, or 9; Academic assessment required; Places subject to enrolment capacity; Out-of-catchment students may apply once in-catchment demand is met; Students must complete enrolment interview appointment
special_conditions: Program places are limited. Places in the Programs of Excellence will only be available to out-of-catchment enrolments once the demand for in-catchment enrolment has been met and sufficient student enrolment capacity has been reserved for future in-catchment growth. The School Enrolment Management Plan is reviewed annually.

overview: Nerang State High School offers the Quality Arts Program of Excellence to students demonstrating exceptional talent in performing arts including dance, drama, and music. The program provides advanced learning opportunities for students entering Years 7-9 in a supportive environment. Students are taught by professional teachers who provide advanced lessons and opportunities to excel in their chosen field of artistic excellence.
description: The Quality Arts Signature Program is a selective program designed for students with exceptional talent in performing arts. The program accommodates 175 students across Years 7-9. The program develops technical, expressive and creative skills for dance and theatrical performance. Dance students explore Hip Hop, Musical Theater, Stepping, Contemporary, Jazz and Fusion Dance Styles. Music is offered to students who have a passion for music and want to develop their knowledge and skills. Drama introduces a variety of styles including Improvisation, Script, Collage Drama, Melodrama and Physical Theatre. Students benefit from a direct pathway to distinct senior programs and are eligible to complete TAFE certificate courses and University programs whilst still at the school. Selection involves assessment of artistic talent, though specific audition or portfolio requirements are not detailed online. Application is through the school's standard enrolment procedures.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7, 8, or 9; Exceptional performing arts ability required; Places subject to enrolment capacity; Out-of-catchment students may apply once in-catchment demand is met; Students must complete enrolment interview appointment
special_conditions: Program places are limited. Places in the Programs of Excellence will only be available to out-of-catchment enrolments once the demand for in-catchment enrolment has been met and sufficient student enrolment capacity has been reserved for future in-catchment growth. The School Enrolment Management Plan is reviewed annually. Students in this program select their specialisation within the program offerings.

overview: Nerang State High School offers the Athlete Development Program of Excellence to students demonstrating exceptional sporting ability. The program provides advanced learning opportunities for student-athletes entering Years 7-9 in a supportive environment. Students are taught by professional teachers who provide advanced lessons and opportunities to excel in athletic pursuits whilst maintaining their academic studies.
description: The Athlete Development Program of Excellence is a selective signature program designed for students with exceptional sporting talent. The program accommodates 84 students across Years 7-9. The program provides advanced coaching and opportunities for selected student-athletes to excel in their chosen sport. Students benefit from a direct pathway to distinct senior programs and are eligible to complete TAFE certificate courses and University programs whilst still at the school. The program balances elite athletic development with a comprehensive curriculum. Selection involves assessment of sporting ability and athletic potential, though specific testing requirements are not detailed online. Application is through the school's standard enrolment procedures for signature programs.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7, 8, or 9; Exceptional sporting ability required; Places subject to enrolment capacity; Out-of-catchment students may apply once in-catchment demand is met; Students must complete enrolment interview appointment
special_conditions: Program places are limited. Places in the Programs of Excellence will only be available to out-of-catchment enrolments once the demand for in-catchment enrolment has been met and sufficient student enrolment capacity has been reserved for future in-catchment growth. The School Enrolment Management Plan is reviewed annually. School facilities include gym, indoor sports hall, cricket pitch, outdoor netball and basketball courts and two large sports fields.

overview: Noosa Christian College offers the Academic Scholarship to reward outstanding achievement in academic performance. This scholarship is designed to acknowledge and recognise academic achievers through an annual end-of-year award based on academic performance from the previous two reports.
description: The Academic Scholarship is a reward-based scholarship offered annually on an end-of-year basis to the top academic achiever at the College based on their performance from the previous two academic reports. The scholarship provides a 25% reduction of tuition fees for the following year. This scholarship is announced at the College Awards Night in December and is payable for 1 year only, though students may reapply in subsequent years if they wish to continue competing for the award. Selection is based purely on demonstrated academic excellence through school reports.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all secondary students at the College; Based on top academic achievement from previous two reports; Must be a current student at Noosa Christian College
special_conditions: Scholarships are for 1 year only. Students may reapply the following year. Awarded annually on an end-of-year basis. Announced at Awards Night in December.

overview: Noosa Christian College offers the Sports Scholarship to recognise and reward students with exceptional sporting talent. This scholarship is designed to support athletes who demonstrate outstanding achievement and commitment in sports competition at the College.
description: The Sports Scholarship is available to students with exceptional sporting talent and provides a 25% reduction on tuition fees for the following year. The scholarship is announced at the College Awards Night in December and is paid on an annual basis. Selection is based on demonstrated exceptional sporting talent and performance. This scholarship is for 1 year only, though students may reapply in subsequent years if they continue to demonstrate exceptional sporting ability. The College recognises achievement in various sports including touch football, basketball, netball, surf skills, and other recreational and competitive sports programs.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students with exceptional sporting talent; Must demonstrate outstanding performance in school or external sports; Must be a current student at Noosa Christian College
special_conditions: Scholarships are for 1 year only. Students may reapply the following year. Announced at Awards Night in December.

overview: Noosa Christian College offers The Arts Scholarship to recognise students with exceptional talent in music, art, or drama. This scholarship supports artistic achievers who demonstrate outstanding commitment and ability in the performing and visual arts.
description: The Arts Scholarship is available to students with exceptional talent in music, art, or drama and provides a 25% reduction on tuition fees for the following year. The scholarship is announced at the College Awards Night in December. Selection is based on demonstrated exceptional artistic talent and performance in music, art, or drama disciplines. This scholarship is for 1 year only, though students may reapply in subsequent years if they continue to demonstrate exceptional artistic ability. The College values creative expression through its choir programs, drama productions, visual arts curriculum, and private music tuition opportunities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students with exceptional talent in music, art, or drama; Must demonstrate outstanding artistic performance; Must be a current student at Noosa Christian College
special_conditions: Scholarships are for 1 year only. Students may reapply the following year. Announced at Awards Night in December.

overview: Noosa Christian College offers the Service/Cultural Scholarship to recognise students with excellence in the College value of Service and/or cultural activities. This scholarship rewards students who demonstrate outstanding commitment to community service and cultural participation.
description: The Service/Cultural Scholarship is available to students with excellence in the College value of Service and/or cultural activities. This scholarship provides a 50% reduction on tuition fees for the following year, offering the highest financial benefit of the main scholarship programs. The scholarship is announced at the College Awards Night in December. Selection is based on demonstrated excellence in service to the College community and/or participation in cultural activities. This scholarship is for 1 year only, though students may reapply in subsequent years. The College emphasises values of compassion, service, and integrity in its community.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students with excellence in service and/or cultural activities; Must demonstrate commitment to community service; Must display strong cultural engagement; Must be a current student at Noosa Christian College
special_conditions: Scholarships are for 1 year only. Students may reapply the following year. Announced at Awards Night in December.

program_name: The William & Hilde Chenhall Research Trust Scholarship
overview: Noosa Christian College offers The William & Hilde Chenhall Research Trust Scholarship to recognise students with exceptional talent and interest in the Sciences. This scholarship is designed to support advanced science students entering the final years of secondary education.
description: The William & Hilde Chenhall Research Trust Scholarship is available to students with exceptional talent and interest in the Sciences and provides full fee remission covering tuition fees, laptop levy, and general levy for the following year. This represents the most substantial scholarship offered by the College. The scholarship is open to students entering Year 11 or Year 12 and is announced at the College Awards Night in December. Selection is based on demonstrated exceptional talent and sustained interest in science disciplines. This scholarship is for 1 year only, though students may reapply in subsequent years if they continue to meet the eligibility criteria for their new year level.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 11 or Year 12; Must demonstrate exceptional talent and interest in the Sciences; Must be a current student or incoming student to Noosa Christian College; Based on demonstrated science excellence
special_conditions: Scholarships are for 1 year only. Students may reapply the following year. Open to students entering Year 11 or Year 12 only. Full remission includes tuition fees, laptop levy, and general levy. Announced at Awards Night in December.

overview: Newman Catholic College offers the Academic Excellence Scholarship to recognise and support highly accomplished students demonstrating outstanding academic achievement. The scholarship is open to current and prospective students across all year levels.
description: Newman Catholic College awards Academic Excellence Scholarships annually to students who demonstrate exceptional academic achievement and potential. The scholarship recognises sustained academic excellence and commitment to learning. Selection is based on academic merit and performance demonstrated by applicants. The application fee of $100 is non-refundable but will be credited to school fees if the student is successful in obtaining the scholarship. Specific financial benefit amounts and detailed eligibility criteria are available through direct application and contact with the college.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current and prospective students; Demonstrated exceptional academic achievement; Scholarship reviewed annually
special_conditions: Scholarship is reviewed annually. Application fee is non-refundable but will be credited to school fees if student is successful. Recipients are expected to maintain high expectations and support the college community.

overview: Newman Catholic College offers the Sports Excellence Scholarship to support students who demonstrate outstanding athletic ability and sporting achievement. The scholarship recognises excellence in sports and commitment to athletic development.
description: Newman Catholic College awards Sports Excellence Scholarships annually to students who demonstrate exceptional sporting talent and achievement. The college offers a Years 7 to 9 Sports Excellence Program that provides students with structured pathways to develop their athletic potential through both theoretical knowledge and practical application. Students are supported through Newman Sports Academies, Interschool Sporting Competitions, and opportunities to compete at State and National School Titles. The application fee of $100 is non-refundable but will be credited to school fees if successful. Recipients are supported in athletic development including strength and conditioning, injury prevention, and performance strategies.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current and prospective students; Demonstrated exceptional sporting ability; Athletic achievement in recognised sports; Commitment to participation in college sporting programs; Scholarship reviewed annually
special_conditions: Scholarship is reviewed annually. Application fee is non-refundable but will be credited to school fees if student is successful. Students in Years 7-9 have access to structured Sports Excellence Program including weekly training sessions and strength and conditioning. Recipients expected to maintain ethical sportsmanship and leadership.

overview: Newman Catholic College offers the Excellence in the Arts Scholarship to recognise and support students with exceptional talent in performing arts including music, drama, and dance. The scholarship supports highly accomplished artists across multiple artistic disciplines.
description: Newman Catholic College awards Excellence in the Arts Scholarships annually to students who demonstrate outstanding ability and achievement in performing arts. This includes music performance, drama, dance, and other artistic disciplines. The college supports students through various performing arts programs and opportunities. The application fee of $100 is non-refundable but will be credited to school fees if the student is successful in obtaining the scholarship. Selection is based on demonstrated artistic excellence, potential, and commitment to the arts.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current and prospective students; Demonstrated exceptional artistic talent in music, drama, dance or performing arts; Commitment to artistic development; Scholarship reviewed annually
special_conditions: Scholarship is reviewed annually. Application fee is non-refundable but will be credited to school fees if student is successful. Recipients are expected to contribute to the college's artistic programs and maintain commitment to their artistic discipline.

overview: Newman Catholic College offers the Excellence in STEM Scholarship to support students who demonstrate exceptional ability and passion in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics. The scholarship recognises excellence in STEM disciplines and supports future innovation.
description: Newman Catholic College awards Excellence in STEM Scholarships annually to students who demonstrate outstanding achievement and potential in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics. The college supports STEM learning through specialised programs and opportunities aligned with its partnership with James Cook University. The application fee of $100 is non-refundable but will be credited to school fees if the student is successful in obtaining the scholarship. Selection is based on demonstrated STEM ability, academic achievement in related subjects, and commitment to STEM fields.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current and prospective students; Demonstrated exceptional ability in science, technology, engineering or mathematics; Strong academic performance in STEM subjects; Commitment to STEM disciplines; Scholarship reviewed annually
special_conditions: Scholarship is reviewed annually. Application fee is non-refundable but will be credited to school fees if student is successful. Recipients benefit from the college's partnership with James Cook University and access to world-class university resources and opportunities.

overview: Newman Catholic College offers the Excellence in Community Leadership Scholarship to recognise and support students who demonstrate exceptional leadership qualities and commitment to community service. The scholarship supports students who are active in contributing to their college and broader community.
description: Newman Catholic College awards Excellence in Community Leadership Scholarships annually to students who demonstrate exceptional leadership potential and commitment to community engagement. The scholarship recognises students who show initiative, responsibility, and dedication to making a positive impact within the college and wider community. The application fee of $100 is non-refundable but will be credited to school fees if the student is successful in obtaining the scholarship. Selection is based on demonstrated leadership qualities, community involvement, and vision for positive change.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current and prospective students; Demonstrated exceptional leadership qualities; Significant community engagement and service; Commitment to contributing positively to college community; Scholarship reviewed annually
special_conditions: Scholarship is reviewed annually. Application fee is non-refundable but will be credited to school fees if student is successful. Recipients are expected to maintain active involvement in community and college initiatives and model positive leadership.

overview: Newman Catholic College offers the Excellence in Catholic Character Scholarship to recognise and support students who demonstrate strong commitment to Catholic values and living out the college's faith-based mission. The scholarship supports students who embody the Newman charism of faith and reason.
description: Newman Catholic College awards Excellence in Catholic Character Scholarships annually to students who demonstrate exceptional commitment to Catholic faith, values, and community engagement aligned with the college's mission. The scholarship recognises students who exemplify the college's charism of faith and reason, living out Gospel values in their daily lives. The application fee of $100 is non-refundable but will be credited to school fees if the student is successful in obtaining the scholarship. Selection is based on demonstrated faith commitment, adherence to Catholic values, and active participation in spiritual and faith formation activities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current and prospective students; Demonstrated commitment to Catholic faith and values; Active participation in faith formation and spiritual programs; Living in accordance with Gospel values and college Catholic identity; Scholarship reviewed annually
special_conditions: Scholarship is reviewed annually. Application fee is non-refundable but will be credited to school fees if student is successful. Recipients are expected to support and actively engage with the college's Catholic ethos and participate in faith-based college activities and liturgical celebrations.

school_name: Noosa District State High School - Pomona Campus

overview: North Rockhampton State High School offers Academic Bursaries to reward and encourage excellence in incoming students. The bursary recognises students who demonstrate outstanding academic achievement and commitment to learning.
description: The Academic Bursary is awarded to students entering Year 7 or Year 10 who demonstrate exceptional academic performance. This bursary supports students with a proven record of academic excellence. The bursary covers resource scheme, ID card, uniform and presentation folio. The award aims to encourage students to strive for greater academic success and to contribute positively to the school community. Selection is based on demonstrated academic achievement and merit.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or Year 10; Must be enrolling at North Rockhampton State High School; Demonstrated exceptional academic performance
special_conditions: The aim of bursaries is to reward, encourage and promote excellence within the school community. By supporting students with a proven record, North Rockhampton State High School encourages continued striving for greater success both for the student and for the school.

overview: North Rockhampton State High School offers Cultural Bursaries to recognise and support students who demonstrate excellence in cultural and performing arts. The bursary encourages talented students to continue developing their abilities in music, drama, dance and other cultural pursuits.
description: The Cultural Bursary is awarded to students entering Year 7 or Year 10 who demonstrate excellence in cultural activities including music, drama, dance and other performing arts. This bursary supports students with a proven track record of cultural achievement and engagement. The bursary covers resource scheme, ID card, uniform and presentation folio. The award recognises and encourages students to pursue cultural excellence and to contribute their talents to the school community. Selection is based on demonstrated cultural achievement and merit.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or Year 10; Must be enrolling at North Rockhampton State High School; Demonstrated excellence in cultural and performing arts; Proven track record in music, drama, dance or similar cultural activities
special_conditions: The aim of bursaries is to reward, encourage and promote excellence within the school community. By supporting students with a proven record, North Rockhampton State High School encourages continued striving for greater success both for the student and for the school.

overview: North Rockhampton State High School offers Sporting Bursaries to recognise and support students who demonstrate excellence in sports. The bursary encourages talented athletes to continue developing their abilities while maintaining academic and school community standards.
description: The Sporting Bursary is awarded to students entering Year 7 or Year 10 who demonstrate excellence in sporting activities at district, regional, state or national levels. This bursary supports students with a proven record of sporting achievement and commitment to athletic excellence. The bursary covers resource scheme, ID card, uniform and presentation folio. The award recognises and encourages athletes to pursue sporting excellence and to contribute their talents to the school community. Selection is based on demonstrated sporting achievement and merit.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or Year 10; Must be enrolling at North Rockhampton State High School; Demonstrated excellence in sporting activities; Proven track record competing at district, regional, state or national level
special_conditions: The aim of bursaries is to reward, encourage and promote excellence within the school community. By supporting students with a proven record, North Rockhampton State High School encourages continued striving for greater success both for the student and for the school.

overview: North Rockhampton State High School offers All-Rounder Bursaries to recognise and support students who demonstrate balanced excellence across multiple areas including academics, culture and sport. The bursary encourages well-rounded students who contribute significantly to the school community.
description: The All-Rounder Bursary is awarded to students entering Year 7 or Year 10 who demonstrate excellence across multiple disciplines combining academic achievement, cultural participation and sporting ability. This bursary supports students with a proven record of well-rounded achievement and positive contribution to the school community. The bursary covers resource scheme, ID card, uniform and presentation folio. The award recognises and encourages students to pursue excellence across diverse areas and to contribute their diverse talents to the school. Selection is based on demonstrated multi-discipline achievement and merit.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or Year 10; Must be enrolling at North Rockhampton State High School; Demonstrated excellence across multiple areas including academics, culture and sport; Proven track record of balanced achievement
special_conditions: The aim of bursaries is to reward, encourage and promote excellence within the school community. By supporting students with a proven record, North Rockhampton State High School encourages continued striving for greater success both for the student and for the school.

overview: Northpine Christian College offers partial strings scholarships to budding musicians throughout the school. The scholarship provides subsidized instrument hire rates for string players, supporting students' musical development and accessibility to quality instruments.
description: Northpine Christian College offers a number of partial strings scholarships annually to students across the school who are learning string instruments. The financial benefit includes a partially-subsidized hire rate for violins and violas, which makes instrument access more affordable for families. For cellists and double bassists, the scholarship covers a full two years of instrument hire. The scholarship supports students throughout the school and selection is based on musical ability and enrollment in the strings program. Interested students should contact the Music Department for application details, and applicants are encouraged to apply quickly as places are limited.
eligibility_criteria: Current enrollment at Northpine Christian College; Active participation in strings program (violin, viola, cello, or double bass)
special_conditions: Places are limited and availability is subject to demand. Application should be made by contacting Mr Callum Kennedy, Music Teacher and Co-curricular Music Coordinator, at callum.kennedy@northpine.qld.edu.au. Scholarship covers instrument hire costs only.

overview: Northside Christian College offers the Academic Scholarship to recognise outstanding academic achievement in both current and prospective students. The scholarship supports students who demonstrate exceptional ability in entrance examinations, consistent academic excellence, and alignment with the College's Christian mission and values.
description: Northside Christian College offers up to 4 individual Academic Scholarships representing up to 40% remission of tuition fees only. These three-year scholarships are available to both current students and external applicants who are Australian citizens or permanent residents and eligible for enrolment at the College. To be awarded an Academic Scholarship, a potential student must score highly in the EduTest examination, demonstrate academic excellence, and display qualities consistent with the College's mission. Following receipt of EduTest results, each scholarship applicant will be assessed by the Scholarship Committee. Select applicants will be invited to attend an interview with the Principal, and if successful, will be offered a scholarship. All scholarship decisions are treated as confidential.
eligibility_criteria: Australian citizens or permanent residents; Eligible for enrolment at Northside Christian College; Available to both current students and external applicants; Must score highly in EduTest examination; Must demonstrate academic excellence based on previous Report Cards; Must display qualities consistent with the College's Christian mission; Pastor's reference required (compulsory); Regular church attendance and involvement required
special_conditions: Scholarships are three-year commitments. All scholarships are confidential and should be treated as such. Recipients must meet Northside Christian College's entry requirements, including acceptance of and participation in the Christian education and practices of the College. Applications for entry in a given year only open the year prior to entry. Families must ensure they can meet College entry requirements before registering for the EduTest examination.

overview: The Instrumental Music Scholarship was established to financially assist Northside Christian College Middle School students in the ongoing pursuit of excellence in Instrumental Music. This scholarship is designed for students passionate about music who wish to advance their skills and continue their musical journey with community support.
description: The Instrumental Music Scholarship provides a value of $1,640, covering one full year of instrumental music tuition at Northside Christian College, including 40 weekly lessons. This is a specialised music scholarship available exclusively to current students enrolled at Northside Christian College in Years 10, 11, or 12 for 2026. The selection process requires applicants to prepare and perform one audition piece (approximately 2-3 minutes) on the instrument or voice of their choice, followed by an interview for the position. Applicants must submit a completed application form. All applicants will receive formal notification of audition results in February.
eligibility_criteria: Must be currently enrolled at Northside Christian College; Must be in Year 10, Year 11, or Year 12 for 2026; Must be receiving or willing to receive school-based instrumental tuition; Passionate about music and pursuing excellence; Must prepare and perform an audition piece; Must participate in interview
special_conditions: Scholarship covers instrumental music tuition only (40 weekly lessons). Available exclusively to current enrolled students in Years 10, 11, or 12. Students must be receiving school-based tuition. The scholarship recognises students passionate about music and pursuing excellence in their instrument or vocal performance.

school_name: Northern Peninsula Area College - Bamaga Junior Campus
Northern Peninsula Area College - Ama Mary Eseli Injinoo Campus
Northern Peninsula Area State College - Senior Campus

overview: OneSchool Global offers the OneSchool Award to recognise outstanding student achievement and community contribution. The award motivates students to achieve and acknowledges exceptional contribution to their group or wider campus community.
description: The OneSchool Award is presented to students who demonstrate an outstanding or exceptional contribution to their group or to aspects of the wider campus community. The award is designed to motivate students to achieve and recognize their commitment to community involvement and peer support. This non-monetary award acknowledges student excellence in leadership and service within the school community. The award process involves campus community assessment and determination by school leadership. Recipients are recognized for their positive impact on campus culture and their contribution to the OneSchool Global community values of integrity, care, respect, responsibility, and commitment.
eligibility_criteria: Students demonstrating outstanding or exceptional contribution to their group; Students making exceptional contribution to wider campus community; Good standing with school conduct and academic expectations
special_conditions: Award determination based on campus community contribution. Recognition is non-monetary but honours student achievement and community leadership.

overview: OneSchool Global offers a Fee Relief Program for families experiencing financial hardship or difficulty meeting school fees. This program provides support to eligible families to enable continued access to education.
description: OneSchool Global's Fee Relief Program is available for parents and families experiencing financial hardship or having difficulty meeting school fees. The program is designed to provide financial assistance to ensure students can continue their education at OneSchool Global campuses. Eligible families can access fee relief support administered through campus administration. The program operates as a needs-based financial assistance initiative and is available to all campuses. Parents experiencing financial difficulties should contact their campus directly to discuss fee relief options and available support. The specific amount of assistance varies based on individual family circumstances and is determined through a confidential assessment process.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; Families having difficulty meeting school fees; Current student enrolment at OneSchool Global
special_conditions: Application involves confidential financial assessment. Available to families experiencing genuine financial hardship. Administered on a case-by-case basis through individual campus offices.

program_name: Financial Assistance / Bursary Support for Special Circumstances
overview: Ohana College offers bursary support to families in special circumstances. The program is administered through the college's Finance Office and provides financial assistance to eligible families to help offset the levy costs.
description: Ohana College provides support to families experiencing financial hardship in special circumstances. While the college does not charge tuition fees as a special assistance school, it does charge a levy of $260 per term to ensure students have access to quality resources and facilities including iPad, stationery, excursions and meals. For families who would benefit significantly from a student attending Ohana College but are unable to afford the levy costs, bursary support may be available. Each student is reviewed on a case-by-case basis, and the college requests evidence documents to assist with the assessment process. Applications are made directly through the Finance Office.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship or special circumstances; case-by-case assessment required; evidence documents required to support application
special_conditions: Each student is reviewed case by case to see if the college can support their needs. Evidence documents are required to assist with this process. As an Independent school, Ohana College does not have access to the government school's database called 'OneSchool', therefore parents/carers or the student's previous school need to provide school evidence for the application.

school_name: Our Lady Help of Christians School
Our Lady Help of Christians School
overview: Our Lady Help of Christians School offers a means-tested concession card discount to eligible families. This program provides financial assistance to parents and carers holding Australian government health care or pensioner concession cards, recognising families facing financial constraints.
description: Parents and carers with a means-tested Australian government health care or pensioner concession card may be eligible to receive a 70% discount to tuition fees. This discount does not apply to school levies. The program is designed to make Catholic education more affordable for families with demonstrated financial need. To access this assistance, families must provide evidence of their concession card status. The discount is calculated on tuition fees only and families remain responsible for all other school levies and charges.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold a valid Australian government health care or pensioner concession card; Open to all students enrolled at the school; Discount applies to tuition fees only, not school levies
special_conditions: Discount applies to tuition fees only and does not apply to school levies. Families must provide evidence of valid concession card status.

school_name: Our Lady Help of Christians School
overview: Our Lady Help of Christians School offers discounts to families with multiple children attending Catholic schools in the Diocese of Cairns. This program recognises the financial commitment of families educating more than one child in the Catholic system.
description: Discounts are offered to families where more than one child is attending Catholic schools in the Diocese of Cairns. The exact discount amount is subject to individual family circumstances and diocesan policy. This is in addition to the school's commitment to making Catholic education affordable. Families with multiple children should contact the Finance Officer to discuss their specific discount eligibility and to arrange implementation of the discount on their account.
eligibility_criteria: More than one child attending Catholic schools in the Diocese of Cairns; Open to all families with eligible children
special_conditions: Discount applies only to families with more than one child enrolled at Catholic schools within the Diocese of Cairns. Families must contact the Finance Officer to confirm eligibility and arrange discount implementation.

school_name: Our Lady Help of Christians School
overview: Our Lady Help of Christians School provides confidential financial assistance to families experiencing genuine financial hardship. The school is committed to ensuring no child is denied a Catholic education due to financial difficulties, making confidential arrangements available to support enrolment.
description: No child will be denied a Catholic education because of genuine financial difficulties. Families experiencing financial hardship can make confidential arrangements with the school. The school recognises that genuine financial hardship may arise from circumstances such as illness, unemployment, or other reasonable causes affecting a family's ability to pay school fees and levies. Families must contact the Finance Officer office to arrange an interview to discuss their circumstances. Financial hardship assistance is flexible and tailored to individual family situations, with the school committed to finding solutions that enable continued enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Experiencing genuine financial hardship due to illness, unemployment, or other reasonable cause; Unable to reasonably pay full school fees and levies; Open to all enrolled and prospective students
special_conditions: Applications must be made confidentially to the Finance Officer. Arrangements are individually negotiated. It is imperative that families who are struggling financially are faithful in paying a contribution to their local school to whatever extent they can afford. Failure to follow school policy and procedures may result in engagement of a Debt Recovery Agency.

overview: Ormiston College offers Academic Excellence Scholarships to students who perform best in the Scholarship Examination. These scholarships recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and achievement in their chosen area of study.
description: Ormiston College awards Academic Excellence Scholarships to students who perform best in the Scholarship Examination. Successful scholarship winners receive part of their private school fees for the extent of the student's secondary education at Ormiston College. The scholarship provides tuition fee reduction for students from Year 7 onwards. Year 7 applicants sit an ACER examination held in the first quarter of the year before entry. Students in Years 8-12 may apply at any point of the academic year at the discretion of the Headmaster. Ongoing scholarship support is subject to an annual review to determine how the student has performed throughout the past year both academically and in adhering to and supporting the Ormiston College ethos and community.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering secondary school (Year 7 and above); Must perform best in the Scholarship Examination; For Year 7: sit ACER exam in first quarter of year prior to entry; For Years 8-12: new entry students only, considered at discretion of Headmaster; Must maintain academic performance and support College ethos; No disciplinary issues affecting scholarship continuation
special_conditions: Scholarship is offered on the basis of part-time fee remission (specific percentage not disclosed). Recipients are expected to remain at the College until graduation from Year 12, barring extraordinary circumstances. College reserves the right to terminate a Scholarship at any time should the student's academic or personal progress and performance be deemed unacceptable. Ongoing support subject to annual review evaluating academic performance and adherence to College ethos.

overview: Ormiston College offers General Excellence Scholarships to students who demonstrate excellence in areas such as sport, drama, music, debating and other notable achievements alongside strong performance in the Scholarship Examination. These scholarships recognise all-round excellence and co-curricular achievement.
description: General Excellence Scholarships are awarded to students who perform well in the Scholarship Examination and demonstrate excellence in areas such as sport, drama, music, debating, and other notable achievements. Successful recipients receive part of their private school fees for the extent of the student's secondary education at Ormiston College. Year 7 applicants must sit the ACER examination and submit an e-Portfolio demonstrating achievements in their area of excellence, which may include sporting achievements, visual arts, performing arts, music, or other contributions. The e-Portfolio must include a reference letter from a principal, teacher, sports coach, music teacher, or similar authority. Students in Years 8-12 may apply at any point of the academic year at the discretion of the Headmaster. Ongoing support is subject to annual review considering both academic performance and sustained excellence in their area of specialty.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 and Years 8-12; Must perform well in the Scholarship Examination; Must demonstrate excellence in one or more of: sport, drama, music, debating, visual arts, performing arts, or other notable achievements; Year 7: Must sit ACER exam and submit e-Portfolio between 30 January and 11 February; Years 8-12: new entry students considered at discretion of Headmaster; Must provide reference letter from coach, teacher, or authority in their field; Must maintain academic performance and excellence in chosen area
special_conditions: Scholarship offered as part-time fee remission (specific percentage not disclosed). Recipients must submit e-Portfolio online demonstrating achievements and contributions in chosen area of excellence. Testing day held on Thursday 12 February 2026 for Year 7 applicants. Shortlisted applicants and parents invited to testing day with full details provided by email. Recipients expected to remain at College until graduation from Year 12 barring extraordinary circumstances. College reserves right to terminate scholarship if student's academic or personal progress deemed unacceptable. Annual review considers both academic performance and ongoing excellence in area of specialty.

school_name: Our Lady of Lourdes Primary School
Our Lady of Fatima Primary School

overview: Our Lady of Dolours School participates in the Mary MacKillop Education Fund, a Brisbane Catholic Education system-wide bursary program. The fund provides financial assistance to students from families experiencing significant financial hardship who wish to access Catholic education.
description: The Mary MacKillop Education Fund (MacKillop Fund) is administered through Brisbane Catholic Education and available to families enrolling at Our Lady of Dolours School. The bursary covers tuition fees and camps for primary school students. The school provides all stationery, texts, resource materials and one full set of uniforms (excluding hat and shoes). Parents contribute a weekly financial contribution negotiated with the school. The bursary is awarded at the discretion of the selection committee based on greatest need and funding availability. The fund is means-tested and does not depend on academic ability. Students must demonstrate a genuine inability to afford Catholic education and families must be supportive of Catholic ethos and values. The fund commits to supporting primary students through to the end of Year 6 as long as the family remains in need of assistance.
eligibility_criteria: Students and families applying for new enrolment at Our Lady of Dolours School; Demonstrated financial hardship and means-tested inability to afford Catholic education; Family supportive of Catholic ethos, values and mission; Special consideration may be given to students who are socially, culturally, spiritually or emotionally disadvantaged; Currently enrolled students in Brisbane Catholic Education schools are NOT eligible to continue receiving bursaries at their current school
special_conditions: Bursary is awarded for the duration of primary school enrolment (through to end of Year 6). The committee meets in May each year to award bursaries for the following school year. Applications must be submitted by 15 April for enrolment in the following school year. Late applications may be considered dependent upon availability of funds. Supporting financial documentation is required with the application. A copy of health care card is requested. Discernment committee maintains strict confidentiality - names and details of applicants and bursary recipients are not publicly revealed. Parents must maintain agreement regarding weekly financial contribution throughout the bursary period.

school_name: Our Lady of Lourdes Catholic School
overview: Our Lady of Lourdes Catholic School offers significant fee discounts to families holding a means-tested Australian Government Health Care Card. This program aims to reduce the financial burden on eligible families seeking a Catholic education.
description: The Catholic Education Office provides a 70% discount on tuition fees for parents or carers with a means-tested Australian Government Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card. This fee relief program is designed to ensure no child will be denied enrolment due to financial difficulty. Families experiencing financial difficulty are encouraged to speak confidentially with the school principal to apply for special consideration. The discount applies to the tuition component of school fees and is reviewed annually in line with inflation.
eligibility_criteria: Hold a means-tested Australian Government Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card; Currently enrolled or applying for enrolment at Our Lady of Lourdes Catholic School
special_conditions: Families must hold a valid means-tested Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card. No child will be denied enrolment due to financial difficulty. Discounts are subject to appropriate documentation being provided.

school_name: Our Lady of Lourdes Catholic School
overview: Our Lady of Lourdes Catholic School offers sibling discounts when multiple children from the same family attend the school. This program supports families with more than one child seeking a Catholic education.
description: Families receive the benefit of sibling discounts when their children attend both Primary and Secondary education at affiliated schools (such as Gilroy Santa Maria College). The discount is only applicable if the same debtor or debtors are financially responsible for the fees for all children enrolled. Families must complete the MSD form to ensure the discount is properly applied to their account. This discount is reviewed annually as part of the fee structure.
eligibility_criteria: Multiple children enrolled at the school; Same financial responsible party/parties for all enrolled children; MSD form must be completed
special_conditions: The MSD form must be completed for the discount to be applied. Discounts are only applicable if the same debtor(s) are financially responsible for the fees for all children enrolled.

school_name: Our Lady of Lourdes Catholic School
overview: Our Lady of Lourdes Catholic School provides special consideration for families experiencing financial difficulty. The school is committed to ensuring that financial circumstances do not prevent families from accessing a Catholic education.
description: Families experiencing financial difficulty are encouraged to speak confidentially with the school principal to apply for special consideration. The school operates on the principle that no child will be denied enrolment due to financial difficulty. The assessment process involves a confidential conversation with the principal who can discuss individual circumstances and explore available options for fee assistance. Special consideration may include fee reductions or alternative payment arrangements tailored to family circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled or seeking enrolment; Experiencing financial hardship or difficulty; Willing to discuss circumstances confidentially with school principal
special_conditions: Applications must be discussed confidentially with the school principal. No child will be denied enrolment due to financial difficulty. Assessment is based on individual family circumstances and supporting documentation.

school_name: Our Lady of Good Counsel School
Our Lady of Mount Carmel School
overview: Our Lady of Mount Carmel Primary School offers fee concessions to eligible families experiencing genuine financial hardship. The concession is assessed using a just and equitable formula consistent with Brisbane Catholic Education guidelines and requires families to agree to direct debit payment.
description: Families who are genuinely unable to make the full school fee commitment are invited to make an appointment with the School Principal to discuss a fee concession. The concession amount is determined based on a just and equitable formula reflecting financial hardship and is consistent with Brisbane Catholic Education policy. The eligibility is assessed on a case-by-case basis during individual consultations with the Principal. The granting of a fee concession is conditional on the family agreeing to pay via direct debit. This program provides partial fee reduction for families in need and is an ongoing support for the duration of enrolment, subject to annual review. Parents are encouraged to contact the school office to initiate discussions about financial assistance before fees become overdue.
eligibility_criteria: Genuine inability to meet full fee commitment; Must agree to direct debit payment method; Assessment based on individual family circumstances; Open to all year levels Prep to Year 6
special_conditions: Granting of fee concession is conditional on the family agreeing to direct debit payment. Families must make an appointment with the School Principal to discuss concession eligibility. Families are expected to contact school office proactively if experiencing financial difficulty rather than allowing accounts to become overdue. The school operates fee concession policy in line with Brisbane Catholic Education Guidelines.

school_name: Our Lady of the Rosary School
Our Lady of the Rosary School
overview: Our Lady of the Rosary School is committed to ensuring that no child is excluded from the school for financial reasons related to inability to pay full fees. The school offers fee remission support to families experiencing financial difficulty who work with the Finance Secretary to access this assistance.
description: No child will be excluded from Our Lady of the Rosary School for financial reasons related to the inability of the family to pay full fees. This commitment is conditional upon the family adhering to the terms of the school fee remission process managed by the school. Families experiencing financial difficulty should make an appointment with the school Finance Secretary by contacting the school office to discuss available support options. The school works with families to provide tailored assistance based on individual circumstances. This is an ongoing support program available to current and prospective families.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all families enrolled at the school; Financial hardship or difficulty paying full school fees; Willingness to adhere to the terms of the fee remission process
special_conditions: Families must adhere to the terms of the school fee remission process. Applications are assessed on a case-by-case basis. Families in financial difficulty should contact the Finance Secretary to discuss options.

school_name: Our Lady of the Angels' School
Our Lady of the Assumption School
overview: Our Lady of the Assumption School provides a fee concession process for families experiencing financial hardship. The school continues to provide assistance to families where needed to ensure access to education regardless of financial circumstances.
description: Our Lady of the Assumption School has in place a fee concession process and continues to provide assistance to families in need. Parents who have concerns about fees should make an appointment to see the Principal to discuss available options. The school works with families to develop sustainable payment arrangements and ensures that financial capacity is not the sole criteria for enrolment. This program recognises that families have varying circumstances and provides confidential support to those experiencing financial difficulty. The financial capacity of parents/guardians will not be the sole criteria for initial or continuing enrolment at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all families enrolled at the school; Demonstrated financial hardship or inability to pay full fees; Requires appointment and discussion with Principal
special_conditions: Families must contact the Principal to discuss their circumstances. All financial information is kept confidential. The school reserves the right to work with families to develop sustainable repayment plans. If fees cannot be paid by the specified due date, the Principal must be informed as soon as possible.

school_name: Our Lady of the Assumption School
overview: Our Lady of the Assumption School offers automatic fee discounts for families with multiple children enrolled. Families with more than one child attending receive progressive discounts for the second, third, and fourth child.
description: Our Lady of the Assumption School provides a sibling discount program to support families with multiple children enrolled at the school. Families are eligible for a discount if they have more than one child attending the school, with progressive reductions for the second, third, and fourth child. All siblings must be charged to the same account holders to be eligible for the discount. This program recognises the financial commitment families make when educating multiple children and provides automatic fee relief through tiered discounts based on the number of children enrolled.
eligibility_criteria: Two or more children enrolled at the school; All siblings must be charged to the same account holders; Discounts applied to second, third, and fourth child
special_conditions: All siblings must be charged to the same account holders to qualify. Discounts are automatically applied to families meeting eligibility criteria. The discount structure varies depending on family circumstances.

school_name: Our Lady of the Assumption School
overview: The Mary MacKillop Education Fund Bursary is available to families applying for enrolment at Brisbane Catholic Education schools, including Our Lady of the Assumption. The bursary covers tuition, fees, camps, stationery, texts, and resource materials for students in need.
description: The Mary MacKillop Education Fund Bursary is a means-tested financial assistance program available to families enrolled in Brisbane Catholic Education schools. The bursary covers tuition, fees, and camps for the duration of a child's enrolment in their current school stage, plus stationery, texts, resource materials, a laptop if required, and one full set of uniforms (excluding hat and shoes). The school provides all stationery and resource materials. Bursaries are awarded based on demonstrated, means-tested inability to afford education at a Brisbane Catholic Education school, with special consideration given to students who are socially, culturally, spiritually or emotionally disadvantaged. Support from the fund is not dependent on academic ability.
eligibility_criteria: Applying for enrolment at a Brisbane Catholic Education school; Demonstrated financial hardship and inability to afford fees; Supporting financial documentation required; Must show family support for Catholic education ethos; Not eligible if currently enrolled and wishing to continue at current school
special_conditions: Bursary is awarded through the Catholic Foundation and is confidential. Committee meets in May each year to award bursaries for the following school year. The MacKillop Fund commits to funding a child through the completion of the stage of school they are enrolled in (e.g., primary through end of Year 6). Late applications may be considered if funds are available at the next May disbursement. Students currently enrolled in a Brisbane Catholic Education school are not eligible for a MacKillop Bursary to continue at their current school. A healthcare card copy will be requested at a later date.

school_name: Our Lady of the Sacred Heart School Waiben - Hammond Island Campus
Our Lady of the Sacred Heart Catholic Primary School
Our Lady of the Southern Cross College
overview: Our Lady of the Southern Cross College offers financial hardship concessions for families experiencing genuine financial difficulty. The college works on a case-by-case basis to support families who may struggle with full fee payment.
description: For families experiencing genuine financial hardship, the College offers concessions available to support those in need. These concessions are administered through the College Business Manager and are considered on an individual basis. Families are encouraged to contact the College office to make a confidential appointment with the Business Manager to discuss their financial circumstances. The college recognises that access to Catholic education should not be a barrier for families facing financial challenges and works to find arrangements that enable continued enrollment while supporting the college's operations.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing genuine financial hardship; Contact with College Business Manager required; Confidential case-by-case assessment
special_conditions: Concessions are assessed on a case-by-case basis. Families must contact the College Business Manager for a confidential discussion to arrange support. The college reserves the right to work with families to create sustainable payment arrangements.

school_name: Our Lady of the Way School
program_name: Financial Assistance for Families in Hardship
overview: Our Lady of the Way School is committed to providing financial support to families experiencing genuine difficulty paying school fees. The school offers assistance to ensure that enrolment is not compromised by financial constraints.
description: Our Lady of the Way School recognises that some families may experience short-term or long-term genuine inability to pay school fees. In response, the school is committed to providing tailored financial support to ensure that financial hardship does not prevent enrolment or continuation at the school. The nature and extent of assistance is determined on a case-by-case basis through discussion with the school administration. Families should contact the school directly to discuss their circumstances and available support options. The school works closely with families to develop solutions that enable access to Catholic education.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing short-term or long-term genuine inability to pay fees; Must be enrolled or seeking enrolment at the school
special_conditions: Support is provided on a case-by-case basis determined through discussion with school administration. Assistance is tailored to individual family circumstances.

school_name: Our Lady of the Sacred Heart Catholic Primary School
program_name: Financial Assistance for Families Experiencing Hardship
overview: Our Lady of the Sacred Heart Catholic Primary School offers fee concessions and financial assistance to families experiencing genuine inability to pay school fees. The school is committed to ensuring that financial difficulty does not compromise a student's enrolment.
description: For families experiencing short-term or long-term genuine inability to pay fees, the school is committed to providing support to ensure that enrolment is not compromised. Families experiencing financial hardship should contact the Principal to discuss any issues relating to school fees. All issues regarding school fees are treated confidentially and respectfully. The school also offers fee concessions for families holding a valid government Concession Card (Health Care or Pensioner Card). Parents should contact the Finance Officer for further information concerning the concession application process. The school recognises that meeting the challenges of education requires a partnership approach and ensures no Catholic student will be denied a place due to genuine inability to pay fees.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing genuine financial hardship; may hold a government Concession Card; families unable to meet full fee obligations; short-term or long-term financial difficulty
special_conditions: Fee concessions and remissions will be considered upon application. The Principal determines the outcome of applications. Issues are treated confidentially and respectfully. No Catholic student will be denied a place in the school due to the parent's/guardian's genuine inability to pay fees. Contact the Finance Officer or Principal for enquiries about the concession application process.

school_name: Our Lady of the Sacred Heart School Waiben
program_name: Fee Reduction Program for Remote Location
overview: Our Lady of the Sacred Heart offers a Fee Reduction Program recognising the economic impacts and financial pressures of living in this beautiful, remote location. The program provides substantial fee reductions for all existing and intending families enrolled at the school.
description: From 2022 onwards, all existing and intending families receive a standard reduction of $400 (fees and levies) plus $100 (textbooks and stationery) per year, per student. Families experiencing financial hardship beyond this standard reduction may receive a further fee reduction through confidential arrangements with the school. This program acknowledges the high cost of living and financial pressures specific to living in the Torres Strait region, supporting families across Thursday Island (Waibeni), Hammond Island (Kiriri), Horn Island (Nurapai), Prince of Wales Island (Muralag), and other inner islands.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all existing and intending families enrolling at the school; Families experiencing financial hardship eligible for further reductions; Confidential arrangements with Principal required for additional assistance
value_aud: $400 per year (fees and levies) + $100 per year (textbooks and stationery)
special_conditions: Those experiencing financial hardship may receive a further fee reduction. Parents are required to contact the school/Principal to arrange a confidential interview to discuss additional support.

school_name: Our Lady of the Sacred Heart School Waiben
program_name: Access for All Initiative - Voluntary Tuition Fees
overview: The Access for All Initiative represents a historic commitment to ensuring Catholic education in the Torres Strait is accessible to every family. From 2026, tuition fees at Our Lady of the Sacred Heart School are entirely voluntary, eliminating financial barriers to enrolment.
description: This bold initiative, launched in December 2025, makes tuition fees completely voluntary from 2026 onwards. The program stands as a public commitment to walk with families, lift financial burdens, and ensure every child in the Torres Strait can experience a Catholic education that knows them, values them, and celebrates who they are. The initiative was launched under the leadership of Principal David Graham and with support from Bishop Joe Caddy and Catholic Education Diocese of Cairns. The school honours the trust families have placed in it for almost 140 years by investing in the future of children through eliminating tuition fee barriers.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students enrolling at Our Lady of the Sacred Heart School Waiben; No income threshold or financial assessment required; Applicable from 2026 onwards for all year levels
special_conditions: Tuition fees entirely voluntary from 2026. This eliminates tuition fee costs but other school levies and charges may still apply. The initiative applies to all families regardless of financial circumstances.

school_name: Our Lady of the Sacred Heart School Waiben
overview: Our Lady of the Sacred Heart recognises that families may experience financial hardship during their child's enrolment. The school offers confidential support and assistance arrangements for families facing financial difficulties.
description: Every attempt is made to make an education at Our Lady of the Sacred Heart School affordable. Families experiencing financial hardship are able to make confidential arrangements with the school to discuss and arrange additional fee support. Beyond the standard Fee Reduction Program, this assistance provides flexible, individualised solutions tailored to specific family circumstances. Parents are required to contact the school and arrange a confidential interview with the Principal to discuss their situation and explore available options for financial support.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; Confidential application through Principal interview; No specific income threshold published; Available to all year levels
special_conditions: Arrangements are confidential. Parents must contact Principal directly to arrange interview. Assessment based on individual family circumstances.

overview: Our Lady's College offers Academic Scholarships for high potential Year 7 students. The scholarship recognises exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning in prospective Year 7 students entering the College.
description: Our Lady's College will provide two Academic Scholarships for high potential Year 7 students. The scholarship covers tuition fees only from Years 7 to 9. As part of the application process, each student must sit the ACER Scholarship Test, which will be held on Tuesday 12 August at 9.00am at Our Lady's College. Registration for the test incurs a $125 fee. All scholarship recipients are expected to contribute to the life and culture of the College including involvement in co-curricular activities and demonstrating qualities compatible with the values and ethos of the College. Scholarship recipients must demonstrate ongoing academic success for the three years covered by the scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must complete the OLC Scholarship Application Form; Must sit the ACER Scholarship Test; Prospective applicants only; Must demonstrate commitment to college life and ethos
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered on a three-year basis covering Years 7-9. Scholarships are applied to tuition fees only. Assistance is provided on the understanding of the obligation of strict confidentiality on both the family and the College Scholarship Committee. Scholarship recipients need to demonstrate ongoing academic success for the three years covered by the scholarship. All scholarship recipients are expected to contribute to the life and culture of the College including involvement in co-curricular activities.

overview: Pacific Lutheran College offers the Academic Scholarship to recognise outstanding academic achievement in students entering Years 6-11. These merit-based scholarships reward students who demonstrate exceptional ability in the Edutest testing program, NAPLAN results, school reports and competition achievements.
description: Pacific offers Academic Scholarships for students entering Years 6-11, based on the results of the Edutest Scholarship testing program, NAPLAN results, school reports, competition results and an interview with the Principal. Both scholarships and bursaries provide for a percentage remission of tuition fees, which is determined by the performance of the candidate. Applicants must complete the Edutest Registration Form and pay the $110 fee by Friday 6th March 2026. The Examination Date is Saturday 14th March 2026 and will be held at the College. Both current and new students are eligible to apply.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 6-11; Current and new students eligible; Must participate in Edutest testing program; NAPLAN results required; Recent school reports required
year_levels: Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Scholarship value determined by performance of the candidate. Interview with Principal required.

overview: Pacific Lutheran College offers the General Excellence Scholarship to recognise academic all-rounders who demonstrate leadership and community service. These merit-based scholarships award students who have achieved excellence across a range of endeavours and display commitment to the College's Christian ethos.
