description: General Excellence Scholarships are offered for students entering Years 6-11 and are awarded to students who have demonstrated excellence across a range of endeavours, display high levels of community commitment and service and are willing to support the Christian ethos of the College. Both scholarships and bursaries provide for a percentage remission of tuition fees, which is determined by the performance of the candidate. Applicants must provide a written letter detailing their contribution to personal excellence, two recent school reports, NAPLAN results, summary of excellence areas, and two relevant references. Applications close on Friday 6th March 2026.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 6-11; Current and new students eligible; Must demonstrate excellence across multiple endeavours; Must display community commitment and service; Must support Christian ethos of the College
year_levels: Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Scholarship value determined by performance of the candidate. Up to five additional pages of references and certificates allowed.

overview: Pacific Lutheran College offers the Music Scholarship to recognise talented musicians who are passionate about developing their musical abilities. These merit-based scholarships reward students who demonstrate musical excellence through a musicianship test and audition.
description: Pacific offers Music Scholarships for students entering Years 6-11, based on the results of a musicianship test and an audition. Both scholarships and bursaries provide for a percentage remission of tuition fees, which is determined by the performance of the candidate. Applications for Music Scholarships close on Friday 6th March 2026 and the tests and auditions will be conducted on Saturday 21st March 2026. To apply, complete the Music Scholarship Form and return to the College Registrar. Both current and new students are eligible to apply.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 6-11; Current and new students eligible; Must complete musicianship test; Must participate in audition
year_levels: Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Musicianship test and audition conducted on Saturday 21st March 2026. Scholarship value determined by performance of the candidate.

overview: Pacific Lutheran College offers the Sport Scholarship to recognise talented athletes who have demonstrated sporting excellence. These merit-based scholarships reward students who have achieved national, state or regional representation and are committed to contributing to the College's sporting programs.
description: Sport Scholarships are awarded to students entering Years 6-11 who have demonstrated sporting excellence and are willing to make a positive contribution to the College's sporting programs and culture. Both scholarships and bursaries provide for a percentage remission of tuition fees, which is determined by the performance of the candidate. Applicants must provide evidence of representation at national, state or regional level in one of the College's sports, a written letter explaining their contribution, at least two references from coaches, and copies of recent academic reports and achievement awards. Applications close on Friday 6th March 2026.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 6-11; Current and new students eligible; Representation at national, state or regional level in Athletics, Swimming, Cross Country, Basketball, Netball, Rugby Union, Soccer, Touch Football, Volleyball or Water Polo; Evidence of talent, potential, leadership, commitment and teamwork; High quality contribution to sporting program and general college culture; Sound academic achievement
year_levels: Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: To maintain continuity of scholarships, students will be required to represent the College in nominated sports. Scholarship value determined by performance of the candidate.

overview: Pacific Lutheran College offers Bursaries to support families experiencing financial hardship. These need-based bursaries provide tuition fee remission to eligible families based on demonstrated financial need.
description: Each year, a small number of bursaries are allocated on the basis of financial need. Both scholarships and bursaries provide for a percentage remission of tuition fees, which is determined by the performance of the candidate and financial circumstances of the family. Bursaries are available to support families who would benefit from financial assistance. The percentage of tuition fee remission varies depending on the level of demonstrated financial need and the College's assessment of family circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; Assessment of financial need required
special_conditions: Small number of bursaries allocated each year. Bursary value determined by financial assessment of family circumstances.

overview: Padua College offers Academic Scholarships through participation in an annual testing program for independent schools throughout Australia. These scholarships recognise outstanding academic achievement and are open to students applying to commence in Years 10 or 11.
description: Padua College participates in the Cooperative Scholarship Testing Program (CSTP), an annual testing program for scholarships for independent schools throughout Australia. Academic Scholarships are available for students not currently enrolled at Padua College and applying to commence in Years 10 or 11 in the following year. The scholarship testing process is conducted by ACER and covers humanities, mathematics and writing. The specific financial value of the scholarship is not publicly stated on the website. Recipients must maintain a GPA of 13.00 or above (or similar criteria for Honours awards as published annually by the College) to retain the scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: Not currently enrolled at Padua College; Applying to commence in Year 10 or Year 11; Must sit ACER scholarship test; Must demonstrate strong academic performance in humanities, mathematics and writing
special_conditions: ACER registration fee is $140. The College's $100 application fee will be waived for all students who have registered for scholarship testing. Testing date is Friday 5 September, 2025. Students on a full scholarship must maintain a GPA of 13.00 or above to retain the scholarship.

overview: Padua College offers Indigenous Scholarships to support Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. The program recognises the College's commitment to reconciliation and providing access to quality education for First Nations students.
description: Padua College offers Indigenous Scholarships for students who identify as Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander. The College is committed to a journey of reconciliation with First Nations Peoples and acknowledges the Turrbal, Jagera, and Gubbi Gubbi Peoples upon whose lands the College stands. Specific details regarding the financial value, duration, selection process, and eligibility criteria are available directly from the College upon enquiry. Interested families should contact the enrolments office for comprehensive information about this program.
eligibility_criteria: Must identify as Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander; Open to boys from Year 5 through Year 12
special_conditions: Enquiries regarding the Indigenous Scholarship should be directed to enrolments@padua.qld.edu.au for specific program details.

overview: Padua College offers Music Scholarships to recognise and support students with demonstrated musical talent and achievement. These scholarships support participation in the Franciscan Colleges Instrumental Program (FCIP) and broader music activities.
description: Padua College offers Music Scholarships for students demonstrating exceptional musical ability and performance. The College has a strong musical tradition with the Franciscan Colleges Instrumental Program (FCIP), which provides tuition on the complete range of band and orchestral instruments. The program also includes vocal tuition and ensembles including the Greccio 'Rock Voices' (Years 5-6), Assisi Vocal Ensemble (Years 8-12), and Liturgy Group Ensemble. The specific financial value of Music Scholarships is not publicly detailed on the main scholarships page. Students are encouraged to participate in ensemble-based music and co-curricular musical activities. Further information should be obtained directly from the College regarding selection criteria and scholarship value.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated musical ability and performance; Open to students across Year 5 to Year 12
special_conditions: Selection based on musical audition and demonstrated performance ability. Scholarship holders are expected to participate actively in the FCIP ensembles and musical performances at the College.

overview: Padua College offers means-tested Bursaries for existing families experiencing financial hardship. These bursaries provide financial assistance to support continued enrolment at the College.
description: Padua College offers means-tested Bursaries for existing families experiencing financial hardship. The College maintains a dedicated Bursary Fund, which is described as providing 'a helping hand to families who, through unexpected events, experience hardship and need the support of the Padua community.' The Bursary Fund reflects the College's ongoing commitment to the values and mission of the Franciscan tradition, emphasising affirmation, acceptance and hospitality. The specific financial value of individual bursaries depends on assessed need. Applications and enquiries should be directed to the College for information regarding eligibility assessment and the application process.
eligibility_criteria: Must be an existing Padua College family; Must demonstrate financial hardship or unexpected financial difficulty; Assessed on means-tested basis
special_conditions: Available only to families already enrolled at Padua College. Assessment is based on demonstrated financial need. The Bursary Fund reflects the College's Franciscan values of community support and assistance.

overview: The Academy of Academic Excellence provides a selective pathway for gifted and talented students to extend and challenge themselves through deep learning and real-world application. Students work with specialist teachers to prepare them for academic and workforce pathways of success as globally competent and future-ready citizens.
description: The Academy of Academic Excellence program is informed by research and best practice in gifted and talented education. Students engage in a compacted curriculum in core subjects of English, Maths, Science and Humanities that is differentiated through abstraction, complexity, and variety. The program uses rigorous, explicit, high-engagement teaching and learning strategies and includes academic competitions as an important component of learning for gifted, talented and highly able students. Students benefit from experiences both inside and outside the classroom that empower them to collaboratively think, create, communicate and problem solve in real-world challenges, with cross-curricular projects allowing them to connect their knowledge and skills to practical applications.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 entry students; Year 5 NAPLAN results required; Year 5 Semester 1 and Semester 2 report cards required; Demonstrated gifted and talented ability; Supporting documents of achievement required
special_conditions: Applications for 2026 are NOW OPEN. Stage one applications close 3.00pm Friday 25 April 2025. A panel will assess all applications. Applications and supporting documents must be emailed to academies@ormeauwoodsshs.eq.edu.au

overview: The Academy of Dance Industries develops versatile dancers who are highly skilled, socially responsible and ready for entry into professional dance industry employment upon graduating from Ormeau Woods State High School. In Years 7-9, students develop skills through sequential learning aligned to the Australian Curriculum, and in Years 10-12 complete a Certificate IV in Dance qualification.
description: The Academy of Dance Industries aims to develop cohesive, versatile and nuanced dance professionals who are diverse and prepared for a multifaceted career. Through Years 7 to 9, students engage in sequential learning aligned to ACARA v9.0 curriculum developing skills necessary for success across dance-related industries. In Years 10 to 12, students complete their Certificate IV in Dance qualification through EmpowerDance PTY LTD. The program philosophy is that students should not have to sacrifice their education and development to pursue their passion and enjoyment of dance. The selection process includes a 90-minute Dance Workshop/Audition requiring students to demonstrate ballet, contemporary and jazz techniques and perform a pre-prepared 1-minute solo in a genre of their choice.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Two most recent Achievement, Effort and Behaviour report cards required; CV outlining dance experience required; Reference from current dance school/teacher required
special_conditions: 90-minute Dance Workshop/Audition in Term 2. Workshop date will be advised via email. Complete Arts Excellence Academies Application form. Students must demonstrate ballet, contemporary and jazz techniques and perform a pre-prepared 1-minute solo. A panel will assess applicants for placement in the Academy. Submission of incorrect or misleading information may result in termination of the application.

overview: The Academy of Fine Arts provides selective entry for students with demonstrated talent and passion in visual arts. The academy nurtures creativity and innovation in visual art practices and prepares students for pathways in the creative industries.
description: The Academy of Fine Arts is part of Ormeau Woods' Arts Excellence program offering. The academy supports students with advanced visual arts capability, providing specialist instruction and opportunities to develop their artistic skills and aesthetic understanding. The program integrates visual arts learning with the Australian Curriculum and provides students with opportunities to develop their own unique personal aesthetic and establish a substantial repertoire of professional practices. Students develop highly skilled and versatile visual arts practitioners ready for entry into professional arts industry employment upon graduation.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Two most recent Achievement, Effort and Behaviour report cards required; Portfolio or artwork samples demonstrating visual arts ability required
special_conditions: Applications submitted through Arts Excellence Academies Application form. Selection based on portfolio assessment and demonstrated visual arts ability. Further details available through academies@ormeauwoodsshs.eq.edu.au

overview: The Academy of Media Industries exposes students to a wide range of professional media production techniques and technologies in a creative and high-expectation environment. The program develops highly skilled, versatile media production artists who are ready to advance to future career options in Photography, Video Production, Graphic Design and New Media platforms.
description: The Academy of Media Industries develops students in professional media production using industry standard software and production processes. Students develop skillsets integral for careers in Photography, Video Production, Graphic Design and New Media platforms and industries. The course aims to develop highly skilled, versatile media production artists ready to advance to future career options. The breadth and depth of topics covered provides a platform for students to develop their own unique personal aesthetic and establish a substantial repertoire of professional practices. Course content engages students with marketing and promotional opportunities, management of community projects, curation of exhibits and opportunities to engage with industry leaders in small- and large-scale collaborative projects.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Two most recent Achievement, Effort and Behaviour report cards required; One original media artwork responding to the concept 'All About Me?' required; Short statement (minimum 100 words) explaining artwork required
special_conditions: Applications for 2026 are NOW CLOSED. Stage one applications closed 3.00pm Friday 25 April 2025. Media artwork must be submitted digitally, or via USB if files are large. Artwork can be created using photography, video or digital image media. Successful applicants will be invited to progress to Stage Two for a workshop assessment.

overview: The Academy of Football Excellence aims to develop the technical and tactical capabilities of all players in a professional and holistic program. Students receive expert training in technical skills, tactical understanding, and physical fitness while maintaining academic excellence.
description: The Academy of Football Excellence provides elite training pathways for students with demonstrated ability in football. The program works with Pro-Player, Feenix Futsal and BioActive to design and deliver the Football, Futsal and Strength & Conditioning curriculum areas. Students receive 8 Football Academy sessions per week in Term 1 and Term 3, comprising 4 Football sessions, 1 Futsal session, 1 Strength & Conditioning session and 2 HPE Theory sessions. The HPE theory units are designed using football as a lens to teach students lifelong skills such as nutrition, mental health and positive relationships. The program includes goalkeeper-specific curriculum designed by UEFA B qualified coaches. Students develop technical and tactical capabilities while preparing for pathways in football, further education and sports careers.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Two most recent Achievement, Effort and Behaviour report cards required; Demonstrated football ability required; Selection through trial process in Term 2
special_conditions: Two-stage application process: Stage 1 - Complete Sport Excellence Academies Application form and submit report cards; Stage 2 - Attend soccer trial at Ormeau Woods State High School. Trial date will be advised via email. Contact Academy of Football Excellence Head Coach Josh Sheldon at jrmeh0@eq.edu.au for further information.

overview: The Academy of Soccer Excellence cultivates a love for soccer while promoting holistic student development. Led by experienced coaches, students receive expert training in technical skills, tactical understanding, and physical fitness integrated with academic learning.
description: The Academy of Soccer Excellence aims to cultivate a love for soccer while promoting holistic student development. Led by experienced coaches, students receive expert training in technical skills, tactical understanding, and physical fitness. Through friendly matches and competitive tournaments, participants showcase their talents and foster teamwork. The program integrates soccer with the Australian Curriculum, providing unique opportunities for academic enrichment through sports. Students thrive both on the field and in the classroom through a balanced approach that combines sporting excellence with educational achievement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Two most recent Achievement, Effort and Behaviour report cards required; Demonstrated soccer ability and passion for the sport required; Selection through trial process
special_conditions: Two-stage application process: Stage 1 - Complete Sport Excellence Academies Application form and attach two most recent report cards; Stage 2 - Attend soccer trial at Ormeau Woods State High School. After completing application form, use SUBMIT button to automatically email application with supporting documents.

overview: The Academy of Rugby League Excellence is an elite training and coaching program designed for gifted and talented rugby league students, empowering them to pursue their sporting aspirations while achieving academic success. The academy provides specialist mentoring, training and education from experienced coaches.
description: The Academy of Rugby League Excellence provides a structured and supportive environment where students with demonstrated ability in rugby league receive specialist mentoring, training, and education from experienced coaches including Ben Hannant as Head Coach. Students benefit from specialist coaching in skills, game strategy, and performance analysis, with opportunities to compete in local competitions to showcase and develop their talent. The academy is equipped with modern training facilities and a structured strength and conditioning program, ensuring students develop the physical attributes needed for elite-level rugby league. Players participate in sport-specific fitness programs that enhance endurance, strength, agility, and injury prevention. The academy integrates rugby league training with academic learning, allowing students to balance sporting commitments with educational excellence.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Two most recent Achievement, Effort and Behaviour report cards required; Demonstrated rugby league ability required; Selection through trial process in Term 2; Must maintain high academic standards and positive behaviour
special_conditions: Two-stage application process: Stage 1 - Complete Sport Excellence Academy Application form indicating preference for Academy of Rugby League Excellence and attach two most recent report cards; Stage 2 - Attend rugby league trial in Term 2. Trial date will be advised via email. Recipients must maintain high academic standards, positive behaviour, and full participation in training and competition.

school_name: Our Lady of Lourdes Primary School
program_name: Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund (MacKillop Fund)
overview: Our Lady of Lourdes Primary School participates in the Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund, which provides bursary support to families experiencing significant financial hardship. The bursary is available to students entering the school and is designed to ensure that financial circumstances do not prevent access to Catholic education.
description: The MacKillop Fund is an Archdiocese of Brisbane initiative that provides financial assistance to families experiencing significant hardship within the Catholic school system. The bursary includes tuition, textbooks, uniforms and local excursions for primary school students. Support is provided on a means-tested basis without regard to academic ability. The bursary is available to new students only and must be applied for in the year before enrolment commences. Award decisions are based on demonstrable financial hardship and family need. Families must provide supporting financial documentation to be considered. The bursary commits to funding a child through the completion of the primary school stage (through Year 6) as long as the family continues to demonstrate need. Parents are typically expected to contribute a weekly negotiated amount in consultation with the school.
eligibility_criteria: Family must be experiencing significant financial hardship; Live within the Archdiocese of Brisbane; New students only; Application must be submitted in the year before enrolment commences; Family must be supportive of Catholic education ethos and values; Demonstrated inability to afford school fees required (e.g. holding a Health Care Card); Supporting financial documentation required
year_levels: Prep, Year 1, Year 2, Year 3, Year 4, Year 5, Year 6
special_conditions: Bursary is awarded at the discretion of the committee, based on greatest need and funding availability. The family must maintain supportive engagement with the Catholic school ethos throughout the period of the bursary. Support from the fund is not dependent on academic ability of the student. Families must provide supporting financial documentation before processing. Bursary funding is subject to continued demonstration of financial need.

school_name: Our Lady of Lourdes Primary School
overview: Our Lady of Lourdes Primary School offers a fee concession program to families who demonstrate financial need and have difficulty meeting the full cost of tuition. The school recognises that some families seeking a quality Catholic education may not be able to afford full fees, and therefore offers concession rates based on individual circumstances.
description: Our Lady of Lourdes Primary School provides fee concessions on a case-by-case basis for families experiencing financial difficulty. The aim is to ensure that a quality Catholic education remains accessible and manageable for most families in the community. Families wishing to discuss a possible concession rate are required to arrange a confidential meeting with the Principal to discuss their financial situation. Concessions are provided based on information and documentation provided at an appropriate time. The level of concession varies depending on individual family circumstances and financial need. Families must complete an application and provide appropriate financial documentation to be considered.
eligibility_criteria: Family experiencing financial difficulty; Must arrange confidential meeting with Principal; Financial documentation required; Current enrolment at school or prospective enrolment
special_conditions: Application must be made directly to the Principal. All discussions are strictly confidential. Concessions are awarded at the discretion of school leadership based on individual family circumstances and demonstrated need. Documentation of financial hardship required.

overview: Park Ridge State High School offers the Instrumental Music Excellence (IMEX) program to develop students who demonstrate musical excellence integrated with academic achievement. The program is designed to provide a clear pathway for students interested in Music throughout their high school years, welcoming students of all ability levels while developing exceptional musical skill at graduation.
description: The Instrumental Music Excellence (IMEX) program integrates musical excellence into academic excellence through an all-inclusive culture for learning and creativity. Students in the program study all core subjects (Maths, Science, History and English) as well as Music in a streamed class of Instrumental Music Students until the end of Year 10, graduating with an equivalent Year 11 result. The program can lead to Music Extension in Year 11-12 or alternative subject focus lines. As a government public school, there is no financial cost associated with the program (Non-monetary). The program includes strong community links through concerts, performances and collaboration with local community ensembles. Teachers are professional performers who maintain high standards in conducting and performance. The IMEX program exceeds national curriculum requirements for the Music Key Learning Area.
eligibility_criteria: Audition required; Interest in music and commitment to exploring music throughout high school; Willingness to participate in community performances; Open to all ability levels; Out-of-catchment students can apply (limited places available); Positions are merit-based
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Guaranteed entry into IMEX upon successful entry to IGNITE program if student is an instrumental music student. Program capacity is 150 students. Limited positions available for out-of-catchment students. Successful applicants chosen on merit.

overview: Park Ridge State High School offers the IGNITE Academic Excellence Program to develop lifelong learners with digital literacies and 21st century skills for careers of the future. The program provides innovative teaching and learning built to meet the needs of extension learners, designed for students committed to academic endeavours and pursuit of university education.
description: IGNITE is the Academic Excellence Program achieving through an innovative approach to teaching and learning built to meet the needs of extension learners with skills for a complex technology-based global workforce. Students study all core subjects (Maths, Science, History and English) in a streamed class of high-achieving students until the end of Year 9 or as long as they maintain program requirements (Non-monetary benefit as a public school). The program includes one-to-one laptop program enabling students to learn through ICTs in every subject, rigorous blended learning program, competitions and challenges, community projects, and automatic invitation to Park Ridge Master Classes. Instrumental music students in IGNITE have guaranteed entry into IMEX upon successful IGNITE entry. The program provides a clear pathway for university-bound students.
eligibility_criteria: High-achieving student status; Commitment to academic endeavours; Willingness to pursue university education pathway; Out-of-catchment students can apply (limited places available); Selected on merit; Students must maintain program requirements to continue
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Students study in streamed classes of high-achieving students until end of Year 9 or as long as they maintain program requirements. Instrumental music students have guaranteed entry into IMEX upon successful entry into IGNITE program. Limited positions available for out-of-catchment students. Successful applicants chosen on merit.

overview: Park Ridge State High School offers the AFL Excellence Program from Years 7-12 to develop students demonstrating sporting excellence integrated with academic excellence. The program is designed for both males and females who show interest in AFL, providing a comprehensive education in leadership, communication and team building skills alongside athletic development.
description: The AFL Excellence Program integrates sporting excellence into academic excellence through an all-inclusive program catering to individual needs. Students engage in a flexible program that immerses them in intense physical training with high quality coaching and training within the school curriculum (Non-monetary benefit as a public school). The program exceeds national curriculum requirements for Health and Physical Education and includes practical and theory elements relating to sport and health. Students have timetabled AFL on Wednesday afternoons with the entire squad, used for training and theory topics including game analysis, film, statistics, and leadership. In close partnership with AFL Queensland, the academy provides exceptional results at regional and state levels. The program develops Sports Excellence, Personal/interpersonal values and skills, Leadership, and Academic Excellence. Entry into Year 7 AFL is not dependent on advanced skills; students are selected based on commitment and willingness to work in a team environment.
eligibility_criteria: Commitment to sporting pursuits and willingness to work within a team environment; Evidence of interest in AFL; Good behaviour; Personal reference required prior to acceptance; Entry into Year 7 not dependent on advanced AFL skills; Out-of-catchment students can apply (limited places available); Other students may be invited to apply in later years if demonstrated commitment to program goals and culture; Selected on merit
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Students wishing to enrol in the AFL Academy Program must provide evidence of their interest in AFL, good behaviour, and a personal reference prior to acceptance. Program applied to Year 7 in final year of primary school, with students starting Year 7 the following year. Students who fail to meet certain criteria may be granted provisional status at discretion of school, allowing time to demonstrate commitment to studies and appropriate conduct and behaviour. Students must adhere to three pillars of the program: Attitude, Commitment and Success. Limited positions available for out-of-catchment students. Direct link in Years 7-10 can lead to electing AFL at senior level in Years 11-12.

overview: Palm Beach Currumbin State High School offers the Academic Excellence Program to develop, extend and accelerate knowledge acquisition in core academic subjects. The program focuses on developing lifelong learners through extended and enriched curriculum delivery in Mathematics, English, Science and Humanities.
description: The Academic Excellence Program, beginning in Year 7, focuses on developing, extending and accelerating knowledge acquisition and thinking in core academic subjects. Students participate in special projects and extension activities at local and national levels. Year 7 students entering the program have the opportunity to apply for an Integrated Learning Program combining English, Humanities and Science in cross-curricular units. A small fee is payable upon acceptance into the program and in each subsequent year, with funds used for technology software, guest speakers, curriculum support material, specialised teacher training. Selection involves sitting the ACER Higher Ability Selection Test (HAST) consisting of four subtests; shortlisted applicants attend group problem-solving activities and personal interviews. Entry is competitive and requires demonstration of exceptional academic ability.
eligibility_criteria: Entry into Year 7 requires sitting ACER HAST test; Year 8-10 entry by merit-based application through school reports, competition achievements and NAPLAN results; Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students, students from non-English speaking backgrounds, and economically disadvantaged students may receive pathway assistance if entrance requirements are satisfied; students must demonstrate high academic achievement to maintain position in the program
special_conditions: Students must maintain high academic achievement whilst in the program. The school reserves the right to review student progress. Non-automatic placement available for out-of-catchment students subject to enrolment capacity. Participants limited. Where necessary, measures will be undertaken to accommodate Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students, students from non-English speaking backgrounds, and economically disadvantaged students.

overview: Palm Beach Currumbin State High School offers the Creative Arts Excellence Program providing opportunities for students with specific talents to develop advanced skills in music, dance, drama and visual arts. Students are selected through competitive audition and interview processes and benefit from specialised instruction from industry-experienced teachers.
description: The Creative Arts Excellence Program provides opportunities for students with specific talents to hone skills to an advanced level in music, dance, drama and visual arts. Students are selected by audition and interview with classes including Years 7-12. The program supports students in refining technical abilities, developing creative voice and preparing for future arts pathways through specialised workshops, extended rehearsal and studio time, folio and audition preparation, and access to industry professionals and mentors. Entry is competitive via audition: Dance Excellence holds group auditions in May; Music Excellence requires video audition and supporting documentation; Visual Arts Excellence requires portfolio of 2-6 artwork pieces. A fee is payable upon acceptance and in each subsequent year, with funds supporting additional resources, guest speakers and specialised training. Information evening held in February each year.
eligibility_criteria: Entry is via Selective Entry @ PBC and is competitive; students must demonstrate high level of artistic ability; selection via audition, interview or portfolio review depending on discipline; Dance Excellence entry by group audition held in May; Music Excellence entry by video audition and supporting documentation; Visual Arts Excellence entry by portfolio submission; students in Years 11-12 may conduct program under Sport and Recreation (Specialist) Study Plan; out-of-catchment students subject to available enrolment capacity
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Students must maintain high artistic ability and continue meeting program requirements. School reserves the right to review student progress. Students may only enrol in one Creative Arts Excellence subject. Priority given to in-catchment students; out-of-catchment offers subject to available capacity. Students expected to contribute to school extra-curricular performances. Non-automatic placement for out-of-catchment students.

overview: Palm Beach Currumbin State High School offers the Sports Excellence Program, an elite training and coaching program developed for gifted and talented sporting students. Established in 1996, the program has made PBC one of Australia's strongest sporting schools with over 700 students currently enrolled across 12 targeted sports.
description: The Sports Excellence Program is an elite training and coaching program developed for gifted and talented sporting students empowering them to pursue sporting dreams. Established in 1996, the program includes over 700 students enrolled across 12 targeted sports: Australian Rules, Basketball, Kayak/Surf League, Netball, Rugby League, Soccer, Surfing, Tennis, Touch, Future Stars, Swimming and Track. The program is modelled around five pillars: Wellbeing, Fitness, Skills, Competition abilities and Mental skills. Students are supported through mentoring, training and education from nationally accredited and industry experienced coaches. A fee is payable upon acceptance and in each subsequent year, with funds supporting curriculum resources, guest speakers and specialised training. Students in Years 11-12 may conduct program under Sport and Recreation (Specialist) Study Plan earning 4 QCE points upon completion. Entry highly competitive requiring trial in selected sport. Information evening held mid-February each year.
eligibility_criteria: Entry is via Selective Entry @ PBC and is highly competitive; applicants required to trial in selected sport; students must demonstrate elite or emerging sporting ability; applications for Surf League close 20 April 2026; applications for other Year 7 sports close 15 May 2026 (Round 1); Year 8-12 applications close 17 July 2026; trials held between specified dates (dates confirmed on website); out-of-catchment students subject to available enrolment capacity; Future Stars program open to Years 7-10 only with no out-of-catchment students eligible; students must maintain high standards of behaviour and attendance
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Students must maintain elite sporting performance and high academic standards to retain position. School reserves right to review progress. Entry highly competitive with limited places available in each sport. Participation fee payable annually. Students expected to participate in scheduled training, competitions and events. Priority given to in-catchment students; out-of-catchment offers subject to available capacity. Non-automatic placement for out-of-catchment students. Students may sit retrial if unsuccessful in round 1.

overview: Palm Beach Currumbin State High School offers the Future Stars Program for emerging athletes in individual and team-based sports not catered for in other Sports Excellence programs. The program is available to students entering Years 7-10 and aims to support diverse athletic abilities.
description: The Future Stars Program is available to students entering Years 7-10 and aims to cater for the diverse needs of emerging athletes who compete in individual-based sports such as swimming, golf, table tennis, squash and gymnastics, and athletes in team-based sports not currently catered for in existing Sports Excellence programs such as cricket, water polo, hockey and volleyball. The program is evaluated on criteria including current performance, coaching history, competition history, team player attributes and potential for growth. Applications from current PBC students and those in-zone will be considered. NO out-of-catchment students will be considered for this program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 to Year 10; individual sport or team sport athletes not catered for in main Sports Excellence programs; current PBC students and in-zone students only - NO out-of-catchment students eligible; applications evaluated on current performance, coaching history, competition history, team player attributes and potential for growth; completed applications and required documentation emailed to excellenceprograms@pbc-shs.eq.edu.au
special_conditions: NO out-of-catchment students eligible. Current PBC students and in-zone students only. Applications evaluated on athletic criteria including performance, coaching history, competition history, team player attributes and potential for growth.

overview: Peace Lutheran College offers the Academic Excellence Scholarship to recognise and support outstanding students who demonstrate exceptional academic performance. The scholarship provides financial assistance for a student's education while acknowledging their hard work and dedication in their academic studies.
description: The Academic Excellence Scholarship is awarded on academic merit as determined through an EduTest educational assessment, a review of submitted portfolio of achievements, and an interview with the Principal. Scholarships provide a percentage remission of tuition fees. Selection is based on academic excellence demonstrated through testing, portfolio review, and principal interview. Recipients are expected to maintain alignment with the College values of Peace, Excellence, Respect, and Community. All applicants must complete the Academic Excellence Scholarship Application Form and prepare a portfolio as outlined in the Scholarship Handbook.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, or Year 10; Currently enrolled in Year 6, 7, 8, or 9 respectively; Must sit EduTest educational assessment; Applicants may only apply for one scholarship
special_conditions: An applicant may only apply for one scholarship. Scholarships are determined through an examination and/or portfolio of achievements as well as an interview with the Principal. All applicants should display examples of their alignment with College values of Peace, Excellence, Respect, and Community.

overview: Peace Lutheran College offers the General Excellence Scholarship to recognise and support outstanding students who demonstrate exceptional performance across various areas, leadership qualities, and a commitment to community service. This scholarship is for academic all-rounders with demonstrated leadership and community service.
description: The General Excellence Scholarship recognises students who have demonstrated excellence across a range of endeavours, display high levels of community commitment and service, and are willing to support the Christian ethos of the College. Selection is competitive and based on a portfolio of achievements, test results, and an interview with the Principal. Recipients must show alignment with College values and evidence of leadership and co-curricular excellence. The scholarship provides a percentage remission of tuition fees based on merit as determined through assessment and interview.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, or Year 10; Must demonstrate excellence across range of endeavours; Must display community commitment and service; Must support Christian ethos of the College; May only apply for one scholarship
special_conditions: General Excellence scholarships are competitive and only a limited number will be offered each year. An applicant may only apply for one scholarship. Recipients must support the Christian ethos of the College.

overview: Peace Lutheran College offers the Cultural Excellence Scholarship to recognise and support students who demonstrate exceptional talent and achievement in music, drama, or visual arts. The scholarship acknowledges students with proven strengths in the cultural and performing arts.
description: The Cultural Excellence Scholarship recognises students with demonstrated excellence in cultural pursuits including music, drama, and visual arts. Selection is based on a portfolio of cultural achievements, performance assessment or audition, and an interview with the Principal. The scholarship provides a percentage remission of tuition fees. Applicants must demonstrate high levels of commitment and achievement in their chosen cultural discipline and align with College values of Peace, Excellence, Respect, and Community.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, or Year 10; Must demonstrate excellence in music, drama, or visual arts; Must provide portfolio of cultural achievements; May only apply for one scholarship
special_conditions: An applicant may only apply for one scholarship. Selection includes portfolio review and may include audition or assessment depending on discipline. All applicants should display examples of their alignment with College values.

overview: Peace Lutheran College supports eligible students from rural, regional, and remote areas through the Commonwealth Regional Scholarship Program. This program provides financial assistance specifically designed for boarding students from areas outside metropolitan Cairns.
description: The Commonwealth Regional Scholarship Program assists students from rural, regional, and remote areas with financial assistance towards boarding fees. This program is designed to support families from regional and remote Queensland, Torres Strait, and surrounding areas who wish their children to board at Peace Lutheran College. The program provides financial support to reduce boarding costs for eligible families. Students must meet Commonwealth Regional Scholarship Program eligibility criteria based on residential location and school boarding requirements.
eligibility_criteria: Reside in rural, regional, or remote area; Enrolled as boarding student at Peace Lutheran College; Students in Year 7 to Year 12; Must meet Commonwealth Regional Scholarship Program eligibility criteria
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Eligibility determined by residential location and Commonwealth Regional Scholarship Program criteria. Financial assistance is specifically towards boarding fees. Applicants must be enrolled as boarding students at the College.

overview: Peregian Beach College offers Academic Excellence Scholarships to reward students who demonstrate outstanding academic achievement and commitment to learning. These scholarships are available to students commencing at the College in Years 7 to 12 and recognise exceptional intellectual ability and scholarly achievement.
description: Peregian Beach College offers full and part scholarships to recognise and reward students who demonstrate academic excellence. These scholarships provide many opportunities for students to pursue excellence in their chosen fields and make a significant contribution to the life of the College. The scholarship may provide full fee remission or partial fee reduction depending on the level of academic achievement demonstrated. If successful students maintain their excellent grades and commitment, the scholarship can be awarded for a second year. Selection is based on demonstrated academic performance through school reports, standardised testing, or other assessment methods specified in the Scholarship Application form.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 to 12; Must be new to the College; Must demonstrate exceptional academic achievement; Must complete Scholarship Application form with supporting documentation including school reports; Successful recipients expected to maintain excellent grades and commitment to scholarship renewal
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Partial Fee Reduction
special_conditions: Scholarship can be awarded for the second year if successful students maintain their excellent grades and commitment. Recipients must submit Scholarship Application along with Enrolment Form and accompanying documents as requested. Scholarships provide both full and part options depending on merit level demonstrated.

overview: Peregian Beach College offers Music Excellence Scholarships to students who demonstrate outstanding musical talent and performance ability. These scholarships are available to students commencing at Years 7 to 12 and recognise students who will make a significant contribution to the College's music program.
description: Peregian Beach College recognises and rewards students who demonstrate Music excellence as part of its commitment to supporting student achievement across all fields of endeavour. The College offers full and part Music scholarships to students new to the College commencing in Years 7 to 12. These scholarships provide many opportunities for students to pursue excellence in music and make a significant contribution to the life of the College. The scholarship may provide full fee remission or partial fee reduction depending on demonstrated musical ability. Selection is based on musical performance auditions, demonstrated experience, and commitment to music. If successful students maintain their excellent grades and commitment, the scholarship can be awarded for a second year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 to 12; Must be new to the College; Must demonstrate exceptional musical ability and performance; Must complete Scholarship Application form; Audition or performance assessment required; Successful recipients expected to maintain commitment to music program and academic standards
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Partial Fee Reduction
special_conditions: Scholarship can be awarded for the second year if successful students maintain their excellent grades and commitment. Recipients must submit Scholarship Application along with Enrolment Form and accompanying documents as requested. Selection based on audition and demonstrated musical excellence.

overview: Peregian Beach College offers Sporting Excellence Scholarships to students who demonstrate exceptional athletic ability and sporting talent. These scholarships are available to students commencing at Years 7 to 12 and recognise students who will make a significant contribution to the College's sporting program.
description: Peregian Beach College recognises and rewards students who demonstrate Sporting excellence as part of its commitment to supporting student achievement across all fields of endeavour. The College offers full and part sporting scholarships to students new to the College commencing in Years 7 to 12. These scholarships provide many opportunities for students to pursue excellence in sport and make a significant contribution to the life of the College. The scholarship may provide full fee remission or partial fee reduction depending on demonstrated sporting ability. Selection is based on sporting performance, demonstrated athletic achievement, and commitment to sporting excellence. If successful students maintain their excellent grades and commitment, the scholarship can be awarded for a second year. The College offers an elite sports training academy in conjunction with partner organisations.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 to 12; Must be new to the College; Must demonstrate exceptional sporting ability and athletic achievement; Must complete Scholarship Application form; Sports assessment or trial attendance required; Successful recipients expected to maintain commitment to sporting program and academic standards
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Partial Fee Reduction
special_conditions: Scholarship can be awarded for the second year if successful students maintain their excellent grades and commitment. Recipients must submit Scholarship Application along with Enrolment Form and accompanying documents as requested. Selection based on demonstrated sporting excellence. College operates elite sports training academy offering soccer, basketball, netball and dance.

overview: Peregian Beach College offers Community Excellence Scholarships to students who demonstrate exceptional commitment to community service and leadership. These scholarships are available to students commencing at Years 7 to 12 and recognise students who contribute meaningfully to the College and wider community.
description: Peregian Beach College recognises and rewards students who demonstrate Community excellence as part of its commitment to supporting student achievement across all fields of endeavour. The College offers full and part scholarships to students new to the College commencing in Years 7 to 12 who show exceptional community service and leadership. These scholarships provide many opportunities for students to pursue excellence in community contribution and make a significant impact on the life of the College. The scholarship may provide full fee remission or partial fee reduction depending on demonstrated community commitment and leadership ability. Selection is based on evidence of community service, leadership initiatives, and commitment to contributing to the College community. If successful students maintain their excellent grades and commitment, the scholarship can be awarded for a second year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 to 12; Must be new to the College; Must demonstrate exceptional community service and leadership commitment; Must complete Scholarship Application form with evidence of community involvement; Successful recipients expected to maintain commitment to community service and academic standards
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Partial Fee Reduction
special_conditions: Scholarship can be awarded for the second year if successful students maintain their excellent grades and commitment. Recipients must submit Scholarship Application along with Enrolment Form and accompanying documents as requested. Selection based on demonstrated community excellence and leadership capability.

overview: Peregian Beach College offers bursary and financial assistance programs to help families experiencing financial difficulties. The College recognises that financial hardship can prevent families from accessing quality education and provides support through flexible payment plans and bursary options.
description: Peregian Beach College understands that families may experience financial difficulties and offers payment plans and bursary options for temporary relief. Families experiencing difficulties meeting their financial obligations are encouraged to contact the Accounts Receivable Officer to discuss available support options. The College considers bursary options on a case-by-case basis and aims to support families in genuine financial hardship to remain enrolled. This is a means-tested financial assistance program rather than a merit-based scholarship. Flexibility in payment arrangements is available including weekly, fortnightly, or monthly instalments by pre-approved Direct Debit. Additional discounts include 5% off tuition fees for families who pre-pay annually, and sibling discounts of 10-30% depending on number of children.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current or prospective families; Must be experiencing financial difficulties or hardship; Contact with Accounts Receivable Officer required; Case-by-case assessment of circumstances; Flexible payment arrangements available
special_conditions: Bursary options available for temporary relief only. Assessment is on a case-by-case basis. Families must contact Accounts Receivable Officer to discuss their circumstances. Flexible payment plans available by pre-approved Direct Debit Request. Sibling discounts of 10% (second child), 20% (third child), and 30% (fourth and subsequent children) apply to tuition fees only; levies must be paid in full for each student.

program_name: Sunshine Coast FC Football Academy Scholarship
overview: Peregian Beach College in partnership with Sunshine Coast FC offers Football Academy Scholarships to young talented footballers on the Sunshine Coast. The scholarship covers both coaching fees for Sunshine Coast FC and tuition at Peregian Beach College.
description: Sunshine Coast FC and Peregian Beach College have partnered to provide a scholarship opportunity for young talented footballers who are identified as promising athletes. The Scholarship Program covers the cost of both coaching provided by Sunshine Coast FC and tuition provided by Peregian Beach College. This dual-benefit scholarship recognises the equal importance of quality education in the overall development of young footballers. Recipients will be enrolled at their appropriate grade level within the College. Initial scholarships are normally provided for a period of 2 to 3 years depending on school year of entry and candidates' compliance with scholarship requirements. Scholarship holders are subject to annual review of their performance as a player and as a student at the College. Both the College and Sunshine Coast FC reserve the right to limit the number of scholarships provided in any one year.
eligibility_criteria: Must be selected as a player or shadow player in one of Sunshine Coast FC's NPL teams; OR must have commenced the development pathway within Sunshine Coast FC Elite Academy or Skills Acquisition Program; Must be willing to enrol at appropriate grade level at Peregian Beach College; Subject to normal discipline requirements of both Sunshine Coast FC and Peregian Beach College; Both boys and girls are eligible to apply
special_conditions: Initial scholarships normally for period of 2 to 3 years depending on school year of entry and candidates' compliance with scholarship requirements. Opportunity for extension of scholarship available. Annual review of scholarship holder's performance as a player and as a student at the College required. Scholarship holders subject to normal discipline requirements of both Sunshine Coast FC and Peregian Beach College. Further details can be obtained by emailing expression of interest to admin@sunshinecoastfc.com.au. Sunshine Coast FC & Peregian Beach College reserve the right to limit the number of scholarships provided in any one year.

overview: Pimlico State High School offers the Program of Excellence in Music commencing in Year 7, providing extension and enrichment to talented musicians. The program recognises students who demonstrate exceptional instrumental or vocal performance ability and musicianship. Students within the program are grouped in specialist music classes and participate in the school's extensive ensemble programs.
description: The Program of Excellence in Music is a selective entry program offered to students entering Year 7 and continuing through Year 12. Students are selected based on their level of skill and musicianship in relation to their selected instrument (including voice) and their knowledge and application of music theory. Within the program, students are grouped in the same Music classes in Years 7 and 8 and provided with opportunities for extension and enrichment using a curriculum based on the Australian Curriculum and incorporating elements of AMEB music theory. All Music Excellence students must be involved in the school's instrumental music or vocal programs and are expected to play a lead role in the school's ensembles and participate in a wide range of school and community events. Music Excellence students receive further opportunities for extension and enrichment through workshops and masterclasses with visiting musicians and ensembles, and involvement in key music events such as the Australian Festival of Chamber Music. This is a non-monetary program offering selective placement and enriched curriculum access.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Demonstrated talent in instrumental or vocal performance; Knowledge of music theory; Commitment to school or community activities; Must participate in school's instrumental music or vocal programs; Entry by application and audition/interview process; Limited number of places available for students without prior musical training who demonstrate commitment to other activities
special_conditions: Entry into the Program of Excellence is by application with shortlisted students required to participate in an audition/interview process. Students are selected based on their level of skill and musicianship in relation to their selected instrument (including voice) and knowledge and application of music theory. Limited scope available for students who have not previously had the opportunity to play a musical instrument, who will be assessed on music theory and demonstrated commitment to other school or community activities. All Music Excellence students must be involved in the school's instrumental music or vocal programs. Students are expected to play a lead role in school ensembles and participate in school and community events. Pimlico's Program of Excellence in Music is recognised within the school's Enrolment Management Plan, allowing places to students from outside the school's defined catchment area.

overview: Pimlico State High School offers a limited number of Music Scholarships each year to reward outstanding musical talent and achievement. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional ability in instrumental or vocal performance. Recipients benefit from financial support towards their musical education and school fees.
description: Each year, a limited number of Music Scholarships are available to students demonstrating exceptional talent in music. Applicants must demonstrate excellence in either instrumental or vocal performance and be selected through an audition and interview process. The exact financial value of the scholarship is not publicly disclosed on the school website, but scholarships are awarded on the basis of demonstrated musical excellence and commitment to the music program. Applications are submitted as part of the enrolment process, and auditions and interviews are conducted in late October for Year 7 entry. Successful recipients are expected to actively participate in the school's ensemble programs and maintain high standards of musicianship throughout their enrolment. The scholarship is contingent on the student's continued engagement with the school's music program and maintaining the values and expectations of the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Demonstrated exceptional talent in instrumental or vocal performance; Successfully shortlisted from audition/interview process; Commitment to participate in school ensemble programs; Entry through application and audition/interview
special_conditions: Audition and interviews for Programs of Excellence in Music and Music Scholarship conducted in late October 2025. Notification of enrolment applications provided in late Term 3. Relevant documentation required to be submitted for entry through Program of Excellence in Music outlined in the Application for Enrolment pack and must be completed by due date. Enrolments accepted for processing from 8am Friday 1 August 2025. Closing date for first round of enrolment offers Thursday 4 September 2025. Scholarship recipients must maintain engagement with school music programs and uphold school values.

overview: Pimlico State High School offers the Global Studies Program as a developmentally sequenced pathway of enrichment and extension across Years 7 to 12. The program recognises students interested in global citizenship, world affairs, and international engagement. Students are prepared as global citizens and leaders ready to make a positive difference in the world.
description: Pimlico's Global Studies Program provides a comprehensive pathway of enrichment and extension across Years 7 to 12, designed to develop students as global citizens and leaders. The program prepares students to have deep understanding of world affairs and global issues and trends, authentic appreciation and respect for social and cultural diversity, and the ability to be competitive and agile members of a global workforce. Students communicate and collaborate confidently with others from diverse backgrounds and through new media and technologies, and generate innovative ideas and solutions to complex, interdisciplinary challenges at the local, national and global level. The program includes study tours to countries including France, Vietnam, and Singapore, and programs such as Young Diplomats that encourage engagement with global issues. The school provides enrichment activities and cultural exchange opportunities. This is a non-monetary program offering selective placement and enriched academic opportunities focused on global competencies and international mindedness.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Interest in global citizenship and world affairs; Demonstrated commitment to international engagement; Selection through application process; Available across Years 7 to 12
special_conditions: Program provides a developmentally sequenced pathway with enrichment and extension opportunities. Includes study tours and international engagement projects. School Council provides strategic oversight of program implementation. Program is part of school's commitment to global education and international mindedness.

program_name: Excellence Programs in Performing Arts and Academics
overview: Pimlico State High School offers Excellence Programs in Performing Arts and Academics, allowing selective entry for students demonstrating exceptional ability. These programs provide out-of-catchment students with priority enrolment pathways and enrichment opportunities. Students benefit from specialised curriculum and extension activities aligned with their demonstrated excellence.
description: Pimlico State High School offers a range of Excellence Programs in Performing Arts and Academics as part of its Enrolment Management Plan. These selective entry programs allow students from outside the school's catchment area to access places based on their demonstrated excellence in these specific areas. The programs are designed to provide enrichment and extension opportunities beyond the core curriculum, attracting high-achieving students through priority enrolment pathways. Students in these programs benefit from specialised facilities and learning experiences, including the state-of-the-art Denise Glasgow Performing Arts Centre, Music Centre of Excellence, and Global Education Centre. The exact nature of the academic program components and selection criteria are detailed in the school's enrolment documentation and available from the school upon request. This is a non-monetary program offering selective placement and enriched educational opportunities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 onwards; Demonstrated excellence in performing arts or academics; Out-of-catchment students eligible for priority enrolment consideration; Selection through application and assessment process; Continued availability contingent on demonstrated achievement
special_conditions: Places in Excellence Programs are available to out-of-catchment enrolments once demand for in-catchment enrolment has been met and sufficient student enrolment capacity has been reserved for future in-catchment growth. The enrolment criteria for the Program of Excellence is available from the school. Subject to available student enrolment capacity and school's criteria for placement in the particular Program of Excellence.

overview: Pinnacle Academic College offers a limited number of scholarships for families in need to access places in the Distance Education programme. The scholarship recognises families experiencing financial hardship and provides support to enable educational access.
description: Pinnacle Academic College understands that life can present challenges for families. In response, the college offers a limited number of scholarships specifically designed for new enrolments in the Distance Education program. These scholarships are intended for families experiencing financial difficulties who wish to provide their children with educational opportunities. The program provides support to make education more accessible and affordable. Terms and conditions apply to all scholarship awards. Interested families should contact the college directly for further information regarding the scholarship application process and eligibility requirements.
eligibility_criteria: Open to families experiencing financial hardship; New enrolments to Distance Education programme only; Terms and conditions apply
year_levels: Year 1, Year 2, Year 3, Year 4, Year 5, Year 6
special_conditions: Terms and conditions apply. Limited number of scholarships available. Contact school directly at admin@pac.qld.edu.au for application details and current availability.

overview: Pine Rivers State High School offers Academic Scholarship Enrolment Entry to recognise outstanding academic achievement in prospective Year 7 students. Students are selected on the basis of an order of merit, demonstrating exceptional academic ability and potential.
description: The Academic Scholarship Enrolment Entry programme at Pine Rivers State High School is designed to attract and support students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability. Successful applicants are selected primarily on the basis of an order of merit ranking. All scholarship recipients must maintain continued excellence in behaviour and effort to remain eligible. The programme recognises students with outstanding academic performance and provides them with guaranteed enrolment at the school. Students are required to demonstrate consistent academic achievement through their school reports and must maintain high standards throughout their enrollment at Pine Rivers to remain in good standing with the scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: Prospective Year 7 students; Must demonstrate exceptional academic achievement; School reports reflecting continued excellence in academia, behaviour and effort required; Selection on basis of order of merit
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients must maintain continued excellence in behaviour and effort to remain eligible. Selection is based on the basis of an order of merit from school reports.

overview: Pine Rivers State High School offers Cultural Scholarship Enrolment Entry to outstanding performers in Dance, Vocal, Visual Art, and Instrumental Music. Students are selected primarily on the basis of performance in their chosen cultural discipline while maintaining academic and behavioural standards.
description: The Cultural Scholarship Enrolment Entry programme recognises exceptional talent in Dance, Vocal, Visual Art, and Instrumental Music at Pine Rivers State High School. Selection is primarily based on the student's artistic performance and demonstrated excellence in their chosen discipline. In addition to demonstrated performance excellence, students are required to maintain continued excellence in behaviour and effort to remain eligible for the scholarship throughout their enrolment. This programme provides a pathway for artistically talented students to pursue their passion while meeting the school's high expectations for conduct and commitment to learning. The school values cultural diversity and the arts as integral to student development and school community.
eligibility_criteria: Prospective Year 7 students; Talent demonstrated in Dance, Vocal, Visual Art or Instrumental Music; Selection primarily on basis of performance; Continued excellence in behaviour and effort required to maintain eligibility
special_conditions: Students are required to demonstrate continued excellence in behaviour and effort to remain eligible for Cultural Scholarship enrolment. Discipline areas include Dance, Vocal, Visual Art, and Instrumental Music.

overview: Pine Rivers State High School offers Sporting Scholarship Enrolment Entry to students demonstrating exceptional sporting achievement and potential. Selection is based on the prospective student's sporting achievements and continued commitment to excellence in sport, behaviour, and academic effort.
description: The Sporting Scholarship Enrolment Entry programme at Pine Rivers State High School is designed to recognise and support students with exceptional sporting talent and achievements. Selection is based primarily on the prospective student's demonstrated sporting achievements and competitive performance. Scholarship recipients must maintain school reports that reflect continued excellence in academic performance, behaviour and effort. This programme provides a pathway for athletically talented students to pursue sporting excellence whilst meeting the school's expectations for academic achievement and conduct. Sports offered by the school include Australian Football, Basketball, Baseball, Cricket, Cross Country, Football, Golf, Hockey, Netball, Rugby League, Rugby Union, Softball, Swimming, Tennis, Touch, Track and Field, Triathlon, Volleyball and Water Polo.
eligibility_criteria: Prospective Year 7 students; Demonstrated sporting achievements; School reports reflecting continued excellence in academia, behaviour and effort required; Selection based on prospective student's sporting achievements
special_conditions: School reports must reflect continued excellence in academia, behaviour and effort. Scholarship recipients must maintain high standards in both academics and sporting participation.

overview: Pimpama State Secondary College offers the Academic Excellence Scholarship to recognise and celebrate students who demonstrate exceptional academic achievement. The scholarship provides a guaranteed position in the prestigious Academic Excellence Program for Year 7 entry, where students learn alongside likeminded peers.
description: The Academic Excellence Scholarship is designed for current Year 6 students enrolling in Year 7 for 2026. This scholarship provides students with guaranteed enrolment in the Academic Excellence Program where they benefit from enrichment and extension opportunities across an academic Academy of their choice. The selection process involves a competitive assessment of academic performance and ability. Applications closed on 30 August 2025. The scholarship offers program placement and enrichment opportunities as its primary benefit, allowing selected students to access specialised academic pathways and extension learning.
eligibility_criteria: Current Year 6 students enrolling in Year 7 for 2026; Demonstrated academic excellence and outstanding achievement; Enrolling at Pimpama State Secondary College
special_conditions: Guaranteed position in Academic Excellence Program. Students learn alongside likeminded peers in enrichment and extension programs. Placement in an academic Academy of student choice.

overview: Pimpama State Secondary College offers Values Scholarships aligned to the school's five core values: Perseverance, Respect, Integrity, Dedication and Empathy. These scholarships are available to existing and future Year 7-12 students who demonstrate outstanding attitude, effort, behaviour and attendance.
description: The Values Scholarships programme recognises three types of scholarships available in 2025 for existing and future Year 7-12 students enrolling at the College. The scholarships are designed for students who demonstrate outstanding attitude, effort, behaviour and attendance in both community and schooling endeavours. Selection is based on referee endorsement and assessment of alignment with school values. The application process requires a completed application form and referee form, with referees typically being school principals, deputy principals, class teachers or sports coaches. The specific financial value of these scholarships and duration of award are not specified on the available materials.
eligibility_criteria: Existing and future Year 7-12 students enrolling at the College; Outstanding attitude, effort, behaviour and attendance; Demonstrated commitment in both community and schooling endeavours; Alignment with school values (Perseverance, Respect, Integrity, Dedication, Empathy)
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Three types of scholarships offered aligned to school values. Referee endorsement required (school personnel such as Principal, Deputy Principal, Class Teacher or Sports Coach). Assessment based on attitude, effort, behaviour and attendance.

school_name: Prince of Peace Lutheran College - Henderson Road Campus
overview: Prince of Peace Lutheran College offers Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding academic achievement in students entering Years 7, 9 or 11. The scholarships recognise students who demonstrate exceptional ability in all major curriculum areas and possess high all-round ability.
description: An Academic Scholarship is a financial bursary awarded by the College to a student achieving significantly high and consistent results in their studies. The bursary is given in the form of a discount on annual tuition fees, provided as a partial fee reduction. Scholarships are awarded for two academic years and are typically granted at Years 7, 9 and 11. Selection is based on a competitive examination conducted by Prince of Peace Lutheran College in conjunction with Edutest, along with previous academic records and a personal interview with the Head of College or Head of Campus. Students should demonstrate a high level of achievement in English, Mathematics, Science and the Humanities, and possess all-round ability. The College may take a family's financial situation into account if flagged on the initial Application Form.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 9, or Year 11; Open to all students including current Prince of Peace students and external students; Must demonstrate high level of achievement in all major curriculum areas including English, Mathematics, Science and Humanities; Must possess a high level of all-round ability; Must be a current student or have completed Prince of Peace online application including payment of $110 application fee before registering; Non-refundable scholarship examination fee of $100 payable to Edutest prior to sitting the academic examination
special_conditions: No 100% fee relief scholarships are awarded. Scholarships are offered for a period of up to two academic years. Scholarship recipients must adhere to Academic Scholarship conditions outlined in letter of offer. All scholarships are reviewed annually and continuation is dependent on the scholarship holder's performance, academic effort, behaviour, participation and current or likely contribution to overall College life.
