Students must participate fully in co-curricular activities of the College in so far as their abilities allow and display leadership by encouraging and supporting other students. Students who receive an Academic Scholarship in Year 7 or Year 9 will be eligible to re-apply in Year 9 or Year 11. Interim review conducted at end of each semester and end of first year, with other reviews as required. Scholarship awards are confidential arrangements between the College and family and must not be discussed with other parties. Testing results remain the property of the College and are not disclosed to applicants. Shortlisted applicants only will be contacted for interview with College Executive team if deemed necessary. Alternate testing dates are not available.

school_name: Prince of Peace Lutheran College - Henderson Road Campus
overview: Prince of Peace Lutheran College administers Buchholz Secondary Schooling Scholarships available through Lutheran Education Queensland (LEQ) to provide access to secondary education for students who would otherwise be precluded from attending due to financial hardship. Scholarships cover tuition fees and compulsory levies for the duration of secondary education.
description: Buchholz Secondary Schooling Scholarships are available through Lutheran Education Queensland (LEQ) to support students who would otherwise be unable to attend secondary school due to financial hardship. The scholarships cover annual tuition fees including compulsory levies at a level between 25-100% depending on the family's financial capacity and available funds. A small number of scholarships are available annually. Scholarships are awarded for the length of the child's secondary education up to a maximum of six years (Years 7 through 12). Eligibility requires Year 6 students not yet enrolled in a Lutheran secondary school, or secondary school-aged children seeking to move to a Lutheran school. Applicants or their families must be communicant members of the Lutheran Church of Australia or active members of other Christian denominations, and families must be experiencing ongoing financial hardship that would impose undue hardship to continue school fee commitment.
eligibility_criteria: Year 6 students not yet enrolled in a Lutheran school offering secondary education; Secondary school-aged children seeking to move to a Lutheran school; Communicant members of the Lutheran Church of Australia or active members of other Christian denominations; Families experiencing ongoing financial hardship that would impose undue financial burden on continued school fee commitment; Demonstrated ability and willingness of child to contribute broadly and positively to school life; Demonstrated capacity of child to cope with academic program demands; School's financial capacity to cater for child's specific learning and behavioural needs; Ability to satisfy school enrolment requirements on ongoing basis
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or 25% Fee Reduction
special_conditions: A small number of scholarships available through Lutheran Education Queensland (LEQ). Scholarships cover annual tuition fees including compulsory levies at level between 25-100% depending on family's capacity to contribute and available funds. Awarded for duration of child's secondary education up to maximum of six years (Years 7-12). Assessment criteria include child's ability and willingness to contribute broadly and positively to school life, capacity to cope with academic program demands, school's financial capacity to cater for child's specific learning and behavioural needs, and ability to satisfy enrolment requirements. Award is confidential arrangement between school and family.

overview: Pioneer State High School offers Academic Scholarships to outstanding Year 6 students from local state primary schools who intend to enrol at Pioneer in Year 7. These scholarships recognise exceptional academic achievement and are sponsored by the School's Parents and Citizens Association.
description: Pioneer State High School's Parents and Citizens Association offers scholarships annually to recognise and reward outstanding academic achievement among incoming Year 7 students. These scholarships are awarded to high-achieving students from state primary schools who demonstrate strong academic performance and commitment to learning. The exact financial value and duration of the scholarships are available through the school's application forms and further information is provided in Term 3. Selection is based on demonstrated academic excellence and assessment through the application process.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a Year 6 student currently enrolled in a state primary school; Must intend on enrolling at Pioneer State High School in Year 7 the following year; Outstanding academic performance required
number_awarded: Up to 6 per year (based on available information that six scholarships are offered)
special_conditions: Application forms for each category are available. Forms can be collected from the school office or from partner primary schools. Further information is provided in Term 3 of each year.

overview: Pioneer State High School offers Cultural Scholarships to outstanding Year 6 students from local state primary schools who demonstrate exceptional talent in cultural pursuits. These scholarships are sponsored by the School's Parents and Citizens Association and recognise excellence in the arts and cultural activities.
description: Cultural Scholarships are available to reward exceptional talent and achievement in cultural activities at Pioneer State High School. The school recognises excellence across a range of cultural pursuits including drama, music, dance and visual arts. These scholarships are sponsored by the Parents and Citizens Association and are available to Year 6 students from state primary schools intending to enrol at Pioneer in Year 7. The exact financial benefits, selection criteria and assessment process are detailed in the school's application forms, with further information provided in Term 3 of each year.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a Year 6 student currently enrolled in a state primary school; Must intend on enrolling at Pioneer State High School in Year 7 the following year; Must demonstrate outstanding cultural talent or achievement
special_conditions: Application forms for each category are available. Forms can be collected from the school office or from partner primary schools. Selection includes assessment of cultural performance or portfolio. Further information is provided in Term 3 of each year.

overview: Pioneer State High School offers Sporting Scholarships to outstanding Year 6 students from local state primary schools who demonstrate exceptional sporting ability and talent. These scholarships are sponsored by the School's Parents and Citizens Association and recognise athletic excellence.
description: Sporting Scholarships are awarded to Year 6 students who demonstrate outstanding sporting ability and achievement. Pioneer State High School recognises excellence across a diverse range of sports including futsal, soccer, touch and volleyball, as well as other sports. These scholarships are sponsored by the Parents and Citizens Association and are available to students from state primary schools intending to enrol at Pioneer in Year 7. The exact financial benefits, duration, selection criteria and assessment process are outlined in the school's application forms, with further information provided in Term 3 of each year.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a Year 6 student currently enrolled in a state primary school; Must intend on enrolling at Pioneer State High School in Year 7 the following year; Must demonstrate exceptional sporting talent or achievement at regional, state or national level
special_conditions: Application forms for each category are available. Forms can be collected from the school office or from partner primary schools. Selection is based on demonstrated sporting achievement and performance. Further information is provided in Term 3 of each year.

program_name: Learning Excellence at Pioneer (LEAP) Enrichment Program
overview: Pioneer State High School offers the LEAP enrichment program, an innovative selective entry program for gifted and talented students. The program is delivered in partnership with CQ University and provides outstanding enrichment and extension opportunities for the most able students entering Year 7.
description: Learning Excellence at Pioneer (LEAP) is an innovative enrichment and extension program offered in partnership with CQ University. The program is designed to provide outstanding enrichment and extension opportunities for the most able students entering Year 7. LEAP recognises and supports students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and intellectual promise. The program involves partnership with a tertiary institution, providing students with access to university-level learning and experiences. Further information about the LEAP program, eligibility criteria, selection process and how to apply is available from the school.
eligibility_criteria: Must be entering Year 7; Must demonstrate exceptional academic ability and intellectual promise; Prospective Year 7 students can apply to join the program
special_conditions: Program is delivered in partnership with CQ University. This is an enrichment and extension program aimed at the most able students. Selection is based on demonstrated exceptional academic ability.

school_name: Queen of Apostles Primary School - Chuter Street Campus
overview: Queen of Apostles Primary School offers fee concessions to families experiencing financial hardship. The school is guided by its Catholic mission and values in determining financial arrangements related to student enrolment, extending compassion and support to families in need.
description: Queen of Apostles Primary School provides fee concessions for families facing financial hardship. Families who are unable to meet full school fees may apply for assistance through the fee concession program. Applications may be made to the school's Finance Officer. This program reflects the school's commitment to its Catholic ethos of compassion and extending support to families who require financial assistance to enable their children to attend the school. The value and duration of any concession are determined on a case-by-case basis according to individual family circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; Demonstrated need for fee assistance
special_conditions: Applications for fee concessions are confidential and assessed on individual family circumstances. Families must demonstrate genuine financial hardship to be considered.

school_name: QCHS - Royal Brisbane and ACT for Kids Campus

overview: The Queensland Academies Isolated Students (QAIS) Bursary provides financial support to families of students living away from home to attend the Queensland Academy for Health Sciences. The bursary assists talented students from regional and remote areas who are not eligible for the Living Away From Home Allowance Scheme (LAFHAS), supporting their access to this selective entry academy.
description: The QAIS Bursary is a form of support for eligible rural and remote students attending Queensland Academy for Health Sciences on the Gold Coast. The bursary provides financial support through travel and student accommodation allowance for students who do not qualify for LAFHAS. Neither payment attracts GST and there is no financial means test. Students must be currently enrolled in and have satisfactory conduct, progress and attendance at the Academy. The student's travel must be at least 3 hours per day from the student's permanent home address to reach the Academy using school or public transport service. A new bursary application must be completed each year. The bursary covers travel and accommodation costs for the duration of enrolment at the Academy.
eligibility_criteria: Student must be currently enrolled at the Academy; Must have satisfactory conduct, progress and attendance; Must not be eligible for the Living Away From Home Allowance Scheme (LAFHAS); Student's nearest Queensland Government state school must be located within Outer Regional Australia, Remote Australia or Very Remote Australia; Travel must be at least 3 hours per day from permanent home address to the Academy using school or public transport
special_conditions: A new bursary application must be completed each year on the approved form. Applications must be submitted via email to the Scholarships and Grants Team at scholarships@qed.qld.gov.au. Bursary covers travel and accommodation allowance for students living away from home. No financial means test is applied.

program_name: Queensland Academies Merit Scholarships for Year 10 Entry
overview: Queensland Academy for Health Sciences offers merit-based scholarships to highly capable students entering Year 10. These scholarships are awarded through the selective entry application process and recognise outstanding academic achievement and aptitude of students who demonstrate excellence across the entrance examination and interview components.
description: Queensland Academy for Health Sciences offers merit-based scholarships for students entering Year 10. Scholarships are valid for three years and cover 100% of the annual International Baccalaureate fee as well as the student resource scheme, providing the student upholds the terms of the scholarship. Eligibility students are identified through the selective entry application process, which includes academic reports, an Edutest entrance examination, and an interview conducted by the Queensland Academies Admissions Office. There is no separate application process for scholarships; eligible students and their parents/carers are contacted by the relevant Principal should opportunities arise. Scholarship recipients must maintain satisfactory conduct, progress and attendance throughout their three-year enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Student entering Year 10; Must be successful in the selective entry application process; Must demonstrate strong performance in entrance examination; Must demonstrate strong performance in interview; Must demonstrate strong academic reports; Must be assessed as suitable candidate for placement at the Academy
special_conditions: Scholarships are valid for three years. Recipients must support the college community and maintain satisfactory conduct, progress and attendance. There is no separate application process for scholarships; eligible and suitable students are identified and contacted by the Principal during the selective entry process. Scholarship is conditional upon recipient remaining at school for the duration of their enrolment and upholds the terms of the scholarship.

overview: The QAIS Bursary provides financial support to eligible rural and remote students attending Queensland Academy for Creative Industries. The bursary assists students living away from home who are not eligible for the Living Away From Home Allowance Scheme (LAFHAS) to meet travel and accommodation costs.
description: The Queensland Academies Isolated Students (QAIS) Bursary is a form of financial support specifically designed for highly capable students from regional and remote areas in Queensland who are attending QACI but living away from home. The bursary provides a travel and student accommodation allowance for eligible students. Neither payment attracts GST and there is no financial means test. Applications must be completed each year on the approved form after reviewing the QAIS information guide. The bursary is available for students meeting all eligibility criteria, including satisfactory conduct, progress and attendance at the Academy.
eligibility_criteria: Not eligible to receive assistance through the Living Away From Home Allowance Scheme (LAFHAS); Home address of student must be in Queensland; The nearest Queensland Government school with appropriate year level is located in a rural or remote education zone; Student attending is at least 3 hours travelling time per day from home address using school or public transport; Student must have satisfactory conduct, progress and attendance at the Academy; Must be currently enrolled at QACI
special_conditions: A new bursary application must be completed each year on the approved form. Scholarship subject to annual review. Student must maintain satisfactory conduct, progress and attendance. Applications must be submitted via email to scholarships@qed.qld.gov.au or by post as detailed on the application form.

overview: The QACI Brilliant Futures Program is a two-year preparation enrichment initiative for highly capable students in Years 7 and 8. This selective-entry program aims to prepare students for future academic challenges and ensure a smooth transition to Queensland Academies Creative Industries for Year 10.
description: The QACI Brilliant Futures Brisbane Program is a dynamic two-year preparation enrichment initiative designed for highly capable students currently in Year 6 seeking entry for Year 7 and Year 8 participation. The program provides impactful, collaborative learning extra-curricular experiences aligned with the International Baccalaureate (IB) learner profile attributes, including inquiry, critical thinking, and reflection. The program requires active engagement throughout the two-year duration, with participation in at least one learning experience each term, preferably both. The program is designed to complement students' existing school and extracurricular activities, with sessions occurring on weekdays after school, weekends (Saturday mornings, afternoons, or full days), and may be held both on the QACI campus and at various locations with university and industry partners. Additionally, students can participate in Industry Experience Workshops within creative subject offerings including Dance, Theatre, Film, Music, Design, and Visual Arts, which may incur additional costs.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6 in 2026; Highly capable students; Must demonstrate strong performance in entrance test components; Must submit semester report cards and NAPLAN reports as they become available; Current academic performance must be maintained consistently across reporting periods; Required to attend on-campus workshops and collaborate with peers; Must be Australian citizen or have appropriate visa status
special_conditions: Active engagement throughout the two-year QACI Brilliant Futures Program is essential for success. Participants must attend on-campus workshops in addition to online activities. Membership may be terminated if a student accepts enrolment into a Queensland Academy for Year 7 or 8. Students who were unsuccessful in gaining a place can re-apply in Year 9 as per general Queensland Academies admission process. Industry Experience Workshops may incur additional costs.

school_name: Queensland Academy for Science Mathematics and Technology
overview: The QAIS Bursary provides financial support to families of highly capable students from rural and remote areas of Queensland who are living away from home to attend QASMT. The bursary assists eligible students with travel and accommodation allowances and is available to those not eligible for the Living Away From Home Allowance Scheme (LAFHAS).
description: The Queensland Academies Isolated Students (QAIS) Bursary provides financial support to families of students living away from home to attend QASMT in Toowong. For those who are not eligible for the Living Away From Home Allowance Scheme (LAFHAS), the Bursary includes a travel and student accommodation allowance. Neither payment attracts GST and there is no financial means test. The bursary is renewable annually with a new application required each year. Eligible students must maintain satisfactory conduct, progress and attendance at the Academy. The financial amount varies based on individual circumstances and is detailed in the 2026 QAIS information guide provided by the Queensland Government Department of Education.
eligibility_criteria: Student must be currently enrolled at QASMT; Student must have satisfactory conduct, progress and attendance at the Academy; Student must not be eligible for Living Away From Home Allowance Scheme (LAFHAS); Home address must be in Queensland; The nearest Queensland Government school with the appropriate year level must be in a rural or remote education zone; Student must be at least three hours travelling time per day from home address using school or public transport; Student must have been nominated or identified as highly capable
special_conditions: A new bursary application must be completed each year on the approved form. The bursary is for students living away from home only. Applications are submitted via email to the Scholarships and Grants Team on scholarships@qed.qld.gov.au or by post. Eligibility requires satisfactory conduct, progress and attendance throughout the year.

school_name: Queensland Academy for Science Mathematics and Technology
overview: The QASMT STEM Scholars Program is a selective enrichment initiative that invites highly capable students from primary schools within the connected school network to participate in academically rigorous after-school workshops. The program provides access to STEM-focused workshops delivered by senior QASMT teachers with a focus on disciplined reasoning and communication skills.
description: The QASMT STEM Scholars Program invites primary schools within the connected school network to nominate high-achieving and highly capable students in Years 3–6 to participate in the initiative. Based on school recommendations aligned with specified selection criteria, individual students may join the program and attend after-school workshops. Face-to-face workshops are held on Thursday afternoons from 4:00 to 5:30 p.m. and operate on a user-pays basis, coordinated through QASMT. The workshops are academically rigorous and require students to apply disciplined reasoning and communication skills to develop deep understanding. A one-off registration fee is charged per student to join the program. New members who have participated are invited to attend the annual QASMT STEM Scholars Celebration Afternoon during National Science Week, where they receive a QASMT STEM Scholars badge and Certificate of Membership.
eligibility_criteria: Students must be in Years 3–6; Students must be enrolled in primary schools within the connected school network; Nomination by primary school principal required; Students must meet specified selection criteria based on high achievement and demonstrated capability in STEM areas
special_conditions: Enrolment is by nomination process coordinated through your local primary school principal and QASMT. Workshops operate on a user-pays basis. Nominated students should contact connections@qasmt.eq.edu.au for more information. Academy tours are available for families interested in applying to QASMT.

school_name: Queensland Academy for Science Mathematics and Technology
overview: The QASMT Young Einsteins Program is an initiative designed specifically for highly gifted and exceptional students to nurture their unique abilities. The program aims to enable identified gifted students to reach their intellectual and creative potential through enrichment activities and mentoring.
description: The QASMT Young Einsteins Program is an initiative designed to support the needs of highly gifted and exceptional students. It aims to further nurture the unique abilities of this identified group, enabling them to reach their intellectual and creative potential. The program is open to students in Years 3–6 who have been formally assessed with IQs of 140 or above. The QASMT Young Einsteins group meets after school at QASMT at specified times throughout the year. Students participate in presentations from senior QASMT students who have prepared research for national and international science networks, develop a critical stance in their work while learning from others' perspectives, and showcase and communicate their projects as part of a student-led exhibition for students, parents, invited guests, and members of the QASMT community. Students are invited to attend the annual QASMT Young Einsteins and their Mentors Celebration Afternoon, where they receive a QASMT Young Einstein badge and Certificate of Membership.
eligibility_criteria: Students must be in Years 3–6; Students must have been formally assessed with IQ of 140 or above; Students must demonstrate exceptional intellectual and creative abilities
special_conditions: Eligibility requires formal IQ assessment of 140 or above. If you meet the criteria and wish to confirm your eligibility for the Young Einsteins Program, contact the Queensland Academies office at connections@qasmt.eq.edu.au. Academy tours are available for families interested in applying to QASMT.

school_name: Queensland Pathways State College - Goodna Campus
Queensland Pathways State College - Moreton Bay Campus
Queensland Pathways State College - Coorparoo Campus
Queensland Pathways State College - Bundamba Campus

Queensland Pathways State College - Townsville Campus

overview: Sacred Heart Catholic Primary School supports families experiencing genuine financial difficulties by offering fee reductions. The school recognises that financial circumstances vary and commits to working with families to ensure no child is excluded due to inability to pay school fees in full or in part.
description: Families experiencing genuine financial difficulties may apply for a reduction in school fees payable. Any reduction given will be determined on a case-by-case basis, and in keeping with the family's current financial circumstances. Concessions given are for the current year only and will be negotiated annually with the school principal. Children will not be excluded from Sacred Heart Yeppoon if genuine financial hardship prevents the payment of school fees in total or in part. The school works with families to make arrangements that suit their circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing genuine financial hardship; Demonstrated financial difficulties preventing full or partial payment of school fees
special_conditions: Concessions given are for the current year only and will be negotiated annually with the school principal. Families must contact the school principal to discuss their circumstances confidentially.

overview: Sacred Heart Catholic Primary School provides automatic fee support to families holding eligible Australian government health care or pensioner concession cards. This initiative assists families who genuinely lack the financial capacity to pay tuition fees in full.
description: Parents and carers with an eligible means-tested Australian government health care or pensioner concession card will automatically receive a 70% discount on tuition fees (this discount does not apply to school levies). This initiative is intended to assist those families who genuinely lack the financial capacity to pay tuition fees in full. The discount is applied automatically when families present their valid concession card to the school. No application or annual negotiation is required for eligible families.
eligibility_criteria: Holder of eligible Australian government health care concession card; Holder of eligible Australian government pensioner concession card; Discount applies to tuition fees only, not school levies
special_conditions: 70% discount applies to tuition fees only and does not apply to school levies. Discount is automatically applied upon presentation of eligible means-tested concession card. This is a diocesan Catholic Education initiative.

overview: San Damiano College provides financial assistance to families requiring support. The school offers fee remissions, family discounts, and bursaries through Brisbane Catholic Education to ensure that no child is refused enrolment due to genuine inability to pay fees.
description: San Damiano College, as part of Brisbane Catholic Education, is committed to making Catholic education accessible to all families. Financial aid is available from all BCE schools, including San Damiano College. Fee remissions, family discounts, bursaries and other options are available for families in need. The college ensures that no child will be refused enrolment because of a family's genuine inability to pay fees. The specific financial benefit amount varies and must be discussed individually with the school. Selection is based on demonstrated financial need. The college works with families to establish appropriate support arrangements on a case-by-case basis. Additional support is extended to students at the margins of society and those in out of home care.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all families; Demonstrated financial need; Brisbane Catholic Education (BCE) families priority; Students at the Margins of Society; Students in out of home care
special_conditions: No child will be refused enrolment because of a family's genuine inability to pay fees. A $50 non-refundable confirmation fee is required to confirm an offer of enrolment. Families should contact the school directly to discuss financial assistance options.

overview: Samford Valley Steiner School provides a Community Bursary Fund to offer financial assistance to families facing financial difficulties. This needs-based support ensures students have access to quality Steiner education regardless of their financial background.
description: The Community Bursary Fund is a crucial initiative that provides financial assistance to families who need it most. The school recognises that families may sometimes face short-term financial challenges. This bursary program is designed to ensure that all students, regardless of their financial background, have access to the quality education and enriching experiences that Samford Valley Steiner School offers. The fund operates as a community-supported initiative, with donations from school stakeholders contributing to help students thrive and reach their full potential. Families experiencing difficulty in meeting payment deadlines should contact the school to discuss special arrangements.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship or difficulty in meeting payment deadlines; Open to all enrolled and prospective students; Assessment based on demonstrated financial need
special_conditions: Families must pro-actively communicate in advance about financial difficulties. Formal arrangements must be made in writing with the school. Contact the Economic Development Manager to make special arrangements for settlement of fees.

overview: Samford Valley Steiner School operates a Financial Contribution Relief Program to support families experiencing temporary financial difficulties. The program provides assistance with school fee payments to ensure access to education.
description: The school's Financial Contribution Relief Program is designed to provide support to families experiencing short-term financial challenges. This program acknowledges that not all families are in the same financial position and aims to ensure that temporary financial difficulties do not prevent students from accessing education at Samford Valley Steiner School. The program is referenced in the school handbook and works in conjunction with the Community Bursary Fund. Families experiencing difficulty in meeting payment deadlines are encouraged to contact the school's Economic Development Manager to make special arrangements. Specific details of amounts and duration are determined on a case-by-case basis.
eligibility_criteria: Experiencing temporary financial difficulty; Currently enrolled families; Willingness to communicate with school in advance of payment issues; Open to families from all year levels
special_conditions: Onus of pro-active communication lies with parents/caregivers. Special arrangements must be mutually agreed in writing in advance. Families must contact the Economic Development Manager to arrange relief. Without prior arrangement, accounts outstanding after due date will be considered overdue and action taken according to the Fee policy.

overview: Samford Valley Steiner School offers a sibling discount to families with multiple children enrolled in the school. This discount recognizes the commitment of families with more than one student and provides fee reduction based on tuition component.
description: The school provides a sibling discount applicable to families with more than one child at Samford Valley Steiner School in Classes Prep to Year 12. The discount applies to the tuition component only and does not extend to other compulsory charges or levies. This discount is designed to support families with multiple children attending the school and provide some financial relief to make education more accessible. The discount is calculated on the tuition fees and is reflected in the annual Parent Contribution Schedule. Note that sibling discount does not apply at Pre-Prep levels as separate Kindergarten discounts are applicable in those year groups.
eligibility_criteria: More than one child enrolled at Samford Valley Steiner School; Both children must be in Classes Prep to Year 12; Discount applies to tuition component only; Pre-Prep siblings are not eligible as separate Kindergarten discounts apply
special_conditions: Discount applies to tuition component only; Pre-Prep students are not included; Compulsory charges and levies are not discounted; Families must have multiple children enrolled concurrently at the school.

overview: Samford Valley Steiner School offers an early payment discount for families who pay their tuition contributions in full in advance. This incentive program rewards families who meet early payment deadlines.
description: The school provides a five percent (5%) discount on tuition contributions only when families pay all contributions, including both tuition and compulsory charges, in full in advance by the applicable Early Payment due date. This discount is designed to encourage families to pay fees early and provide some financial relief for those able to do so. The discount applies to the tuition component exclusively and does not extend to compulsory charges, technology fees, or other levies. The early payment due dates are specified in the annual Parent Contribution Schedule provided to families. Families should refer to their current year schedule for specific payment deadlines to qualify for this discount.
eligibility_criteria: All tuition and compulsory charges paid in full in advance; Payment must be made by the applicable Early Payment due date; Discount applies to tuition component only
special_conditions: Discount applies to tuition contributions only; Compulsory charges and levies are not discounted; Full payment of all charges required in advance; Specific early payment due dates apply and are outlined in the annual Parent Contribution Schedule.

program_name: Community Bank Sarina Senior Schooling Scholarships
overview: Community Bank Sarina Bendigo Bank offers annual senior schooling scholarships to Sarina State High School students entering Year 11. The scholarships support recipients with their studies and personal development throughout their senior schooling years.
description: The Community Bank Sarina Senior Schooling Scholarships are awarded annually to deserving Year 11 students at Sarina State High School. Each scholarship provides $2,000 in financial support. The program has been partnered with Sarina State High School since 2017. Recipients are selected based on their demonstrated commitment to their studies and personal development. The scholarship funds are intended to support educational expenses such as subject-specific costs, educational trips, and course fees (e.g., Certificate III programs). The selection process involves a written application followed by feedback to applicants. Scholarships are reviewed annually with recipients able to reapply for subsequent years.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 11 at Sarina State High School; Currently enrolled at the school; Demonstrated commitment to studies and personal development
special_conditions: Recipients must demonstrate commitment to their studies and personal development. Partnership between Community Bank Sarina Bendigo Bank and Sarina State High School has been active since 2017. Applicants receive detailed feedback regardless of selection outcome to help guide future applications.

overview: Sarina State High School offers entry year scholarships to Year 7 students commencing their secondary education. The scholarship provides financial support for educational expenses in the first year of attendance.
description: The Entry Year Scholarship program at Sarina State High School provides Year 7 students entering the school with $200 towards any educational expense incurred during their first year of attendance. This includes costs such as uniforms, textbooks, school fees, excursions, and other school-related expenses. The scholarship is awarded to selected Year 7 students and is intended to support the transition to secondary education. Recipients are advertised in school publications and the local community. The selection process details are not fully specified on the school website but applications are received during the enrolment period.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Enrolled as a new student at Sarina State High School; Completion of enrolment form and interview process
special_conditions: Recipients are advertised in Sarina State High School publications and in the local community. Financial support is specifically for educational expenses incurred during the first year of attendance.

overview: The Excelsior Academic Program is an innovative program for academically talented students in Years 7-10. The program allows students to tailor their learning experiences in an environment specifically targeted towards enrichment and extension across English, Mathematics, Science and Humanities.
description: The Excelsior Academic Program is an extension program offered by Sandgate District State High School for students in Years 7 to 10 who demonstrate exceptional academic ability. The program allows students to tailor their learning experiences in an environment specifically targeted towards enrichment (onwards) and extension (upwards). Excelsior program classes are offered for English, Mathematics, Science and Humanities. In Excelsior English, the mandatory curriculum is compressed to provide opportunities for enrichment portfolios, allowing students to pursue relevant interests and further develop their strengths. Excelsior Mathematics students study at year level while developing problem-solving and mathematical modelling skills through term-long tasks. Excelsior Science provides enriched critical thinking and creative learning opportunities. Excelsior Humanities enables deeper engagement with History, Geography, Civics and Citizenship, Economics and Business through active citizenship projects. Admission requires completion of an entrance exam with payment; bookings are available after Expression of Interest submission.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or current students in Years 8, 9, 10; Students must demonstrate exceptional academic ability; Entrance exam required; Applications required each year during subject selection process; Students must maintain level of achievement, effort, behaviour, collaboration and creativity to remain in program each year
special_conditions: Applications for membership are required each year during the subject selection process. Students will be required to maintain a level of achievement, effort, behaviour, collaboration and creativity to remain in each Excelsior program class each year. Bookings and payment required prior to testing.

overview: The Athlete Development Program (ADP) is a specialist sports program providing young athletes with coaching and experiences in all facets of skill acquisition as well as strength and conditioning. The program aims to progress sporting capability in any context through high quality coaching and fitness development.
description: The Athlete Development Program at Sandgate District State High School is aimed at providing young athletes with coaching and experiences in all facets of skill acquisition as well as strength and conditioning, to progress their sporting capability in any context. In the Athlete Development Program, athletes learn from high quality coaches, take part in running training and analysis sessions, and have access to physiotherapy and mobility training. Students must be currently registered with a sporting club in their chosen sport, demonstrate ability to follow behavioural expectations, and provide one reference from their current sporting coach. Prospective students must attend a compulsory Athlete Development Testing Day consisting of a range of Physical Fitness tests that determine suitability for the program. To apply, students must complete an Expression of Interest and then receive an application form for ADP. Students in Years 7-9 study the program all year instead of Physical Education.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or current students in Year 8-12; Must be currently registered with a sporting club in their chosen sport; Demonstrated ability to follow the behavioural expectations of the school; Demonstrate an enthusiasm and desire to be part of the program; A desire to further advance their techniques, skills and personal fitness; Provide one reference from their current sporting coach; Must participate in compulsory physical testing day
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: In accordance with the School's mission statement and core values, several requirements must be met by the applicant to be admitted into the Athlete Development Program. Compulsory Athlete Development Testing Day must be attended. ADP students study the program all year instead of Physical Education in Years 7-9 and can choose Health and Physical Education or ADP in Year 10.

overview: The Music Excellence Program (MEP) is an extension program with a focus on performance for students in Years 7-12 who demonstrate exceptional instrumental or vocal talent. The program engages students in performance through a variety of ensemble groups, solo and chamber performances in a wide range of musical styles.
description: The Music Excellence Program (MEP) is an extension program at Sandgate District State High School with a focus on performance for students in Years 7 to 12 who demonstrate exceptional music talent, both instrumental and/or vocal. The program focuses on engaging students in performance through a variety of ensemble groups, solo and chamber performances in a wide range of musical styles that suit their music style. The program is staffed by specialists in performance who have specific skills for their ensembles. Students are extended through personalised learning experiences and develop musicianship through workshops and a varied performance program. Students develop music theory, composition, improvisation, and performance skills through weekly tutorials provided by school staff. Students have the opportunity to interact with like-minded students across the school and work with senior mentors as they continue their music development. To apply, students must complete an application form and attend an audition. Audition requirements include 2 contrasting pieces of music from a method book or as prepared for an exam (not concert band or orchestra pieces).
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Year 7 to Year 12; Must demonstrate exceptional music talent, instrumental and/or vocal; Must complete application form and attend audition; Audition requirement: 2 contrasting pieces of music from a method book or as prepared for an exam (not concert band or orchestra pieces)
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Students wishing to be considered for the Music Excellence Program must complete an application form and attend an audition. Audition requirements: 2 contrasting pieces of music from a method book or as prepared for an exam (not concert band or orchestra pieces).

overview: The Dance Excellence Program (DEP) is an extension program with a focus on performance for students in Years 7-12 who demonstrate exceptional talent in dance. The program provides specialised training and performance opportunities for talented dancers.
description: The Dance Excellence Program is an extension program at Sandgate District State High School with a focus on performance for students in Years 7 to 12 who demonstrate exceptional talent in Dance. This program is designed for students who have demonstrated high levels of dance ability and commitment to dance training. To enrol in the Dance Excellence Program, students must complete an application form and participate in a group audition. Auditions for Year 7 2026 enrolments are held at the SDSHS Dance Studios (Centre for Creative Industries). Auditions for Year 8-12 2026 enrolments are also held at the same location. Students should attend the audition in tights. The program operates as an extracurricular program during sport time and outside school hours. Program applicants must first complete an Expression of Interest to Enrol (EOI) and then receive an invitation to apply for the Dance Excellence Program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Year 7 to Year 12; Must demonstrate exceptional talent in dance; Must complete application form; Must participate in group audition at SDSHS Dance Studios (Centre for Creative Industries); Students should attend audition in tights
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Dance Excellence Program operates as an extracurricular program during sport time and outside school hours. Group auditions are held at the SDSHS Dance Studios (Centre for Creative Industries). Students should attend the audition in tights. Applicants must complete an Expression of Interest to Enrol (EOI) first, then receive an invitation to apply for the program.

overview: Runcorn State High School offers Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding students who have succeeded academically. The scholarship recognises high-achieving students who demonstrate excellence in their academic performance and serve as good role models to their peers.
description: The Academic Scholarship is valued at between $450 and $950 and may cover some or all of school fees. Scholarships are available to students enrolling in Year 7, 8, 9 or 10 in 2024. Selection is based on responses to an online scholarship application and submission of supporting documentation demonstrating academic achievement. Scholarship winners receive a certificate at a Scholarship Ceremony held at the school during the start of term 1 each year.
eligibility_criteria: Applicants must be enrolling in Year 7, 8, 9 or 10 in 2024; Must demonstrate outstanding academic achievement; Must submit an online scholarship application with supporting documentation; Good role models to peers
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered on a one-year basis. Scholarship winners are presented with a certificate at a Scholarship Ceremony held at the school during the start of term 1 each year. Applicants must be enrolling in the specific year mentioned; scholarships are not available to students in any other year level at this stage.

overview: Runcorn State High School offers Sport Scholarships to recognise outstanding students who are active participants in sporting activities. The scholarship rewards students who demonstrate exceptional sporting ability and commitment to their sport.
description: The Sport Scholarship is valued at between $450 and $950 and may cover some or all of school fees. Scholarships are available to students enrolling in Year 7, 8, 9 or 10 in 2024. Selection is based on responses to an online scholarship application and submission of supporting documentation demonstrating sporting excellence and achievement. Scholarship winners receive a certificate at a Scholarship Ceremony held at the school during the start of term 1 each year.
eligibility_criteria: Applicants must be enrolling in Year 7, 8, 9 or 10 in 2024; Must be active participants in community and sporting activities; Must submit an online scholarship application with supporting documentation; Good role models to peers
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered on a one-year basis. Scholarship winners are presented with a certificate at a Scholarship Ceremony held at the school during the start of term 1 each year. Applicants must be enrolling in the specific year mentioned; scholarships are not available to students in any other year level at this stage.

overview: Runcorn State High School offers Creative Industries Scholarships to reward outstanding students who are active participants in cultural and creative activities. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate excellence in the performing and creative arts.
description: The Creative Industries Scholarship is valued at between $450 and $950 and may cover some or all of school fees. Scholarships are available to students enrolling in Year 7, 8, 9 or 10 in 2024. Selection is based on responses to an online scholarship application and submission of supporting documentation demonstrating achievement in creative industries and cultural activities. Scholarship winners receive a certificate at a Scholarship Ceremony held at the school during the start of term 1 each year.
eligibility_criteria: Applicants must be enrolling in Year 7, 8, 9 or 10 in 2024; Must be active participants in cultural and creative activities; Must submit an online scholarship application with supporting documentation; Good role models to peers
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered on a one-year basis. Scholarship winners are presented with a certificate at a Scholarship Ceremony held at the school during the start of term 1 each year. Applicants must be enrolling in the specific year mentioned; scholarships are not available to students in any other year level at this stage.

overview: Runcorn State High School offers Community and Leadership Scholarships to recognise outstanding students who demonstrate leadership qualities and active participation in community service. The scholarship rewards students who are good role models and contribute positively to their community.
description: The Community and Leadership Scholarship is valued at between $450 and $950 and may cover some or all of school fees. Scholarships are available to students enrolling in Year 7, 8, 9 or 10 in 2024. Selection is based on responses to an online scholarship application and submission of supporting documentation demonstrating leadership positions held at school, clubs, bands, community organisations or groups. Scholarship winners receive a certificate at a Scholarship Ceremony held at the school during the start of term 1 each year.
eligibility_criteria: Applicants must be enrolling in Year 7, 8, 9 or 10 in 2024; Must demonstrate leadership positions at school, clubs, bands, community organisations or groups; Must be good role models to peers; Must submit an online scholarship application with supporting documentation
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered on a one-year basis. Scholarship winners are presented with a certificate at a Scholarship Ceremony held at the school during the start of term 1 each year. Applicants must be enrolling in the specific year mentioned; scholarships are not available to students in any other year level at this stage.

overview: Shalom College offers the Support Scholarship Program through the Parents and Friends Association to provide financial assistance for students experiencing hardship who wish to participate in college immersion activities and educational trips. The scholarship recognises the personal and spiritual benefits of service learning and community engagement.
description: The Shalom College Support Scholarship Program is designed for families experiencing financial hardship who seek assistance to enable their child's participation in college-organised immersion activities and educational trips. The scholarship provides up to 50% of the travel cost for approved trips, supporting students of good standing within the College community. Recipients are expected to share their experiences and learning upon return to contribute to the broader College community. The scholarship is administered by the Shalom Parents and Friends (P&F) Association and is based on demonstrated financial need and commitment to service learning. Applicants must complete the formal application form and be in good standing academically and behaviourally. The program specifically recognises the pastoral ethos of the school, emphasising caring for those in need and supporting personal and spiritual growth.
eligibility_criteria: Student must be enrolled at Shalom College; Must be in good standing within the College; Family experiencing financial hardship; Student must have indicated commitment to participate in the specific immersion activity or educational trip; Good academic and behavioural standing required
special_conditions: Successful applicants must support the college pastoral ethos and community values. Recipients are expected to share their experiences within the College community upon return from the trip or immersion activity. Scholarship subject to annual review based on demonstrated need and impact.

overview: Shalom College provides a fee discount for families holding eligible Concession Cards, such as Care or Pensioner Concession cards. This assistance program helps reduce the cost of schooling for eligible families meeting government-defined financial criteria.
description: Shalom College offers a discount on school fees for families who hold eligible Care or Pensioner Concession Cards. The concession applies to the All-Inclusive Education fee structure and must meet specific eligibility criteria as defined by government regulations. Eligible families should provide evidence of their concession card status when enrolling or during the fee statement process. The discount is automatically applied to fees where eligibility is confirmed. Families requiring assistance navigating the fee structure or discussing their eligibility should contact the School Finance Office directly. All enquiries are handled with confidentiality to support families in maximising available support.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold a valid Care or Pensioner Concession Card; Card must meet eligibility criteria specified by the school; Family must be enrolled at Shalom College; Eligibility must be verified and documented
special_conditions: Concession card must be current and meet school eligibility criteria. Discount applies to All-Inclusive Education fee structure. Families must provide evidence of concession card status. Discount subject to annual verification of eligibility.

overview: Shalom College offers a discretionary fee concession program administered by the Principal for families experiencing genuine financial hardship. The Principal will consider applications from families providing appropriate supporting evidence of financial difficulty.
description: Shalom College recognises that some families may experience unexpected or genuine financial hardship that impacts their ability to pay full school fees. The Principal considers applications for school fee concession on a case-by-case basis from families experiencing demonstrable financial difficulty. Families must provide appropriate supporting evidence of their financial circumstances to be considered for this discretionary assistance. Applications are made directly to the Principal to ensure confidentiality is maintained throughout the assessment process. The school is committed to ensuring financial barriers do not prevent deserving students from accessing Catholic education at Shalom. Families experiencing hardship are encouraged to contact the Finance Office early to discuss available support options and begin the application process.
eligibility_criteria: Family experiencing genuine financial hardship; Must provide appropriate supporting documentation of financial difficulty; Student must be enrolled or seeking enrolment at Shalom College; Application made directly to Principal; Confidentiality maintained throughout process
special_conditions: Application is discretionary and considered on individual merit and circumstances. Confidentiality is maintained throughout the application process. Supporting evidence of financial hardship required. All applications assessed by the Principal directly. Concession may be varied or reviewed annually.

overview: Shalom College offers flexible payment plan arrangements for families wishing to pay school fees by instalments rather than in full. Payment plans can accommodate weekly, fortnightly, or monthly instalment schedules to suit family circumstances.
description: Shalom College recognises that not all families are able to pay school fees in full by the due date. The College offers flexible payment plan arrangements that allow fees to be paid through weekly, fortnightly, or monthly instalments according to family circumstances. Families wishing to establish a payment plan must contact the School Finance Office prior to the fee statement due date to make suitable arrangements. Payment plan arrangements are available for all families and do not require demonstrated financial hardship, though those experiencing difficulty are encouraged to discuss options with the Finance Office. Families with approved payment plan arrangements who miss a payment should contact the Finance Office immediately to make alternative arrangements. The school requests that families requiring any payment assistance contact the Finance Office to discuss available options.
eligibility_criteria: Must be enrolled or seeking enrolment at Shalom College; Family must contact Finance Office to establish arrangement; Contact must be made before fee statement due date; Must maintain regular payment schedule
special_conditions: Payment plan must be established by contacting Finance Office before fee due date. Weekly, fortnightly, or monthly instalments available. Families missing payments must contact Finance Office immediately. All payments must be made according to agreed schedule. Prompt payment discount of 2.5% available if fees paid in full within 30 days.

overview: Silkwood School offers the Young Entrepreneur Scholarship to support students who demonstrate entrepreneurial vision and ambition. The scholarship recognises students with a clear vision for their future and a commitment to developing their own business or enterprise ideas while studying at Silkwood.
description: The Silkwood Young Entrepreneur Scholarship provides recipients with full tuition fee remission for up to 7 years of schooling at Silkwood School. The scholarship is designed to help students turn their entrepreneurial vision into reality through The Silkwood Way's personalised learning approach, which emphasises real-world learning, mentorship, and business development. Upon acceptance of the scholarship, recipients must pay a $100 enrolment fee to secure placement, and all other fees (enrolment bond, capital levy, camps, excursions, materials, and extracurricular activities) remain the responsibility of parents or guardians. The scholarship is subject to renewal conditions and cannot be recommenced if a recipient leaves and re-enrolls at the school for any reason. Silkwood School reserves the right to recover financial assistance if a student withdraws for reasons other than graduation or change of location.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students seeking placement at Silkwood School; Demonstrated entrepreneurial vision and business ideas; Must accept the Silkwood Enrolment Commitment; Must support the rules and learning programs of the School; Conduct requirements as per Silkwood Enrolment Contract
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients must pay a $100 enrolment fee upon acceptance to secure placement. All fees outside of tuition (Family Enrolment Bond, capital levy, camps, excursions, materials, and extracurricular activities) are payable by parents or guardians. The scholarship cannot be recommenced if the recipient leaves and then re-enrolls at the school for any reason. Silkwood School reserves the right to recover any financial assistance provided if a student leaves for reasons other than graduating or change of location. Recipients must accept the Silkwood Enrolment Commitment and support the rules and learning programs of the School.

overview: Silkwood School offers the Young Entrepreneur Scholarship to support students with a passion for entrepreneurship and innovation. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate entrepreneurial thinking, a vision for their future, and the commitment to develop business ideas. It provides recipients with up to 7 years of free tuition at Silkwood School.
description: The Silkwood Young Entrepreneur Scholarship provides successful applicants with free tuition for their years of schooling at Silkwood School, valued at up to 7 years of tuition remission. This prestigious scholarship is designed to support students who have a business, creative or innovative idea that they are passionate about developing. Recipients gain access to a real-world learning network of expert mentors from various fields who work with young people to develop their entrepreneurial thinking and help turn their vision into reality. An enrolment fee of $100 is payable upon acceptance of the scholarship. The scholarship is awarded to new and current students seeking placement from Term 1 onwards. Additional fees outside of tuition (Family Enrolment Bond, capital levy, camps, excursions, materials, and extracurricular activities) remain the responsibility of parents or guardians.
eligibility_criteria: Students with a business, creative or innovative idea that they are passionate about developing; Must have a vision for their future; Open to both new and current students; Students must be seeking placement at Silkwood School; From Term 1 2020 onwards, open to both new and current students
special_conditions: Free to apply for the Young Entrepreneur Scholarship. $100 Enrolment Fee is payable upon acceptance of the Scholarship in order to secure placement. Any fees outside of tuition are payable by the parent(s) or guardian(s) as per normal conditions of enrolment, i.e., the Family Enrolment Bond, the capital levy, camps, excursions, materials, and extracurricular activities. Conditions of the Silkwood Enrolment Contract apply where not in reference to tuition fees. The parent(s) or guardian(s) and students undertake to accept the Silkwood Enrolment Commitment and support the rules and learning programs of the School. Silkwood School reserves the right to recover any financial assistance provided for a student if the recipient leaves Silkwood for reasons other than graduating or change of location. The Young Entrepreneur Scholarship can not be recommenced if the recipient leaves and then re-enrols in the school for any reason.

overview: Sheldon College offers Academic Scholarships to encourage and support students who demonstrate excellence in academic achievement. These scholarships recognise outstanding academic ability and commitment to learning, and are awarded to students entering Years 7 to 12.
description: Sheldon College operates an academic scholarship program for students entering Years 7 to 12 to reward those who demonstrate high academic achievement and excellence. Scholarship recipients are selected through competitive assessment and examination processes. The exact financial value and duration of academic scholarships are not specified in publicly available materials, though recipients must maintain academic standards and adherence to College policies. Applicants are evaluated based on their demonstrated academic performance and potential to contribute to the College community. Recipients have a responsibility to engage deeply with their learning, contribute to all areas of College life, and uphold the College philosophy of Love, Laughter and Learning.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Years 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, or 12; Must demonstrate high academic achievement; Must maintain commitment to academic studies; Adherence to College policies and Code of Conduct expected
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients have a responsibility to the Sheldon Community to engage deeply with their learning, to contribute to all areas of College life and to consistently uphold the College philosophy of Love, Laughter and Learning.

overview: Sheldon College operates a limited sporting scholarship program to reward and retain current students and to attract highly talented sportspeople to the College. These scholarships recognise excellence in sporting pursuits and are available to students entering Year 7 and above.
description: Sheldon College operates a limited sporting scholarship program designed to attract and retain highly talented athletes who demonstrate excellence in their chosen sport. The program is open to both prospective external students and current students seeking additional support. Scholarships are offered to students entering Year 7 and above to reward sporting excellence and develop future champions. The exact financial benefits and values are determined on an individual basis. Prospective external scholarship applicants seeking Year 7 entry should make their initial enquiry before the end of Term 2 of Year 5, while those seeking entry in Year 8 or above should contact the College as soon as possible. Current students should make enquiries no later than the end of Term 3 for consideration of scholarships starting the following year.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Year 7 and above; Must demonstrate excellence in sport at high level; External applicants seeking Year 7 entry should enquire before end of Term 2, Year 5; For Year 8+ entry enquiries should be made at earliest convenience; Current students must enquire by end of Term 3 for following year
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Limited number of scholarships available. Scholarship recipients must maintain their focus on sporting development and participate fully in College sporting programs. Demonstrated commitment to academic studies and adherence to College policies and Code of Conduct are also expected.

program_name: Music Scholarship - Trombone and Lower Brass
overview: Sheldon College currently offers Music Scholarships particularly in the area of trombone and lower brass instruments. These scholarships recognise outstanding musical ability and commitment to the College's music program, with candidates required to maintain focus on music development and participate fully in ensemble activities.
description: Sheldon College is currently offering Music Scholarships with particular emphasis on trombone and lower brass instruments as part of its comprehensive Performance Music Program. Candidates applying for a Music Scholarship must have attained at least a minimum Level/Grade 4 AMEB/Trinity or equivalent on their chosen instrument, and must also demonstrate a high level of academic achievement. Scholarship recipients are expected to maintain their focus on music development for the duration of the scholarship and participate fully in the College's music program, which features over 35 ensembles including Symphony Orchestra, Concert Bands, Jazz Bands, and various Chamber Groups. The exact financial value of scholarships is not specified in publicly available materials but varies based on individual circumstances. Recipients must display demonstrated commitment to their academic studies and adherence to College policies and Code of Conduct.
eligibility_criteria: Minimum Grade 4 AMEB/Trinity equivalent on chosen instrument; Must demonstrate high level of academic achievement; Must maintain commitment to music development and College music program participation; Adherence to College policies and Code of Conduct expected; Open to students entering various year levels
special_conditions: Candidates must maintain focus on music development for duration of scholarship. Participation in College's music program is mandatory. Demonstrated commitment to academic studies and adherence to College policies and Code of Conduct are essential. Applicants should contact the music department for further information and application details.

overview: Sheldon College values excellence in the arts and offers scholarships to support talented students in performing arts, visual arts, drama, dance, and film and television. These scholarships recognise outstanding artistic ability and commitment to the College's comprehensive arts program.
description: Sheldon College offers Arts Scholarships through its Australian School of The Arts (ASTA) program, which serves over 1000 students across multiple arts disciplines. The ASTA program includes the ASTA Academy and an audition-based 'Professionals' Arts Excellence program. Scholarships are available in performing arts including music, drama, dance, musical theatre, visual arts including fashion and costume design, and film and television. Recipients must demonstrate exceptional talent in their chosen arts discipline and maintain high academic standards. The College's arts program features extensive on-site training, industry partnerships, and performance opportunities throughout the year including the Sheldon College Arts Festival and other major events. Financial values vary based on individual circumstances and are determined through the application process.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated excellence in chosen arts discipline; Must meet audition or portfolio requirements; Must maintain high academic achievement; Adherence to College policies and Code of Conduct expected; Open to students in Preparatory to Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients have a responsibility to the Sheldon Community to engage deeply with their learning, contribute to all areas of College life and consistently uphold the College philosophy of Love, Laughter and Learning. Participation in ASTA programs and performances is expected. Recipients must maintain commitment to arts development and academic excellence.

overview: Sheldon College offers Aviation Academy Scholarships as part of its partnership with the Australian Wings Academy. These scholarships support students interested in aviation, space design, and engineering studies with pathways to professional qualifications including a Private Pilot's Licence.
description: The Sheldon College Aviation and Space Academy offers scholarships to support students who wish to pursue studies in aviation, space design, and engineering. The College has partnered with the Australian Wings Academy (AWA) based at the Gold Coast Airport to provide comprehensive aviation training. Programs include Work Experience, scholarships, Aviation traineeships, and pathways into a Diploma of Aviation, which includes a Private Pilot's Licence. Students in this program have access to world-class facilities and expert instruction in aerospace and aviation fields. Scholarship values and specific financial benefits vary based on individual applications and are determined through the selection process.
eligibility_criteria: Interest in aviation, aerospace, space design, or engineering; Academic achievement required; Commitment to aviation studies expected; Participation in work experience and training programs required
special_conditions: Recipients must commit to aviation program participation and work experience requirements. Scholarships support access to aviation traineeships and diploma pathways. Recipients must maintain academic standards and College conduct expectations.

overview: Somerville House awards Academic Scholarships to students entering from Year 7 onwards based on their scholarship application, supporting documents, and performance in the ACER Academic Scholarship Test. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning.
description: Academic Scholarships are awarded to students entering from Year 7 onwards and are based on performance in the ACER Academic Scholarship Test. The school offers both full and partial scholarships to eligible students. Scholarships are tenable for the duration of the student's education at Somerville House, subject to satisfactory academic performance and contribution to school life. All applicants must complete the ACER Academic Scholarship Test. Awards are typically offered on a multi-year basis and are means-tested.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering from Year 7 onwards; Australian citizens or residents; Must demonstrate academic excellence; Must complete ACER Academic Scholarship Test; Subject to satisfactory academic performance and contribution to school life
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Partial Fee Remission
special_conditions: Scholarships are tenable for the duration of the student's education at Somerville House, subject to satisfactory academic performance and contribution to School life. Awards typically offered on a multi-year basis. Eligible students may apply for multiple scholarships, but only one can be awarded per student.

overview: Somerville House offers Boarding Scholarships for girls from regional or rural Queensland entering Years 7 or 8 as new boarders. The scholarship is based on academic achievement in the ACER Academic Scholarship Test and supports access to boarding facilities.
description: Boarding Scholarships are available for girls from regional or rural Queensland who will be commencing at Somerville House as new boarders in Years 7 or 8. Selection is based on academic achievement in the ACER Academic Scholarship Test. Students are required to sit academic assessment at the school. The scholarship provides support for boarding costs and tuition fees. Scholarships are tenable for the duration of the student's education at Somerville House, subject to satisfactory academic performance and contribution to school life.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 or 8 as new boarders; Must be from regional or rural Queensland; Australian citizens or residents; Must demonstrate academic achievement in ACER Academic Scholarship Test; Must complete school-based academic assessment
special_conditions: Applicants must be from regional or rural Queensland and be commencing as new boarders. Scholarships are tenable for the duration of the student's education at Somerville House, subject to satisfactory academic performance and contribution to school life.

overview: Somerville House offers a limited number of Music Scholarships each year for students who demonstrate outstanding musical ability. The scholarship recognises students with proficiency in specified instruments and commitment to the school's music program.
description: A limited number of Music Scholarships are available each year to students who demonstrate outstanding musical ability in specified instruments. To qualify, students must achieve a minimum A/Honours Grade in their instrument: Brass (Trumpet, French Horn, Trombone, Euphonium, Tuba) - Grade 4; Percussion - Grade 5; Piano - Grade 7; Strings (Double Bass) - Grade 5, (Viola/Cello) - Grade 6, (Violin) - Grade 7; Woodwind (Flute, Clarinet) - Grade 5, (Saxophone, Oboe, Bassoon) - Grade 4. Preference may be given to instruments in higher demand such as Bassoon, Double Bass, Euphonium, Oboe, Trombone, and Tuba. Scholarships support part of tuition fees.
eligibility_criteria: Open to secondary year students; Must demonstrate outstanding musical ability; Must meet minimum grade standard in chosen instrument as specified; Instruments in higher demand receive preference; Australian citizens or residents
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Minimum musical grade standards required as specified for each instrument. Preference may be given to instruments in higher demand within the School's ensemble program including Bassoon, Double Bass, Euphonium, Oboe, Trombone, and Tuba. Scholarships are awarded on the understanding that students continue their musical participation and meet contribution expectations.

overview: Somerville House offers a limited number of Sport Scholarships for students entering Years 7 to 11 who demonstrate outstanding ability in Cross Country, Swimming, or Track and Field. The scholarship supports tuition fees for exceptional athletes.
description: Somerville House offers a limited number of Sport Scholarships for students entering Years 7 to 11. These scholarships support part of the tuition fees for students who demonstrate outstanding ability in Cross Country, Swimming, or Track and Field, and can also participate in at least one team sport. Examples of outstanding ability include exceptional times in these sports or selection in Regional (State) or higher teams. All applicants must complete the ACER Academic Scholarship Test. Scholarships are means-tested and awarded following assessment, typically offered on a multi-year basis subject to ongoing academic progress and engagement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 to 11; Must demonstrate outstanding ability in Cross Country, Swimming, or Track and Field; Must be able to participate in at least one team sport; Australian citizens or residents; Must complete ACER Academic Scholarship Test; Examples of outstanding ability include exceptional times or selection in Regional (State) or higher teams
special_conditions: Must demonstrate outstanding ability in Cross Country, Swimming, or Track and Field with exceptional times or Regional (State) or higher team selection. Must be able to participate in at least one additional team sport. All applicants complete ACER Academic Scholarship Test. Means-tested and subject to ongoing academic progress, engagement, and positive contribution to school life.

overview: The Somerville House Foundation supports access to a Somerville House education through a range of full and partial scholarships for Australian students. These scholarships recognise academic merit and potential while providing support to families who may not otherwise access a Somerville House education.
description: The Somerville House Foundation awards a range of academic, music, sports and arts scholarships on a competitive basis. Foundation scholarships are available to both Day and Boarding students across the secondary years (Years 7 to 12). These scholarships are awarded based on merit and/or equity, which may include hardship considerations, to Australian citizens or residents. The Foundation offers both full and partial scholarships.
