Specific named Foundation scholarships include: Sidonie Thompson Memorial Scholarship (100% tuition from Year 7 for six years); Elizabeth Gilchrist AO Scholarship (50% tuition from Year 7); Dr Russell Bird Scholarship (50% tuition from Year 7); and Elizabeth Kirby Scholarships. The majority of Foundation Scholarships are multi-year awards.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Australian citizens or residents; Students in secondary years (Year 7 to 12) both day and boarding; Awards based on merit and/or equity including hardship; May be allocated to students now enrolled at the school or new students; Subject to satisfactory academic performance and contribution to school life
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or 50% Fee Reduction
special_conditions: Awards are typically offered on a multi-year basis and subject to ongoing academic progress, engagement, and a positive contribution to school life. Majority of Foundation Scholarships (Sidonie Thompson Memorial, Elizabeth Gilchrist AO, Dr Russell Bird, and Elizabeth Kirby Scholarships) are multi-year and currently allocated on a competitive basis to students now enrolled at the school. Award tenable for the student's education at the school, subject to satisfactory academic performance and contribution to school life.

overview: Somerville House Foundation provides bursaries to assist current students experiencing significant financial hardship. The Foundation plays an important role in supporting families who face financial challenges while their daughters are enrolled at the school.
description: The Somerville House Foundation provides bursaries to support current students experiencing significant financial hardship. This program is separate from the main scholarship offerings and is designed to help families who encounter unexpected or difficult financial circumstances during their daughter's time at the school. The Foundation matches donations to the Scholarships & Bursaries Program dollar-for-dollar to expand the support available. Bursary support is administered through the Foundation Office and requires application demonstrating financial need. The foundation has allocated dedicated funds to support families facing challenges, particularly following difficult economic periods.
eligibility_criteria: Current students at Somerville House; Demonstrated significant financial hardship; Australian citizens or residents; Application required demonstrating financial circumstances
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Available to current students experiencing significant financial hardship. Application process requires demonstration of financial circumstances. Foundation matches qualifying donations to the Scholarships & Bursaries Program dollar-for-dollar.

program_name: First Peoples Emerging Leaders Incentive Scheme
overview: The First Peoples Emerging Leaders Incentive Scheme is a holistic and equality-based approach to Indigenous scholarships and student development within Townsville Catholic Education secondary schools. The scheme supports First Nations students to achieve academic excellence and develop leadership skills through mentoring and subsidized activities.
description: The First Peoples Emerging Leaders Incentive Scheme is available to all First Nations students across the 12 secondary schools in the Townsville Catholic Education Diocese, including Southern Cross Catholic College. Each student completes a Personal Leadership Agreement with support from a mentor and their school's Emerging Leaders Coordinator. The scheme provides subsidies to participate in activities that support attendance, academic improvement, and engagement and application across multiple areas. Students set goals based on three pillars of the program and receive support to achieve them. The program was introduced in 2018 in partnership with the Australian Government and has seen more than 4,000 students transition successfully through the program. Support includes mentoring from volunteer teachers and school staff, as well as First Nations Homerooms and Study Clubs at some schools.
eligibility_criteria: First Nations/Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander background; Enrolled in secondary school (Years 7-12) at a participating TCE school; Must complete a Personal Leadership Agreement; Open to all secondary schools in the Townsville Catholic Education Diocese
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Requires completion of Personal Leadership Agreement with student, family, and school. Recipients must maintain engagement with the program and work toward agreed goals across the three pillars. Support includes subsidized participation in activities promoting attendance, academic improvement, and co-curricular engagement. The program operates through individual schools within the Townsville Catholic Education Diocese.

program_name: Catholic Education Deus Caritas Est Award
overview: The Catholic Education Deus Caritas Est Award is the most prestigious student award within Townsville Catholic Education and is presented during Catholic Education Week. It recognizes secondary students who demonstrate exceptional commitment to Christian living through how they interact with and treat others in their school community.
description: The Catholic Education Deus Caritas Est Award is the most prestigious student award within Townsville Catholic Education and is presented during Catholic Education Week at the Catholic Education Week Mass. Each secondary school in the Townsville Catholic Education Diocese nominates one representative who has demonstrated outstanding commitment to Christian living in their interactions with others. The award recognizes students who exemplify service, compassion, kindness, and faith-based values. Recipients are celebrated at the annual Catholic Education Week Mass. The award is non-monetary and recognizes excellence in Christian character and community service. Southern Cross Catholic College, Annandale is one of the 12 secondary schools in the Diocese eligible to nominate a student for this award annually.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled in secondary school (Year 7-12) at Southern Cross Catholic College; Demonstrate commitment to Christian living in daily interactions; Show kindness, respect, and compassion toward others; Active in school community and faith-based activities; Nominated by school; Open to all secondary students in the Townsville Catholic Education Diocese
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Recipients are nominated by their school and selected by Townsville Catholic Education. Award is presented during Catholic Education Week. One nomination per secondary school per year. Award recognizes character and service aligned with Catholic virtues and the school's mission statement.

overview: The Mary MacKillop Fund is named in honour of Australia's first saint, St Mary of the Cross MacKillop, who spent a lifetime trying to make Catholic education available to the poor. Southern Cross Catholic College participates in this bursary program to help families from all walks of life experiencing extreme hardship, trauma and financial devastation access Catholic education.
description: The Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund is a bursary program that supports families experiencing extreme hardship, trauma and financial devastation who have a deep desire for their child to receive a Catholic education. The bursary includes tuition for either primary school or secondary school, textbooks, uniforms and local excursions. Families can access support through the Catholic Foundation website or make tax-deductible donations directly through the Catholic Foundation to provide bursaries for children in the local community. The funding aims to give children a chance to fulfil their unique potential through a Mary MacKillop Bursary, recognising that education is a pathway to opportunity and dignity for disadvantaged families.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing extreme financial hardship; Demonstrated financial need; Students from Prep to Year 12; Families with genuine desire for their child to receive Catholic education; Families from all walks of life regardless of background
special_conditions: Applications are made through the Catholic Foundation. Donations are tax-deductible. The bursary covers tuition, textbooks, uniforms and local excursions. Funding is used to complement and build on existing school and community programs.

program_name: QATSIF Scholarship - Queensland Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Foundation Scholarship
overview: The QATSIF Scholarship provides Year 11 Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students with two-year scholarship funding through the Queensland Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Foundation. This scholarship aims to increase Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander student retention in Years 11 and 12, supporting students to complete their senior secondary education.
description: This scholarship program provides a Year 11 Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander student with two-year scholarship funding to support their completion of Years 11 and 12. The funding is used to complement and build on existing school and community programs and supports individual students in their educational journey. The QATSIF Scholarship recognises the importance of Indigenous student success and aims to reduce barriers to completion of senior secondary education. The scholarship funding provides financial support to help students from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander backgrounds access the full benefits of secondary education at Southern Cross Catholic College.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander background; Entering or in Year 11; Demonstrated commitment to completing Years 11 and 12; Support from school and community programs
special_conditions: Open only to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. Two-year funding commitment for Years 11 and 12. Funding complements existing school and community programs. Students must maintain engagement with the program and school.

overview: The Mons. Frawley Bursary Program is a financial assistance initiative at Southern Cross Catholic College that provides equity support to students experiencing financial hardship. Named after Monsignor Bartholomew Frawley, one of the College's founders, the program demonstrates the College's commitment to making Catholic education accessible to all students regardless of financial circumstance.
description: The Mons. Frawley Bursary Program is part of Southern Cross Catholic College's commitment to provide equity and inclusion for all students. The program offers financial assistance to families experiencing genuine hardship who wish to provide their children with a Catholic education. As a cornerstone of the College's inclusive enrolment policy, this bursary program recognises the value of education in transforming lives and building community. The program is named after Monsignor Bartholomew Frawley, one of the founding figures of Catholic education on the Redcliffe Peninsula, reflecting the College's commitment to continuing the legacy of providing accessible Catholic education for all.
eligibility_criteria: Students from Prep to Year 12; Families experiencing genuine financial hardship; Demonstrated financial need; Inclusive enrolment policy compliance; Students seeking Catholic education
special_conditions: Part of the College's inclusive enrolment policy. Financial assessment required. Application through College administration. Support is provided as part of broader equity initiatives and community support programs.

overview: The Garanyali Program is Southern Cross Catholic College's dedicated Indigenous student support program that provides comprehensive support to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. The program, named with a word meaning 'to call' in the Gubi Gubi language, aims to heighten cultural identity, maintain high expectations, promote active leadership and build strong community relationships for Indigenous students.
description: The Garanyali Program is a specialist Indigenous education support program at Southern Cross Catholic College that extends beyond financial assistance to provide holistic support for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. The program is led by dedicated staff and focuses on heightening cultural identity, maintaining high expectations, promoting active leadership and building strong community relationships. The program recognises the unique needs of Indigenous students and provides culturally responsive support mechanisms. Through the Garanyali Program, the College creates a learning environment where unity in diversity is highly valued, and where Indigenous students experience pride in their identity and a true sense of belonging within the College community.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander background; Enrolled at Southern Cross Catholic College; Seeking cultural and educational support; Students from Prep to Year 12
special_conditions: Open to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. Provides cultural identity development, leadership opportunities and community support. Led by experienced staff member. Part of College's commitment to excellence in Indigenous Education. Non-financial support program emphasising cultural identity and belonging.

overview: The Emmaus Program is Southern Cross Catholic College's learning enrichment and exceptional learners program. The program provides targeted support and extension opportunities for gifted and talented students, as well as students with additional learning needs, recognising the diverse abilities within the student population.
description: The Emmaus Program is a specialist learning enrichment initiative at Southern Cross Catholic College designed to support exceptional learners and provide extension opportunities for gifted and talented students. The program also supports students with learning differences and additional needs. Through the Emmaus Program, the College provides individualised learning pathways that challenge students to extend their abilities and achieve their full potential. The program is part of the College's broader commitment to exceptional learners enrichment and extension, recognising that every student has unique abilities and learning needs. Support is provided through specialist assessment, targeted interventions and enrichment activities tailored to individual student profiles.
eligibility_criteria: Identified as gifted and talented student; Students with exceptional learning abilities; Students requiring extension and enrichment; Students with diverse learning needs; Assessed through specialist evaluation
special_conditions: Requires identification as gifted, talented or having additional learning needs. Specialist assessment may be undertaken. Support includes enrichment activities and extension opportunities. Individualised learning pathways are developed. Non-financial support providing educational services.

overview: Somerset College offers the Craig Bassingthwaighte Scholarship to recognise outstanding academic achievement in high-achieving students. The scholarship is awarded at the Headmaster's discretion to support students who demonstrate exceptional commitment to learning and College life.
description: The Craig Bassingthwaighte Scholarship is available for entry into Years 7, 9, 10 and 11 in 2027. Scholarships provide between 25% and 75% fee remission on tuition fees only, for the duration of the student's secondary education at Somerset College. Recipients must maintain high standards of achievement and actively contribute to College life while embodying the College's values of integrity, leadership and dedication. The scholarship is awarded at the Headmaster's discretion and requires applicants to demonstrate academic excellence through previous school reports and assessment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7, 9, 10 and 11 in 2027; Must demonstrate academic excellence; Must maintain high standards of achievement and actively contribute to College life; Must embody College values of integrity, leadership and dedication; Somerset College does not require applicants to sit a Scholarship Examination
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded for the duration of the student's secondary education provided the recipient continues to be fully involved in College activities and meets all expectations. Scholarships apply to tuition fees only unless otherwise specified. Additional fees and charges are payable by the scholarship recipient's family.

overview: Somerset College offers Academic Scholarships to reward students who excel in their studies and demonstrate outstanding academic ability. The scholarship recognises students with strong commitment to learning and intellectual excellence.
description: Academic Scholarships are available for entry into Years 7, 10 and 11 and provide fee remission of between 25% and 75% on tuition fees for the duration of the student's secondary education. The scholarship selection process involves assessment of academic achievement through school reports and may include testing. Recipients must maintain high standards of academic performance, actively contribute to College life, and support the College's values. All other fees and charges beyond tuition must be paid by the scholarship recipient's family.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7, 10 and 11; Must demonstrate academic excellence; Scholarship testing may occur in February each year; Must maintain high standards of achievement throughout secondary education; Must actively contribute to College life
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded at the Headmaster's discretion for the duration of secondary education provided the student continues to be fully involved in College activities and meets all expectations. Parents and students undertake to support the rules, regulations and programmes of work of the College. College reserves the right to recover any financial assistance if the recipient leaves before completing Year 12 unless relocation is the cause.

overview: Somerset College offers Music Scholarships to recognise and support students with exceptional musical talent and ability. The scholarship is designed for students who demonstrate outstanding musicianship and commitment to the performing arts.
description: Music Scholarships are available for entry into Years 7, 10 and 11 and provide fee remission of between 25% and 75% on tuition fees for the duration of secondary education. The selection process includes academic testing followed by a music test covering theoretical and aural components. Students sit testing in February, with no instruments required at the initial assessment. Preference may be given to applicants who can demonstrate ability to play oboe, bassoon, French horn, lower brass instruments, lower saxophones, double bass or drum kit. Recipients must maintain high standards of musicianship and College involvement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7, 10 and 11; Must demonstrate exceptional musical talent; Required to sit academic testing and music theory/aural test in February; No instruments required for initial testing; Preference given for applicants with proficiency in specific instruments: oboe, bassoon, French horn, lower brass, lower saxophones, double bass, drum kit
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded at the Headmaster's discretion for the duration of secondary education provided the recipient continues to be fully involved in College activities and meets all expectations. Music tests include theoretical and aural components. All other fees and charges beyond tuition fees must be paid by the scholarship recipient's family.

overview: Somerset College offers Sports Scholarships to recognise and support students with exceptional athletic talent and ability. The scholarship supports students who demonstrate outstanding sporting achievement and dedication to excellence in sport.
description: Sports Scholarships are available for entry into Years 7, 9, 10 and 11 in 2027 and provide fee remission of between 25% and 75% on tuition fees for the duration of secondary education. The College offers a wide range of sports including Athletics, AFL, Basketball, Chess, Cross Country, Cricket, Cycling, Sailing, Golf, Hockey, Netball, Rowing, Rugby, Soccer, Softball, Swimming, Tennis, Touch Football, Volleyball and Water Polo. The selection process involves assessment of sporting achievement and potential. Recipients must maintain high standards of athletic performance and active involvement in College sporting programs.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7, 9, 10 and 11 in 2027; Must demonstrate exceptional sporting talent and achievement; Assessment of athletic ability and potential; Must maintain high standards of performance and involvement in College sports programs; Must actively contribute to College life and support College values
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded at the Headmaster's discretion for the duration of secondary education provided the recipient continues to be fully involved in College activities and meets all expectations. Recipients are expected to actively contribute to College sporting programs. All fees and charges beyond tuition fees must be paid by the scholarship recipient's family.

overview: Somerset College offers Performing Arts Scholarships to recognise and support students with exceptional talent in drama, music theatre, and other performing arts. The scholarship supports students who demonstrate outstanding ability and commitment to excellence in the performing arts.
description: Performing Arts Scholarships are available for entry into Years 7, 9, 10 and 11 in 2027 and provide fee remission of between 25% and 75% on tuition fees for the duration of secondary education. The College has an acclaimed Performing Arts programme with annual Junior School and Senior School musicals produced and performed. The selection process assesses artistic talent and potential in performing arts disciplines. Recipients must maintain high standards of performance and active involvement in College performing arts activities and productions.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7, 9, 10 and 11 in 2027; Must demonstrate exceptional performing arts talent in areas such as drama, music theatre, dance or vocal performance; Assessment of artistic ability and potential; Must maintain high standards of performance and involvement in College performing arts programs; Must actively contribute to College life
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded at the Headmaster's discretion for the duration of secondary education provided the recipient continues to be fully involved in College activities and meets all expectations. Recipients are expected to actively contribute to College performing arts programs and productions. All fees and charges beyond tuition fees must be paid by the scholarship recipient's family.

overview: The Alumni Association Scholarship recognises the achievements of students who have demonstrated a genuine commitment to College life across several areas. The scholarship celebrates well-rounded students who excel in multiple disciplines.
description: The Alumni Association Scholarship is available for entry into Years 7, 9, 10 and 11 in 2027 and provides fee remission of between 25% and 75% on tuition fees for the duration of secondary education. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate excellence across multiple areas of College life including academics, co-curricular activities, leadership, service and community involvement. Selection is based on demonstrated commitment across several areas of achievement and involvement. Recipients must maintain high standards of engagement and continue contributing to College life throughout their secondary education.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7, 9, 10 and 11 in 2027; Must demonstrate genuine commitment to College life across several areas; Must show excellence in multiple disciplines including academics and co-curricular activities; Must demonstrate leadership, service and community involvement; Must embody College values of integrity and dedication
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded at the Headmaster's discretion for the duration of secondary education provided the recipient continues to be fully involved in College activities and meets all expectations. Recipients are expected to maintain involvement across multiple areas of College life. All fees and charges beyond tuition fees must be paid by the scholarship recipient's family.

school_name: Spinifex State College - Mount Isa Education and Training Precinct
program_name: QATSIF (Queensland Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Foundation) Scholarship
overview: Spinifex State College offers QATSIF scholarships to support First Nations students in Years 11 and 12. The scholarship recognises Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students pursuing senior secondary studies and provides financial assistance to support their educational costs.
description: QATSIF provides educational scholarships of $500 per semester ($2,000 across Years 11 and 12) to support eligible First Nations students with the educational costs of their senior studies. Recipients must be of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent and maintain an attendance rate of at least 85%. Students must wear the school uniform with pride and schools apply on behalf of Year 10 students for the following years. The program aims to increase retention rates of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students in senior secondary education.
eligibility_criteria: Must be Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent; Eligible for Years 11 and 12; Attendance rate of at least 85%; Must wear school uniform; Schools apply on behalf of Year 10 students
special_conditions: Attendance requirement of minimum 85% must be maintained. Conduct standards regarding school uniform must be upheld. Scholarship operates across two years (Years 11-12). All funds directed to educational costs support.

school_name: Spinifex State College - Mount Isa Education and Training Precinct
overview: Spinifex State College offers a bursary program in partnership with Xstrata/Glencore (now Glencore) for Year 11 students. The program provides selected students with practical industry experience and work placement opportunities in mining and trade sectors.
description: The Xstrata Bursary program, established in 2004 in partnership with Mount Isa Mines, provides selected Year 11 students with one day per week attendance at the mining operations during the school year and continued participation during school holidays. Students gain practical industry experience in mining operations and can progress to structured apprenticeships or traineeships (SAT) once they have identified their area of preference. The program focuses on creating pathways into vocational and professional careers in the minerals and energy sectors. Support includes vacation work placements related to mining operations.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 11 students; Selected through school nomination process; Interest in mining or trade careers; Demonstrated commitment to practical learning; Available to eligible Mount Isa area students
special_conditions: Participation requires attendance one day per week during school year and during school holidays. Program may progress to SAT (Structured Apprenticeship/Traineeship) after initial year based on identified preferences and performance. Partnership with Glencore (formerly Xstrata) provides ongoing support and vacation work opportunities.

school_name: Spinifex State College - Mount Isa Education and Training Precinct
overview: Spinifex State College offers Student Excellence Scholarships through Glencore to Year 12 graduates pursuing tertiary studies. The scholarship recognises academic achievement and supports pathways into mining-related degrees with guaranteed vacation work placements.
description: Glencore awards $5,000 annually to selected Year 12 graduates pursuing tertiary studies, with particular focus on mining-related degrees. Recipients benefit from guaranteed vacation work placements with Glencore, providing practical industry experience and career development opportunities in the minerals sector. The scholarship aims to support students transitioning from secondary to tertiary education while building a pipeline of skilled professionals for the mining industry. Scholarships are awarded based on academic excellence and demonstrated commitment to mining or related technical fields.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 graduates; Pursuing tertiary studies; Preference for mining-related degrees; Selected based on academic merit; Commitment to mining industry career pathway
special_conditions: Available to Year 12 graduates only. Includes guaranteed vacation work placements with Glencore. Focus on mining-related tertiary programs. Annual award to support transition to tertiary study.

school_name: Spinifex State College - Mount Isa Education and Training Precinct
overview: Spinifex State College offers the Skills for the Future program to Year 9 students to explore trade and technical careers in mining. The program provides hands-on experience at the Xstrata/Glencore Skills Centre in Mount Isa.
description: Launched in 2007, the Skills for the Future program provides Year 9 students with the opportunity to attend the Xstrata Skills Centre (now operating under Glencore) to experience trade skills and technical training relevant to the mining industry. Students participate in workshops and practical sessions exploring various trade careers including electrical, automotive, mechanical, and sustainable energy fields. The program aims to give younger students early exposure to vocational pathways and industry opportunities, helping them make informed decisions about future career directions. No specific financial value is attached as the program is facilitated through partner industry support.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 9 students; Interest in trade and technical careers; Demonstrated engagement in school programs; Available to Spinifex State College students
special_conditions: Program operated through Glencore Queensland Metals and Xstrata Skills Centre. Provides exposure to various Certificate I and II trade qualifications. Participation facilitates early career exploration and potential pathways into apprenticeships and traineeships.

school_name: Spinifex State College - Mount Isa - Junior Campus
program_name: QATSIF Queensland Certificate of Education Scholarship Program
overview: Spinifex State College partners with the Queensland Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Foundation to offer QATSIF scholarships to First Nation students in Years 11 and 12. The scholarship recognises and supports Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students who demonstrate commitment to completing their senior studies.
description: QATSIF provides educational scholarship funding of $500 per semester ($2,000 across Years 11 and 12) to support eligible First Nations students with the educational costs of their senior studies. The scholarship is awarded to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students who meet eligibility criteria and demonstrate commitment to completing Years 11 and 12. Recipients must maintain an attendance rate of at least 85% and wear the school uniform with pride. Schools apply on behalf of Year 10 students during the application round. The scholarship supports students in completing their Queensland Certificate of Education.
eligibility_criteria: Must be of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent; Open to students in Year 10 (applying for Years 11 and 12); Must have an attendance rate of at least 85%; Maintain good conduct and wear school uniform with pride; Must complete Years 11 and 12 at the school
special_conditions: Scholarship is available each year. School applies on behalf of Year 10 students. Recipients must maintain an attendance rate of at least 85%. Recipients must wear the school uniform with pride. The scholarship supports students through Years 11 and 12.

school_name: Spinifex State College - Mount Isa - Junior Campus
overview: Spinifex State College offers the Xstrata Bursary Program in partnership with Xstrata Mount Isa Mines for Year 11 and Year 12 students. The program provides selected students with work experience and development opportunities within the mining industry while offering financial support.
description: In partnership with Xstrata Mount Isa Mines, Spinifex State College introduced a Bursary program for Year 11 students in 2004. Selected students attend Xstrata one day a week during Year 11 and during school holidays, gaining practical experience in the mining industry. The program is then progressed into a Structured Apprenticeship Traineeship (SAT) once students have identified their area of preference. The bursary provides financial assistance to selected students as they develop skills and pathways into the mining and resources sector. The program supports students in Years 11 and 12 who demonstrate interest in mining, engineering, and related trades.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Year 11; Selected students nominated by school; Interest in mining, engineering or trade careers; Willingness to participate in one day per week work experience at Xstrata; Ability to identify specific career preference for SAT progression
special_conditions: Program commenced in 2004 in partnership with Xstrata Mount Isa Mines. Students attend one day per week at Xstrata workplace during Year 11 and during school holidays. Program can progress to a Structured Apprenticeship Traineeship (SAT) in Year 12 based on student's identified career preference. Students must maintain satisfactory attendance and conduct.

school_name: Spinifex State College - Mount Isa - Junior Campus
overview: Spinifex State College Year 12 students are eligible to apply for the Glencore Student Excellence Scholarship, awarded to students demonstrating outstanding academic achievement and commitment to future studies. The scholarship recognises excellence across academic disciplines and supports tertiary education pathways.
description: The Glencore Student Excellence Scholarship is awarded to Spinifex State College Year 12 students who have demonstrated outstanding academic achievement and commitment to pursuing further education at university. Recipients are selected based on academic excellence and have previously been pursuing studies in disciplines including Mathematics, Engineering, Occupational Therapy, and Medicine. The scholarship provides financial support for tertiary education and recognises students who have participated in additional development programs offered through Glencore's partnership with the school, including Girls for Mining, Work Experience programs, and QMEA Trades Camp. Award recipients are encouraged to maintain engagement with Glencore's pathway and development initiatives.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 12 students; Demonstrated outstanding academic achievement; Commitment to pursuing tertiary education; Participation in Glencore pathway programs recommended (Girls for Mining, Work Experience, QMEA Trades Camp); Strong academic record across multiple disciplines
special_conditions: Awarded to students with outstanding academic achievement. Applicants should have participated in Glencore partnership programs including Girls for Mining, Work Experience, and QMEA Trades Camp. Scholarship recognises students pursuing tertiary education in STEM and other university-level disciplines.

school_name: Southern Cross Catholic College - Kippa-Ring Campus
program_name: Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund (MacKillop Fund)
overview: Southern Cross Catholic College offers the Mary MacKillop Fund, a bursary program named in honour of Australia's first saint, St Mary of the Cross MacKillop, who dedicated her life to making Catholic education available to the poor. The bursary supports families experiencing extreme hardship, trauma and financial devastation who desire a Catholic education for their child.
description: The Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund provides comprehensive financial support to families from all walks of life experiencing extreme hardship, trauma and financial devastation. The bursary includes full tuition coverage for either primary or secondary school, textbooks, uniforms and local excursions. The program is administered through the Catholic Foundation and accepts tax-deductible donations to support children in the local community. The fund is designed to give children the opportunity to fulfil their unique potential through a Catholic education. Support is available for families requiring assistance with school fees and associated educational costs.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing extreme hardship, trauma or financial devastation; Genuine desire for child to receive Catholic education; Open to all year levels
special_conditions: Funding is administered through the Catholic Foundation. Tax-deductible donations can be made directly through the Catholic Foundation to support children in the local community receiving Mary MacKillop Bursaries.

school_name: Southern Cross Catholic College - Kippa-Ring Campus
overview: Southern Cross Catholic College offers the Monsignor Frawley Bursary Program as part of its commitment to providing equity for all students. This program supports students and families requiring financial assistance to access Catholic education at the College.
description: The Monsignor Frawley Bursary Program is a financial assistance initiative that forms part of Southern Cross Catholic College's equity support structures. The program provides financial assistance to eligible families to help them access Catholic education. The bursary is designed to complement the College's inclusive enrolment policy and works alongside other programs such as the Garanyali Indigenous student program and Emmaus learning enrichment program to ensure all students have equitable access to education. Specific details regarding the value, application process and award criteria are managed through the College's enrolment and pastoral care offices.
eligibility_criteria: Families requiring financial assistance; Commitment to College Catholic ethos; Open to all year levels
special_conditions: Bursary recipients are expected to support the College's Catholic Christian ethos and values. Application and award decisions are made through the College's pastoral care and enrolment offices on a case-by-case basis.

school_name: Southern Cross Catholic College - Kippa-Ring Campus
program_name: QATSIF Scholarship - Queensland Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Foundation
overview: Southern Cross Catholic College offers the QATSIF Scholarship to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students entering Year 11. This scholarship provides two-year funding support to increase student retention through Years 11 and 12 for Indigenous students.
description: The QATSIF Scholarship (Queensland Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Foundation) is specifically designed for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students and provides two-year scholarship funding for eligible Year 11 students. The scholarship aims to increase Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander student retention in Years 11 and 12 by providing targeted financial support. The funding complements and builds on existing school and community programs while supporting individual students. The scholarship recognises the importance of Indigenous student success and works to remove financial barriers to completing secondary education. Support is tailored to individual student needs through collaborative planning with the College's Garanyali Indigenous student program.
eligibility_criteria: Must be Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander student; Entering or in Year 11; Commitment to completing Year 12; Open to Australian residents
special_conditions: Scholarship covers two years (Year 11 and Year 12). Funding is used to complement existing school and community programs. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress and attendance. The scholarship works in conjunction with the College's Garanyali Indigenous student support program.

school_name: Southern Cross Catholic College - Kippa-Ring Campus
overview: Southern Cross Catholic College operates the Garanyali program, an Indigenous student support initiative that provides holistic educational and pastoral support for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. The program is named using the Gubi Gubi word meaning 'to call'.
description: The Garanyali Indigenous Student Program (Gubi Gubi word meaning 'to call') is a comprehensive support initiative for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students at Southern Cross Catholic College across all campuses and year levels. The program provides culturally responsive educational support, pastoral care and community connection. It forms part of the College's equity framework alongside the Monsignor Frawley Bursary Program and Emmaus program for learning enrichment. The Garanyali committee works to ensure Indigenous students feel welcomed, supported and valued within the College community. The program is integrated into the College's commitment to acknowledge connections with Indigenous custodians of the land and promote inclusive participation in all College activities and events.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander descent; Open to all year levels; All students in Prep to Year 12
special_conditions: Non-monetary support program focusing on pastoral care, cultural inclusion and educational support. Students are automatically enrolled in the program upon identification as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander. The program complements other support structures including the QATSIF Scholarship and Monsignor Frawley Bursary.

school_name: Southern Cross Catholic College - Kippa-Ring Campus
overview: Southern Cross Catholic College offers the Emmaus program, a learning enrichment initiative for students demonstrating exceptional academic ability and exceptional learning needs. The program provides differentiated educational experiences to nurture each student's unique potential.
description: The Emmaus program is a learning enrichment initiative at Southern Cross Catholic College designed to support students with exceptional academic ability and to provide enriched learning experiences. The program is part of the College's commitment to providing equity and support for all learners through tailored educational approaches. The Emmaus program complements other support structures including the Monsignor Frawley Bursary Program and Garanyali Indigenous student program to create a comprehensive support network. Students in the program receive differentiated curriculum delivery and extension opportunities aligned with the College's vision for developing knowledge, love and service in a Catholic learning community.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated exceptional academic ability or learning needs; Teacher recommendation or identification; Open to all year levels
special_conditions: Program is based on teacher identification and assessment of student learning needs. Students receive tailored educational support and enrichment opportunities. Non-monetary benefits include curriculum differentiation, extension activities and specialised teaching strategies.

school_name: Southern Cross Catholic College - Woody Point Campus
overview: Southern Cross Catholic College offers the Mary MacKillop Fund to support families experiencing extreme hardship, trauma and financial devastation who share a deep desire for their child to receive a Catholic education. The bursary is named in honour of Australia's first saint, St Mary of the Cross MacKillop, who spent a lifetime making Catholic education available to the poor.
description: The Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund provides comprehensive financial support to eligible families. The bursary includes tuition coverage (for either primary or secondary school), textbooks, uniforms and local excursions. Support is provided through the generosity of individuals and organisations via the Catholic Foundation. The fund aims to ensure that financial circumstances do not prevent any child from accessing a Catholic education at Southern Cross. Families interested in applying for this support or wishing to make a donation should contact the Catholic Foundation directly.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing extreme hardship, trauma or financial devastation; Demonstrated desire for child to receive Catholic education; Open to Prep to Year 6 (primary) and Year 7 to Year 12 (secondary) students
special_conditions: Bursary covers tuition, textbooks, uniforms and local excursions. Support is provided through the duration of the stage of education (primary through Year 6 or secondary through Year 12) as long as family remains in need of assistance. Families should contact the Catholic Foundation for information about eligibility and application procedures.

school_name: Southern Cross Catholic College - Woody Point Campus
overview: Southern Cross Catholic College offers the Mons. Frawley Bursary Program to provide equity and support for students in financial need. This program is part of the College's commitment to ensuring all families, regardless of financial circumstances, can access Catholic education.
description: The Mons. Frawley Bursary Program is one of several equity initiatives at Southern Cross Catholic College designed to support students and families facing financial challenges. Named after one of the College's founders, Monsignor Bartholomew Frawley, the program reflects the College's commitment to inclusive enrolment and support for all students. Families seeking financial assistance should contact the College directly through the main office or finance department. The program aims to remove financial barriers to education and ensure all students can participate fully in College life and activities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students requiring financial assistance; Students demonstrating financial need; Open to both primary (Prep to Year 6) and secondary (Year 7 to Year 12) students
special_conditions: Program provides equity support as part of the College's inclusive enrolment policy. Families should contact the College Finance Office on 07 3480 3643 for detailed information about eligibility and application procedures.

school_name: Southern Cross Catholic College - Woody Point Campus
overview: Southern Cross Catholic College's Garanyali Program provides dedicated support for Indigenous students. The program name 'Garanyali' comes from the Gubi Gubi word meaning 'to call', reflecting the College's commitment to supporting Indigenous student success.
description: The Garanyali Program is designed to support Indigenous students at Southern Cross Catholic College through coordinated support services and community building. The program works to heighten cultural identity, maintain high expectations for student achievement, promote active leadership, and build strong community relationships. Led by dedicated staff members, the program ensures that Indigenous students receive culturally responsive support throughout their schooling journey. The program operates across all campuses and is integrated into the College's broader commitment to equity and inclusion.
eligibility_criteria: Identified as Indigenous Australian student; Open to all year levels from Prep to Year 12; Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students
special_conditions: Program provides cultural support, leadership development and community building. Students should contact their campus office for information about accessing program services and support.

school_name: Southern Cross Catholic College - Woody Point Campus
overview: Southern Cross Catholic College offers the Emmaus Program for learning enrichment and acceleration. The program supports students who demonstrate advanced learning capabilities and provides opportunities for intellectual extension.
description: The Emmaus Program is designed to provide learning enrichment opportunities for students at Southern Cross Catholic College. The program supports students identified as requiring enrichment and acceleration in their learning. It forms part of the College's comprehensive approach to differentiated learning and ensuring all students, particularly high-achievers, are appropriately challenged. The program integrates with the College's curriculum to provide additional depth and complexity in student learning. Details about specific program offerings, selection criteria and application procedures should be obtained directly from the College.
eligibility_criteria: Students identified as requiring learning enrichment; Demonstrated advanced academic capability; Open to students across primary and secondary years
special_conditions: Program provides enrichment and acceleration for high-achieving students. Selection is based on academic identification and assessment. Students and families should contact the College directly for information about program participation.

school_name: Southern Cross Catholic College - Woody Point Campus
overview: The Lasallian Youth Leader Program at Southern Cross Catholic College seeks to enhance the spiritual formation of senior students in Years 11 and 12. The program develops student leaders whose ministry to younger students is Christ-centred and reflects the Lasallian tradition.
description: The Lasallian Youth Leader Program is a leadership development initiative for selected students in Years 11 and 12 at Southern Cross Catholic College. The program focuses on enhancing spiritual formation through faith-building experiences and mentoring younger students with a Christ-centred approach. Student leaders participate in formation experiences including retreats and immersion activities that develop their understanding of Lasallian charism and leadership. The program prepares senior students to model Catholic values and provide peer support and mentoring to junior students across the College. Selection is based on demonstrated leadership potential and commitment to spiritual development.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 11 or Year 12; Demonstrated leadership qualities and spiritual commitment; Interest in peer mentoring and youth leadership; Nomination or application through College pastoral processes
special_conditions: Program includes participation in retreats and immersion experiences. Student leaders must demonstrate commitment to spiritual formation and mentoring of younger students. Selection involves College pastoral staff assessment. Program is integrated with broader College spiritual and pastoral development activities.

school_name: Spinifex State College - Mount Isa - Senior Campus
program_name: QATSIF – Queensland Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Foundation Scholarship
overview: Spinifex State College offers QATSIF scholarships available annually to First Nations students in Years 11 and 12. The scholarship recognises Indigenous students demonstrating academic commitment and cultural excellence.
description: QATSIF – Queensland Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Foundation scholarships are available each year to First Nations students in Years 11 and 12 that meet specific criteria. Recipients must be of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent, maintain an attendance rate of at least 85%, and wear the school uniform with pride. The scholarship supports Indigenous students in their senior secondary education and recognises their achievement and potential. Selection is based on meeting the stated eligibility criteria.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Year 11 and Year 12; Must be of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent; Must have an attendance rate of at least 85%; Must wear the school uniform with pride
special_conditions: Minimum 85% attendance required. Recipients must wear the school uniform with pride and maintain cultural pride in being part of the QATSIF program.

school_name: Spinifex State College - Mount Isa - Senior Campus
overview: Spinifex State College offers the Xstrata Bursary Program in partnership with Mount Isa Mines (Xstrata/Glencore) for Year 11 students. The program provides students with industry mentorship and work experience opportunities in mining and trade skills.
description: The Xstrata Bursary Program was introduced in 2004 in partnership with Xstrata, Mount Isa Mines for Year 11 students. Selected students attend Xstrata one day a week during Year 11 and during school holidays, gaining valuable industry exposure and mentorship. This leads to progression into a School-based Apprenticeship/Traineeship (SAT) once students have identified their area of preference. The program provides authentic learning opportunities in the mining and resources sector. The bursary recognises students with potential in trade and technical fields.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Year 11; Selected students based on identified interest and aptitude in trade and technical fields; Attendance commitment of one day per week during term and school holidays required
special_conditions: Recipients must attend Xstrata one day per week during Year 11 school terms and during school holidays. Program participants may progress to School-based Apprenticeship/Traineeship upon completion based on identified career preference. This is an ongoing partnership that provides work-integrated learning.

school_name: Spinifex State College - Mount Isa - Senior Campus
overview: Spinifex State College offers the Diane Willis Memorial Bursary to recognise outstanding female students demonstrating leadership and community contribution. The bursary is presented annually in partnership with Zonta to a deserving Year 12 student.
description: The Diane Willis Memorial Bursary is awarded annually to a female Year 12 student at Spinifex State College. The bursary is presented in partnership with Zonta International, a global organisation dedicated to advancing women's opportunities. Recipients are recognised for demonstrating leadership qualities, community contribution, and personal achievement. The bursary honours the legacy of Diane Willis and celebrates outstanding young women at the school. Awardees are announced at the school's annual Awards Night.
eligibility_criteria: Open to female students in Year 12; Demonstrated leadership and community contribution required; Selection based on overall achievement and contribution to school and community
special_conditions: Award presented annually at the school's Awards Night. Recipients recognised for demonstrating outstanding leadership and community service. Part of the school's recognition of female student achievement.

school_name: Spinifex State College - Mount Isa - Senior Campus
overview: Spinifex State College offers Gifted and Talented Programs to identify and support exceptional students. Year 7 students can access a dedicated program in Mathematics, Science, The Arts and English.
description: Spinifex State College offers Gifted and Talented Programs to provide enriched learning opportunities for students demonstrating exceptional ability. The Year 7 Gifted and Talented Program focuses on Mathematics, Science, The Arts and English, providing accelerated learning and extension activities for students identified as gifted. The program aims to challenge and engage students operating above year level expectations and support the development of their talents and passions. Identification may be based on teacher recommendation, prior assessment data, or demonstrated achievement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 students; Demonstrated excellence in Mathematics, Science, The Arts or English; Selection based on identification as gifted and talented learner; Potential for above-year-level performance
special_conditions: Program provides enriched curriculum and extension learning opportunities for identified gifted students. Students selected through assessment and identification processes at Year 7 entry.

school_name: St Andrew's Catholic College Redlynch Valley
program_name: Scholarship and Bursary Program - Academic, Sports, and Arts
overview: St Andrew's Catholic College offers a diverse range of scholarships and bursaries to current and prospective students. The programs recognise excellence in academics, sports, and the arts for students who will make significant contributions to the College's outstanding academic and co-curricular programs.
description: St Andrew's Scholarship and Bursary Program is committed to supporting quality education opportunities for students demonstrating excellence. The program encourages learning opportunities, academic excellence, diversity, and creative thinking. The College offers scholarships in academic, sports, and the arts categories for students who will contribute to the College's programs. Scholarships are available for entry into Year 7 (Middle Years) and Year 11 (Senior Years). Scholarship students are expected to act as ambassadors for the College internally and externally. Specific financial values, selection processes, and detailed eligibility criteria are outlined in the College's 2026 Scholarships and Bursary Program document.
eligibility_criteria: Open to prospective and current students; Entry to Year 7 (Middle Years) and Year 11 (Senior Years); Students must be willing to act as ambassadors for the College; Demonstrated excellence in academics, sports, or the arts
special_conditions: Scholarship students are expected to act as ambassadors for the College internally and externally. The 2027 Scholarship Application Program opens on Monday 3 November 2025. Additional details and specific program information are available in the 2026 Scholarships and Bursary Program document available for download from the College website.

program_name: Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund (MacKillop Fund)
overview: St Ann's School offers the Mary MacKillop Fund, a bursary program named in honour of Australia's first Saint. The program supports families experiencing extreme hardship, trauma and financial devastation to access Catholic education at St Ann's. The bursary is designed to help families from all walks of life who have a deep desire for their child to receive a Catholic education.
description: The Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund is administered through the Catholic Foundation and provides comprehensive financial assistance to eligible families. The bursary includes full coverage of tuition fees (for either primary or secondary school), textbooks, uniforms, and local excursions. The program specifically supports families experiencing extreme hardship, trauma and financial devastation. Applications are made through the Catholic Foundation website. Tax-deductible donations can be made directly to the Catholic Foundation to support the program and help children in the local community fulfil their unique potential through a Mary MacKillop Bursary.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing extreme hardship, trauma or financial devastation; Deep desire for child to receive Catholic education; Demonstrated financial need; Open to all students at St Ann's School
special_conditions: Bursary includes tuition, textbooks, uniforms and local excursions. Application and assessment is conducted through the Catholic Foundation. All matters are dealt with on a confidential basis. The program is funded through donations to the Catholic Foundation.

overview: St Ann's School offers a Fee Concession program for eligible families experiencing financial hardship. This program provides fee assistance through an application process based on demonstrated financial need, using an equitable formula consistent with Brisbane Catholic Education guidelines.
description: St Ann's School provides fee concessions for eligible families facing financial hardship. Families can apply for a fee concession on a case-by-case basis through submission of an Application or Concession on Fees form, available from the school office or Principal. The eligibility assessment uses a just and equitable formula reflecting financial hardship and is consistent with Brisbane Catholic Education guidelines. The granting of a fee concession is conditional on the family agreeing to use the direct debit method of school fee payment, including Centrepay. Fee concessions are reviewed annually to ensure ongoing eligibility and appropriateness of assistance provided. All matters are handled confidentially.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated financial hardship; Agreement to pay fees via direct debit or Centrepay; Annual review and renewal; Open to all school sectors
special_conditions: Granting of fee concession is conditional on family agreeing to direct debit method of payment (including Centrepay). Fee concessions are reviewed annually. All matters are dealt with on a confidential basis. In cases of genuine financial hardship, the school works with families to support enrolment.

school_name: St Anthony's Catholic College - Primary Campus
St Anthony's Catholic College - Secondary Campus

overview: St Anne's Catholic Primary School offers a 70% discount on tuition fees for families who hold a means-tested Australian government health care or pensioner concession card. This assistance programme provides significant fee relief for eligible families experiencing financial hardship.
description: St Anne's provides a 70% discount on tuition fees (does not apply to school levies) for families who hold a valid means-tested Australian government health care or pensioner concession card. The discount is automatically applied once eligibility is verified by the front office. This represents a tuition fee reduction of 70%. Families who believe they are eligible should contact Mrs Barnard at the front office to arrange the discount. The school follows a straightforward verification process where eligible concession cards must be presented to confirm qualification.
eligibility_criteria: Holder of a means-tested Australian government health care or pensioner concession card; Concession card must be current and valid; Must provide card details to front office for verification
special_conditions: Discount applies to tuition fees only and does not apply to school levies or building levies. Families must contact the front office and provide proof of concession card eligibility. The discount is automatically applied once verified.

overview: St Anne's Catholic Primary School provides flexible fee assistance to families experiencing genuine financial hardship, whether or not they hold a concession card. The principal works confidentially with families to arrange alternate fee discount arrangements suited to their circumstances.
description: St Anne's recognises that some families may experience financial hardship and offers confidential support beyond standard concession card discounts. New or existing parents who are experiencing financial hardship and may or may not hold a concession card are encouraged to discuss alternate fee discounts directly with the principal. These discussions are treated with the utmost confidentiality. The school's flexible approach allows for negotiated fee arrangements to ensure no family is disadvantaged due to financial circumstances. The specific discount amount varies depending on individual family circumstances and is determined through discussion with school leadership.
eligibility_criteria: Experiencing genuine financial hardship; Open to new and existing families; Must discuss arrangements directly with the principal; Discussions treated confidentially; No requirement to hold concession card
special_conditions: Families must approach the principal directly to discuss their circumstances. All discussions are confidential. Fee arrangements are negotiated on a case-by-case basis depending on individual family needs. Families are encouraged to seek this support early.

overview: St Anne's Catholic Primary School, as part of the Catholic Education – Diocese of Rockhampton system, provides family discounts to families with multiple children enrolled. The discount increases with the number of siblings attending CEDR schools.
description: Families with two or more children attending Catholic Education – Diocese of Rockhampton (CEDR) schools qualify for a Family Discount applied to the All-Inclusive Education fee. For families with 2 siblings attending CEDR schools, a 7.5% discount is applied to the All-Inclusive Education fee of all siblings. For families with 3 siblings attending CEDR schools, a 20% discount is applied. For families with 4 or more siblings attending CEDR schools, a 30% discount is applied. Family discounts are applied for each year by completing the Confirmation of Enrolment form issued to all families. The discount applies to the All-Inclusive Education fee component of fees.
eligibility_criteria: Must have 2 or more children attending Catholic Education – Diocese of Rockhampton schools; Discount applied based on total number of siblings; Must complete Confirmation of Enrolment form annually; Applies only to All-Inclusive Education fees
value_aud: 7.5% Fee Reduction or 20% Fee Reduction or 30% Fee Reduction
special_conditions: Discount applies to All-Inclusive Education fees only. Discounts are tiered: 7.5% for 2 siblings, 20% for 3 siblings, 30% for 4+ siblings. Must complete Confirmation of Enrolment form annually to receive discount. Applies across all CEDR schools, not just St Anne's.

overview: St Andrews Lutheran College offers Academic Scholarships to recognise and reward outstanding academic achievement in secondary students. These scholarships are available to both current and prospective students entering from Year 7 onwards who demonstrate exceptional ability in academic examinations.
description: Academic Scholarships are awarded to students who achieve outstanding results in the Edutest academic examination. The scholarship provides a remission of the tuition fee component of school fees, with the exact remission amount determined by the Principal. Scholarships are typically held from Years 7 to 9 or from Years 10 to 12, and are reviewed at the commencement of Year 9 where students and parents may be asked to attend a scholarship review meeting. A letter outlining an offer of scholarship for Year 10 to 12 will be sent after the scholarship review has been completed. Selection is based on external academic examination results and, if shortlisted, a subsequent interview.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current and external students who will be in Years 7 to 12 in 2026; Must pass the Edutest academic examination; Shortlisted candidates must attend interview; Demonstrated high degree of excellence in academic studies combined with personal commitment
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarship is a remission of the tuition fee component only. The remission amount is determined by the Principal. Scholarship conditions will be outlined in the letter of offer for successful students. Students must participate fully in co-curricular activities of the College in so far as their abilities allow. Students must show support to the leadership role that is implicit for all scholarship holders, including being a positive role model for younger students, displaying leadership by encouraging and supporting other students and fostering team and College spirit. The College will reserve the right to terminate the scholarship in the event of gross or continued breaches of expectations, unwarranted decline in academic achievement or application, or persistent failure to support the College in its co-curricular program. Examination is both online and paper-based comprising 4 online tests of 30 minutes each and a 15 minute written test. Students must supply their own laptop device (no iPads).

overview: St Andrews Lutheran College offers General Excellence Scholarships to students who demonstrate outstanding achievement across multiple areas including leadership, community service, and co-curricular involvement. These scholarships recognise all-round excellence beyond academic achievement.
description: General Excellence Scholarships are awarded to students who exhibit excellence across a number of areas including leadership, community service, service to the school community and co-curricular involvement. The scholarship provides a remission of the tuition fee component of school fees, with the exact remission amount determined by the Principal. Scholarships are typically held from Years 7 to 9 or from Years 10 to 12. Year 7 to 9 scholarships will be reviewed at the commencement of Year 9 where students and parents may be asked to attend a scholarship review meeting. Selection is based on academic examination results, portfolio submission and, if shortlisted, subsequent interview.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current and external students who will be in Years 7 to 12 in 2026; Must demonstrate excellence across multiple areas including leadership and community service; Must submit both Edutest examination registration and portfolio; Shortlisted candidates must attend interview; Demonstrated high degree of excellence combined with personal commitment
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarship is a remission of the tuition fee component only. The remission amount is determined by the Principal. Both registration for the Academic Scholarship Exam and a Portfolio must be received prior to the closing date for the application to be valid. If examination registration has been received but portfolio has not, the student will only be assessed for an Academic Scholarship and application for General Excellence Scholarship will be voided. Students must participate fully in co-curricular activities and show leadership support. The College will reserve the right to terminate the scholarship in the event of gross or continued breaches of expectations, unwarranted decline in academic achievement or application, or persistent failure to support the College in its co-curricular program.

overview: St Andrews Lutheran College offers Performing Arts Scholarships to students who have achieved a high level in Music, Dance or Speech and Drama. These scholarships recognise excellence in performance and artistic achievement.
description: Performing Arts Scholarships are awarded to students who have achieved a high level in Music, Dance or Speech and Drama. The scholarship provides a remission of the tuition fee component of school fees, with the exact remission amount determined by the Principal. Scholarships are typically held from Years 7 to 9 or from Years 10 to 12. Selection is based on a portfolio submission and, if shortlisted, an audition. Current students at St Andrews need to have achieved at least a Silver Cultural Award the previous year to be eligible for application. Recipients must participate fully in the co-curricular activities of the College, contributing to the College Arts program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current and external students who will be in Years 7 to 12 in 2026; Must have achieved high level in Music, Dance or Speech and Drama; Current St Andrews students must have achieved at least Silver Cultural Award in previous year; Must submit portfolio; Shortlisted candidates must complete audition; Demonstrated high degree of excellence combined with personal commitment
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarship is a remission of the tuition fee component only. The remission amount is determined by the Principal. Selection is based on portfolio submission and, if shortlisted, audition. Current St Andrews students must have achieved at least Silver Cultural Award the previous year to be eligible. Recipients must participate fully in co-curricular activities of the College, contributing to the College Arts program. Students must show support to the leadership role that is implicit for all scholarship holders. The College will reserve the right to terminate the scholarship in the event of gross or continued breaches of expectations, unwarranted decline in academic achievement or application, or persistent failure to support the College in its co-curricular program.

overview: St Andrews Lutheran College offers Creative Arts Scholarships to students who have achieved a high level in Media Arts or Visual Art. These scholarships recognise outstanding achievement in creative arts disciplines.
description: Creative Arts Scholarships are awarded to students who have achieved a high level in Media Arts or Visual Art. The scholarship provides a remission of the tuition fee component of school fees, with the exact remission amount determined by the Principal. Scholarships are typically held from Years 7 to 9 or from Years 10 to 12. Selection is based on a portfolio submission and, if shortlisted, subsequent interview. Applicants should be the recipient of a Major Award at Regional level (Capture Photography Competition / Energies Exhibition etc) or at a similar standard as evidenced in their portfolio submission.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current and external students who will be in Years 7 to 12 in 2026; Must have achieved high level in Media Arts or Visual Art; Applicants should be recipient of Major Award at Regional level or similar standard; Must submit portfolio with cover sheet prior to closing date; Shortlisted candidates must attend interview; Demonstrated high degree of excellence combined with personal commitment
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarship is a remission of the tuition fee component only. The remission amount is determined by the Principal. Selection is based on portfolio submission and, if shortlisted, interview. Applicants should be recipient of Major Award at Regional level or similar standard. Both the Cover Sheet and Portfolio must be received prior to closing date for application to be valid. Late portfolio submissions will not be accepted. Short-listed applicants invited for interview will be notified via email; non-attendance will result in application being voided. The College will reserve the right to terminate the scholarship in the event of gross or continued breaches of expectations, unwarranted decline in academic achievement or application, or persistent failure to support the College in its co-curricular program.

overview: St Andrews Lutheran College offers Sport Scholarships to students who achieve high performance levels across a range of sports. These scholarships recognise students performing at regional or state level in school or club representative sport.
description: Sport Scholarships are available for students achieving high performance levels in AFL, Athletics, Basketball, Cross Country, Futsal, Mountain Biking, Netball, Rugby Union, Soccer, Surfing, Swimming, Tennis, Touch Football and Volleyball. The scholarship provides a remission of the tuition fee component of school fees, with the exact remission amount determined by the Principal. Scholarships are typically held from Years 7 to 9 or from Years 10 to 12. Students must be performing at a high level and participate regularly in school or club representative sport. Applicants should be capable of achieving at a regional or state level. Selection is based on portfolio submission detailing representation in school and/or club sport programs and, if shortlisted, subsequent interview.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current and external students who will be in Years 7 to 12 in 2026; Must be performing at high level in nominated sport; Must participate regularly in school or club representative sport; Applicants must be capable of achieving at regional or state level; Must submit portfolio with cover sheet showing representation details; Shortlisted candidates must attend interview; Demonstrated high degree of excellence combined with personal commitment
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarship is a remission of the tuition fee component only. The remission amount is determined by the Principal. Scholarships are available only for sports that are part of the St Andrews sporting program. Selection is based on portfolio submission and interview. Information about specific achievements in competition/representation must be submitted via cover sheet and portfolio and be evidenced in portfolio. Both the Cover Sheet and Portfolio must be received prior to closing date for application to be valid. There is no cost associated with Sport Scholarship application. Late portfolio submissions will not be accepted. Recipients must participate in nominated school-based sporting activities such as training sessions, competitions and events as notified. Recipients must agree, within parameters determined by Principal and/or Head of Sport, to participate in College activities before outside engagements. Students must show leadership support. The College will reserve the right to terminate the scholarship.

overview: St Anthony's School Kedron provides fee concession assistance to families experiencing genuine financial hardship. The school is committed to ensuring that enrolment is not compromised by financial circumstances and offers support through its fee concession application process.
description: The school recognises that some families may experience short or long-term genuine inability to pay school fees. St Anthony's School offers fee concession assistance for families in financial hardship. Eligible families should contact the Principal or Finance Office to discuss their circumstances and apply for a fee concession. The financial value of assistance varies based on individual family circumstances and is determined through the application and assessment process. Support is available to ensure that no family is prevented from enrolling their child due to financial constraints. The concession application process is confidential and families are encouraged to communicate early with the school about their financial situation.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing genuine financial hardship; Unable to pay school fees due to illness, unemployment, or other reasonable cause; Must apply through the Principal or Finance Office
special_conditions: Genuine financial hardship must be demonstrated. Families are advised to contact the school early and communicate openly about their financial situation. Concession arrangements are treated with absolute discretion and confidentiality.

program_name: Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund
overview: The Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund provides bursary support to families experiencing significant financial hardship within the Archdiocese of Brisbane. This system-wide fund assists students at St Anthony's School Kedron and other Brisbane Catholic Education schools to access Catholic education.
