description: The Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund is a charitable bursary program administered by the Catholic Foundation for the Archdiocese of Brisbane. It supports families experiencing extreme hardship, trauma, and financial devastation who wish to provide their child with a Catholic education. The bursary covers full tuition fees, textbooks, uniforms, and local excursions for the duration of enrolment at primary school (through to Year 6). For primary school students, the bursary provides funding through to the end of Year 6 as long as the family continues to need assistance. Parents contribute a weekly financial amount negotiated with the school. Applications must be made in the year before enrolment commences and are available for new students only.
eligibility_criteria: Family experiencing significant financial hardship; Live within the Archdiocese of Brisbane; New students only; Must apply in the year before enrolment commences; Families must demonstrate genuine financial need
special_conditions: Available to new students only. Applications must be submitted to the Catholic Foundation in the year before enrolment commences. Bursary includes tuition, textbooks, uniforms, and local excursions. Parents agree to contribute a weekly negotiated amount. Bursary funding continues through to the end of Year 6 as long as the family remains in financial need.

program_name: Diocesan Fee Concession - Concession Card Discount
overview: St Anthony's provides a 40% Concession Card Discount on school fees for families holding current, means-tested Australian Government Health Care or Pensioner Concession Cards. This discount is available to eligible families to ensure no child is denied a Catholic education due to financial constraints.
description: The Diocese of Rockhampton operates a Concession Card Discount program available at all Catholic schools including St Anthony's Catholic Primary School. Families holding a current means-tested Australian Government Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card are eligible for a 40% discount on the All-Inclusive Education fee. The card holder must be the person legally responsible for paying school fees and the child's name must appear as a dependent. The discount applies to the current billing period and is reviewed annually. Families experiencing financial hardship who do not hold a valid concession card are encouraged to apply for special consideration through the financial hardship concession process.
eligibility_criteria: Holder of current means-tested Australian Government Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card; Card holder must be person legally responsible for paying school fees; Child's name must appear as dependent on the card; Card must be current on any day of the billing period
special_conditions: Card must be sighted by school administration. Discount applies to All-Inclusive Education fee only. Concession is reviewed annually and must be renewed each year. Discount applies to current billing period onwards only.

overview: St Anthony's provides fee concession assistance for families experiencing genuine financial hardship. The school operates a formal application process to ensure no student is denied enrolment due to financial difficulties, reflecting Catholic principles of equity and inclusion.
description: Families at St Anthony's Catholic Primary School experiencing genuine financial hardship are encouraged to apply for fee concession assistance through a formal application process. Genuine financial hardship is defined as a situation where a family is unable, reasonably, because of illness, unemployment or other reasonable cause, to discharge their financial obligations to pay school fees. Families must complete a Fee Concession Application form with required documentation and meet with the School Principal to discuss their circumstances confidentially. The application is assessed by the Principal who determines eligibility and level of fee concession. Concessions granted are subject to annual review with up-to-date financial information required each year. Financial hardship can be of limited or long-term duration.
eligibility_criteria: Family experiencing genuine financial hardship; Unable to reasonably discharge financial obligations due to illness, unemployment or other reasonable cause; Requires completion of Fee Concession Application with supporting documentation; Must meet confidentially with School Principal
special_conditions: Application must be submitted with required documented supporting evidence. Assessment carried out confidentially with School Principal. Concessions are subject to annual review. Family will be advised in writing within two weeks of interview. No child will be denied enrolment due to genuine financial hardship. Concessions do not continue automatically from year to year.

overview: St Anthony's offers tiered family discounts on school fees for families with multiple children enrolled in Catholic Education Diocese of Rockhampton schools. The discount increases with each additional sibling to make Catholic education more accessible for larger families.
description: The Diocese of Rockhampton provides a Family Discount program available to all families with children enrolled in multiple CEDR schools. The discount is applied to the All-Inclusive Education fee for all siblings and increases based on the number of children enrolled. Two siblings attending CEDR schools receive a 7.5% discount applied to all siblings' fees. Three siblings attending CEDR schools receive a 20% discount. Four or more siblings attending CEDR schools receive a 30% discount applied to the All-Inclusive Education fee. Family discounts are applied for each year by completing the Confirmation of Enrolment form issued to all families annually.
eligibility_criteria: Family has 2 or more children enrolled in Catholic Education Diocese of Rockhampton schools; Discount applies to All-Inclusive Education fee; Applies to siblings enrolled at any CEDR school; Applied annually by completing Confirmation of Enrolment form
value_aud: 7.5% Fee Reduction; 20% Fee Reduction; 30% Fee Reduction
special_conditions: Discount applied annually; Confirmation of Enrolment form must be completed each year; Two siblings: 7.5% discount; Three siblings: 20% discount; Four or more siblings: 30% discount; Discount applied to All-Inclusive Education fee only.

overview: St Augustine's College offers the Heavey Scholarship to one student entering Year 7. The scholarship recognises and supports exceptional academic ability and potential as students commence their secondary education. It provides full tuition fee coverage through Year 10, conditional on sustained academic performance.
description: The Heavey Scholarship is available to one student entering Year 7 and is tenured from the commencement of Year 7 until the end of Year 10. The scholarship covers 100% of tuition fees. The award is made based on three assessment criteria: an academic test conducted by ACER, a personal interview, and the student's report card from their present school. Successful applicants must demonstrate continued academic success to maintain the scholarship throughout their tenure. The selection process is rigorous and competitive, recognising only the highest-achieving students with the potential to excel at St Augustine's College.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6; Entering Year 7; Academic performance in top percentile; Must demonstrate sustained academic success; Open to boys only
special_conditions: Scholarship is conditional on continued academic success. Award covers 100% of tuition fees only and does not include additional levies, music fees, or optional activities.

overview: St Augustine's College offers the Gildas Scholarship to one student entering Year 11, available to both day and boarding students. The scholarship recognises outstanding academic achievement and supports continuation of secondary education at the highest level. It provides full tuition fee coverage through Year 12, conditional on maintained academic excellence.
description: The Gildas Scholarship is available to one student entering Year 11, either as a day or boarding student. The scholarship is tenured from the commencement of Year 11 until the end of Year 12. The award covers 100% of tuition fees and is conditional on continued academic success. Selection is based on three criteria: an academic test conducted by ACER, a personal interview, and the student's report card from their present school. The scholarship recognises exceptional academic ability in students transitioning to senior secondary education and provides meaningful financial support during Years 11 and 12.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 10; Entering Year 11; Academic performance demonstrating excellence; Must be prepared for day or boarding option; Conditional on continued academic success; Open to boys only
special_conditions: Scholarship is conditional on continued academic success throughout Years 11 and 12. Award covers 100% of tuition fees only and does not include additional levies, music fees, or optional activities.

overview: St Augustine's College offers the Music Scholarship to Year 7 music students who currently play a music ensemble instrument. The scholarship recognises musical talent and commitment to ensemble participation. It covers 100% of tuition and music fees through Year 12, conditional on continued participation and success in music ensembles.
description: The Music Scholarship is offered to Year 7 music students who currently play a music ensemble instrument. The scholarship is awarded based on an audition and interview assessment. The Music Scholarship covers 100% of tuition and music fees for the duration of the student's enrolment. It is tenured from the commencement of Year 7 until the end of Year 12. The scholarship is conditional on participation and success in music ensembles, as well as continued enrolment in Music as an academic subject. This prestigious award supports talented young musicians throughout their entire secondary education at St Augustine's College.
eligibility_criteria: Currently in Year 6; Entering Year 7; Must currently play a music ensemble instrument; Must continue Music as an academic subject; Must actively participate in music ensembles; Open to boys only; Music performance standard demonstrated at audition
special_conditions: Scholarship is conditional on participation and success in music ensembles and continued enrolment in Music as an academic subject. Recipients must maintain active involvement in the college's music program throughout Years 7-12. Award covers 100% of tuition and music fees.

overview: St Augustine's College partners with the Australian Indigenous Education Foundation (AIEF) to offer scholarship funding for First Nations students. The AIEF Scholarship provides financial support to help Indigenous students complete their secondary education, with boarding options available for regional and remote students.
description: St Augustine's College proudly collaborates with the Australian Indigenous Education Foundation (AIEF) to offer scholarship funding for First Nations students to complete Year 12. The scholarship is designed to support students from diverse backgrounds and address systemic barriers to education by increasing access to secondary education for Indigenous Australians. AIEF provides comprehensive support including mentoring, leadership development, and career guidance to help the successful transition from school to employment or tertiary studies. The college offers AIEF Scholarships for boarding students, making education accessible to students from Cairns, remote areas of Queensland, interstate and overseas. The partnership reflects the college's commitment to equity and supporting First Nations education.
eligibility_criteria: First Nations student; Enrolled or accepted for secondary education at St Augustine's College; AbStudy eligible; Demonstrated potential to complete Year 12; Commitment to succeeding in education
special_conditions: Administered by AIEF. Recipients receive mentoring and leadership support, with career support to facilitate transition from school to employment or tertiary studies. Contact Indigenous Liaison Officer on 07 4052 9135 for further details. AIEF/Smith Family Scholarship recipients are liable for 100% of fees incurred if departing college.

program_name: The Smith Family's Indigenous Youth Leadership Program (IYLP) Scholarship
overview: St Augustine's College partners with The Smith Family to offer the Indigenous Youth Leadership Program (IYLP) Scholarship to First Nations students. The IYLP provides comprehensive financial and mentoring support to enable Indigenous youth to complete secondary education with emphasis on leadership development.
description: St Augustine's College collaborates with The Smith Family's Indigenous Youth Leadership Program (IYLP) to offer scholarship funding for First Nations students to complete Year 12. The IYLP is designed to support Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander youth from communities in very remote areas and regional communities. Recipients benefit from comprehensive support including mentoring, leadership development, and career guidance to facilitate successful transition from school to employment or tertiary studies. The program focuses on increasing access to quality secondary education and empowering Indigenous young people with the motivation and skills to achieve their educational and career goals. Both AIEF and IYLP scholarships operate collaboratively at St Augustine's College to provide targeted support.
eligibility_criteria: First Nations student (Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander); Demonstrated potential and motivation to complete Year 12; AbStudy eligible; Enrolled or applying to St Augustine's College; Commitment to educational success; From remote or regional communities preferred
special_conditions: Administered by The Smith Family. Scholarships are offered for one year initially with student progress reviewed frequently. Recipients undergo interview process with parent/guardian if shortlisted. Recipients receive mentoring and leadership support, with career guidance to support transition from school to employment or tertiary studies. Contact enrolments/admissions at school as soon as possible before applying.

school_name: St Aidan's Anglican Girls School - Thalia Court Campus

program_name: Tuition Fee Concessions for Financial Hardship
overview: St Benedict's Primary School offers concessions on tuition fees to families experiencing genuine financial hardship. The school is committed to ensuring no Catholic student is denied a place due to parents' inability to pay full or part school fees.
description: St Benedict's Catholic Primary School provides tuition fee concessions to families experiencing genuine financial difficulties. Applications must be made via the Concession Application Form and submitted along with supporting documentation. All information remains confidential. Concessions are available for tuition fees as part of the school's commitment to accessibility. The school recognises that from time to time some parents find themselves in financial difficulties and aims to ensure no student is denied enrolment due to genuine inability to pay fees. Forms can be obtained by contacting the Senior Finance Officer in the school office. The school operates under Brisbane Catholic Education guidelines.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing genuine financial hardship; Ability to demonstrate financial need through confidential means-testing; Submission of completed Concession Application Form with supporting documentation
special_conditions: All applications and financial information remain strictly confidential. Forms must be obtained from and submitted to the Senior Finance Officer in the school office. School fees policy states that the aim is no Catholic student will be denied a place in the school due to parents' genuine inability to pay full or even part school fees.

overview: Springfield Central State High School offers Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding achievement in core subject areas. The scholarships recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability in Mathematics, Science, English, and Humanities.
description: The Academic Scholarship program at Springfield Central State High School awards two scholarships annually to students who demonstrate excellence across all CORE subject areas including Mathematics, Science, English and Humanities. Recipients are selected based on their academic performance and commitment to learning. The scholarships support students entering Year 7 and are designed to recognise and reward sustained academic excellence throughout their secondary education.
eligibility_criteria: Excellence in all CORE subject areas of Mathematics, Science, English and Humanities; Open to Year 7 entry; Merit-based selection

overview: Springfield Central State High School offers Sports Scholarships to support students demonstrating excellence in sporting pursuits. The scholarships recognise students who display outstanding athletic ability and commitment to sport.
description: Springfield Central State High School awards two Sports Scholarships annually to students who demonstrate excellence in a sporting area. The scholarships support students entering Year 7 and are awarded to those who show outstanding athletic performance and potential. Recipients are expected to represent the school at interschool and representative levels while maintaining academic standards.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstration of excellence in a sporting area; Outstanding athletic ability; Open to Year 7 entry

overview: St Benedict's College offers annual scholarships recognising the high accomplishments and holistic formation of students as they progress through the College. The scholarship programme rewards and supports students' commitment to their holistic education across academic, co-curricular and community dimensions.
description: St Benedict's College's scholarship programme recognises students who demonstrate high academic achievement, excellence in co-curricular involvement, community contribution, and exemplify the College's values of Service, Balance and Community. The financial value of scholarships is not publicly specified on the official webpage, though scholarships are merit-based and awarded annually to current students or those with confirmed enrolment. Selection is based on consistently high academic achievement, excellent work ethic as demonstrated in school reports, excellent behaviour and attendance, active participation in co-curricular activities, active community involvement, and participation in College functions and events. Applicants must complete an application form addressing selection criteria with supporting evidence including the latest school report card. Scholarship holders may reapply annually, and scholarships are subject to conditions including maintaining attendance, enrolment compliance, good academic progress, and continued involvement with the College.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled at the College or confirmed enrolment for following year; Consistently high academic application and achievement; Excellent work ethic as demonstrated in school reports; Excellent behaviour and attendance; Actively participating in co-curricular activities; Actively participating in the community; Actively involved in College functions and events; Qualities exhibited represent the ethos and values of St Benedict's College of Service, Balance and Community
special_conditions: Scholarship is dependent on meeting conditions of all College expectations. Continued involvement, representation and participation for the College is required. Scholarship could be withdrawn during the year if conditions of attendance, enrolment compliance and good academic progress are not adhered to. Students can reapply for a scholarship for the following year by the date set. Evidence is reviewed by the selection panel with the final decision resting with the Principal.

overview: St Benedict's College operates the Honoratus Gifted and Talented Program, an extracurricular offering that provides rigorous, relevant and engaging learning opportunities for gifted and talented students. The programme develops individual learning needs, strengths, interests and goals through a variety of specialised streams.
description: The Honoratus program comprises multiple extracurricular streams delivered by enthusiastic teachers skilled in providing meaningful experiences for gifted and talented students. Students are selected to participate by invitation or audition based on a variety of factors including overall academic excellence, high scores on formal tests, special interests, work ethic, teacher recommendation, and relevant skills. Each Honoratus stream provides students with access to specific activities requiring critical and creative thinking, problem-solving, and development of responses and dispositions in both individual and collaborative contexts. The programme is designed to challenge gifted students in new and creative ways, extending their learning beyond standard curriculum offerings.
eligibility_criteria: Overall academic excellence; High scores on a range of formal tests; Special interests and demonstrated skills; Strong work ethic; Teacher recommendation; Selection by invitation or audition depending on the specific stream
special_conditions: Selection is by invitation or audition based on demonstrated excellence and skill in relevant areas. Participation is extracurricular and requires commitment from students.

school_name: St Catherine's Catholic College The Whitsundays
overview: St Catherine's Catholic College recognises QATSIF Scholarship awardees as part of its Year 12 leadership cohort. The scholarship identifies and supports students demonstrating exceptional achievement and leadership.
description: St Catherine's Catholic College awards QATSIF Scholarships to recognise outstanding student achievement. QATSIF Scholarship recipients are formally inducted alongside College Captains and House Leaders as part of the College's leadership recognition ceremony. The scholarship supports secondary students who demonstrate excellence in their academic pursuits and engagement with the school community. Recipients are acknowledged publicly at the College's annual leadership induction ceremony, reflecting the significance of their achievement within the St Catherine's community.
eligibility_criteria: Secondary students; Demonstrated academic excellence; Leadership qualities; Selection by College leadership
special_conditions: Selection and award is at the discretion of the College Principal and leadership team.

school_name: St Catherine's Catholic College The Whitsundays
overview: St Catherine's Catholic College offers a Sports Excellence Program to secondary students seeking to extend their sporting achievements. The program provides training, support and guidance to help students achieve their sporting goals.
description: The Sports Excellence Program is an elite selective club offered to secondary students during their secondary years at St Catherine's Catholic College. The program is designed to help students achieve existing sporting goals and provide support for them to reach new achievements. Students in the program participate in focused training designed to improve fitness using a variety of training methods, learn about the physiology of their bodies, and are guided to set sporting goals. The program includes motivation from guest speakers and high-achieving athletes. Students can re-apply each year and the program provides support in training, fitness, and nutrition.
eligibility_criteria: Secondary students; Very interested in extending sporting achievements; Demonstrated sporting commitment; Must complete application form and interview
special_conditions: The selection process includes students filling in an application form and then going through an interview process. Candidates will then be selected from there. Students can re-apply each year for the Sports Excellence Program.

program_name: SBC Old Boys Foundation Scholarship - Academic
overview: St Brendan's College offers the SBC Old Boys Foundation Scholarship to retain exceptional students who demonstrate outstanding academic prowess. The scholarship is assessed based on school report results and is intended to recognise and support academic excellence.
description: The SBC Old Boys Foundation Scholarship is one of four distinct categories offered by the Foundation. Academic scholarships are assessed based on school report results and cover a portion of fees for the academic year. Applications are meticulously processed by the SBC Old Boys Foundation Board of Directors, ensuring a thorough and fair evaluation of each applicant's achievements and potential. The exact financial value and number of awards are not specified on the website. Applicants must submit a comprehensive written application in the form of a resume, including a letter of no more than two pages introducing the candidate and outlining their qualities, achievements, and aspirations.
eligibility_criteria: Current students at St Brendan's College; Assessed based on school report results; Outstanding academic ability required
special_conditions: The decision of the Foundation regarding the awarding of scholarships is final. Scholarships are offered across four distinct categories recognising diverse talents.

program_name: SBC Old Boys Foundation Scholarship - Sport
overview: St Brendan's College offers the SBC Old Boys Foundation Scholarship for sport to retain exceptional students who excel in athletic endeavours. The scholarship recognises verifiable achievement at regional, State, or National level.
description: The SBC Old Boys Foundation Scholarship for sport is assessed based on verifiable achievement at regional, State, or National level. Preference is given to those excelling in multiple disciplines. Applications are meticulously processed by the SBC Old Boys Foundation Board of Directors, ensuring a thorough and fair evaluation of each applicant's achievements and potential. The scholarship covers a portion of fees for the academic year. Applicants must submit a comprehensive written application in the form of a resume, including a letter of no more than two pages introducing the candidate and outlining their qualities, achievements, and aspirations.
eligibility_criteria: Current students at St Brendan's College; Verifiable achievement at regional, State, or National level in sport; Preference given to students excelling in multiple sporting disciplines
special_conditions: The decision of the Foundation regarding the awarding of scholarships is final. Scholarships are offered across four distinct categories recognising diverse talents.

program_name: SBC Old Boys Foundation Scholarship - Music
overview: St Brendan's College offers the SBC Old Boys Foundation Scholarship for music to retain exceptional students who excel in musical performance. The scholarship is assessed on Australian Music Education Board results or equivalent performance results.
description: The SBC Old Boys Foundation Scholarship for music is one of four distinct categories recognised by the Foundation. The scholarship is assessed on Australian Music Education Board results or equivalent performance results. Applications are meticulously processed by the SBC Old Boys Foundation Board of Directors, ensuring a thorough and fair evaluation of each applicant's achievements and potential. The scholarship covers a portion of fees for the academic year. Applicants must submit a comprehensive written application in the form of a resume, including a letter of no more than two pages introducing the candidate and outlining their qualities, achievements, and aspirations.
eligibility_criteria: Current students at St Brendan's College; Australian Music Education Board results or equivalent performance results required; Musical excellence in performance
special_conditions: The decision of the Foundation regarding the awarding of scholarships is final. Scholarships are offered across four distinct categories recognising diverse talents.

program_name: SBC Old Boys Foundation Scholarship - Leadership
overview: St Brendan's College offers the SBC Old Boys Foundation Scholarship for leadership to retain exceptional students who demonstrate outstanding leadership qualities. The scholarship is assessed based on school report results.
description: The SBC Old Boys Foundation Scholarship for leadership is one of four distinct categories offered by the Foundation. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional leadership qualities, assessed based on school report results. Applications are meticulously processed by the SBC Old Boys Foundation Board of Directors, ensuring a thorough and fair evaluation of each applicant's achievements and potential. The scholarship covers a portion of fees for the academic year. Applicants must submit a comprehensive written application in the form of a resume, including a letter of no more than two pages introducing the candidate and outlining their qualities, achievements, and aspirations.
eligibility_criteria: Current students at St Brendan's College; Assessed based on school report results; Demonstrated leadership qualities and potential
special_conditions: The decision of the Foundation regarding the awarding of scholarships is final. Scholarships are offered across four distinct categories recognising diverse talents.

overview: St Brendan's College is committed to providing financial assistance through the Edmund Rice Bursary, a vital support structure for eligible students. The bursary is designed to address demonstrated financial need and support families experiencing financial hardship.
description: The Edmund Rice Bursary at St Brendan's College is tailored to address unique financial situations of families through a comprehensive formula. Unlike a one-size-fits-all approach, the bursaries take into account various factors, including family circumstances such as single or dual-parent households, the number of children, employment status, and housing costs. Applications are confidentially processed by the Business Manager against specific criteria and treated with the utmost confidentiality. The exact financial value varies based on individual family circumstances. Applicants must apply for each academic year; applications are not sequential and students are required to reapply annually. The supporting documentation must be compiled for thorough evaluation.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated financial need; Family circumstances considered including household type, number of children, employment status, and housing costs; Annual reapplication required; All students eligible
special_conditions: Applicants must apply for each academic year. Applications are not sequential and students are required to reapply annually. Information is reviewed exclusively by the Business Manager and Finance Staff and treated with the utmost confidentiality. Supporting documentation is required for thorough evaluation of applications.

overview: St Columban's College offers the Academic Excellence Program to recognise and support students demonstrating exceptional academic achievement. The program provides merit-based support for high-achieving students entering Year 7 who perform strongly on the ACER scholarship assessment.
description: The Academic Excellence Program is designed to reward students with outstanding academic ability and potential. Students must sit the ACER Scholarship Test to qualify for consideration of both full and partial scholarships for entry into Year 7. The program recognizes student achievement through testing that assesses acquired knowledge and intellectual ability. The financial support provided may include full or partial fee remission, though specific percentage amounts are not detailed on the school's public pages. Students selected for the program are expected to maintain high academic standards throughout their enrolment at the College.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must sit the ACER Scholarship Test for entry assessment; Demonstrated exceptional academic ability
special_conditions: Scholarship testing is conducted via ACER. Specific application dates vary by year and should be confirmed directly with the College's enrolments office.

overview: St Columban's College offers the Music Excellence Program to support musically talented students. The program recognizes students with demonstrated musical ability and provides support for their participation in the College's music offerings.
description: The Music Excellence Program supports students with exceptional musical talent. The College mentions a Music Excellence Program as part of its award-winning student success pathways. Music students at the College participate in instrumental tuition programs with qualified tutors, and those accepted into the Academy of Music Excellence (AME) program receive tuition fees covered on one auditioned instrument. The program includes participation in school music ensembles, performances, and music-making activities. Duration extends throughout enrolment at the College, subject to maintaining participation standards.
eligibility_criteria: Musical talent demonstrated through audition; Commitment to music education at the College; Participation in school music ensembles expected
special_conditions: Academy of Music Excellence (AME) participants receive tuition fee coverage for one auditioned instrument. Any additional instrumental music lessons are charged at regular rates. Participation in school ensembles and music-making activities is required.

overview: St Columban's College offers the Sporting Excellence Program to identify and support athletes demonstrating exceptional sporting talent. The program recognizes students with elite athletic ability and potential for high-level competition.
description: The Sporting Excellence Program supports students with demonstrated sporting talent and potential. As part of the College's award-winning student success pathways, the program includes Sports Excellence Pathways and Mentor Programs that have historically supported students achieving outstanding athletic outcomes at state and national levels. The College has a strong sporting tradition and competes across multiple sports including rugby union, netball, athletics, touch football, and many others through the TAS Competition. Specific financial benefits and selection criteria for the program are not detailed on publicly available pages. The program operates throughout a student's enrolment, subject to maintaining sporting performance and standards.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated sporting excellence and talent; Eligibility to meet entrance criteria for the School; Expected to participate at high level in school sports
special_conditions: Students must demonstrate sporting excellence and commitment to participating at high level in school sports programs. Program is subject to application/audition as required and participation in sporting programs at the College.

overview: The MacKillop Catholic School Access Fund provides financial assistance to families experiencing significant hardship who wish to access Catholic education at St Edward the Confessor School. This bursary is available for new student applications and is administered through the Catholic Foundation on behalf of the Archdiocese of Brisbane.
description: The MacKillop Catholic School Access Fund (MacKillop Fund) provides financial bursary support for families living within the Archdiocese of Brisbane who are experiencing significant financial hardship. The bursary covers tuition fees and other school-related costs including camps for each year of primary school enrolment. The school provides all stationery, texts, and resource materials, including a laptop if required and one full set of uniforms (excluding hat and shoes). Families who receive the bursary are required to negotiate a weekly financial contribution agreement with the school. Applications must be submitted by 15 April for enrolment in the following school year, with the committee meeting in May to award bursaries. The bursary is available to new students only.
eligibility_criteria: Family must demonstrate significant financial hardship (holding a healthcare card or equivalent documentation); Family must be supportive of Catholic education ethos, values and mission; Family must reside within the Archdiocese of Brisbane; Application must be submitted by 15 April for the following school year; Open to Prep to Year 6 students
special_conditions: This bursary is available to new students only and must be applied for in the year before enrolment commences. Supporting financial documentation is necessary before any expression of interest form can be processed. Special consideration may be given to students who are socially, culturally, spiritually or emotionally disadvantaged. Funding is subject to availability and late applications may be considered at the next disbursement if funds are not immediately available. Parents provide a weekly financial contribution in an agreement negotiated with the school.

overview: St Elizabeth's School provides fee assistance to families experiencing genuine financial hardship. The school reviews all concession applications annually and considers each case individually based on demonstrated need.
description: St Elizabeth's School offers fee concessions and financial assistance to families experiencing genuine financial hardship. All concessions are reviewed annually and families experiencing hardship should address their situation to the School Principal in the first instance. The school notes that it hopes all families will make a concerted effort to meet their financial obligations, but genuine cases of hardship will be considered. The amount of assistance varies depending on individual circumstances and the school's capacity to provide support. Families may request support at any time during the school year by contacting the principal.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all families experiencing genuine financial hardship; Must contact the School Principal to request consideration; Cases reviewed annually
special_conditions: All genuine cases of hardship will be considered. Concessions are reviewed annually and should be addressed to the School Principal. The school encourages all families to make a concerted effort to meet their financial obligations.

program_name: Financial Hardship Concession (Means-Tested Pension and Health Care Card Holders)
overview: St Augustine's School offers automatic tuition fee concessions to families in genuine financial difficulty who hold means-tested pension and health care cards. This program reflects the school's commitment to Gospel values and ensures no child is refused a Catholic education due to financial hardship.
description: St Augustine's School provides automatic financial assistance for eligible families experiencing genuine financial difficulty. The program offers a 70% tuition fee discount (does not apply to levies) for holders of means-tested pension and health care cards. Families who do not hold a concession card but are experiencing financial difficulty are encouraged to discuss fee concessions with the school principal to apply for special consideration. Applications are treated with understanding, sensitivity and confidentiality. The school operates on the principle that in keeping with Gospel values, no child will be refused a Catholic education due to genuine financial difficulty.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold means-tested pension and health care cards; OR must be experiencing genuine financial difficulty; OR be in need of fee concession consideration; May apply for special consideration with school principal
special_conditions: Automatic discount applies to holders of means-tested pension and health care cards. Discount does not apply to school levies, only tuition fees. Families without concession cards may request special consideration by discussing with the school principal. All discussions are treated confidentially.

program_name: Family Discount (Multiple Children in Catholic Schools)
overview: St Augustine's School offers family discounts for families with more than one child attending Catholic schools in the Diocese of Cairns. This program recognizes the financial impact of educating multiple children in Catholic education.
description: St Augustine's School provides family discounts applicable to families with more than one child attending Catholic schools within the Diocese of Cairns. For example, if one child attends St Augustine's School, Mossman and another child attends one of Cairns Catholic Education's secondary colleges, the family is eligible for a family discount on tuition fees at both institutions. This discount is designed to make Catholic education more affordable for families managing multiple school fees.
eligibility_criteria: Must have more than one child; Children must be attending different Catholic schools in the Diocese of Cairns; Examples include one child at St Augustine's Mossman and another at a secondary college in Cairns Diocese
special_conditions: Discount applies to families with multiple children in Catholic schools across the Diocese of Cairns. Discount percentage varies and families should contact the school for specific discount amounts applicable to their situation.

overview: St Augustine's School, as a school in a rural location with limited access to Catholic secondary education, offers an additional country school discount to support families in the region. This program recognizes the geographic disadvantage of the Mossman area.
description: St Augustine's School attracts an additional Country School Discount as it is located in a rural area with limited access to Catholic secondary colleges. This discount is designed to make Catholic education more affordable for families in the Mossman region who have limited alternatives for secondary Catholic education. The discount applies on top of other available discounts and is recognition of the school's role in providing accessible Catholic education to the rural Far North Queensland community.
eligibility_criteria: Must be enrolled at or applying to St Augustine's School Mossman; Must reside in the rural Mossman area with limited access to Catholic secondary colleges
special_conditions: Discount is automatic for students at St Augustine's School Mossman due to the school's rural location. Exact discount percentage should be confirmed with the school office. May be combined with other available discounts.

program_name: School Fee Discounts for Financial Hardship
overview: St Francis Xavier's School offers tuition fee discounts to families experiencing genuine financial difficulties. The school recognises the importance of educational access and works confidentially with families to arrange fee assistance based on demonstrated financial need.
description: St Francis Xavier's School provides tuition fee discounts available to holders of means tested Health Care and Pensioner Concession Cards, and to families experiencing genuine financial difficulties. The discount amount varies and is determined on an individual basis through confidential consultation with the school's Finance Officer. Families experiencing financial hardship are encouraged to contact the school's Finance Officer to arrange an interview that will be handled with confidentiality and sensitivity. Those families who have Health Care Concession (HCC) cards must submit a new form at the beginning of every year. The school recognises that financial circumstances can change and reviews applications individually.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing genuine financial difficulties; Holders of means tested Health Care Concession Cards; Holders of Pensioner Concession Cards; Requires interview with Finance Officer; Assessed on individual basis
special_conditions: Applications require confidential interview with Finance Officer. New HCC card holders must submit form at the beginning of each year. Payment plans available via monthly or fortnightly instalments including credit card and direct debit options.

overview: St Francis Xavier's School offers tuition fee discounts for families with multiple children enrolled in Catholic schools within the Cairns Diocese. This program recognises the financial burden of educating multiple children and provides automatic fee relief for eligible families.
description: St Francis Xavier's School provides automatic tuition fee discounts to families when there are two or more students enrolled in Catholic Primary and/or Secondary schools in the Cairns Diocese. The discount amount is set by Catholic Education Services Cairns and applied to eligible families' accounts. The scheme operates automatically once enrolment is confirmed for multiple siblings across the diocesan school system. Discounts are applied quarterly when accounts are issued. Payment plans can be arranged through the Finance Officer for greater flexibility in managing school fees.
eligibility_criteria: Two or more students enrolled in Catholic Primary and/or Secondary schools; Enrollment must be in the Cairns Diocese; Automatically applied upon confirmation of multiple enrolments
special_conditions: Applies only when two or more students are enrolled in Catholic Primary and/or Secondary schools within the Cairns Diocese. Discounts are set by Catholic Education Services Cairns and applied quarterly.

program_name: Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund Bursary
overview: St Francis College supports families experiencing extreme financial hardship through the Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund. This means-tested bursary is designed to provide Catholic education access to children whose families otherwise could not afford tuition.
description: The Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund is a needs-based bursary program administered through the Catholic Foundation that provides financial assistance to eligible families. The bursary covers tuition fees for either primary school or secondary school, textbooks, uniforms and local excursions. The fund is designed for families experiencing extreme hardship, trauma and financial devastation who share a deep desire for their child to receive a Catholic education. The bursary commitment extends through the completion of the student's current school stage - primary school students are funded through Year 6, and secondary students through Year 12, provided the family continues to demonstrate financial need. Applications are made through the Catholic Foundation and the selection committee meets in May each year to award bursaries for the following school year. The application deadline is 15 April, though late applications may be considered depending on funding availability.
eligibility_criteria: Students must be new to Brisbane Catholic Education schools or currently seeking initial enrolment; Families must demonstrate extreme financial hardship, trauma or financial devastation; Primary school students must be entering Year 1-6; Secondary students must be entering Year 7-12; Students currently enrolled in a Brisbane Catholic Education school are not eligible to continue receiving the bursary at the same school; Families must demonstrate a genuine desire for child to receive Catholic education
year_levels: Year 1, Year 2, Year 3, Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: The bursary is means-tested and subject to annual review by the school principal. The Mary MacKillop Fund commits to funding a child through the completion of the school stage in which they are enrolled. Applications are administered by the Catholic Foundation, not the school directly. A copy of a health care card will be requested as part of the application verification process. Brisbane Catholic Education maintains privacy of bursary recipients.

school_name: St Francis Xavier Catholic Primary School
overview: St Francis Xavier Catholic Primary School provides a 70% tuition fee discount for families holding Australian Government Health Care and Pensioner Concession cards. This financial assistance program supports families experiencing financial hardship and ensures access to Catholic education.
description: A 70% Tuition Fee Discount is available for families with 'means-tested' Australian Government Health Care and Pensioner Concession cards. These cards must meet eligibility criteria for this concession to apply. The discount applies directly to tuition fees, reducing the annual education cost significantly for eligible families. This is a diocesan policy-based concession provided by the Diocese of Rockhampton Catholic schools system. Eligible families must provide valid concession card documentation to the school administration.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold a valid Australian Government Health Care Concession Card or Pensioner Concession Card; Card must meet eligibility criteria for the concession; Application to school with documentation required
special_conditions: Concession cards must meet eligibility criteria to apply. Review occurs as per diocesan policy requirements.

school_name: St Francis Xavier Catholic Primary School
overview: St Francis Xavier Catholic Primary School offers Principal's Concessions to new or existing parents experiencing financial hardship. This flexible fee assistance program is handled confidentially by the school principal and is reviewed annually to ensure equitable support.
description: New or existing parents who are experiencing financial hardship, and may or may not hold a concession card, are able to discuss alternate fee discounts directly with the school principal, who will treat the situation confidentially. The principal has discretionary authority to approve fee reductions or exemptions for families in genuine financial difficulty. Diocesan policy states that exemptions are made and reviewed every 12 months, ensuring ongoing assessment of family circumstances. This confidential approach allows families to access support without stigma or public disclosure of their financial situation.
eligibility_criteria: Experiencing documented financial hardship; New or existing parents eligible; No requirement to hold concession card; Confidential discussion required with principal
special_conditions: Application by confidential discussion with school principal. Exemptions and fee reductions are reviewed and renewed every 12 months. Principal has discretionary authority to determine assistance level based on individual family circumstances.

school_name: St Francis Xavier Catholic Primary School
overview: St Francis Xavier Catholic Primary School provides extensions and adjustments for students identified as gifted and talented. The school offers differentiated learning opportunities, enrichment activities, and educational modifications to support advanced learners.
description: Students identified as gifted and talented receive adjustments and modifications made in consultation with classroom teachers to extend and support their learning. The school provides extracurricular activities that extend students including sport, music, Battle of the Brains lunchtime activities, Maths Teams Challenge and various academic competitions and enrichment programs throughout the year. The Inclusive Curriculum Program coordinates with classroom teachers to ensure appropriate challenge and extension for gifted learners. These programs aim to develop higher-order thinking skills, problem-solving abilities, and creative application of knowledge beyond the standard curriculum.
eligibility_criteria: Identified as gifted and talented by school staff; Demonstrated advanced academic ability; Referral by classroom teacher or parent consultation
special_conditions: Identification through school-based assessment and observation. Program participation is based on demonstrated need and teacher recommendation. Modifications are made in consultation with teachers and parents.

overview: St James College offers the Bursary Scholarship Program to support students who would otherwise be unable to access a Catholic education at the college. The program recognises students who have faced significant barriers in life and financially lack access to an Edmund Rice Education Australia Catholic education, providing financial assistance to enable participation.
description: The St James College Bursary Scholarship Program was established in 2019 through the Catholic Foundation and the Archdiocese of Brisbane to support students experiencing financial hardship. Applications are welcome from all students including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students who are encouraged to apply. The program provides financial assistance through a perpetual endowment fund supported by individual donors, alumni, and college friends. Students awarded a Bursary Scholarship must demonstrate consistent application to their studies and co-curricular participation, and must be willing to enrich the college community. Administrative assistance, one-on-one support and translation services are provided on-site to help families complete application forms. The specific level of financial support varies based on individual circumstances and funding available.
eligibility_criteria: Students who would otherwise be unable to access a St James College education; Must have demonstrated consistent application to studies and co-curricular participation; Must show evidence of significant barriers and financial need; Open to Year 5, Year 7, and Year 10 entry points; Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students are especially encouraged to apply
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients must enrich the community and culture of the college by immersing themselves in educational pathway opportunities at St James College to realise their potential. Administrative assistance, one-on-one support and translation services can be provided on-site at the College to help families complete application forms. Interested applicants should lodge an expression of interest in Semester One each year for commencement in the following year.

overview: St John Fisher College offers the Bursary Fund to support students whose families are experiencing financial hardship. This initiative, established by Year 9 students in 2019, provides financial assistance to help students start or continue their education at the College.
description: The St John Fisher Bursary Fund was established in 2019 by a group of Year 9 students in collaboration with the College Leadership Team to create educational access for families experiencing financial hardship. The Fund provides varying levels of financial assistance based on demonstrated financial need. Distributions from the Fund help qualifying students to start or continue their schooling at St John Fisher College. A dedicated Bursary Committee has stewardship over all disbursements and is charged with the responsibility of awarding bursaries to students with the greatest financial need. The Fund is administered through the Catholic Foundation in partnership with the College and receives donations from the community to expand its capacity to support deserving students.
eligibility_criteria: Students whose families are experiencing financial hardship; Open to current and prospective students; Demonstrated financial need; Eligibility assessed on individual basis
special_conditions: Awards are determined by a Bursary Committee based on demonstrated financial need. The Fund accepts donations and operates through the Catholic Foundation. Inquiries and applications can be made through the College's Enrolments Office.

overview: St John's Catholic Primary School, as part of Catholic Education – Diocese of Rockhampton, offers a Family Discount to families with multiple children enrolled at CEDR schools. This systemic program recognises the financial commitment families make when supporting multiple children's education.
description: The Family Discount is applied to the All-Inclusive Education fee of all siblings attending CEDR schools. The discount structure provides: 2 siblings attending CEDR schools receive a 7.5% discount applied to the All-Inclusive Education fee of all siblings; 3 siblings attending CEDR schools receive a 20% discount applied to the All-Inclusive Education fee of all siblings; 4 or more siblings attending CEDR schools receive a 30% discount applied to the All-Inclusive Education fee of all siblings. Family discounts are applied for each year by completing the Confirmation of Enrolment form issued to all families. This discount applies specifically to the All-Inclusive Education fee component of school fees and is available to all eligible families without need to demonstrate financial hardship.
eligibility_criteria: 2 or more siblings enrolled at Catholic Education – Diocese of Rockhampton schools; Families must complete Confirmation of Enrolment form annually
special_conditions: Discount is applied for each year. Families must complete the Confirmation of Enrolment form issued to all families annually to maintain eligibility. This is a systemic CEDR program available across all Diocese of Rockhampton schools.

overview: St John's Catholic Primary School, as part of Catholic Education – Diocese of Rockhampton, offers a Concession Card Discount to families who hold a government-issued means-tested concession card. This program provides financial support to eligible families demonstrating financial need.
description: A Concession Card Discount is offered to families who hold a 'means tested' Government Health Care or Pensioner Concession card. These cards must meet eligibility criteria for this concession to apply. The discount is applied to the All-Inclusive Education fee and recognises families facing financial hardship. This is a means-tested financial assistance program administered at the diocesan level and available to eligible families across all CEDR schools. Families holding eligible concession cards should contact the school to confirm their eligibility and arrange the discount application.
eligibility_criteria: Families must hold a valid 'means tested' Government Health Care Concession card or Pensioner Concession card; Card must meet specified eligibility criteria; Open to all enrolled students
special_conditions: Concession card must be current and meet eligibility criteria as determined by the Diocese of Rockhampton. This is a systemic CEDR program. Families should contact the school directly to arrange and verify eligibility for the discount.

overview: St John's Catholic Primary School offers practical assistance to families experiencing financial difficulty in providing prescribed school uniforms. Parents are invited to discuss assistance options directly with the principal.
description: Where there is financial difficulty for parents to provide the prescribed school uniform, parents are invited to discuss practical assistance with the principal. This informal support program recognises that uniform costs can present a barrier to some families' ability to send their children to school fully uniformed. The school takes a pastoral approach to uniform hardship, working directly with families to find practical solutions. This assistance is provided at the school level through the principal's office and does not require formal application. Families experiencing difficulty with uniform costs should contact the principal's office to discuss available options.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial difficulty providing prescribed school uniform; Open to all year levels; Direct discussion with principal required
special_conditions: This is an informal, pastoral support program administered by the principal. Families must discuss their circumstances directly with the principal. The nature and extent of assistance is determined on a case-by-case basis.

school_name: St John's Anglican College - Alpine Place Campus
overview: St John's Anglican College offers Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding academic achievement in incoming Year 7 and Year 10 students. The scholarships recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning, with recipients expected to participate in the broader extracurricular life of the College.
description: Academic Scholarships are awarded to students who demonstrate outstanding academic abilities and reflect the values of St John's Anglican College. The scholarship provides up to 75% fee remission of tuition fees. Selection is based on a comprehensive process including results from the Edutest examination, a detailed portfolio review, and a formal interview with the Principal or their designated representative. Scholarships are reviewed regularly and continue to be subject to maintenance of high academic standards and adherence to College policies and procedures. Scholarships apply to tuition fees only and do not cover uniforms, textbooks, stationery, extracurricular activities, camps, or other general expenses.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or Year 10; Must be Australian Citizens or Permanent Residents; Must demonstrate high academic achievement (top 10% on national level); Candidates should have strong NAPLAN results; Must participate in extracurricular life of the College; Shortlisted applicants must attend interview with parents; Late applications will not be accepted
special_conditions: Scholarship holders are expected to continue developing their talents, represent the College with pride and integrity, actively participate in all aspects of College life, and embody the values and spirit of the community. Scholarships are reviewed regularly to celebrate ongoing achievement. Scholarship holders must maintain high academic standards and adhere to College policies and procedures. Scholarships for 2026 have already been awarded; applications for 2027 entry open Term 4 2025. Portfolios must be submitted with Edutest registration and will not be accepted on the day of testing.

school_name: St John's Anglican College - Alpine Place Campus
overview: St John's Anglican College offers Sporting Scholarships through the RISE program, a talent identification and development program for students who excel in any sport. The program seeks to attract and mentor young athletes who are capable of competing at representative level on behalf of the College.
description: The RISE (Representing, Inspiring, Succeeding, Excellence) Sporting Scholarship is a talent and identification development program seeking students who excel in any sport to be mentored and developed through representative pathways and within The Associated Schools (TAS) sports competition. Scholarships provide up to 75% fee remission of tuition fees and are available for students commencing Years 7 and 10. The program focuses on early identification and nurturing of young athletes from a variety of backgrounds, providing opportunities for advanced skills development. Selection is based on physical sports testing and assessment of sporting capability. Scholarships are reviewed each semester and are subject to continued excellence in sports performance and adherence to College policies and procedures.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or Year 10; Must be Australian Citizens or Permanent Residents; Must excel in any sport; Must demonstrate capability for competing at representative level; Must have desire and willingness to participate in wide range of co-curricular activities; Subject to continued excellence in sports performance and adherence to College policies
special_conditions: Scholarships are reviewed each semester and are subject to continued excellence in sports performance and adherence to the College policies and procedures. Scholarship holders are expected to represent the College as appropriate, participate fully in all College programs, and uphold College values and expectations. The awarding and continuation of a scholarship is at the discretion of the Principal. Scholarships apply to tuition fees only and do not cover uniforms, textbooks, stationery, extracurricular activities, camps, or other general expenses.

school_name: St John's Anglican College - Alpine Place Campus
overview: St John's Anglican College offers Performing Arts Scholarships to high-achieving students in vocal, instrumental, drama, and dance pursuits. The scholarships recognise excellence in performing arts and provide opportunities within the college's state-of-the-art Performing Arts Centre of Excellence.
description: Performing Arts Scholarships are awarded to students demonstrating exceptional ability and achievement in music (vocal and instrumental), drama, or dance. The scholarship provides up to 75% fee remission of tuition fees for students commencing Years 7 and 10. Selection is audition-based, with shortlisted applicants invited to attend an interview together with their families with the Principal or their delegate. Scholarships are awarded based on the audition result, portfolio review, and interview performance. The scholarships provide access to the college's purpose-built Auditorium, drama rooms, and recording studio for rehearsal and performance. Scholarships are reviewed each semester and are subject to continued excellence in academic performance and adherence to College policies and procedures.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or Year 10; Must be Australian Citizens or Permanent Residents; Must demonstrate high achievement in vocal or instrumental music, drama, or dance; Must submit portfolio of evidence; Shortlisted applicants must attend interview with parents; Must maintain academic standards and adhere to College policies
special_conditions: Performing Arts scholarships are audition-based. Shortlisted applicants will be invited, together with their families, for an interview with the Principal or their delegate. Scholarships are awarded at the sole discretion of the Principal. Scholarships will be reviewed each semester and are subject to continued excellence in academic performance and adherence to College policies and procedures. Scholarship holders must represent the College appropriately, participate fully in all College programs, and uphold College values and expectations. Scholarships apply to tuition fees only and do not cover uniforms, textbooks, stationery, extracurricular activities, camps, or other general expenses.

school_name: St John's Anglican College - Alpine Place Campus
overview: St John's Anglican College offers Home Boarding Rural Scholarships to students from regional and rural Australia who live more than 200km away from the College. The scholarship provides support for students commencing in Year 7 or Year 10 who wish to access St John's education through the College's home boarding program with trusted homestay families.
description: The Home Boarding Rural Scholarship is available for students who live more than 200km away from the College and are commencing in Year 7 or Year 10. Successful applicants are expected to demonstrate a desire and willingness to participate in a wide range of co-curricular activities outside of the academic program of the College. The scholarship provides support for home boarding through the college's long-established homestay program, which has been operating for over 20 years. St John's Home Boarding offers flexibility with weekly or term-time private boarding options with trusted homestay families. The program fosters independence, resilience, personal growth, and lifelong friendships while students maintain the option to return home on weekends. A $290 private boarding placement fee is charged per student in addition to fees, and $190 is charged if private boarding relocation is required.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or Year 10; Must live more than 200km away from the College; Must be Australian Citizens or Permanent Residents; Must demonstrate desire and willingness to participate in wide range of co-curricular activities; Must meet academic and behavioural standards
special_conditions: A $290 private boarding placement fee is charged per student, in addition to the above fees. If a private boarding relocation is required, $190 will be charged. The Home Boarding Scholarship is available through the college's established homestay program with trusted families. Students must be willing to participate in co-curricular activities and maintain academic standards. The scholarship allows students to return home on weekends. Home Boarding is available for students from Years 6 to 12, but scholarship is only for Years 7 and 10 entry.

overview: St John's offers Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding academic achievement. These scholarships recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic abilities and reflect the values of St John's Anglican College. Recipients are expected to participate in the broader extracurricular life of the College.
description: Academic Scholarships are awarded to students who demonstrate outstanding academic abilities and reflect the values of St John's Anglican College. Scholarships provide partial remission of tuition fees up to 75%. Selection is based on results of the Edutest Scholarship Exam for Academic, a comprehensive portfolio assessment, and an interview with the Principal. Academic testing takes place at St John's Anglican College Secondary Campus during Term 1 2026. Shortlisted applicants, accompanied by one or both parents, will be invited to an interview. Scholarships are reviewed each semester and subject to continued excellence in academic performance and adherence to College policies. Recipients must maintain development in their scholarship pursuit, represent the College appropriately, and participate fully in all College programs while upholding College values and expectations.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or Year 10; Australian Citizens or Permanent Residents only; Must meet minimum academic requirements as outlined in portfolio submission; Online applications via Edutest open during Term 4 2025 for 2027 entry; Non-enrolled applicants must complete St John's enrolment application and pay $220 application fee
special_conditions: Scholarships are free of means test and available to Australian Citizens or Permanent Residents only. Late applications will not be accepted. All applicants must submit a named portfolio when registering with Edutest. All supporting materials must be received by the closing date to be considered. Scholarships will be reviewed each semester and are subject to continued excellence in academic performance and adherence to College policies and procedures. Not all students who attend an interview will be awarded a scholarship.

overview: RISE is St John's sporting development program offering scholarships to talented athletes. This talent identification and development program seeks students who excel in any sport to be mentored and developed, providing opportunities through representative sport pathways and participation in The Associated Schools (TAS) competition.
description: The RISE Sporting Scholarship is a talent and identification development program seeking students who excel in any sport to be mentored and developed, providing opportunities through representative pathways and within The Associated Schools (TAS) sports competition. Scholarships are available for students commencing Years 7 and 10, providing part remission of tuition fees with a maximum offer of 75%. The program seeks to attract students capable of competing in a wide selection of sports on behalf of the College. Selection is based on a physical sports test and demonstrated athletic excellence. The program focuses on early identification and nurturing of young athletes from a variety of backgrounds, providing opportunities for advanced skills development. Scholarships are reviewed each semester and are subject to continued excellence in sports performance and adherence to College policies and procedures.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or Year 10; Australian Citizens or Permanent Residents only; Must demonstrate excellence in any sport; Non-enrolled applicants must complete St John's enrolment application and pay $220 application fee; Applicants must have satisfactory academic and behavioural records
special_conditions: Scholarships are free of means test and available to Australian Citizens or Permanent Residents only. Selection is based on physical sports test results. Scholarships will be reviewed each semester and are subject to continued excellence in sports performance and adherence to College policies and procedures. Scholarship holders are expected to maintain development in their sporting pursuit, represent the College as appropriate, and participate fully in all College programs while upholding College values and expectations.

overview: St John's offers Performing Arts Scholarships to recognise excellence in vocal and instrumental music. These scholarships are designed for high-achieving students who demonstrate notable talent in performing arts and wish to develop their skills within a supportive environment.
description: Performing Arts Scholarships are audition-based awards for students demonstrating excellence in vocal and instrumental pursuits. Scholarships are available for students commencing Years 7 and 10, providing part remission of tuition fees with a maximum offer of 75%. The St John's Performing Arts Centre of Excellence provides a purpose-built Auditorium, drama rooms, and recording studio offering fantastic opportunities for students to rehearse, perform, and forge industry links with national and international performances. Selection is based on audition performance, portfolio review, and interview with the Principal or delegate. Scholarships are reviewed each semester and are subject to continued excellence in academic performance and adherence to College policies. Scholars are awarded at the sole discretion of the Principal.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or Year 10; Australian Citizens or Permanent Residents only; Must demonstrate high-achieving performance in vocal and/or instrumental music; Non-enrolled applicants must complete St John's enrolment application and pay $220 application fee; Shortlisted applicants invited for interview with family
special_conditions: Scholarships are free of means test and available to Australian Citizens or Permanent Residents only. Performing Arts scholarships are audition-based. Shortlisted applicants will be invited, together with their families, for an interview with the Principal or delegate. Performing Arts scholarships are awarded based on audition, portfolio review, and interview. Scholarships are awarded at sole discretion of the Principal. Scholarships will be reviewed each semester and are subject to continued excellence in academic performance and adherence to College policies and procedures.

overview: St John's offers Home Boarding Rural Scholarships to support students from regional and rural Australia. This scholarship provides access to quality education for students who live more than 200km from the College, offering flexible boarding options with trusted homestay families.
description: A Home Boarding Scholarship is available for students who live more than 200km away from St John's Anglican College and are entering Year 7 or 10. The scholarship supports students from regional and rural Australia to access the College's educational program through flexible home boarding arrangements. St John's Home Boarding offers weekly or term-time private boarding with long-established and trusted homestay families for students from Years 6 to 12. The program provides opportunities to develop independence, resilience, and personal growth while fostering a sense of family and belonging. Successful applicants are expected to demonstrate a desire and willingness to participate in a wide range of co-curricular activities outside of the academic program. The scholarship is subject to annual review and continuation is based on satisfactory academic progress and adherence to College policies.
eligibility_criteria: Students must live more than 200km away from the College; Open to students entering Year 7 or Year 10; Australian Citizens or Permanent Residents only; Must demonstrate desire and willingness to participate in co-curricular activities; Must meet minimum academic and behavioural standards
special_conditions: Available for students living more than 200km from the College. A $290 private boarding placement fee is charged per student in addition to tuition fees. If a private boarding relocation is required, $190 will be charged. St John's Home Boarding operates with long-established and trusted homestay families. Successful applicants are expected to demonstrate a desire and willingness to participate in a wide range of co-curricular activities. Scholarship subject to annual review with continuation based on satisfactory academic progress and adherence to College policies.

overview: St John's College offers fee concessions to families experiencing financial hardship to ensure enrolment is not compromised. Concessions are assessed using the standard Brisbane Catholic Education poverty line model to ensure fairness across all schools.
description: St John's College provides financial assistance through fee concessions for families experiencing genuine financial hardship. Concession applications can be submitted at any stage through Terms 1, 2, and 3, with no applications accepted after the start of Term 4. Concessions are issued for a maximum period of 12 months within a calendar year, and consideration for a subsequent 12-month period requires a new application. The financial value of concessions varies depending on individual circumstances and is assessed using the standard Brisbane Catholic Education poverty line model. Families are encouraged to contact the Principal, Business Manager, or member of the finance team as early as possible to discuss their situation, with all contact remaining strictly confidential.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; Application must be made at any stage through Terms 1, 2, and 3 only; Concessions valid for maximum 12 months per calendar year; New application required for subsequent years
special_conditions: Concessions are assessed using the standard Brisbane Catholic Education poverty line model. All contact regarding concession applications is strictly confidential. Concessions are valid for one year only. No applications will be accepted after the start of Term 4.

program_name: Health Care Card and Pensioner Concession Card Tuition Fee Discount Scheme
overview: St Gerard Majella School offers a tuition fee discount scheme for families holding eligible Health Care and Pensioner Concession Cards. The school is committed to ensuring no Catholic child is denied a Catholic education due to financial difficulties.
description: St Gerard Majella School provides a 70% discount on tuition fees for account holders with eligible Health Care and Pensioner Concession Cards.
