Cardholders must complete the HCC Tuition Fee Discount Scheme Form and present their card in person at the school office prior to the term fees being processed. Generally, concession cards must be held in the name of the account holder. However, if the enrolled student holds a government-issued Health Care Card, carers may be eligible for a discount on fees if they receive a Carers Allowance from Centrelink. The concessions linked to government cards require an application form to be completed and submitted to the school office.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold eligible Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card; Card must be held in name of account holder or enrolled student must hold Health Care Card; Carers must receive Carers Allowance from Centrelink if claiming under student's card
special_conditions: Cardholders must present their card in person at the school office prior to term fees being processed. Concession cards must be held in the name of the account holder, or if the enrolled student holds a government-issued Health Care Card, carers may be eligible if they receive a Carers Allowance from Centrelink. Application form must be completed and submitted to school office for government card concessions.

overview: St John's School Roma offers the ICPA Scholarship to recognise and support student contributions to the school community through co-curricular activities. The scholarship acknowledges active participation in school activities including events such as Eisteddfod and Opti-MINDS competitions.
description: The ICPA Scholarship is valued at $500 and is awarded to students who have made significant contributions to the school community through multiple extra-curricular activities. The scholarship recognises outstanding participation in co-curricular programs and represents the school's commitment to supporting well-rounded student development. Selection is based on demonstrated involvement and contributions across various school activities. This scholarship acknowledges students who balance academic responsibilities with active community engagement.
eligibility_criteria: Current student at St John's School Roma; demonstrated involvement in multiple co-curricular activities; nominated by school staff for contributions to school community
special_conditions: Scholarship is awarded following nomination by school staff based on contributions to school community through co-curricular engagement.

overview: St Joseph's Catholic Primary School offers a Family Discount to families with multiple children enrolled across Catholic Education Diocese of Rockhampton schools. Families with 2 siblings attending CEDR schools receive a 7.5% discount on the All-Inclusive Education fee.
description: The Family Discount program provides financial assistance to families with 2 or more children attending Catholic Education – Diocese of Rockhampton (CEDR) schools. For families with exactly 2 siblings attending CEDR schools, a 7.5% discount is applied to the All-Inclusive Education fee of all siblings. This discount is applied annually by completing the Confirmation of Enrolment form issued to all families. The discount reduces the total tuition and fee burden for multi-child families, making Catholic education more accessible. The discount is applied automatically upon enrolment of the second child in the CEDR system.
eligibility_criteria: 2 or more children enrolled in Catholic Education Diocese of Rockhampton schools; Must complete Confirmation of Enrolment form annually; Siblings must be attending CEDR schools simultaneously
special_conditions: Discount is applied for each year by completing the Confirmation of Enrolment form. The discount applies to the All-Inclusive Education fee only.

overview: St Joseph's Catholic Primary School offers an enhanced Family Discount to families with 3 children enrolled across Catholic Education Diocese of Rockhampton schools. Families with 3 siblings attending CEDR schools receive a 20% discount on the All-Inclusive Education fee.
description: The Family Discount program provides enhanced financial assistance to families with 3 children attending Catholic Education – Diocese of Rockhampton (CEDR) schools. For families with 3 siblings attending CEDR schools, a 20% discount is applied to the All-Inclusive Education fee of all siblings. This substantial discount is intended to support families with larger numbers of children in the Catholic education system. The discount is applied annually by completing the Confirmation of Enrolment form issued to all families. This represents significant financial relief for families managing education costs across multiple children.
eligibility_criteria: 3 children enrolled in Catholic Education Diocese of Rockhampton schools; Must complete Confirmation of Enrolment form annually; All 3 siblings must be attending CEDR schools simultaneously
special_conditions: Discount is applied for each year by completing the Confirmation of Enrolment form. The discount applies to the All-Inclusive Education fee only.

overview: St Joseph's Catholic Primary School offers maximum Family Discount assistance to families with 4 or more children enrolled across Catholic Education Diocese of Rockhampton schools. Families receive a 30% discount on the All-Inclusive Education fee.
description: The Family Discount program provides maximum financial assistance to families with 4 or more children attending Catholic Education – Diocese of Rockhampton (CEDR) schools. For families with 4 or more siblings attending CEDR schools, a 30% discount is applied to the All-Inclusive Education fee of all siblings. This is the highest tier of the sibling discount program, recognizing the significant financial commitment required to educate multiple children in the Catholic system. The discount is applied annually by completing the Confirmation of Enrolment form issued to all families. This substantial discount provides meaningful financial relief for large families.
eligibility_criteria: 4 or more children enrolled in Catholic Education Diocese of Rockhampton schools; Must complete Confirmation of Enrolment form annually; All siblings must be attending CEDR schools simultaneously
special_conditions: Discount is applied for each year by completing the Confirmation of Enrolment form. The discount applies to the All-Inclusive Education fee only.

overview: St Joseph's Catholic Primary School offers a Concession Card Discount to families holding Government Health Care or Pensioner Concession Cards. Eligible families receive financial assistance with school fees based on their concession card status.
description: The Concession Card Discount program provides financial assistance to families who hold a 'means tested' Government Health Care or Pensioner Concession card. These cards must meet eligibility criteria for the concession to apply. This program is designed to support families experiencing financial hardship by reducing the cost of education at the school. The exact discount amount is determined based on the specific concession card held and government eligibility criteria. Families should contact the School Finance Office to discuss their eligibility and the discount that would apply to their situation. This assistance is means-tested and reflects the family's financial circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Hold a means-tested Government Health Care or Pensioner Concession card; Must meet eligibility criteria for the specific concession card; Concession card status must be verified with the school
special_conditions: Concession cards must be verified as current and meet specific eligibility criteria. Families must provide evidence of their concession card status to the school.

overview: St Joseph's Catholic Primary School provides financial assistance to families facing genuine financial hardship. Families experiencing unexpected financial difficulties or economic hardship may be eligible for fee relief or alternative payment arrangements.
description: St Joseph's Catholic Primary School recognizes that families may experience financial hardship due to unforeseen circumstances or changed economic situations. The school provides a support mechanism for families facing genuine financial hardship. Families experiencing financial difficulties are encouraged to contact the School Finance Office to discuss their circumstances and explore available options. Financial hardship assistance may include fee relief, extended payment arrangements, or other support mechanisms negotiated with the school based on individual family circumstances. The school aims to ensure that financial barriers do not prevent students from receiving a Catholic education. Assistance is assessed on a case-by-case basis and is determined by the extent of demonstrated hardship.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrate genuine financial hardship; Willingness to discuss circumstances with the School Finance Office; Support of Catholic education ethos; Commitment to communication with the school regarding financial situation
special_conditions: Assistance is determined on a case-by-case basis. Families must contact the School Finance Office to discuss their circumstances. The school maintains confidentiality in all hardship discussions. Arrangements are subject to review and may change based on ongoing financial circumstances.

overview: St Joseph's Catholic School offers fee discounts for families with more than one child enrolled at the school. As of 2015, families receive discounted tuition fees for the second, third and subsequent children across both primary and secondary systemic Catholic schools in the Townsville Diocese.
description: Families with more than one child at St Joseph's receive fee discounts for the second, third and subsequent children. As of 2015, a new fee structure gives families discounted fees for the second, third and subsequent children across both secondary and primary systemic Catholic schools in the Townsville Diocese. To take advantage of these extended fee discounts, parents/carers should contact the principal at St Joseph's, informing them of older siblings attending a systemic Catholic secondary school. Diocesan tuition fee discounts will be applied accordingly.
eligibility_criteria: Must have more than one child enrolled at St Joseph's; Applies to second, third and subsequent children; Must be enrolled in systemic Catholic schools in Townsville Diocese
special_conditions: Discount applies to second, third and subsequent children. Parents must contact the principal to inform of older siblings attending systemic Catholic secondary school for Diocesan tuition fee discounts to be applied accordingly.

program_name: Health Care Card / Pensioner Concession Card Discount
overview: St Joseph's Catholic School provides a 70% tuition fee discount to families who hold a means-tested Australian Government Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card. This program supports families experiencing financial difficulty.
description: Parents/carers with a means-tested Australian Government Health Care or Pensioner Concession Card may be eligible to receive a 70% discount to tuition fees. This represents a significant fee reduction for eligible families. Families without either of these cards who are experiencing financial difficulty should speak to the school principal to apply for special consideration. The discount is applied to tuition fees only and is based on presentation of valid card documentation.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold a valid Australian Government Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card; Card must be means-tested; Must be current at time of application
special_conditions: Discount requires proof of valid Australian Government Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card. The card must be means-tested. Families without these cards experiencing financial difficulty may apply for special consideration by speaking with the school principal.

overview: St Joseph's Catholic School provides special consideration and financial support to families experiencing financial difficulty who do not hold a Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card. Families can apply to the principal for assistance with tuition fees.
description: Families without a Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card who are experiencing financial difficulty should speak to the school principal to apply for special consideration. The school recognises that families may face unexpected financial hardship and provides a pathway to discuss fee assistance options. This support is tailored to individual family circumstances and is determined at the discretion of the school principal. Families requiring assistance with fees or seeking further information should call the office on 07 4742 1633.
eligibility_criteria: Currently experiencing financial difficulty; Do not hold a means-tested Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card; Must apply to school principal for special consideration
special_conditions: Applications are assessed on individual circumstances. The amount of assistance is at the discretion of the school principal and depends on family financial situation. Families must contact the principal directly to discuss eligibility and available support options.

school_name: St Joseph's Catholic School, The Strand
St John the Baptist Catholic Primary School

overview: St Joseph's College, Gregory Terrace offers the Academic Merit Entry Program to recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and potential. The program provides a 50% reduction in tuition fees based on performance in the ACER Cooperative Scholarship Testing Program, supporting talented students who may not otherwise have the opportunity to attend the College.
description: The Academic Merit Entry program at St Joseph's College, Gregory Terrace is designed to support students who excel academically and demonstrates the College's commitment to recognising achievement. Students receive a 50% reduction in tuition fees based on their performance in the ACER Cooperative Scholarship Testing Program. The College offers two testing dates each year to provide flexibility for applicants, with testing held at the Main College Campus. All offers are reviewed and approved at the discretion of the College Principal. Applicants must demonstrate a commitment to learning, the ideals of Catholic education, and the traits of a 'Gentleman of Terrace' – Knowledge, Humility, and Wisdom. The scholarship is available for entry into Year 7 and Year 10.
eligibility_criteria: Open to prospective students entering Year 7 or Year 10; Must sit ACER Cooperative Scholarship Testing Program; Demonstration of commitment to learning and Catholic education ideals; Must demonstrate traits of a 'Gentleman of Terrace' – Knowledge, Humility, and Wisdom; All offers subject to Principal discretion
special_conditions: All Academic Merit Entry offers are reviewed and approved at the discretion of the College Principal. Applicants are required to sit all aspects of the ACER Cooperative Scholarship Testing Program. High performing students on the ACER testing will have the opportunity to demonstrate their commitment through the admissions process, including an enrolment interview.

overview: St Joseph's College, Gregory Terrace offers Sport Merit Entry pathways to support students who demonstrate elite or outstanding performance in sporting activities. The program recognises exceptional sporting talent and provides either tuition fee relief or accelerated enrolment consideration to facilitate access for talented athletes who may not otherwise have the opportunity to attend the College.
description: The Sport Merit Entry program at St Joseph's College, Gregory Terrace is designed to support students who demonstrate elite or outstanding performance in their chosen sporting field. This pathway is available to prospective students entering Years 7 to 11. The program may offer tuition fee relief (subject to financial need) or provide accelerated enrolment consideration. Applicants must submit an Expression of Interest (EOI) form from the College Enrolments Office. The College Director of Sport will review the EOI and determine if an invitation to submit a formal application will be extended. Consideration is available only for GPS Sport activities in which the College competes. Not all sports are available for all year levels. Families should apply only when the student's elite performance can be clearly quantified. All offers of Merit Entry are made at the discretion of the College Principal.
eligibility_criteria: Open to prospective students entering Years 7 to 11; Demonstration of elite or outstanding performance in GPS sport; Sport activities must be those in which the College competes; Student's performance must be clearly quantifiable; Commitment to learning and Catholic education ideals; Must demonstrate traits of a 'Gentleman of Terrace' – Knowledge, Humility, and Wisdom; All offers subject to Principal discretion
special_conditions: Sporting and Cultural Merit Entry Applications must be returned to the College by 30 June in the year preceding entry to the College. Merit Entry pathway requires students to actively participate in the co-curricular program that the College has recognised to have merit and other appropriate co-curricular activities. This entails prioritising the representation of the College in co-curricular activities over non-school-based activities and entities such as sporting clubs, etc. Families are expected to cooperate with the college in prioritising merit entry students' academic and co-curricular commitments.

overview: St Joseph's College, Gregory Terrace offers Culture Merit Entry pathways for students who demonstrate elite or outstanding performance in cultural activities including music, drama, and performing arts. The program provides enrolment support for talented cultural performers who may not otherwise have the opportunity to attend the College.
description: The Culture Merit Entry program at St Joseph's College, Gregory Terrace is designed to support students who demonstrate elite or outstanding performance in cultural activities recognised by the College. This pathway is available to prospective students entering Years 7 to 11. The program may offer tuition fee relief (subject to financial need) or provide accelerated enrolment consideration. Applicants must submit an Expression of Interest (EOI) form from the College Enrolments Office. The College Director of Culture will review the EOI and determine if an invitation to submit a formal application will be extended. Consideration is available only for cultural activities in which the College participates and competes. Students should have achieved equivalent of Grade 5 AMEB in cultural activities or demonstrated equivalent excellence. All offers of Merit Entry are made at the discretion of the College Principal.
eligibility_criteria: Open to prospective students entering Years 7 to 11; Demonstration of elite or outstanding performance in cultural activities; Grade 5 AMEB equivalent achievement or demonstrated equivalent excellence; Cultural activities must be those in which the College participates; Commitment to learning and Catholic education ideals; Must demonstrate traits of a 'Gentleman of Terrace' – Knowledge, Humility, and Wisdom; All offers subject to Principal discretion
special_conditions: Sporting and Cultural Merit Entry Applications must be returned to the College by 30 June in the year preceding entry to the College. Merit Entry pathway requires students to actively participate in the co-curricular program that the College has recognised to have merit and other appropriate co-curricular activities. This entails prioritising the representation of the College in co-curricular activities over non-school-based activities and entities such as sporting clubs, etc. Families are expected to cooperate with the college in prioritising merit entry students' academic and co-curricular commitments.

overview: St Joseph's College, Gregory Terrace offers the Edmund Rice Bursary Program to provide financial assistance for students from disadvantaged backgrounds. The program is designed to assist families facing financial hardship and is particularly welcoming to students who identify as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander, or those who have arrived in Australia as refugees.
description: The Edmund Rice Bursary Program at St Joseph's College, Gregory Terrace is supported by the Gregory Terrace Foundation and offers financial assistance for students to access a Catholic education at the College. The program is available to students from Years 7 to 12 and is designed to assist families facing financial hardship. The program provides financial assistance for tuition fees, levies, uniforms, books, and digital devices such as laptops. It is especially welcoming to families from Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander backgrounds, and those who have arrived in Australia as refugees. The College currently financially supports 23 students of various backgrounds including Aboriginal, Torres Strait Islander and refugees across Years 7-12. Applicants must demonstrate a commitment to learning, the ideals of Catholic education, and the traits of a 'Gentleman of Terrace' – Knowledge, Humility, and Wisdom. Applications are accepted until 30 June of the year before entry.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Australian citizens or permanent residents; Open to students entering Years 7–12; Families facing financial hardship; Particular focus on Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander students; Particular focus on students from refugee backgrounds; Demonstration of commitment to learning and Catholic education ideals; Must demonstrate traits of a 'Gentleman of Terrace' – Knowledge, Humility, and Wisdom; International students not eligible
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Bursary provides financial assistance for tuition fees, levies, uniforms, books, and digital devices such as laptops. Wherever possible, families who are able to contribute toward costs are asked to do so, helping the College support more students across the community. Applicants must complete the Bursary Application Pack, provide educational and sacramental background, references from school principal and parish priest, and complete a Statement of Financial Affairs including income, expenses, assets, liabilities, and supporting documents such as tax returns, payslips, and Centrelink statements. The program reflects the Edmund Rice mission of providing liberating education and supporting every student's potential.

school_name: St Joseph's College - Waterford Place Campus
overview: St Joseph's College, Gregory Terrace offers the Academic Merit Entry program to reward outstanding academic achievement in students entering Year 7. The program recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic excellence and a commitment to learning through performance in the ACER Cooperative Scholarship Testing Program.
description: The Academic Merit Entry program provides a 50% reduction in tuition fees based on performance in the ACER Cooperative Scholarship Testing Program. Students entering Year 7 are assessed through this testing pathway. The program is open to prospective students and those currently enrolled at the College. Selection is based on performance in the ACER testing, with offers reviewed and approved at the discretion of the College Principal. All Academic Merit Entry offers include a commitment to the College's co-curricular programs and ideals of Catholic education. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability across a range of subjects and demonstrated high-level engagement in their learning.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; prospective and currently enrolled students; must sit the ACER Cooperative Scholarship Testing Program; demonstration of commitment to learning and ideals of Catholic education; must demonstrate traits of a 'Terrace Gentleman' – Knowledge, Humility and Wisdom
special_conditions: All Academic Merit Entry offers are reviewed and approved at the discretion of the College Principal. Merit Entry students must actively participate in the co-curricular program that the College has recognised and other appropriate co-curricular activities. Families are expected to cooperate with the college in prioritising merit entry students' academic and co-curricular commitments.

school_name: St Joseph's College - Waterford Place Campus
overview: St Joseph's College, Gregory Terrace offers the Culture/Sport Merit Entry program to support students who demonstrate elite or outstanding performance in GPS cultural or sporting activities. The program provides enrolment pathways and potential tuition fee relief for talented athletes and performers who may not otherwise have the opportunity to attend the College.
description: The Culture/Sport Merit Entry program offers an enrolment pathway for students who demonstrate elite or outstanding performance in their chosen field. This program is open to prospective students entering Years 7 to 11. Applications are processed in the year before entry through an Expression of Interest (EOI) form. The College Director of Sport or Culture will review the EOI and determine if an invitation to submit a formal application will be extended. Benefits may include tuition fee relief or accelerated enrolment consideration. All Sport and Culture Merit Entry applications must be returned to the College by 30 June in the year preceding entry. Consideration is available only for GPS Sport or Culture activities in which the College competes.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 to 11; prospective students demonstrating elite or outstanding performance in GPS-recognised sports or cultural activities; must submit Expression of Interest in year before entry; not all sports available for all year levels; families should apply only when student's elite performance can be clearly quantified; must demonstrate commitment to learning and ideals of Catholic education; must demonstrate traits of a 'Terrace Gentleman' – Knowledge, Humility and Wisdom
special_conditions: Applications are processed in the year before entry; early submissions will not be considered. Eligibility consideration is available only for GPS Sport or Culture activities in which the College competes. Merit Entry students must actively participate in the co-curricular program that the College has recognised and other appropriate co-curricular activities. This acceptance entails prioritising the representation of the College in co-curricular activities over non-school-based activities and entities such as sporting clubs. Families are expected to cooperate with the college in prioritising merit entry students' academic and co-curricular commitments. All offers of Merit Entry are made at the discretion of the College Principal.

school_name: St Joseph's College - Waterford Place Campus
overview: The Gregory Terrace Foundation supports the Edmund Rice Bursary program, offering financial assistance for students to access a Catholic education at St Joseph's College, Gregory Terrace. The bursary is open to students from Years 7 to 12, with particular focus on those who identify as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander, or come from a refugee background.
description: The Edmund Rice Bursary program is designed to assist families facing financial hardship. The program is available to students from Years 7 to 12 and is open to Australian citizens or permanent residents applying to enter Years 7–10. The College is unable to consider applications from international students. Through the support of the Gregory Terrace Foundation, the program provides financial assistance for tuition fees, levies, uniforms, books, and digital devices such as laptops. Wherever possible, the College asks families who are able to contribute toward costs to do so, helping to support more students across the community. The application process requires submission of a Bursary Application Pack, educational and sacramental background information, connection to the College, references from school principal and parish priest, and a Statement of Financial Affairs including income, expenses, assets, liabilities and supporting documents such as tax returns and Centrelink statements.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Years 7 to 12; Australian citizens or permanent residents; particular focus on Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander students; those from refugee backgrounds; must demonstrate commitment to learning; must demonstrate ideals of Catholic education; must demonstrate traits of a 'Gentleman of Terrace' – Knowledge, Humility, and Wisdom; must demonstrate financial hardship or inability to afford fees without assistance
special_conditions: Applications are accepted until 30 June of the year before entry. The College currently financially supports 23 students of various backgrounds including Aboriginal, Torres Strait Islander and refugees across Years 7-12. The Bursary is open to Australian citizens or permanent residents; international students cannot be considered. Wherever possible, families who are able to contribute toward costs are asked to do so. The program supports tuition fees, levies, uniforms, books, and digital devices. The goal is to provide ongoing support for up to 30 young Terracians through an endowment fund.

overview: St Joseph's Primary School offers a Fee Concession Program for families experiencing financial hardship. As a Parish School within the Archdiocesan Catholic Education system, no one is ever turned away through inability to pay. Special consideration is available for families with financial difficulties and arrangements can be made with the School Principal.
description: The Fee Concession Program provides flexible financial arrangements for families experiencing short or long-term genuine inability to pay school fees. This program is designed to ensure that enrolment is not compromised by financial circumstances. Families experiencing financial difficulties may apply for a fee concession, which is processed through the Finance Officer. Concessions are issued for a maximum period of 12 months within a calendar year. Applications are accepted at the commencement of each year or at any point initiated by the family. The school also offers alternative payment arrangements including regular instalments spread throughout the year to assist families in managing their school fee payments. All matters related to concession applications are dealt with confidentially by the school.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing short or long-term genuine inability to pay fees; Open to all students regardless of year level; Financial hardship assessment required
special_conditions: Concession applications are handled confidentially through the Finance Officer or Principal. Applications may be made at the commencement of the school year or at any point during the year as needed. Concessions are issued for a maximum period of 12 months within a calendar year. Families should contact the Principal for further information concerning the concession application process.

overview: The MacKillop Catholic School Access Fund provides financial assistance to families experiencing significant hardship who wish to enrol their children at St Joseph's Primary School. This program is administered through the Archdiocese of Brisbane and is available to eligible Catholic families within the Brisbane Archdiocese.
description: The MacKillop Catholic School Access Fund provides financial assistance through the Catholic Foundation to families experiencing significant financial hardship. The bursary covers tuition fees, school fees, and camps for each year of primary school (immersion trips to foreign countries are excluded). The school provides all stationery, texts, and resource materials, including a laptop if required and one full set of uniforms (excluding hat and shoes). Parents provide a weekly financial contribution in an agreement negotiated with the school. Applications must be submitted in the year before enrolment commences, and the committee meets in May each year to award bursaries for the following school year. All Expression of Interest forms must reach the committee no later than 15 April.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing significant financial hardship; Must live within the Archdiocese of Brisbane; Must demonstrate inability to afford fees (e.g. holder of health care card); Family must be supportive of Catholic ethos, values, and mission of Catholic education; New students only; Application must be submitted in year before enrolment; Special consideration may be given to students who are socially, culturally, spiritually or emotionally disadvantaged
special_conditions: Bursary covers tuition, fees, and camps for each year of primary school (immersion trips excluded). School provides stationery, texts, resource materials, laptop if required, and one full set of uniforms (excluding hat and shoes). Parents negotiate weekly financial contribution with school. Bursary awards are at discretion of committee based on greatest need and funding availability. Supporting financial documentation is necessary before application can be processed.

overview: St Joseph's Primary School offers fee concession assistance for families experiencing genuine financial hardship. The school is committed to ensuring that enrolment is not compromised by short or long-term financial difficulties.
description: Fee concession applications are accepted at the commencement of each year or at any point initiated by the family experiencing financial hardship. Concessions are issued for a maximum period of 12 months within a calendar year, with consideration for subsequent 12-month periods requiring a new application. A compassionate and just approach under the mission and values of Brisbane Catholic Education and St Joseph's School is used when reviewing applications. The same assessment process is adopted by all Brisbane Catholic Education schools for assessing eligibility. Families wishing to apply should contact the Finance Officer in the first instance for a Fee Concession Application form. All matters are dealt with on a confidential basis.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing short or long-term genuine inability to pay fees; Must contact Principal or Finance Officer to initiate process; Applications accepted at any point during the school year; Assessment based on compassionate approach aligned with school Catholic mission and values
special_conditions: Concessions issued for maximum 12 months within calendar year; Subsequent 12-month periods require new application; All matters handled confidentially; Same assessment process used across all Brisbane Catholic Education schools; Finance Officer should be first point of contact

program_name: Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund (MacKillop Fund)
overview: St Joseph's School Nundah offers the Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund to assist families experiencing financial hardship. The bursary provides support with tuition, textbooks, uniforms and local excursions for students seeking Catholic education. This program ensures no child is turned away from school due to inability to pay.
description: The Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund (MacKillop Fund) is designed to help families from all walks of life experiencing extreme hardship, trauma and financial devastation. The bursary covers tuition fees (for either primary school or secondary school), textbooks, uniforms and local excursions. The fund is named in honour of Australia's first saint, St Mary of the Cross MacKillop, who spent her life making Catholic education available to the poor and founded the Sisters of St Joseph, who established St Joseph's School at Nundah over one hundred years ago. The school continues Mary MacKillop's charism and ministry by supporting families in need. Applications and eligibility criteria are managed through the Catholic Foundation website.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing genuine financial hardship; seeking to provide Catholic education to their child; demonstrated financial devastation or trauma; all year levels P-6 eligible
special_conditions: Applications and further information available through the Catholic Foundation website. Non-refundable application process. As stated in school policy: 'As St Joseph's is a Parish School within the Archdiocesan Catholic Education system, no one is ever turned away through inability to pay.' Special consideration available for families with financial difficulties; arrangements can be made with the School Principal.

overview: St Joseph's School provides fee concessions for families experiencing genuine financial difficulty. Parents may apply for a concession on tuition fees to ensure affordability of Catholic education. This assistance program recognises the importance of access regardless of financial circumstances.
description: St Joseph's School offers a formal fees concession policy for parents experiencing genuine financial difficulty. Parents may apply directly to the school to request a concession on tuition fees. The school recognises that financial circumstances vary and aims to ensure that no family is excluded from Catholic education due to inability to pay fees. Arrangements can be made directly with the School Principal. The school offers flexible payment options including ten monthly, twenty fortnightly, or forty weekly payments on a Direct Debit plan throughout the year. Parents should speak with the school secretary about the most appropriate payment option for their circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing genuine financial difficulty; current or prospective students; all year levels P-6; demonstrated financial hardship
special_conditions: Application and negotiation directly with the School Principal. Flexible payment arrangements available. School policy states: 'Special consideration is available for families with financial difficulties and arrangements can be made with the School Principal.' Prompt payment of agreed fees and levies is appreciated.

overview: St Joseph's Nudgee College offers means-tested bursaries to students who might otherwise be excluded from the College for financial reasons. The College Bursary recognises families facing demonstrated financial hardship and provides assistance based on individual need assessment.
description: The College Bursary is a means-tested financial assistance program designed to ensure access to Catholic education for all boys regardless of individual family financial circumstances. The bursary amount and duration varies based on individual family circumstances as determined by the Bursary Committee. Applications must include detailed financial information and supporting documentation. Bursary offers are reviewed annually and recipients are expected to maintain satisfactory academic progress and conduct. The level of support is individually determined in April, the year prior to entry, with the amount remaining confidential to each family.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Australian citizens or permanent residents; Available from Year 7 to Year 10 where funds allow; Applications must be made simultaneously with enrolment application; Families who have already accepted an enrolment offer are ineligible; Demonstrated financial need and inability to afford Nudgee College education; Preference given to families committed to College values of faith, courage, authenticity, community, excellence and justice
special_conditions: Bursaries are offered on the understanding that they represent a debt of honour, with recipients expected to repay in the future when able to give another young man the chance to have a bursary place at St Joseph's Nudgee College. Annual review required. Complete supporting financial documentation is necessary before any application can be processed. Sibling discount still applies to one bursary per family presumption.

overview: The Jack Ross Bursary provides financial assistance to new students entering Years 7-10 who display great character and leadership values. This bursary is awarded for equity reasons and gives young men of all backgrounds the gift of education at Nudgee College.
description: The Jack Ross Bursary is an initiative of the Nudgee College Foundation and the Nudgee College Old Boys' Association, named after John (Jack) Elliott Ross, a highly respected College student, teacher and coach with connections to the College from 1908 to 1972. The bursary provides financial assistance to students whose families have suffered financial misfortune. One bursary is typically awarded per year level (Years 7, 8, 9 or 10). The bursary amount covers educational expenses and is funded through tax-deductible donations and community support. Recipients are reviewed annually and the offer is provided to the family in April, the year prior to entry.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students entering Years 7, 8, 9 or 10 only; Must display great character and leadership values; Awarded for equity reasons; Australian citizens or permanent residents; Demonstrated financial hardship; Commitment to College values in the Edmund Rice tradition
special_conditions: One bursary awarded in Years 7, 8, 9 or 10 annually. Not an open application process; College will conduct talent identification where required. Offers reviewed annually. Funded through tax-deductible donations, with donations over $2.00 being tax-deductible. Recipients must exemplify character and leadership qualities aligned with College mission.

overview: St Joseph's Nudgee College offers merit-based Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding achievement in academic excellence. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability as determined by the ACER Scholarship Test and enrolment application.
description: Academic Scholarships are awarded at a 25% concession on annual tuition fees to students demonstrating exceptional academic performance. To be eligible, students must complete the ACER Scholarship Test (test administration by independent provider). Scholarships are offered after completion and review of the ACER test results and the College Application for Enrolment form. The 25% tuition fee reduction provides significant ongoing financial support for the duration of enrolment. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress and conduct. Scholarships are reviewed annually and offers are provided in April, the year prior to entry.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must complete ACER Scholarship Test; Selection based on test results and enrolment application review; Achievement and involvement in other areas of excellence such as performing arts, visual arts, sports, music, debating considered; Sitting the test does not guarantee a scholarship offer
special_conditions: Sitting the ACER Scholarship Test does not guarantee a scholarship offer. Selection based on both test results and enrolment application form. Applications closed for 2026 entry; applications for 2028 will open in Term 4 2027. Scholarships reviewed annually.

overview: St Joseph's Nudgee College offers merit-based Performing Arts Scholarships in Music, Debating and Chess to high-achieving students. The scholarship acknowledges and rewards excellence in performing arts as determined by academic performance, results in Music, Chess and Debating, and successful interview.
description: Performing Arts Scholarships are available in Music, Debating and Chess for Years 7, 8, 9 or 10. The scholarships acknowledge and reward high-achieving students as determined by academic performance, results in Music, Chess and Debating activities, and a successful interview. Students can present on one or more instruments including Snare Drum, Marimba or Vibraphone at Grade 3 standard or equivalent. The scholarship provides recognition and financial support for students demonstrating excellence in performing arts. Offers are provided in April, the year prior to entry, with scholarships reviewed annually.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Years 7, 8, 9 or 10 entry; Must demonstrate strong academic performance; Excellence in Music, Chess and/or Debating required; Successful interview required; Achievement and involvement in other areas of excellence such as visual arts, sports and other relevant areas considered; Must meet specific instrumental or performance standards
special_conditions: Applications for 2026 are now closed. 2027 Applications will open in Term 4 2026. Successful interview required. Scholarships reviewed annually. College may specify exact performance standards for instrumental presentations.

overview: St Joseph's Nudgee College offers a very limited number of Sport Scholarships through a Talent Identification (TID) selection process. The scholarship recognises students demonstrating exceptional athletic ability and potential to contribute to the College's sporting programs.
description: Sport Scholarships are available through invitation only via the Talent Identification (TID) process with Sport Directors identifying candidates. This is not an open application process; however, the College does invite expressions of interest to provide a summary of sporting achievements. The College does not offer 100% scholarships, and parents and caregivers are required to pay the balance of all tuition fees and charges. Many talented athletes attend the College as full-fee paying students to further develop their skills. Partial fee concessions are offered to selected scholarship recipients.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students at multiple year levels; Talent Identification selection by College Sport Directors (invitation only); Not an open application process; Expression of Interest may be submitted to summarise sporting achievements; Parents must pay balance of tuition fees and charges; Achievement and involvement in other areas of excellence such as performing arts, visual arts, music, debating considered
special_conditions: Very limited number of scholarships available. Talent Identification (TID) selection process - not an open application. College does not offer 100% scholarships; full fees are not remitted. Parents required to pay balance of all tuition fees and charges. Expression of Interest welcomed to profile sporting achievements.

program_name: Fee Concession Program for Financial Hardship
overview: St Joseph's School offers fee concessions to families experiencing genuine financial hardship in meeting school fees. The school is committed to ensuring that no family is denied a Catholic education due to financial difficulties, with applications reviewed confidentially on an individual basis.
description: St Joseph's School maintains an expressed policy to assist any family experiencing personal financial hardship in meeting school fees. Families who will experience genuine financial hardship should contact the principal to confidentially discuss fee concessions. The program provides full or partial fee exemption based on individual circumstances. Concessions are granted on an annual basis only, and families must apply each year if continued assistance is needed. The school defines genuine financial hardship as a situation where a family is unable, reasonably, because of illness, unemployment or other reasonable cause, to discharge their financial obligations to pay school fees. All applications are treated with absolute discretion and confidentiality.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all current and prospective families; Must be experiencing genuine financial hardship; Must be unable to pay school fees due to illness, unemployment or other reasonable cause; Preference given to families demonstrating commitment to school's Catholic ethos; Families with health care cards may be eligible for additional support with camps and excursion charges
special_conditions: Applications for fee concessions are accepted at the commencement of each year or at any point initiated by the family. If full or partial exemption is granted, it is done only on an annual basis. Families must apply each year for continued assistance. All matters are dealt with on a confidential basis. School operates under Catholic Social Teaching and Gospel principles ensuring no student is denied Catholic education due to financial hardship.

program_name: Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund
overview: St Joseph's Tobruk Memorial School supports the Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund, named in honour of Australia's first saint, St Mary of the Cross MacKillop. This program provides bursaries to families from all walks of life experiencing extreme hardship, trauma, and financial devastation who wish their children to receive a Catholic education.
description: The Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund provides comprehensive support to eligible families experiencing genuine financial hardship. The bursary includes tuition fees, textbooks, uniforms, and local excursions. This initiative is part of Brisbane Catholic Education's broader commitment to ensure that cost does not prevent any child from accessing a quality Catholic education. Families can apply through the Catholic Foundation or contact the school directly for information about eligibility and the application process. The program recognizes St Mary MacKillop's lifetime commitment to making Catholic education available to the poor.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing genuine financial hardship; Families with extreme hardship, trauma or financial devastation; Open to all students at St Joseph's Tobruk Memorial School; Application through Catholic Foundation or direct school contact
special_conditions: Bursary includes tuition, textbooks, uniforms and local excursions. Requires demonstration of genuine financial hardship. Applications must be reviewed and approved by the Catholic Foundation.

overview: St Joseph's Tobruk Memorial School offers fee concessions for families experiencing genuine financial hardship. The school is committed to ensuring that financial constraints do not prevent enrolment or continued attendance at the school.
description: St Joseph's Tobruk Memorial School recognises that families may experience varying levels of financial constraints and is committed to working with families to ensure educational access is not compromised. In cases of financial hardship, an application may be made for a fee concession. These concessions are means-tested in line with Federal Government Poverty Guidelines and consider all sources of income including wages, Youth Allowance, Centrelink payments, and housing costs such as rent or mortgage. Applications are accepted at the commencement of each year or when hardship occurs within the family. Concessions are not ongoing and must be resubmitted annually.
eligibility_criteria: Genuine inability to pay fees demonstrated through financial assessment; Means-tested in line with Federal Government Poverty Guidelines; Open to all year levels; All income considered including wages, Centrelink payments, rent and mortgage costs
special_conditions: Applications accepted at commencement of each year or when hardship occurs. Concessions are not ongoing and must be resubmitted each school year. All families are obliged to contribute to school fees and levies even if a concession is granted. Contact the Principal and/or Finance Secretary to apply.

program_name: Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund
overview: St Kieran's Primary School offers financial assistance through the Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund. This bursary supports families experiencing financial hardship, trauma or financial devastation, enabling access to quality Catholic education.
description: The Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund is a needs-based bursary program administered through the Catholic Foundation. The bursary includes coverage for tuition, textbooks, uniforms and local excursions. The fund is named in honour of Australia's first saint, St Mary of the Cross MacKillop, who dedicated her life to making Catholic education available to the poor. St Kieran's Primary School participates in this initiative to ensure a holistic and inclusive educational environment for every child. The school aims to extend educational opportunity to those in the community who do not have the means to provide Catholic education to their children. Eligibility and application information are available directly through the Catholic Foundation website.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing extreme financial hardship, trauma or financial devastation; Catholic faith preference or shared values with school; demonstrated commitment to Catholic education
special_conditions: Bursary includes tuition, textbooks, uniforms and local excursions. Information about eligibility and application procedures is available via the Catholic Foundation website. Non-refundable confirmation required.

program_name: Fee Concession Program for Financial Hardship
overview: St Kevin's School offers fee concession applications for families experiencing genuine financial inability to pay fees. The school is committed to ensuring that financial circumstances do not compromise student enrolment and provides support through a compassionate and just approach aligned with Brisbane Catholic Education values.
description: St Kevin's School provides fee concession support for families experiencing short or long-term genuine inability to pay school fees. Concession applications may be made at the commencement of each year or at any point initiated by the family. Concessions are issued for a maximum period of 12 months within a calendar year, with consideration for subsequent 12-month periods requiring a new application. A compassionate and just approach under the mission and values of Brisbane Catholic Education and St Kevin's School is used when reviewing applications. The same process is adopted by all Brisbane Catholic Education Schools for assessing eligibility. All matters are dealt with on a confidential basis. Families experiencing financial difficulty should contact the Finance Officer to discuss the fee concession process and application requirements.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing genuine financial hardship; Open to all enrolled students; Applications must demonstrate inability to pay school fees; Assessment based on individual family circumstances; Brisbane Catholic Education assessment process applied
special_conditions: Concession applications are accepted at the commencement of each year or at any point initiated by the family. Concessions are issued for a maximum period of 12 months within a calendar year. Consideration for a subsequent 12-month period will require a new application. All matters are dealt with on a confidential basis. Contact the Finance Officer for confidential discussion and application forms.

overview: St Kevin's School provides structured leadership development opportunities for Year 5 and Year 6 students. The program is based on the model of servant leadership as exemplified by Jesus, fostering in students an understanding of their place in the school, broader, and global communities. The school recognises that every student has the ability to demonstrate leadership through serving the community.
description: At St Kevin's School, structured opportunities and preparation for servant leadership begins in Year 5 and continue in Year 6. The school aims to foster servant leadership in its students as they develop an understanding of their place in the school community, the broader community, and the global community. Growing Leaders at St Kevin's aims to raise students' awareness of the needs of others and develop their attitude of empathy and compassion. Strong leadership is demonstrated through selfless actions and without the need of a title or the wearing of a badge. The school provides Year 6 students with both the support and opportunity to experience and develop their leadership skills and potential. Year 6 students are able to build their capacity as leaders in the community through various structured leadership positions and opportunities provided by the school.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 5 or Year 6; Demonstrated commitment to community service; Selection by school leadership based on student potential and readiness for leadership roles
special_conditions: Structured leadership opportunities begin in Year 5 with expanded opportunities in Year 6. Selection is made by school leadership. Students are expected to demonstrate commitment to serving the school and broader community.

overview: St Luke's Anglican School offers the Academic Scholarship to reward outstanding achievement in students entering Middle (Years 7–9) and Senior (Years 10–12) School. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability, including strong academic records, awards, and NAPLAN results.
description: St Luke's is committed to pursuing academic excellence and provides school tuition support through the Academic Scholarship. Students are selected based on their academic record, academic awards achieved, NAPLAN results, and upon interview and review with the Principal. Successful scholarship recipients receive a discount on part of the tuition fees covering normal costs associated with education but excluding enrolment fees, deposits, capital levies, textbook and uniform purchases. Recipients are expected to maintain a high academic average performance throughout their enrolment at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Years 7–9 (Middle School) or Years 10–12 (Senior School); Strong academic record and performance in top academic tiers; NAPLAN results demonstrating academic excellence; Must attend interview with Principal; Preference given to students who would greatly benefit from a St Luke's education
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Students are expected to maintain a high academic average performance. Annual review of scholarship considers ongoing academic performance. Scholarships cover normal costs associated with education but do not include enrolment fees, deposits, capital levies, textbook and uniform purchases.

overview: St Luke's Anglican School offers the Performing Arts Scholarship to support students pursuing excellence in music, drama, visual arts, or cultural activities. The scholarship provides school tuition support and can include payment of ongoing private music tuition.
description: St Luke's is committed to assisting students in pursuit of their performing arts and cultural achievements through school tuition support and in some cases, payment of ongoing private music tuition. Applicants must demonstrate excellence in visual art, music, drama or cultural pursuits. Students should provide a music resume including AMEB or similar exam achievements, along with any Eisteddfod awards or certificates. Successful scholarship recipients receive a discount on part of the tuition fees. Scholarships cover normal educational costs but exclude enrolment fees, deposits, capital levies, textbook and uniform purchases.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Years 7–9 (Middle School) or Years 10–12 (Senior School); Must demonstrate excellence in visual art, music, drama or cultural pursuits; Music resume required including AMEB or similar exam achievements; Eisteddfod awards or certificates included with application; Must attend interview with relevant Head of Department
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: An expectation of students to participate and achieve ongoing high performance standards in their chosen instrument or Performance Art discipline at time of entry. Participation in an alternative instrument or Performance Art discipline as approved by the School. Music resume must include AMEB or similar exam achievements and any Eisteddfod awards or certificates.

overview: The Choral Scholarship is available to students entering Senior School (Years 10–12) who will be actively involved with vocal and choral contributions to a church choir nominated by mutual agreement between the Principal and the Anglican Parish of Bundaberg.
description: St Luke's Anglican School offers the Choral Scholarship specifically for students entering Senior School (Years 10–12) who demonstrate commitment to choral performance. Students will be actively involved with vocal and other contributions to a church choir, typically at Christ Church, Bundaberg or another appropriate venue, nominated by mutual agreement between the Principal and the Anglican Parish. Recipients receive tuition fee discounts covering normal educational costs but excluding enrolment fees, deposits, capital levies, textbook and uniform purchases. This is a specialized scholarship requiring both musical excellence and ongoing parish involvement.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Senior School (Years 10–12); Must be actively involved in vocal and choral contributions to a church choir nominated by mutual agreement between Principal and Anglican Parish of Bundaberg; Commitment to one singing lesson, one choral practice and one choral performance per week during term time; Must maintain active involvement in church choir throughout tenure of scholarship
special_conditions: Commit typically to one singing lesson, one choral practice and one choral performance per week during term time, usually at Christ Church, Bundaberg or another appropriate venue. Students are expected to maintain active involvement in church choir and parish music activities. Scholarship dependent upon endorsement by local Anglican Parish Priest.

overview: St Luke's Anglican School offers the Sporting Performance Scholarship to students demonstrating elite achievement in sport. The scholarship supports students pursuing sporting aspirations and excellence through school tuition assistance and access to a Personalised Performance Program.
description: St Luke's is committed to supporting students in pursuit of their sporting aspirations and excellence and provides school tuition support through the Sporting Performance Scholarship. To qualify, students must achieve a minimum level of state representation in a single sport, or regional representation across a number of sporting codes. St Luke's can also offer a Personalised Performance Program, which may allow for alterations to subject timetables and greater access to training facilities for those looking to concentrate on sporting careers. Successful scholarship recipients receive a discount on part of the tuition fees covering normal educational costs but excluding enrolment fees, deposits, capital levies, textbook and uniform purchases.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Years 7–9 (Middle School) or Years 10–12 (Senior School); Minimum level of state representation in a single sport OR regional representation across multiple sporting codes; Must demonstrate ongoing high performance standards in chosen sport; Participating in sporting codes listed in St Luke's co-curricular activities list; Must provide reference from sporting organisation or association; Must attend interview with Principal and/or relevant Head of Department
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: An expectation of students to participate and achieve ongoing high performance standards in their chosen sport at time of entry. Participation in an alternative sport as approved by the School. Personalised Performance Program may allow alterations to subject timetables and greater access to training facilities. Sporting codes generally limited to those listed in St Luke's co-curricular activities list.

overview: The Deanery of the Burnett Bursary is available to new students of Anglican faith who are actively involved in an area of Ministry or Service within their Anglican Parish, providing financial assistance through tuition fee support.
description: St Luke's Anglican School offers the Deanery of the Burnett Bursary specifically for new students of Anglican faith who are actively involved in areas of Ministry or Service within their Anglican Parish. This bursary recognises and supports students engaged in parish activities such as singing with a church choir, serving as a musician in a church musical group, teaching Sunday School, serving as an Altar Server at parish worship, or leadership roles in children's or youth groups. Recipients receive tuition fee discounts covering normal educational costs but excluding enrolment fees, deposits, capital levies, textbook and uniform purchases. Application must include endorsement from the local Anglican Parish Priest.
eligibility_criteria: New students entering Years 7–12; Must be of Anglican faith; Must be actively involved in an area of Ministry or Service within their Anglican Parish; Examples of involvement include: singing with a church choir, musician in church musical group, teaching Sunday School, leadership in children's or youth group, or Altar Server at parish worship; Application must be endorsed by local Anglican Parish Priest; Must commit to maintaining active involvement during tenure of bursary
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Students must be of Anglican faith. Students are expected to maintain their active involvement in their area of Ministry or Service within their Anglican Parish during tenure of bursary. Application must be accompanied by endorsement of local Anglican Parish Priest. Student must state in introductory letter how they have involved themselves in Ministry or Service within parish and intend to continue doing so during tenure of bursary.

overview: St Luke's Anglican School offers the Financial Assistance Bursary to support existing families who experience altered employment conditions, changed family relationships, or natural disasters. The bursary provides tuition concessions at the discretion of the Principal.
description: St Luke's Anglican School recognises that existing families can at times be impacted by altered employment conditions, altered family relationships, or natural disasters. The Financial Assistance Bursary was established to provide tuition concessions at the discretion of the Principal to help families in financial difficulty remain at the school. Families can submit applications in confidence for financial assistance consideration. The bursary covers normal educational costs but excludes enrolment fees, deposits, capital levies, textbook and uniform purchases. All applications are reviewed by the Principal and assessed based on demonstrated financial need and circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Existing students enrolled at St Luke's experiencing financial hardship; Evidence of changed circumstances such as altered employment conditions, altered family relationships, or natural disasters; Demonstrated financial need; Preference given to those who would greatly benefit from continued St Luke's education but are unable to attend without financial assistance; Applications reviewed at Principal's discretion
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications are submitted in confidence to the School. Tuition concessions are granted at the discretion of the Principal. Families experiencing altered employment, changed family relationships, or natural disasters may be eligible. Assessment is case-by-case based on individual circumstances and demonstrated financial need.

overview: St Mark's School offers fee concessions to families experiencing financial hardship. The school recognises its tradition of ministry to children and families from lower socio-economic groups and provides flexibility in fee arrangements to support access to Catholic education.
description: Families experiencing financial hardship may apply to the Principal for a fee concession. The school maintains an Agreed Payment Plan option that can be negotiated with the Principal and/or Finance Secretary to clear school fees accounts. Extensions to payment plans must be negotiated with school leadership. The financial assistance is non-monetary but provides fee reduction or deferral arrangements. No specific dollar amount or percentage reduction is published, as concessions are determined on a case-by-case basis by the Principal. Eligibility is based on demonstrated financial hardship and families must make an appointment with the Principal to discuss their circumstances and explore available options.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; Must make appointment with Principal; Demonstrated financial need required
special_conditions: Application must be made directly to the Principal. Concessions are discretionary and determined on a case-by-case basis. No formal application form is published on the school website; families must initiate contact with school leadership.

overview: Saint Mary's Catholic College offers the Mary MacKillop Education Fund Bursary to support families experiencing financial hardship who wish to access Catholic education. The bursary provides comprehensive financial support including tuition, fees, and school-related costs to eligible students through the completion of their chosen stage of schooling.
description: The Mary MacKillop Fund is a financial assistance program designed for families experiencing extreme hardship or financial devastation. For primary school students, funding covers tuition, fees and camps through the end of Year 6. For secondary school students, funding extends through the completion of Year 12. The bursary covers all tuition, fees and camps for each year of primary or secondary school, with immersion trips to foreign countries excluded. The school provides all stationery, texts, resource materials, one laptop if required, and one full set of uniforms (excluding hat and shoes). Parents contribute a weekly financial contribution negotiated directly with the school. This is a comprehensive need-based program that enables families without financial means to access quality Catholic education. The bursary is available to new students only and must be applied for through the Catholic Foundation Archdiocese of Brisbane in the year before enrolment commences, with applications typically closing in April each year.
eligibility_criteria: New students only; Must apply via Catholic Foundation Archdiocese of Brisbane; Application must occur in the year before enrolment commences; Demonstrated financial hardship or difficulty; Desire for child to receive Catholic education; Family willing to provide weekly financial contribution as negotiated with school
special_conditions: The Mary MacKillop Fund commits to funding a child through completion of the stage of school in which they are enrolled. Funding is conditional upon the family remaining in need of assistance. The bursary covers tuition, fees and camps but excludes international immersion trips. School provides all stationery, texts, resource materials, one laptop if required, and one uniform set. Parents must provide a negotiated weekly financial contribution. Application must be submitted to Catholic Foundation Archdiocese of Brisbane in the year before enrolment, with applications normally closing in April.

program_name: Fee Concessions for Families in Special Circumstances
overview: St Mary MacKillop Primary School offers fee concessions for families experiencing special circumstances. The school recognises that families have differing abilities to pay fees and makes concessions available on a case-by-case basis. Non-payment of fees is not a reason to withdraw a child from the Catholic primary school.
description: Fee concessions are made at the discretion of the school for families in special circumstances and applications must be made to the Principal each year. The school endeavours to meet families' differing ability to pay fees through this flexible concession process. The specific financial value of concessions is negotiated individually with each family based on their demonstrated financial need and circumstances. Applications require discussion with the school Principal to assess eligibility and determine appropriate support. This program reflects the school's commitment to ensuring that financial circumstances do not prevent families from accessing Catholic education.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing special financial circumstances; Annual application required; Assessment conducted on case-by-case basis
special_conditions: Applications must be made annually to the Principal. Assessment is conducted on a case-by-case basis. The school maintains flexibility to support families while ensuring sustainable school operations.

program_name: Mary MacKillop Education Fund Bursary (Brisbane Catholic Education)
overview: The Mary MacKillop Education Fund Bursary is administered through the Catholic Foundation for students enrolled or seeking enrolment at Brisbane Catholic Education schools, including St Mary MacKillop Primary School. The bursary supports families with demonstrated financial hardship, enabling access to Catholic education. Support is awarded based on greatest financial need and availability of funds.
description: The Mary MacKillop Education Fund Bursary is a means-tested financial assistance program available to families seeking enrolment at Brisbane Catholic Education schools. The bursary provides partial or full fee assistance for families demonstrating significant financial hardship and inability to afford Catholic school fees. Primary school recipients receive bursary support through to the end of Year 6 as long as the family remains in demonstrated need. The bursary is awarded at the discretion of a confidential discernment committee and is based on greatest need and available funding. Selection is not based on academic ability. Families must provide supporting financial documentation (such as health care card copies) and complete an expression of interest form. The committee meets in May each year to award bursaries for the following school year. Applications must reach the committee by 15 April for enrolment in the following year, though late applications may be considered depending on fund availability.
eligibility_criteria: Seeking new enrolment at a Brisbane Catholic Education school; Demonstrated means-tested inability to afford fees; Family must be supportive of Catholic education ethos, values and mission; Special consideration given to students who are socially, culturally, spiritually or emotionally disadvantaged; Students currently enrolled in a Brisbane Catholic Education school are NOT eligible for MacKillop Bursary at their current school; Financial documentation required (health care card)
special_conditions: Bursary support continues for the duration of enrolment (primary through to end of Year 6) as long as the family demonstrates continued financial need. A discernment committee considers applications on a strictly confidential basis. The names and details of students who apply for or are awarded bursaries are not publicly revealed. Supporting financial documentation is necessary before any expression of interest form or renewal can be processed. Weekly financial contributions negotiated with school may be required. Parents should contact school principal for initial enquiries before submitting to Catholic Foundation.

overview: St Mary's Catholic College offers an Academic Scholarship to recognise and reward outstanding academic achievement. The scholarship is available to students entering secondary education and is based on demonstrated academic excellence.
description: The College awards Academic Scholarships to students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning. The scholarship supports students who display high levels of academic achievement. Scholarships are awarded based on merit as determined by application and interview. Further details regarding the specific financial value, duration, and selection process are available from the College office.
eligibility_criteria: Ability to commit to the College values; Demonstrated academic excellence
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded based on merit as determined by an application and interview. Recipients must support the College Catholic ethos and values.

overview: St Mary's Catholic College offers a Music Scholarship to recognise and support students with exceptional musical talent. The scholarship is available to students entering secondary education and values commitment to musical excellence.
description: The College awards Music Scholarships to students who demonstrate outstanding musical ability and commitment to music. Scholarships are awarded based on audition and interview. The specific financial value and terms are available from the College office. Recipients are expected to participate in College music ensembles and maintain high standards of musical performance.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated musical ability; Commitment to music participation; Ability to support the College values
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded based on audition and interview. Recipients must maintain participation in College music ensembles and support the College values.

overview: St Mary's Catholic College offers a Sports Scholarship to recognise and support students with exceptional sporting ability. The scholarship is available to students entering secondary education and values commitment to sporting excellence and representation.
description: The College awards Sports Scholarships to students who demonstrate outstanding sporting ability and commitment to sport. Scholarships recognise students who represent the College at high levels in sporting competition. The specific financial value, selection criteria, and terms are available from the College office. Recipients are expected to maintain high standards of sporting performance and conduct.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated sporting ability at high level; Commitment to representing the College; Ability to support the College values
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded based on demonstrated sporting ability and interview. Recipients must maintain commitment to representing the College in sport and support the College values.

overview: St Mary's College offers two Merit Scholarships for Year 7 2026 students to reward outstanding achievement and commitment to a holistic education. The scholarships recognise students who demonstrate potential for a commitment to the values of Service, Courage and Justice at St Mary's College.
description: St Mary's College provides two (2) Merit Scholarships available at a value of $1,000 per year over three (3) years of attendance at the College. Scholarships are awarded based on merit as determined by an application and interview process. Recipients must maintain the College values as a student to continue receiving the Scholarship support over the three-year period. The selection process includes a written application, an interview, and assessment of the student's potential to commit to the College's holistic education approach. Scholarships are renewable annually subject to maintaining academic standards and College values.
