eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 in 2026; Must be currently enrolled in Year 6; Selection based on merit as determined by application and interview; Demonstrated potential for commitment to holistic education and College values of Service, Courage and Justice
special_conditions: Over the three years, the Scholarship recipient must maintain the College values as a student to continue receiving the Scholarship support over the outlined period. Scholarship is offered on a three-year basis from Year 7.

overview: St Mary's College administers the Queensland Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Student Fund (QATSIF) scholarships for Year 11 students. The program provides two-year scholarship funding to support eligible Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students commencing in Year 11 with the goal of increasing student retention and completion of their Queensland Certificate of Education.
description: Under the QCE Scholarship Program, QATSIF provides two-year scholarship funding to support students commencing in Year 11. St Mary's College, as a successful school under the QATSIF program, provides scholarships to individual eligible students. The intention of the funding is to increase Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander student retention in Years 11 and 12 rather than simply sustain existing retention rates. Funding must be used to complement and build on existing school and community programmes and support for individual students. St Mary's College makes applications on behalf of eligible students; interested families should contact the College Office directly. The exact scholarship value per student varies based on QATSIF funding allocation.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a person of Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander descent who identifies as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander and is accepted as such by the community in which they live; Must be an Australian citizen and resident of Queensland; Must be enrolled at a Queensland school as a student or have enrolment confirmed at a Queensland school; Must intend to undertake and complete their Queensland Certificate of Education during the school year and have the ability and school's confidence of fully completing their QCE by Year 12 Graduation
special_conditions: St Mary's College makes applications on behalf of eligible students. Contact the College Office for application details and eligibility confirmation. Scholarship duration is two years (Year 11 and Year 12). Selection determined by QATSIF criteria and school recommendations.

overview: St Mary's College offers Bursaries to families experiencing financial hardship. Bursaries are awarded on the basis of demonstrable, means-tested inability to afford a Catholic education and support is not dependent on the student's academic ability.
description: St Mary's College provides Bursaries to assist families who demonstrate an inability to afford a Catholic education. Bursaries are awarded on the basis of demonstrable, means-tested inability to afford fees at a Catholic school. Support from the bursary fund is not dependent on academic ability of the student. Examples of demonstrable inability to afford fees include families with a health care card or equivalent evidence of financial hardship. The bursary program is designed to ensure that financial circumstances do not prevent capable students from accessing a Catholic education at St Mary's College. Applications are considered on individual merit and financial circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated inability to afford fees at a Catholic school; Evidence of financial hardship such as holding a health care card or equivalent proof; Student must be enrolled or seeking enrolment at St Mary's College
special_conditions: Bursaries are means-tested and require demonstration of financial hardship. Support is determined on individual circumstances and is not dependent on academic performance. Families should contact the College directly to discuss their financial situation and bursary options.

program_name: Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund Bursary
overview: St Mary's Primary School Ipswich is eligible to receive the Mary MacKillop Bursary through Brisbane Catholic Education. This bursary supports families experiencing significant financial hardship who wish to enrol their child at a Brisbane Catholic Education school.
description: The Mary MacKillop Bursary is available to families experiencing significant hardship who are seeking to enrol students at Brisbane Catholic Education schools, including St Mary's Primary School Ipswich. For primary school students, the bursary funds tuition, fees, textbooks, uniforms and local excursions through to the end of Year 6. The MacKillop Fund commits to funding through the completion of primary school as long as the family is in need of assistance. The bursary is awarded at the discretion of the committee based on greatest need and funding available, determined by demonstrated means-tested inability to afford education at a Brisbane Catholic Education school. Support is not dependent on academic ability of the student, and special consideration may be given to students who are socially, culturally, spiritually or emotionally disadvantaged.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrates inability to afford fees at a Brisbane Catholic Education school; Family is supportive of the ethos, values and mission of Catholic education; Student is not currently enrolled in a Brisbane Catholic Education school; Family lives within the Archdiocese of Brisbane
special_conditions: Supporting financial documentation is necessary before any expression of interest form or renewal of bursary can be processed. Students currently enrolled in a Brisbane Catholic Education school are not eligible for a MacKillop Bursary to continue at their current school. Names and details of students who apply for or are awarded bursaries are not publicly revealed and treated with strict confidentiality.

overview: St Monica's College offers AIEF Scholarships for boarding students. These scholarships support First Nations students pursuing secondary education at the college. The college maintains a significant Indigenous program with 77 students in 2024 across Years 7 to 12.
description: St Monica's College, Cairns offers AIEF Scholarships specifically for boarding students from First Nations backgrounds. The college has a dedicated Indigenous program that provides pathways to formally recognised qualifications upon graduation from school. As part of the college's mission to Walk in the Path of Justice and live out the Mercy Charism, these scholarships support the empowerment of First Nations young women to act justly, lead with integrity, and succeed in their chosen endeavours. The college continues to provide increasing opportunities for all students, staff, and families to access and celebrate Indigenous culture and acknowledge Indigenous history. Selection is through the AIEF application process. The scholarship covers boarding student support and access to the college's comprehensive educational and spiritual environment.
eligibility_criteria: First Nations/Indigenous students; Boarding students; Years 7 to 12; Must be eligible for AIEF scholarship support
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients are required to support the college's Catholic ethos and Mercy charism values. Boarding is mandatory for scholarship recipients. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress and conduct standards. Specific AIEF terms and conditions apply.

overview: St Michael's College offers the Academic Excellence Bursary to high achieving students entering Year 7 who demonstrate outstanding academic achievement. The bursary recognises students who excel in their academic pursuits and are committed to maintaining excellence throughout their secondary education.
description: The Academic Excellence Bursary is awarded to students who have demonstrated a high standard of academic achievement and are expected to continue this excellence throughout their secondary education. The bursary provides a value of $1,000 per year over three years of attendance at the College, deducted from College fees. Recipients must support the College's Christian ethos and are expected to contribute enthusiastically and with commitment to the St Michael's community. Selection is based on academic merit, considering school reports and information supplied at the time of application. Only shortlisted candidates will be invited for an interview. Recipients must continue to be fully involved in College activities and maintain academic grades to above the average level throughout their bursary tenure.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Confirmed enrolment at the College for Year 7; Demonstrated high standard of academic achievement; Support for the College's Christian ethos; Australian Citizen or Permanent Resident (if applicable under BCE policy); Commitment to maintaining grades above average level
special_conditions: Bursary is offered on a three-year basis. Recipients must continue to be fully involved in College activities and maintain excellence in their chosen pursuit, as well as maintaining academic grades to above the average level. The bursary amount is deducted from College fees. Scholarship subject to annual review.

overview: St Michael's College offers the General Excellence Bursary to high achieving students entering Year 7 who demonstrate excellence in areas such as sport, the arts, leadership or community service. The bursary recognises well-rounded students who excel across multiple disciplines and are committed to contributing to College life.
description: The General Excellence Bursary is awarded to students who have demonstrated a high standard of achievement in areas including, but not limited to, sport, the arts, leadership or community service, combined with positive and enthusiastic contribution to school life. Recipients must maintain academic grades to above the average level and demonstrate genuine commitment to their chosen pursuit. The bursary provides a value of $1,000 per year over three years of attendance at the College, deducted from College fees. Selection is based on merit as determined by a panel, with consideration of school reports and supporting information. Only shortlisted candidates will be invited for an interview. Recipients are expected to remain fully involved in College activities throughout their bursary period.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Confirmed enrolment at the College for Year 7; Demonstrated achievement in sport, arts, leadership or community service; Positive and enthusiastic contribution to school life; Academic grades above average level; Support for the College's Christian ethos; Australian Citizen or Permanent Resident (if applicable under BCE policy)
special_conditions: Bursary is offered on a three-year basis. Recipients must continue to be fully involved in College activities and maintain excellence in their chosen pursuit, as well as maintaining academic grades to above the average level. The bursary amount is deducted from College fees. Scholarship subject to annual review.

overview: The Mary MacKillop Fund Bursary honours Australia's first saint, St Mary of the Cross MacKillop, who dedicated her life to making Catholic education accessible to the poor. This bursary provides comprehensive financial support to families experiencing extreme hardship or financial devastation who wish to give their child a Catholic education at St Michael's College.
description: The Mary MacKillop Fund Bursary is designed to support families from all walks of life who are experiencing extreme hardship, trauma and financial devastation and have a deep desire for their child to receive a Catholic education. The bursary includes tuition fees, textbooks, uniforms and local excursions, providing comprehensive financial assistance to eligible families. Named in honour of St Mary of the Cross MacKillop, Australia's first saint, who spent her lifetime making Catholic education available for the poor, this program reflects the College's commitment to equity and access. Funding is made available through tax-deductible donations to the Catholic Foundation. Families interested in accessing support should contact the College directly or engage with the Catholic Foundation for donation and bursary information.
eligibility_criteria: Open to secondary students Years 7-12; Demonstrated significant financial hardship or financial devastation; Family commitment to Catholic education; Student and family support for the College's Christian ethos and values; Families from diverse backgrounds and circumstances
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Bursary is variable and dependent on assessed financial need. Funding is sourced through the Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund via tax-deductible donations. Support may be ongoing subject to continued financial hardship demonstration and good academic standing. Recipients must support the Catholic ethos of the College.

overview: St Patrick's College offers the Edmund Rice Bursary to provide financial assistance to Catholic students entering Year 7 who would otherwise be unable to afford a St Patrick's education. The bursary is awarded based on financial need and family circumstance, recognizing the Edmund Rice tradition of supporting young men from families facing financial hardship.
description: Through the generosity of the St Patrick's College Foundation, the College offers the Edmund Rice Bursary to assist Catholic families with the cost of education. The bursary provides financial assistance for tuition fees, with the value varying based on individual family circumstances. Applications are assessed based on financial need and family circumstance, not academic or sporting ability. The selection process involves submission of an application form with supporting documentation including birth certificate, baptism certificate, school reports, and NAPLAN results, followed by an interview with the Principal where a comprehensive financial report is required. The bursary is conditional upon recipients remaining at the college and maintaining satisfactory academic progress and conduct. Applications typically close in June for entry in the following year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be currently enrolled in or recently attending a Catholic Primary School; Must be Catholic; Application based on financial need and family circumstance; Not offered on basis of academic or sporting ability; Family must demonstrate financial hardship
special_conditions: Applicants must be Catholic and currently attending a Catholic Primary School. St Patrick's College does not offer sporting or academic scholarships. Scholarship may be temporarily or permanently withdrawn if recipient fails to make satisfactory progress or is unsatisfactory in any other way. Recipients must provide written completion of a comprehensive report of financial situation at interview stage, which remains confidential. Full terms and conditions of the bursary are discussed at the interview stage.

overview: St Patrick's College awards McGarry Bursaries each year to students enrolled at the college. The bursaries are made available through a bequest by the late Miss Joan McGarry and are designed to recognise and support exceptional academic achievement.
description: McGarry Bursaries are established through a perpetual bequest by the late Miss Joan McGarry, a nurse, established in honour of her parents Fred and Rose McGarry and her brother Jim McGarry. Bursaries are awarded annually to students who have achieved exceptional academic results. In recognition of Joan McGarry's lifelong dedication to nursing, the student with the highest ATAR entering Nursing also receives a bursary. The bursaries are awarded to students once properly enrolled in a tertiary course after Year 12, with study commencing in the year of the bequest. The amount is substantial and represents a gift to the future and a belief in young people.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a Year 12 graduate; Must be enrolled in a tertiary course; Academic achievement in ATAR results; Special consideration for highest ATAR entering Nursing; Study must commence in year of bequest
special_conditions: Bursaries are awarded to students once properly enrolled in a tertiary course after Year 12, with study to commence in the year of the bequest. The bursaries are not about money alone, but represent a gift to the future and a belief in young people.

overview: The Class of 1974 Bursary is sponsored by members of the Class of 1974 who have made outstanding contributions to the Mackay community. The bursary provides fee support to Year 12 students who have demonstrated dedication and commitment in overcoming adversity to achieve academic success.
description: The Class of 1974 Bursary is sponsored by members of the Class of 1974, who have collectively made an outstanding contribution to the Mackay community and beyond in fields including clergy, politics, business, law, and trades. Through their generosity, a bursary which provides a rebate from the student's college fees has been made available to a current St Patrick's College Year 12 student. The selected student will have demonstrated great dedication and commitment throughout Year 11 in overcoming adversity or circumstances to achieve academic success.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a Year 12 student; Must have demonstrated dedication and commitment throughout Year 11; Evidence of overcoming adversity or difficult circumstances; Academic success
special_conditions: Award recognises students who have demonstrated great dedication and commitment throughout Year 11 in overcoming adversity or circumstances to achieve academic success. Bursary provides a rebate from the student's college fees.

overview: The Aspire to Teach Awards are Catholic Education Awards designed to promote teaching as a career choice for senior students. These awards recognise students who demonstrate qualities suitable for the teaching profession and commitment to Christian faith.
description: The Aspire to Teach Awards are Catholic Education Awards and the purpose of the award is to promote the career of teaching for students in the Senior Phase of Learning. Awards are presented to one male and one female student. Recipients must demonstrate the following qualities: witness to Christian faith, qualities of a good teacher such as care, compassion, positivity, collaboration, helpfulness and mentoring of peers, good academic and behaviour record, broad involvement in college and co-curricular activities, and evidence of student leadership. These awards recognise students who embody the values necessary to become effective educators.
eligibility_criteria: Must be in Senior Phase of Learning (Year 11 or 12); Demonstrated witness to Christian faith; Display qualities of a good teacher (care, compassion, positivity, collaboration, helpfulness, mentoring); Good academic record; Good behaviour record; Broad involvement in college and co-curricular activities; Evidence of student leadership
special_conditions: Award is presented to one male and one female student. Nominees are selected based on demonstrated qualities and peer/staff nominations.

program_name: St Patrick's Medal – The Bishop Bernard Wallace Award for Dux of the College
overview: St Patrick's Medal, also known as the Bishop Bernard Wallace Award for Dux of the College, recognises the highest academic achiever among graduating Year 12 students. The award acknowledges the college patron Saint Patrick and past Bishop Bernard Wallace, who had a great interest in education.
description: The St Patrick's Medal is awarded to the Dux of the College, recognising the student who achieves the highest ATAR score in Year 12. This award acknowledges the College Patron, St Patrick, the great Irish Saint, and The Most Reverend Bernard Wallace, a past Bishop of Rockhampton who had a great interest in education. The criteria for this award is based solely on a student's ATAR score, with the highest-scoring student receiving this prestigious honour. The award represents academic excellence and outstanding achievement.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a Year 12 student; Must have the highest ATAR score among graduating cohort
special_conditions: Award is based on student's ATAR score. The student receiving this award is the Dux of the College.

overview: The Forte Instrumental Music Program is a scholarship immersion program offered at Mercy Campus to students who wish to learn or start a new instrument. The program provides structured music lessons during class time with the opportunity to progress to ensemble participation.
description: The Forte Instrumental Music Program is a scholarship immersion program offered at Mercy Campus to students that wish to learn or start a new instrument. The program offers 30-minute weekly music lessons to students on a rotational timetable during class time. It allows first-time students the opportunity to work in a group of between 2-4 students on similar instruments. Once confident on their chosen instrument, students then have the knowledge and ability to move into the Concert Band or String Ensemble and have the option to continue their lessons in either a group or private structure. The program offers tuition on brass, woodwind and percussion instruments. Students making a commitment to tuition should sustain their commitment for at least one semester to allow a full opportunity to assess the appropriateness of the instrument.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Mercy Campus students (Year 7-10); Wish to learn or start a new instrument; Commitment to at least one semester of lessons; Willingness to undertake regular and consistent practice
special_conditions: Students must sustain their commitment to tuition for at least one semester to allow full assessment of instrument suitability. Students are expected to undertake regular and consistent practice to ensure full benefit of tuition offered. Students can progress to Concert Band or String Ensemble after demonstrating confidence.

overview: St Patrick's Primary School offers fee concessions to families experiencing financial difficulty. The program ensures that no Catholic family will be denied a Catholic education because of their financial situation.
description: St Patrick's Primary School provides fee reductions through its Fee Concession program, available to families when enrolling or when financial circumstances change during a student's enrolment. The school uses a just and equitable method to assess individual circumstances and determine the level of fee reduction assistance. Families should contact the Principal to discuss their situation and explore available options. The program is based on demonstrated financial need and the school's commitment to ensuring Catholic education remains accessible to all families regardless of financial circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial difficulty; Applicable at enrolment or when financial circumstances alter; No specific year level restriction; Open to all students currently enrolled or enrolling
special_conditions: Applications should be made directly to the Principal. Fee reductions are determined on a case-by-case basis using a just and equitable assessment method. No Catholic family will be denied the right of a Catholic Education because of their financial situation.

program_name: Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund
overview: St Patrick's Primary School offers the Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund to support families experiencing financial hardship. This program aims to ensure that all children have access to quality Catholic education regardless of their financial circumstances.
description: The Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund is designed to provide financial assistance to families from all walks of life who may be experiencing extreme hardship, trauma, or financial devastation. Through the generosity of donors and the school community, the fund helps families in need to access Catholic education at St Patrick's Primary School. The program supports families by providing assistance with school fees and related educational costs. Eligible families can apply for support to help make Catholic education more affordable and accessible. The fund operates on a case-by-case basis, with assessments made by the school to determine the level of assistance required. Recipients of support from the MacKillop Fund demonstrate commitment to the school's Catholic values and educational mission.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship or extreme hardship; Open to all families regardless of background; Demonstrated need based on family financial circumstances; Commitment to school's Catholic values and educational mission
special_conditions: Assistance is provided on a case-by-case basis. The school works directly with families to assess their financial circumstances and determine appropriate support. Families should contact the school to discuss their situation and explore available assistance.

overview: All Brisbane Catholic Education schools in the South Burnett region, including St Patrick's Primary School, offer a sibling discount to families with multiple children enrolled at BCE schools. This discount recognizes the financial commitment of families with more than one child in Catholic education.
description: Brisbane Catholic Education schools in the South Burnett offer a sibling discount program for families who have children attending more than one BCE school. This discount is applied to school fees for families with multiple children enrolled in the BCE school system within the South Burnett region. The discount helps to reduce the overall tuition burden for families supporting Catholic education across multiple students. Families with siblings enrolled at any Brisbane Catholic Education school in the South Burnett area automatically qualify for this discount. The specific percentage or amount of the discount varies and should be confirmed directly with the school office. This is an automatic benefit for eligible families and does not require a separate application process.
eligibility_criteria: Families with multiple children attending Brisbane Catholic Education schools; At least two children enrolled in BCE schools in South Burnett region; Families must be enrolled at the time of fee payment; Open to all families regardless of background
special_conditions: Discount is automatically applied for families with multiple children at Brisbane Catholic Education schools in the South Burnett region. No separate application is required. The discount applies to school fees at the time of billing.

overview: St Patrick's College Townsville is a partner school of the Australian Indigenous Education Foundation (AIEF) and offers scholarships for First Nations boarding students from Years 9-12. The AIEF Scholarship Program provides funding that enables Indigenous students to pursue their education at leading Australian boarding schools, with career support to help them make a successful transition to employment.
description: St Patrick's College Townsville partners with AIEF to provide scholarship funding for Indigenous boarding students. Under a parent and school-led model, AIEF provides co-funding to enable Indigenous students to complete their studies at St Patrick's College as an AIEF Partner School. The scholarship removes financial barriers hindering Indigenous students and their families from choosing a quality boarding school education. The AIEF Pathways Program complements the scholarship by providing career support to ensure all AIEF Scholarship Students make a successful transition from school to further studies or employment. Specific financial value and duration details are available in the AIEF Handbook provided on the scholarship page.
eligibility_criteria: Open to First Nations / Indigenous students; Boarding students only; Years 9 to 12; Must meet AIEF eligibility criteria as detailed in the AIEF Handbook; Students must be supported by Indigenous families seeking boarding school education
special_conditions: Students must remain enrolled at St Patrick's College for the duration of their studies to maintain scholarship eligibility. Scholarship is subject to satisfactory academic progress and compliance with school policies. The AIEF Pathways Program provides ongoing career support beyond school completion.

overview: St Patrick's College awards McGarry Bursaries to eligible Year 12 students annually. The bursaries were established through a bequest by the late Miss Joan McGarry and recognise students in financial need. Recipients are presented with their awards at the annual Opening College Mass.
description: The McGarry Bursaries provide financial assistance to Year 12 students who demonstrate financial need. A bequest by the late Miss Joan McGarry has established perpetual bursaries to be presented each year to St Patrick's College students. The bursaries are presented at the Opening College Mass at the commencement of the academic year. The exact monetary value is not publicly specified on the college website, but the bursaries represent a commitment to supporting students in financial hardship. Selection is based on demonstrated financial need and eligibility as a Year 12 student at St Patrick's College.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 students; Demonstrated financial need; Enrolled at St Patrick's College
special_conditions: Bursaries are presented annually at the Opening College Mass. Eligibility restricted to Year 12 students.

overview: St Patrick's College offers the Forte Instrumental Music Program as a scholarship immersion program for students at the Mercy Campus who wish to learn or commence a new instrument. The program provides access to structured music tuition during school time through a rotational timetable.
description: The Forte Instrumental Music Program is a scholarship immersion program offered at the Mercy Campus to students in Years 7 to 10 who wish to learn or start a new instrument. The program offers 30-minute weekly music lessons to students on a rotational timetable during class time. It allows first-time students the opportunity to work in small groups of 2-4 students on similar instruments. Once students develop confidence on their chosen instrument, they have the option to progress into the Concert Band or String Ensemble and continue with lessons in either group or private structure. The program covers tuition on brass, woodwind, and percussion instruments. Students are expected to undertake regular and consistent practice and commit to their lessons for at least one semester to allow full assessment of instrument suitability.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Mercy Campus students Years 7-10; First-time or beginner instrumentalists; Commitment to sustain lessons for minimum one semester
special_conditions: Students must make a commitment to tuition and sustain their commitment for at least one semester. Regular and consistent practice is required. Students are expected to undertake consistent practice to ensure full benefit of tuition offered.

overview: St Peter Claver College offers financial concessions to families experiencing financial difficulties. Concessions are available on tuition fees only and require annual reapplication, with applications submitted to the College office and closing at the end of Term 1.
description: St Peter Claver College provides concessions for families who are experiencing financial difficulties. Concessions are available on tuition fees only. Applications for concession are available from the office and must be resubmitted at the commencement of each school year. The process involves providing supporting documentation to prove financial status, including wages, Austudy, Family Allowance and Child Support. All information collected is kept strictly confidential. Families eligible for concession are required to pay their account via direct debit or Centrepay. Concessions are not ongoing and applications need to be resubmitted at the commencement of each school year.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial difficulties; Supporting documentation required (wages, Austudy, Family Allowance, Child Support); Payment by direct debit or Centrepay required; Annual reapplication required
special_conditions: Concessions are available on tuition fees only. All information collected is kept strictly confidential. Families eligible for concession must pay via direct debit or Centrepay. Concessions are not ongoing and applications must be resubmitted at the commencement of each school year.

program_name: Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund
overview: The Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund provides financial support for families in genuine financial hardship to enable their children to attend St Peter Chanel Primary School. This needs-based bursary is administered through Brisbane Catholic Education and the Catholic Foundation.
description: The Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund is available to families experiencing demonstrable financial hardship or extenuating circumstances. The bursary provides school fee support and is assessed on a means-tested basis. Families with genuine inability to afford school fees are not denied a Catholic education. The fund operates on a confidential basis, with applications processed by Brisbane Catholic Education using a standardized assessment process across all Catholic schools. Concession applications are accepted at the commencement of each year or when hardship occurs within the family. Concessions are calculated and applied for a maximum period of 12 months within a calendar year.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing significant financial hardship; Must demonstrate means-tested inability to afford Catholic education; Open to all year levels at St Peter Chanel Primary School; Applications accepted at commencement of each year or when hardship arises; Assessed on confidential basis; No student will be denied a Catholic education due to genuine inability to pay
special_conditions: All matters are dealt with on a confidential basis. Concessions are calculated and applied for a maximum period of 12 months within a calendar year. Consideration for a subsequent concession will require a new application. Payment plans are available for families managing fees. Extensions to payment plans must be negotiated with the Principal and/or Finance Officer.

overview: St Peter Chanel Primary School offers a fee concession program for families facing genuine financial difficulty. This school-level support ensures that no student is denied a Catholic education due to inability to pay fees.
description: The Fee Concession Program at St Peter Chanel Primary School is designed to support families experiencing financial hardship. In cases of genuine financial difficulty, eligible families may apply for fee concessions or reduced fee arrangements. Applications are assessed based on financial need and extenuating circumstances using a standardized process aligned with Brisbane Catholic Education policy. The school offers various payment options including regular instalments through Agreed Payment Plans to assist families in managing school fees. Concessions are typically granted for a maximum 12-month period and families may reapply in subsequent years if hardship continues.
eligibility_criteria: Genuine financial hardship; Extenuating circumstances affecting family finances; Open to families of students in all year levels; Applications assessed on individual basis; Confidential assessment process
special_conditions: Applications are accepted at the commencement of each year or when hardship arises. Concessions are calculated for a maximum period of 12 months within a calendar year. New applications required for subsequent concessions. All matters handled on a confidential basis. School offers Agreed Payment Plans as alternative support mechanism.

overview: St Paul's School offers Academic Scholarships to recognize outstanding academic achievement in students entering Year 7. The scholarship is awarded on the basis of a student's academic ability demonstrated through examination performance.
description: The Academic Scholarship at St Paul's School provides up to a 50% fee reduction for students in Year 7 to Year 12. The scholarship is awarded to current St Paul's School students in Year 6 (2025) and external students entering Year 7 in 2026, based solely on performance in the scholarship examination conducted by EduTest. The selection process involves completing the online EduTest registration form and sitting the scholarship exam on Saturday 22 March 2025 at St Paul's School. Registration for the exam closes on Friday 14 March 2025. An application fee of $100 applies to sit the examination. Scholarships are awarded in June 2025, and all applicants must be Australian residents. The scholarship provides tuition fee reduction only.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current St Paul's School students in Year 6 (2025); Open to external students entering Year 7 in 2026; Australian residents only; Must sit EduTest scholarship examination; Academic ability demonstrated through exam performance
special_conditions: Scholarship is offered to Australian residents only. Scholarships provide up to 50% reduction in fees for Year 7 to Year 12. Enrolment fee ($500) is waived for new scholarship recipients. Scholarships are awarded on the results of the EduTest examination held in March.

overview: St Paul's School offers Performing Arts Scholarships to recognize excellence in music, drama, and dance. These specialist scholarships are designed for students who demonstrate exceptional talent and commitment to the performing arts.
description: The Performing Arts Scholarship recognizes students who excel in music, drama, or dance. Students applying for this specialist scholarship must sit the academic scholarship exam on Saturday 22 March 2025 and submit a specialist scholarship application form to the Principal by 4:00pm on Friday 14 March 2025. The selection process includes shortlisting for interview or audition with the Principal. Successful applicants receive a letter of offer from the Principal. Specialist scholarships are available for new students entering the school and are not available for current St Paul's School students. The scholarship provides partial fee assistance, with the specific value determined by individual performance and school assessment. An information pack detailing selection criteria, documentation requirements, and recipient expectations is available from the school.
eligibility_criteria: Excel in music, drama or dance; Open to external students entering the school; Not available for current St Paul's School students; Australian residents only; Must sit the EduTest academic scholarship examination; Must submit specialist scholarship application form with supporting documentation
special_conditions: Specialist scholarships are not available for current St Paul's School students. Shortlisted applicants may be required to attend an interview or audition with the Principal. Enrolment fee is waived for new scholarship recipients. The information pack must be reviewed for complete selection criteria and documentation requirements. Recipients must meet school conduct and academic progress expectations.

overview: St Paul's School offers Sport Scholarships to recognize excellence in sporting achievement. These specialist scholarships are designed for students who demonstrate exceptional athletic ability and commitment to their chosen sport.
description: The Sport Scholarship recognizes students who excel in any given sport at an elite level. Students applying for this specialist scholarship must sit the academic scholarship exam on Saturday 22 March 2025 and submit a specialist scholarship application form to the Principal by 4:00pm on Friday 14 March 2025. The selection process includes shortlisting for interview or audition with the Principal to assess sporting ability, commitment, and potential. Successful applicants receive a letter of offer from the Principal. Specialist scholarships are available for new students entering the school and are not available for current St Paul's School students. The scholarship provides partial fee assistance, with the specific value determined by individual performance and school assessment. An information pack detailing selection criteria, documentation requirements, and recipient expectations is available from the school.
eligibility_criteria: Excel in any given sport; Open to external students entering the school; Not available for current St Paul's School students; Australian residents only; Must sit the EduTest academic scholarship examination; Must submit specialist scholarship application form with supporting documentation
special_conditions: Specialist scholarships are not available for current St Paul's School students. Shortlisted applicants may be required to attend an interview or audition with the Principal. Enrolment fee is waived for new scholarship recipients. The information pack must be reviewed for complete selection criteria and documentation requirements. Recipients must meet school conduct and academic progress expectations.

overview: St Paul's School offers Vocational Scholarships to support students commencing in Year 10 who wish to follow a vocational educational pathway in Years 11 and 12. These specialist scholarships recognize commitment to technical and vocational education.
description: The Vocational Scholarship supports students entering Year 10 who are pursuing a vocational educational pathway in Years 11 and 12. Students applying for this specialist scholarship must sit the academic scholarship exam on Saturday 22 March 2025 and submit a specialist scholarship application form to the Principal by 4:00pm on Friday 14 March 2025. The selection process includes shortlisting for interview or audition with the Principal to assess commitment to vocational studies and career readiness. Successful applicants receive a letter of offer from the Principal. Specialist scholarships are available for new students entering the school and are not available for current St Paul's School students. The scholarship provides partial fee assistance to support students pursuing VET courses. An information pack detailing selection criteria, documentation requirements, and recipient expectations is available from the school.
eligibility_criteria: Commencing in Year 10; Pursuing a vocational educational pathway in Years 11 and 12; Open to external students entering the school; Not available for current St Paul's School students; Australian residents only; Must sit the EduTest academic scholarship examination; Must submit specialist scholarship application form with supporting documentation
special_conditions: Specialist scholarships are not available for current St Paul's School students. Shortlisted applicants may be required to attend an interview or audition with the Principal. Enrolment fee is waived for new scholarship recipients. Students must demonstrate commitment to pursuing a vocational pathway through VET courses in Years 11 and 12. The information pack must be reviewed for complete selection criteria and documentation requirements.

overview: St Paul's School offers Indigenous Scholarships to provide financial support to students with Indigenous heritage who may not have the financial means to attend the school. The scholarship provides full or partial tuition fee coverage from Year 7 to Year 12.
description: The Indigenous Scholarship provides full or partial scholarship funding to cover the cost of tuition at St Paul's School. Depending on the family's level of financial need, a St Paul's Indigenous Scholarship will cover 20% to 100% of the cost of tuition from Year 7 to the end of Year 12. Students wishing to enter the school at another grade level may be considered. Awards are made on the basis of family financial need and Indigenous heritage status. Applicants must complete the Indigenous Student Scholarship Application Form and provide evidence of Indigenous heritage. Students are expected to put in their best effort to their studies and participate actively in the School's Extra-Curricular Program. The scholarship is offered to Australian residents only. All inquiries should be directed to the Registrar.
eligibility_criteria: Members of the community with Indigenous heritage; Australian residents only; May not have the financial means to pay full tuition; Expected to put best effort into studies; Required to participate in the School's Extra-Curricular Program; Open from Year 7 to Year 12; Students entering at other grade levels may be considered
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Partial Fee Remission
special_conditions: Scholarship covers 20% to 100% of tuition costs depending on family financial need. Students entering at grade levels other than Year 7 may be considered. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress and participate in extra-curricular programs. The level of assistance provided is determined on a case-by-case basis considering family financial circumstances. Enrolment fee is waived for new scholarship recipients.

overview: St Paul's School offers Bursaries to provide financial assistance to families who would otherwise be unable to afford a place at the school. Bursaries are awarded on the basis of family financial need and are designed to make education accessible.
description: St Paul's School offers bursaries to assist families with demonstrated financial need. Bursaries are awarded on the basis of family need and are determined by the family's financial situation. Each bursary is considered on a case-by-case basis and takes into account income, realisable assets, and other relevant factors. The level of assistance provided depends on individual circumstances and may range from partial bursaries to cover a portion of fees, though specific financial amounts are not predetermined. Bursary recipients have their enrolment fee ($500) waived. Families interested in applying for a bursary should contact the Registrar to discuss their circumstances and application process. Bursaries provide ongoing support for students already enrolled or may be awarded to provide short-term assistance during unexpected financial hardship.
eligibility_criteria: Family financial need must be demonstrated; Open to all school sectors; Students must meet school academic and residence requirements; Eligible for students joining the school or existing students facing unexpected financial difficulties
special_conditions: Bursaries are awarded on the basis of family need and are determined by individual financial circumstances. Each case is considered separately taking into account income, realisable assets and other relevant factors. Enrolment fee is waived for bursary recipients who are new to the school. Contact the Registrar for information on how to apply for a bursary.

program_name: MacKillop Catholic School Access Fund (MacKillop Bursary)
overview: The MacKillop Catholic School Access Fund provides access to Catholic education for families experiencing significant financial hardship who live within the Archdiocese of Brisbane. The bursary covers tuition, fees, and camps for each year of primary school and is available to new students only.
description: The MacKillop Bursary is designed to support families in the Archdiocese of Brisbane who are experiencing demonstrable, means-tested inability to afford a Catholic education. The bursary covers tuition fees, levies, and camps for each year of primary school enrolment. The school provides all stationery, texts, and resource materials. Parents provide a weekly financial contribution negotiated with the school. Bursaries are awarded at the discretion of the discernment committee based on greatest need and funding availability. Support is not dependent on the student's academic ability. This bursary is available to new students only and must be applied for in the year before enrolment commences.
eligibility_criteria: New students entering Brisbane Catholic Education schools; Families experiencing significant financial hardship; Demonstrable means-tested inability to afford Catholic education fees; Family supportive of Catholic education ethos and values; Possession of health care card or equivalent financial documentation required; Living within the Archdiocese of Brisbane
year_levels: Prep, Year 1, Year 2, Year 3, Year 4, Year 5, Year 6
special_conditions: This bursary is available to new students only and must be applied for in the year before enrolment commences. Students currently enrolled in a Brisbane Catholic Education school are not eligible to apply for a MacKillop Bursary to continue at their current school. Expression of Interest forms must reach the committee no later than 15 April for enrolment in the following school year. Late expressions of interest may be considered dependent upon availability of funds. The discernment committee meets in May each year to award bursaries for the following school year. All information regarding bursary awards is held in strictest confidence and student names are not publicly revealed.

overview: St Peters Springfield offers Academic Scholarships to recognise and reward outstanding academic achievement in students entering Year 7 or Year 10. These scholarships provide part remission of school fees to students demonstrating exceptional academic ability and commitment to excellence.
description: Academic Scholarships at St Peters Springfield provide partial fee remission to students entering Year 7 or Year 10. All applicants are required to sit the Edutest Academic Scholarship examination. The examination is held in February at St Peters Indooroopilly campus. Applicants must have completed a St Peters online enrolment application, including payment of the enrolment application fee, before registering for the scholarship exam. The College does not disclose testing results, and results remain the property of the College with no transferring to other schools. Recipients benefit from reduced school fees, allowing greater access to the College's state-of-the-art facilities and comprehensive curriculum.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or Year 10 in 2026; Must be a current St Peters student or have completed a St Peters online application with enrolment fee payment; Must sit the Edutest Academic Scholarship examination; Domestic students only
special_conditions: Applicants must sit the examination at St Peters Indooroopilly campus on the designated date. St Peters does not disclose testing results. Testing results remain the property of the College. No sharing or transferring of results to or from other schools.

overview: St Peters Springfield offers Excellence Scholarships to recognise and reward students skilled in cocurricular activities. These scholarships are available to new and existing students in Years 7–11 and provide partial remission of school fees based on demonstrated merit in cocurricular pursuits.
description: Excellence Scholarships (Cocurricular Activities) at St Peters Springfield provide partial fee remission to students in Years 7–11 who demonstrate exceptional merit in cocurricular activities including music, performing arts, visual arts, sports, and other co-curricular pursuits. Scholarships are awarded based on merit at the discretion of the Head of Springfield or delegate in consultation with the Scholarship Committee. Applications are now open for students entering Years 7–11 in 2025 and 2026. The College explicitly states that it does not offer 100% scholarships. Prospective applicants should contact the Enrolments office for full details about the application process and specific requirements.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new and existing students; Years 7–11; Must demonstrate merit in cocurricular activities; Awarded at discretion of Head of Springfield and Scholarship Committee; Domestic students only
special_conditions: St Peters does not offer 100% scholarships. Awards are based on merit and discretion of the Head of Springfield and Scholarship Committee. Selection process involves application form and guidelines submission. Applications for 2025 and 2026 are currently open.

scholarship_url: Not found - no dedicated scholarships page
overview: St Philomena School offers Hardship Discounts to assist families experiencing financial hardship. The school awards six discounts every six months to families in most need, with applications considered twice yearly during application windows.
description: St Philomena School provides Hardship Discounts as a form of financial assistance for families facing financial hardship. Six discounts are available every six months, applied for in January for Terms 1 and 2, and in June for Terms 3 and 4. All applications received by 31 January and 30 June are considered and awarded to families in most need. Families must reapply for consideration in each subsequent round. The exact financial value of each discount is not specified and varies based on individual circumstances and the number of qualifying applications. Recipients must maintain current payment status on their account as at 31 January to remain eligible.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; Applications submitted by 31 January for Terms 1 & 2 and 30 June for Terms 3 & 4; Must be up-to-date with payments as at 31 January; Must reapply each round for consideration
special_conditions: Hardship Discount applications are assessed twice yearly with cycles in January and June. Families must reapply if they wish to be considered for subsequent rounds. Recipients must maintain current payment status on 31 January to remain eligible. The discount value is discretionary based on demonstrated financial need and available funds.

overview: St Peters Lutheran College offers Academic Scholarships to recognise outstanding academic achievement in students entering Year 7 or Year 10. These scholarships provide up to 50% remission of tuition fees and are awarded based on performance in the Edutest Academic Scholarship Examination.
description: Academic Scholarships providing up to 50% fee reduction are available to new and existing students commencing Year 7 or Year 10 at St Peters Lutheran College Indooroopilly. The scholarship is awarded on the results of the Edutest Academic Scholarship Examination, prepared independently and held in Term 1 of the year prior to entry (scheduled for Saturday 14 February 2026 for 2027 entry). Scholarships apply to tuition fees only and do not apply to levies or other costs such as uniforms. The College does not offer 100% scholarships. Recipients must be Australian citizens, New Zealand citizens, or Permanent Residents, and are expected to actively participate in a minimum of two co-curricular activities. Scholarships are reviewed each semester and subject to continued excellence in academic performance and adherence to College policies.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or Year 10; Must be Australian citizens, New Zealand citizens, or Permanent Residents; Temporary visa holders not eligible; Current students or those completing a St Peters online application (with application fee paid); Must participate in minimum 2 co-curricular activities; Free of means test
special_conditions: If student transfers to another Brisbane high school before end of Year 12, parents must repay scholarship percentage received. Scholarships form a confidential arrangement between College and family. Scholarships apply to tuition fees only, not levies or other costs.

overview: St Peters Lutheran College offers Excellence in Music Scholarships to students demonstrating exceptional musical ability and commitment. These scholarships recognise students in Years 7-11 who excel in instrumental, vocal, or ensemble performance.
description: Excellence in Music Scholarships are offered to new and existing students in Years 7-11. Applications are now open for 2027 entry. To apply, students must supply a high-quality digital video recording of approximately 10 minutes of performance and a statement of recent repertoire, prizes, awards, and other relevant material. A live audition may also be requested. Applicants must be Australian citizens, New Zealand citizens, or Permanent Residents. Temporary visa holders are not eligible. The College does not offer 100% scholarships. Recipients must participate in a minimum of two co-curricular activities and maintain adherence to College policies. Further details are available in the Excellence Scholarship Guidelines.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7-11; Must be Australian citizens, New Zealand citizens, or Permanent Residents; Temporary visa holders not eligible; Current students or new applicants to the College; Must demonstrate musical excellence; Required to participate in minimum 2 co-curricular activities
special_conditions: High-quality video recording of approximately 10 minutes of performance required; Statement of recent repertoire and awards required; Live audition may be requested; Scholarships do not offer 100% coverage; Recipients must participate in minimum 2 co-curricular activities; Scholarships are confidential arrangements between College and family.

overview: St Peters Lutheran College offers Boarding Scholarships to rural and remote students who wish to board at the College. These scholarships provide an opportunity for rural or regional students who exemplify the values and ethos of St Peters to join as a boarder, typically in Year 10.
description: Boarding Scholarships at St Peters Lutheran College offer a maximum 20% reduction in tuition fees only for students who are Australian citizens or Permanent Residents residing in a rural or remote region of Australia. Applications for 2027 are now open. All applicants must be current students or have completed a St Peters online application (including payment of application fee) before a scholarship application can be considered. Applicants must reside in a rural or remote region of Australia to be eligible. The scholarship applies to tuition fees only and does not cover levies or other costs such as uniforms. The College does not offer 100% scholarships. Recipients must adhere to College policies and maintain good standing. Please review the Boarding Scholarship guidelines for full eligibility criteria prior to submitting an application.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students who reside in rural or remote regions of Australia; Must be Australian citizens or Permanent Residents; Must be current students or have completed St Peters online application with fee payment; Reside more than 200km from school boarding facilities (typical requirement); Must exemplify College values and ethos
special_conditions: Maximum 20% reduction in tuition fees only (not levies or other costs). Scholarships form confidential arrangement between College and family. Does not offer 100% scholarships. Applicants must be from rural or remote regions. Review Boarding Scholarship guidelines for full eligibility.

overview: St Peters Lutheran College offers Rural Leadership Scholarships to exceptional students from remote or rural regions whose families may be unable to consider St Peters due to financial reasons. These scholarships recognise leadership potential and excellence across academic, music, drama, visual arts, and sporting fields.
description: Rural Leadership Scholarships are offered to exceptional students who live more than 200km away from St Peters Indooroopilly and whose families may be unable to consider St Peters due to financial reasons. These scholarships provide the opportunity to join as a boarder in Year 10 and benefit from the breadth of opportunities the College offers. Recipients are paired with a mentor in the business and leadership community. To be eligible, applicants must demonstrate exceptional leadership and academic, music, drama, visual art, or sporting excellence, plus a desire and willingness to participate in a wide range of activities outside the academic program (including leadership and service to others). Applicants must be Australian citizens or Permanent Residents only. Applications for 2026 are now closed but will reopen for 2027 candidates in February 2026. The scholarship is funded by the Richards Foundation.
eligibility_criteria: Live more than 200km away from St Peters Indooroopilly; Families unable to afford fees due to financial reasons; Must demonstrate exceptional leadership qualities; Academic, music, drama, visual art or sporting excellence required; Desire to participate in wide range of cocurricular activities and service to others; Must be Australian citizens or Permanent Residents only; Available for Year 10 entry
special_conditions: Recipients are paired with a mentor in business and leadership community. Mentors provide guidance on life and leadership decisions. Scholarships form confidential arrangement. Recipients must actively participate in minimum 2 co-curricular activities. Funded by Richards Foundation. Students must reside more than 200km from school. Available for Year 10 entry.

overview: St Paul's School offers Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding achievement in students entering Year 7. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning.
description: St Paul's School's Academic Scholarship provides up to a 50% reduction in fees for Year 7 to Year 12. Scholarships are awarded on the results of the scholarship examination administered by EduTest. The exam assesses academic capability across key learning areas. Academic scholarships are available for current St Paul's students in Year 6 in 2025 and external students who would enter St Paul's in Year 7 in 2026. There is a $100 application fee applicable to sit the examination. Scholarships provide valuable financial assistance to eligible students.
eligibility_criteria: Open to external students entering Year 7; Open to current St Paul's Year 6 students; Australian residents only; Must sit the scholarship examination; Academic ability demonstrated through exam performance
special_conditions: Scholarship exam held on Saturday 22 March at St Paul's School. Registration made through EduTest. Scholarships valid for Year 7 to Year 12. Remote testing available for students living interstate, overseas or significant distance from school with 3 weeks' prior notice.

overview: St Paul's School offers Performing Arts Scholarships for students who excel in music, drama or dance. These specialist scholarships recognise exceptional talent in the performing arts and support students pursuing excellence in these disciplines.
description: St Paul's School provides Performing Arts Scholarships for students demonstrating excellence in music, drama, or dance. The scholarship program supports talented performers with financial assistance for fees. Students applying for a specialist scholarship must sit the academic scholarship test in addition to providing specialist performance evidence. Applications require submission of a specialist scholarship application form to the Principal by the specified deadline. The value and duration of awards provide meaningful support for Year 7 to 12 students. Selection is based on audition, portfolio or interview assessment of artistic talent and commitment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Excellence in music, drama or dance; Australian residents only; Must submit specialist scholarship application form; Must sit the academic scholarship examination; Application deadline 4:00pm Friday 14 March 2025
special_conditions: Specialist scholarship applications due to Principal by 4:00pm Friday 14 March 2025. Applicants must sit the academic scholarship exam. Specialist category includes music, drama and dance talent. Scholarships offered in June 2025.

overview: St Paul's School offers Sport Scholarships for students who excel in any given sport. The scholarship recognises exceptional athletic ability and commitment to sporting excellence.
description: St Paul's School provides Sport Scholarships for students demonstrating exceptional athletic ability in any sport. Students applying for a specialist sport scholarship must sit the academic scholarship test in addition to providing evidence of sporting achievement. Applications require submission of a specialist scholarship application form to the Principal with sporting credentials and evidence of excellence. The scholarship provides financial support for Year 7 to 12 students pursuing sporting excellence. Selection is based on demonstrated sporting ability, competition results, and commitment to sport at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Excellence in any sport; Australian residents only; Must submit specialist scholarship application form; Must sit the academic scholarship examination; Application deadline 4:00pm Friday 14 March 2025
special_conditions: Specialist scholarship applications due to Principal by 4:00pm Friday 14 March 2025. Applicants must sit the academic scholarship exam. Sport scholarship category for students excelling in any given sport. Scholarships offered in June 2025.

overview: St Paul's School offers Indigenous Scholarships to provide scholarship funding for members of the community with Indigenous heritage who may not have the financial means. Full or part scholarships are offered to Indigenous students to cover the cost of tuition.
description: St Paul's School's Indigenous Scholarship program provides full or part scholarships covering tuition costs for Indigenous students. Depending on the family's level of financial need, a St Paul's Indigenous Scholarship will cover 20% to 100% of the cost of tuition at St Paul's School from Year 7 to the end of Year 12. Students wishing to enter the school at another grade level may be considered. The scholarship is awarded based on family financial need and commitment to education. Indigenous heritage status and financial assessment determine the level of support provided to eligible students and families.
eligibility_criteria: Must have Indigenous heritage; Australian residents; Open from Year 7 to Year 12; Family financial need assessment required; Students may enter at other year levels; Must demonstrate commitment to education and school participation
special_conditions: Scholarship covers 20% to 100% of tuition costs based on financial need. Students required to put in best effort to studies and participate in school's extra-curricular program. All bursary and scholarship applications and offers are to be treated as private and confidential.

overview: St Paul's School offers Bursaries awarded on the basis of family need. These means-tested financial assistance programs support families who demonstrate financial difficulty in paying full school fees.
description: St Paul's School provides Bursaries to support families experiencing financial need. Bursaries are awarded on the basis of family need through a formal financial assessment process. The school works with families to understand their financial circumstances and provide appropriate support. Applications should be directed to the Registrar at the school. Bursary offers are treated as confidential and private. Financial assistance is provided to ensure worthy students can access St Paul's education regardless of financial barriers. The application and assessment process is conducted separately from scholarship assessments.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all year levels; Family financial need must be demonstrated; Formal financial assessment required; Contact registrar for application process; Confidential application process
special_conditions: All Bursary applications and offers are to be treated as private and confidential. Families assessed on individual financial circumstances. Award amount varies based on demonstrated need. Contact Registrar for application forms and requirements.

overview: St Rita's College offers the Nano Nagle Bursary program to support education opportunities for girls from families who without financial assistance would not be able to afford a St Rita's College education. The program recognises excellence or high potential in academic, music or sporting disciplines and is means-tested.
description: The Nano Nagle Bursary program specifically supports education to girls from families who without financial assistance would not be able to attend St Rita's and experience the benefits of a St Rita's College education. Bursaries are awarded based on financial need assessment, with financial information including parental taxation returns, statements of assets and liabilities, and bank statements submitted as part of the application. Bursaries are awarded for one or more years and not more than a maximum of eight years. A Bursary awarded for a period longer than one year will be subject to annual review by the Principal. The bursary amount is credited to the student's fee account and not paid directly to the student or parent/guardian.
eligibility_criteria: Open to non-St Rita's College students; Year levels 5-12; Demonstrated financial need; Australian citizen or permanent resident of Australia; Commitment to the ethos of St Rita's College; Adherence to all aspects of the Student Behaviour Code; Commitment to the academic and/or cocurricular learning expectations of the College; Student is committed to the Catholic faith; Commitment to involvement in College life; Student will be enrolled at the College for no less than one year; Submitted financial documentation including parental/guardian taxation returns, statement of assets/liabilities/income/expenditure, and bank statements not more than 12 months old
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications are open all year round for students that have lodged an enrolment to commence at the College in Years 5-12. Applications are not open to current students of the College. Bursaries are awarded for one or more years and not more than a maximum of eight years. A Bursary awarded for a period longer than one year will be subject to annual review by the Principal. The College holds the right to revoke a Bursary should information leading to the awarding of a Bursary be found to be false or misleading or the awarded student is found to be in breach of College policies. If shortlisted, student and family attend an interview with the Principal.

overview: St Rita's College offers the Nano Nagle Bursary for Academic Excellence to girls demonstrating exceptional academic ability who come from families requiring financial assistance. This bursary recognises and supports academic excellence combined with financial need.
description: The Nano Nagle Bursary program offers financial assistance to girls demonstrating excellence or high potential in academic disciplines whose families without financial assistance would not be able to afford a St Rita's College education. Applicants can apply in the academic performance category. The program is means-tested, requiring submission of comprehensive financial documentation including parental taxation returns, statements of assets, liabilities, income and expenditure for the previous 12 months, and bank statements. Bursaries are awarded for one or more years and not more than a maximum of eight years, with annual review for awards longer than one year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to non-St Rita's College students; Year levels 5-12; Demonstrated academic excellence or high potential; Financial need demonstrated through means-testing; Australian citizen or permanent resident of Australia; Commitment to the ethos of St Rita's College; Adherence to all aspects of the Student Behaviour Code; Commitment to the academic and/or cocurricular learning expectations of the College; Student is committed to the Catholic faith; Commitment to involvement in College life
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications are open all year round for students that have lodged an enrolment to commence at the College in Years 5-12. Applications are not open to current students of the College. Shortlisted applicants and families attend an interview with the Principal. Bursary amount is not paid to student or parent but credited to the fee account. Annual review required for awards longer than one year.

overview: St Rita's College offers the Nano Nagle Bursary for Music Excellence to girls demonstrating excellence or high potential in music performance who come from families requiring financial assistance. This bursary recognises musical talent combined with demonstrated financial need.
description: The Nano Nagle Bursary program offers financial assistance to girls demonstrating excellence or high potential in music disciplines whose families without financial assistance would not be able to afford a St Rita's College education. Applicants can apply in the music performance category. The program is means-tested, requiring submission of comprehensive financial documentation. Selection involves assessment of musical ability and potential alongside financial circumstances. Bursaries are awarded for one or more years and not more than a maximum of eight years, with annual review conducted for awards longer than one year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to non-St Rita's College students; Year levels 5-12; Demonstrated excellence or high potential in music; Financial need demonstrated through means-testing; Australian citizen or permanent resident of Australia; Commitment to the ethos of St Rita's College; Adherence to all aspects of the Student Behaviour Code; Commitment to the academic and/or cocurricular learning expectations of the College; Student is committed to the Catholic faith; Commitment to involvement in College life
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications are open all year round for students that have lodged an enrolment to commence at the College in Years 5-12. Applications are not open to current students of the College. Applicants will undergo an audition as part of the selection process. Shortlisted applicants and families attend an interview with the Principal. Bursary amount is not paid to student or parent but credited to the fee account.

overview: St Rita's College offers the Nano Nagle Bursary for Sports Excellence to girls demonstrating excellence or high potential in sporting disciplines who come from families requiring financial assistance. This bursary recognises sporting achievement and potential combined with demonstrated financial need.
description: The Nano Nagle Bursary program offers financial assistance to girls demonstrating excellence or high potential in sporting disciplines whose families without financial assistance would not be able to afford a St Rita's College education. Applicants can apply in the sports performance category. The program is means-tested, requiring submission of comprehensive financial documentation including taxation returns, asset and liability statements, and bank statements not more than 12 months in issue. Selection involves assessment of sporting ability and potential alongside financial circumstances. Bursaries are awarded for one or more years and not more than a maximum of eight years, with annual review for longer-term awards.
eligibility_criteria: Open to non-St Rita's College students; Year levels 5-12; Demonstrated excellence or high potential in sports; Financial need demonstrated through means-testing; Australian citizen or permanent resident of Australia; Commitment to the ethos of St Rita's College; Adherence to all aspects of the Student Behaviour Code; Commitment to the academic and/or cocurricular learning expectations of the College; Student is committed to the Catholic faith; Commitment to involvement in College life
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications are open all year round for students that have lodged an enrolment to commence at the College in Years 5-12. Applications are not open to current students of the College. Shortlisted applicants and families attend an interview with the Principal. Bursary amount is not paid to student or parent but credited to the fee account. Annual review required for awards longer than one year.

program_name: Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund (MacKillop Fund)
overview: St Sebastian's Primary School participates in the Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund, which provides bursary assistance to families experiencing significant financial hardship. The fund aims to ensure Catholic education remains accessible to children in need within the Archdiocese of Brisbane.
description: The Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund (MacKillop Fund) provides financial assistance to families experiencing extreme hardship, trauma and financial devastation who wish to access Catholic education through St Sebastian's. The bursary includes full tuition, textbooks, uniforms and local excursions for primary school students. Families must be experiencing significant financial hardship and reside within the Archdiocese of Brisbane. Applications must be submitted in the year before enrolment commences, and the bursary can continue through the duration of the student's primary schooling as long as the family remains in need of assistance. This fund is named in honour of Saint Mary of the Cross MacKillop, Australia's first saint who dedicated her life to ensuring poor and marginalised children had access to Catholic education.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing significant financial hardship; Must reside within the Archdiocese of Brisbane; Must apply in the year before enrolment commences; New students only; Must demonstrate genuine financial need through application process; Open to all year levels Prep to Year 6
special_conditions: Applications must be submitted in the year before enrolment commences. Bursary is available for new students only. Bursary funding continues through to completion of primary schooling as long as the family remains in need of assistance. The fund is administered by Brisbane Catholic Education through the Catholic Foundation. For further information contact mackillopfund@bne.catholic.edu.au or phone 07 3033 7454.

overview: St Teresa's Catholic College offers fee concessions to families experiencing genuine financial hardship. The program provides support to ensure that enrolment is not compromised for families facing short or long-term inability to pay fees.
description: St Teresa's Catholic College has a formal fee concession program available to families experiencing financial hardship. For families facing short or long-term genuine inability to pay school fees, the college is committed to providing support to ensure that enrolment is not compromised.
