Families must apply directly through the College Finance Office and provide supporting financial documentation. Applications are reviewed using the Vision, Mission, and Strategic Directions of Brisbane Catholic Education and St Teresa's Catholic College. The concession application process is handled confidentially by the Finance Office. The exact level of fee relief varies based on individual family circumstances and is determined on a case-by-case basis.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing genuine financial hardship; Demonstrable short or long-term inability to pay school fees; Application with supporting financial documentation; Compliance with the ethos and values of St Teresa's Catholic College
special_conditions: All matters are dealt with on a confidential basis. Applications are reviewed using Brisbane Catholic Education policies and procedures. Families must contact the College Business Manager for further information and to obtain the concession application form.

overview: St Rita's Primary School offers a Fee Concession Program to families experiencing financial hardship. The program recognises families who cannot otherwise afford the full tuition fees and provides fee relief based on demonstrated financial need using an equitable formula consistent with Brisbane Catholic Education guidelines.
description: St Rita's Primary School offers fee concessions to eligible families experiencing financial hardship. The concession is available on the basis of a just and equitable formula reflecting financial hardship and is consistent with Brisbane Catholic Education guidelines. The financial assistance provides a reduction in annual school fees (exact amount varies based on individual family circumstances and financial assessment). The granting of a fee concession is conditional on the family agreeing to a regular payment plan for school fee payments. Fee concessions are reviewed annually to ensure ongoing appropriateness and need assessment. Parents seeking fee concessions must contact the school Finance Coordinator to obtain and submit an Application for Concession on Fees form.
eligibility_criteria: Open to enrolled students and prospective students; Families must demonstrate financial hardship; Application required showing financial circumstances; Agreement to regular payment plan required; Annual review and reapplication required
special_conditions: Granting of a fee concession is conditional on the family agreeing to a regular payment plan for school fee payments. Fee concessions are reviewed annually and eligibility assessed on a just and equitable formula reflecting financial hardship consistent with Brisbane Catholic Education guidelines.

overview: St Stephen's Catholic College offers scholarships for students entering Year 7. The college recognises exceptional academic ability and provides scholarship opportunities to support students commencing secondary education.
description: St Stephen's Catholic College provides scholarships available for Year 7 students entering secondary education at the college. The scholarship program forms part of the college's commitment to recognising and supporting academic excellence. Specific financial value, selection criteria, and application procedures are determined through the college's enrolment process. Interested families should contact the college directly or refer to the enrolment handbook for complete scholarship application details and requirements.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Applicants must be commencing secondary education; Selection based on academic merit
special_conditions: Specific scholarship details are referenced in the 2024 Scholarship Applications page on the college website. Contact the college directly for full application details, selection criteria, and financial value information.

overview: St Rita's School South Johnstone offers financial hardship assistance to families experiencing genuine financial difficulty. No child will be denied a Catholic education because of financial hardship.
description: Families experiencing genuine financial hardship are able to make confidential arrangements with the Principal to discuss fee concessions. The school has implemented a supportive approach where no child is denied a Catholic education due to financial difficulties. Families can access the Fees and Levies Hardship Concession Form to formally apply for assistance. The current annual fee is $600 per student per year ($150 per term), which is significantly reduced from previous levels to support the local South Johnstone community. Sibling discounts also apply for families with two or more students enrolled.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students enrolled or enrolling at St Rita's School South Johnstone; Must demonstrate genuine financial hardship; Family must make confidential arrangements with the Principal
special_conditions: Applications are confidential and handled through the Principal. Families may be required to complete the Fees and Levies Hardship Concession Form. The school reserves the right to review circumstances annually.

overview: St Therese's Catholic Primary School offers family discounts for families with multiple children enrolled in Catholic Education – Diocese of Rockhampton schools. This support program recognises the financial commitment families make when educating more than one child in the Catholic system.
description: St Therese's Catholic Primary School, as part of Catholic Education – Diocese of Rockhampton (CEDR), offers substantial family discounts to families with 2 or more children attending diocesan schools. A 7.5% discount is applied to the All-Inclusive Education fee for families with 2 siblings attending CEDR schools; a 20% discount is applied for 3 siblings; and a 30% discount is applied for 4 or more siblings. The discount is applied annually by completing the Confirmation of Enrolment form issued to all families. These discounts are intended to make Catholic education more affordable and accessible for families with multiple children.
eligibility_criteria: Families with 2 or more children enrolled in Catholic Education – Diocese of Rockhampton schools; Must complete Confirmation of Enrolment form annually
special_conditions: Discount applied annually. Families must complete the Confirmation of Enrolment form to activate the family discount.

overview: St Therese's Catholic Primary School provides a 40% discount on school fees for families holding eligible Australian government Health Care or Pensioner Concession Cards. This support program ensures families experiencing financial hardship can access Catholic education.
description: St Therese's Catholic Primary School, as part of the Diocese of Rockhampton Catholic system, offers a 40% discount applied to the All-Inclusive Education fee for families who hold a valid means-tested Australian government Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card. The concession applies to eligible families meeting the specific card requirements. The discount is applied after family discounts have been applied. This program commenced in Term 1, 2017. If a family presents a valid concession card part way through the term, the concession will apply for that whole term. The discount applies where the applicant's Health Care or Pensioner Card is valid.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold a valid means-tested Australian government Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card; Card must meet eligibility criteria; Applied after family discounts
special_conditions: Families experiencing financial hardship without a concession card are encouraged to discuss fee discounts directly with the school Principal, who will treat the situation confidentially. Total or partial exemption from payment of school fees may be arranged according to individual family circumstances. Families must advise the school of changes to card validity.

overview: St Therese's Catholic Primary School offers a 2.5% discount on school fees for families who pay their fees in full within 30 days of the statement issue date. This incentive program rewards prompt payment of school fees.
description: St Therese's Catholic Primary School provides a Prompt Payment Discount of 2.5% applied to the All-Inclusive Education fee for families who pay their fees in full within 30 days of the statement issue date. This discount is designed to encourage timely payment of school fees and assist families with their education expenses. The discount applies to the total fee balance and is part of the Diocese of Rockhampton's fee structure designed to make Catholic education accessible to families.
eligibility_criteria: Must pay school fees in full within 30 days of statement issue date
special_conditions: Discount applies only when full payment is made within 30 days of statement issue date.

overview: St Therese's Catholic Primary School provides confidential support for families experiencing financial hardship who may not hold a concession card. The school works with families to develop affordable fee arrangements tailored to individual circumstances.
description: St Therese's Catholic Primary School, in line with Diocese of Rockhampton policy, recognises that some families experience financial hardship and may not hold a concession card. New or existing parents/carers/guardians experiencing financial difficulty are encouraged to discuss fee discounts directly with the school Principal in a confidential manner. Through this consultation process, total or partial exemption from payment of school fees may be arranged according to individual family circumstances. The Diocese and school are committed to ensuring that no family is denied a Catholic school education due to financial constraints. The School Finance Officer is available to assist families in navigating the school fee structure.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; May or may not hold a concession card; Must discuss directly with school Principal
special_conditions: All discussions regarding financial hardship are treated confidentially. Arrangements are determined on an individual family basis. Total or partial exemption from school fees may be arranged. School Finance Officer contact: 4166 1654 or stm@rok.catholic.edu.au

overview: St Ursula's College offers scholarships each year to prospective students wishing to enrol at the College. Scholarships are also awarded to currently enrolled students who demonstrate extraordinary effort and achievement in one or more areas.
description: Scholarships are available each year to prospective students wishing to enrol at St Ursula's College and are also awarded to students currently attending St Ursula's College who demonstrate extraordinary effort and achievement in one or more areas. Scholarships are subject to availability of funds and are made possible due to the generous donations from Past Students and the Presentation Sisters. Detailed financial information and specific program details for 2027 enrolment will be available on the website in Term 1, 2026. Applications should be submitted to the Business Manager and will be treated in confidence.
eligibility_criteria: Open to prospective students wishing to enrol at St Ursula's College; Open to current students demonstrating extraordinary effort and achievement in one or more areas; Scholarships subject to availability of funds
special_conditions: Scholarships are subject to availability of funds and are made possible due to the generous donations from Past Students and the Presentation Sisters. More detailed information will be available in Term 1, 2026.

overview: St Ursula's College offers financial assistance and bursaries to families experiencing financial difficulty. These concessions are provided to ensure accessibility for families who might otherwise be unable to afford a St Ursula's College education.
description: St Ursula's College provides financial assistance and bursaries to families facing financial difficulty. Concessions are considered where a demonstrated need exists due to financial circumstances. In the event of financial difficulty, parents are urged to contact the Principal or Business Manager to discuss special payment arrangements. Applications for assistance are to be submitted on the relevant form to the Business Manager and will be treated in confidence. Extended payment arrangements may be available to support families with ongoing financial needs.
eligibility_criteria: Families facing financial difficulty; Demonstrated financial need; Open to both prospective and current families
special_conditions: Applications treated in confidence. Concessions are considered where demonstrated need exists due to financial circumstances. Students cannot hold multiple scholarships or bursaries simultaneously.

overview: St Vincent's Primary School offers fee concession assistance to families experiencing financial hardship. The school provides flexible support options to ensure that financial circumstances do not prevent enrolment.
description: For families experiencing short or long-term genuine inability to pay fees, St Vincent's Primary School is committed to providing support to ensure that enrolment is not compromised. Concession applications are accepted at the commencement of each year or at any point initiated by the family. In cases of financial hardship, an application may be made for a fee concession. Concessions are issued for a maximum period of 12 months within a calendar year. Consideration for a subsequent 12-month period will require a new application. A compassionate and just approach under the mission and values of Brisbane Catholic Education and St Vincent's Primary School is used when reviewing applications.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing short or long-term genuine inability to pay fees; Application may be made at any time during the school year; Demonstrated financial hardship required
special_conditions: Concessions are issued for a maximum period of 12 months within a calendar year. Consideration for a subsequent 12-month period will require a new application. A compassionate and just approach under the mission and values of Brisbane Catholic Education and St Vincent's Primary School is used when reviewing applications.

program_name: MacKillop Catholic School Access Fund (MacKillop Bursary)
overview: The MacKillop Fund provides access to Catholic education for new families experiencing significant financial hardship who live within the Archdiocese of Brisbane. The bursary covers tuition, fees and camps for each year of primary school, supporting demonstrated financial need.
description: The MacKillop Catholic School Access Fund is available to new students only and must be applied for in the year before enrolment commences. Bursaries are awarded on the basis of demonstrable, means-tested inability to afford a Catholic education. Support from the fund is not dependent on academic ability of the student. The bursary covers tuition, fees and camps for each year of primary school. Parents typically provide a weekly financial contribution in an agreement negotiated with the school. Bursaries are awarded at the discretion of the committee and is based on greatest need, and funding available. Supporting financial documentation is necessary before any expression of interest form can be processed.
eligibility_criteria: New students only; Must apply in year before enrolment commences; Demonstrated financial hardship required; Must hold health care card or equivalent financial documentation; Family must be supportive of Catholic ethos, values and mission; Special consideration given to students who are socially, culturally, spiritually or emotionally disadvantaged
special_conditions: New students only. Application must be submitted by 15 April for enrolment in the following school year. Committee meets in May every year to award bursaries for the following school year. Late expressions of interest may be considered if funding available. Currently enrolled students at Brisbane Catholic Education schools are not eligible for a MacKillop Bursary to continue at their current school. Strictly confidential assessment process; names and details of recipients are not publicly revealed. Parents provide weekly financial contribution negotiated with school. Supporting documentation including health care card required.

program_name: Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund
overview: St William's Primary School participates in the Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund, which provides financial assistance to Catholic families experiencing significant hardship. The bursary recognises students whose families have a demonstrated inability to afford Catholic education whilst sharing the values and mission of the school.
description: The Mary MacKillop Brisbane Catholic School Access Fund provides tuition assistance to students whose families are experiencing significant financial hardship, trauma, and financial devastation. The bursary includes tuition (for primary school), textbooks, uniforms, and local excursions. The program is open to new students only and applications must be submitted in the year before enrolment commences. The selection process includes a comprehensive financial assessment demonstrating means-tested inability to afford Catholic education. Awards are based on demonstrated financial need rather than academic ability, and families must support the ethos and values of Catholic education. The bursary value varies depending on individual family circumstances and available funds. Applications are assessed annually with awards determined in May each year for the following school year.
eligibility_criteria: New students only; Must apply in the year before enrolment commences; Families experiencing significant financial hardship; Demonstrated inability to afford Catholic education (e.g. possession of health care card); Family supportive of Catholic education ethos, values and mission; Must live within the Archdiocese of Brisbane; Expression of interest deadline is 15 April for enrolment in the following school year
special_conditions: This bursary is available to new students only and must be applied for in the year before enrolment commences. Bursaries are awarded on the basis of demonstrable, means-tested inability to afford a Catholic education. Support from the fund is not dependent on academic ability. Late expressions of interest may be considered depending on availability of funds. The committee meets in May every year to award bursaries for the following school year. For more information contact mackillopfund@bne.catholic.edu.au or phone 07 3033 7454.

program_name: Fee Concessions for Families in Financial Difficulty
overview: St William's Primary School offers fee concessions to families experiencing financial difficulty, in accordance with Catholic educational principles. The school recognises that no child should be refused a Catholic education due to financial inability or difficulty.
description: In keeping with Gospel values and Catholic educational principles, St William's Primary School acknowledges that no child should be refused a Catholic education due to financial inability or difficulty. New or existing parents who find themselves in financial hardship are encouraged to discuss fee concessions with the school Principal. The discussion will be treated with understanding, sensitivity and confidentiality. Parents experiencing difficulties in paying their school fees should contact the office finance staff as soon as possible, preferably before the due date, to discuss available options and arrangements. Fee concessions are available for both current and new families and are handled on a case-by-case basis at the discretion of the Principal. The school recognises that families may need an avenue for discussion about the procedure and capacity to pay school fees.
eligibility_criteria: Experiencing financial difficulty or hardship; Existing or new families; Discussion required with Principal; Ability to demonstrate financial need
special_conditions: All discussions are treated with understanding, sensitivity and confidentiality. Parents should contact the office finance staff before the fee due date to discuss their situation. This is a direct school-based program separate from the MacKillop Fund and is handled at the discretion of the school Principal.

overview: St Thomas' School provides automatic tuition fee discounts to families holding a means-tested Australian government health care or pensioner concession card. This program ensures that families experiencing financial hardship can access Catholic education without being excluded due to inability to pay fees.
description: St Thomas' School offers a 70% discount to tuition fees (not including school levies) for parents and carers who hold a means-tested Australian government health care or pensioner concession card. Card holders must advise the school of their concession card at enrolment or when the card is gained. Once the card is sighted by the school's administration, tuition fees will be adjusted accordingly. This discount is part of the school's commitment to ensuring no child is denied enrolment due to financial difficulty. The school's expressed policy is to assist any family facing hardship in meeting fees.
eligibility_criteria: Hold a valid means-tested Australian government health care or pensioner concession card; Advise school at enrolment or when card is gained; Card must be sighted by school administration
special_conditions: Discount applies to tuition fees only and does not include school levies. Discount is conditional upon card sighting by school administration. The school's policy states that no child will be denied enrolment due to financial difficulty.

overview: St Thomas' School provides a hardship concession program for families experiencing genuine financial difficulty in paying tuition fees. The school is committed to ensuring that financial circumstances do not prevent students from accessing Catholic education.
description: St Thomas' School offers special consideration for families experiencing genuine financial hardship. Families not holding a means-tested concession card who are experiencing financial difficulty in paying fees can speak with their school Principal or Parish Priest to apply for special consideration. The same process used for concession card holders will apply in determining an appropriate fee concession arrangement. The school's formal policy states that no child will be denied enrolment due to financial difficulty. A Hardship Concession Form is available to facilitate applications. Fees can be paid on a weekly, fortnightly, monthly, or term basis according to family preference.
eligibility_criteria: Experiencing genuine financial hardship; Contact Principal or Parish Priest for application; Apply using Hardship Concession Form; Families not eligible for concession card discounts
special_conditions: Assessed on a case-by-case basis by Principal or Parish Priest. Discretionary fee arrangements are determined based on individual family circumstances. The school's policy ensures no child is denied enrolment due to financial difficulty.

school_name: Star of the Sea Catholic Primary School
overview: Star of the Sea Catholic Primary School offers financial assistance to families requiring support with school fees. The school recognises that circumstances vary for each family and is committed to supporting those experiencing financial difficulty in accessing quality Catholic education.
description: Star of the Sea Gladstone provides financial assistance to families who require support with school fees. Families experiencing financial hardship are encouraged to contact the School Finance Officer to discuss their situation. The school operates an All-Inclusive Education fee structure that covers the curriculum offering, plus a Building Levy that funds capital costs and contemporary educational spaces. The school may also offer specific purpose charges for activities outside the standard curriculum. Families can discuss flexible payment arrangements and assistance options directly with the Finance Officer on 4972 1350 or sosg_finance@rok.catholic.edu.au. The school takes a confidential, individualised approach to assessing financial assistance based on family circumstances and demonstrated need.
eligibility_criteria: Open to families enrolled at Star of the Sea Catholic Primary School; Families experiencing financial hardship; Demonstrated need based on family financial circumstances; Confidential assessment by school finance team
special_conditions: Families must contact the school directly to discuss their financial circumstances. Confidentiality is maintained throughout the process. The school works with families to develop individualised support arrangements. Any assistance offered is discretionary and based on demonstrated financial need and the school's available resources.

overview: Stretton State College offers the Lighthouse Academic Excellence Program to highly capable students who demonstrate strong interest and commitment toward academic achievement. The program provides an academically rigorous curriculum with extended learning opportunities, grouping students with like-minded peers to develop higher order thinking skills.
description: The Lighthouse Academic Excellence Program prepares students for the digital world while ensuring they are highly numerate, literate and creative. High ability students are grouped in one class per Year level with a focus on developing, extending and enriching knowledge acquisition and higher order thinking from Years 4-9. Students are exposed to virtual classrooms and preloaded content enabling them to pre-read, accelerate assessment timelines and extend in project areas of interest. The program is highly competitive, with places awarded based on performance on the Australian Council for Educational Research General Ability Test, short writing task (Years 4-6), and school-based academic data. Places may also be offered based on merit and academic performance should positions become available during the year. Excellent academic performance and learning behaviours must be maintained throughout the year to retain position in the program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 4-9; Demonstrated high academic ability via ACER General Ability Test; School-based academic data and performance considered; Short writing task required for Years 4-6 only; Excellent behaviour and learning engagement required; Must maintain strong academic performance and learning behaviours to retain position; Places are highly competitive with large number of applicants annually
year_levels: Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9
special_conditions: All Lighthouse students are required to supply their own ICT device as part of the school's BYOD Program. For Years 4 and 5 the approved device is an iPad only. For Years 6-9 a range of devices are acceptable. Positions are awarded annually and must be maintained through continued excellent academic performance and learning behaviours.

overview: Stretton State College offers the Sports Academy Excellence Program to elite athletes in Years 4-12 who wish to develop their skills at a high level in football, rugby league and volleyball. The program allows students to play in regional, state and national competitions while maintaining academic achievement.
description: The Stretton State College Sports Academy provides an opportunity for athletes in Years 4-12 to develop their skills and compete at a high level in Football, Rugby League and Volleyball. Students train for the duration of the year in their chosen sport and have the opportunity to participate in local, state and interstate competitions. All students wishing to be part of the Sports Academy are required to submit an application and complete a trial for their respective sport each year. Students must provide a copy of their most recent academic report cards. Academic achievement, behaviour and effort results are taken into consideration when selections are made. Students in the Sports Academy also attend a sports excellence camp. Students are expected to maintain their academic achievement as well as their effort and behaviour in all classes. The program offers pathways to compete in elite sports competitions across South East Queensland.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 4-12; Must participate in trial for chosen sport (Football, Rugby League or Volleyball); Required to provide most recent academic report cards; Academic achievement must be satisfactory; Behaviour and effort results considered in selection; Expected to maintain academic achievement while in program; Students must engage in all extra-curricular engagements provided by program
year_levels: Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Each year all students wishing to be part of the Sports Academy are required to submit an application and complete a trial for their respective sport. Academic achievement, behaviour and effort results are taken into consideration in selections. Students must maintain academic achievement and effort in other classes. Students are expected to engage in all extra-curricular engagements provided by the program. The school currently has capacity to enrol 675 students in the Sports Academy Program of Excellence.

overview: Stretton State College offers an Instrumental Music Program to Year 3 students who are selected through an audition and selection process. Students receive specialist instrumental tuition in brass, woodwind, percussion and string instruments and participate in multiple performing ensembles.
description: Stretton State College offers an extensive instrumental music program where students have the opportunity to learn how to play an instrument from an instrumental music specialist. Through group tuition students have the opportunity to learn a woodwind, brass, percussion or string instrument. A range of performing ensembles allow students to rehearse and perform in large group settings that cater for student abilities and interests. Each year, students in Year 3 have the opportunity to be selected for the instrumental music program. Students interested undertake a selection process outlined in the Stretton State College Instrumental Music Parent Handbook. All instrumental music students receive lessons with a specialist teacher. Timetables are provided each term and students may negotiate to reschedule lessons if they cannot attend their scheduled time. The program has a proud history of success including Education Queensland's Fanfare Festival Regional Finalists status, international performances and multiple ensemble opportunities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 3 students; Must undertake selection process as outlined in Instrumental Music Parent Handbook; Selection based on demonstrated interest and aptitude for instrumental music; Unconditional commitment required in specified areas upon acceptance; Families must consider program requirements before committing; Information about recruitment process sent to Year 3 families via email
special_conditions: Students who accept an offer to join the Instrumental Music Program must provide unconditional commitment as outlined in the IM Handbook. Families should consider these requirements before committing. Students continue lessons throughout subsequent years with specialist instrumental music teachers. Instruments available include brass, woodwind, percussion and strings. 18 different college ensembles are available including Concert Bands, String Ensembles, Jazz Ensembles, Percussion Ensembles and others catering to different skill levels.

school_name: Star of the Sea Catholic School

overview: St Ursula's College offers Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding achievement in prospective and current students. The scholarships recognise exceptional academic ability and are awarded based on scholarship examination results.
description: Academic Scholarships provide a reduction in tuition fees and, where relevant, boarding fees will be considered. Scholarships are awarded for the duration of either two or three years at the discretion of the Principal. Selection is based on performance in scholarship exams conducted in late Term One each year. Recipients must finish their secondary education, maintain attendance, uphold high academic and behavioural standards, and actively engage in all facets of College life including the co-curricular program. The Principal makes the final determination of awards.
eligibility_criteria: Open to prospective and current students; Must meet minimum academic qualification requirements; Students must demonstrate ability to meet high academic standards; Students must be willing to actively engage in College life and co-curricular programs
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded for either two or three years at the discretion of the Principal. Recipients must finish their secondary education, maintain attendance, uphold high academic and behavioural standards, and actively and positively engage in all facets of College life, encompassing the co-curricular program. The successful recipients of a scholarship would be expected to represent the College in their chosen field, as well as giving back in their chosen field through support of other students. Annual review required; failure to meet established criteria at review checkpoint will result in discontinuation of funding.

overview: St Ursula's College offers Sporting Scholarships to outstanding athletes who represent the College at high competitive levels. These scholarships recognise excellence and commitment in sporting pursuits.
description: Sporting Scholarships provide a reduction in tuition fees and, where relevant, boarding fees will be considered. Scholarships are awarded for either two or three years at the discretion of the Principal. To be eligible, students should be representing at state or national level in at least one of their chosen sports. As part of the application, a support letter from their coach or governing body is required to advise what the athlete has achieved and/or the pathway program they are on in their chosen sport. Recipients are expected to represent the College in their sporting field and support other students in their area.
eligibility_criteria: Open to prospective and current students; Must represent at state or national level in at least one chosen sport; Requires support letter from coach or governing body; Must maintain high academic and behavioural standards; Must actively engage in College life and co-curricular program
special_conditions: Sporting Scholarships will be awarded for either two or three years at the discretion of the Principal. Students must be representing at state or national level in at least one of their chosen sports. Recipients are required to finish their secondary education, maintain attendance, uphold high academic and behavioural standards, and actively and positively engage in all facets of College life, encompassing the co-curricular program. Recipients are expected to represent the College in their chosen field and support other students. Annual review required.

overview: St Ursula's College offers Cultural Scholarships to talented young women who excel in the arts. These scholarships recognise outstanding ability and commitment in music, drama, dance, and visual arts.
description: Cultural Scholarships provide a reduction in tuition fees and, where relevant, boarding fees will be considered. Scholarships are awarded for either two or three years at the discretion of the Principal. To apply, students must demonstrate extensive involvement in areas of the arts they excel in, such as AMEB exams or eisteddfod placings. For those who sing, dance, act or play a musical instrument, the application includes a video submission with up to 2 minutes of the student performing. Where appropriate, students should submit a portfolio of their work. A panel including the Head of Faculty reviews the work submitted. A support letter from someone who works with the student to endorse their application is required.
eligibility_criteria: Open to prospective and current students; Must demonstrate extensive involvement and excellence in arts (music, drama, dance or visual arts); Must provide evidence of achievement such as AMEB exams or eisteddfod placings; Must submit video or portfolio as required; Must provide endorsement letter from coach or mentor; Must maintain high academic and behavioural standards
special_conditions: Cultural Scholarships will be awarded for either two or three years at the discretion of the Principal. Applicants must demonstrate extensive involvement in areas of the arts they excel in. Video submission required for singers, dancers, actors and musicians (up to 2 minutes). Portfolio submission required where appropriate. Support letter from someone who works with the student required. Recipients are required to finish their secondary education, maintain attendance, uphold high academic and behavioural standards, and actively and positively engage in all facets of College life. Recipients are expected to represent the College in their chosen cultural field and support other students. Annual review required.

overview: St Ursula's College offers the Fee Financial Assistance Program to support young women whose families might not otherwise afford a St Ursula's College education. The program helps families manage educational costs through bursaries or extended payment arrangements.
description: The Fee Financial Assistance Program is designed to help young women whose families face financial difficulties. This assistance can be provided in the form of a bursary or extended payment arrangement. The program is open to both prospective and current families, ensuring students can start or continue their education at St Ursula's College. It is an annual, means-tested, application-based program requiring families to meet eligibility criteria and sign a statutory declaration. A commitment to the Catholic and Ursuline Tradition is essential, demonstrated through positive behaviour, high attendance, and active participation in the College's co-curricular and spiritual life. Applications are submitted to the Business Manager and treated in confidence.
eligibility_criteria: Open to prospective and current students and families; Must demonstrate genuine financial need through means-testing; Must commit to Catholic and Ursuline Tradition; Must demonstrate positive behaviour and high attendance; Must actively participate in College co-curricular and spiritual life; Families must sign statutory declaration
special_conditions: Annual means-tested, application-based program. Families must sign statutory declaration. A commitment to the Catholic and Ursuline Tradition is essential, demonstrated through positive behaviour, high attendance, and active participation in the College's co-curricular and spiritual life. Concessions will be considered where demonstrated need exists due to financial circumstances. Students may not hold multiple scholarships or bursaries simultaneously. Bursaries are awarded annually at the Principal's discretion.

overview: St Ursula's College offers the Indigenous Youth Leadership Program (IYLP) in partnership with the Smith Family for First Nations students. This scholarship provides opportunities for Indigenous young women to access secondary education and develop leadership skills.
description: The IYLP is a scholarship opportunity for First Nations students in partnership with The Smith Family. St Ursula's College is the only school in Southern Queensland approved for this collaboration. It is available to First Nations students across Years 7 to 10 (new and continuing students). Applications are due in May of each year for the following year. The program includes an interview process as part of the application. Families should note that these scholarships are determined by The Smith Family. Following the application process which includes an interview, successful applicants will be notified by The Smith Family. The IYLP supports scholarships and leadership opportunities, helping Indigenous students achieve their educational aspirations and supporting the development of positive role models and future leaders.
eligibility_criteria: Must be First Nations (Indigenous Australian) student; Open to Years 7 to 10; Open to new and continuing students; Applications submitted by May each year for following year; Application must include interview component
special_conditions: Scholarship is determined by The Smith Family, not St Ursula's College. Applications must be submitted by May of each year for enrolment in the following year. Application process includes an interview. Successful applicants are notified by The Smith Family. Applicants should contact the school for application details and process.

school_name: Star of the Sea Primary School

overview: Sunshine Coast Grammar School offers Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding academic achievement in students entering Years 7 to 11. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning through examination performance.
description: Academic Scholarships are offered for students entering Years 7 to 11 who demonstrate outstanding academic achievement. Scholarships are awarded on the basis of performance in entrance examination administered by Academic Assessment Services (AAS). The exact financial value varies depending on merit ranking, though third-party sources indicate scholarships typically provide substantial fee reduction worth thousands of dollars annually. Scholarships are awarded from the commencement of the following school year and continue subject to annual review and maintenance of specific ongoing criteria including academic performance and conduct standards.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, or Year 11; Must demonstrate outstanding academic achievement; Must sit AAS scholarship examination on Saturday 7 March 2026; Current applicants must be enrolled in respective year levels
special_conditions: Scholarships awarded come into effect from the commencement of the following school year. Students are expected to meet specific ongoing criteria. Scholarships are subject to annual review. College reserves the right to withdraw any scholarship in the event of unsatisfactory academic progress or conduct issues.

overview: Sunshine Coast Grammar School offers General Excellence Scholarships to recognise well-rounded students entering Years 7-11 who demonstrate high academic achievement combined with leadership qualities and significant co-curricular involvement. This scholarship celebrates excellence across multiple areas of school life.
description: General Excellence Scholarships are offered to students entering Years 7-11 who demonstrate high levels of academic achievement, evidence of leadership or community service and significant co-curricular participation. Candidates for General Excellence Scholarship apply as Academic candidates and are shortlisted from the applicants in the Academic Scholarship category based on demonstrated leadership, community involvement, and co-curricular achievements. The exact financial value varies based on merit ranking and selection outcome. Scholarships are awarded from the commencement of the following school year and are subject to specific ongoing criteria and annual review.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, or Year 11; Must demonstrate high levels of academic achievement; Must provide evidence of leadership or community service; Must show significant co-curricular participation; Selection by shortlisting from Academic Scholarship applicants
special_conditions: Candidates do not apply directly for General Excellence Scholarship; they are shortlisted from applicants in the Academic Scholarship category. Scholarships awarded come into effect from the commencement of the following school year. Students are expected to meet specific ongoing criteria. Scholarships are subject to annual review.

overview: The Principal's Scholarship recognises an exceptional candidate who demonstrates outstanding achievements across multiple areas of school life including academics, leadership, and personal contributions. This prestigious award is designed to support students who would not otherwise be able to access a Grammar education due to financial circumstances.
description: The Principal's Scholarship is awarded to students who demonstrate exceptional achievements across multiple areas of school life, including academics, leadership, and personal contributions. This scholarship is specifically designed to provide substantial financial assistance to an outstanding candidate who, through financial circumstances, would otherwise be unable to achieve a Grammar education. Candidates should apply as Academic candidates and will be evaluated based on overall excellence and demonstrated need. The scholarship provides significant financial support, with the specific value to be determined based on individual circumstances. Award comes into effect from the commencement of the following school year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students of exceptional academic, leadership and personal achievement; Demonstration of significant financial hardship or difficulty accessing Grammar education; Application as Academic candidate required; Assessment of financial need and overall merit
special_conditions: Awarded to a single outstanding candidate per year. Candidates should apply as Academic candidates. Scholarships awarded come into effect from the commencement of the following school year. Students are expected to meet specific ongoing criteria.

overview: Sunshine Coast Grammar School offers Music Scholarships to recognise students who demonstrate a high level of performance on at least one instrument and/or vocal performance. The scholarship supports musically talented students entering secondary education.
description: Music Scholarships are offered to students who demonstrate a high level of performance on at least one instrument and/or vocal performance. Auditions are open across all instrumentalists and vocalists. Students are required to submit short video performances on their chosen instrument/s and/or vocal performances at the time of application. Students will be shortlisted from these auditions, and successful shortlisted applicants will be invited to attend an in-person audition in March 2026. The exact financial value of the scholarship varies based on musical ability and performance level demonstrated. Scholarships come into effect from the commencement of the following school year and are subject to ongoing musical participation and conduct standards.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students demonstrating high level of performance on at least one instrument; Open to vocalists demonstrating high vocal performance ability; Must submit video performance(s) at time of application; Must be shortlisted for in-person audition; All instrumentalists and vocalists eligible
special_conditions: Students must submit short video performances on chosen instrument/s and/or vocal performances at time of application. Shortlisted applicants invited to in-person audition in March 2026. Scholarships awarded come into effect from the commencement of the following school year. Students are expected to meet specific ongoing criteria including musical participation.

overview: Sunshine Coast Grammar School offers Sport Scholarships to support students passionate about sport who are entering Years 7 to 11 in 2027. The scholarship recognises elite athletes with regional, state or national level representation and commitment to representing the school with excellence.
description: Sunshine Coast Grammar School offers Sport Scholarship opportunities for students entering Year 7 to Year 11 in 2027. The school highly values and supports students who are passionate about sport and is committed to supporting aspiring athletes as they work to achieve their goals. The school has introduced the Roar Performance program to assist scholarship holders on their sporting journeys, and all successful applicants will be automatically accepted into this program. The exact financial value varies based on athlete level and sport. Scholarships come into effect from the commencement of the following school year and require ongoing sporting representation at school and club level.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 to Year 11 in 2027; Must demonstrate evidence of representation in sport/sports at a high level (regional, state or national level); Must demonstrate commitment to representing Grammar with passion and purpose; Expectation to represent school in both school and club sports where applicable
special_conditions: All successful applicants automatically accepted into the Roar Performance Program. Scholarship holders are expected to represent the school in both school and club sports where an applicable club team exists. Scholarships awarded come into effect from the commencement of the following school year. Students are expected to meet specific ongoing criteria.

overview: The PMSA Teacher Education Scholarship is available to current Year 12 students or recent graduates (within the last two years) from PMSA schools. The scholarship supports students entering teacher education or early childhood education university degrees by providing substantial annual financial support for four years of study.
description: The PMSA Teacher Education Scholarships are now open for 2026. Available to either current Year 12 students or recent graduates (last two years) from PMSA schools, the scholarships are each worth $2,000 per year for up to four years of study for suitable students entering an undergraduate teacher education degree or early childhood education course. This is a great opportunity for graduates looking to pursue a future career in teaching and early childhood education. The scholarship supports students transitioning to tertiary study and recognises commitment to the teaching profession.
eligibility_criteria: Must be current Year 12 student OR recent graduate from PMSA school (last two years); Must be entering undergraduate teacher education degree; OR must be entering early childhood education course; Suitable student status determined by PMSA assessment
special_conditions: Scholarships worth $2,000 per year for up to four years of study. For more information applicants should visit the Scholarships page on the PMSA website. Limited to PMSA school students and recent graduates only.

overview: Sunshine Beach State High School offers the CREST Scholars Program for Year 7 students. The program provides enrichment opportunities for highly motivated students to develop critical thinking, communication, collaboration, creativity and innovation across STEM focus areas.
description: The Year 7 CREST scholars program is designed to provide enrichment opportunities for highly motivated students to develop their skills in critical thinking, communication, collaboration, creativity and innovation under the focus areas of STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics). Students must meet appropriate criteria for enrolment at Sunshine Beach State High School, such as residing within the school catchment. The program aims to support students pursuing their academic goals through advanced learning experiences in science, technology, engineering and mathematics. Selection is based on academic performance and demonstrated aptitude in STEM subjects. Program value and financial benefits are non-monetary, comprising enhanced curriculum access and enrichment activities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 students; Must meet Sunshine Beach State High School enrolment criteria; Must reside within school catchment or meet out-of-catchment criteria; Highly motivated students; Demonstrated aptitude in STEM
special_conditions: Applications for 2025 are now closed. School reserves the right to determine eligibility and selection criteria annually.

overview: Sunshine Beach State High School offers the Year 8 Academic Pipeline Program. The program enhances STEAM learning (Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts and Mathematics) to equip students with 21st Century skills and knowledge to be innovative and aspirational problem solvers.
description: The Year 8 Academic Pipeline Program enhances the vision for STEAM (Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts and Mathematics) at SBSHS to equip students with the necessary 21st Century skills (6 Cs) and knowledge to be innovative and aspirational problem solvers of the future. STEAM learning experiences inspire creativity and critical thinking in order to encourage students to be empowered lifelong learners. The program provides advanced curriculum experiences across interconnected STEM and Arts disciplines. Selection is based on academic merit and demonstrated potential in creative problem-solving. Program provides non-monetary benefits through enhanced educational opportunities and specialised instruction.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 8 students; Must meet Sunshine Beach State High School enrolment criteria; Demonstrated academic achievement; Interest in STEAM disciplines
special_conditions: Program focuses on 21st Century skills development including critical thinking, communication, collaboration, creativity and innovation. Students must demonstrate commitment to STEAM learning.

overview: Sunshine Beach State High School offers the Music Extension class for Year 7 students. The program is designed to encourage the pursuit of performance and academic excellence in Music throughout Years 7 and 8. Students must audition to join this selective program.
description: Students entering Year 7 have the opportunity to audition to join the Music Extension class – a program designed to encourage the pursuit of performance and academic excellence in Music throughout Years 7 and 8. The program provides specialised music instruction and performance opportunities for students demonstrating outstanding musical ability. Audition-based selection identifies students with exceptional musical talent and commitment to developing performance skills. The program offers non-monetary benefits including advanced music tuition, performance opportunities, and access to music ensemble groups. Students work with experienced music educators and participate in school music performances and competitions.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must audition to demonstrate musical ability; Must meet Sunshine Beach State High School enrolment criteria; Commitment to music performance and learning required
special_conditions: Selection by audition. Program runs for Years 7 and 8. Performance in school music events expected. Students must maintain musical standards and participate in ensemble activities.

overview: Sunshine Beach State High School operates the Performance Music Program as a Program of Excellence. This selective program provides 225 places for students with outstanding musical ability to pursue music performance and academic excellence.
description: The Performance Music Program is a Program of Excellence at Sunshine Beach State High School with allocated capacity for 225 students. This selective entry program recognises and develops students with outstanding performance ability in music. The program provides small group music lessons in multiple areas including woodwind, brass and percussion instruments, and vocal training. Students are supported with expert tuition and performance opportunities within a school that prioritises music excellence. Selection is through demonstrated musical ability and commitment to music performance. The program offers non-monetary benefits through specialised music tuition and regular performance opportunities with the school's ensembles and orchestras.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated outstanding musical ability; Audition or portfolio required; Must meet Sunshine Beach State High School enrolment criteria; In-catchment students prioritised; Out-of-catchment places available if capacity permits; Commitment to music performance required
special_conditions: Program has defined capacity of 225 places. In-catchment enrolment prioritised over out-of-catchment. Out-of-catchment students only considered once in-catchment demand met. Enrolment criteria available from school.

program_name: Excellence in Surfing Program of Excellence
overview: Sunshine Beach State High School offers the Excellence in Surfing Program of Excellence for students with exceptional surfing ability. This selective program aims to provide talented surfers the opportunity to pursue sporting excellence in a supportive educational environment.
description: Sunshine Beach State High School operates the Excellence in Surfing Program as a Program of Excellence with 64 allocated enrolment places. The program aims to equip students for success in life through the sport of surfing. The award-winning Excellence in Surfing Program is an extension of the Physical Education program aiming to enhance the technical skills, tactical awareness, physiological development and psychological aspects of the surfers' performance. The program brings together the very best surfers in the school region and provides expert coaching from qualified instructors as well as community experts from Queensland surfing. Physiotherapy, sports psychology and nutrition support are also available to students in the program. The program is by invitation only and requires demonstrated excellence in surfing at regional, state or national level.
eligibility_criteria: Outstanding surfing ability; Competitive experience at regional, state or national level; Must meet Sunshine Beach State High School enrolment criteria; In-catchment students prioritised; By invitation only
special_conditions: Program capacity is 64 places. In-catchment enrolment prioritised. Out-of-catchment students considered only after in-catchment demand met. Invitation required. Students must demonstrate commitment to both education and sporting excellence. Support services available including physiotherapy and sports psychology. Come and Experience Days available.

school_name: Tagai State College - Dauan Island Campus

overview: Tagai State College Secondary Campus offers the EXCEL Scholarship Class to challenge, nurture and develop high achieving students with outstanding academic ability. The class is designed to provide enhanced learning opportunities for students who demonstrate exceptional achievement in core subjects and consistent high standards of behaviour and effort.
description: The EXCEL Class is a selective program for Year 7 students entering Tagai State College Secondary Campus. The program is designed to challenge, nurture and develop students with outstanding academic ability. Selection to participate in the EXCEL Class will be based on evidence of high levels of academic achievement in core subjects and evidence of consistently high standards of behaviour and effort in all subjects. Academic results are reviewed every semester and class compositions are adjusted accordingly. Students must maintain their high standard in academics, behaviour and effort in order to remain in the EXCEL Class. The EXCEL class is named to reference the school motto and desired learning culture of 'Strive to Excel'. Entry to this program is by application only.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 students; Evidence of high levels of academic achievement in core subjects; Evidence of consistently high standards of behaviour and effort in all subjects; Application required
special_conditions: Requirement for students to maintain their high standard in academics, behaviour and effort in order to remain in the EXCEL Class. Academic results will be reviewed every semester and class compositions adjusted accordingly.

school_name: Tagai State College - Darnley Island Campus
overview: Tagai State College Secondary Campus offers the EXCEL Scholarship Class for high-achieving students entering Year 7. The program is designed to challenge, nurture and develop students with outstanding academic ability and supports the school motto 'Strive to Excel'.
description: The EXCEL Scholarship Class is a specialized academic program available at Tagai State College Secondary Campus (Thursday Island) for students entering Year 7. This program is designed to challenge, nurture and develop students with outstanding academic ability. Entry to the program is by application only. Students selected for the EXCEL Class must maintain high standards in academics, behaviour and effort to remain in the program. Academic results are reviewed every semester and class compositions are adjusted accordingly. The selection process is based on evidence of high levels of academic achievement in core subjects and consistent high standards of behaviour and effort across all subjects. Financial benefits and specific fee remission details were not published on accessible pages. Students must demonstrate sustained academic excellence to continue their enrollment in the EXCEL Class throughout their secondary education.
eligibility_criteria: Entry to Year 7 EXCEL Scholarship Class at Tagai State College Secondary Campus; Evidence of high levels of academic achievement in core subjects; Demonstrated consistently high standards of behaviour and effort in all subjects; Completion of application form; Maintenance of academic and behavioural standards required to remain in the class
special_conditions: Requirement for students to maintain their high standard in academics, behaviour and effort in order to remain in the EXCEL Class. Academic results will be reviewed every semester and class compositions adjusted accordingly. Selective entry is limited to students demonstrating outstanding academic ability.

school_name: Tagai State College - Badu Island Campus
overview: Tagai State College Secondary Campus offers the EXCEL Scholarship Class to high-achieving students entering Year 7. The EXCEL Class is designed to challenge, nurture and develop students with outstanding academic ability and is named after the school motto 'Strive to Excel'.
description: The EXCEL Class is designed to challenge, nurture and develop students with outstanding academic ability. Entry to this program is by application only. The program provides targeted digital technology skills with EXCEL students issued a personal school laptop. Students are expected to maintain high standards in academics, behaviour and effort to remain in the EXCEL Class, with academic results reviewed every semester and class compositions adjusted accordingly. Selection to participate in the EXCEL Class will be based on evidence of high levels of academic achievement in core subjects and evidence of consistently high standards of behaviour and effort in all subjects. Students are expected to participate in school life and represent the school within the community through involvement in one or more of the following areas: Student Representative Council, Sports, Arts, Leadership opportunities, JT Academy, Community programs, and Inter-school competitions.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Evidence of high levels of academic achievement in core subjects; Evidence of consistently high standards of behaviour and effort in all subjects; Entry to this program is by application only; Must maintain high standards in academics, behaviour and effort to remain in program; Academic results reviewed every semester
special_conditions: Requirement for students to maintain their high standard in academics, behaviour and effort in order to remain in the EXCEL Class. Academic results will be reviewed every semester and class compositions adjusted accordingly. EXCEL students are issued a personal school laptop. An expectation to participate in school life and represent the school within the community, through involvement in one or more of the following areas: Student Representative Council, Sports, Arts, Leadership opportunities, JT Academy, Community programs, Inter-school competitions.

school_name: Tagai State College - Mabuiag Island Campus
overview: Tagai State College's EXCEL Scholarship Class is designed to challenge, nurture and develop students with outstanding academic ability entering Year 7. The program is named to reference the school motto, 'Strive to Excel', and represents the desired learning culture of the college.
description: The EXCEL Class provides an accelerated and enriched academic program for gifted Year 7 students at Tagai State College Secondary Campus on Thursday Island. As a government school, tuition is free (no fee remission needed). Entry to this program is by application only and is selective. Selection is based on evidence of high levels of academic achievement in core subjects and consistently high standards of behaviour and effort in all subjects. Students must maintain their high standards in academics, behaviour and effort to remain in the class. Academic results are reviewed every semester and class compositions may be adjusted accordingly. This is a selective entry program that recognises and supports gifted learners within the Torres Strait context.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Evidence of high levels of academic achievement in core subjects; Evidence of consistently high standards of behaviour and effort in all subjects; Requirement to maintain high standards to remain in the EXCEL Class; Academic results reviewed every semester
special_conditions: Entry to this program is by application only. Students must maintain their high standard in academics, behaviour and effort in order to remain in the EXCEL Class. Academic results will be reviewed every semester and class compositions adjusted accordingly.

school_name: Tagai State College - Horn Island Campus
overview: Tagai State College Secondary Campus offers the EXCEL Scholarship Class to challenge, nurture and develop students with outstanding academic ability. The program is designed for high achieving Year 7 students and is named to reference the school motto 'Strive to Excel'. Entry to this program is by application only.
description: The EXCEL Scholarship Class is a selective academic program at Tagai State College Secondary Campus designed to provide enhanced educational opportunities for students demonstrating exceptional academic excellence. Selection to participate in the EXCEL Class will be based on evidence of high levels of academic achievement in core subjects and evidence of consistently high standards of behaviour and effort in all subjects. Students must maintain their high standard in academics, behaviour and effort in order to remain in the EXCEL Class. Academic results will be reviewed every semester and class compositions adjusted accordingly. The program recognises and nurtures outstanding academic ability through a challenging curriculum tailored to gifted learners.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate evidence of high levels of academic achievement in core subjects; Must demonstrate consistently high standards of behaviour and effort in all subjects; Enrolment at Tagai State College required; Application required for entry
special_conditions: Requirement for students to maintain their high standard in academics, behaviour and effort in order to remain in the EXCEL Class. Academic results will be reviewed every semester and class compositions adjusted accordingly. Entry to this program is by application only.

school_name: Tagai State College - Malu Kiwai Campus
overview: Tagai State College Secondary Campus offers the EXCEL Scholarship Class for high achieving students in Year 7. The class is designed to challenge, nurture and develop students with outstanding academic ability. Entry to this program is by application only.
description: The EXCEL Scholarship Class is a selective academic program for Year 7 students demonstrating exceptional ability. The program is designed to challenge, nurture and develop students with outstanding academic ability and is named to reference the school motto 'Strive to Excel'. Students must maintain high standards in academics, behaviour and effort to remain in the class. Academic results are reviewed every semester and class compositions adjusted accordingly. Selection is based on evidence of high levels of academic achievement in core subjects and consistently high standards of behaviour and effort in all subjects. The exact financial value of the scholarship (fee reduction or remission) is not specified on the available information.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Evidence of high levels of academic achievement in core subjects; Evidence of consistently high standards of behaviour and effort in all subjects; Entry by application only
special_conditions: Students must maintain their high standard in academics, behaviour and effort in order to remain in the EXCEL Class. Academic results will be reviewed every semester and class compositions adjusted accordingly.

overview: Sunnybank State High School offers the Instrumental Music Program as a signature program designed to promote learning and engagement in music performance. The program recognises students with musical talent and passion for instrumental music across percussion, woodwind and brass instruments.
description: The Instrumental Music Program is a selective entry signature program that provides students with the opportunity to develop instrumental music skills in percussion, woodwind and brass instruments. Students performing in this program participate in a range of contexts including school events and performances. Applications for the program can be submitted at any time, and suitable applicants are required to participate in an audition to determine their eligibility. To remain within this program, students are expected to maintain a 'C' standard in achievement, effort and behaviour as a minimum, and must maintain attendance of 85% or higher. Failure to meet these requirements may result in suspension or exclusion from the program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all year levels; Suitable applicants required to participate in audition; Must maintain minimum 'C' standard in achievement, effort and behaviour; Must maintain minimum 85% attendance; Applications can be submitted at any time
special_conditions: Suitable applicants must participate in an audition to determine eligibility. Students must maintain a minimum 'C' standard in achievement, effort and behaviour. Attendance must remain at 85% or higher. Failure to meet these requirements may result in suspension or exclusion from the program.

overview: Sunnybank State High School offers the Volleyball Program as a signature program designed to promote learning and engagement in volleyball. The program recognises students with sports talent and commitment to excellence in volleyball competition and participation.
description: The Volleyball Program is a selective entry signature program providing students with the opportunity to develop volleyball skills and participate in competitive volleyball activities. Applications for the program can be submitted at any time, and suitable applicants are required to participate in a trial to determine their eligibility. To remain within this program, students are expected to maintain a 'C' standard in achievement, effort and behaviour as a minimum, and must maintain attendance of 85% or higher. Failure to meet these requirements may result in suspension or exclusion from the program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all year levels; Suitable applicants required to participate in trial; Must maintain minimum 'C' standard in achievement, effort and behaviour; Must maintain minimum 85% attendance; Applications can be submitted at any time
special_conditions: Suitable applicants must participate in a trial to determine eligibility. Students must maintain a minimum 'C' standard in achievement, effort and behaviour. Attendance must remain at 85% or higher. Failure to meet these requirements may result in suspension or exclusion from the program.
