The SCOTS PGC College - Lock Street Campus
overview: SCOTS PGC College offers Academic Scholarships to current and new students entering Years 7, 10 and 11 who demonstrate high levels of achievement in ACER testing. The scholarship recognises students who excel academically and in some instances, may extend to students who excel in various endeavours.
description: Academic Scholarships are awarded annually based on performance in the external ACER Scholarship test, which is conducted in February for entry the following year. Applicants must complete a College Scholarship Application form and sit the external examination on Saturday 28 February 2026 at the College campus. All examinations are managed and marked by ACER who provide the College with results and scholarship recommendations. The College may extend scholarship offers to students who excel in various endeavours beyond academic achievement. Financial value and duration are subject to the College's determination based on performance.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new and current students entering Years 7, 10 and 11 in the year following the ACER Scholarship Test; Must be Australian residents; Must demonstrate high levels of achievement in ACER testing; Registration deadline 8 February 2026
special_conditions: Test conducted on Saturday 28 February 2026 at SCOTS PGC College Campus. All examinations managed and marked by ACER. Results and scholarship recommendations provided by ACER.

school_name: The SCOTS PGC College - Lock Street Campus
overview: SCOTS PGC College offers the Scholarship of General Excellence to current and new students entering Years 7 to 11 who demonstrate excellence in a particular field. Past scholarship holders have displayed talents in a combination of academic, sporting, music, art, drama, equestrian and agricultural pursuits.
description: The Scholarship of General Excellence is designed for students who excel in one or more areas of school life, including academic achievement, sporting prowess, music performance, art, drama, equestrian activities, or agricultural pursuits. Applications are open all year round, with closing dates of 30 June for commencement the following year. Applicants must demonstrate their excellence in a particular field and sit an external examination as part of the selection process. The scholarship recognises and rewards all-round achievement and contribution to the College community.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current and new students entering Years 6-12; Must demonstrate excellence in a particular field; Must sit external examination; Applications close 30 June annually; Must be Australian residents
special_conditions: Students must demonstrate excellence in a particular field. Selection based on external examination and demonstrated excellence. Applications accepted year-round with closing date 30 June for commencement the following year.

school_name: The SCOTS PGC College - Lock Street Campus
overview: SCOTS PGC College offers the Rural Boarding Scholarship as a way of ensuring regional families can access a SCOTS PGC education. The scholarship recognises those values and characteristics synonymous with growing up in the bush, including authenticity, good humour and a never-give-up attitude.
description: The Rural Boarding Scholarship is open all year round and is designed to support rural and remote students to enrol as full-time boarding students at the College. Applications are accepted for students entering Years 5 to 12. Applicants must be from a rural background with a strong connection to the land, demonstrate willingness to do their best academically and participate in College activities, and show a track record of hard work, commitment and resilience. Applicants must complete a College scholarship application form and provide either a letter or short video addressed to the Principal outlining how they meet the eligibility criteria. The scholarship specifically supports regional families for whom boarding fees may be a barrier to accessing the College's education.
eligibility_criteria: Must be new students wishing to enter College in Years 5-12 as full-time boarding student; Must be from rural background with strong connection to the land; Must demonstrate willingness to do best academically and participate in range of College activities; Must show track record of hard work, commitment and never-give-up attitude; Applications open year-round with closing date 30 June; Must be Australian residents
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications open all year round. Applicants must submit College scholarship application form plus letter or short video to Principal. Selection based on rural background, academic commitment, co-curricular participation, and personal qualities. Designed for full-time boarders only.

school_name: The SCOTS PGC College - Lock Street Campus
overview: SCOTS PGC College offers the Foundation Scholarship Fund to support students who wish to attend the College but are not currently enrolled. This bursary is offered to students whose financial circumstances are such that they would benefit substantially from financial assistance.
description: The Foundation Scholarship Fund is established to support students who wish to attend SCOTS PGC College but whose financial circumstances would otherwise prevent their enrolment. The bursary is offered to students whose family situations are such that they would benefit substantially from financial assistance. This program is designed to ensure that talented and worthy students have access to the College's education regardless of financial barriers. Applications are accepted on an ongoing basis, with closing date of 30 June annually. The bursary recognises the College's commitment to providing educational opportunity based on merit and need rather than financial capacity alone.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students wishing to attend College but not currently enrolled; Must demonstrate financial hardship or circumstances where family would benefit substantially from financial assistance; Must be Australian residents; Applications close 30 June annually
special_conditions: Established to support students whose financial circumstances would prevent enrolment at the College. Based on demonstrated financial need. Applications accepted year-round with annual closing date 30 June.

school_name: The SCOTS PGC College - Lock Street Campus
overview: SCOTS PGC College provides financial support to existing College families facing hardship. Financial assistance can be extended by the Principal to support students' continued enrolment when families experience unforeseen financial difficulties.
description: Financial support can be extended by the Principal to existing College families who are facing hardship or unforeseen financial circumstances. This bursary program is designed to help families who are already at the College but who encounter unexpected financial difficulties that might otherwise force them to withdraw their students. Support is provided on a case-by-case basis following an assessment of circumstances and the College's ability to assist. This reflects the College's commitment to supporting current families through temporary or ongoing financial challenges.
eligibility_criteria: Open to existing College families only; Must demonstrate financial hardship or unforeseen financial circumstances; Assessment on individual case-by-case basis; Applications reviewed by Principal
special_conditions: Available to existing College families only. Financial support extended by Principal on case-by-case basis. Subject to individual assessment of financial circumstances and College's ability to assist. Applications reviewed individually without set closing dates.

overview: SCOTS PGC College offers Academic Scholarships to current and new students entering Years 7 to 11 who demonstrate high levels of achievement in ACER testing. The scholarship recognises students who excel in academic performance and have exceptional intellectual ability as measured by the external ACER Scholarship Examination.
description: Academic Scholarships are awarded to students demonstrating high achievement in ACER testing. The selection process involves completion of a College Scholarship Application form and sitting an external ACER Scholarship examination at the SCOTS PGC College campus on Saturday 28 February 2026. All examinations are managed and marked by ACER who provide the College with results and scholarship recommendations. The financial value varies and may be full or partial fee reduction based on merit. In some instances, the College may also extend a scholarship offer to students who excel in various endeavours beyond academic testing. The scholarship is renewable annually subject to continued academic performance.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current and new students; Entering Years 7, 10 and 11 in the year following the ACER Scholarship Test; Must be an Australian resident; Must demonstrate high levels of achievement in ACER testing; Selection based on ACER examination results and interview performance
special_conditions: Open to new and current students. ACER testing is conducted annually in February for entry in Years 7, 10 and 11 the following year. The scholarship is offered based on a combination of ACER examination performance and interview results.

overview: SCOTS PGC College offers the Scholarship of General Excellence to current and new students entering Years 7 to 11 (with eligibility extending to Year 6-12) who demonstrate excellence in a particular field beyond traditional academics. The scholarship recognises and rewards students who display talent in academic, sporting, music, art, drama, equestrian or agricultural pursuits.
description: The Scholarship of General Excellence is designed for students who excel in one or more fields and wish to attend SCOTS PGC College. Applicants must demonstrate their excellence in a particular field and sit an external examination as part of the selection process. Past scholarship holders have displayed talents in a combination of academic, sporting, music, art, drama, equestrian and agricultural pursuits. Applications are open all year round and close on 30 June for commencement the following year. The financial value and specific benefits vary depending on the area of excellence demonstrated and individual circumstances. The selection process includes demonstration of excellence in the chosen field and external testing.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current and new students; Entering Years 6 to 12 (primary emphasis on Years 7-11); Must be an Australian resident; Must demonstrate excellence in a particular field; Must sit an external examination; Past examples show talent in academic, sporting, music, art, drama, equestrian or agricultural areas
year_levels: Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications for General Excellence scholarships are open all year round with a closing date of 30 June for commencement the following year. Applicants must provide evidence of excellence in their chosen field.

overview: SCOTS PGC College offers Rural Boarding Scholarships to support rural and remote students to enrol as full-time boarding students at the College. The scholarship is designed to ensure regional families can access a SCOTS PGC education and recognises the values and characteristics synonymous with growing up in rural areas.
description: The Rural Boarding Scholarship is designed to support rural and remote students who wish to become full-time boarding students at the College. Applicants must be new students wishing to enter the College in Years 5 to 12 as a full-time boarding student. The scholarship recognises those values and characteristics synonymous with growing up in the bush – authenticity, good humour and a 'never-give-up' attitude. Applications are open all year round with a closing date of 30 June for the following year. The financial value varies based on individual circumstances and boarding requirements. The selection process involves assessing rural background connection to the land, academic willingness, and participation in College activities. Successful recipients must demonstrate commitment to academic progress and involvement in a range of College activities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students only; Wishing to enter Years 5 to 12; Must be from a rural background with strong connection to the land; Must demonstrate willingness to do their very best academically; Must be willing to participate in a range of College activities; Must commit to full-time boarding at the College; Must be an Australian resident
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Open all year round. The scholarship is designed as a country school for country kids initiative. Recipients must be full-time boarding students. Testing / assessment dates include: 05/03/2026 and 10/03/2026 sessions. Selection based on rural background, academic commitment, and willingness to participate fully in College life.

overview: The SCOTS PGC College Foundation Scholarship Fund is established to support students who wish to attend SCOTS PGC College but are not currently enrolled. The bursary is offered to students whose financial circumstances are such that they would benefit substantially from this financial assistance.
description: The Foundation Scholarship Fund is a need-based bursary designed to support new students whose financial circumstances would otherwise prevent them from enrolling at SCOTS PGC College. The Foundation Board meets annually to review and select scholarship recipients. Successful applicants will be notified during Term 3 prior to their expected entry. Applicants must be Australian citizens or permanent residents and must demonstrate that their financial circumstances would otherwise preclude them from enrolling at the College. All applicants must complete a Foundation scholarship application form and address the key criteria noted within. The financial value is determined on a case-by-case basis and is designed to substantially support students facing financial hardship. Applications close on 30 June annually for the following year's entry.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students only; Not currently enrolled at the College; Must be an Australian citizen or permanent resident; Must be able to demonstrate that financial circumstances would otherwise preclude College enrolment; Must complete Foundation scholarship application form; Must address key assessment criteria
special_conditions: The Foundation Board meet annually to review and select scholarship recipients. Successful applicants notified during Term 3 prior to expected entry. Financial circumstances are the primary selection criterion. This is a means-tested bursary for new students only.

overview: SCOTS PGC College provides financial support to existing College families facing hardship. This bursary is extended by the Principal to families experiencing genuine financial difficulties that would otherwise impact their ability to continue their child's education at the College.
description: Financial support can be extended by the Principal to existing College families facing hardship. This bursary program is designed to support families experiencing temporary or ongoing financial difficulties that would otherwise prevent them from continuing their child's education at the College. The process involves direct application to the Director of Admissions, and support is considered on an individual case-by-case basis. The amount of assistance provided varies depending on individual circumstances and the nature of the hardship experienced. Existing families may apply during any term or year of their child's enrolment. The financial value is determined based on assessment of individual family circumstances and needs.
eligibility_criteria: Open to existing College families only; Currently enrolled students; Experiencing genuine financial hardship; Must apply to the Principal or Director of Admissions; Assessment on individual case-by-case basis
special_conditions: Available to existing College families only. Applications are assessed on an individual, case-by-case basis by the Principal. The amount of support varies depending on individual circumstances and demonstrated need.

school_name: The Springfield Anglican College - Primary Campus
overview: The Springfield Anglican College offers Academic Scholarships to recognise outstanding academic achievement in students entering Year 7. These partial scholarships are awarded based on academic merit determined through the Edutest examination and provide recipients with access to the College's academic enrichment program.
description: Academic Scholarships are partial scholarships for students entering Year 7 and are awarded for three years (Years 7, 8 and 9). Scholarships are awarded based on academic merit and determined through the Edutest examination. For top-ranking academic students, the College offers a 25% tuition fee reduction. The amount awarded for all other academic scholarship recipients is at the Principal's discretion. Scholarship recipients will have access to The Springfield Anglican College's academic enrichment program. All applicants must apply to the College by completing an online Application for Enrolment ($100 non-refundable fee payable), then complete and submit the Academic Scholarship Application by the deadline.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students currently enrolled at The Springfield Anglican College (Year 6) OR prospective students from another school; Must sit Edutest examination; Open to all school sectors
special_conditions: Scholarships are partial scholarships for three years (Years 7, 8 and 9). Scholarship recipients will have access to The Springfield Anglican College's academic enrichment program. The number of Academic Scholarships offered depends on students' test performance each year. For top-ranking academic students, the College offers a 25% tuition fee reduction. The amount awarded for all other scholarships is at the Principal's discretion. Students may apply for multiple scholarships but can only be awarded one.

school_name: The Springfield Anglican College - Primary Campus
overview: The Springfield Anglican College offers General Excellence Scholarships to exceptional students entering Years 10-12 who demonstrate outstanding academic achievements, leadership experience, cultural achievements, and/or sporting achievements. These scholarships recognise all-round excellence across multiple dimensions of student achievement.
description: General Excellence Scholarships are available to exceptional students who demonstrate exceptional leadership experience, and/or academic achievements, and/or cultural achievements, and/or sporting achievements. These partial scholarships are for students in Senior Secondary and are for three years (Years 10, 11 and 12). The scholarships are offered to students entering Years 10 to 12 and include the TSAC AFL Officials Scholarships to Umpires. Applications for the General Excellence Scholarship are available throughout the year. The amount awarded is at the Principal's discretion. All scholarship applicants must either be a current TSAC student or have applied to enrol at the College.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 10, 11, and 12; Open to students currently enrolled at The Springfield Anglican College OR prospective students from another school; Must demonstrate exceptional leadership experience, and/or academic achievements, and/or cultural achievements, and/or sporting achievements; Including TSAC AFL Officials Scholarships to Umpires
special_conditions: Partial scholarships for three years (Years 10, 11 and 12). Applications for the General Excellence Scholarship are available throughout the year. The amount awarded for all scholarships is at the Principal's discretion. Students may apply for multiple scholarships but can only be awarded one. Interviews for short-listed candidates are held progressively throughout the year before entry.

school_name: The Springfield Anglican College - Primary Campus
overview: The Springfield Anglican College offers Music Scholarships to talented musicians entering secondary school. Creative Arts is a vibrant and active department within the College and the wider community, with scholarships awarded in the discipline of Music to recognise exceptional musical ability.
description: Music Scholarships are available to talented musicians currently enrolled at The Springfield Anglican College OR prospective students from another school. Creative Arts scholarship opportunities are awarded in the discipline of Music. There are opportunities for students to be involved in percussion ensembles, stage bands, woodwind ensembles, string ensembles, guitar ensembles, concert bands, choirs, and various other music groups. All staff within the Music subject area are highly qualified and experienced educators who regularly participate in professional development within their respective fields. Private individual tuition is available. The amount awarded for Music Scholarships is at the Principal's discretion.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students currently enrolled at The Springfield Anglican College OR prospective students from another school; Must demonstrate exceptional musical ability; Audition required
special_conditions: Partial scholarships. All scholarship applicants must either be a current TSAC student or have applied to enrol at the College. Students may apply for multiple scholarships but can only be awarded one. Selection involves audition and interview. The amount awarded is at the Principal's discretion.

school_name: The Springfield Anglican College - Primary Campus
overview: The Springfield Anglican College offers Dance Scholarships to talented dancers entering secondary school. Creative Arts is a vibrant and active department within the College and the wider community, with scholarships awarded in the discipline of Dance to recognise exceptional dance ability.
description: Dance Scholarships are available to talented dancers currently enrolled at The Springfield Anglican College OR prospective students from another school. Creative Arts scholarship opportunities are awarded in the discipline of Dance. Students have opportunities to be involved in various dance troupes and performances. All staff within the Dance subject area are highly qualified and experienced educators who regularly participate in professional development within their respective fields. The amount awarded for Dance Scholarships is at the Principal's discretion. Applicants must submit the Dance Scholarship Application Form by the deadline.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students currently enrolled at The Springfield Anglican College OR prospective students from another school; Must demonstrate exceptional dance ability; Audition required
special_conditions: Partial scholarships. All scholarship applicants must either be a current TSAC student or have applied to enrol at the College. Students may apply for multiple scholarships but can only be awarded one. Selection involves audition and interview. The amount awarded is at the Principal's discretion.

school_name: The Springfield Anglican College - Primary Campus
overview: The Springfield Anglican College offers Sports Scholarships to talented athletes who demonstrate exceptional sporting ability. The College provides sporting opportunities for all students in Secondary School and awards scholarships to recognise excellence in sports performance.
description: Sports Scholarships are available to talented athletes who are currently enrolled at The Springfield Anglican College OR prospective students from another school. Successful applicants will be required to represent the College in the GBC Sport Competition (Greater Brisbane Conference). Applications are accepted year-round and can be submitted by the deadline. The amount awarded for Sports Scholarships is at the Principal's discretion. The College competes in Saturday sports competitions across multiple sports through the GBC framework. Applicants must demonstrate outstanding performance in their chosen sport.
eligibility_criteria: Open to talented athletes currently enrolled at The Springfield Anglican College OR prospective students from another school; Must demonstrate exceptional sporting ability; Must commit to representing the College in GBC Sport Competition; Sound academic ability required
special_conditions: Partial scholarships. Successful applicants will be required to represent the College in the GBC Sport Competition. Applications are accepted year-round. All scholarship applicants must either be a current TSAC student or have applied to enrol at the College. Students may apply for multiple scholarships but can only be awarded one. The amount awarded is at the Principal's discretion.

school_name: The Springfield Anglican College - Primary Campus
overview: The Springfield Anglican College offers Church Scholarships to students who demonstrate Christian values and active participation in parish life at their local Church. The scholarship recognises students who embody Christian commitment and desire to contribute meaningfully to College life.
description: TSAC's Church Scholarship is offered based on an application and interview, plus a reference from a pastor or parish priest. Applicants must demonstrate Christian values and active participation in parish life at their local Church. Recipients will be expected to commit to studies and make a significant contribution to College life at The Springfield Anglican College. The amount awarded for Church Scholarships is at the Principal's discretion. Involvement in a local Anglican Parish is required and reviewed annually. All applicants must apply to the College by completing an online Application for Enrolment ($100 non-refundable fee payable).
eligibility_criteria: Must demonstrate Christian values; Must have active participation in parish life at local Church; Must provide reference from a pastor or parish priest; Must be willing to commit to studies and make significant contribution to College life; Involvement in a local Anglican Parish (reviewed annually)
special_conditions: Partial scholarships. Application requires interview and reference from a pastor or parish priest. Involvement in a local Anglican Parish is reviewed annually. Recipients will be expected to commit to studies and make a significant contribution to College life at The Springfield Anglican College. Students may apply for multiple scholarships but can only be awarded one. The amount awarded is at the Principal's discretion.

overview: The Springfield Anglican College offers Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding achievement in students entering Year 7. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability through the Edutest examination and provides access to the College's academic enrichment program.
description: Academic Scholarships are partial scholarships available to students entering Year 7 from both current TSAC students in Year 6 and prospective students from other schools. These scholarships are for three years (Years 7, 8 and 9) and are awarded based on academic merit determined through the Edutest examination. For top-ranking academic students, the College offers a 25% tuition fee reduction. Scholarship recipients will have access to The Springfield Anglican College's academic enrichment program. The number of scholarships offered depends on students' test performance each year. All applicants must submit an online Application for Enrolment with a $100 non-refundable fee.

special_conditions: Scholarship recipients must maintain access to and participate in the College's academic enrichment program. Students may apply for multiple scholarships but can only be awarded one.

overview: The Springfield Anglican College offers the Junior General Excellence Scholarship to students demonstrating exceptional leadership, academic, cultural, or sporting aptitude entering Year 7. These partial scholarships recognise well-rounded excellence across multiple areas of College life.
description: The Junior General Excellence Scholarship is available to students entering Year 7 who are either currently enrolled at The Springfield Anglican College or prospective students from another school. These partial scholarships are for three years (Years 7, 8 and 9). Recipients are recognised for exceptional leadership experience, academic achievements, cultural achievements, and/or sporting achievements. The amount awarded is at the Principal's discretion based on individual merit and circumstances. All applicants must submit an online Application for Enrolment with a $100 non-refundable fee and complete the General Excellence Scholarship Application Form. Applications are available throughout the year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Currently enrolled TSAC students OR prospective students from another school; Demonstrate exceptional leadership experience and/or academic achievements and/or cultural achievements and/or sporting achievements; Must apply to or be enrolled at the College
special_conditions: Award amount determined at Principal's discretion. Students may apply for multiple scholarships but can only be awarded one. Interviews for shortlisted candidates are held progressively throughout the year before entry.

overview: The Springfield Anglican College offers the Senior General Excellence Scholarship to students demonstrating exceptional leadership, academic, cultural, or sporting aptitude entering Years 10-12. These partial scholarships recognise well-rounded excellence across multiple areas of senior College life.
description: The Senior General Excellence Scholarship is available to students entering Year 10 who are either currently enrolled at The Springfield Anglican College or prospective students from another school. These partial scholarships are for three years (Years 10, 11 and 12) and include the TSAC AFL Officials Scholarships to Umpires. Recipients are recognised for exceptional leadership experience, academic achievements, cultural achievements, and/or sporting achievements. The amount awarded is at the Principal's discretion based on individual merit and circumstances. All applicants must submit an online Application for Enrolment with a $100 non-refundable fee and complete the Senior General Excellence Scholarship Application Form. Interviews for shortlisted candidates are held progressively throughout the year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 10; Currently enrolled TSAC students OR prospective students from another school; Demonstrate exceptional leadership experience and/or academic achievements and/or cultural achievements and/or sporting achievements; Must apply to or be enrolled at the College
special_conditions: Award amount determined at Principal's discretion. Includes TSAC AFL Officials Scholarships to Umpires. Students may apply for multiple scholarships but can only be awarded one. Interviews for shortlisted candidates are held progressively throughout the year before entry.

overview: The Springfield Anglican College offers Creative Arts Scholarships to students demonstrating exceptional talent in Music and Dance. These scholarships recognise students with extended musical aptitude and interest in performance and creative expression.
description: Creative Arts Scholarship opportunities are awarded in the disciplines of Music and Dance at The Springfield Anglican College. The scholarship is available to students currently enrolled at the College or prospective students from another school. The Creative Arts department is vibrant and active within the College and wider community, with all staff being highly qualified and experienced educators who regularly participate in professional development within their respective fields. Students have opportunities to be involved in percussion ensembles, stage bands, woodwind ensembles, string ensembles, guitar ensembles, concert bands, choirs, and dance troupes. The amount awarded is at the Principal's discretion. All applicants must submit an online Application for Enrolment with a $100 non-refundable fee and complete the Creative Arts Scholarship Application Form.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students; Currently enrolled TSAC students OR prospective students from another school; Demonstrate exceptional talent in Music OR Dance; Must apply to or be enrolled at the College
special_conditions: Award amount determined at Principal's discretion. Students may apply for multiple scholarships but can only be awarded one. Auditions required for assessment of musical and dance performance abilities.

overview: The Springfield Anglican College offers Sports Scholarships to talented athletes entering Year 7. These scholarships recognise students with exceptional sporting ability and commit recipients to representing the College in Greater Brisbane Conference competitions.
description: Sports Scholarships are available to talented athletes currently enrolled at The Springfield Anglican College or prospective students from another school entering Year 7. Successful applicants will be required to represent the College in the GBC Sport Competition throughout their secondary schooling. The amount awarded is at the Principal's discretion. Applications are accepted year-round and interviews for shortlisted candidates are held progressively throughout the year before entry. All applicants must submit an online Application for Enrolment with a $100 non-refundable fee and complete the Sports Scholarship Application Form.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Currently enrolled TSAC students OR prospective students from another school; Demonstrate exceptional sporting ability; Must commit to representing College in GBC Sport Competition; Must apply to or be enrolled at the College
special_conditions: Award amount determined at Principal's discretion. Recipients must represent the College in GBC Sport Competition. Applications accepted year-round. Students may apply for multiple scholarships but can only be awarded one.

overview: The Springfield Anglican College offers the Church Scholarship to students demonstrating Christian values and active participation in parish life. This scholarship recognises students committed to their faith and Church community involvement.
description: TSAC's Church Scholarship is offered based on an application, interview, and a reference from a pastor or parish priest. Applicants must demonstrate Christian values and active participation in parish life at their local Church. Recipients will be expected to commit to studies and make a significant contribution to College life at The Springfield Anglican College. The scholarship is available to students currently enrolled at the College or prospective students from another school. The amount awarded is at the Principal's discretion. All applicants must submit an online Application for Enrolment with a $100 non-refundable fee and complete the Church Scholarship Application Form. Involvement in a local Anglican Parish is reviewed annually.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students; Currently enrolled TSAC students OR prospective students from another school; Demonstrate Christian values and active participation in parish life; Must have reference from pastor or parish priest; Involvement in local Anglican Parish (reviewed annually); Must apply to or be enrolled at the College
special_conditions: Award amount determined at Principal's discretion. Requires reference from pastor or parish priest. Annual review of ongoing involvement in local Anglican Parish required to maintain scholarship. Recipients expected to make significant contribution to College life. Students may apply for multiple scholarships but can only be awarded one.

school_name: The Rockhampton Grammar School - Quarry Street Campus

overview: Toowoomba Grammar School offers the Trustees' Academic Bursary to boys entering Year 7. This bursary recognises exceptional academic ability and is awarded by the Board of Trustees to support talented students with demonstrated academic merit.
description: The Trustees' Academic Bursary is awarded annually by the Board of Trustees of Toowoomba Grammar School to boys entering Year 7 in the following year. Recipients are selected based on their performance in the ACER scholarship examination and academic reports. The bursary provides partial fee remission for eligible students. Applications typically begin when boys are in Year 5, with registration for the ACER scholarship examination opening in February of their Year 5. The examination assesses academic ability across English, Mathematics, Reasoning and other key areas. Bursary offers are made in April or May of the year prior to entry. The bursary continues for the duration of the student's enrolment at the school, subject to satisfactory academic progress and compliance with school expectations.
eligibility_criteria: Open to boys entering Year 7; Applications begin in Year 5; Must sit ACER scholarship examination; Academic performance in top tier demonstrated by examination results and reports; Must not have already enrolled at another school if from outside the school
special_conditions: Bursary offers are made in April or May of the year prior to entry. The bursary is subject to annual review and continuation depends on satisfactory academic progress and maintaining school expectations. Scholarship recipients must support the school's ethos and values.

overview: Toowoomba Grammar School offers Special Ability Bursaries to boys entering Years 7 to 11 who demonstrate special talents in co-curricular areas. These bursaries recognise and support students with exceptional abilities in music, drama, visual arts, sports, or all-round talent across multiple fields.
description: Special Ability Bursaries are available to new boys entering Toowoomba Grammar School at any year level from Year 7 to Year 11. These bursaries may be awarded to boys who possess special co-curricular talents in areas such as art, music, drama, or sporting abilities, and they may also be offered to boys who demonstrate all-round talent across a number of fields. Recipients are selected based on their achievements and perceived potential in their respective fields, however applicants must have reached a high standard of achievement to be eligible. Applications for Special Ability Bursaries can be made throughout the year and are assessed on a rolling basis. The bursary provides partial fee remission to support talented students who would otherwise face financial barriers to attendance. Selection considers the student's ability to contribute to the school community through their special talents.
eligibility_criteria: New boys entering Year 7 to Year 11; Demonstrated special talent in co-curricular areas (music, drama, visual arts, sports); Must have reached high standard of achievement in their discipline; May also demonstrate all-round talent across multiple fields; Applications accepted throughout the year
special_conditions: Applications can be made throughout the year and are open to new boys starting at Toowoomba Grammar School. Selection is based on demonstrated achievement and perceived potential. Bursary is subject to annual review and satisfactory continuation in their field of talent.

overview: Toowoomba Grammar School partners with Yalari to provide the Rosemary Bishop Indigenous Scholarship to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students from regional, rural, and remote communities. This scholarship provides full boarding and tuition support for secondary education from Year 7 through Year 12.
description: The Rosemary Bishop Indigenous Scholarship, administered through Yalari in partnership with Toowoomba Grammar School, covers full boarding and tuition costs for Indigenous students from regional, rural, and remote communities across Australia. The scholarship supports approximately 50 Indigenous students annually across Yalari partner schools, with up to a number being placed at Toowoomba Grammar School. Recipients are selected through a rigorous assessment process that includes application review, family home interviews, and school interviews. The scholarship provides comprehensive support beyond financial assistance, including pastoral, educational, personal and cultural support through Yalari's network of Student Support Officers. In 2022, there were over 250 students on Yalari scholarships nationally with an alumni group of 380 studying at universities or undertaking further training. The scholarship is available for the full duration of secondary education from Year 7 to Year 12.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander students; From regional, rural, or remote communities in Australia; Entering secondary education (Year 7); Must demonstrate commitment to 100% engagement with studies; Assessed on academic potential, character, and community contribution; Shortlisted candidates interviewed at home and at school
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarship is administered through Yalari, a not-for-profit organisation. Recipients receive holistic support including educational, pastoral, personal and cultural support through Yalari's Student Support Officers and wellbeing programs. Applicants must be from regional, rural, or remote communities and demonstrate willingness to engage 100% with education. Yalari camps, workshops and cultural activities connect students across partner schools. Scholarship continues for full secondary education term subject to satisfactory progress.

overview: Toowoomba Grammar School offers a Sibling Discount for families with multiple boys attending the school simultaneously. The eldest boy receives a 25% fee remission when three or more boys from the same family are enrolled.
description: The Sibling Discount is a fee remission program for families with three or more boys attending Toowoomba Grammar School at the same time. Under this program, the eldest boy receives a 25% remission of tuition fees, recognising the financial commitment families make when educating multiple children at the school. The discount applies to the tuition fee component and is automatically applied to families who meet the eligibility criteria of having three or more sons enrolled simultaneously. The 25% remission continues for the duration of enrolment at the school, subject to all three or more boys remaining enrolled. This program provides meaningful financial relief for larger families choosing independent education.
eligibility_criteria: Three or more boys from same family attending school simultaneously; Discount applied to eldest boy; All three or more boys must remain enrolled to maintain eligibility; Applies to tuition fees
special_conditions: Automatically applied to eligible families. The discount applies to the eldest boy when three or more boys from the same family attend the school simultaneously. Discount continues as long as three or more boys remain enrolled.

school_name: Townsville Community Learning Centre - A State Special School
Townsville Flexible School - Cunningham Street Campus

overview: Townsville Grammar School offers Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding achievement in students entering Years 5, 7, 10 and 11. The scholarships recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability through competitive ACER examinations and provide partial remission of fees.
description: Academic Scholarships are awarded on the basis of academic merit, with top-ranking students generally offered a 40% reduction in fees. Most other successful applicants receive between 10% and 20% fee reduction. The scholarships are for two to three years duration depending on entry year: Year 5 scholarships cover Years 5-6; Year 7 scholarships cover Years 7-9; Year 10 scholarships cover Years 10-12; Year 11 scholarships cover Years 11-12. Scholarships are subject to annual review of academic results and conduct. Assessment is conducted through the ACER Combined Scholarship Exam delivered and assessed independently by ACER (Australian Council for Educational Research).
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 5, 7, 10 and 11 in 2027; Current and prospective students eligible; Must be registered with ACER to sit the exam; Subject to annual review of academic results and conduct; Generally top 20% of test performers awarded scholarships
special_conditions: Academic Scholarships are not able to be combined with other bursaries, with the exception of the IB Scholarship. Round 1 (major round) registrations open September 2025 and close 8 February 2026, with testing on 28 February 2026. Round 2 (minor round) registrations open February 2026 and close 8 May 2026, with testing on 27 May 2026. Round 2 offers are based on a limited pool pending Round 1 outcomes. Families are encouraged to apply for Round 1.

overview: Townsville Grammar School offers Music & Performing Arts Bursaries to support talented and motivated students in Music, Dance, and Theatre. These bursaries are available to students entering Years 7-11 and recognise exceptional potential in the performing arts.
description: Music & Performing Arts Bursaries provide partial remission of fees for students with exceptional talent and achievement in music, dance, or theatre. The bursaries are available to both external and internal students entering Years 7-11 in the following year. Selection is based on auditioned performance in the student's chosen performing arts discipline. Two rounds of applications are offered each year with separate audition dates. Round 1 closes 2 April 2026 with auditions on 29 April 2026. Round 2 closes 6 October 2026 with auditions on 21 October 2026. Shortlisted applicants will be notified of audition details.

Students must sit the ACER Scholarship Exam held at Townsville Grammar School, with two application rounds offered each year. eligibility_criteria: Open to students (both current and prospective) entering Years 5, 7, 10 and 11 in 2027; Students must be registered with ACER; Testing conducted for students currently in Years 4, 6, 9 or 10
value_aud: 10% Fee Reduction, 20% Fee Reduction, 40% Fee Reduction
special_conditions: Round 1 is the major round with registrations opening September 2025 closing February 8, 2026, with testing on February 28, 2026. Round 2 is a minor round with limited offers based on remaining scholarship funds, registrations opening February 2026 closing May 8, 2026, with testing on May 27, 2026. Scholarships subject to annual review of student results and conduct. Academic Scholarships cannot be combined with other bursaries with exception of IB Scholarships.

overview: Townsville Grammar School offers Music & Performing Arts Bursaries to recognise students with exceptional talent and achievement in music and performing arts. These bursaries support talented and motivated students entering Years 7-11 who demonstrate exceptional potential in music, dance, and theatre.
description: Music & Performing Arts Bursaries provide partial remission of fees for students with exceptional talent and achievement in music, dance, or theatre. Students must complete auditions to demonstrate their artistic abilities, with separate audition dates for each round. Round 1 auditions are held in late April with applications closing in early April. Round 2 auditions are held in October with applications closing in early October. The bursary recognises exceptional potential and provides opportunities for further musical and artistic development. This bursary cannot be combined with other scholarships or bursaries. Students applying for music bursaries must demonstrate significant musical achievement and talent through a formal audition process.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students (both current and prospective) entering Years 7-11 in the following year; Must successfully complete audition process; Cannot be combined with other scholarships or bursaries
special_conditions: Round 1 applications close Thursday, 2 April 2026 with auditions Wednesday, 29 April 2026. Round 2 applications close Tuesday, 6 October 2026 with auditions Wednesday, 21 October 2026. Short-listed applicants will be notified. This bursary cannot be combined with other scholarships or bursaries.

overview: Townsville Grammar School offers Sport Bursaries to students with exceptional talent and achievement in sport. These bursaries recognise students selected in representative teams at state or national level and support talented athletes within the school's extensive sports program.
description: Sport Bursaries are available for students with exceptional talent and/or achievement in areas of sport, such as selection in representative teams at state or national level. The school's sports program is extensive, offering club rugby, club netball, and various other sporting opportunities across multiple age groups and year levels. The bursaries provide partial fee remission for eligible students. Applications are accepted throughout the year on a rolling basis, with the school evaluating candidates based on their sporting achievements and talent. Sport Bursaries cannot be combined with other scholarships or bursaries. Interested families should contact the Director of Advancement to discuss eligibility and application process.
eligibility_criteria: Students with exceptional talent and/or achievement in sport; Selection in representative teams at state or national level; Applications accepted throughout the year
special_conditions: This bursary is not able to be combined with another scholarship or bursary. Applications are accepted throughout the year. Families should contact the Director of Advancement for more information.

overview: Townsville Grammar School offers General Excellence & Leadership Bursaries for exceptional students applying to enter the school. These bursaries recognise outstanding achievement across leadership, academics, cultural pursuits, and sporting excellence.
description: General Excellence & Leadership Bursaries are available for exceptional students applying to enter Townsville Grammar School and provide partial fee remission. The bursary recognises achievement across multiple dimensions including leadership experience such as school captain roles, strong academic achievements, cultural achievements in music and drama, and sporting achievements at state or national level. Applications for the General Excellence Bursary are available throughout the year, with families submitting completed application forms to the Director of Enrolments. The assessment considers the student's overall contribution and excellence across these diverse areas. This bursary supports all-rounder students who demonstrate excellence in multiple disciplines.
eligibility_criteria: Exceptional students applying to enter Townsville Grammar School; Leadership experience such as school captain; Academic achievements; Cultural achievements in music, drama, or performing arts; Sporting achievements at state or national level; Applications available throughout the year
special_conditions: Applications available throughout the year. Completed application forms must be submitted to the Director of Enrolments at 45 Paxton Street North Ward QLD 4810. Bursary assessment considers leadership experience, academic achievements, cultural achievements, and sporting achievements.

overview: Townsville Grammar School offers Boarding Bursaries to support students who require financial assistance to access boarding at the school. These bursaries provide partial fee remission for eligible boarding students.
description: Boarding Bursaries provide partial remission of boarding fees for students who require financial assistance. The school recognises that boarding can be an important option for students from rural and remote areas, as well as families who need boarding support. Applications for Boarding Bursaries are assessed based on financial need, with shortlisted applicants invited to interview with the Principal and the Director of Boarding to discuss their circumstances and eligibility. The bursary is assessed annually and cannot be combined with other scholarships or bursaries. This allows the school to focus support on families with demonstrated financial need who benefit from boarding education.
eligibility_criteria: Students requiring boarding at Townsville Grammar School; Demonstrated financial need; Shortlisted applicants undergo interview with Principal and Director of Boarding; This bursary cannot be combined with other scholarships or bursaries
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications for Boarding Bursaries for 2026 close Thursday, 31 July 2025. Shortlisted applicants will be invited to the School for an interview with the Principal and the Director of Boarding. This bursary is not able to be combined with another scholarship or bursary.

overview: Townsville Grammar School offers IB Diploma Scholarships to students undertaking the International Baccalaureate Diploma Program in Year 11. These scholarships recognise the commitment to the rigorous IB curriculum and provide partial fee remission for two years of study.
description: IB Diploma Scholarships provide partial remittance of fees for students who undertake the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme in Years 11 and 12. This is the only scholarship that can be held concurrently with an Academic Scholarship, allowing students to combine both awards. The IB Diploma Programme is an alternative academic course for Year 11 and 12 students and is recognised internationally by universities worldwide, with many offering scholarships, bonus points, or subject credits for IB graduates. The IB emphasises high academic standards, critical thinking, intercultural understanding, and respect for others, with a broad curriculum designed to prepare students for tertiary education globally.
eligibility_criteria: Students undertaking the International Baccalaureate Diploma Program; Entering Year 11 in the following year; Must fit the IB Learner Profile; This is the only scholarship that can be combined with an Academic Scholarship
special_conditions: This scholarship is available to students undertaking the International Baccalaureate Diploma Program in Year 11 in the following year. This is the only scholarship that can be held concurrently with an Academic Scholarship. Families should contact the IB Co-Ordinator or Director of Enrolments for more information about IB Learner Profile requirements.

overview: Trinity Lutheran College offers the Academic Scholarship to recognize outstanding academic achievement in exceptional students. The scholarship celebrates students who demonstrate strong talent, dedication and potential in their academic pursuits and are driven by curiosity and a commitment to learning excellence.
description: The Academic Scholarship at Trinity Lutheran College recognizes students demonstrating exceptional academic ability across all subjects. Recipients receive tuition fee support of up to 50% as determined by the College Principal. Applicants must register for and sit the Academic Exam provided by EduTest, which is held in February 2026. After completion of the exam, shortlisted applicants will be invited to an interview as part of the selection process. Scholarship duration extends for the remainder of the student's enrolment at the College up to Year 12. The scholarship is open to both new external students and current Trinity Lutheran College students entering Years 6 and 7.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 6 or Year 7 in 2027; External and current Trinity students eligible; Must complete enrolment application form if not currently enrolled; Must register for EduTest Academic Exam
special_conditions: All scholarships granted cover a percentage of the tuition fees at the discretion of the College Principal. Scholarship lasts for the remainder of student's enrolment at the College (up to Year 12). There is a registration fee for the EduTest exam paid directly to the test provider. No fee charged for scholarship application itself.

overview: Trinity Lutheran College offers the Performing Arts Scholarship to celebrate talented student performers in music, dance and drama. The scholarship recognizes students who demonstrate strong talent, dedication and potential in the performing arts with support from expert staff and specialized facilities.
description: The Performing Arts Scholarship supports exceptional student performers across music, dance, and drama disciplines. Recipients receive tuition fee support of up to 50% as determined by the College Principal. Applicants are required to provide a 3-4 minute video to showcase their talent in visual format as part of the selection process. Shortlisted applicants will be invited to an interview. The scholarship duration extends for the remainder of the student's enrolment at the College up to Year 12. This scholarship is open to both new external students and current Trinity students entering Years 6 and 7.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 6 or Year 7 in 2027; External and current Trinity students eligible; Talent in music, dance or drama required; Must complete enrolment application form if not currently enrolled; Must provide 3-4 minute video demonstration
special_conditions: All scholarships granted cover a percentage of the tuition fees at the discretion of the College Principal. Scholarship lasts for the remainder of student's enrolment at the College (up to Year 12). Video submission required as part of application. No fee charged for scholarship application.

overview: Trinity Lutheran College offers the Sports Scholarship to recognize elite student athletes who demonstrate outstanding sporting talent and achievement. The scholarship celebrates students who show strong talent, dedication and potential in competitive sport at school, club or representative level.

Recipients receive tuition fee support of up to 50% as determined by the College Principal. Applicants must submit a portfolio showcasing their involvement and achievements in sport at school, club or representative level. Shortlisted applicants will be invited to an interview as part of the selection process. Scholarship duration extends for the remainder of the student's enrolment at the College up to Year 12. The scholarship is open to both new external students and current Trinity students entering Years 6 and 7.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 6 or Year 7 in 2027; External and current Trinity students eligible; Demonstrated sporting excellence at school, club or representative level; Must complete enrolment application form if not currently enrolled; Must submit sport portfolio
special_conditions: All scholarships granted cover a percentage of the tuition fees at the discretion of the College Principal. Scholarship lasts for the remainder of student's enrolment at the College (up to Year 12). Portfolio submission required demonstrating sporting involvement and achievements. No fee charged for scholarship application.

overview: Trinity Lutheran College offers the Academic Excellence Scholarship to recognise and reward outstanding academic achievement in students. The scholarship celebrates students who demonstrate exceptional intellectual curiosity and a commitment to excellence in their academic pursuits.
description: Trinity Lutheran College awards Academic Excellence Scholarships to students who demonstrate strong academic achievement and intellectual potential. Scholarships cover up to 50% of tuition fees for eligible students. Applicants must register for the Academic Exam provided by EduTest and pay a registration fee directly to the test provider. Shortlisted applicants will be invited to an interview where their academic potential and intellectual engagement will be further assessed. The scholarship is awarded for the duration of enrolment at the College (up to Year 12), with the specific percentage of fees covered determined at the discretion of the College Principal.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 6 and 7 in 2027; Current students and external students eligible; Must be entering Year 6 or Year 7; Completion of online enrolment form required if not currently enrolled; Registration with EduTest required
special_conditions: EduTest registration incurs a fee paid directly to test provider. All scholarships granted cover a percentage of tuition fees at discretion of College Principal. Scholarship lasts for remainder of enrolment at College up to Year 12. Students may reapply if unsuccessful in relevant intake year levels.

overview: Trinity Lutheran College awards Performing Arts Talent Scholarships to exceptional students in music, dance, and drama. The scholarship recognises and celebrates creative excellence and artistic dedication in the performing arts.
description: Trinity Lutheran College offers Performing Arts Talent Scholarships covering up to 50% of tuition fees for students demonstrating exceptional talent and commitment in music, dance, or drama. Applicants must provide a 3-4 minute video showcasing their talent in visual format. The College supports students through expert staff and exceptional performing arts facilities. Scholarships are awarded for the duration of enrolment at the College (up to Year 12), with the percentage of fees covered determined by the College Principal.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 6 and 7 in 2027; Current students and external students eligible; Must demonstrate talent in music, dance or drama; Completion of online enrolment form required if not currently enrolled; Video submission required

All scholarships granted cover percentage of tuition fees at discretion of College Principal. Scholarship lasts for remainder of enrolment at College up to Year 12. Applicants can apply for multiple scholarship categories with separate applications required for each.

overview: Trinity Lutheran College offers Sports Excellence Scholarships to talented athletes who demonstrate exceptional commitment and skill in competitive sport. The scholarship recognises students who excel in school, club, or representative level competition.
description: Trinity Lutheran College awards Sports Excellence Scholarships covering up to 50% of tuition fees to students demonstrating exceptional athletic talent and dedication. Applicants must submit a portfolio showcasing their involvement and achievements in sport at school, club, or representative level. Shortlisted applicants will be invited to an interview to discuss their sporting achievements, competitive experience, and athletic potential. The College supports athletes with competitive teams in the Associated Private Schools (APS) competition and specialised excellence programs. Scholarships are awarded for the duration of enrolment at the College (up to Year 12), with the percentage of fees covered at the discretion of the College Principal.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 6 and 7 in 2027; Current students and external students eligible; Must demonstrate involvement and achievements in sport at school, club or representative level; Completion of online enrolment form required if not currently enrolled; Portfolio submission required

All scholarships granted cover percentage of tuition fees at discretion of College Principal. Scholarship lasts for remainder of enrolment at College up to Year 12. Applicants can apply for multiple scholarship categories with separate applications required for each.

overview: Trinity College offers the Mary MacKillop Fund Bursary as a Brisbane Catholic Education initiative to provide temporary financial assistance to families experiencing hardship. The bursary helps ensure students can remain fully engaged in their learning while families work through challenging circumstances.
description: The Mary MacKillop Fund is named in honour of Australia's first saint, St Mary of the Cross MacKillop, who spent her lifetime making Catholic education available to the poor. This bursary includes tuition fees, textbooks, uniforms, and local excursions. The fund is designed to support families from all walks of life who have experienced extreme hardship, trauma, or financial devastation but have a deep desire for their child to receive a Catholic education. Applications and eligibility information are available via the Catholic Foundation website. More information about how to apply and the application process is available through the Finance Team at Trinity College.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated financial hardship; Families experiencing challenging financial circumstances; Registered enrolment at Trinity College
special_conditions: Applications and eligibility information are managed through the Catholic Foundation. Families should contact the Finance Team at Trinity College for guidance through the application process.

school_name: Tropical North Learning Academy - Trinity Beach State School
program_name: Junior Learning Academy (Young Scholars) Program of Excellence
overview: Trinity Beach State School offers the Junior Learning Academy (Young Scholars) Program of Excellence for students demonstrating exceptional academic ability and potential. This selective program recognises students with advanced intellectual capability and provides enriched learning experiences in sustainability, entrepreneurship, philosophy and STEAM disciplines.
description: The Junior Learning Academy (Young Scholars) Program of Excellence is a selective entry program at Trinity Beach State School designed to cater to gifted and talented students. The program provides enriched and extended learning experiences focused on sustainability, entrepreneurship, philosophy, and Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts and Mathematics (STEAM). Students in this program engage with advanced curriculum content and participate in workshops through partnerships with Smithfield State High School and James Cook University. As a Queensland state school program, there is no tuition fee remission required. The school currently has the capacity to enrol 10 students in the Junior Learning Academy (Young Scholars) Program of Excellence per year. Selection criteria and enrolment requirements are available directly from the school, and places are allocated based on demonstrated academic ability and eligibility within the school's catchment area prioritisation.
eligibility_criteria: Selective entry program; Enrolment capacity: 10 places per year; In-catchment students prioritised; Out-of-catchment enrolments considered when in-catchment demand met; Queensland state school eligibility requirements apply; Specific selection criteria available from school
special_conditions: This is a selective entry program within a Queensland state school. Enrolment criteria are available directly from the school. Trinity Beach State School operates under an equidistant catchment area. Places in Programs of Excellence are only available to out-of-catchment students once demand from in-catchment enrolments has been met and capacity reserved for future in-catchment growth.

school_name: Tropical North Learning Academy - Trinity Beach State School
overview: Trinity Beach State School offers the Jazz Academy Program of Excellence for students with demonstrated musical talent in jazz performance. This selective program recognises students with exceptional ability in jazz music and provides specialised learning opportunities.
description: The Jazz Academy Program of Excellence is a selective entry program at Trinity Beach State School designed for students demonstrating exceptional talent in jazz music. The program provides focused instruction and performance opportunities in jazz music performance. As a Queensland state school program, there is no tuition fee remission provided. Students in the Jazz Academy participate in specialised jazz music tuition and are part of the school's specialist music programs. The school operates a defined number of places for the Jazz Academy program based on student capacity and musical talent identification. Specific selection criteria, audition requirements, and application procedures are available directly from the school.
eligibility_criteria: Selective entry program; Jazz music experience and demonstrated talent required; Queensland state school eligibility requirements apply; Specific audition/selection criteria available from school; Catchment area prioritisation applied
special_conditions: This is a selective entry program within a Queensland state school. Students are selected based on jazz music ability and audition performance. Trinity Beach State School operates under an equidistant catchment area. Places in Programs of Excellence are allocated with priority to in-catchment students.

school_name: Tropical North Learning Academy - Trinity Beach State School
overview: Trinity Beach State School offers the Soccer Academy Program of Excellence for students with demonstrated talent in soccer. This selective program recognises students with exceptional sporting ability in soccer and provides specialised training and development opportunities.
description: The Soccer Academy Program of Excellence is a selective entry program at Trinity Beach State School designed for students demonstrating exceptional talent in soccer. The program provides focused soccer coaching, skill development, and training opportunities. As a Queensland state school program, there is no tuition fee remission provided. Students in the Soccer Academy participate in specialised soccer training and development as part of the school's extension co-curricular sports programs. The school operates a defined number of places for the Soccer Academy program. Specific selection criteria, trials procedures, and application information are available directly from the school. Selection is based on demonstrated soccer ability and athletic performance.
eligibility_criteria: Selective entry program; Demonstrated soccer ability or success in soccer trials required; Queensland state school eligibility requirements apply; Specific sporting criteria available from school; Catchment area prioritisation applied
special_conditions: This is a selective entry program within a Queensland state school. Students are selected based on soccer ability and performance in school or district trials. Trinity Beach State School operates under an equidistant catchment area. Places in Programs of Excellence are allocated with priority to in-catchment students.

school_name: Tropical North Learning Academy - Smithfield State High School
overview: Smithfield State High School offers the James Cook University Learning Academy, a cornerstone program of the Tropical North Learning Academy partnership. This selective academic program is designed for high-performing students to support their intellectual development and help them reach their full potential through advanced learning opportunities.
description: The James Cook Learning Academies are a cornerstone partnership between Smithfield State High School, Trinity Beach State School, and James Cook University. The program is designed for high-performing students with an expectation of high academic achievement. Students develop attributes of lifelong learners including deep understanding, critical thinking, active investigation, effective communication, independence, and self-directed learning. The academy programs support intellectual development and help students reach their full potential. Selection is based on proof of eligibility such as recent school reports showing level of achievement and NAPLAN results. The program offers no direct monetary value but provides enhanced academic pathways and preferred entry to James Cook University courses.
eligibility_criteria: Proof of eligibility required such as report cards showing level of achievement; NAPLAN results; high academic performance
special_conditions: Service guarantee: All academy students exit with an OP 1 to 10 with preferred entry to James Cook University courses. Applicant must write a short statement supporting their application (maximum 200 words). Must sign the Junior Learning Academy Charter of Expectations for students.

school_name: Tropical North Learning Academy - Smithfield State High School
overview: Smithfield State High School offers the Jazz Academy, an elite music program focused on jazz and contemporary styles. This selective program is designed for talented and accomplished musicians to develop performance, improvisation, and music theory skills with specialist mentoring and world-class instruction.
description: The Tropical North Learning Academy Jazz Program offers a curriculum providing solid grounding in performance fundamentals with emphasis on jazz stylings. The program is supported by jazz drummer John Morrison and vocalist Jacki Cooper, and led by highly qualified teachers with extensive background in music education and performance. Students develop skills in performance, improvisation, and music theory with focus on jazz. The program provides specialist pathways for talented and accomplished musicians, mentoring from experienced musicians, and regular performance opportunities across the Cairns region. Selection is based on successful audition (completion of audition). Auditions may be attended in person or submitted digitally on USB or disc format. The program offers non-monetary benefits including specialist instruction and performance opportunities.
eligibility_criteria: Years 7 to 12; Completion of successful audition required; Must indicate musical instrument played; May submit digital audition if unable to attend in person
special_conditions: Auditions held on allocated days or digital submission accepted. Prior music study or instrumental experience is advantageous but not required. Specialised pathways for talented and accomplished musicians with mentoring from world renowned musicians.

school_name: Tropical North Learning Academy - Smithfield State High School
overview: Smithfield State High School offers the Football Academy, a high-performance program designed to produce elite football players. This selective academy partners with Trinity Beach State School and employs experienced coaches to develop individual skills, tactics, and game awareness.
description: The Tropical North Learning Academy Smithfield State High School Football Academy offers a high performance program designed to produce elite football players. The Football Academy is a partnership with Trinity Beach State School and employs experienced coaches who focus on developing a player's individual skills and tactics along with team tactics and game awareness. Football Academy students have the opportunity to play in local and national school competitions for both outdoor football and futsal. They also have opportunities to play at district, regional and state levels. Community support is provided by Marlin Coast Rangers Football Club and Football Queensland. Selection is based on completion of successful trial, and applicants must provide photocopies of documents verifying achievements in soccer or netball. The program offers non-monetary benefits including elite coaching, competition opportunities, and community partnerships.
eligibility_criteria: Years 7 to 10; Completion of successful trial required; Must provide documentation verifying football/soccer achievements; Applicants must attend trials on allocated days
special_conditions: Partnership with Trinity Beach State School. Completion of successful trial at allocated times. Community support from Marlin Coast Rangers Football Club. Supported by Football Queensland. Opportunities to represent school at district, regional and state levels.

school_name: Tropical North Learning Academy - Smithfield State High School
overview: Smithfield State High School offers the Netball Academy, a high-performance program designed to produce elite netball players. This selective academy partners with Trinity Beach State School and employs experienced coaches focused on skill development and tactical excellence.
description: Tropical North Learning Academy Smithfield State High School offers the Netball Academy, a high performance program designed to produce elite netball players. The Netball Academy is a partnership with Trinity Beach State School and employs experienced coaches who focus on developing a player's individual skills, fitness development, court awareness and tactics. Academy students have the opportunity to play in local and national school competitions. Community support is provided by Trinity Beach Bulldogs Netball Club. Selection is based on completion of successful trial, and applicants must provide documentation verifying achievements in netball or soccer. The program offers non-monetary benefits including elite coaching instruction, competition opportunities at local and national level, and community club partnerships.
eligibility_criteria: Years 7 to 10; Completion of successful trial required; Must provide documentation verifying netball/soccer achievements; Applicants must attend trials on allocated days
special_conditions: Partnership with Trinity Beach State School. Completion of successful trial at allocated times. Community support from Trinity Beach Bulldogs Netball Club. Opportunities to play in local and national school competitions.

school_name: Tropical North Learning Academy - Smithfield State High School
overview: Smithfield State High School offers the Soccer Academy as an extra-curricular specialist program for students interested in developing elite soccer skills. This selective program provides training and competitive opportunities for accomplished soccer players.
description: Smithfield State High School offers the Soccer Academy as an extra-curricular specialist program designed to develop soccer skills and provide competitive opportunities. The program provides pathways for aspiring and accomplished soccer players to develop their football talents through specialist coaching and competition. The program is listed as an extra-curricular offering in the course guides for Years 7-10. Selection is based on demonstrated soccer ability and achievement. The program provides non-monetary benefits including specialist instruction, structured training, and participation in school competitions at local and potentially regional levels.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students demonstrating soccer ability; Years 7 to 10; Completion of successful trial or assessment of soccer skills
special_conditions: Extra-curricular program. Specialist coaching and competitive opportunities provided. Part of broader sporting programs offered by school.

school_name: Tropical North Learning Academy - Smithfield State High School
overview: Smithfield State High School delivers the Queensland Virtual STEM Academy@FNQ, a selective program engaging highly capable students from Years 5 to 9 in innovative virtual STEM learning. This program connects students across Far North Queensland through partnerships with James Cook University and Thuringowa State High School.
