description: The Queensland Virtual STEM Academy (QVSA) is an exciting initiative focused on engaging and challenging the best and brightest students from Years 5 to 9. As a network of schools across Queensland, the QVSA delivers innovative digital learning opportunities focused on current, real world science, technology, engineering and mathematics challenges and research that enrich and enhance students' learning in STEM. Smithfield State High School delivers QVSA@FNQ online courses using an innovative, real-time, virtual platform. The program is a unique partnership between the Department of Education, Tropical North Learning Academy (Smithfield State High School), James Cook University, and Thuringowa State High School. The program aims to boost engagement in Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics for students. Selection is based on demonstration of high ability in STEM subjects. The program provides non-monetary benefits including access to cutting-edge virtual learning, connection with leading STEM educators, and collaboration opportunities with other high-achieving students.
eligibility_criteria: Years 5 to 9; High capability in STEM subjects; Demonstrated interest in science, technology, engineering and mathematics; Highly capable or high potential students
special_conditions: Partnership between Department of Education, Tropical North Learning Academy, James Cook University (JCU), and Thuringowa State High School. Delivered via innovative real-time virtual platform. Brings together like-minded, highly capable STEM students. Aligned to Australian Curriculum with enrichment beyond standard curriculum. Contact Peter Thompson pthom80@eq.edu.au for more information.

school_name: Tropical North Learning Academy - Smithfield State High School
overview: Smithfield State High School offers the French Immersion Program as an innovative educational opportunity for Year 9 students to study Humanities via French Immersion. This program provides students with advanced language skills and global citizenship development.
description: An Immersion Program is an innovative program which immerses students in a language while studying another subject. Participation in this program gives young people the opportunity to develop skills and attributes that will ultimately assist them to become global citizens of the future. Learning via immersion provides a unique and stimulating environment where students with academic ability are provided with challenge and rigor. Students gain understanding of pathways to pursue language and/or global studies post secondary schooling. At Smithfield State High School, students in Year 9 have the opportunity to study Humanities via French Immersion. The course saturates students in French for the first six weeks and, when base knowledge and understanding is evident, transitions to Humanities where the student continues to develop understanding through French. The program provides non-monetary benefits including advanced language proficiency, enriched understanding of global studies, and pathways to language-based post-secondary options.
eligibility_criteria: Year 9 students; Academic ability and interest in language learning; Demonstrated capability in French language or willingness to develop French language skills
special_conditions: Year 9 program offered as part of Humanities curriculum. Course structure: first six weeks focuses on French language saturation, then transitions to Humanities with continued French language development. For academically able students seeking challenge and rigor.

overview: Tully State High School offers Year 7 Bursaries to support students entering Year 7. These bursaries assist families with demonstrated financial need to access education at the school.
description: Year 7 Bursaries are awarded to incoming Year 7 students to provide financial assistance. The school recognises that some families may experience financial hardship and provides bursary support to ensure all eligible students can attend. The exact financial value and selection criteria are not publicly detailed on the school website, but bursaries were reported to have been awarded for the 2025 school year. Application and selection processes appear to be managed by the school administration, with details available through direct contact with the school office.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Demonstrated financial need; Current Year 6 students or transfer students
special_conditions: Selection and eligibility details are managed by the school administration. Interested families should contact the school directly for specific application procedures and criteria.

overview: The John Wilson Memorial Bursary is awarded annually to boys at Tully State High School who have achieved distinction in at least one sport and maintain strong academic performance. This bursary honours the legacy of John Wilson, a former teacher and outstanding athlete.
description: The John Wilson Memorial Bursary is presented each year to one or more deserving boys who have demonstrated athletic excellence and academic achievement. The bursary recognises students who have achieved some distinction in at least one sport while performing well academically. John Wilson was a former teacher and outstanding athlete fondly remembered by staff as 'Willo'. The Lions Club assists with the selection process by evaluating a shortlist of potential recipients provided by the school. The exact financial value is not publicly specified.
eligibility_criteria: Male student enrolled at Tully State High School; Demonstrated distinction in at least one sport; Strong academic performance; Shortlisted by school administration
special_conditions: Recipients are selected from a shortlist provided by the school to the Lions Club. Award is given annually to recognise both sporting achievement and academic performance.

overview: The Lions Bursary is a special award presented annually to girls at Tully State High School who have achieved distinction in at least one sport and demonstrate strong academic performance. The award is provided through partnership with the Lions Club.
description: The Lions Bursary is awarded each year to one or more deserving girls who have achieved some distinction in at least one sport while performing well academically. The Lions Club partners with Tully State High School to provide this recognition, selecting recipients from a shortlist of potential recipients provided by the school. The bursary provides financial support to students who balance sporting achievement with academic excellence, recognising their contribution to school life.
eligibility_criteria: Female student enrolled at Tully State High School; Demonstrated distinction in at least one sport; Strong academic performance; Shortlisted by school administration
special_conditions: Recipients are selected from a shortlist provided by the school to the Lions Club. Award is given annually to recognise both sporting achievement and academic performance.

overview: The Harding Miller Education Foundation Scholarship is available to high-potential girls from low socioeconomic backgrounds. Eligible students at Tully State High School and other Queensland schools can apply for this four-year, $20,000 scholarship for Year 9 entry.
description: The Harding Miller Education Foundation Scholarship provides comprehensive support valued at approximately $20,000 over four years to high-potential girls experiencing socioeconomic disadvantage. Successful applicants receive financial assistance for educational resources including technology, textbooks, uniforms, excursions, and co-curricular activities. Scholars also receive access to an Enhanced Learning study skills program and Future Success career and educational opportunities. Each scholar is allocated a personal coach who provides guidance throughout their four-year scholarship tenure. Selection is based on academic results or potential, motivation to achieve at school, and demonstrated financial hardship.
eligibility_criteria: Female student; Currently in Year 8 entering Year 9; Enrolled in Australian public high school; Experiencing socioeconomic hardship; Showing high academic results or potential; Demonstrating motivation to achieve at school and pursue tertiary education
special_conditions: Scholarship is for four-year duration from Year 9 to Year 12. First-year recipients receive a high-quality laptop. All scholars receive personal coaching, access to study skills programs, and career development opportunities. Applications open in July and close in September each year. Applicants must be Year 8 students applying for Year 9 entry.

overview: Victory College offers General Scholarships to recognise and support students in their pursuit of excellence. The scholarship provides financial assistance to further students' education and is part of the College's commitment to encouraging academic and personal achievement.
description: Victory College's General Scholarship program is designed to support deserving students who demonstrate commitment to excellence across multiple areas. The scholarships cover full or partial tuition fees depending on how well the student meets the scholarship criteria. All scholarships are subject to an annual review based on the student's performance and commitment to their educational progress consistent with the scholarship guidelines. Scholarships do not cover textbooks, uniforms, individual subject fees or other fees or levies that are not tuition fees. Recipients must maintain the conditions of the scholarship to retain it for its tenure or until the student leaves Victory College.
eligibility_criteria: Students must demonstrate excellence in academics and character; Meet the College's academic and behavioural standards; Participate in school co-curricular programs; Show commitment to the College's Christian ethos
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Partial Fee Remission
special_conditions: All scholarships are subject to an annual review based on the student's performance and commitment to his or her educational/sporting progress consistent with the scholarship guidelines and enrolment contract. Scholarships will be offered for varying durations depending on the type of scholarship. If the conditions of the scholarship continue to be met, the student retains the scholarship for its tenure or until the student leaves Victory College.

overview: Victory College's Academic Scholarship recognises students demonstrating outstanding academic achievement and excellence in their studies. The scholarship supports students who have achieved high academic standards and wish to pursue further academic excellence at the College.
description: The Academic Scholarship is awarded to students who demonstrate exceptional academic performance and commitment to their studies. Scholarships cover full or partial tuition fees depending on the student's academic merit and how well they meet the scholarship criteria. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress and demonstrate commitment to the College's values and ethos throughout their enrolment. The scholarship is subject to annual review, with renewal conditional upon continued academic performance consistent with scholarship guidelines. Textbooks, uniforms, and other individual subject fees are not covered by the scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrate exceptional academic performance; Achieve top academic ranking in current year level; Show commitment to academic pursuits; Meet the College's conduct and behaviour standards; Participate in College co-curricular programs
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Partial Fee Remission
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered for varying durations depending on the type of scholarship. All scholarships are subject to an annual review based on the student's academic performance and commitment to educational progress. Recipients must maintain scholarship conditions throughout their tenure at Victory College.

overview: Victory College's Sporting Scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional talent and commitment in sports. The scholarship is designed to support students in pursuing their sporting aspirations while maintaining their academic and personal development at the College.
description: The Sporting Scholarship provides financial support to students who demonstrate elite sporting ability and commitment to their chosen sport. Scholarships cover full or partial sporting costs and tuition fees, depending on the student's athletic merit and how well they meet the criteria. The College offers opportunities in equestrian, AFL, netball, rugby union, athletics, cricket, soccer and basketball. Recipients must maintain satisfactory sporting performance and academic standards, with annual reviews based on continued commitment to their sporting and educational progress consistent with scholarship guidelines. Sporting costs covered include tuition fees but not textbooks, uniforms, or individual subject fees.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrate exceptional sporting ability and talent; Achieve excellence in chosen sport; Show commitment to training and competition; Maintain satisfactory academic standards; Participate in College co-curricular programs; Comply with College code of conduct
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Partial Fee Remission
special_conditions: Scholarships cover full or partial tuition fees/sporting costs to the successful applicant depending on the scholarship and how well the student meets the criteria. Scholarships do not cover textbooks, uniforms, individual subject fees or any other fees or levies that are not tuition fees. All scholarships are subject to an annual review based on the student's performance and commitment to sporting progress consistent with the scholarship guidelines and enrolment contract.

overview: Urangan State High School offers the Academic Scholarship – Curious Learners to recognize and support exceptional Year 6 students transitioning into Year 7. The scholarship is designed for students demonstrating outstanding academic performance, leadership, and community involvement.
description: The Academic Scholarship recognises students with exceptional academic achievement and leadership potential. Recipients receive a full Student Resource Scheme (SRS) entitlement and a uniform allowance for the duration of their enrolment at the school. Applicants must submit recent school reports, NAPLAN results, and complete the Urangan SHS ACE Academic examination as part of the selection process. Scholarship recipients are expected to maintain high academic standards and meet behavioural expectations; failure to do so may result in removal from the program.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6; Must be transitioning into Year 7; Demonstrate outstanding academic performance; Show leadership qualities and community involvement; Must demonstrate strength in previous endeavours and have capacity for involvement in school activities
special_conditions: Recipients receive a full Student Resource Scheme (SRS) entitlement and a uniform allowance. Scholarship recipients must maintain high academic results and meet high behaviour expectations required. Failure to do so may result in removal from the program. Applicants must complete the Urangan SHS ACE Academic exam and provide evidence of academic achievement such as certificates from external competitions and documentation of leadership roles and community service.

overview: Urangan State High School offers the Music Scholarship – Creative Thinkers for exceptional Year 6 students with musical talent transitioning into Year 7. The scholarship is aimed at musically gifted students and provides access to the ACE Music Program.
description: The Music Scholarship recognises students demonstrating exceptional musical ability and talent. Recipients receive a full Student Resource Scheme (SRS) entitlement and a uniform allowance for the duration of their enrolment at the school. Applicants must complete an audition for the ACE Music Program and provide evidence of musical achievements, such as participation in school or community ensembles. The selection process evaluates musical talent through audition and portfolio of musical experience. Scholarship recipients must maintain high standards in music and academics and meet behavioural expectations.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6; Must be transitioning into Year 7; Demonstrate musical giftedness and talent; Have evidence of musical achievements such as participation in school or community ensembles; Must demonstrate strength in previous endeavours and capacity for involvement in school activities
special_conditions: Recipients receive a full Student Resource Scheme (SRS) entitlement and a uniform allowance. Applicants must participate in an audition for the ACE Music Program. Scholarship recipients must maintain high musical and academic standards and meet behaviour expectations; failure to do so may result in removal from the program. Applicants should provide documentation of musical achievements and participation in school or community ensembles.

overview: Urangan State High School offers the Cultural Scholarship – Connected Humans for exceptional Year 6 students transitioning into Year 7. The scholarship recognises students excelling in creative arts, sports, or community service.
description: The Cultural Scholarship is designed to support students with exceptional achievement in creative arts (such as dance, visual arts, drama), sports, or demonstrated commitment to community service and leadership. Recipients receive a full Student Resource Scheme (SRS) entitlement and a uniform allowance for the duration of their enrolment at the school. Applicants should provide documentation of involvement in activities like dance, visual arts, drama, or leadership roles, along with recent school reports. The selection process evaluates cultural and co-curricular achievements through portfolio submission and documentation of community involvement.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6; Must be transitioning into Year 7; Demonstrate excellence in creative arts (dance, visual arts, drama), sports, or community service; Provide documentation of involvement in school or community activities; Must demonstrate strength in previous endeavours and capacity for involvement in school activities
special_conditions: Recipients receive a full Student Resource Scheme (SRS) entitlement and a uniform allowance. Applicants must provide documentation of involvement in creative arts (dance, visual arts, drama), sports, or leadership roles, along with recent school reports. Scholarship recipients must maintain high standards and meet behaviour expectations; failure to do so may result in removal from the program.

overview: Upper Coomera State College offers scholarships to students in the STEM Academy who exemplify initiative, drive and success in Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional ability in STEM and are achieving well above the standard for their year level in academics, behaviour and effort.
description: The STEM Academy Scholarship is awarded to students already successful in their STEM Academy application and who are achieving well above the standard for their year level in academics, behaviour and effort. The scholarship provides financial support in amounts of $100, $200, or $300 to support the student's educational journey in the STEM Academy. At the Principal's discretion, scholarships may also be awarded to Upper Coomera State College students who achieve outstanding results during a full calendar year. An annual fee in addition to the school resource scheme fee is payable upon acceptance into the program and each subsequent year that the student is part of the program. The selection process involves evaluation of the written application, report card review assessing achievement, effort, behaviour and attendance, and a trial or audition by invitation only if required. Students already enrolled at Upper Coomera State College are not required to complete written application and report card review steps.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7-9; Achieving well above the standard for year level in academics; Demonstrating strong behaviour and effort; Students living both inside and outside the catchment area eligible
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded to those students already successful in their Academy application and achieving well above the standard for their year level in academics, behaviour and effort. At the Principal's discretion, scholarships may also be awarded to UCSC students who achieve outstanding results during a full calendar year. An annual fee is payable upon acceptance and each subsequent year of participation. Academy Programs applications are open to students living both inside and outside the catchment area.

overview: Upper Coomera State College offers scholarships to students in the Creative Arts Academy who exemplify initiative, drive and success in Dance, Drama, Film, Music, and Visual Art. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional creativity and talent in the arts while achieving well above the standard for their year level in academics, behaviour and effort.
description: The Creative Arts Academy Scholarship is awarded to students already successful in their Creative Arts Academy application and who are achieving well above the standard for their year level in academics, behaviour and effort. The scholarship provides financial support in amounts of $100, $200, or $300 to support the student's educational journey in the Creative Arts Academy, which encompasses five areas: Dance, Drama, Film, Music, and Visual Art. At the Principal's discretion, scholarships may also be awarded to Upper Coomera State College students who achieve outstanding results during a full calendar year. An annual fee in addition to the school resource scheme fee is payable upon acceptance and each subsequent year of participation. The selection process involves written application, review of report card assessing achievement, effort, behaviour and attendance, and a trial or audition by invitation only if required. Existing Upper Coomera State College students are not required to complete the written application and report card steps.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 7-12; Demonstrating talent and passion in one or more arts disciplines; Achieving well above the standard for year level in academics; Demonstrating strong behaviour and effort; Students living both inside and outside the catchment area eligible
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Creative Arts Academy covers five areas: Dance, Drama, Film, Music, and Visual Art. Scholarships are awarded to those students already successful in their application and achieving well above the standard for their year level in academics, behaviour and effort. Students are expected to participate in College events such as the Annual ArtsShowcase, Variety Concert, and school productions. At the Principal's discretion, scholarships may also be awarded to UCSC students who achieve outstanding results during a full calendar year. An annual fee is payable upon acceptance and each subsequent year of participation.

overview: Upper Coomera State College offers scholarships to students in the Sports Academy who exemplify initiative, drive and success in sporting pursuits. The scholarship recognises student-athletes who demonstrate exceptional ability and commitment to sporting development while maintaining strong academic standards, behaviour and effort.
description: The Sports Academy Scholarship is awarded to students already successful in their Sports Academy application and who are achieving well above the standard for their year level in academics, behaviour and effort. The scholarship provides financial support in amounts of $100, $200, or $300 to support the student's educational journey in the Sports Academy. The Sports Academy combines physical training with theoretical knowledge to provide well-rounded sports education, with partnerships with the Gold Coast Academy of Sport and the Australian Institute of Sport's online training portal. At the Principal's discretion, scholarships may also be awarded to Upper Coomera State College students who achieve outstanding results during a full calendar year. The selection process involves written application, review of report card assessing achievement, effort, behaviour and attendance, and a trial or audition by invitation only if required. Existing Upper Coomera State College students are not required to complete the written application and report card steps.
eligibility_criteria: Open to student-athletes in Years 7-12; Demonstrating strong sporting ability and commitment; Achieving well above the standard for year level in academics; Demonstrating strong behaviour and effort; Students living both inside and outside the catchment area eligible
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Sports Academy scholarships support student-athletes from Years 7 to 12 through a structured and inclusive pathway. The Academy combines physical training with theoretical knowledge and partners with leading sporting organisations. An annual fee is payable upon acceptance and each subsequent year of participation. At the Principal's discretion, scholarships may also be awarded to UCSC students who achieve outstanding results during a full calendar year. Academy Programs applications are open to students living both inside and outside the catchment area.

overview: Victoria Point State High School offers the Academic Academy to support highly academic students who intend embarking on university studies. The Academy is targeted at students demonstrating exceptional academic ability across English, Mathematics, Science and the Humanities. Successful applicants are eligible for scholarships in the Academy program.
description: The Academic Academy runs across Years 7 to 9 (full year), and in Year 10 (semester 1) with transition in semester 2 to align senior subjects with chosen pathways. Students are enriched through breadth of study, relevance of assessment tasks and engagement in highly skilled practices. The Academy sits across four core areas: English, Mathematics, Humanities and Science, specifically targeting highly academic students intending to pursue university studies. The goal is to equip students with specific skills that enhance future studies and provide opportunity to achieve highest ATAR to maximise university options. Students participate in a range of extra-curricular activities aimed at recognising, supporting and enriching their abilities. In recognition of demonstrated commitment, the school co-funds all activities to maximise opportunities. Selection is based on Academic GPA, Diagnostic Test Results, Student Report Card, and Professional Judgement. Ongoing monitoring occurs through Visible Tracking, TrackEd and OneSchool Student Profile, with semester reviews and recommendations for class changes discussed by Heads of Department.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 students; Academic GPA based on core subjects – English, Mathematics, Science and Humanities; Diagnostic Test Results; Student Report Card; Professional Judgement; Ongoing monitoring and semester reviews required
special_conditions: Students must apply to be enrolled in an Academy. Scholarships are offered to successful applicants. Placement in the Academy is reviewed at semester end with recommendations for class changes discussed by Heads of Department. In Years 11-12 students may choose to follow either a full ATAR pathway, combined QCE and ATAR pathway or full QCE pathway.

overview: Victoria Point State High School offers the Creative Industries Academy – Dance to students seeking to develop dance excellence. The Dance Excellence Program provides students with opportunity to develop the skills, techniques and thinking skills required to be an outstanding dance student. Successful applicants are eligible for scholarships in the Dance Academy.
description: The Creative Industries Academy – Dance runs across Year 7 (Foundation), Year 8 (Intermediate), Year 9 (Advanced), and Year 10 Semester 1 with transition to Dance – General in Year 10 Semester 2 and Years 11-12. Students are enriched through intensive dance training, performance opportunities and engagement with industry practices. The program develops technical proficiency, creative thinking and performance excellence. Selection is by application and audition. Application fees are payable prior to timetabling: Years 7-9 cost $200.00; Year 10 Semester 1 costs $100.00. Students apply digitally through the Dance Academy Application Form. Ongoing monitoring and support ensures student progress and development throughout their enrolment in the program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, and Year 10 Semester 1; Must apply to be enrolled; Entrance by application and audition; Demonstration of dance proficiency and commitment; Academic performance and behavioural conduct expected
special_conditions: Students must apply to be enrolled in an Academy. Application via Dance Academy Application Form (digital submission). Audition required for entrance. Application fee of $200 (Years 7-9) or $100 (Year 10 S1) payable prior to timetabling. Academy shirts are worn only by students enrolled in dance academy for the length of enrolment, worn as alternative to regular uniform shirt.

overview: Victoria Point State High School offers the Creative Industries Academy – VPtv Media to students interested in intensive media and screen studies. The program develops intensive and focused pathways for potential futures in speciality media areas. Successful applicants are eligible for scholarships in the Media Academy.
description: The Creative Industries Academy – VPtv Media develops intensive and focused pathways for students seeking careers in media production. The program creates an environment of motivation, engagement and success, developing skills and opportunities for students to work with and learn from guaranteed industry professionals both on location and in workshops. Students demonstrate media proficiency through exposure to 21st Century Skills and Employability Skills, fundamental to learning and engagement. The school offers state-of-the-art industry-compliant computer rooms for Adobe Premiere Pro editing. Students in Years 11-12 completing Certificate III Screen and Media gain direct entry into tertiary qualifications in Animation and Screen and Media through Brisbane TAFE partnership. Selection is by application based on Academic GPA, Diagnostic Test Results, Student Report Card and Professional Judgement. Application fees: Years 7-9 $200.00, Certificate II $150.00, Certificate III $200.00 payable prior to timetabling.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, and vocational students; Must apply to be enrolled; Entrance by application; Academic GPA, Diagnostic Test Results, Student Report Card and Professional Judgement considered; Interest in media production and screen studies
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Students must apply to be enrolled in an Academy. Application via Media Academy Application Form (digital submission). Selection based on Academic GPA, Diagnostic Test Results and Professional Judgement. Application fees: Years 7-9 $200.00, Certificate II $150.00, Certificate III $200.00 payable prior to timetabling. Students monitored through Visible Tracking, TrackEd and OneSchool Student Profile. VET pathway available with Certificate qualifications.

overview: Victoria Point State High School offers the STEM Technologies Academy – Robotics to students with keen interest in coding and engineering. The Robotics Academy allows students to experience an interactive course in which they design, build and program their robots. Successful applicants are eligible for scholarships in the Robotics Academy.
description: The STEM Technologies Academy – Robotics provides an interactive course where students design, build and program robots, targeted at students with keen interest in coding and engineering. Students work predominantly with LEGO Mindstorm kits, building a basic autonomous mobile robot through which they explore fundamentals of movement, design and programming before moving into more complex problem scenarios. Students later learn to program micro:bit electronics and Arduino microcontrollers to tackle real world scenarios. A critical thinking and problem-solving approach to the design process is used throughout. Students have opportunity to pathway their studies through various qualification levels. Selection is by application. Application package may be collected from Administration reception or requested via email. The program develops 21st Century Skills essential for future employment and contributes to growing workforce demand in robotics and engineering fields.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, and Year 10; Must apply to be enrolled; Demonstrated interest in coding and engineering; Academic performance expected to support program success; Keen aptitude for technology and problem-solving
special_conditions: Students must apply to be enrolled in an Academy. Application package may be collected from Administration reception or requested via email. Selection based on Academic GPA, Diagnostic Test Results and Professional Judgement. Scholarships are offered to successful applicants. Students participate in industry-relevant learning through LEGO Mindstorm kits, micro:bit and Arduino microcontrollers.

overview: Victoria Point State High School offers the Vikings Sport Academy to high performing sport students. The Vikings Sport Academy mission is to provide high performing Vikings Sport students with the greatest possible opportunity to engage, succeed and excel in their sporting and educational endeavours. Successful applicants are eligible for scholarships in the Sport Academy.
description: The Vikings Sport Academy provides high performing students with opportunities to engage, succeed and excel in sporting and educational endeavours. The Academy runs across Years 7 to 9 (full year) and transitions in Year 10 to align sports training with chosen pathways. Students are enriched through competitive sport participation, skill development and engagement with professional coaching practices. The program supports students balancing elite sport commitments with academic excellence. Students participate in a range of extra-curricular activities aimed at recognising and enriching their abilities. In recognition of demonstrated commitment to the Academy, the school co-funds sport-related activities and opportunities to maximise student development. Selection is based on demonstrated sporting excellence, athletic proficiency and commitment. Scholarships are available to support participation in the Academy.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7, Year 8, Year 9 and Year 10 students; Demonstrated high performance in sport; Must apply to be enrolled; Athletic excellence and sporting proficiency required; Commitment to balancing sport and academic performance expected
special_conditions: Students must apply to be enrolled in an Academy. Scholarships are offered to successful applicants. In recognition of demonstrated commitment, school co-funds activities to maximise opportunities. Selection based on demonstrated sporting excellence and athletic proficiency. Academy runs across Years 7-9 full year with transition in Year 10 to align with chosen pathways.

overview: Victoria Point State High School offers the QATSIF (Queensland Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Foundation) scholarship to Indigenous students in Years 11 and 12. The scholarship assists with Indigenous students' schooling and improves educational outcomes. The school's Indigenous education officer provides individual or group educational support for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students.
description: The QATSIF scholarship program supports Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students in Years 11 and 12 to complete secondary education successfully. The Queensland Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Foundation was created to give Queensland's Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander families increased educational choices and life opportunities through using the interest of the Stolen Wages. The scholarship assists with schooling costs and improves educational outcomes for eligible Indigenous students. Victoria Point State High School supports Indigenous students through dedicated Indigenous education officer who provides individual or group educational support. The school promotes development of respectful relationships between Indigenous and Non-Indigenous students, staff and the broader community. The school is committed to improving educational outcomes and experiences of Indigenous students, raising cultural awareness and developing cross-culturally inclusive curriculum and policies.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 11 and Year 12 students; Must identify as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander; Currently enrolled at Victoria Point State High School; Commitment to ongoing schooling through Year 12 required
special_conditions: Scholarship offered through Queensland Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Foundation (QATSIF). Support provided through school's Indigenous education officer. School promotes culturally inclusive teaching and learning environments. School developing Reconciliation Action Plan to support reconciliation movement and improve Indigenous educational outcomes.

overview: Villanova College offers the Terry Hendle Bursary to assist families facing financial hardship. Established in memory of former student Terry Hendle who served his country during the Vietnam War, this bursary provides tuition fee support to eligible students.
description: The Terry Hendle Bursary is a managed trust registered with the Australian Charities and Not-for-profits Commission. Established by the Class of 1964 and the Villanova Old Boys Association Inc., the bursary provides financial assistance for tuition fees to eligible families demonstrating financial need. The bursary does not provide funds for extracurricular or other school-related expenses. Availability of each bursary is dependent on funds raised through donations and fundraising efforts. Families and students receiving the bursary are kept strictly confidential.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated financial hardship; Student must be enrolled at Villanova College; Open to all year levels
special_conditions: The bursary is made possible solely through donations and fundraising efforts, as such the availability of each bursary is dependent on the funds raised. Bursary support is for tuition fees only; does not extend to extracurricular or other school-related expenses. Names of families and students are kept strictly confidential.

overview: Villanova College offers the Wieneke Family Music Bursary to support students pursuing music education. This bursary recognises demonstrated enthusiasm for music and provides support based on financial hardship and commitment to music learning.
description: The Wieneke Family Music Bursary is a managed trust registered with the Australian Charities and Not-for-profits Commission. Made possible through a kind gift from Mrs Edna Wieneke, the bursary provides eligible students with music tuition fee support. Recipients are selected based on demonstrated financial hardship, demonstrated enthusiasm for music, and a demonstrated desire to continue their music education. Financial support can also extend to equipment and tours for the student. The bursary is made possible solely through donations and fundraising efforts, so availability is dependent on funds raised. Student and family names are kept strictly confidential.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated financial hardship; Demonstrated enthusiasm for music; Demonstrated desire to continue music education; Student must be enrolled at Villanova College
special_conditions: The bursary is made possible solely through donations and fundraising efforts, as such the availability of each bursary is dependent on the funds raised. Financial support can extend to music tuition fees, equipment, and tours. Student and family names are kept strictly confidential.

overview: Warrigal Road State School celebrates and recognises academic excellence every semester through its Academic Awards Program. The program acknowledges students in Years 4 to 6 who demonstrate exceptional achievement across all Key Learning Areas, with awards based on end-of-semester report card results.
description: Introduced in 2021, Warrigal Road State School's Academic Awards Program recognises students in Years 4 to 6 who demonstrate exceptional achievement across all Key Learning Areas. The program is based on end-of-semester report card results, providing recognition and acknowledgement rather than financial awards. This is an internal school recognition program that celebrates academic excellence within the school community. The awards recognise consistent high performance across the curriculum and encourage continued academic achievement throughout a student's primary school years.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled at Warrigal Road State School; Currently in Year 4, Year 5, or Year 6; Demonstrated exceptional achievement across all Key Learning Areas in semester report card results
special_conditions: Awards are based on end-of-semester report card results. Recognition is presented each semester.

overview: Westside Christian College offers a Bursary Scheme to eligible students who demonstrate academic ability and commitment to school excellence. The scheme recognises students based on their academic performance, work ethic and achievement in various co-curricular areas including music, drama, sport and community service.
description: The Bursary Scheme is available to students in Years 4-12 and provides fee reductions ranging from 25%-100% of tuition fees for the duration of the student's schooling, provided performance standards are maintained. Applications are evaluated on the basis of the student's academic ability and work ethic while candidates may also demonstrate significant achievement in areas such as music, performing arts, drama, dance, musical theatre, sports, visual arts or community service. Consideration is also given to a candidate's record of leadership and community service. Students must meet ongoing performance standards to retain the bursary. The Building Fund Levy still applies to successful applicants. Bursaries do not apply to subject levies, camps, uniforms, textbooks, outside hours school care, college bus service or private music tuition.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 4-12; Must be enrolled in the college or applying for enrolment; Must complete Bursary Application form with supporting documentation; Evaluation based on academic ability, work ethic and achievement in co-curricular areas; Financial need assessed for means-tested applications; Student and family commitment to the college ethos required; No significant disciplinary issues
year_levels: Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
value_aud: 25% Fee Reduction; 50% Fee Reduction; 75% Fee Reduction; Full Fee Remission
special_conditions: Bursary awards are subject to annual review by the Head of Primary/Secondary for academic, behavioural and co-curricular contribution, and by the Business Office for financial circumstances. Successful applicants must maintain agreed payment plans and notify the college of financial changes. Bursaries are made possible through college community support and recipients are expected to show commitment to the college through attendance at school activities and contributions to school life. Building Fund Levy still applies and cannot be waived.

overview: Westside Christian College offers Family Assistance to families who can demonstrate genuine financial hardship. This need-based program reduces tuition fees to a level families can afford, providing support to current and prospective families experiencing financial difficulty.
description: Family Assistance is a needs-based tuition assistance program that helps eligible families reduce the cost of their tuition fees to a level they can afford. Applications are evaluated on the basis of demonstrated financial need, with the college considering factors such as family income, assets, unusual expenses and family size. Applications for a particular college academic year must be received by a due date established by the college, typically in the year prior to the academic year for which assistance is sought. Late applications may be considered until the last day of Term 4 only where there has been a significant change in the applicant's circumstances, and will be subject to reduced assistance amounts. Family Assistance is granted for one college academic year at a time and requires annual reapplication. All assistance applications must be fully completed with complete disclosure of financial information.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students; Must demonstrate genuine financial hardship; Current and prospective families eligible; Applicants must complete Family Assistance Application Form; Full disclosure of financial information required; Annual application required; Late applications considered only with significant change in circumstances
special_conditions: Families must request assistance in writing to the Business Manager. Assistance is granted for one academic year at a time. Families receiving assistance are not eligible for the on-time payment rebate as the college is accepting partial payment rather than full fees on time. Applications must be accompanied by supporting financial documentation. Interview with the Business Manager may be required. College decision is final. Annual review required with potential adjustment based on changed financial circumstances.

overview: Westside Christian College maintains the Richard Jones Bursary Fund to provide targeted financial assistance to students in need. This named fund represents a legacy donation supporting the college's commitment to accessibility and student welfare.
description: The Richard Jones Bursary Fund is a named charitable fund at Westside Christian College dedicated to providing financial assistance to students and families experiencing hardship. The fund operates as part of the college's broader commitment to making Christian education accessible to deserving students regardless of financial circumstances. Details about specific award amounts and selection criteria are available from the college Business Office. Interested donors can contribute to this fund to support future recipients, and families seeking assistance from this fund should contact the college directly.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in financial need; Contact required with Business Office for eligibility assessment; Evaluated on demonstrated financial hardship and college commitment
special_conditions: Fund donations are tax deductible. Applications and inquiries should be directed to the Business Office. Recipients determined on a case-by-case basis according to available funding.

school_name: Westside Christian College - Alice St Campus
overview: Westside Christian College offers the Bursary Scheme to support students demonstrating academic excellence and achievements in co-curricular areas. The program provides tuition fee reduction to qualifying students at Years 4-12, recognising outstanding academic ability and significant achievement in music, performing arts, visual arts, sports, or community service.
description: A limited number of bursaries are offered to students who meet certain criteria and demonstrate exceptional academic ability combined with significant achievement in one of the following areas: music, performing arts (drama and dance), visual arts, sports, or community service. Successful applicants will receive a reduction in tuition fees ranging from 25%-100% for the duration of their Primary or Secondary school years, provided performance standards are maintained by the student and the conditions which qualify for bursary assistance still apply. Applications are evaluated on the basis of the student's academic ability and work ethic, with consideration also given to leadership and community service. The Building Fund Levy still applies to successful applicants. Bursaries do not apply to subject levies, camps, uniforms, textbooks, Outside Hours School Care, College bus service or private music tuition, which must be paid in full. Families are expected to make weekly/fortnightly contributions as per payment plan.
eligibility_criteria: Students enrolled in Years 4-12; Must demonstrate strong academic ability and work ethic; Must demonstrate significant achievement in one of the following areas: music, performing arts, visual arts, sports or community service; Applicants should have a demonstrable record of leadership and community service; College reserves right to conduct interviews for students with significant achievements; Annual review of academic, behavioural and overall contribution by Head of Primary/Secondary required
year_levels: Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Successful applicants will receive a reduction in tuition fees (of 25%-100%) for the duration of their Primary/Secondary school years, provided performance standards are maintained by the student and the conditions which qualify for bursary assistance still apply. Bursary Awards are subject to annual review. The College will conduct an annual review in two parts: (1) A review by the Head of Primary/Secondary of the student's academic, behavioural and overall contribution in his/her area of significant achievement; (2) A review of the family's financial situation by the College Business Office. In the event that financial position improves significantly during the term of the Bursary Award, families must advise the College so assistance may be reallocated to those in greater need. Families are expected to have bus, Outside Hours School Care and private music tuition accounts promptly paid and up to date, and must not have been followed up by the Business Office in relation to late or rejected payments.

school_name: Westside Christian College - Alice St Campus
overview: Westside Christian College offers Family Assistance to support families experiencing financial hardship or need. The program provides tuition fee reduction to qualifying families, enabling them to afford enrolment at the College while maintaining their children's education.
description: Where there is a demonstrated financial need, WCC may be able to help by reducing the cost to some families of their tuition fees to a level they can afford. Families must complete and return the Family Assistance Application Form. An assessment of the information provided determines the level of assistance that may be offered. Current families may apply at any time during a year for Family Assistance. Family Assistance is provided for up to 12 months, after which a new application must be made if continuing assistance is required. The College Board has discretion to recommend fee concessions based on financial need of existing College families. Applications are evaluated based on submitted documentation and may require a confidential interview with the College Business Office to discuss financial circumstances. Late applications will be considered until the last day of Term 4 only where there has been a significant change in the applicant's circumstances subsequent to the due date, though late applications successful will be subject to reduced assistance amounts.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students at the College experiencing genuine financial hardship; Demonstrated financial need required; Current families may apply at any time during the year; Applications based on submitted financial documentation; Families experiencing matrimonial separation or changes in financial circumstances may apply; Full and frank disclosure required on application forms
special_conditions: Family Assistance is only granted for one College academic year at a time. Applications for a particular College academic year must be received by the due date (or next available business day) of the year prior to the relevant academic year for which Family Assistance is sought (due to budgetary processes). Returned Family Assistance Application forms which are not fully completed will not be considered. Applicants must answer all questions as set out on the form. Fee accounts receiving Family Assistance will not be eligible for the on-time payment rebate, as the College is not receiving the full fees on time but is accepting partial payment. The College does not allow split billing for separated parents. In the event that financial position improves significantly, families must advise the College so assistance may be reallocated to those in greater need. If parents/guardians believe they are unable to pay full College Fees and Levies because of genuine financial hardship, they must request in writing to the Business Manager to be sent a Family Assistance Application Form.

program_name: LEAP - Leadership Enrichment and Acceleration Program
overview: Wellington Point State High School offers the LEAP program to high-achieving students in Junior Secondary. LEAP provides qualifying students the opportunity to extend their learning and leadership skills through rigorous, challenging academic work in a supportive environment.
description: LEAP (Leadership Enrichment and Acceleration Program) is Wellington Point State High School's selective entry program offered to high-achieving students in Junior Secondary (Years 7-9). The program is designed for students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and strong leadership potential. Students accepted into LEAP are challenged and engaged in rigorous learning in a space where it is safe to question, explore and invent. The program provides extended learning opportunities and leadership development. Currently, the school has the capacity to enrol 100 students from Years 7-9 in the LEAP program. Requirements to maintain a position in LEAP include maintaining 90% attendance (medical documentation accepted for prolonged absences), leading by example as a role model who follows school rules including uniform compliance, and sustaining strong academic performance. LEAP applications are submitted via email to LEAP@wellingtonpointshs.eq.edu.au.
eligibility_criteria: High-achieving students in Junior Secondary; Open to Years 7, 8, and 9; Students must demonstrate exceptional academic ability; Students must meet school's academic entry threshold; Minimum 90% attendance required; Must follow school rules and maintain exemplary behaviour; No financial assistance component
special_conditions: Scholarship subject to annual review. Enrollment in LEAP may be withdrawn if students do not maintain 90% attendance or fail to follow school rules including uniform compliance. Recipients must maintain strong academic performance throughout their enrolment.

overview: Wellington Point State High School offers ENCORE, the Arts Excellence Program, to qualifying students from Years 7-12 who excel or display strong potential in the Arts. ENCORE recognises students who demonstrate exceptional ability and commitment in performing arts, visual arts, and related disciplines.
description: ENCORE is Wellington Point State High School's Arts Excellence Program offered to qualifying students from Years 7-12 who excel or display strong potential in the Arts, including performing arts (drama, dance, music) and visual arts. Students in ENCORE are provided with specialized training, workshops, excursions, and performance opportunities. The program involves participation in rehearsals every rehearsal afternoon (either Wednesday or Thursday afternoons), participation in excursions and performances, and attendance at all workshops and exhibitions. Students accepted into ENCORE must maintain an attendance rate of above 95% in all subjects and in ENCORE. Academic entry requirements state that students must be receiving 'Very Good' or 'Excellent' for Effort and Behaviour on all academic reports. Students must study Arts subjects where possible and achieve A or B standards in their chosen Arts subject. An annual levy of $70 is required, and students must purchase an ENCORE polo shirt (approximately $40). Applications are completed online and submitted by email to encore@wellingtonpointshs.eq.edu.au.
eligibility_criteria: Years 7 to 12; Must excel or display strong potential in the Arts (performing or visual); Academic reports must demonstrate 'Very Good' or 'Excellent' for Effort and Behaviour; Must maintain above 95% attendance; Must study Arts subjects where possible; Must achieve A or B standards in chosen Arts subject; Willing to attend all rehearsals, workshops, excursions and performances
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Annual levy of $70 required. Students must purchase ENCORE polo shirt (approximately $40). Attendance at 95% or above required in all subjects and ENCORE. Students must commit to attend all rehearsals, workshops, excursions, events, exhibitions and performances. Workshop trials typically held in June (23rd June audition with 25th June trial) and August/September (29th August audition with 2nd September trial). Students can only select one arts discipline for their initial audition.

overview: Wellington Point State High School offers ESAP, the Elite Sporting Achievers Program, to talented athletes from Years 7-12. The program provides dynamic sporting opportunities for athletes with great potential, offering specialized training, mentoring, and coaching to help balance elite athletic performance with academic excellence.
description: ESAP (Elite Sporting Achievers Program) is Wellington Point State High School's selective entry program designed for talented athletes from Years 7 to 12 who demonstrate great sporting potential. The program provides specialized training, mentoring, and coaching support to help elite athletes balance their sporting commitments with academic achievement. The program supports athletes across multiple sports, with the school having capacity to enrol 400 students in ESAP. Athletes accepted into the program receive access to specialized coaching, training workshops, and mentoring support. The program aims to develop young athletes while maintaining high academic standards. Currently, the school has the capacity to enrol 400 students from Years 7-12 in the ESAP program. Application forms are available from the school's website.
eligibility_criteria: Years 7 to 12; Must demonstrate elite sporting potential; Must be currently enrolled in Year 6 or above (for Year 7 entry); Talented athletes in recognized sports; Willing to balance academic and sporting commitments; No minimum academic threshold explicitly stated; School seeks athletes aspiring to represent the school at district, metropolitan, regional and state levels
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Students selected to represent the school in all sports if and when selected throughout their time at the School. Enrollment is subject to annual review. Students must maintain appropriate academic standards while participating in elite sporting training and competition.

overview: Wellington Point State High School offers the Acceleration Program to gifted Year 10 students. The program allows exceptional students to undertake Year 11 subjects in Year 10, providing advanced learning opportunities and the option of beginning a tertiary course during their senior year.
description: The Acceleration Program is Wellington Point State High School's unique program that allows gifted Year 10 students to undertake Year 11 subjects, providing an accelerated learning pathway. This program is designed for students who demonstrate exceptional intellectual ability and are ready for advanced academic work. Students who successfully complete Year 11 subjects in Year 10 have the option of beginning a tertiary course during their senior year (Year 11 or 12), allowing them to commence university study while still enrolled at secondary school. The program recognizes and supports students with an insatiable appetite for learning and strong intellectual curiosity. No specific financial component or application fee is associated with this program, as it is a pathway rather than a scholarship or selective entry program requiring competitive application. Students are identified through school assessment processes.
eligibility_criteria: Year 10 students; Must be identified as gifted; Must demonstrate exceptional intellectual ability and readiness for advanced academic work; Must have strong academic performance; Must be capable of managing accelerated curriculum demands
special_conditions: Enrollment in acceleration program allows eligible Year 10 students to commence Year 11 subjects with the possibility of beginning a tertiary course in Year 11 or 12. Participation is subject to demonstrated ability to manage advanced curriculum and school approval.

overview: West Moreton Anglican College offers Academic Scholarships to recognise exceptional academic achievement in prospective and current students. The scholarship rewards demonstrated excellence and potential through rigorous assessment and examination.
description: Academic Scholarships at West Moreton Anglican College are awarded based on results of the Scholarship Exam, which takes place at the College. The scholarship programme is highly competitive and strictly limited, offering deserving students the opportunity to further their growth with access to expert teachers and unique enrichment experiences. Applicants must sit the College's Scholarship Exam as a core component of the selection process. The scholarship is available across multiple year levels. Financial value and renewal conditions are determined on an individual basis and subject to the Scholarship Information Handbook.
eligibility_criteria: Must sit the Scholarship Exam at West Moreton Anglican College; Current school results required; Demonstrated exceptional academic achievement; Open to prospective and existing students
special_conditions: Applications close 20 February 2026. All applicants must sit the Scholarship Exam which takes place at the College. Scholarship Information Handbook must be reviewed for specific requirements.

overview: West Moreton Anglican College offers Special Talent Scholarships in Music to recognise and support exceptional musical ability and talent. The scholarship provides opportunities for students to develop their musical excellence with specialist coaches and enrichment experiences.
description: Special Talent Scholarships in Music are awarded based on the Scholarship Exam, current school results, practical performance (audition), and interview. The scholarship programme is highly competitive and strictly limited. Applicants must demonstrate significant musical ability through practical performance assessment. Selection involves multiple evaluation criteria including examination results, school reports, and audition performance. Financial value and duration are determined individually and subject to review outlined in the Scholarship Information Handbook.
eligibility_criteria: Must sit the Scholarship Exam at West Moreton Anglican College; Current school results required; Demonstrated exceptional musical ability; Audition/practical performance required; Interview required
special_conditions: Applications close 20 February 2026. Must provide Scholarship Resume with supporting documents including report cards, NAPLAN results, awards and certificates. Audition and interview required.

overview: West Moreton Anglican College offers Special Talent Scholarships in Sports to recognise and develop exceptional sporting talent. The scholarship supports dedicated athletes with access to specialist coaches and high-level training opportunities.
description: Special Talent Scholarships in Sports are awarded based on the Scholarship Exam, current school results, practical performance, and interview. The scholarship programme is highly competitive and strictly limited, offering deserving student athletes the opportunity to develop their sporting excellence with specialist coaches and unique enrichment experiences. Applicants must demonstrate exceptional athletic ability through performance assessment. Selection involves examination results, school reports, and sports performance evaluation. Financial value and duration are individual and subject to the Scholarship Information Handbook.
eligibility_criteria: Must sit the Scholarship Exam at West Moreton Anglican College; Current school results required; Demonstrated exceptional sporting ability; Practical performance/trials required; Interview required
special_conditions: Applications close 20 February 2026. Must provide Scholarship Resume with supporting documents including report cards, NAPLAN results, awards and certificates. Performance trials and interview required.

program_name: Special Talent Scholarship - Performing Arts (Drama, Dance)
overview: West Moreton Anglican College offers Special Talent Scholarships in Performing Arts (Drama and Dance) to support students with exceptional talent in theatre and dance. The scholarship recognises artistic excellence and provides access to specialist instruction.
description: Special Talent Scholarships in Performing Arts are awarded based on the Scholarship Exam, current school results, practical performance (audition), and interview. The scholarship programme is highly competitive and strictly limited. Applicants must demonstrate exceptional artistic ability through audition and performance assessment. Selection criteria include examination results, school reports, and artistic performance evaluation. Financial value and renewal conditions are determined individually and subject to the Scholarship Information Handbook requirements.
eligibility_criteria: Must sit the Scholarship Exam at West Moreton Anglican College; Current school results required; Demonstrated exceptional performing arts ability; Audition/practical performance required; Interview required
special_conditions: Applications close 20 February 2026. Audition required. Must provide Scholarship Resume with supporting documents including report cards, NAPLAN results, awards, certificates and evidence of artistic achievement.

overview: West Moreton Anglican College offers Special Talent Scholarships in Visual Arts to recognise and develop exceptional artistic talent. The scholarship supports students pursuing excellence in visual arts with access to specialist instruction and facilities.
description: Special Talent Scholarships in Visual Arts are awarded based on the Scholarship Exam, current school results, practical performance through portfolio submission, and interview. The scholarship programme is highly competitive and strictly limited. Applicants must demonstrate exceptional visual arts ability through portfolio assessment. Selection involves examination results, school reports, and artistic portfolio evaluation. Financial value and duration are determined individually and subject to terms outlined in the Scholarship Information Handbook.
eligibility_criteria: Must sit the Scholarship Exam at West Moreton Anglican College; Current school results required; Demonstrated exceptional visual arts ability; Portfolio required; Interview required
special_conditions: Applications close 20 February 2026. Portfolio submission required as part of application. Must provide Scholarship Resume with supporting documents including report cards, NAPLAN results, awards and artistic certificates.

overview: West Moreton Anglican College offers Special Talent Scholarships in Agriculture to recognise students with demonstrated excellence and passion in agricultural studies. The scholarship leverages the College's 38-hectare campus and specialised agricultural facilities.
description: Special Talent Scholarships in Agriculture are awarded based on the Scholarship Exam, current school results, practical performance, and interview. The scholarship programme is highly competitive and strictly limited, offering deserving students access to expert agriculture teachers and the College's extensive 38-hectare rural campus with specialist facilities. Applicants must demonstrate exceptional ability and interest in agricultural studies. Selection involves examination results, school reports, and practical assessment. Financial value and renewal conditions are determined individually per the Scholarship Information Handbook.
eligibility_criteria: Must sit the Scholarship Exam at West Moreton Anglican College; Current school results required; Demonstrated exceptional ability or interest in agriculture; Practical assessment may be required; Interview required
special_conditions: Applications close 20 February 2026. Must provide Scholarship Resume with supporting documents including report cards, NAPLAN results, and awards related to agriculture or rural studies.

overview: West Moreton Anglican College offers Special Talent Scholarships in Language to recognise students with exceptional language learning ability and achievement. The scholarship supports linguistic excellence and cultural understanding.
description: Special Talent Scholarships in Language are awarded based on the Scholarship Exam, current school results, practical performance (language assessment), and interview. The scholarship programme is highly competitive and strictly limited. Applicants must demonstrate exceptional language ability through assessment and school results. Selection involves examination results, school reports, and language proficiency evaluation. Financial value and renewal conditions are determined individually and subject to the Scholarship Information Handbook.
eligibility_criteria: Must sit the Scholarship Exam at West Moreton Anglican College; Current school results required; Demonstrated exceptional language learning ability; Language proficiency assessment required; Interview required
special_conditions: Applications close 20 February 2026. Must provide Scholarship Resume with supporting documents including report cards, NAPLAN results, language certificates and achievements.

overview: West Moreton Anglican College offers Special Talent Scholarships in Equestrian to recognise exceptional horse riding ability and equestrian talent. The scholarship provides support for elite equestrian athletes.
description: Special Talent Scholarships in Equestrian are awarded based on the Scholarship Exam, current school results, practical performance assessment, and interview. The scholarship programme is highly competitive and strictly limited, offering deserving equestrian athletes access to specialist coaching and enrichment opportunities. Applicants must demonstrate exceptional equestrian ability through practical assessment. Selection involves examination results, school reports, and equestrian performance evaluation. Financial value and duration are determined individually and subject to the Scholarship Information Handbook.
eligibility_criteria: Must sit the Scholarship Exam at West Moreton Anglican College; Current school results required; Demonstrated exceptional equestrian ability; Practical riding assessment required; Interview required
special_conditions: Applications close 20 February 2026. Practical riding assessment required. Must provide Scholarship Resume with supporting documents including report cards, NAPLAN results, equestrian certificates and competition results.

overview: Whitfield State School offers a Gifted Education Program that accelerates high-achieving students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability. The program provides enriched and accelerated learning opportunities within the school's structured literacy and numeracy framework.
description: The Gifted Education Program at Whitfield State School is designed for students who demonstrate advanced academic abilities and high achievement across core subject areas. Students identified for this program receive accelerated curriculum delivery and enriched learning experiences that challenge them beyond standard grade-level expectations. The program focuses on developing critical thinking, problem-solving, and advanced reasoning skills. Selection is based on teacher identification, academic performance data, and standardized assessment results. Students in the gifted program are integrated into regular classes but receive differentiated instruction and additional extension activities. The program operates throughout the school year and students remain in the program for the duration of their enrolment at the school, subject to annual review of progress and continued suitability.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated exceptional academic ability; Top academic performance across core subjects; Teacher nomination and identification; Academic progress meeting or exceeding advanced benchmarks; Current enrolment at Whitfield State School or applying for enrolment
special_conditions: Students must demonstrate sustained academic excellence and engagement with the enriched curriculum. Program participation is subject to annual review. Students identified for the program receive accelerated instruction within the regular classroom setting.

overview: Whitfield State School offers a Sporting Excellence Program that identifies and develops students with advanced sporting abilities. The program provides specialized coaching from regional and state-level coaches to help talented athletes reach the highest levels of competition.
description: The Sporting Excellence Program at Whitfield State School identifies students who possess and demonstrate particular advanced sporting skills and invites them to participate in skill-based testing and competitive development. Students who are selected into the program receive coaching from coaches sourced from the wider sporting community who have either played or coached at representative sport levels. The program is funded by the federal government's Sporting Schools program and provides regular training sessions outside of school hours. Students can participate in multiple excellence programs in different sports if selected across different sporting disciplines. The program recognizes elite sporting talent and provides pathway opportunities for students to compete at state and national levels. Selection is ongoing throughout the school year based on demonstrated skill level and performance in skills-based assessments.
eligibility_criteria: Identified possession of advanced sporting skills; Demonstrated excellence in a particular sport; Participation in skills-based testing; Invitation to join excellence squad; Current enrolment at Whitfield State School
special_conditions: Program participation occurs outside school hours. Students must commit to coaching sessions throughout the term. Selection is based on skills-based testing and demonstrated sporting ability. Funding is provided by the federal government's Sporting Schools program. Students may participate in more than one excellence program if chosen across different sports.

overview: Whitfield State School offers the PEAK Academy Program which connects students of similar interests and abilities through a wide range of quality extra-curricular activities. The program fosters leadership development and personal growth through structured clubs and activities.
description: The PEAK Academy Program at Whitfield State School provides structured opportunities for students to explore and develop their talents and interests through clubs and lunchtime activities. The program is designed to connect students with similar abilities and interests, creating communities of learners who engage in quality extra-curricular experiences. PEAK academies operate throughout the school year and offer diverse activities ranging from academic clubs to arts, sports, and cultural pursuits. The program supports students in developing leadership skills, collaboration abilities, and confidence in their chosen areas of interest. Participation in PEAK academies enhances students' overall school experience and contributes to their holistic development as well-rounded individuals. Activities are offered during lunchtime and after-school periods to accommodate student schedules.
eligibility_criteria: Current enrolment at Whitfield State School; Interest in specific PEAK academy focus area; Demonstrated commitment to participation; Open to all students regardless of prior experience
special_conditions: PEAK academies meet during lunchtime and after-school hours. Activities are designed to connect students of similar interests and abilities. Students are encouraged to develop leadership skills and contribute to the school community. Participation supports overall personal development and wellbeing.

overview: Wilsonton State High School offers an Academic Scholarship for outstanding students entering the Year 7 Academy ONE program. The scholarship recognises and supports high-achieving students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability in core curriculum areas and are selected for the Academy ONE extension program.
description: Students considering the Year 7 Academy ONE program may apply for an Academic Scholarship through guidelines outlined in the Scholarship Program documents. The Academy ONE Program is an extension opportunity for invited bright minded students who display high academic achievement in core curriculum areas. Specific financial benefits, application deadlines, and selection criteria are detailed in the scholarship program documents available from the school office. The scholarship supports qualified students entering Year 7 who demonstrate exceptional academic performance and eligibility for the school's selective extension program. Recipients must maintain academic standards to continue in the Academy ONE program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be invited to and enrolled in the Year 7 Academy ONE program; Must demonstrate high academic achievement in core curriculum areas; Eligible for students from all school sectors
special_conditions: Scholarship program documents and guidelines must be accessed from the school office. Selection is based on demonstrated high academic achievement in core curriculum areas and invitation to the Academy ONE program. Specific conditions regarding renewal and duration are contained in official scholarship program documents.

overview: William Ross State High School offers the Leading Learner Program to recognise and support academically gifted students in Years 7 to 10. The program provides extended learning in English, Mathematics, Science and Humanities to better prepare students for senior secondary studies.
