description: The Leading Learner Program extends student learning in English, Mathematics, Science and Humanities in order to better prepare them for senior school. This accelerated program is designed for students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to advanced learning. Students are required to sit an entrance examination during Grade 6 in order to be considered for admission to the Leading Learner classes. The program provides differentiated curriculum delivery and higher-order thinking opportunities across core academic subjects to challenge and extend gifted learners throughout Years 7-10.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must sit entrance examination in Grade 6; Must demonstrate exceptional academic ability; Open to all students within the school's catchment area
special_conditions: Students must maintain satisfactory academic progress to continue in the program. Program focuses on extension and enrichment across core academic subjects.

overview: William Ross State High School offers the RHINOS Sports Excellence Program to support talented athletes competing at regional, state and national levels. The program caters to elite athletes in basketball, cricket, hockey, netball, rugby union, soccer and touch football.
description: The RHINOS Sports Excellence Program is designed for junior and senior students who demonstrate elite sporting ability and competitive excellence. The program includes specialised coaching, training schedules and development pathways for students excelling in basketball, cricket, hockey, netball, rugby union, soccer and touch football. Athletes participate in extended training and competition opportunities aimed at developing regional, state and national level performance. The program recognises students who combine outstanding sporting achievement with academic commitment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 8-12; Must demonstrate elite sporting ability in one of the designated sports; Must be selected through school trials and assessment; Must maintain satisfactory academic and behavioural standards
special_conditions: Selection based on demonstrated sporting excellence and potential. Students must maintain minimum academic standards and acceptable behaviour to remain in the program. Participation may require additional training commitments and travel for competitions.

overview: William Ross State High School provides an Instrumental Music Program offering tuition in brass, woodwind, percussion and strings instruments. Students learn from qualified Education Queensland instrumental music teachers in a traditional program format.
description: The school runs a traditional Instrumental Music program that caters for brass, woodwind, percussion and strings students. Lessons are provided in small groups during the regular weekly timetable for half-hour sessions. Qualified Education Queensland instrumental music teachers provide instruction. Students must provide their own instruments for home practice, although some instruments can be borrowed from school for lessons. The program develops musical performance skills and provides opportunities for students to participate in school ensembles and music performances. Keyboard lessons are provided free of charge. Other instrumental tuition incurs a fee: Brass, Percussion, Strings, Woodwind: $40 per year; Guitar: $50 per term (payable at the beginning of each term).
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students; Small group lessons provided during timetabled periods; Must provide own instrument or borrow from school; Fees apply for most instruments except keyboard
special_conditions: Keyboard lessons are free of charge. Other instrumental tuition fees apply: Brass, Percussion, Strings, Woodwind $40 per year; Guitar $50 per term payable at beginning of each term. Students must provide own instruments except where school loan arrangements apply.

overview: William Ross State High School provides financial assistance to families experiencing hardship. Support is available through the Student Resources Scheme and Bring Your Own Device program to ensure all students can access learning resources and technology.
description: The school recognises that families may experience financial hardship and provides assistance through multiple support mechanisms. Parents experiencing financial difficulty with Student Resources Scheme participation can contact the school to make arrangements. The Department of Education provides a Textbook and Resource Allowance (TRA) to eligible Years 7-12 students, which is applied to reduce the cost of the SRS participation. Families facing hardship in accessing Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) technology can make arrangements through the school to access a device. The school works with families to ensure students are not disadvantaged due to financial circumstances and can fully participate in the curriculum and learning activities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students and families experiencing financial hardship; Eligible for Textbook and Resource Allowance through Department of Education; Must contact school to arrange assistance
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Parents must contact the school office to discuss financial hardship and arrange support. The school works on a case-by-case basis. Department of Education TRA is applied to reduce SRS costs for eligible students. Support includes device access for BYOD participants facing financial difficulty.

overview: William Ross State High School offers the award-winning STEAM Lab program to all students as a co-curricular opportunity. The program provides practical learning in science, technology, engineering and mathematics through innovative projects including robotics, aquaculture and renewable energy initiatives.
description: The award-winning STEAM Lab program is offered to all students as a co-curricular opportunity. The program offers a range of learning activities including participation in the QLD Human Powered Vehicle super series competition. Science-based projects address water conservation, renewable energy and sustainable food production through the use of aquaculture, aquaponics and permaculture in the school's aquatics facility. Mathematics and Technology combine to offer students the opportunity to develop drone pilot skills and programming with robotics and drones. The program provides hands-on, project-based learning that integrates science, technology, engineering, arts and mathematics across multiple disciplines.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students; Co-curricular participation; Interest in STEM subjects and practical project-based learning
special_conditions: Open to all students as a co-curricular opportunity. Program focuses on practical problem-solving and project-based learning in STEM fields.

overview: Whitsunday Anglican School offers Academic Scholarships to students who have consistently achieved a very high level of academic success. The scholarship recognises students who have performed in the top 4% of the Academic Assessment Services Test and demonstrates exceptional academic merit.
description: The Academic Scholarship is awarded to students entering Years 5 to 11 who achieve outstanding results in the annual scholarship examination administered by Academic Assessment Services (AAS). Selection is based on performance in the top 4% of the AAS Test. The scholarship provides financial assistance for tuition and/or boarding fees for the duration of the scholarship. Scholarships are subject to annual review based on the student's all-round performance and active involvement in school life, adherence to the School's Core Values and behavioural expectations. The Principal reserves the right to offer whole or part scholarships, or to award no scholarships at all.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 5 to Year 11; Must achieve top 4% performance on Academic Assessment Services Test; Academic performance and merit-based only; All school sectors eligible
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Scholarships are subject to annual review of the student's all-round performance and active involvement in school life, adherence to the School's Core Values and behavioural expectations and the family's ongoing financial position. In all awards, the Principal reserves the right to offer whole or part Scholarships, or to award no Scholarships at all. Normally, Scholarships will continue to apply for the stated duration of the scholarship.

overview: Whitsunday Anglican School offers the Indigenous Senior Scholarship to assist Senior School students with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander heritage to attend the school. The scholarship recognises current achievements and supports and encourages future endeavours and the development of Indigenous leadership.
description: The Indigenous Senior Scholarship provides financial assistance to Senior School students (Year 7 to Year 11) with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander heritage. The scholarship assists families in accessing a Whitsunday Anglican School education by providing support for tuition and/or boarding fees. Selection recognises current achievements and supports future endeavours while encouraging the development of Indigenous leadership. Interested candidates may submit an Expression of Interest at any time. No application fee applies to this program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 to Year 11; Must identify as Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander; Indigenous heritage required; Expression of Interest submission required; No application fee
special_conditions: Interested candidates may submit an Expression of Interest at any time. No fee applies to applications.

overview: Whitsunday Anglican School offers needs-based Bursaries or Equity Scholarships to families who, without financial assistance, would be unable to attend or continue to attend the school. The bursary recognises demonstrated financial hardship and provides support to ensure access to quality education.
description: Whitsunday Anglican School offers needs-based Bursaries or Equity Scholarships to families who, without financial assistance, would be unable to attend, or continue to attend, the School. A Bursary may cover all or part of tuition and/or boarding fees for the duration of the bursary. Each Bursary is subject to annual review of the student's all-round performance and active involvement in school life, adherence to the School's Core Values and behavioural expectations and the family's ongoing financial position. Current students can also apply for a Bursary through a demonstrated needs basis due to a significant change in circumstances. The Principal reserves the right to offer whole or part Bursaries, or to award no Bursaries at all.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all year levels; Demonstrated financial need required; Must be unable to attend or continue without financial assistance; Subject to annual review of student performance and family financial position; Adherence to School Core Values required; Available to current and new students
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Partial Fee Remission
special_conditions: Each Bursary is subject to annual review of the student's all-round performance and active involvement in school life, adherence to the School's Core Values and behavioural expectations and the family's ongoing financial position. Bursaries are not transferrable and apply only to the recipient's education. In all awards, the Principal reserves the right to offer whole or part Bursaries, or to award no Bursaries at all. Decisions communicated by the Principal are final. No communication will be accepted or entered into beyond the Principal's decision.

overview: Whitsunday Anglican School offers rural and boarding bursaries to assist rural and remote families access a Whitsunday Anglican School education. The bursary supports families in regional and remote areas who wish to provide their children with access to quality boarding education.
description: Whitsunday Anglican School offers rural and boarding bursaries specifically designed to assist rural and remote families access a Whitsunday Anglican School education. These bursaries provide financial support for both tuition and boarding fees, making the school accessible to families from regional and remote areas of Queensland. The bursaries recognise the geographic challenges faced by rural families and aim to remove financial barriers to attendance. Selection and award levels are subject to the school's assessment of family circumstances and demonstrated need.
eligibility_criteria: Open to rural and remote families; Boarding requirement likely; Geographic location criteria; Demonstrated need for rural access; Subject to school financial assessment
special_conditions: Specifically designed for rural and remote families. Bursaries are subject to annual review of the student's performance and the family's ongoing financial position.

overview: Wisdom College recognises that families can experience both short-term and long-term financial hardship as a result of loss of income through illness or other circumstances beyond their control. The College offers fee concessions and assistance to eligible families facing genuine financial difficulty.
description: Wisdom College provides financial hardship assistance to families experiencing genuine difficulty in meeting the fee requirements of the College. The program involves completing a Student Hardship Form and undergoing a financial assessment to determine eligibility. Following assessment, an interview with the Principal is conducted, and supporting documentation may be requested to enable a compassionate assessment of the family's circumstances. Fee concessions are available for situations of hardship, though the exact amount varies based on individual circumstances. Applications must be lodged before the end of Term 3 of each academic year. An account holder who has been awarded assistance must have no outstanding account balance for the current year in order to receive the current year's assistance.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; Must complete Student Hardship Form; Must provide supporting documentation of financial difficulty; Must not have outstanding account balance to receive assistance; Applications must be lodged before end of Term 3
special_conditions: An account holder who has been awarded a scholarship must have no outstanding account balance for the current year in order to receive the current year's scholarship. Supporting documentation may be requested to enable a compassionate assessment of the family's circumstances. Applications must be lodged before the end of term 3 of each academic year.

overview: Windaroo Valley State High School offers the Academic Scholarship to recognise and reward students who demonstrate outstanding academic achievement. The scholarship is designed for students who excel academically and meet the school's academic, behaviour and attendance criteria.
description: The Academic Scholarship covers the cost of the Student Resource Scheme for eligible Year 7 students. Students must achieve English grade A, Mathematics grade A, and all other subjects grade B or higher in their current year. The scholarship recognises academic excellence combined with evidence of leadership activities and participation in academic activities and competitions. Recipients can reapply annually in Years 8 and 9 for continued support. Applications require submission of supporting certificates and documentation demonstrating academic achievement and involvement in academic programs.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 students; Academic achievement in current year – English A; Mathematics A; All other subjects B or higher; Evidence of leadership activities; Participation in academic activities and competitions; Must meet behaviour and attendance criteria
special_conditions: Scholarships can be reapplied for each year in Years 8 and 9. Students must maintain behaviour and attendance standards to retain eligibility.

overview: Windaroo Valley State High School offers the High Performance Scholarship to recognise and support students who demonstrate excellence in sporting or cultural pursuits. The scholarship is designed for students who excel in a sporting or arts area and meet specified criteria.
description: The High Performance Scholarship covers the cost of the Student Resource Scheme for Year 7 students who excel in a sporting or arts area. The scholarship recognises excellence in elite sports or cultural performance, combined with positive behaviour and attendance records. Students must meet a specified sporting or cultural, behaviour and attendance criteria as determined by the school. Recipients can reapply annually in Years 8 and 9. Applications require supporting documentation such as competition results, performance records, or cultural achievement certificates to demonstrate excellence in their chosen area.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 students; Must excel in a sporting or arts area; Must meet specified sporting or cultural performance criteria; Must meet behaviour and attendance criteria
special_conditions: Scholarships can be reapplied for each year in Years 8 and 9. Students must maintain behaviour and attendance standards to retain eligibility. Applicants must provide evidence of excellence in sporting or cultural areas.

overview: Windaroo Valley State High School offers the S.T.E.M Scholarship to recognise and support students who excel and have a demonstrated interest in Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics. The scholarship is designed for students who show strong capability in STEM disciplines.
description: The S.T.E.M Scholarship covers the cost of the Student Resource Scheme for Year 7 students who excel in and have demonstrated interest in Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics. The scholarship supports students with strong STEM aptitude and engagement in science and mathematics learning. Students must demonstrate excellence in STEM-related subjects and meet the school's behaviour and attendance criteria. Recipients can reapply annually in Years 8 and 9 for continued support. Applications require submission of supporting documentation demonstrating STEM achievement, interest and participation in science, technology, engineering or mathematics competitions or programs.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 students; Must excel in STEM subjects; Must have demonstrated interest in Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics; Must meet behaviour and attendance criteria
special_conditions: Scholarships can be reapplied for each year in Years 8 and 9. Students must maintain behaviour and attendance standards to retain eligibility.

overview: The Catalyst Program is Woodcrest State College's extension program designed to develop and challenge high-performing students in order to maximize their potential. Catalyst students are encouraged to be critical and creative thinkers who use imagination and innovation in both teacher-facilitated and collaborative, inquiry-based learning activities.
description: The Catalyst Program is a four-year program that enables students to engage in academically rigorous, inquiry-based learning in order to attain academic excellence. Students from Years 7–10 participate in this program. Catalyst students are encouraged to be critical and creative thinkers who use imagination and innovation in both teacher-facilitated and collaborative, inquiry-based learning activities. The program develops and challenges high-performing students to maximize their potential through advanced academic content and extension activities. Financial value and selection processes are not fully documented in publicly available sources.
eligibility_criteria: Open to high-performing students; Students in Years 7 to 10; Academic excellence demonstrated

overview: Woodcrest State College offers the AFL Excellence Program to provide male and female students with talent in Australian Rules Football with an opportunity to develop their skills and provide a genuine learning pathway in AFL and sports studies through schooling and beyond.
description: The AFL Excellence Program is designed to provide male and female students in Years 7-9 who have identified AFL talent with an opportunity to develop their skills and provide a genuine learning pathway in AFL and sports studies through schooling and beyond. Students undertake AFL as one of their subjects while completing their Secondary schooling. The program requires students to abide by school behaviour expectations and policies, demonstrate sportsmanship and fair play at all times, and maintain school uniform standards. All students will be required to pay an annual fee which includes training shirt, referee costs, transport costs, equipment, competition levies and the cost of extra-curricular activities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to male and female students; Students in Years 7-9; Must have identified AFL talent; Must abide by school Code of Conduct; Must demonstrate sportsmanship and fair play; Must participate in all school AFL games and Woodcrest Sport carnivals
special_conditions: Annual fee required which includes training shirt, referee costs, transport costs, equipment, competition levies and extra-curricular activities. Students must attend practice sessions on time. Minimum 90% attendance expected at all school AFL games and carnivals. Academy shirts only to be worn during training sessions. School uniform must be worn correctly at all times.

overview: Woodcrest State College offers a Basketball Academy program as part of its Academy Sports Programs, providing elite-level coaching and access to competitions for students demonstrating exceptional basketball ability.
description: The Basketball Academy is one of four Academy programs offered at Woodcrest State College. Students selected in the Basketball Academy program are offered high-level coaching with access to elite competitions. The program is designed to develop students who demonstrate exceptional basketball ability and provides a pathway for continued development in the sport. Specific financial value, duration, and detailed selection processes are not publicly documented in available sources, but the program requires student commitment to training and competition participation.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students demonstrating exceptional basketball ability; Selection required for entry; Must commit to training and competition schedule

overview: Woodcrest State College offers a Volleyball Academy program as part of its Academy Sports Programs, providing elite-level coaching and access to competitions for students with exceptional volleyball ability.
description: The Volleyball Academy is one of four Academy programs offered at Woodcrest State College. Students selected in the Volleyball Academy program are offered high-level coaching with access to elite competitions. The program is designed to develop students who demonstrate exceptional volleyball ability and provides a pathway for continued development in the sport. Specific financial value, duration, and detailed selection processes are not publicly documented in available sources, but the program requires student commitment to training and competition participation.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students demonstrating exceptional volleyball ability; Selection required for entry; Must commit to training and competition schedule

overview: Woodcrest State College offers a Netball Academy program as part of its Academy Sports Programs, providing elite-level coaching and access to competitions for students with exceptional netball ability.
description: The Netball Academy is one of four Academy programs offered at Woodcrest State College. Students selected in the Netball Academy program are offered high-level coaching with access to elite competitions. The program is designed to develop students who demonstrate exceptional netball ability and provides a pathway for continued development in the sport. Specific financial value, duration, and detailed selection processes are not publicly documented in available sources, but the program requires student commitment to training and competition participation.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students demonstrating exceptional netball ability; Selection required for entry; Must commit to training and competition schedule

overview: Woree State High School offers STEM scholarships to recognise and support students with exceptional ability in Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics. These scholarships are available to students entering Year 7 from local primary schools within the school's catchment area.
description: In 2026, Woree State High School is offering five (5) Science, Technology Engineering and Mathematics (STEM) scholarships to students enrolled in local primary schools who live within the Woree State High School catchment area. The financial value of these scholarships has not been publicly disclosed on the official scholarship page. Eligible students must be enrolled in local primary schools and reside within the defined catchment area as specified in the school's Enrolment Management Plan. The selection process and specific assessment criteria are detailed in the application documentation available from the school. Students may apply for both the STEM scholarship and the Instrumental Music scholarship in the same application cycle.
eligibility_criteria: Students enrolled in local primary schools; Must live within the Woree State High School catchment area (Enrolment Management Plan); Entry into Year 7
special_conditions: Students may apply for both STEM and Instrumental Music scholarships. Scholarships are offered to students from the Woree State High School catchment area only.

overview: Woree State High School offers Instrumental Music scholarships to recognise and support students with exceptional musical ability and talent in instrumental performance. These scholarships are available to students entering Year 7 from local primary schools within the school's catchment area.
description: In 2026, Woree State High School is offering five (5) Instrumental Music scholarships to students enrolled in local primary schools who live within the Woree State High School catchment area. The financial value of these scholarships has not been publicly disclosed on the official scholarship page. Eligible students must be enrolled in local primary schools and reside within the defined catchment area as specified in the school's Enrolment Management Plan. Selection is based on demonstrated instrumental musical ability, which is typically assessed through audition. Students may apply for both the Instrumental Music scholarship and the STEM scholarship in the same application cycle.
eligibility_criteria: Students enrolled in local primary schools; Must live within the Woree State High School catchment area (Enrolment Management Plan); Entry into Year 7
special_conditions: Students may apply for both Instrumental Music and STEM scholarships. Scholarships are offered to students from the Woree State High School catchment area only.

overview: Woodridge State High School offers the Academics & Leadership Program to develop students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and strong leadership potential. This selective program is designed to foster empowered, resilient, world-changing students with commitment to excellence.
description: The Academics & Leadership Program is one of three Excellence Programs at Woodridge State High School. Students selected for this program participate in advanced academic opportunities and leadership development. The school provides scholarships to ensure accessibility to the program for all eligible students regardless of financial circumstances. Selection is based on demonstrated academic achievement, leadership capacity, and commitment to learning. Applicants must submit their most recent report card and NAPLAN results, participate in the application process which may include interviews with the Head of Department and members of the Executive, and demonstrate evidence of skill level and potential. Scholarships are awarded on the basis of merit and financial need.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering secondary school; Must demonstrate strong academic performance; Must show leadership potential; Catchment area students prioritised; Out-of-catchment applications considered once catchment capacity met
special_conditions: Students can apply for more than one Excellence Program but can only participate in ONE Excellence Program. Applications require completion of enrolment form and submission of report card and NAPLAN results. If there are large numbers of applicants, further supporting evidence may be required including video performance or trial day participation. Students must accept or decline offers by 3pm on Friday 31 October (Term 4 Week 5).

overview: Woodridge State High School's Junior Academy of Music program offers specialized music education and scholarships for students demonstrating exceptional musical talent and commitment. This program supports musically gifted students to develop their abilities within a structured excellence framework.
description: The Junior Academy of Music is one of three Excellence Programs at Woodridge State High School designed to nurture musically talented students. The program includes scholarships to ensure accessibility for all eligible students regardless of financial circumstances. Selection involves submission of video performances or participation in audition days, with applicants potentially required to provide further evidence of musical skill level at interview stage. The selection process includes interviews with the Head of Department and members of the Executive. Applicants must provide their most recent report card, NAPLAN results if available, and demonstrate musical competency through audition or portfolio. Scholarships support student participation in the program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering secondary school; Must demonstrate musical talent and achievement; Must be willing to participate in performances and ensembles; Catchment area students prioritised; Out-of-catchment applications considered once catchment capacity met
special_conditions: Students can apply for more than one Excellence Program but can only participate in ONE Excellence Program. May require video submission of musical performance or participation in trial audition day. Interviews with Head of Department and Executive member required for shortlisted applicants. Students must accept or decline offers by 3pm on Friday 31 October (Term 4 Week 5).

overview: Woodridge State High School's High Performance Sport program offers scholarships for students demonstrating elite athletic talent and sporting commitment. The program supports high-performing athletes within a framework of academic and character development alongside sporting excellence.
description: The High Performance Sport (HPS) program is one of three Excellence Programs at Woodridge State High School providing pathways for elite student-athletes. Scholarships are offered to ensure accessibility to the program for all eligible students regardless of financial circumstances. Selection is based on demonstrated sporting excellence, performance in relevant sports, and commitment to the program. The selection process may involve trial days and video submission of sporting performance, with shortlisted applicants proceeding to interviews with the Head of Department and Executive members. Applicants must provide their most recent report card, NAPLAN results if available, and evidence of sporting achievement and capability. The school aims to develop students as empowered, resilient, world-changing athletes.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering secondary school; Must demonstrate elite sporting ability or potential; Must show commitment to athletic development; Catchment area students prioritised; Out-of-catchment applications considered once catchment capacity met
special_conditions: Students can apply for more than one Excellence Program but can only participate in ONE Excellence Program. May require participation in trial day or submission of video performance demonstrating sporting ability. Interviews with Head of Department and Executive member required for shortlisted applicants. Students must accept or decline offers by 3pm on Friday 31 October (Term 4 Week 5).

school_name: Y Schools Queensland - Brisbane South (Parkinson Campus)
Y Schools Queensland - Ipswich (Limestone St Campus)

overview: Xavier Catholic College has established the College Endowment Fund as a perpetual fund to support students experiencing extreme financial hardship. This bursary provides annual financial assistance to eligible families who would otherwise struggle to afford tuition at the college.
description: The Xavier Catholic College Endowment Fund is a perpetual financial assistance program established to provide annual bursaries for children experiencing extreme financial hardship. The fund aims to ensure that financial circumstances do not prevent deserving students from accessing a Xavier Catholic College education. Bursaries are awarded to families whose financial situation would make it impossible for them to afford the tuition fees. The program operates on an annual basis with applications typically reviewed at designated times during the school year. Recipients are expected to maintain acceptable academic standards and conduct throughout their enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Experiencing extreme financial hardship; Current or prospective student at Xavier Catholic College; Family financial circumstances prevent access to education; All year levels eligible
special_conditions: Financial information and supporting documentation required to demonstrate extreme financial hardship. Bursaries are reviewed annually and continued based on ongoing financial need and satisfactory academic progress.

overview: Xavier Catholic College recognises outstanding academic achievement through its Academic Awards program. Students demonstrating consistent and sustained academic application and effort across a range of college subjects are eligible for recognition through bronze, silver, and gold tiered awards.
description: Academic Awards at Xavier Catholic College are presented annually to recognise students who demonstrate consistent and sustained academic application and effort in a range of college subjects. The program operates on a tiered system with three levels: Bronze, Silver, and Gold categories. These awards acknowledge academic excellence and reward students who maintain high standards of study and engagement with their coursework throughout the school year. Awards are presented at school assemblies and ceremonies, providing recognition of student achievement within the broader school community. While the awards themselves may carry non-monetary recognition value, they celebrate academic merit and encourage continued excellence.
eligibility_criteria: Consistent and sustained academic application across college subjects; Demonstrated academic effort in multiple subjects; Current enrolment at Xavier Catholic College; All year levels eligible
special_conditions: Awards are divided into three categories: Bronze, Silver, and Gold based on level of achievement. Selection is based on school assessment of academic performance and application throughout the school year. No separate application required.

overview: Xavier Catholic College honours student achievement in cultural pursuits through its Cultural Awards program. These awards recognise students demonstrating consistent and sustained participation and effort in extracurricular cultural experiences throughout the college.
description: Cultural Awards at Xavier Catholic College are presented to recognise students who demonstrate consistent and sustained application and effort across extracurricular cultural experiences. The awards celebrate participation and achievement in the broad range of cultural activities offered by the college, which may include music, drama, visual arts, debating, and other performance opportunities. Students are recognised through a tiered system similar to Academic Awards, with Bronze, Silver, and Gold categories reflecting different levels of involvement and achievement. These awards encourage students to engage with the cultural life of the college and recognise the importance of cultural development as part of holistic education. The awards are presented at school award assemblies and ceremonies throughout the year.
eligibility_criteria: Consistent and sustained participation in extracurricular cultural activities; Demonstrated application and effort in cultural experiences; Current enrolment at Xavier Catholic College; All year levels eligible
special_conditions: Awards are divided into three categories: Bronze, Silver, and Gold based on level of participation and achievement in cultural activities. Selection is based on school staff assessment of student involvement and contribution to cultural programs throughout the school year. No separate application required.

overview: Xavier Catholic College recognises student achievement in sports through its Sports Merit Awards program. These awards honour students who demonstrate consistent participation and contribution across a broad range of sporting teams and activities at the college.
description: Sports Merit Awards at Xavier Catholic College are presented annually to recognise students demonstrating consistent participation and contribution across a broad range of sporting teams at the college. The awards celebrate athletic involvement and sporting achievement within the wider college community and acknowledge the importance of physical education and sports participation in student development. Like Academic and Cultural Awards, Sports Merit Awards are organised into three tiered categories: Bronze, Silver, and Gold, reflecting different levels of involvement and sporting achievement. Students may receive recognition for team participation, individual performance, sportsmanship, and contribution to the college's sporting culture. These awards are presented at school assembly ceremonies throughout the year and provide public recognition of sporting achievement.
eligibility_criteria: Participation across a broad range of sporting teams; Demonstrated contribution to college sporting programs; Current enrolment at Xavier Catholic College; All year levels eligible
special_conditions: Awards are divided into three categories: Bronze, Silver, and Gold based on level of sporting participation and achievement. Recognition is based on school assessment of student participation across multiple sporting teams and contribution to college sports. No separate application required.

school_name: Y Schools Queensland - Logan (Victoria Point Campus)
Y Schools Queensland - Moreton Bay (Mango Hill Campus)

overview: Wynnum State High School offers an Academic Extension Program in Years 7, 8 and 9 for academically gifted and talented students. This selective class provides extension and enrichment through the Australian Curriculum in core subject areas including English, Humanities, Mathematics and Science. The program allows students to develop their academic talent in a collegiate atmosphere while developing leadership skills, personal confidence, and resilience.
description: The Academic Extension Program is a selective class offered in Years 7, 8 and 9 that provides extension and enrichment through the Australian Curriculum in core subject areas (English, Humanities, Mathematics and Science) for academically gifted and talented students. Students develop their academic talent in a collegiate atmosphere and develop leadership skills, personal confidence, and resilience. The curriculum is designed with careful consideration to a level of abstraction and complexity appropriate to the needs of high achieving learners. Curriculum provisions include an accelerated pace with fewer repetitions whilst still providing clear instruction and scaffolding; adjusted tasks to allow students to process more complex and abstract information and encourage higher order thinking skills; and opportunities to engage in learning with students with similar academic goals and shared interests. Special projects and participation in extension activities such as university visits, programs through local industry partners, Young Writer workshops, and entry to STEM competitions aim to challenge and extend student learning and expose students to real life opportunities. Selection is determined through analysis of the child's AGAT (Acer General Ability Test) score, two most recent semester report cards demonstrating high academic achievement, behaviour and effort, and any other supporting documentation such as ICAS or PAT test reports. There is no direct financial benefit but admission is selective.
eligibility_criteria: Current Year 6 students in catchment area; AGAT score analysis required; High level of academic achievement in recent report cards; Students applying when in Year 6 through enrolment packs; Application forms included in enrolment documentation
special_conditions: Application for entry to the academic extension class is completed when students are in Year 6 through an application included in enrolment packs. Families will be emailed exact details after applications have closed. If you have any queries about the academic extension class, contact the Head of Department Junior School on 3906 7333 or jgaff24@eq.edu.au.

overview: Wynnum State High School offers a time-honoured and well-established Instrumental Music Program designed for students from Years 7 to 12. The program values the development of creative thinkers and young people as emerging artists. Students who participate in the program play in performance ensembles such as the Symphonic Band, Big Band or Concert Band and receive weekly group lessons with qualified Instrumental Music teachers.
description: Wynnum State High School has a time-honoured and well-established Instrumental Music Program designed for students from Years 7 to 12 that values the development of creative thinkers and young people as emerging artists. The program includes large ensembles and group lessons where students will participate in one or more performance ensembles or bands. There are ensembles for instruments and vocalists not involved in the traditional Instrumental Music Program including Piano, Guitar and Singing. Students will engage in one practical tutorial each week in addition to their instrumental music lesson, with additional lessons occurring before or after school. All ensemble and band rehearsals are held before or after school. The program has a fee of approximately $160-$276 depending on year level. The program is selective and selection is determined through an audition on the student's instrument or voice, plus any other supporting documentation such as past music exams. Application forms are to be submitted to the school administration by early September. Students in the Instrumental Music Program may be charged additional fees over and above the SRS amount.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 7 to 12; Audition on student's instrument or voice required; Supporting documentation such as past music exams; Co-curricular activity participation
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Application forms are to be submitted to the school administration by Friday 5th September. For queries about the Instrumental Music Program, contact the Head of Department Timothy Cooper. Instrumental Music Program fees include external conductors/masterclasses, theory exam, sheet music, ensemble competition fees, equipment servicing/incidental accessories and catering for student/parents at some performance events. Not included is the theory book, optional practical instrumental exam, practical instrumental repertoire book, eisteddfod fees, tours, and excursions.

overview: Wynnum State High School offers a Music Pathways Program that provides students with a diverse range of extension opportunities in instrumental music, from Symphonic and Big Bands to Vocal Groups, Strings Ensemble, solo repertoire, and theory lessons. The program emphasizes musical excellence, individual growth, collaboration, and a supportive musical environment where students of all backgrounds can flourish.
description: The Music Pathways Program offers a diverse range of extension opportunities from Symphonic and Big Bands to Vocal Groups, Strings Ensemble, solo repertoire, and theory lessons. The program emphasizes musical excellence, individual growth, collaboration, and a supportive musical environment where students of all backgrounds can flourish. Instrumental music includes large ensembles and group lessons where students will participate in one or more performance ensembles or bands, including ensembles for instruments and vocalists not involved in the traditional Instrumental Music Program such as Piano, Guitar and Singing. Students will engage in one practical tutorial each week in addition to their instrumental music lesson, with additional lessons occurring before or after school. All ensemble and band rehearsals are held before or after school. The Music Pathways Program fee is $240, though those students in the Instrumental Music Program already paying fees will receive a $120 discount. Fees include external conductors/masterclasses, theory exam, sheet music, ensemble competition fees, equipment servicing/incidental accessories and catering for student/parents at some performance events. Selection is determined through audition on the student's instrument or voice and any other supporting documentation such as past music exams.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students from Years 7 to 12; Audition on student's instrument or voice required; Any other supporting documentation such as past music exams acceptable; Currently enrolled students or prospective students
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Students in the Instrumental Music Program already paying fees will receive a $120 discount on the Music Pathways Program fee. Prices are reviewed annually and are subject to change. Application forms are to be submitted to the school administration by Friday 5th September 2025. For queries about the Music Pathways Program, contact the Head of Department Timothy Cooper. Fees include external conductors/masterclasses, theory exam, sheet music, ensemble competition fees, equipment servicing/incidental accessories and catering for student/parents at some performance events. Not included is the theory book, optional practical instrumental exam, practical instrumental repertoire book, eisteddfod fees, tours, and excursions.

overview: Wynnum State High School offers a Dance Program for students from Years 7 to 12. The school has talented Dance Troupe students who participate in competitions and performances. The program allows students to develop as dancers and explore various dance styles and choreographic techniques.
description: Wynnum State High School offers a Dance Program as part of its Creative Arts offerings for students from Years 7 to 12. The program allows students to study dance as an elective throughout their secondary education. All Arts programs are designed to teach students the skills and knowledge they need to develop as artists in their selected field of creative endeavour, to promote creative thinking and problem solving and to allow students the freedom to express themselves artistically in a safe and supportive environment. Students in the Dance Program engage with various dance forms including Bollywood, Indigenous, Japanese Kabuki, Greek Zorba and African dance styles. They learn choreographic devices and how they can be utilised within dance works. Students participate in performance programs, competitions such as the Quota Dance Eisteddfod, and other dance competitions. The program involves additional fees over and above the SRS amount, approximately $150-$276 depending on year level. Selection involves demonstration of dance ability and interest in participating in performances and competitions.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 7 to 12; Demonstration of dance ability or interest required; Interest in participating in performances and competitions; Additional fees payable beyond standard SRS
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Dance Program students are charged a fee over and above the SRS amount: approximately $150-$200 for Years 7-10 and $276 for Years 11-12 (based on 2020-2021 data; prices subject to review). Students participate in dance competitions, performances and eisteddfods. Coaches and teachers provide guidance and support for performance and choreography development.

program_name: SHAPE Program (Sport, Health and Physical Education)
overview: Wynnum State High School offers the SHAPE (Sport, Health and Physical Education) Program that aims to extend students who excel in Health and Physical Education. The highest performing HPE students are invited into the program for Years 8 to 10. Selected students then have the opportunity to extend themselves further in Years 9 and 10 PE. The major goal is to develop students with potential to study Physical Education when they enter their senior schooling years.
description: The SHAPE (Sport, Health and Physical Education) Program aims to extend students who excel in Health and Physical Education. The highest performing HPE students are invited into the program for Years 8 to 10. Selected students then have the opportunity to extend themselves further in Years 9 and 10 PE. The major goal of the program is to develop students with potential to study Physical Education when they enter their senior schooling years. SHAPE involves an alternate program to offer students a unique HPE experience. Students will still cover the core theory units, however, they will cover a wider range of content. They will also undergo an integration of sport-related theory through participation in their studied physical activity and additional training sessions. Training sessions will run Thursdays from 7.45 am to 8.40 am before school. The program is selective and based on demonstrated excellence in Health and Physical Education. There is no additional financial cost to students already paying the Student Resource Scheme.
eligibility_criteria: Highest performing HPE students selected for Years 8-10; Demonstration of excellence in Health and Physical Education required; Ability to attend Thursday morning training sessions from 7.45-8.40 am; Students identified by HPE department
special_conditions: Training sessions run Thursdays from 7.45-8.40 am. The school understands some students may have other training or commitments that may clash with these times and requests students notify the HPE department if this is the case. Students extend themselves through participation in studied physical activity and additional training sessions. Students cover a wider range of content than core theory units.

school_name: Y Schools Queensland - Moreton Bay (Kepnock Campus)

overview: Yeronga State High School offers the STEM Academy for high achieving and goal-driven students interested in science, technology, engineering and mathematics. The academy places like-minded students in a challenging and rigorous environment designed to nurture and celebrate their unique talents through real-world problem solving.
description: The STEM Academy at Yeronga State High School teaches STEM using a cross-disciplinary approach with a focus on real-world problem solving through project-based and inquiry-based learning. Students in this academy develop skills, attributes, talents, and academic knowledge beyond the standard classroom setting. The program features professional partnerships with tertiary and business organisations to ensure real-world applications. Students gain hands-on experience in areas such as advanced manufacturing, design, prototyping and manufacturing. There is no direct financial cost stated for this program, as it is part of the government school's curriculum offerings. Interested students must complete an application form and may be assessed on their academic performance and potential.
eligibility_criteria: Open to high achieving and goal-driven students; Must demonstrate interest and aptitude in STEM subjects; Application required; Open to all school sectors
special_conditions: Part of the government school's specialist program offerings. Students can be withdrawn from the program if academic performance or behaviour standards are not maintained.

overview: Yeronga State High School offers The Arts Academy for young artists of all types who wish to develop their creative abilities and artistic interests. The academy mentors and supports students' artistic development through rich, creative experiences and connection to the wider art world.
description: The Arts Academy is designed for a range of young artists including performers, visual artists, dancers, and other creative practitioners. The program places an intense focus on students' art journey through school, ensuring rich, creative experiences, access to artistic experts and connection to the outside art world are threaded through their school lives and education. Each student develops an individualised 'art map' each year guiding their growth and development. The academy partners with Melbourne University and aligns with competency-based philosophy to ensure academic rigor. There is an additional annual cost of $200 for students participating in the Arts Academy. Students must maintain academic performance and behaviour standards to remain in the program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to young artists demonstrating interest and ability in the arts; Must complete Arts Academy application form; Participation requires commitment to artistic development; Students can be withdrawn if performance or behaviour standards are not maintained; Annual $200 fee required
special_conditions: Students must maintain academic performance and behaviour standards to remain in the program. Students can be withdrawn from the Specialist Academy Program if such standards are not maintained. Additional annual cost of $200 required for participation.

overview: Yeronga State High School offers The Athletes Academy for outstanding and aspiring young athletes who wish to develop their sporting capabilities while maintaining their academic pursuits. The academy has been co-designed with industry representatives to provide a platform for ambitious young athletes to follow their passion and refine their fitness.
description: The Athletes Academy provides high-performing and aspiring young athletes with the opportunity to train with highly qualified coaches and receive guidance from elite athletes while acquiring theoretical knowledge about becoming a successful athlete. The academy offers individualised high performance training programs suitable for any sport. Students learn fundamentals to develop athletic performance including strength and conditioning, nutrition for the athlete, sports psychology, injury management and prevention, goal setting and careers in sport. Athletes are given various opportunities to refine their practice and test their skills in competitions throughout the year. There is no direct tuition cost for this program as it is part of the government school's offerings. Students can participate in Years 7-10 (Junior) or Years 11-12 (Senior) with separate application forms required for each level.
eligibility_criteria: Open to outstanding and aspiring young athletes; Demonstrated sporting ability and commitment required; Must submit appropriate application form for year level (Junior Years 7-10 or Senior Years 11-12); Participation contingent on maintaining academic performance and behaviour standards; Open to all school sectors
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Students must submit either the Junior Athletes Academy Application Form (Year 7-10) or Senior Athletes Academy Application Form (Year 11-12). Participants must maintain satisfactory academic performance and behaviour standards or can be withdrawn from the program. Students participate in competitions throughout the year to test and refine their skills.

overview: YOS Lawnton provides a fee-free education to all enrolled students as part of its unique Model of Care. The school supports young people aged 14-18 who have disengaged from mainstream education due to individual, relational or social factors, with the school distributing 100% of Commonwealth and State education funding to benefit students and the community.
description: YOS Lawnton, a Special Assistance School operated by The Salvation Army Independent Schools, provides full fee remission for all Year 10-12 students. The school's unique Model of Care ensures no students incur tuition fees. The Salvation Army distributes 100% of all Commonwealth and State education funding to the schools for the benefit of students and the communities served. This fee-free approach removes financial barriers to education for young people who have struggled with adversities including mental and physical health challenges, legal matters, and other educational barriers. The program is available for the duration of enrolment at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students aged 14-18 years; Enrolled in Year 10, 11, or 12; Must have disengaged from or be at significant risk of disengaging from mainstream education due to individual, relational or social factors; No financial need assessment required due to universal fee-free structure
special_conditions: As a Special Assistance School, YOS Lawnton is designed specifically for students who have disengaged from mainstream education. Students receive additional support through youth workers, teacher aides, and specialized programs. The school also provides accommodation, life skills development, and driving programs as part of its holistic support model. Students participate in vocational programs including Certificate II qualifications in Hospitality and Self Awareness and Development.
